description: An expedition explores a massive artificial ring encircling a star, discovering that the megastructure's builders have vanished and its systems are failing.

title: Unbroken
author: Laura Hillenbrand
description: An Olympic runner survives a plane crash in the Pacific, 47 days on a raft, and years of brutal captivity in Japanese POW camps during World War II.

title: Seabiscuit
author: Laura Hillenbrand
description: The true story of a knobby-kneed racehorse, his one-eyed jockey, and the team of misfits who turned him into a Depression-era symbol of hope.

title: The Looming Tower
author: Lawrence Wright
description: The road to 9/11 traced through the rise of al-Qaeda, the rivalry between the FBI and CIA, and the failures that allowed the attacks to happen.

title: Six of Crows
author: Leigh Bardugo
description: Six outcasts attempt an impossible heist on a heavily fortified prison in a fantasy world inspired by Dutch mercantile culture, gangs, and magic.

title: Crooked Kingdom
author: Leigh Bardugo
description: Betrayed and outgunned, the crew returns to Ketterdam to settle scores and pull off an even more audacious con against the city's most powerful figures.

title: Shadow and Bone
author: Leigh Bardugo
description: An orphaned mapmaker discovers she has the power to summon light and may be the key to destroying the darkness that divides her Russia-inspired nation.

title: Anna Karenina
author: Leo Tolstoy
description: A married aristocrat's passionate affair with a dashing officer unravels her life and reputation, paralleled by a landowner's quieter search for meaning in work and faith.

title: War and Peace
author: Leo Tolstoy
description: Five aristocratic families navigate love, ambition, and death during Napoleon's invasion of Russia, in a novel that captures history through the lives of individuals.

title: The Death of Ivan Ilyich
author: Leo Tolstoy
description: A successful judge falls ill and faces death, realizing in his final days that his entire respectable life was lived wrongly.

title: Hadji Murad
author: Leo Tolstoy
description: A Chechen warrior defects to the Russians during the Caucasus Wars, caught between two empires that both use and discard him.

title: Resurrection
author: Leo Tolstoy
description: A nobleman recognizes a prostitute on trial as the servant girl he once seduced and ruined, and follows her to Siberia seeking redemption.

title: The Magicians
author: Lev Grossman
description: A brilliant but depressed young man is admitted to a secret college of magic and discovers that even in a fantasy world, unhappiness follows you.

title: The Magician King
author: Lev Grossman
description: Now a king of Fillory, Quentin embarks on a quest to the edge of the world while Julia's harrowing secret origin as a hedge witch is revealed.

title: The Magician's Land
author: Lev Grossman
description: Exiled from Fillory, Quentin returns to Brakebills and undertakes a magical heist, while the land he loved faces its apocalyptic end.

title: The Three-Body Problem
author: Liu Cixin
description: A physicist discovers that an alien civilization in a triple-star system has targeted Earth, and a secret faction has been communicating with them for decades.

title: The Dark Forest
author: Liu Cixin
description: Earth prepares for invasion over four centuries while a sociology professor is recruited as a secret strategist, developing a terrifying theory about why the universe seems empty.

title: Death's End
author: Liu Cixin
description: An aerospace engineer wakes from hibernation to find that deterrence against the alien fleet is failing, and the universe's darkest laws begin to operate on the solar system.

title: Under the Volcano
author: Malcolm Lowry
description: An alcoholic British consul in Mexico drinks himself to death on the Day of the Dead, in a novel that turns one man's collapse into a symphonic vision of hell on earth.

title: The Autobiography of Malcolm X
author: Malcolm X
description: Malcolm X traces his journey from street hustler to prison convert to Nation of Islam leader to his break with Elijah Muhammad and pilgrimage to Mecca, completed just before his assassination.

title: In Search of Lost Time
author: Marcel Proust
description: A narrator recalls his life from childhood through old age in seven volumes, exploring memory, time, art, and desire in the most expansive novel ever written.

title: Meditations
author: Marcus Aurelius
description: A Roman emperor's private journal of Stoic philosophy, written on military campaigns, on how to endure suffering, govern justly, and accept death.

title: The Handmaid's Tale
author: Margaret Atwood
description: In a theocratic America where fertile women are enslaved as breeders, one handmaid narrates her constrained existence and tiny acts of resistance.

title: Oryx and Crake
author: Margaret Atwood
description: The apparent last human survivor watches over a tribe of genetically engineered post-humans, remembering the brilliant friend who destroyed civilization to create them.

title: The Blind Assassin
author: Margaret Atwood
description: An elderly woman recounts her family's destruction across the 20th century, embedding within her memoir a pulp sci-fi novel written by her dead sister's secret lover.

title: Cat's Eye
author: Margaret Atwood
description: A painter returns to Toronto for a retrospective and is forced to confront the childhood bullying that shaped her art and her inability to trust other women.

title: Alias Grace
author: Margaret Atwood
description: A young servant convicted of a double murder in 1840s Canada tells her story to a psychiatrist, and the truth remains tantalizingly out of reach.

title: Housekeeping
author: Marilynne Robinson
description: Two orphaned sisters in a small Idaho town are raised by their eccentric, transient aunt, whose pull toward the wilderness threatens to dissolve the boundaries of domestic life.

title: Gilead
author: Marilynne Robinson
description: An aging Iowa minister writes a letter to his young son, reflecting on his life, his father, the Civil War, and the return of his best friend's troubled namesake son.

title: Home
author: Marilynne Robinson
description: A prodigal son returns to his father's house in Gilead, and his sister watches their mutual need and failure of understanding unfold with heartbreaking clarity.

title: Lila
author: Marilynne Robinson
description: A drifter with a hard past marries the old minister of Gilead and struggles to believe she deserves the grace and stability he offers.

title: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn
author: Mark Twain
description: A boy and a runaway slave raft down the Mississippi, encountering con men, feuds, and the moral crisis of whether to turn in his friend or follow his conscience.

title: The Adventures of Tom Sawyer
author: Mark Twain
description: A mischievous boy in a Mississippi River town whitewashes fences, witnesses a murder, hunts for treasure, and gets lost in a cave with the girl he loves.

title: A Connecticut Yankee in King Arthur's Court
author: Mark Twain
description: A 19th-century American wakes up in Arthurian England and tries to modernize the kingdom with technology, exposing the absurdity of monarchy and the Church.

title: The Sparrow
author: Mary Doria Russell
description: A Jesuit mission to an alien world ends in catastrophe, and the sole survivor returns to Earth physically and spiritually broken, forced to explain what went wrong.

title: The Liars' Club
author: Mary Karr
description: A memoir of growing up in an East Texas refinery town with an alcoholic father, a volatile mother, and the storytelling that held the family together and apart.

title: Frankenstein
author: Mary Shelley
description: A young scientist creates a living being from dead tissue and abandons it, setting off a chain of murder and grief as the creature demands a companion or revenge.

title: A Vindication of the Rights of Woman
author: Mary Wollstonecraft
description: Wollstonecraft argues that women appear inferior only because they are denied education and independence, making the first major feminist case for equal rights.

title: I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings
author: Maya Angelou
description: Angelou's memoir of her childhood in segregated Arkansas, her rape at age eight, her years of silence, and her discovery of literature and her own voice.

title: Titus Groan
author: Mervyn Peake
description: In the vast, decaying castle of Gormenghast, a baby heir is born while a kitchen boy begins a ruthless climb to power through the castle's suffocating rituals.

title: Gormenghast
author: Mervyn Peake
description: The young Earl Titus grows restless under the castle's ancient traditions while the scheming Steerpike consolidates power in a Gothic world of ritual and madness.

title: Titus Alone
author: Mervyn Peake
description: Titus escapes Gormenghast and wanders into a surreal modern world, struggling to prove the castle was real and to find his place outside its walls.

title: The Hours
author: Michael Cunningham
description: Three women across different eras—Virginia Woolf writing Mrs Dalloway, a 1950s housewife reading it, and a modern New Yorker living it—are linked by the novel and the question of how to live.

title: The Neverending Story
author: Michael Ende
description: A bullied boy reads a magical book and is drawn into the fantasy world of Fantastica, where he must save it from the Nothing by giving its empress a new name.

title: Elric of Melniboné
author: Michael Moorcock
description: An albino emperor bound to a soul-drinking black sword wanders a dying world, torn between chaos and law in the foundational anti-heroic fantasy.

title: The English Patient
author: Michael Ondaatje
description: In a bombed Italian villa at the end of World War II, a nurse tends a dying burn victim whose memories of a love affair in the Sahara unfold in fragments.

title: Essays
author: Michel de Montaigne
description: The inventor of the essay examines himself—his fears, habits, body, and reading—with radical honesty, creating a new literary form from the raw material of a single life.

title: Don Quixote
author: Miguel de Cervantes
description: A minor nobleman driven mad by chivalric romances sets out as a knight-errant with his loyal squire, tilting at windmills and inventing the modern novel.

title: The Master and Margarita
author: Mikhail Bulgakov
description: The Devil visits 1930s Moscow with a retinue that includes a giant cat, wreaking havoc on the literary establishment while a writer and his lover pursue redemption through a novel about Pontius Pilate.

title: The Unbearable Lightness of Being
author: Milan Kundera
description: Two couples in Prague before and after the 1968 Soviet invasion navigate love, infidelity, and the weight of political life in a novel that philosophizes as it narrates.

title: The Book of Laughter and Forgetting
author: Milan Kundera
description: Seven variations on the themes of memory, forgetting, and political erasure in Communist Czechoslovakia, blending fiction, autobiography, and philosophy.

title: Pachinko
author: Min Jin Lee
description: Four generations of a Korean family in Japan endure discrimination, poverty, and the struggle for identity from the 1910s through the 1980s.

title: The Tale of Genji
author: Murasaki Shikibu
description: The romantic and political life of a prince in Heian-era Japan, written a thousand years ago and often called the world's first novel.

title: The Fifth Season
author: N.K. Jemisin
description: On a continent wracked by apocalyptic seismic events, a woman with the power to control earthquakes searches for her kidnapped daughter while civilization collapses.

title: The Obelisk Gate
author: N.K. Jemisin
description: Essun shelters in an underground community while her daughter Nassun comes into terrifying power, and the obelisks floating in the sky reveal their ancient purpose.

title: The Stone Sky
author: N.K. Jemisin
description: Mother and daughter converge on opposite sides of a choice that will either restore the moon and end the Seasons or destroy the Earth entirely.

title: The Hundred Thousand Kingdoms
author: N.K. Jemisin
description: A young woman is summoned to the floating palace of the world's ruling family, where enslaved gods serve the court and she becomes a pawn in divine politics.

title: The Power
author: Naomi Alderman
description: Women develop the ability to generate electrical jolts, and as the power balance between genders shifts overnight, the world reorganizes—violently.

title: Spinning Silver
author: Naomi Novik
description: A moneylender's daughter boasts she can turn silver into gold, drawing the attention of a winter king who takes her at her word in a fairy tale retelling set in a frozen kingdom.

title: Uprooted
author: Naomi Novik
description: A young woman is taken by a wizard to serve in his tower near a malevolent forest, discovering her own wild magic that defies his orderly methods.

title: Snow Crash
author: Neal Stephenson
description: A pizza-delivering hacker-samurai investigates a drug/virus that crashes both computers and human minds, tracing it to ancient Sumerian neurolinguistics.

title: Cryptonomicon
author: Neal Stephenson
description: World War II codebreakers and their present-day descendants converge on a hoard of Nazi gold and the creation of a data haven in Southeast Asia.

title: Anathem
author: Neal Stephenson
description: Monks on an alien world who practice pure mathematics instead of religion are called out of seclusion when a spacecraft arrives from another cosmos.

title: The Diamond Age
author: Neal Stephenson
description: A stolen interactive book designed to educate an elite girl falls into the hands of a slum child, transforming her life in a nanotech-powered future.

title: American Gods
author: Neil Gaiman
description: A recently released convict becomes bodyguard to a conman who is actually Odin, recruiting old gods brought to America by immigrants for a war against the new gods of technology and media.

title: The Sandman
author: Neil Gaiman
description: The Lord of Dreams escapes decades of imprisonment and must rebuild his realm, encountering gods, demons, and ordinary humans across a mythic ten-volume epic.

title: Neverwhere
author: Neil Gaiman
description: A London businessman helps an injured girl and falls into London Below, a shadow city beneath the streets populated by saints, monsters, and an angel who is anything but.

title: The Ocean at the End of the Lane
author: Neil Gaiman
description: A man returns to his childhood home and remembers the terrifying supernatural events that occurred when he was seven, and the ancient family of women who saved him.

title: Good Omens
author: Terry Pratchett & Neil Gaiman
description: An angel and a demon who have grown fond of Earth team up to prevent the Apocalypse, but the Antichrist has been misplaced and raised by the wrong family.

title: The Prince
author: Niccolò Machiavelli
description: A treatise on acquiring and maintaining political power, advising rulers that it is better to be feared than loved and that the ends justify the means.

title: The Naked and the Dead
author: Norman Mailer
description: An infantry platoon's assault on a Japanese-held island in World War II, exposing the brutality of combat and the power struggles within the American military itself.

title: On Earth We're Briefly Gorgeous
author: Ocean Vuong
description: A letter from a Vietnamese-American son to his illiterate mother, moving between war-torn Vietnam, Hartford's working class, and a doomed love affair with an opioid-addicted boy.

title: Kindred
author: Octavia Butler
description: A Black woman in 1970s Los Angeles is repeatedly pulled back in time to a pre-Civil War plantation, where she must save the life of the white slaveholder who is her ancestor.

title: Parable of the Sower
author: Octavia Butler
description: In a near-future America of walled communities and roving gangs, a young woman with hyperempathy syndrome founds a new religion and leads a group of survivors north.

title: Parable of the Talents
author: Octavia Butler
description: Lauren Olamina's Earthseed community faces destruction by a Christian fascist president, and her daughter narrates the cost of her mother's visionary ambition.

title: Dawn
author: Octavia Butler
description: After nuclear war, a woman wakes on an alien ship where her rescuers want to trade humanity's survival for the right to genetically merge with the species.

title: Bloodchild
author: Octavia Butler
description: A collection including the Hugo-winning title story about humans living on an alien planet who serve as incubators for their hosts' larvae—a love story told through body horror.

title: The Man Who Mistook His Wife for a Hat
author: Oliver Sacks
description: A neurologist's case studies of patients with bizarre brain disorders, told with compassion and wonder as windows into the strangeness of human consciousness.

title: Awakenings
author: Oliver Sacks
description: Sacks administers L-DOPA to patients who have been frozen in a post-encephalitic state for decades, watching them return to life and then, heartbreakingly, regress.

title: Ender's Game
author: Orson Scott Card
description: A gifted child is recruited to a military academy in space where war games prepare him to command humanity's fleet against an alien invasion—or so he's told.

title: Speaker for the Dead
author: Orson Scott Card
description: Three thousand years later, Ender travels to a colony where a second alien species has been found, seeking to atone for the genocide he committed as a child.

title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
author: Oscar Wilde
description: A beautiful young man's portrait ages and decays while he remains youthful, pursuing a life of sensation and cruelty until the painting's corruption becomes unbearable.

title: The Importance of Being Earnest
author: Oscar Wilde
description: Two men maintain fictional alter egos to escape social obligations, and their deceptions collide in a comedy that skewers Victorian earnestness with every line.

title: The Windup Girl
author: Paolo Bacigalupi
description: In a future Bangkok flooded by climate change and dominated by biotech corporations, a genetically engineered woman fights for survival and autonomy.

title: The Name of the Wind
author: Patrick Rothfuss
description: A legendary figure now running a quiet inn tells the true story of his youth—an orphan who bluffed his way into a university of magic and hunted the beings who killed his family.

title: The Wise Man's Fear
author: Patrick Rothfuss
description: Kvothe continues his story: expelled from the university, he trains with mercenaries, studies with a fae seductress, and learns the martial arts of a warrior culture.

title: Just Kids
author: Patti Smith
description: Smith's memoir of her relationship with Robert Mapplethorpe, from their meeting as struggling artists in late-1960s New York through fame, art, and his death.

title: Tinkers
author: Paul Harding
description: A dying man hallucinating in a hospital bed relives his own memories and those of his father, an itinerant clock repairman, in prose as intricate as clockwork.

title: The Snow Leopard
author: Peter Matthiessen
description: A naturalist treks through the Himalayas searching for the rare snow leopard, but the real journey is through grief, Buddhism, and the question of what we seek when we seek.

title: The Last Unicorn
author: Peter S. Beagle
description: A unicorn learns she may be the last of her kind and sets out to find the others, joined by a bumbling magician and a tough woman, in a lyrical meditation on mortality and beauty.

title: Blindsight
author: Peter Watts
description: A crew of radically modified humans investigates an alien presence at the edge of the solar system, confronting the possibility that consciousness is an evolutionary dead end.

title: Echopraxia
author: Peter Watts
description: A baseline human is swept into a conflict between hive-minded monks and military posthumans as Earth reels from the implications of first contact.

title: Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep?
author: Philip K. Dick
description: A bounty hunter in a post-nuclear San Francisco hunts rogue androids so realistic they may be more human than the humans who fear them.

title: The Man in the High Castle
author: Philip K. Dick
description: In an America divided between Nazi Germany and Imperial Japan after losing World War II, a banned novel describes a world where the Allies won—which may be the real reality.

title: Ubik
author: Philip K. Dick
description: After an explosion, a group of psychics find reality deteriorating around them—objects regressing to older versions, money decaying—while a spray-can product called Ubik may be the only salvation.

title: A Scanner Darkly
author: Philip K. Dick
description: An undercover narcotics agent becomes so addicted to the drug he's investigating that his brain splits and he begins surveilling himself.

title: VALIS
author: Philip K. Dick
description: A man who may be Philip K. Dick is struck by a pink beam of light and becomes convinced an ancient intelligence is communicating with him, blurring fiction, theology, and madness.

title: The Golden Compass
author: Philip Pullman
description: A girl and her shape-shifting daemon travel to the Arctic to rescue kidnapped children, uncovering a conspiracy involving a mysterious particle called Dust.

title: The Subtle Knife
author: Philip Pullman
description: Lyra enters a parallel Oxford and meets a boy with a knife that cuts between worlds, as the war against the Authority begins to take shape.

title: The Amber Spyglass
author: Philip Pullman
description: Lyra and Will journey to the land of the dead and back as armies gather for the final battle against the Kingdom of Heaven.

title: American Pastoral
author: Philip Roth
description: A perfect American's life is destroyed when his daughter bombs a post office during the Vietnam era, and his pastoral dream of Newark gives way to chaos.

title: Portnoy's Complaint
author: Philip Roth
description: A young Jewish man pours out his sexual obsessions, mother fixation, and guilt to his psychoanalyst in a monologue that is both hilarious and harrowing.

title: The Human Stain
author: Philip Roth
description: A disgraced classics professor hiding a secret about his racial identity begins an affair with an illiterate janitor during the Clinton impeachment year.

title: Silent Spring
author: Rachel Carson
description: Carson documents how pesticides—especially DDT—are poisoning wildlife, water, and humans, launching the modern environmental movement.

title: Invisible Man
author: Ralph Ellison
description: A nameless Black man recounts his journey from the South to Harlem, where he discovers that everyone—from white patrons to Black nationalists—refuses to see him as he is.

title: Fahrenheit 451
author: Ray Bradbury
description: In a future where firemen burn books instead of putting out fires, one fireman begins to read and joins an underground resistance of people who memorize literature.

title: The Martian Chronicles
author: Ray Bradbury
description: Linked stories of humanity's colonization of Mars—the destruction of its native civilization, the loneliness of settlers, and the ruins they leave when Earth calls them back.

title: Something Wicked This Way Comes
author: Ray Bradbury
description: A sinister carnival arrives in a small Illinois town, and two thirteen-year-old boys must confront its evil proprietor who feeds on people's darkest desires.

title: The Illustrated Man
author: Ray Bradbury
description: Eighteen stories framed by a man whose tattoos come alive at night, each illustration a window into a different tale of space, technology, and human frailty.

title: The Immortal Life of Henrietta Lacks
author: Rebecca Skloot
description: The story of a poor Black woman whose cancer cells were taken without consent in 1951 and became one of the most important tools in medicine, while her family couldn't afford healthcare.

title: Watership Down
author: Richard Adams
description: A group of rabbits flee their doomed warren and search for a new home, facing predators, rival warrens, and tyranny in an epic that treats animal society with the gravity of human myth.

title: I Am Legend
author: Richard Matheson
description: The last human in a world of vampires barricades himself in his house each night and hunts during the day, until he discovers what he has become to the new civilization.

title: The Overstory
author: Richard Powers
description: Nine Americans whose lives are shaped by trees converge in a last-ditch effort to save the continent's few remaining acres of virgin forest.

title: The Making of the Atomic Bomb
author: Richard Rhodes
description: The definitive history of the Manhattan Project, from the physics that made fission possible to the moral reckoning of Hiroshima.

title: Native Son
author: Richard Wright
description: A young Black man in 1930s Chicago accidentally kills a white woman and is consumed by the racist machinery of the justice system, in a novel that refuses to let anyone look away.

title: Black Boy
author: Richard Wright
description: Wright's memoir of growing up Black in the Jim Crow South, enduring hunger, violence, and enforced ignorance before escaping to Chicago and becoming a writer.

title: Starship Troopers
author: Robert A. Heinlein
description: A young soldier fights alien bugs in powered armor while the novel debates whether military service should be the price of citizenship.

title: The Moon Is a Harsh Mistress
author: Robert A. Heinlein
description: Lunar colonists stage a revolution against Earth with the help of a self-aware computer, in Heinlein's most politically charged novel.

title: Stranger in a Strange Land
author: Robert A. Heinlein
description: A human raised by Martians returns to Earth with psychic abilities and founds a free-love religion that challenges every human institution.

title: The Power Broker
author: Robert Caro
description: The biography of Robert Moses, who shaped New York City for decades through bridges, highways, and parks while wielding unelected power that destroyed neighborhoods.

title: Spin
author: Robert Charles Wilson
description: One night the stars vanish behind a membrane that accelerates time outside Earth—billions of years pass beyond while decades pass within—and three friends face the end of everything.

title: The Eye of the World
author: Robert Jordan
description: A group of young villagers flees their home when agents of the Dark One attack, beginning a journey that one of them is prophesied to lead against the coming apocalypse.

title: The Great Hunt
author: Robert Jordan
description: Rand al'Thor hunts for a stolen artifact while the Seanchan invade from across the sea and the Black Ajah threatens from within the White Tower.

title: The Shadow Rising
author: Robert Jordan
description: Rand enters the Aiel Waste to prove himself as the prophesied leader, while Perrin returns home to find his village under siege by Trollocs and Whitecloaks.

title: Assassin's Apprentice
author: Robin Hobb
description: The bastard son of a prince is trained as a royal assassin while his kingdom faces raiders from the sea who turn their victims into mindless husks.

title: Royal Assassin
author: Robin Hobb
description: Fitz returns to court to find the king dying, a usurper plotting, and his bond with a wolf deepening as he becomes the weapon his king needs.

title: Assassin's Quest
author: Robin Hobb
description: Left for dead, Fitz undertakes a desperate quest to find the true king, crossing a continent with only his wolf companion and a fading hope.

title: Ship of Magic
author: Robin Hobb
description: A living ship awakens when its captain dies, and his family splinters over who will control it as piracy and slavery threaten the trading coast.

title: Fool's Errand
author: Robin Hobb
description: Years after his apparent death, Fitz is drawn back to court by his oldest friend the Fool to rescue the crown prince from a cult that wants to destroy all magic.

title: Lord of Light
author: Roger Zelazny
description: On a colonized planet, the crew of the original ship has used technology to become Hindu gods, and one among them takes on the role of Buddha to challenge their tyranny.

title: Nine Princes in Amber
author: Roger Zelazny
description: An amnesiac man discovers he is one of nine princes competing for the throne of Amber, the one true world of which all others—including Earth—are shadows.

title: A Fine Balance
author: Rohinton Mistry
description: Four strangers in 1975 India—two tailors, a student, and a widow—are thrown together during the Emergency, and their brief happiness is crushed by the machinery of state.

title: Normal People
author: Sally Rooney
description: Two Irish classmates from different social strata orbit each other from school through university, never quite managing to be together despite an undeniable connection.

title: Beautiful World, Where Are You
author: Sally Rooney
description: Four friends in their late twenties exchange emails about love, class, and the end of civilization while navigating their own messy romantic lives in contemporary Ireland.

title: Conversations with Friends
author: Sally Rooney
description: A college student and her ex-girlfriend become entangled with a married couple, and the affair that follows exposes every fault line in all four relationships.

title: Midnight's Children
author: Salman Rushdie
description: A man born at the exact moment of India's independence discovers he is telepathically linked to every child born in that midnight hour, and their powers mirror the nation's turbulent history.

title: The Satanic Verses
author: Salman Rushdie
description: Two Indian actors survive a plane explosion and are transformed—one into an angel, one into a devil—in a novel about migration, faith, and identity that provoked a global controversy.

title: Shame
author: Salman Rushdie
description: A thinly veiled allegory of Pakistan's political history told through two families whose rivalry, corruption, and violence mirror the nation's.

title: Dhalgren
author: Samuel R. Delany
description: A young man enters a ruined American city where time, identity, and reality have broken down, in a famously circular, experimental novel.

title: Babel-17
author: Samuel R. Delany
description: A poet and linguist is recruited to decode an enemy language that turns out to be a weapon—anyone who learns it begins to think like the enemy.

title: Nova
author: Samuel R. Delany
description: A starship captain obsessively pursues a rare element at the heart of a dying star, dragging his diverse crew through a galactic economic war.

title: The Lies of Locke Lamora
author: Scott Lynch
description: A gang of gentleman thieves runs elaborate cons on the nobility of a Venice-like city, until a far more dangerous criminal disrupts their game.

title: Red Seas Under Red Skies
author: Scott Lynch
description: Locke and Jean attempt to rob the most secure casino in the world, but are blackmailed into becoming pirates, a profession neither knows anything about.

title: Silence
author: Shusaku Endo
description: A Portuguese Jesuit priest in 17th-century Japan watches his converts tortured and executed, and faces the devastating choice between apostasy and their suffering.

title: The Emperor of All Maladies
author: Siddhartha Mukherjee
description: A biography of cancer from ancient Egypt to modern immunotherapy, tracing the disease and humanity's long, often failed, war against it.

title: The Interpretation of Dreams
author: Sigmund Freud
description: Freud's foundational work argues that dreams are disguised fulfillments of repressed wishes, introducing the unconscious mind to Western thought.

title: The Second Sex
author: Simone de Beauvoir
description: De Beauvoir argues that 'one is not born, but rather becomes, a woman,' analyzing how history, myth, biology, and culture have constructed femininity as the Other.

title: Solaris
author: Stanisław Lem
description: Scientists orbiting a sentient ocean planet find it manifesting their most painful memories as physical beings, and cannot determine whether it is communicating or merely reflecting.

title: The Cyberiad
author: Stanisław Lem
description: Two robot constructors travel the galaxy building increasingly absurd machines, in a collection of comic fables about technology, power, and the limits of creation.

title: His Master's Voice
author: Stanisław Lem
description: Scientists attempt to decode a signal from the stars and fail, not because the message is too complex but because human cognition cannot escape its own assumptions.

title: Gardens of the Moon
author: Steven Erikson
description: A vast empire's military campaign against a free city draws in gods, ascendants, and ancient powers in the opening of the most ambitious fantasy epic ever attempted.

title: Deadhouse Gates
author: Steven Erikson
description: A continent-spanning rebellion, a brutal desert march, and the opening of a portal to the realm of the dead converge in the series' most devastating installment.

title: Memories of Ice
author: Steven Erikson
description: Former enemies ally against a cannibalistic religious army, and the siege of Capustan becomes one of fantasy's most harrowing battle sequences.

title: Jonathan Strange & Mr Norrell
author: Susanna Clarke
description: Two magicians revive English magic during the Napoleonic Wars—one reclusive and scholarly, the other reckless and brilliant—and their rivalry awakens dangers they cannot control.

title: Piranesi
author: Susanna Clarke
description: A man lives alone in an infinite house of halls and staircases filled with ocean tides and classical statues, and gradually pieces together how he got there.

title: The Bell Jar
author: Sylvia Plath
description: A brilliant young woman's summer internship in New York dissolves into a mental breakdown, and the bell jar of depression descends over her life.

title: The Once and Future King
author: T.H. White
description: The education and reign of King Arthur, from his boyhood tutoring by Merlyn through the founding of the Round Table to its tragic collapse.

title: Between the World and Me
author: Ta-Nehisi Coates
description: A letter from a Black father to his teenage son about what it means to live in a Black body in America, confronting police violence, history, and the myth of the American Dream.

title: Season of Migration to the North
author: Tayeb Salih
description: A Sudanese man returns from England to his village and encounters a mysterious neighbor whose own time in London ended in seduction, violence, and a secret trial.

title: Exhalation
author: Ted Chiang
description: Nine stories exploring free will, memory, time travel, and what it means to be human, each built on a single speculative premise pursued with philosophical rigor.

title: Stories of Your Life and Others
author: Ted Chiang
description: Eight stories including 'Story of Your Life' (the basis for Arrival), in which a linguist learning an alien language begins to perceive time nonlinearly.

title: Small Gods
author: Terry Pratchett
description: A god reduced to a tortoise by lack of true belief finds his one genuine worshipper—a simple novice—in a theocratic empire that has forgotten what faith actually means.

title: Guards! Guards!
author: Terry Pratchett
description: The Night Watch of Ankh-Morpork—a drunk captain and three misfits—must stop a dragon summoned by a secret society, in the first book about Sam Vimes.

title: Mort
author: Terry Pratchett
description: Death takes on an apprentice—a gangly teenager—who makes the mistake of saving a princess who was supposed to die, creating a reality-threatening paradox.

title: Night Watch
author: Terry Pratchett
description: Sam Vimes is hurled back in time to the revolution he survived as a young man and must guide his younger self through it while trying not to change history.

title: Going Postal
author: Terry Pratchett
description: A convicted con man is given a choice: run the derelict post office or be hanged. He chooses the post office and takes on a corrupt telegraph monopoly.

title: More Than Human
author: Theodore Sturgeon
description: Six misfits—a telepath, a telekinetic, twins who teleport, a Mongoloid with a supercomputer mind, and a baby—merge into a single gestalt organism that may be humanity's next step.

title: Tess of the d'Urbervilles
author: Thomas Hardy
description: A poor country girl is raped by a wealthy cousin and later condemned by the husband she loves for the very victimization he should have forgiven.

title: Far from the Madding Crowd
author: Thomas Hardy
description: An independent woman inherits a farm and must choose among three suitors—a steadfast shepherd, a reckless soldier, and a wealthy older farmer—as Hardy's Wessex countryside looks on.

title: The Return of the Native
author: Thomas Hardy
description: On the wild Egdon Heath, a restless woman marries a man she doesn't love while pining for the one she does, and the heath itself seems to dictate their fates.

title: Camp Concentration
author: Thomas M. Disch
description: A conscientious objector is imprisoned in an underground facility where inmates are injected with a genius-enhancing syphilis derivative that kills them within months.

title: The Magic Mountain
author: Thomas Mann
description: A young man visits a Swiss sanatorium for three weeks and stays seven years, as philosophical debates about progress, illness, time, and death mirror Europe's slide toward World War I.

title: Buddenbrooks
author: Thomas Mann
description: Four generations of a wealthy German merchant family decline from bourgeois vigor to artistic sensitivity to physical frailty, in Mann's first and most accessible novel.

title: Death in Venice
author: Thomas Mann
description: An aging writer on vacation in Venice becomes obsessed with a beautiful Polish boy, and his dignified facade crumbles as cholera spreads through the city.

title: Doctor Faustus
author: Thomas Mann
description: A composer makes a pact with the devil for genius, and his friend narrates the story as Germany itself makes its own Faustian bargain with Nazism.

title: Common Sense
author: Thomas Paine
description: Paine's pamphlet arguing for American independence from Britain, written in plain language that turned colonial grumbling into revolutionary conviction.

title: The Structure of Scientific Revolutions
author: Thomas S. Kuhn
description: Kuhn argues that science does not progress steadily but through paradigm shifts—revolutionary breaks where the old framework is replaced wholesale.

title: This Boy's Life
author: Tobias Wolff
description: A memoir of a boy's turbulent childhood in 1950s America—a con-artist mother, an abusive stepfather, and his own talent for reinvention and lying.

title: The Right Stuff
author: Tom Wolfe
description: The story of America's first astronauts and the test pilots who preceded them, exploring the swagger, competition, and courage that defined the space race.

title: The Bonfire of the Vanities
author: Tom Wolfe
description: A Wall Street bond trader's life implodes after a wrong turn in the Bronx leads to a hit-and-run that becomes a media circus exploiting race, class, and ambition.

title: There There
author: Tommy Orange
description: Twelve urban Native Americans in Oakland converge at a powwow, carrying histories of addiction, violence, identity, and the search for belonging far from the reservation.

title: Beloved
author: Toni Morrison
description: A formerly enslaved woman is haunted by the ghost of the daughter she killed rather than allow to be taken back into slavery, and the past will not stay buried.

title: Song of Solomon
author: Toni Morrison
description: A young Black man travels south to find a family treasure and instead discovers his ancestry, including a great-grandfather who could fly.

title: Sula
author: Toni Morrison
description: Two Black women in a small Ohio town grow up together, diverge radically, and test the limits of friendship, freedom, and the community's capacity for difference.

title: The Bluest Eye
author: Toni Morrison
description: A young Black girl prays for blue eyes, believing whiteness equals beauty, while Morrison dismantles the racial standards that have destroyed her self-worth.

title: Jazz
author: Toni Morrison
description: In 1920s Harlem, a man shoots his young lover and his wife tries to cut the girl's face at the funeral, and the novel itself becomes the music that holds the story together.

title: Paradise
author: Toni Morrison
description: The men of an all-Black Oklahoma town attack a nearby convent of women, and the novel unravels why—tracing generations of exclusion, purity, and fear.

title: The Soul of a New Machine
author: Tracy Kidder
description: The inside story of a team of engineers racing to build a new minicomputer, capturing the obsessive, sleep-deprived culture of early Silicon Valley.

title: In Cold Blood
author: Truman Capote
description: The true story of the murder of a Kansas farm family by two drifters, told with novelistic technique that invented the true-crime genre.

title: Personal Memoirs of Ulysses S. Grant
author: Ulysses S. Grant
description: The Civil War general and president's memoir, written while dying of cancer, widely considered the finest military autobiography in the English language.

title: The Name of the Rose
author: Umberto Eco
description: A Franciscan friar investigates a series of murders in a medieval Italian monastery, where the killings are linked to a forbidden book in a labyrinthine library.

title: Foucault's Pendulum
author: Umberto Eco
description: Three editors invent a conspiracy theory linking the Templars to every secret society in history, then discover that someone is taking their invention seriously—and killing for it.

title: The Left Hand of Darkness
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: An envoy from a galactic federation tries to persuade a planet of ambisexual people to join, and must cross a glacier with the one politician who believed him.

title: The Dispossessed
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: A physicist from an anarchist moon travels to the capitalist planet his ancestors fled, discovering that neither society has achieved the freedom it claims.

title: A Wizard of Earthsea
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: A young wizard's reckless use of power releases a shadow that hunts him across the world, and he must chase it to the end of the sea to face what it is.

title: The Tombs of Atuan
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: A girl raised as priestess of a dark labyrinth meets the wizard Ged, and must choose between the dead powers she serves and the freedom he offers.

title: The Farthest Shore
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: Magic is draining from Earthsea, and the Archmage sails to the land of the dead to confront the source—a wizard who broke the barrier between life and death.

title: The Lathe of Heaven
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: A man whose dreams alter reality is exploited by a psychiatrist who tries to use the power to fix the world, making it worse with each attempt.

title: Life and Fate
author: Vasily Grossman
description: A vast novel of the Battle of Stalingrad and the Soviet home front, showing how totalitarianism crushes individual freedom from both the Nazi and Soviet sides.

title: A Fire Upon the Deep
author: Vernor Vinge
description: A malevolent superintelligence is released at the galaxy's edge, and the key to stopping it lies with two children stranded on a planet of dog-like pack minds.

title: A Deepness in the Sky
author: Vernor Vinge
description: Two human fleets converge on an alien civilization that hibernates for centuries between brief periods of activity, and one fleet enslaves the other using mind-control technology.

title: Les Misérables
author: Victor Hugo
description: An ex-convict pursued by a relentless inspector tries to build a new life in 19th-century France, against the backdrop of revolution, poverty, and the possibility of redemption.

title: The Hunchback of Notre-Dame
author: Victor Hugo
description: A deformed bell-ringer, a beautiful dancer, a tormented priest, and the cathedral itself are caught in a web of obsession and cruelty in medieval Paris.

title: The Sympathizer
author: Viet Thanh Nguyen
description: A communist spy embedded among South Vietnamese refugees in Los Angeles writes a confession that is both a spy thriller and a savage critique of American culture.

title: Man's Search for Meaning
author: Viktor Frankl
description: A psychiatrist's account of surviving Auschwitz and the therapeutic philosophy he built from it: that meaning can be found in any circumstance, even the worst.

title: Mrs Dalloway
author: Virginia Woolf
description: A single day in London as a society hostess prepares for a party and a shell-shocked veteran walks the same streets, their inner lives flowing in parallel streams of consciousness.

title: To the Lighthouse
author: Virginia Woolf
description: A family's planned trip to a lighthouse is postponed for a decade by weather, death, and war, and when it finally happens, everything has changed.

title: Orlando
author: Virginia Woolf
description: A young Elizabethan nobleman lives for centuries, changes sex, and arrives in the 20th century as a woman, in Woolf's playful exploration of gender, time, and biography.

title: The Waves
author: Virginia Woolf
description: Six friends' interior monologues flow from childhood to old age, charting consciousness itself as it rises and falls like the waves framing each section.

title: Lolita
author: Vladimir Nabokov
description: A European intellectual's confession of his obsession with a twelve-year-old girl, written in prose so dazzling it forces the reader to reckon with beauty in the service of evil.

title: Pale Fire
author: Vladimir Nabokov
description: A 999-line poem by a dead poet is annotated by a deranged neighbor who hijacks the commentary to tell his own story of exile and assassination.

title: Ada, or Ardor
author: Vladimir Nabokov
description: A lifelong love affair between cousins—who are actually siblings—unfolds on an alternate Earth in Nabokov's most complex and linguistically extravagant novel.

title: Speak, Memory
author: Vladimir Nabokov
description: Nabokov's memoir of his aristocratic Russian childhood, exile in Europe, and butterfly-hunting, rendered in prose as precise and shimmering as his fiction.

title: The Souls of Black Folk
author: W.E.B. Du Bois
description: Du Bois introduces the concept of double consciousness and the color line, blending sociology, history, and personal essay to define the Black American experience at the century's turn.

title: Angle of Repose
author: Wallace Stegner
description: A retired historian in a wheelchair researches his grandmother's life in the 19th-century American West, finding parallels to his own failed marriage.

title: Crossing to Safety
author: Wallace Stegner
description: Two academic couples form a friendship that spans decades, testing loyalty, ambition, and the different ways marriages hold together or fall apart.

title: A Canticle for Leibowitz
author: Walter M. Miller Jr.
description: Monks in a post-nuclear desert preserve the fragments of scientific knowledge through centuries of dark age, only for humanity to rebuild and destroy itself again.

title: The Sound and the Fury
author: William Faulkner
description: The decline of a Southern family told through four narrators, including a man with an intellectual disability whose fractured perceptions open the novel.

title: As I Lay Dying
author: William Faulkner
description: A poor Mississippi family hauls their mother's coffin across the county to bury her in her hometown, each narrating their private obsessions as the journey goes wrong.

title: Absalom, Absalom!
author: William Faulkner
description: Two Harvard roommates piece together the story of a man who carved a plantation from Mississippi wilderness, and the dynasty-destroying secret of race at its center.

title: Light in August
author: William Faulkner
description: A pregnant woman walks to Mississippi seeking her baby's father, crossing paths with a man whose uncertain racial identity makes him a target in a world obsessed with categories.

title: Neuromancer
author: William Gibson
description: A washed-up hacker is hired for one last job: to break into the most powerful AI in the world, in the novel that defined cyberpunk and predicted the internet.

title: Count Zero
author: William Gibson
description: Three storylines converge in the Sprawl: a mercenary, an art dealer, and a young hacker who stumbles onto something in cyberspace that may be God.

title: Mona Lisa Overdrive
author: William Gibson
description: Four characters are drawn toward the matrix's merger with something beyond human comprehension, concluding Gibson's foundational cyberpunk trilogy.

title: Pattern Recognition
author: William Gibson
description: A coolhunter with an allergy to brand logos is hired to trace the source of mysterious film clips appearing online, in Gibson's first novel set in the present day.

title: Lord of the Flies
author: William Golding
description: Boys stranded on a deserted island after a plane crash attempt to govern themselves and descend into savagery, hunting one of their own.

title: The Varieties of Religious Experience
author: William James
description: James catalogs and analyzes firsthand accounts of mystical experiences, conversion, saintliness, and prayer, arguing for religion's psychological validity.

title: Sophie's Choice
author: William Styron
description: A young Southern writer in Brooklyn befriends a Holocaust survivor and her volatile lover, gradually learning the impossible choice she was forced to make at Auschwitz.

title: Homegoing
author: Yaa Gyasi
description: Two half-sisters in 18th-century Ghana begin parallel family lines—one sold into slavery, one married to a slaver—and their descendants' stories unfold across three centuries.

title: Snow Country
author: Yasunari Kawabata
description: A Tokyo dilettante visits a remote hot-spring town and becomes entangled with a geisha, in a novel of exquisite beauty and emotional distance.

title: The Master of Go
author: Yasunari Kawabata
description: A championship Go match between an aging master and a young challenger becomes a meditation on tradition, modernity, and the aesthetics of defeat.

title: We
author: Yevgeny Zamyatin
description: A mathematician in a glass-walled totalitarian state falls in love and joins a rebellion, in the dystopian novel that inspired both Huxley and Orwell.

title: The Temple of the Golden Pavilion
author: Yukio Mishima
description: A stuttering young monk becomes so obsessed with the beauty of a Kyoto temple that he burns it down, in a novel based on a real act of arson.

title: The Sea of Fertility
author: Yukio Mishima
description: A tetralogy following a man who witnesses the reincarnation of his best friend across four lives, spanning 20th-century Japan from imperial grandeur to postwar emptiness.

title: Confessions of a Mask
author: Yukio Mishima
description: A young Japanese man hides his homosexuality behind an elaborate social performance, dissecting his desires with clinical precision in Mishima's breakthrough novel.

title: White Teeth
author: Zadie Smith
description: Two wartime friends—one English, one Bangladeshi—and their families collide across generations in multicultural North London, tangling with identity, religion, and genetics.

title: On Beauty
author: Zadie Smith
description: Two academic families—one liberal, one conservative—clash over art, politics, and infidelity in a novel modeled on Howards End and set near a Boston university.

title: NW
author: Zadie Smith
description: Four people from a Northwest London housing estate take divergent paths through class, race, and ambition, and a chance encounter pulls them back to where they started.

title: Their Eyes Were Watching God
author: Zora Neale Hurston
description: A Black woman in 1930s Florida tells the story of her three marriages and her journey to selfhood, in prose steeped in the dialect and rhythms of the rural South.

title: Barracoon
author: Zora Neale Hurston
description: Hurston's interview with the last known survivor of the Atlantic slave trade, recorded in 1927 but unpublished for nearly a century.

title: The Dragon Reborn
author: Robert Jordan
description: Rand al'Thor flees his companions to pursue his destiny alone while Egwene, Nynaeve, and Mat are drawn into their own quests as the Dragon Reborn approaches the Stone of Tear.

title: The Fires of Heaven
author: Robert Jordan
description: Rand leads the Aiel out of the Waste to conquer the wetlands while Nynaeve hunts the Black Ajah and Mat discovers he is destined to lead armies.

title: Lord of Chaos
author: Robert Jordan
description: Rand establishes an amnesty for male channelers while the Aes Sedai split into factions, culminating in his capture and a battle that reshapes the world.

title: A Crown of Swords
author: Robert Jordan
description: Rand pursues the renegade Forsaken Sammael while Mat is trapped in Ebou Dar and the Seanchan return to the mainland in force.

title: The Path of Daggers
author: Robert Jordan
description: Rand drives the Seanchan back with a dangerous weapon while the rebel Aes Sedai march on Tar Valon and Elayne claims the Lion Throne.

title: Winter's Heart
author: Robert Jordan
description: Rand goes into hiding to attempt the cleansing of saidin while Perrin desperately hunts for his kidnapped wife Faile.

title: Crossroads of Twilight
author: Robert Jordan
description: The world reacts to the cleansing of saidin as Perrin negotiates for Faile's release and Mat flees Ebou Dar with the Daughter of the Nine Moons.

title: Knife of Dreams
author: Robert Jordan
description: The Last Battle approaches as Mat marries Tuon, Perrin rescues Faile, and Egwene undermines Elaida from within the White Tower.

title: The Gathering Storm
author: Robert Jordan
description: Rand darkens under the weight of his burden while Egwene reunites the White Tower in the first volume completed by Brandon Sanderson after Jordan's death.

title: Towers of Midnight
author: Robert Jordan
description: Perrin forges his power-wrought hammer, Mat enters the Tower of Ghenjei, and the nations prepare for Tarmon Gai'don.

title: A Memory of Light
author: Robert Jordan
description: The Last Battle unfolds across hundreds of pages as every character converges for the final confrontation with the Dark One at Shayol Ghul.

title: House of Chains
author: Steven Erikson
description: A young warrior of the Teblor people is drawn into the Malazan world as the rebellion in Seven Cities reaches its climax.

title: Midnight Tides
author: Steven Erikson
description: On a new continent, the Tiste Edur clash with the Letherii Empire in a war driven by economics and an enslaved god.

title: The Bonehunters
author: Steven Erikson
description: The Bonehunters army faces annihilation in a city under siege while the gods play an increasingly dangerous game for control of the Crippled God.

title: Reaper's Gale
author: Steven Erikson
description: The Bonehunters invade the Letherii Empire as multiple convergences of power threaten to tear the world apart.

title: Toll the Hounds
author: Steven Erikson
description: In the city of Darujhistan, the dead are rising and an ancient god of darkness seeks to reclaim his throne in the series' most philosophical entry.

title: Dust of Dreams
author: Steven Erikson
description: The Bonehunters march into the Wastelands toward a final battle while an ancient civilization's awakening threatens all life.

title: The Crippled God
author: Steven Erikson
description: Every storyline converges as the Bonehunters make their last stand to free the Crippled God and end the war of the gods.

title: The Mad Ship
author: Robin Hobb
description: The living ship Vivacia falls into the hands of a pirate king while Althea seeks another liveship to reclaim her family's vessel.

title: Ship of Destiny
author: Robin Hobb
description: The sea serpents must reach their cocooning grounds or perish, and every faction's fate depends on the liveships and the secrets of their creation.

title: Golden Fool
author: Robin Hobb
description: Fitz navigates court intrigue in disguise while training the Skill-talented prince, and the Fool's visions grow darker.

title: Fool's Fate
author: Robin Hobb
description: Fitz journeys to the icy Outislands to help the prince slay a dragon, but the Fool's prophecy demands a far greater sacrifice.

title: The Alloy of Law
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: Three hundred years after the original trilogy, a frontier lawman returns to the city and uses Allomancy and Feruchemy to solve crimes in a Wild West-meets-industrial setting.

title: Shadows of Self
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: Wax investigates a shapeshifting killer terrorizing Elendel while uncovering uncomfortable truths about the god who remade the world.

title: The Bands of Mourning
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: Wax's crew hunts for the Lord Ruler's legendary bracers in the frozen south, discovering a civilization that survived the Catacendre.

title: The Lost Metal
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: Wax faces his final mission as threats from another world converge on Elendel and the cosmere's larger conflicts arrive on Scadrial.

title: Tress of the Emerald Sea
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: A girl sets out across seas of spores to rescue her beloved from a sorceress, in a fairy tale narrated by a familiar cosmere character.

title: Yumi and the Nightmare Painter
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: A spirit summoner and a nightmare painter from different worlds mysteriously swap places, each forced to learn the other's role.

title: The Colour of Magic
author: Terry Pratchett
description: The Discworld's first tourist and his hapless wizard guide stumble off the edge of the world in the opening of Pratchett's beloved comic fantasy series.

title: Equal Rites
author: Terry Pratchett
description: A dying wizard accidentally passes his staff to a girl, who must fight for admission to the all-male Unseen University.

title: Wyrd Sisters
author: Terry Pratchett
description: Three witches meddle in the affairs of a kingdom where a duke has murdered the king, in a riotous retelling of Macbeth.

title: Reaper Man
author: Terry Pratchett
description: Death is fired and given a mortal lifespan, taking a job as a farmhand while the undead pile up and shopping malls invade Ankh-Morpork.

title: Men at Arms
author: Terry Pratchett
description: The Night Watch recruits its first troll, dwarf, and werewolf as Vimes investigates a murder committed with the Disc's first firearm.

title: Feet of Clay
author: Terry Pratchett
description: Vimes investigates the poisoning of the Patrician while the city's golem workforce begins to develop something that looks like free will.

title: The Truth
author: Terry Pratchett
description: Ankh-Morpork gets its first newspaper and its editor immediately discovers that the truth is the most dangerous commodity in the city.

title: Thud!
author: Terry Pratchett
description: On the anniversary of an ancient dwarf-troll battle, a dwarf is murdered and Vimes must solve it before the city erupts in racial violence.

title: The Shepherd's Crown
author: Terry Pratchett
description: Tiffany Aching must defend the Chalk and the boundaries between worlds in Pratchett's final novel, completed shortly before his death.

title: The Shining
author: Stephen King
description: A recovering alcoholic takes a winter caretaker job at a remote hotel with his family, and the hotel's malevolent spirits amplify his rage while his psychic son sees the horrors ahead.

title: It
author: Stephen King
description: Seven friends who defeated a shape-shifting evil as children in 1958 must return to their hometown twenty-seven years later when the killings start again.

title: The Stand
author: Stephen King
description: A superflu kills 99% of humanity and the survivors divide into two camps—one drawn to a benevolent old woman, the other to a demonic figure in Las Vegas.

title: Misery
author: Stephen King
description: A novelist crashes his car in a blizzard and is rescued by his number one fan, who holds him captive and forces him to write a new book on her terms.

title: Pet Sematary
author: Stephen King
description: A family discovers a burial ground beyond the pet cemetery that brings the dead back to life—but what returns is not what was buried.

title: 'Salem's Lot
author: Stephen King
description: A writer returns to a small Maine town and discovers it is being slowly taken over by a vampire who has moved into the old Marsten House.

title: The Dark Tower: The Gunslinger
author: Stephen King
description: The last gunslinger pursues the Man in Black across a desert in a dying world, beginning an epic quest for the Dark Tower that stands at the center of all universes.

title: The Drawing of the Three
author: Stephen King
description: Roland draws three people from 1980s New York through magical doorways on a beach, forming the ka-tet that will accompany him to the Tower.
altTitles: The Dark Tower II: The Drawing of the Three

title: The Waste Lands
author: Stephen King
description: Roland's ka-tet follows the Beam toward the Dark Tower, rescuing a boy from New York and riding Blaine the Mono through a devastated landscape.
altTitles: The Dark Tower III: The Waste Lands

title: Wizard and Glass
author: Stephen King
description: Roland tells his companions the story of his youth—his first love, his first quest, and the tragedy that shaped him into the gunslinger.
altTitles: The Dark Tower IV: Wizard and Glass

author: Stephen King
description: A teacher discovers a portal to 1958 and attempts to prevent the Kennedy assassination, finding that the past is obdurate and fights back.

title: Doctor Sleep
author: Stephen King
description: Danny Torrance, now a middle-aged drifter, must protect a girl with powerful psychic abilities from a tribe of quasi-immortals who feed on children's shine.

title: Carrie
author: Stephen King
description: A bullied teenage girl with telekinetic powers is pushed past her breaking point at the prom, unleashing destruction on her tormentors and her entire town.

title: Murder on the Orient Express
author: Agatha Christie
description: Hercule Poirot is trapped on a snowbound train with a murdered passenger and twelve suspects, and the solution challenges every assumption about justice.

title: And Then There Were None
author: Agatha Christie
description: Ten strangers are lured to an island where they are killed one by one according to a nursery rhyme, and no one can figure out who among them is the murderer.

title: The Murder of Roger Ackroyd
author: Agatha Christie
description: Poirot investigates the murder of a wealthy man in a village, and the narrator's identity makes this the most controversial twist in detective fiction.

title: Death on the Nile
author: Agatha Christie
description: On a Nile cruise, a beautiful heiress is murdered and Poirot must untangle a web of jealousy, debt, and romantic obsession among the passengers.

title: The ABC Murders
author: Agatha Christie
description: A serial killer taunts Poirot with alphabetical murders, each victim's name and location matching the next letter, but the pattern hides a simpler motive.

title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
author: Stieg Larsson
description: A disgraced journalist and a brilliant hacker investigate a forty-year-old disappearance on a wealthy Swedish island, uncovering a family's history of murder and abuse.

title: The Girl Who Played with Fire
author: Stieg Larsson
description: Lisbeth Salander is framed for three murders and goes underground while Blomkvist races to prove her innocence and expose a government conspiracy.

title: The Girl Who Kicked the Hornets' Nest
author: Stieg Larsson
description: Salander lies in intensive care while Blomkvist builds a case against the secret government unit that has controlled her life since childhood.

title: Gone Girl
author: Gillian Flynn
description: A wife disappears on her fifth wedding anniversary and the husband becomes the prime suspect, but alternating narratives reveal that nothing about this marriage is what it seems.

title: Sharp Objects
author: Gillian Flynn
description: A journalist returns to her small Missouri hometown to cover the murders of two girls and confronts the toxic family she fled.

title: Dark Places
author: Gillian Flynn
description: A woman whose family was massacred when she was seven revisits the crime after a true-crime club convinces her that her jailed brother may be innocent.

title: The Hunger Games
author: Suzanne Collins
description: In a dystopian nation, a girl volunteers to fight to the death in a televised arena to save her sister, and her defiance sparks a revolution.

title: Catching Fire
author: Suzanne Collins
description: Katniss is forced back into the arena as the Capitol punishes her for inspiring rebellion, while the districts prepare for war.

title: Mockingjay
author: Suzanne Collins
description: Katniss becomes the symbol of the revolution against the Capitol, but the cost of war and the manipulation from both sides nearly destroy her.

title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
author: J.K. Rowling
description: An orphan living under the stairs discovers he is a wizard and enters a magical school where a dark lord's return is already stirring.

title: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets
author: J.K. Rowling
description: A mysterious force is petrifying students at Hogwarts and Harry must find the legendary Chamber before the school is closed.

title: Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban
author: J.K. Rowling
description: An escaped prisoner believed to be a servant of Voldemort targets Harry, but the truth about his parents' betrayal is far more complicated.

title: Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire
author: J.K. Rowling
description: Harry is mysteriously entered into a dangerous magical tournament that turns out to be a trap designed to resurrect Lord Voldemort.

title: Harry Potter and the Order of the Phoenix
author: J.K. Rowling
description: The Ministry of Magic denies Voldemort's return while Harry trains a secret student army and suffers visions that connect him to the Dark Lord's mind.

title: Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince
author: J.K. Rowling
description: Dumbledore privately tutors Harry about Voldemort's past and the Horcruxes that must be destroyed, leading to a devastating betrayal.

title: Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows
author: J.K. Rowling
description: Harry, Ron, and Hermione abandon school to hunt Horcruxes as Voldemort takes over the wizarding world, building to the Battle of Hogwarts.

title: The Kite Runner
author: Khaled Hosseini
description: An Afghan man in America returns to Taliban-controlled Kabul to rescue the son of his childhood friend—the servant boy he betrayed decades ago.

title: A Thousand Splendid Suns
author: Khaled Hosseini
description: Two Afghan women from different generations are forced into marriage with the same brutal man, and their bond becomes their only strength through decades of war.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A Spanish shepherd boy travels to Egypt in search of treasure, learning that the real journey is discovering one's Personal Legend.

title: The Book Thief
author: Markus Zusak
description: Narrated by Death, a girl in Nazi Germany steals books and shares them with the Jewish man hidden in her foster family's basement.

title: Life of Pi
author: Yann Martel
description: A boy survives 227 days on a lifeboat with a Bengal tiger after a shipwreck, in a story that asks whether the better story is the true one.

title: The Night Watchman
author: Louise Erdrich
description: Based on Erdrich's grandfather's life, a night watchman in 1950s North Dakota fights Congress's attempt to terminate his Chippewa tribe's rights.

title: The Round House
author: Louise Erdrich
description: A thirteen-year-old boy on an Ojibwe reservation investigates the brutal assault of his mother after the legal system fails to act.

title: Love Medicine
author: Louise Erdrich
description: Interconnected stories follow two Ojibwe families on a North Dakota reservation from the 1930s to the 1980s, tracing love, betrayal, and survival across generations.

title: Circe
author: Madeline Miller
description: The witch of Greek myth tells her own story—exiled to an island, she hones her craft and encounters Odysseus, the Minotaur, and the gods who fear her power.

title: The Song of Achilles
author: Madeline Miller
description: Patroclus narrates his life with Achilles from their boyhood through the Trojan War, reimagining the Iliad as a devastating love story.

title: The Nightingale
author: Kristin Hannah
description: Two sisters in Nazi-occupied France take different paths of resistance—one sheltering a downed airman, the other leading Allied pilots over the Pyrenees.

title: The Great Alone
author: Kristin Hannah
description: A family moves to Alaska in 1974 seeking a fresh start, but the father's PTSD and the brutal wilderness push them to the edge of survival.

title: Where the Crawdads Sing
author: Delia Owens
description: A girl abandoned in the marshes of North Carolina raises herself, becomes a naturalist, and is suspected of murdering the town's golden boy.

title: Educated
author: Tara Westover
description: A woman raised by survivalist parents in rural Idaho—never sent to school or doctor—educates herself and earns a PhD from Cambridge, at the cost of her family.

title: All the Light We Cannot See
author: Anthony Doerr
description: A blind French girl and a German orphan boy's paths converge in occupied Saint-Malo, connected by a legendary diamond and a forbidden radio broadcast.

title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A sweeping history of humankind from the cognitive revolution to the present, arguing that shared myths—from religion to money—are what enabled Homo sapiens to dominate the planet.

title: Homo Deus
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: Having conquered famine, plague, and war, Harari asks what humanity will pursue next—immortality, happiness, and divinity—and what we might lose in the process.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: A Nobel laureate explains the two systems of thought—fast intuition and slow reasoning—and the systematic biases that lead us to make irrational decisions.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: A practical framework for building good habits and breaking bad ones, based on the idea that tiny changes compound into remarkable results.

title: The Subtle Art of Not Giving a F*ck
author: Mark Manson
description: A counterintuitive self-help book arguing that the key to a good life is accepting limitations and choosing carefully what to care about.

title: Outliers
author: Malcolm Gladwell
description: Gladwell examines why some people succeed far beyond others, arguing that opportunity, timing, and cultural legacy matter more than individual talent.

title: The Tipping Point
author: Malcolm Gladwell
description: How small changes can trigger epidemics of behavior—from fashion trends to crime drops—by reaching the right people at the right moment.

title: Blink
author: Malcolm Gladwell
description: The power and peril of snap judgments: how our unconscious mind can be brilliantly accurate or dangerously biased in the first two seconds.

title: A Court of Thorns and Roses
author: Sarah J. Maas
description: A mortal huntress is dragged into a magical land of faeries as punishment for killing a wolf, and discovers the beast who captured her is not what he seems.

series.name: A Court of Thorns and Roses

title: A Court of Mist and Fury
author: Sarah J. Maas
description: Feyre discovers the true villain of the faerie world and trains her new powers with the High Lord of the Night Court, who is nothing like his reputation.

series.name: A Court of Thorns and Roses

title: A Court of Wings and Ruin
author: Sarah J. Maas
description: Feyre returns to the Spring Court as a spy while war looms against a king who would enslave all of Prythian.

series.name: A Court of Thorns and Roses

title: Throne of Glass
author: Sarah J. Maas
description: A teenage assassin is pulled from a slave labor camp to compete for the chance to serve as the king's champion, uncovering dark magic in the castle.

title: It Ends with Us
author: Colleen Hoover
description: A woman falls for a neurosurgeon while struggling with feelings for her first love, and must confront the cycle of domestic abuse she grew up with.

title: Verity
author: Colleen Hoover
description: A struggling writer hired to finish a bestselling author's series finds a hidden manuscript that reveals horrifying truths about the woman's life.

title: It Starts with Us
author: Colleen Hoover
description: The sequel follows Lily and Atlas as they navigate new beginnings while her ex-husband refuses to let go.

title: The Poppy War
author: R.F. Kuang
description: A war orphan tests into the most elite military academy in the empire and discovers she can call on the power of an ancient, destructive god.

title: The Dragon Republic
author: R.F. Kuang
description: Rin allies with a warlord to overthrow the empress but discovers that revolution demands compromises as monstrous as the regime she's fighting.

title: The Burning God
author: R.F. Kuang
description: Rin seizes power and wages total war against foreign invaders, becoming the very monster she once fought, in the trilogy's devastating conclusion.

title: Babel
author: R.F. Kuang
description: A Chinese orphan brought to Oxford discovers that the British Empire's power runs on magical translation, and must choose between privilege and revolution.

title: Yellowface
author: R.F. Kuang
description: A white writer steals her dead Asian American friend's manuscript and publishes it as her own, spiraling into paranoia as the lie grows.

title: Mexican Gothic
author: Silvia Moreno-Garcia
description: A glamorous socialite investigates her cousin's mysterious illness at a decaying English mansion in 1950s Mexico, where the house itself seems alive.

title: The House in the Cerulean Sea
author: TJ Klune
description: A caseworker for magical youth is sent to evaluate an orphanage on a remote island, where six dangerous children and their mysterious caretaker change everything he believes.

title: The Seven Husbands of Evelyn Hugo
author: Taylor Jenkins Reid
description: An aging Hollywood icon finally tells the true story of her seven marriages—and the great love of her life that she kept secret.

title: Daisy Jones & The Six
author: Taylor Jenkins Reid
description: An oral history of a fictional 1970s rock band's meteoric rise and sudden breakup, told through interviews with each member decades later.

title: Malibu Rising
author: Taylor Jenkins Reid
description: Four famous siblings throw an epic end-of-summer party in 1983 Malibu that goes down in legend, while their family secrets burn through the night.

title: The Silent Patient
author: Alex Michaelides
description: A famous painter shoots her husband and never speaks again, and the psychotherapist determined to get her to talk has his own reasons for obsession.

title: The Midnight Library
author: Matt Haig
description: A woman between life and death enters a library where each book lets her live a different version of her life, searching for the one worth staying alive for.

title: Anxious People
author: Fredrik Backman
description: A failed bank robber takes hostages during an apartment open house, and the investigation reveals that everyone—robber and hostages alike—is barely holding it together.

title: A Man Called Ove
author: Fredrik Backman
description: A curmudgeonly widower planning suicide is repeatedly interrupted by his new neighbors, and their persistence slowly draws him back to life.

title: Eleanor Oliphant Is Completely Fine
author: Gail Honeyman
description: A socially awkward office worker whose rigid routines mask deep trauma slowly opens up through an unlikely friendship with a bumbling IT guy.

title: The Vanishing Half
author: Brit Bennett
description: Twin sisters from a small Black community choose opposite paths—one passes as white, the other stays—and their daughters' lives unfold the consequences across generations.

title: Such a Fun Age
author: Kiley Reid
description: A young Black babysitter is accused of kidnapping her white charge in a grocery store, and the incident exposes the liberal family's performative allyship.

title: The Invisible Life of Addie LaRue
author: V.E. Schwab
description: A woman makes a Faustian bargain to live forever but is cursed to be forgotten by everyone she meets—until she walks into a bookshop and the clerk remembers her name.

title: Ninth House
author: Leigh Bardugo
description: A working-class girl is recruited to monitor Yale's secret societies, which practice real magic, and must solve a murder that connects the campus to the spirit world.

title: Children of Blood and Bone
author: Tomi Adeyemi
description: In a West African-inspired world where magic has been suppressed, a young woman discovers she can restore it and leads a rebellion against the monarchy.

title: An American Marriage
author: Tayari Jones
description: A newlywed Black man is wrongly convicted of a crime, and the five years of imprisonment test whether his marriage—and his wife's loyalty—can survive injustice.

title: Little Fires Everywhere
author: Celeste Ng
description: A free-spirited artist and her daughter upend a wealthy Cleveland suburb, igniting conflicts over motherhood, race, and the meaning of a perfect life.

title: Everything I Never Told You
author: Celeste Ng
description: A Chinese-American family in 1970s Ohio unravels after their favorite daughter is found dead in a lake, and each member's secrets come to light.

title: The Priory of the Orange Tree
author: Samantha Shannon
description: A dragonrider, a mage, a queen, and a spy converge to stop the return of an ancient evil in a standalone epic spanning multiple continents.

title: Maus
author: Art Spiegelman
description: A son interviews his Holocaust-survivor father, depicting Jews as mice and Nazis as cats in the graphic novel that proved comics could be serious literature.

title: Persepolis
author: Marjane Satrapi
description: A girl grows up during the Islamic Revolution in Iran, navigating childhood rebellion, exile in Vienna, and return to a changed country in stark black-and-white panels.

title: Watchmen
author: Alan Moore
description: In an alternate 1985 where Nixon is still president, retired superheroes investigate the murder of one of their own as nuclear war looms.

title: Fun Home
author: Alison Bechdel
description: A daughter reconstructs her closeted father's life and her own coming out, finding parallels between their experiences in a literary memoir drawn with forensic precision.

title: The Count of Monte Cristo
author: Alexandre Dumas
description: A sailor wrongly imprisoned for fourteen years escapes, finds a hidden treasure, and reinvents himself to systematically destroy the men who betrayed him.

title: The Three Musketeers
