author: Alexandre Dumas
description: A young Gascon joins three inseparable musketeers in swashbuckling adventures involving court intrigue, Cardinal Richelieu, and a dangerous femme fatale.

title: Dracula
author: Bram Stoker
description: A Transylvanian count travels to England to spread the undead curse, told through journals and letters as a group of friends races to stop him.

title: The Strange Case of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
description: A London lawyer investigates the connection between a respectable doctor and a violent criminal, discovering they are the same person transformed by a potion.

title: Treasure Island
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
description: A boy finds a pirate's treasure map and sails to a distant island, where the charming one-legged cook turns out to be the most dangerous man aboard.

title: The Odyssey
author: Homer
description: After the Trojan War, Odysseus spends ten years trying to get home, facing monsters, gods, and temptation while his wife fends off suitors who assume he is dead.

title: The Iliad
author: Homer
description: The final weeks of the Trojan War, centered on the rage of Achilles—his withdrawal from battle, the death of his companion, and his furious return.

title: Hamlet
author: William Shakespeare
description: A Danish prince feigns madness while plotting revenge against his uncle, who murdered his father and married his mother.

title: Macbeth
author: William Shakespeare
description: A Scottish general murders his king after a witches' prophecy and his wife's urging, then descends into paranoia and blood as the crown destroys them both.

title: King Lear
author: William Shakespeare
description: An aging king divides his realm among his daughters based on their flattery, banishing the one who truly loves him, and the consequences are catastrophic for everyone.

title: Othello
author: William Shakespeare
description: A Moorish general in Venice is manipulated by his ensign into believing his wife is unfaithful, and jealousy drives him to murder the woman he loves.

title: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest
author: Ken Kesey
description: A charming criminal fakes insanity to serve his sentence in a mental hospital instead of prison, and wages war against the tyrannical Nurse Ratched.

title: The Color Purple
author: Alice Walker
description: A poor Black woman in the rural South writes letters to God and to her sister, enduring abuse until she finds her voice, her sexuality, and her freedom.

title: The Giver
author: Lois Lowry
description: A boy in a colorless utopia is chosen to inherit the community's suppressed memories of pain and joy, and must decide what to do with the truth.

title: The Perks of Being a Wallflower
author: Stephen Chbosky
description: A shy freshman writes letters about his first year of high school—first love, music, trauma, and the friends who teach him to participate in life.

title: The Fault in Our Stars
author: John Green
description: Two teenagers with cancer fall in love at a support group and travel to Amsterdam to meet their favorite author, confronting mortality with humor and honesty.

title: Twilight
author: Stephenie Meyer
description: A teenager moves to a small town and falls in love with a vampire who is drawn to her blood, igniting a romance complicated by predatory instinct and rival werewolves.

title: Divergent
author: Veronica Roth
description: In a future Chicago divided into five factions based on virtues, a girl discovers she doesn't fit any category and uncovers a conspiracy to destroy the system.

title: The Maze Runner
author: James Dashner
description: A boy wakes in a glade surrounded by a massive maze with no memory of who he is, and must help the other trapped teens find a way out before time runs out.

title: Percy Jackson & the Olympians: The Lightning Thief
author: Rick Riordan
description: A twelve-year-old discovers he is the son of Poseidon and must retrieve Zeus's stolen lightning bolt to prevent a war among the gods.

title: Eragon
author: Christopher Paolini
description: A farm boy finds a dragon egg and becomes the first new Dragon Rider in generations, joining a rebellion against the tyrannical king.

title: The Graveyard Book
author: Neil Gaiman
description: A toddler whose family is murdered wanders into a graveyard and is raised by ghosts, learning the ways of the dead while the living killer still hunts him.

title: Coraline
author: Neil Gaiman
description: A girl discovers a door in her new house that leads to a mirror world where her Other Mother wants to keep her forever—if she'll just let her sew buttons over her eyes.

title: Stardust
author: Neil Gaiman
description: A young man crosses a wall into a magical land to retrieve a fallen star for the woman he loves, but the star is a person and others want her too.

title: The Wheel of Time: New Spring
author: Robert Jordan
description: The prequel tells how Moiraine and Lan first met and began their search for the Dragon Reborn, set during the Aiel War twenty years before the main series.

title: A Gentleman in Moscow
author: Amor Towles
description: A count sentenced to house arrest in a luxury hotel for the rest of his life finds purpose, love, and adventure without ever leaving the building as decades of Soviet history pass outside.

title: The Lincoln Highway
author: Amor Towles
description: Two escaped juvenile delinquents hijack a young man's plan to drive from Nebraska to California, redirecting the journey to New York in ten chaotic days.

title: Rules of Civility
author: Amor Towles
description: A sharp-witted young woman navigates the upper echelons of 1938 New York society after a chance encounter with a handsome banker on New Year's Eve.

title: Lessons in Chemistry
author: Bonnie Garmus
description: A brilliant female chemist in the 1960s is forced out of her lab and becomes the unlikely star of a cooking show that teaches women they are capable of anything.

title: Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow
author: Gabrielle Zevin
description: Two friends create a groundbreaking video game together, and their decades-long creative partnership tests the boundaries of love, ambition, and identity.

title: Fourth Wing
author: Rebecca Yarros
description: A fragile young woman enters a brutal war college where students bond with dragons or die trying, and her commanding officer has been ordered to kill her.

title: Iron Flame
author: Rebecca Yarros
description: Violet returns for her second year at Basgiath, but a conspiracy within the leadership and a war on the borders force her to question everything she's been taught.

title: The Passage
author: Justin Cronin
description: A government experiment to create supersoldiers using a virus from South American bats goes wrong, unleashing vampiric creatures that destroy civilization.

title: Ready Player One
author: Ernest Cline
description: In a dystopian future, a teenager hunts for an Easter egg hidden in a massive virtual reality world, competing against corporate villains for control of the digital universe.

title: The Testaments
author: Margaret Atwood
description: Three women narrate the fall of Gilead from within: an Aunt, a daughter raised in the regime, and a girl who grew up in Canada knowing nothing of her origin.

title: Demon Copperhead
author: Barbara Kingsolver
description: A modern retelling of David Copperfield set in Appalachian Virginia, following a boy through foster care, addiction, and the opioid crisis.

title: The Poisonwood Bible
author: Barbara Kingsolver
description: A Baptist preacher drags his wife and four daughters to the Belgian Congo in 1959, and each woman narrates how Africa transforms and breaks the family.

title: The Covenant of Water
author: Abraham Verghese
description: Three generations of a family in Kerala, India, are linked by a mysterious affliction—a tendency to drown—across a century of love, medicine, and colonial history.

title: Cutting for Stone
author: Abraham Verghese
description: Twin brothers born in Ethiopia from a secret union between an Indian nun and a British surgeon are separated by betrayal and reunited by the medicine that shaped them both.

title: The Women
author: Kristin Hannah
description: A young nurse volunteers for Vietnam and returns to an America that doesn't want to hear what she survived, in a novel about the women who served.

title: The Dead Zone
author: Stephen King
description: A man wakes from a five-year coma with psychic powers and must decide whether to assassinate a politician he foresees will cause nuclear war.

title: Cujo
author: Stephen King
description: A rabid Saint Bernard traps a mother and her son in a broken-down car during a sweltering Maine summer.

title: Christine
author: Stephen King
description: A teenager buys a 1958 Plymouth Fury that is alive, possessive, and willing to kill anyone who gets in the way.

title: Needful Things
author: Stephen King
description: A shopkeeper sells everyone exactly what they want—and the price is doing a prank on your neighbor that escalates into chaos.

title: The Green Mile
author: Stephen King
description: A death row guard discovers a gentle giant inmate has miraculous healing powers.

title: Under the Dome
author: Stephen King
description: An invisible dome seals off a small Maine town and the community descends into authoritarianism.

title: Revival
author: Stephen King
description: A boy befriends a minister obsessed with electricity whose experiments take an increasingly dark direction over decades.

title: Billy Summers
author: Stephen King
description: A hitman posing as a writer in a small town finds the cover story becoming real.

title: Different Seasons
author: Stephen King
description: Four novellas including Shawshank Redemption and The Body—proving King writes far beyond horror.

title: Wolves of the Calla
author: Stephen King
description: Roland defends a farming community from raiders who steal children.
altTitles: The Dark Tower V: Wolves of the Calla

title: Song of Susannah
author: Stephen King
description: Susannah is transported to 1999 New York while Roland must find Stephen King himself.
altTitles: The Dark Tower VI: Song of Susannah

title: The Dark Tower
author: Stephen King
description: Roland reaches the Dark Tower at last and discovers what waits at the top.

title: The Light Fantastic
author: Terry Pratchett
description: Rincewind and Twoflower continue misadventures as a red star threatens the Discworld.

title: Sourcery
author: Terry Pratchett
description: A sourceror with unlimited power threatens the balance of Discworld magic.

title: Pyramids
author: Terry Pratchett
description: A young pharaoh trained as an assassin finds his kingdom warps time by building pyramids.

title: American Tabloid
author: James Ellroy
description: Three rogue operatives navigate the CIA, the mob, and the Kennedys in the years leading to the JFK assassination.

title: Get Shorty
author: Elmore Leonard
description: A Miami loan shark goes to Hollywood to collect a debt and discovers the movie business is just like the mob, only less organized.

title: Out of Sight
author: Elmore Leonard
description: A bank robber and a federal marshal share a locked car trunk during an escape, sparking an attraction that complicates everything.

title: Rum Punch
author: Elmore Leonard
description: A flight attendant caught smuggling money plays the feds and the arms dealer against each other to keep the cash for herself.

title: Killshot
author: Elmore Leonard
description: A couple in witness protection is hunted by a hitman and his unstable partner across the Midwest.

title: Red Dragon
author: Thomas Harris
description: An FBI profiler who caught Hannibal Lecter is pulled out of retirement to find a serial killer called the Tooth Fairy, and must consult Lecter for help.

title: The Silence of the Lambs
author: Thomas Harris
description: A young FBI trainee must trade personal revelations with the imprisoned Hannibal Lecter to catch a killer who skins his victims.

title: Hannibal
author: Thomas Harris
description: Lecter is free in Florence while Clarice Starling's career crumbles, and a disfigured victim plots revenge that will bring them all together.

title: Fight Club
author: Chuck Palahniuk
description: An insomniac office worker and a charismatic soap salesman form an underground fighting ring that evolves into an anarchist movement.

title: Choke
author: Chuck Palahniuk
description: A sex-addicted con man deliberately chokes in restaurants so Good Samaritans will feel responsible for him and send money.

title: Survivor
author: Chuck Palahniuk
description: The last surviving member of a death cult dictates his life story into the black box of a hijacked plane.

title: Invisible Monsters
author: Chuck Palahniuk
description: A disfigured model teams up with a transgender woman on a cross-country road trip of pharmaceutical theft and identity reinvention.

title: American Psycho
author: Bret Easton Ellis
description: A Wall Street investment banker's obsession with status, grooming, and business cards masks—or perhaps produces—his life as a serial killer.

title: Less Than Zero
author: Bret Easton Ellis
description: A college student returns to Los Angeles for winter break and drifts through a world of parties, drugs, and moral vacancy among the wealthy young.

title: Trainspotting
author: Irvine Welsh
description: A group of heroin addicts in Edinburgh try and fail to get clean in a novel written in thick Scottish dialect that captures addiction's grip with savage humor.

title: Post Office
author: Charles Bukowski
description: A drunk working at the post office drinks, gambles, womanizes, and suffers through the deadening routine of American labor.

title: Ham on Rye
author: Charles Bukowski
description: Bukowski's alter ego Henry Chinaski grows up poor and abused in Depression-era LA, finding refuge in alcohol and the public library.

title: Women
author: Charles Bukowski
description: An aging writer achieves literary fame and drinks his way through a series of relationships with women who are drawn to and destroyed by him.

title: Fear and Loathing in Las Vegas
author: Hunter S. Thompson
description: A journalist and his attorney drive to Las Vegas loaded with drugs to cover a motorcycle race and find the American Dream, in the definitive gonzo document.

title: Hell's Angels
author: Hunter S. Thompson
description: Thompson embeds with the Hell's Angels motorcycle gang for a year, producing a classic of immersive journalism that ends with his beating.

title: The Sisters Brothers
author: Patrick deWitt
description: Two assassin brothers ride from Oregon City to San Francisco during the Gold Rush to kill a man, but the journey tests their bond and their profession.

title: French Exit
author: Patrick deWitt
description: A broke Manhattan socialite and her aimless son move to Paris with the last of her money and a cat that may contain her dead husband's spirit.

title: The Amazing Adventures of Kavalier & Clay
author: Michael Chabon
description: Two Jewish cousins in 1940s New York create a hit comic book superhero, channeling the horrors of WWII and their own longing for escape into art.

title: The Yiddish Policemen's Union
author: Michael Chabon
description: A detective in an alternate-history Jewish settlement in Alaska investigates a murder that connects to a messianic conspiracy.

title: Clockers
author: Richard Price
description: A homicide detective and a young crack dealer in the New Jersey projects circle each other after a murder that may have been committed by the dealer's brother.

title: Brooklyn
author: Colm Tóibín
description: A young Irish woman emigrates to 1950s Brooklyn and builds a new life, then is called home by a crisis that forces her to choose between two countries and two men.

title: The Master
author: Colm Tóibín
description: A novel of Henry James in the 1890s, exploring his loneliness, his suppressed desires, and the cost of turning life into art.

title: The New York Trilogy
author: Paul Auster
description: Three interconnected novellas use the form of detective fiction to explore identity, coincidence, and the act of writing itself in a labyrinthine New York.

title: Moon Palace
author: Paul Auster
description: A young man's picaresque journey from a New York rooftop to the Utah desert, connecting three generations of fathers and sons through coincidence and loss.

title: Crazy Rich Asians
author: Kevin Kwan
description: An American-born Chinese economics professor discovers her boyfriend is the heir to one of Asia's wealthiest families when she visits Singapore.

title: China Rich Girlfriend
author: Kevin Kwan
description: Rachel discovers her birth father is one of China's richest men while Nick's family faces new scandals and extravagances.

title: Rich People Problems
author: Kevin Kwan
description: The Young family's matriarch is on her deathbed and everyone converges on Singapore for the inheritance battle to end all inheritance battles.

title: The Humans
author: Matt Haig
description: An alien takes over a Cambridge professor's body to suppress a mathematical discovery, but starts falling in love with the man's messy human life.

title: How to Stop Time
author: Matt Haig
description: A man who ages extremely slowly has lived for centuries, and in the present must choose between the safety of hiding and the risk of connection.

title: An Absolutely Remarkable Thing
author: Hank Green
description: A young woman becomes famous overnight after posting a video of a mysterious robot sculpture that has appeared simultaneously in cities worldwide.

title: Bridge of Clay
author: Markus Zusak
description: Five brothers in a broken family navigate grief, and one of them builds a bridge—literally and figuratively—to reconnect with the father who abandoned them.

title: I Am the Messenger
author: Markus Zusak
description: An aimless cabdriver starts receiving playing cards with addresses on them, each sending him on a mission to help—or confront—a stranger.

title: Ready Player Two
author: Ernest Cline
description: Wade inherits a new VR technology that lets users experience other people's lives, but an AI trapped in the system has its own plans.

title: Dark Matter
author: Blake Crouch
description: A physicist is kidnapped and wakes up in an alternate version of his life, and must navigate infinite parallel worlds to find his way back to his family.

title: Recursion
author: Blake Crouch
description: A neuroscientist's memory technology allows people to relive their pasts, but it causes reality-destroying paradoxes as false memories overwrite the timeline.

title: Upgrade
author: Blake Crouch
description: A gene-protection agent is exposed to a virus that upgrades his DNA, making him smarter and stronger—but someone has done the same to his sister with opposite intentions.

title: Leviathan Wakes
author: James S.A. Corey
description: A detective and a ship's officer investigate a missing girl and a derelict ship in the asteroid belt, uncovering a conspiracy that threatens the solar system.

title: Caliban's War
author: James S.A. Corey
description: A Martian marine, a UN politician, and a botanist searching for his daughter converge as a protomolecule soldier is unleashed on Ganymede.

title: Abaddon's Gate
author: James S.A. Corey
description: The protomolecule builds a massive ring gate beyond Uranus, and a fleet of ships from all factions enters to discover what lies beyond.

title: Cibola Burn
author: James S.A. Corey
description: Settlers and a corporation clash on the first planet beyond the ring gates, while the alien ruins beneath them begin to wake up.

title: Nemesis Games
author: James S.A. Corey
description: The crew of the Rocinante scatters and Earth is attacked by a radical faction, in the most personal and devastating entry in the series.

title: Babylon's Ashes
author: James S.A. Corey
description: The Free Navy controls the gates while the remnants of civilization try to rebuild and reclaim the solar system.

title: Persepolis Rising
author: James S.A. Corey
description: Thirty years later, an authoritarian empire from beyond the gates invades the solar system with ships powered by alien technology.

title: Tiamat's Wrath
author: James S.A. Corey
description: The Laconian empire experiments with protomolecule technology to challenge the alien entities that destroyed the gate builders.

title: Leviathan Falls
author: James S.A. Corey
description: The final book resolves the mystery of the gate builders and the dark entities, as Holden faces a choice that will determine the future of consciousness.

title: We Are Legion (We Are Bob)
author: Dennis E. Taylor
description: A man's consciousness is uploaded into a Von Neumann probe and he replicates himself across the galaxy, discovering alien life and saving humanity.

title: For We Are Many
author: Dennis E. Taylor
description: The Bobs spread across the galaxy facing alien threats and human politics while trying to find a new home for humanity.

title: Old Man's War
author: John Scalzi
description: On your 75th birthday, you can join the Colonial Defense Forces—you get a young body and sent to fight aliens across the galaxy.

title: The Ghost Brigades
author: John Scalzi
description: A consciousness is transferred into a super-soldier body to find a traitor, but the new being develops its own identity and loyalties.

title: Redshirts
author: John Scalzi
description: The crew of a starship realizes they're characters on a bad TV show and that away missions are death sentences for low-ranking officers.

title: The Kaiju Preservation Society
author: John Scalzi
description: A laid-off worker discovers a parallel Earth where building-sized kaiju roam, and joins the team that studies and protects them.

title: Dogs of War
author: Adrian Tchaikovsky
description: A bioengineered super-soldier dog fights in a corporate war, then must prove he deserves rights as a person when the war ends.

title: Elder Race
author: Adrian Tchaikovsky
description: An anthropologist stranded on a planet is seen as a wizard by the locals who beg him to defeat a spreading evil—which may be real.

title: Pandora's Star
author: Peter F. Hamilton
description: Humanity discovers an alien barrier around a distant star system and sends an expedition to investigate, accidentally releasing a predatory alien species.

title: Judas Unchained
author: Peter F. Hamilton
description: The Commonwealth fights a desperate war against the Primes while a traitor works from within to ensure humanity's defeat.

title: The Long Way to a Small, Angry Planet
author: Becky Chambers
description: A diverse crew on a tunneling ship takes a job boring wormholes through space, and the journey matters more than the destination.

title: A Closed and Common Orbit
author: Becky Chambers
description: An AI in a human body and a girl raised in a factory explore what it means to be a person in a galaxy full of aliens.

title: Legends & Lattes
author: Travis Baldree
description: A retired barbarian adventurer opens a coffee shop in a city that has never heard of coffee, seeking a simple life after years of violence.

title: Bookshops & Bonedust
author: Travis Baldree
description: A young barbarian recovering from injury in a small town discovers the pleasures of a local bookshop and slower living.

title: Kings of the Wyld
author: Nicholas Eames
description: An aging band of mercenaries reunites for one last gig: crossing a monster-infested wilderness to rescue their former leader's daughter.

title: Prince of Thorns
author: Mark Lawrence
description: A thirteen-year-old prince leads a band of outlaws on a bloody path to reclaim his father's throne in a post-apocalyptic medieval world.

title: King of Thorns
author: Mark Lawrence
description: Jorg defends his small kingdom against a massive invading army while flashbacks reveal the dark bargains that brought him to the throne.

title: Emperor of Thorns
author: Mark Lawrence
description: Jorg seeks to become Emperor of the Broken Empire, confronting the Dead King and the truth about the world's destruction.

title: The Rage of Dragons
author: Evan Winter
description: A young man in a society inspired by East Africa seeks vengeance for his people's oppression through mastery of the sword and forbidden magic.

title: Promise of Blood
author: Brian McClellan
description: A field marshal leads a coup against his king using gunpowder mages—soldiers who gain power from ingesting gunpowder—and discovers a divine prophecy may doom them all.

title: The Black Echo
author: Michael Connelly
description: LAPD detective Harry Bosch investigates a body in a drainage tunnel and connects the murder to a Vietnam-era tunnel rat network and a bank heist.

title: The Black Ice
author: Michael Connelly
description: Bosch investigates a narcotics officer's apparent suicide and uncovers a drug operation reaching across the Mexican border.

title: The Concrete Blonde
author: Michael Connelly
description: Bosch faces a civil trial for shooting a suspected serial killer while a new victim surfaces, suggesting the real killer is still free.

title: The Last Coyote
author: Michael Connelly
description: Suspended from duty, Bosch investigates his mother's thirty-year-old unsolved murder.

title: The Lincoln Lawyer
author: Michael Connelly
description: A defense attorney who operates out of his Lincoln Town Car takes on a wealthy client accused of assault and realizes the case connects to an old injustice.

title: The Reversal
author: Michael Connelly
description: Mickey Haller prosecutes a case for the first time—a convicted child killer getting a retrial—and partners with Bosch to find the truth.

title: The Late Show
author: Michael Connelly
description: Detective Renée Ballard works the late shift and catches two cases in one night—a nightclub assault and a quadruple murder—that no one wants her to solve.

title: The Dark Hours
author: Michael Connelly
description: Ballard and Bosch team up to hunt a serial rapist-turned-killer while a cold case pulls them into a decades-old conspiracy.

title: Tell No One
author: Harlan Coben
description: Eight years after his wife's murder, a pediatrician receives an email that suggests she may still be alive.

title: The Stranger
author: Harlan Coben
description: A stranger tells a man a secret about his wife that unravels his entire life and connects to a web of deception across his community.

title: Gone for Good
author: Harlan Coben
description: A man still haunted by his girlfriend's murder discovers his presumed-dead brother may be alive and connected to the crime.

title: The Boy from the Woods
author: Harlan Coben
description: A man who was found living feral in the woods as a child uses his unique skills to find a missing girl on the eve of a political scandal.

title: The Charm School
author: Nelson DeMille
description: An American tourist in Russia stumbles onto a KGB school where captured US pilots have been teaching Soviet agents to pass as Americans for decades.

title: Plum Island
author: Nelson DeMille
description: A wounded NYPD detective investigates the murder of a couple who worked at a government biolab on Plum Island, where the motives range from bioterrorism to buried treasure.

title: The Hunt for Red October
author: Tom Clancy
description: A Soviet submarine captain attempts to defect with his country's most advanced nuclear sub, and a CIA analyst is the only one who believes it.

title: Patriot Games
author: Tom Clancy
description: Jack Ryan foils an IRA attack on the Prince of Wales and becomes a target for revenge.

title: Clear and Present Danger
author: Tom Clancy
description: The US wages a covert war against Colombian drug cartels, and Ryan discovers the operation has been kept from Congress.

title: The Sum of All Fears
author: Tom Clancy
description: Terrorists build a nuclear weapon from a lost Israeli bomb and detonate it at the Super Bowl, nearly triggering World War III.

title: Transfer of Power
author: Vince Flynn
description: Terrorists seize the White House while the president hides in a bunker, and CIA operative Mitch Rapp is sent in alone.

title: Consent to Kill
author: Vince Flynn
description: Rapp is targeted by a contract killing team hired by a Saudi billionaire, and the attack devastates his personal life.

title: American Assassin
author: Vince Flynn
description: The origin story of Mitch Rapp: recruited by the CIA after his girlfriend is killed in the Lockerbie bombing, he becomes their most lethal weapon.

title: Absolute Power
author: David Baldacci
description: A cat burglar witnesses the president involved in a murder and must survive a cover-up that reaches the highest level of government.

title: The Camel Club
author: David Baldacci
description: Four eccentric outsiders who meet near the White House to discuss conspiracy theories stumble onto a real one involving the president and terrorism.

title: The Terminal List
author: Jack Carr
description: A Navy SEAL whose team was ambushed discovers the attack was orchestrated by his own government and methodically works down a list of those responsible.

title: True Believer
author: Jack Carr
description: James Reece is pulled out of hiding to track a sniper targeting Americans overseas with connections to a broader terror plot.

title: The Lions of Lucerne
author: Brad Thor
description: A Secret Service agent survives a kidnapping of the president during a ski trip and uncovers a treasonous conspiracy.

title: The Big Sleep
author: Raymond Chandler
description: Private detective Philip Marlowe is hired by a dying millionaire to handle a blackmailer and wades into a tangle of pornography, murder, and missing persons.

title: Farewell, My Lovely
author: Raymond Chandler
description: Marlowe searches for the missing girlfriend of a recently released convict and stumbles into a web of corruption connecting the LA underworld to high society.

title: The Long Goodbye
author: Raymond Chandler
description: Marlowe helps a charming drunk flee the country after his wife's murder, then refuses to believe his friend is guilty even as everyone else moves on.

title: The Maltese Falcon
author: Dashiell Hammett
description: Sam Spade investigates his partner's murder and gets entangled with three criminals searching for a priceless jeweled statue.

title: The Thin Man
author: Dashiell Hammett
description: A retired detective and his wealthy wife investigate a murder while drinking their way through 1930s New York.

title: Red Harvest
author: Dashiell Hammett
description: A detective hired to clean up a corrupt Montana mining town plays the rival gangs against each other until the bodies pile up.

title: The Godfather
author: Mario Puzo
description: The Corleone family's rise and reign in the American Mafia, as a reluctant youngest son becomes the most ruthless don of all.

title: Shōgun
author: James Clavell
description: An English navigator shipwrecked in feudal Japan becomes entangled in the political machinations of a lord seeking to become Shogun.

title: Tai-Pan
author: James Clavell
description: A Scottish merchant builds a trading empire in newly founded Hong Kong while battling rivals, pirates, and the Chinese government.

title: Lonesome Dove
author: Larry McMurtry
description: Two aging former Texas Rangers drive a stolen herd of cattle from Texas to Montana in the last great cattle drive, in the definitive American Western novel.

title: Terms of Endearment
author: Larry McMurtry
description: A widow and her daughter navigate love, loss, and each other in Houston and beyond.

title: Master and Commander
author: Patrick O'Brian
description: A British naval captain and a ship's surgeon form a lifelong friendship during the Napoleonic Wars in the first of twenty novels.

title: The Last Kingdom
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: A Saxon boy raised by Vikings must choose sides as Alfred the Great fights to save England from Danish conquest.

title: The Pale Horseman
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Uhtred serves Alfred reluctantly as the Danes overrun Wessex, and only one last stand in the marshes can save the kingdom.

title: Sharpe's Eagle
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: A rifleman in Wellington's army must capture a French imperial eagle while fighting incompetent officers on his own side.

title: Heart-Shaped Box
author: Joe Hill
description: An aging rock star who collects occult objects buys a ghost online and discovers it is the vengeful spirit of his dead girlfriend's stepfather.

title: NOS4A2
author: Joe Hill
description: A woman who can find lost things with her bicycle faces off against an immortal who kidnaps children to his nightmarish Christmasland in a Rolls-Royce with sinister plates.

title: Horns
author: Joe Hill
description: A man suspected of his girlfriend's rape and murder wakes up with devil horns that compel people to confess their darkest thoughts to him.

title: The Fireman
author: Joe Hill
description: A plague of spontaneous combustion spreads globally, and a pregnant nurse joins a commune of infected people led by a man who can control the fire.

title: A Head Full of Ghosts
author: Paul Tremblay
description: A family allows a reality TV crew to film their daughter's apparent demonic possession, and the younger sister tells the story decades later.

title: The Cabin at the End of the World
author: Paul Tremblay
description: A family on vacation is held hostage by four strangers who demand they choose one member to sacrifice to prevent the apocalypse.

title: The Daughter of Doctor Moreau
author: Silvia Moreno-Garcia
description: A reimagining of The Island of Doctor Moreau set in 19th-century Yucatán, where a doctor creates human-animal hybrids on a remote estate.

title: Velvet Was the Night
author: Silvia Moreno-Garcia
description: A lonely secretary and a government spy search 1970s Mexico City for a missing woman, drawn into the violence of the Dirty War.

title: The Deep Blue Good-By
author: John D. MacDonald
description: Travis McGee, a self-described salvage consultant living on a houseboat, recovers stolen property for people who have no other recourse.

title: Moneyball
author: Michael Lewis
description: The Oakland A's general manager uses statistical analysis instead of traditional scouting to build a competitive team on a tiny budget.

title: The Big Short
author: Michael Lewis
description: A handful of outsiders bet against the housing market before the 2008 crash and are proven catastrophically right.

title: The Blind Side
author: Michael Lewis
description: A homeless Black teenager is adopted by a wealthy white family and becomes an NFL star, while Lewis examines how football changed to value left tackles.

title: Flash Boys
author: Michael Lewis
description: A group of Wall Street outsiders discovers that the stock market is rigged by high-frequency traders and builds a fairer exchange.

title: The Devil in the White City
author: Erik Larson
description: The parallel stories of the architect behind the 1893 Chicago World's Fair and the serial killer who used it as a hunting ground.

title: Dead Wake
author: Erik Larson
description: The sinking of the Lusitania told through the passengers, the submarine captain, and the geopolitical forces that made the tragedy inevitable.

title: In the Garden of Beasts
author: Erik Larson
description: The American ambassador to Berlin in 1933 watches the Nazis consolidate power while his daughter romances Gestapo officers.

title: Killers of the Flower Moon
author: David Grann
description: The Osage Nation murders in 1920s Oklahoma, where wealthy Native Americans were systematically killed for their oil rights and the FBI's early investigation exposed a conspiracy.

title: The Lost City of Z
author: David Grann
description: A British explorer vanishes in the Amazon searching for an ancient civilization, and the author retraces his steps to discover what happened.

title: A Short History of Nearly Everything
author: Bill Bryson
description: Bryson explains the history of science from the Big Bang to the rise of civilization, making complex topics accessible and entertaining.

title: A Walk in the Woods
author: Bill Bryson
description: Bryson and a spectacularly unprepared friend attempt to hike the Appalachian Trail, producing a hilarious account of nature, history, and middle-aged folly.

title: In a Sunburned Country
author: Bill Bryson
description: Bryson travels across Australia encountering deadly wildlife, vast emptiness, and the cheerful resilience of Australians.

title: The Body
author: Bill Bryson
description: A head-to-toe tour of the human body explaining how it works, what can go wrong, and the remarkable stories behind medical discoveries.

title: The Obstacle Is the Way
author: Ryan Holiday
description: Modern Stoic philosophy applied to overcoming obstacles, using historical examples from Marcus Aurelius to Steve Jobs.

title: Ego Is the Enemy
author: Ryan Holiday
description: How ego undermines success at every stage—aspiration, achievement, and failure—with lessons from history's humblest high performers.

title: Stillness Is the Key
author: Ryan Holiday
description: Drawing on Stoic, Buddhist, and Epicurean wisdom, Holiday argues that stillness is the secret to peak performance and inner peace.

title: The 48 Laws of Power
author: Robert Greene
description: Forty-eight principles of power distilled from three thousand years of history, from Sun Tzu to Machiavelli to Henry Kissinger.

title: Mastery
author: Robert Greene
description: How history's greatest minds—from Leonardo to Darwin—achieved mastery through apprenticeship, creative thinking, and relentless focus.

title: The Art of Seduction
author: Robert Greene
description: A study of seduction as a form of power, drawing on historical figures from Cleopatra to Casanova.

title: 12 Rules for Life
author: Jordan Peterson
description: A clinical psychologist draws on mythology, religion, and clinical experience to offer twelve practical rules for living a meaningful life.

title: Deep Work
author: Cal Newport
description: In a world of constant distraction, the ability to focus deeply is both rare and valuable—and this book shows how to cultivate it.

title: Digital Minimalism
author: Cal Newport
description: A philosophy for reducing screen time and reclaiming attention by being intentional about which technologies you let into your life.

title: Steve Jobs
author: Walter Isaacson
description: The authorized biography of Apple's co-founder, based on forty interviews with Jobs and over a hundred with family, friends, and rivals.

title: Leonardo da Vinci
author: Walter Isaacson
description: A biography built from da Vinci's notebooks, revealing a genius driven by insatiable curiosity about everything from anatomy to optics to pageantry.

title: Elon Musk
author: Walter Isaacson
description: Two years of shadow access to Musk reveals the childhood trauma, risk addiction, and relentless drive behind Tesla, SpaceX, and the Twitter acquisition.

title: Einstein
author: Walter Isaacson
description: Einstein's life from patent clerk to icon, showing how his rebellious personality fueled both his physics and his politics.

title: Player Piano
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: In a near-future America where machines have replaced most workers, an engineer leads a doomed rebellion against automation and the technocratic elite.

title: God Bless You, Mr. Rosewater
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: A millionaire philanthropist tries to give his fortune to the poor while a lawyer schemes to have him declared insane and redirect the money.

title: Bluebeard
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: An aging Abstract Expressionist painter hides a secret in his potato barn and narrates his life among the New York art world and the Armenian genocide.

title: Galápagos
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: A ghost narrates a million years of human evolution after a plague strands a small group on the Galápagos Islands, where natural selection shrinks the human brain.

title: Hocus Pocus
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: A Vietnam veteran turned college professor is imprisoned after a prison break and writes his memoirs on scraps of paper, tallying everyone he killed and everyone he slept with.

title: Jailbird
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: A Watergate conspirator is released from prison and stumbles through a New York controlled by a secret corporate empire run by a bag lady.

title: Deadeye Dick
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: A pharmacist in Midland City recounts how he accidentally shot a pregnant woman as a child and has lived as a neuter ever since.

title: Slapstick
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: Grotesque twins separated at birth—one becomes president and proposes giving everyone new middle names to create artificial extended families in a collapsing America.

title: Timequake
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: A timequake forces everyone to relive the last decade on autopilot, and when free will resumes, the world collapses because no one remembers how to make decisions.

title: Welcome to the Monkey House
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: Twenty-five stories spanning Vonnegut's career, including Harrison Bergeron, Who Am I This Time?, and the title story about mandatory birth control.

title: Factotum
author: Charles Bukowski
description: Chinaski drifts through dozens of dead-end jobs across wartime America, getting fired from each one for drinking, fighting, or simply not caring.

title: Hollywood
author: Charles Bukowski
description: Chinaski writes a screenplay and navigates the absurdity of the movie business, discovering Hollywood is even more depraved than skid row.

title: Pulp
author: Charles Bukowski
description: A hapless detective hunts for a missing author, the Red Sparrow, and Death herself in Bukowski's final novel—a surreal, boozy parody of hardboiled fiction.

title: Love Is a Dog from Hell
author: Charles Bukowski
description: Poems about drinking, women, horse racing, and the small humiliations of being alive, in Bukowski's most popular poetry collection.

title: South of No North
author: Charles Bukowski
description: Short stories of barflies, prostitutes, gamblers, and working stiffs, each one a small portrait of life at the bottom.

title: Notes of a Dirty Old Man
author: Charles Bukowski
description: A collection of Bukowski's underground newspaper columns mixing autobiography, fiction, and provocation.

title: Journey to the End of the Night
author: Louis-Ferdinand Céline
description: A young Frenchman's picaresque odyssey through World War I, colonial Africa, America, and the slums of Paris, narrated in revolutionary spoken-language prose soaked in disgust and dark comedy.

title: Death on the Installment Plan
author: Louis-Ferdinand Céline
description: Ferdinand's childhood and adolescence in turn-of-the-century Paris—poverty, beatings, failed apprenticeships—told in Céline's hallucinatory, punctuation-shattering style.

title: Castle to Castle
author: Louis-Ferdinand Céline
description: Céline recounts his exile in the German castle of Sigmaringen with Vichy collaborators as the Reich collapses around them.

title: North
author: Louis-Ferdinand Céline
description: The flight through bombed Germany continues as Céline, his wife, and their cat try to reach Denmark through apocalyptic landscapes.

title: Rigadoon
author: Louis-Ferdinand Céline
description: The final novel, published posthumously, completing Céline's war trilogy as the refugees finally reach Copenhagen through the wreckage of Europe.

title: Tropic of Cancer
author: Henry Miller
description: An American writer in 1930s Paris lives in squalor, sleeps with everyone, and writes about it in a book so explicit it was banned in the US for decades.

title: Tropic of Capricorn
author: Henry Miller
description: Miller's fictionalized account of his years working at a telegraph company in New York, raging against American conformity and celebrating the chaos of the body.

title: Black Spring
author: Henry Miller
description: A collection of surrealist sketches and stories bridging the two Tropics, mixing Brooklyn childhood memories with Parisian fever dreams.

title: Sexus
author: Henry Miller
description: The first volume of The Rosy Crucifixion chronicles Miller's affair with his second wife June and his transformation into a writer, in graphic and obsessive detail.

title: Plexus
author: Henry Miller
description: Miller and June live in Brooklyn as he tries to write and she hustles money from admirers in the bohemian underworld.

title: Nexus
author: Henry Miller
description: The triangular relationship between Miller, June, and her friend Jean collapses as Miller prepares to leave for Paris and become the writer he was meant to be.

title: Big Sur and the Oranges of Hieronymus Bosch
author: Henry Miller
description: Miller's account of living in Big Sur, California, celebrating the landscape, the eccentric community, and the freedom of dropping out of the rat race.

title: The Colossus of Maroussi
author: Henry Miller
description: Miller's ecstatic account of traveling through Greece just before World War II, which he considered his finest book.

title: Sabbath's Theater
author: Philip Roth
description: A disgraced puppeteer mourns his dead mistress in an orgy of grief, lust, and rage against death that is Roth's most extreme and uninhibited novel.

title: The Ghost Writer
author: Philip Roth
description: A young writer visits a reclusive master and fantasizes that the mysterious woman staying there is Anne Frank, who survived and lives in secret.

title: Zuckerman Unbound
author: Philip Roth
description: After publishing a scandalous bestseller, Nathan Zuckerman is stalked by a fan, sued by his family, and can't walk down the street in peace.

title: The Anatomy Lesson
author: Philip Roth
description: Zuckerman, crippled by back pain and unable to write, decides to become a doctor at forty and goes on a drug-fueled bender through Chicago.

title: The Counterlife
author: Philip Roth
description: Multiple versions of Zuckerman's life contradict each other—Israel, England, New Jersey—in Roth's most formally daring novel.

title: I Married a Communist
author: Philip Roth
description: A radio star in the McCarthy era is destroyed by his ex-wife's tell-all memoir, exposing the human cost of political betrayal.

title: The Plot Against America
author: Philip Roth
description: An alternate history where Charles Lindbergh wins the 1940 election and steers America toward fascism, told through a Jewish family in Newark.

title: Goodbye, Columbus
author: Philip Roth
description: A working-class Jewish man's summer affair with a wealthy suburban girl exposes the class tensions within American Jewish life, in Roth's debut novella and stories.

title: The Dying Animal
author: Philip Roth
description: A sixty-two-year-old professor's obsessive affair with a twenty-four-year-old Cuban student destroys his emotional detachment.

title: Everyman
author: Philip Roth
description: A man's life told backward from his funeral through his medical crises, marriages, and the slow betrayal of the body.

title: Exit Ghost
author: Philip Roth
description: An aging Zuckerman returns to New York after eleven years of seclusion and finds the city, his body, and his relevance have all changed.

title: Nemesis
author: Philip Roth
description: A playground director in 1944 Newark faces a polio epidemic and a crisis of faith as children around him fall ill.

title: Indignation
author: Philip Roth
description: A Newark butcher's son at a conservative Ohio college in 1951 rebels against conformity and is destroyed by his own stubbornness and bad luck.

title: My Life as a Man
author: Philip Roth
description: A novelist trapped in a catastrophic marriage tries to write his way out, producing two fictional versions of his story before telling the 'true' one.

title: Operation Shylock
author: Philip Roth
description: Philip Roth discovers an impostor using his name in Israel to promote the return of Jews to Europe, and travels to confront him during the Demjanjuk trial.

title: The Breast
author: Philip Roth
description: A literature professor wakes up transformed into a giant female breast and must come to terms with his new existence.

title: Deception
author: Philip Roth
description: A novel written entirely in dialogue between a Philip Roth-like writer and his English mistress, their conversations becoming the affair itself.

title: Straight Man
author: Richard Russo
description: The chairman of an English department at a failing Pennsylvania college navigates budget cuts, faculty warfare, and a midlife crisis with self-deprecating wit.

title: Bridge of Sighs
author: Richard Russo
description: A man who has never left his upstate New York hometown and his childhood friend who fled to Venice trade perspectives on loyalty, escape, and the roads not taken.

title: That Old Cape Magic
author: Richard Russo
description: A screenwriter drives up and down the New England coast with his dead parents' ashes, unable to scatter them or let go of their toxic influence.

title: Everybody's Fool
author: Richard Russo
description: Sully and the residents of North Bath return in the sequel to Nobody's Fool, still broke, still stubborn, and somehow still surviving.

title: Chances Are...
author: Richard Russo
description: Three college friends reunite on Martha's Vineyard and confront the disappearance of a woman they all loved forty years ago.

title: The Gambler
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: A young tutor in a fictional European resort becomes consumed by roulette, mirroring the ruinous gambling addiction Dostoevsky himself battled.

title: The Double
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: A minor government clerk is driven mad when an identical double appears and begins taking over every aspect of his life.

title: Poor Folk
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: An impoverished clerk and a young seamstress exchange letters about their desperate lives in St. Petersburg, in Dostoevsky's first novel.

title: The House of the Dead
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: A thinly fictionalized account of Dostoevsky's four years in a Siberian prison camp, observing the criminals, the cruelty, and the human capacity to endure.

title: The Adolescent
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: An illegitimate son seeks to become a Rothschild through sheer will, but his obsessive plan collides with his desire for his father's love and approval.

title: The Eternal Husband
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: A cuckolded husband tracks down his dead wife's lover and the two men form a grotesque, codependent relationship of hatred and need.

title: White Nights
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: A lonely dreamer meets a young woman on a St. Petersburg bridge over four white nights and falls in love, knowing she is waiting for another man.

title: Ask the Dust
author: John Fante
description: A young Italian-American writer in Depression-era Los Angeles struggles with poverty, ambition, and a doomed love affair with a Mexican waitress, in the novel Bukowski called his Bible.

title: Wait Until Spring, Bandini
author: John Fante
description: A boy in a poor Italian family in Colorado endures a harsh winter while his bricklayer father drinks, gambles, and has an affair.

title: The Road to Los Angeles
author: John Fante
description: Young Bandini works in a fish cannery while declaring himself a genius destined for literary greatness, in the novel rejected in Fante's lifetime.

title: Dreams from Bunker Hill
author: John Fante
description: An aging Bandini in Hollywood reflects on his failed screenwriting career and the distance between his dreams and reality, dictated by a blind and diabetic Fante.

title: Full of Life
author: John Fante
description: A screenwriter's life is upended when his pregnant wife converts to Catholicism and his Italian father arrives to fix the sagging kitchen floor.

title: The Brotherhood of the Grape
author: John Fante
description: A writer returns home to mediate his elderly parents' latest separation and is recruited by his mason father for one last job building a stone smokehouse.

title: 1933 Was a Bad Year
author: John Fante
description: A seventeen-year-old Italian-American in Colorado dreams of becoming a baseball pitcher while his family crumbles around him in the Depression.

title: Guignol's Band
author: Louis-Ferdinand Céline
description: Ferdinand flees to London during WWI and falls in among pimps, prostitutes, and gas-mask inventors in a delirious underworld picaresque.

title: London Bridge
author: Louis-Ferdinand Céline
description: The sequel to Guignol's Band continues Ferdinand's London misadventures among occultists, spies, and the Zeppelin bombings of 1915.

title: Conversations with Professor Y
author: Louis-Ferdinand Céline
description: Céline interviews a fictional professor about his own literary style, producing a manic, self-justifying manifesto on his invention of the spoken-voice novel.

title: When She Was Good
author: Philip Roth
description: A young woman in small-town 1940s America tries to impose moral order on her alcoholic father and weak husband, destroying everyone including herself.

title: Our Gang
author: Philip Roth
description: A savage political satire in which a Nixonesque president invades Denmark to protect the unborn and is assassinated by his own cabinet.

title: The Professor of Desire
author: Philip Roth
description: David Kepesh's journey from erotic adventures in Europe to the quiet pleasures of domesticity, which he knows he will eventually betray.

title: The Facts
author: Philip Roth
description: Roth's autobiography—followed by a letter from his character Nathan Zuckerman telling him the fiction was more truthful than the facts.

title: Patrimony
author: Philip Roth
description: Roth's memoir of his father's final years and death from a brain tumor, unflinching about the body's indignities and the son's helpless love.

title: Letting Go
author: Philip Roth
description: A young English instructor navigates romantic entanglements and the burden of responsibility in 1950s Chicago, in Roth's long, Jamesian first novel.

title: The Humbling
author: Philip Roth
description: A great stage actor loses his talent overnight and grasps at a last-chance love affair to stave off collapse.

title: Erections, Ejaculations, Exhibitions and General Tales of Ordinary Madness
author: Charles Bukowski
description: Raw, transgressive stories from the LA underground—drunks, whores, losers, and occasional moments of accidental grace.

title: The Most Beautiful Woman in Town
author: Charles Bukowski
description: Short stories spanning Bukowski's career, from brutal realism to moments of unexpected tenderness.

title: Burning in Water, Drowning in Flame
author: Charles Bukowski
description: Selected poems from 1955-1973 covering drinking, horse racing, skid row, and the small daily war of staying alive.

title: The Days Run Away Like Wild Horses Over the Hills
author: Charles Bukowski
description: Early poems written during Bukowski's years as a postal worker, raw and unpolished, about loneliness, alcohol, and the American underclass.

title: War All the Time
author: Charles Bukowski
description: Poems from 1981-1984, written during his years of late fame, about aging, the racetrack, and the absurdity of becoming a literary celebrity.

title: You Get So Alone at Times That It Just Makes Sense
author: Charles Bukowski
description: Late-period poems that are mellower but no less sharp, about cats, classical music, his wife Linda, and watching the world from his window.

title: The Last Night of the Earth Poems
author: Charles Bukowski
description: One of Bukowski's final poetry collections, more reflective and death-haunted, still funny, still mean, still alive.

title: Shakespeare Never Did This
author: Charles Bukowski
description: A journal of Bukowski's first trip to Europe for poetry readings—drinking through Germany and France while hating every minute of being a public figure.

title: Run with the Hunted
author: Charles Bukowski
description: A career-spanning anthology arranged as autobiography, tracing Chinaski/Bukowski from childhood through old age.

title: The Insulted and Humiliated
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: A young writer entangled in the lives of two families watches a corrupt prince manipulate everyone around him while the innocent suffer.

title: The Village of Stepanchikovo
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: A petty tyrant dominates a country household through psychological manipulation, in Dostoevsky's most purely comic novel.

title: A Gentle Creature
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: A pawnbroker's monologue over his dead wife's body, reconstructing their marriage and his failure to understand her until it was too late.

title: Uncle's Dream
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: A provincial widow schemes to marry her daughter to a senile old prince, resulting in a farcical social catastrophe.

title: Netochka Nezvanova
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: An unfinished novel about a girl's childhood—her father's delusional musical ambitions, her passage through wealthy households, and her intense friendships with other women.

title: Bagombo Snuff Box
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: Twenty-three uncollected stories from the 1950s and 1960s, mostly from magazines, with Vonnegut's own introductions reflecting on his early career.

title: A Man Without a Country
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: Vonnegut's final collection of essays and speeches—funny, furious, and despairing about the state of America and the human race.

title: Palm Sunday
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: An autobiographical collage of speeches, reviews, and essays that Vonnegut assembles into something like a memoir.

title: Fates Worse Than Death
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: Autobiographical essays on censorship, the Gulf War, his family's suicides, and why humor is the only sane response to an insane world.

title: Armageddon in Retrospect
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: Previously unpublished stories and essays about war, including Vonnegut's letter home after being liberated from the POW camp where he survived the Dresden bombing.

title: While Mortals Sleep
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: Sixteen previously unpublished stories from the 1950s and 1960s, discovered after Vonnegut's death.

title: Look at the Birdie
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: Fourteen previously unpublished stories found after Vonnegut's death, ranging from domestic comedy to dystopian satire.

title: The Air-Conditioned Nightmare
author: Henry Miller
description: Miller returns to America after a decade in Paris and drives across the country, horrified by its materialism, conformity, and spiritual emptiness.

title: Remember to Remember
author: Henry Miller
description: The sequel to The Air-Conditioned Nightmare continues Miller's love-hate confrontation with America, mixing travel, art criticism, and personal reflection.

title: The Books in My Life
author: Henry Miller
description: Miller's account of the books that shaped him, from Dostoevsky and Nietzsche to forgotten authors no one else reads.

title: Quiet Days in Clichy
author: Henry Miller
description: Two Americans in 1930s Paris drift through cheap hotels and cafés, picking up women and arguing about art in a slim, cheerful companion to the Tropics.

title: The Smile at the Foot of the Ladder
author: Henry Miller
description: A fable about a clown who wants to make audiences cry instead of laugh, and his search for something beyond performance.

title: Stand Still Like the Hummingbird
author: Henry Miller
description: Essays on art, literature, and the writing life, including pieces on Thoreau, Rimbaud, and the meaning of creative freedom.

title: The Wine of Youth
author: John Fante
description: Short stories set among Italian-American families in Colorado and California, many featuring early versions of Bandini.

title: Dago Red
author: John Fante
description: Interconnected stories of an Italian-American boy growing up in Boulder, Colorado, struggling with Catholicism, poverty, and his parents' volatile marriage.

title: West of Rome
author: John Fante
description: Two novellas—one about a Hollywood screenwriter's dog who becomes a celebrity, another about a man building a stone wall for his mother—published posthumously.

title: The Risk Pool
author: Richard Russo
description: A boy in upstate New York is raised by his reckless, charming father after his mother is institutionalized, learning to navigate a world of pool halls and blue-collar chaos.

title: Mohawk
author: Richard Russo
description: Russo's debut novel set in a declining upstate New York mill town, where a network of connected lives struggle against economic decay and personal failings.

title: Breakfast at Tiffany's
author: Truman Capote
description: A young writer befriends Holly Golightly, a charming, reckless socialite in 1940s Manhattan who is running from her past as fast as she can.

title: Other Voices, Other Rooms
author: Truman Capote
description: A thirteen-year-old boy travels to a decaying Louisiana mansion to find his father and discovers a Gothic world of eccentrics and buried secrets.

title: The Grass Harp
author: Truman Capote
description: A boy, his elderly cousin, and a retired judge take refuge in a tree house to escape a domineering relative, forming a community of misfits.

title: A Christmas Memory
author: Truman Capote
description: A boy and his elderly cousin bake fruitcakes and make each other kites in a tender memoir of childhood friendship and loss.

title: Music for Chameleons
author: Truman Capote
description: Capote's final collection mixing fiction, reportage, and conversational portraits, including his own account of attending a murder trial.

title: The Inheritors
author: William Golding
description: A band of gentle Neanderthals encounters Homo sapiens for the first time and is destroyed by the cunning and violence of the new species.

title: Pincher Martin
author: William Golding
description: A naval officer clings to a rock in the Atlantic after his ship is torpedoed, but his survival may be less real than it appears.

title: Rites of Passage
author: William Golding
description: A young aristocrat's journal of a sea voyage to Australia becomes the story of a parson's humiliation and death aboard ship.

series.name: To the Ends of the Earth

title: The Spire
author: William Golding
description: A medieval dean is obsessed with building an impossible spire on his cathedral, driving the project forward through faith while the foundations crack.

title: The Confessions of Nat Turner
author: William Styron
description: A fictionalized account of the 1831 Virginia slave rebellion, narrated by its leader as he awaits execution.

title: Lie Down in Darkness
author: William Styron
description: A dysfunctional Virginia family unravels through alcoholism, adultery, and the father's favoritism as they gather for a daughter's funeral.

title: Darkness Visible
author: William Styron
description: Styron's memoir of his descent into suicidal depression and slow recovery, one of the most honest accounts of mental illness ever written.

title: Set This House on Fire
author: William Styron
description: Americans in a small Italian town are drawn into a web of violence, guilt, and corruption when a wealthy psychopath's cruelty reaches its peak.

title: Something Happened
author: Joseph Heller
description: A middle-aged executive narrates his corporate life, his failing marriage, and his mounting dread in a suffocating monologue about American emptiness.

title: Good as Gold
author: Joseph Heller
description: A Jewish-American professor seeks a government position while writing a book about the Jewish experience, satirizing Washington politics and ethnic identity.

title: Closing Time
author: Joseph Heller
description: The sequel to Catch-22 follows Yossarian and his friends into old age in a surreal, apocalyptic America.

title: God Knows
author: Joseph Heller
description: King David on his deathbed retells his life story in modern American idiom, complaining about God, Bathsheba, and his ungrateful children.

title: The Executioner's Song
author: Norman Mailer
description: The life and execution of Gary Gilmore, the first person put to death in America after the reinstatement of capital punishment, told in a thousand pages of reported detail.

title: An American Dream
author: Norman Mailer
description: A war hero, professor, and TV personality murders his wife and spends a harrowing night navigating New York's criminal underworld.

title: The Armies of the Night
author: Norman Mailer
description: Mailer's account of the 1967 March on the Pentagon, mixing autobiography, journalism, and political analysis in what he calls a novel-as-history.

title: Why Are We in Vietnam?
author: Norman Mailer
description: A Texas teenager narrates a bear-hunting trip in Alaska that becomes an allegory for American violence and the madness driving the Vietnam War.

title: Ancient Evenings
author: Norman Mailer
description: Three reincarnations in ancient Egypt spanning the reigns of Ramses II and III, dense with sex, magic, and Mailer's attempt at an Egyptian national epic.

title: Harlot's Ghost
author: Norman Mailer
description: A massive, unfinished novel about the CIA from the 1950s through the Kennedy assassination, told by a WASP agent recruited at Yale.

title: The Sicilian
author: Mario Puzo
description: Michael Corleone is sent to Sicily to bring back the legendary bandit Salvatore Giuliano, in a novel set between Godfathers I and II.

title: The Last Don
author: Mario Puzo
description: A Mafia don who wants his family to go legitimate faces a final war as Hollywood, Las Vegas, and Wall Street collide.

title: Fools Die
author: Mario Puzo
description: A writer navigates the intersecting worlds of Las Vegas gambling, Hollywood, New York publishing, and the military-industrial complex.

title: Omertà
author: Mario Puzo
description: Puzo's final novel, published posthumously, about an aging don who grooms a young protégé to take down a Sicilian Mafia boss threatening the American families.

title: The Temple of My Familiar
author: Alice Walker
description: An epic spanning half a million years of human history through reincarnation, connecting Africa, Europe, and America in a novel about memory, race, and love.

title: Possessing the Secret of Joy
author: Alice Walker
description: A woman from an African tribe undergoes female genital mutilation and spends her life struggling with the trauma, from Africa to America to a prison cell.

title: Meridian
author: Alice Walker
description: A young Black woman in the civil rights movement sacrifices everything—motherhood, marriage, health—for the cause, becoming a wandering saint of the struggle.

title: In Search of Our Mothers' Gardens
author: Alice Walker
description: Essays on Black women's creativity, spirituality, and survival, including the essay that coined the term 'womanist.'

title: Gather Together in My Name
author: Maya Angelou
description: The second memoir follows Angelou through her late teens as a single mother, drifting through jobs, relationships, and the fringes of the criminal world.

title: Singin' and Swingin' and Gettin' Merry Like Christmas
author: Maya Angelou
description: Angelou's third memoir covers her marriage, her career as a performer in Porgy and Bess, and her growing guilt over leaving her son behind.

title: The Heart of a Woman
author: Maya Angelou
description: Angelou moves to New York, joins the civil rights movement, and follows her son's father to Africa in the fourth memoir.

title: Baudolino
author: Umberto Eco
description: A peasant boy adopted by Frederick Barbarossa becomes the greatest liar of the Middle Ages, fabricating relics and an entire lost kingdom.

title: The Island of the Day Before
author: Umberto Eco
description: A 17th-century nobleman is shipwrecked within sight of an island he cannot reach, and the novel spirals through memory, science, and unreliable narration.

title: The Prague Cemetery
author: Umberto Eco
description: A forger in 19th-century Europe fabricates the Protocols of the Elders of Zion, connecting every conspiracy of the era in Eco's most controversial novel.

title: Numero Zero
author: Umberto Eco
description: Journalists at a never-to-be-published newspaper in 1990s Milan stumble onto a conspiracy involving Mussolini's fake death and the secret history of postwar Italy.

title: How to Travel with a Salmon
author: Umberto Eco
description: Comic essays on the absurdities of modern life, from operating a coffee machine to negotiating with airline staff.

title: The Toilers of the Sea
author: Victor Hugo
description: A fisherman battles a giant octopus and the sea itself to salvage a wrecked steamship engine and win the woman he loves.

title: Ninety-Three
author: Victor Hugo
description: Three men in the French Revolution—a royalist commander, a republican general, and a revolutionary priest—are bound by blood and torn apart by duty.

title: The Man Who Laughs
author: Victor Hugo
description: A boy whose face was carved into a permanent grin by criminals becomes a carnival attraction, then discovers he is a lord—but the world sees only the smile.

title: A Tramp Abroad
author: Mark Twain
description: Twain's comic account of walking through Europe, encountering opera, dueling students, and the Alps while barely walking at all.

title: The Prince and the Pauper
author: Mark Twain
description: A prince and a pauper who look identical switch places in Tudor England, each discovering the other's world.

title: Pudd'nhead Wilson
author: Mark Twain
description: A slave switches her light-skinned baby with her master's, and the children grow up in each other's places, exposing the absurdity of racial categories.

title: Life on the Mississippi
author: Mark Twain
description: Twain's memoir of his years as a steamboat pilot on the Mississippi River and his return to the river decades later.

title: Roughing It
author: Mark Twain
description: Twain's semi-autobiographical account of his years in the Wild West—silver mining, frontier journalism, and encounters with outlaws and Mormons.

title: The Beautiful and Damned
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A handsome young heir and his beautiful wife drink and party through the 1920s waiting for an inheritance, and the waiting destroys them.

title: The Hotel New Hampshire
author: John Irving
description: An eccentric family operates a series of hotels while enduring rape, terrorism, and the father's blind optimism in Irving's most picaresque novel.

title: A Widow for One Year
author: John Irving
description: A writer's life from childhood through middle age, shaped by her parents' disastrous marriage and the deaths that haunt their family.

title: Last Night in Twisted River
author: John Irving
description: A cook and his son flee a logging town after an accidental killing and spend decades on the run while the son becomes a novelist.

title: Setting Free the Bears
author: John Irving
description: Two young men in 1960s Vienna plot to free all the animals in the zoo, intercut with a historical narrative about Austria under the Nazis.

title: Avenue of Mysteries
author: John Irving
description: An aging novelist returns to the Mexico of his childhood, where he and his crippled sister grew up among Jesuits and dump kids.

title: In One Person
author: John Irving
description: A bisexual writer recounts his sexual awakening in a Vermont town and the losses of the AIDS epidemic that followed.

title: Play It as It Lays
author: Joan Didion
description: A model and actress drives the Los Angeles freeways in a nihilistic daze as her marriage, career, and sanity disintegrate.

title: A Book of Common Prayer
author: Joan Didion
description: An American woman in a Central American country refuses to accept her daughter's involvement in terrorism, and the narrator watches her delusion with clinical detachment.

title: Democracy
author: Joan Didion
description: A senator's wife and a CIA operative carry on an affair across decades of American politics, from Vietnam to the fall of Saigon.

title: Blue Nights
author: Joan Didion
description: Didion's meditation on aging and the death of her daughter Quintana Roo, two years after losing her husband.

title: Where I Was From
author: Joan Didion
description: Didion dismantles the myths of California—pioneer self-reliance, golden opportunity—and her own family's place in them.

title: Salvador
author: Joan Didion
description: Didion's report from El Salvador during the civil war: death squads, American complicity, and the terror of a country coming apart.

title: Miami
author: Joan Didion
description: The Cuban exile community in Miami and its entanglement with American Cold War politics, CIA operations, and the mythology of return.

title: Mao II
author: Don DeLillo
description: A reclusive novelist is drawn out of hiding by a hostage crisis, and the novel asks whether the terrorist has replaced the writer as the shaper of consciousness.

title: Cosmopolis
author: Don DeLillo
description: A billionaire's stretch-limo ride across Manhattan to get a haircut becomes an odyssey through financial markets, protests, and assassination.

title: Falling Man
author: Don DeLillo
description: A survivor of the World Trade Center attacks walks out of the dust and tries to resume his life while his marriage and his mind come apart.

title: Zero K
author: Don DeLillo
description: A man visits a remote compound where the dying are cryogenically preserved, and his billionaire father plans to join his dying wife in the pods.

title: The Names
author: Don DeLillo
description: An American risk analyst in Athens investigates a cult that murders people whose initials match the initials of the place where they die.

title: Americana
author: Don DeLillo
description: A network executive drives across America making a film about himself and the country, in DeLillo's debut road novel about media and identity.

title: Ratner's Star
author: Don DeLillo
description: A teenage math prodigy is recruited to decode a message from outer space, and the scientific establishment unravels into absurdity around him.

title: Great Jones Street
author: Don DeLillo
description: A rock star at the height of his fame retreats to a Lower East Side apartment, pursued by fans, dealers, and a shadowy group that wants a stolen drug.

title: Players
author: Don DeLillo
description: A Wall Street worker drifts into a terrorist cell while his wife retreats into an affair and thoughts of suicide in DeLillo's coldest novel.

title: Running Dog
author: Don DeLillo
description: A journalist, a senator, and various intelligence operatives compete for an erotic film allegedly made in Hitler's bunker.

title: End Zone
author: Don DeLillo
description: A college football player at a West Texas school becomes obsessed with nuclear war strategy in DeLillo's second novel about language, systems, and apocalypse.

title: Amsterdam
author: Ian McEwan
description: Two old friends make a euthanasia pact and then betray each other when their careers are threatened, in a dark comedy about moral vanity.

title: Enduring Love
author: Ian McEwan
description: A balloon accident brings together two men—one of whom becomes pathologically convinced they are destined to be together.

title: The Cement Garden
author: Ian McEwan
description: Four orphaned children bury their mother in cement in the basement and try to carry on as a family before the outside world intervenes.

title: The Children Act
author: Ian McEwan
description: A family court judge must decide whether to force a blood transfusion on a Jehovah's Witness teenager, and her visit to the boy changes both their lives.

title: Machines Like Me
author: Ian McEwan
description: In an alternate 1980s London, a man buys one of the first synthetic humans and forms a love triangle with it and his upstairs neighbor.

title: Nutshell
author: Ian McEwan
description: A retelling of Hamlet narrated by a fetus in the womb who overhears his mother and uncle plotting his father's murder.

title: Black Dogs
author: Ian McEwan
description: A couple's marriage is defined by an encounter with two terrifying dogs in rural France just after WWII, splitting them into opposing worldviews for life.

title: Solar
author: Ian McEwan
description: A Nobel Prize-winning physicist, fat, selfish, and past his prime, steals a dead colleague's work on solar energy in McEwan's comic novel about climate change.

title: Lessons
author: Ian McEwan
description: A man's life from the 1940s to the pandemic, shaped by a childhood abuse that echoes through decades of political upheaval and personal failure.

title: Gone, Baby, Gone
author: Dennis Lehane
description: A child vanishes from a Boston neighborhood and two private detectives discover the truth is more morally complicated than anyone imagined.

title: A Drink Before the War
author: Dennis Lehane
description: Two Boston PIs are hired by politicians to find a cleaning woman who stole important documents, and the case exposes the city's racial fault lines.

title: The Given Day
author: Dennis Lehane
description: The 1919 Boston police strike told through a Black veteran and an Irish cop, spanning the molasses flood, the Red Scare, and Babe Ruth.

title: World Gone By
author: Dennis Lehane
description: A gangster in WWII-era Tampa lives a double life while someone has put a contract on his life, and he can't find out who.

title: Since We Fell
author: Dennis Lehane
description: A journalist's agoraphobia after a breakdown becomes the least of her problems when she discovers her husband has a secret life.

title: Small Mercies
author: Dennis Lehane
description: A woman in 1974 South Boston searches for her missing daughter during the school busing crisis while a body turns up on the train tracks.

title: A Column of Fire
author: Ken Follett
description: The third Kingsbridge novel spans the Elizabethan era as religious wars between Catholics and Protestants tear Europe apart.

title: The Evening and the Morning
author: Ken Follett
description: The prequel to Pillars of the Earth, set in the Dark Ages as a boatbuilder, a noblewoman, and a monk fight to build a new town.

title: Fall of Giants
author: Ken Follett
description: Five families across five countries navigate World War I, the Russian Revolution, and the women's suffrage movement.

title: Winter of the World
author: Ken Follett
description: The same five families face World War II, the Holocaust, and the dawn of the nuclear age.

title: Edge of Eternity
author: Ken Follett
description: The Cold War, civil rights, Vietnam, and the fall of the Berlin Wall through the third generation of the five families.

title: The Lords of the North
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Uhtred journeys north to Northumbria to reclaim his ancestral fortress of Bebbanburg from his uncle.

title: Sword Song
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Uhtred must retake London from the Danes for Alfred, even as he distrusts Alfred's piety and political ambitions.

title: The Burning Land
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Alfred is dying and Uhtred must decide where his loyalty lies as a new Danish invasion threatens to undo everything.

title: Death of Kings
author: Bernard Cornwell
