description: Alfred dies and Uhtred must protect his son Edward's claim to the throne while Saxon England fragments.

title: The Pagan Lord
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Stripped of his lands, Uhtred launches a reckless raid into Danish territory that changes the balance of power in England.

title: The Empty Throne
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Uhtred lies near death while rival claimants fight over Mercia and the dream of a united England.

title: Warriors of the Storm
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: A massive Norse fleet invades as Uhtred defends the borders and navigates the political demands of multiple Saxon rulers.

title: The Flame Bearer
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Uhtred finally makes his move on Bebbanburg, the fortress stolen from him as a boy.

title: War of the Wolf
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: A Norse warlord with an army of outcasts threatens the fragile peace while Uhtred's enemies multiply at court.

title: Sword of Kings
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Uhtred is dragged into the succession crisis after the death of King Edward, as multiple claimants battle for England's throne.

title: War Lord
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: The final Saxon story: Uhtred, old but unbroken, fights the decisive battle that will determine whether England becomes one nation.

title: Post Captain
author: Patrick O'Brian
description: Aubrey on half-pay courts two women and dodges debtors while Maturin becomes a secret agent for British naval intelligence.

title: HMS Surprise
author: Patrick O'Brian
description: Aubrey sails to India on a diplomatic mission while Maturin rescues a colleague from a French prison and pursues the woman he loves.

title: The Mauritius Command
author: Patrick O'Brian
description: Aubrey is given a squadron to capture the island of Mauritius from the French, his first independent command.

title: Desolation Island
author: Patrick O'Brian
description: Aubrey transports convicts to Australia, including a woman spy, while Maturin interrogates her and a Dutch warship gives chase.

title: The Fortune of War
author: Patrick O'Brian
description: Aubrey is shipwrecked and imprisoned by the Americans during the War of 1812 while Maturin is hunted by French intelligence.

title: The Surgeon's Mate
author: Patrick O'Brian
description: Maturin marries Diana Villiers and is sent on a secret mission to the Baltic while Aubrey searches for a new command.

title: Porno
author: Irvine Welsh
description: The Trainspotting crew reunites ten years later as Sick Boy hatches a scheme to make a porn film and Renton returns from Amsterdam.

title: Filth
author: Irvine Welsh
description: A corrupt Edinburgh detective investigates a murder while a tapeworm living in his intestines narrates his psychological collapse.

title: Marabou Stork Nightmares
author: Irvine Welsh
description: A man in a coma drifts between an African fantasy adventure and the memories of sexual violence and football hooliganism that put him there.

title: Skagboys
author: Irvine Welsh
description: The prequel to Trainspotting showing how Renton, Sick Boy, Spud, and Begbie first fell into heroin in Thatcher-era Edinburgh.

title: The Blade Artist
author: Irvine Welsh
description: Begbie has reinvented himself as a successful artist in California, but a family crisis drags him back to Edinburgh and his violent past.

title: Dead Men's Trousers
author: Irvine Welsh
description: The final Trainspotting novel reunites the four friends as middle-aged men, each chasing one last score in a globalized world.

title: Heart of a Dog
author: Mikhail Bulgakov
description: A professor transplants human glands into a stray dog, which becomes a boorish Soviet citizen, satirizing the new communist man.

title: A Country Doctor's Notebook
author: Mikhail Bulgakov
description: A young doctor in rural Russia treats horrifying cases with inadequate supplies and becomes addicted to morphine.

title: Sometimes a Great Notion
author: Ken Kesey
description: A fiercely independent Oregon logging family defies a union strike, told in shifting perspectives that rival Faulkner in ambition.

title: Sailor Song
author: Ken Kesey
description: A near-future Alaskan fishing village is invaded by a Hollywood film crew in Kesey's ecological satire.

title: The Committed
author: Viet Thanh Nguyen
description: The unnamed spy from The Sympathizer arrives in 1980s Paris, dealing drugs among Vietnamese exiles while still interrogating questions of identity and ideology.

title: The Refugees
author: Viet Thanh Nguyen
description: Eight stories about the Vietnamese diaspora—ghostly visitations, generational divides, and the long afterlife of the war in ordinary lives.

title: Drown
author: Junot Díaz
description: Stories of Dominican-American life in New Jersey and the island—poverty, immigration, machismo, and the longing for belonging.

title: This Is How You Lose Her
author: Junot Díaz
description: Yunior's romantic disasters across decades, told in Díaz's Spanglish-laced voice, as infidelity destroys every woman who loves him.

title: Wandering Stars
author: Tommy Orange
description: The sequel to There There follows the survivors of the powwow shooting and traces their ancestors back to the Sand Creek Massacre.

title: Gathering Blue
author: Lois Lowry
description: In a post-apocalyptic village, an orphaned girl with a talent for embroidery is conscripted to maintain the community's history—but the history may be a lie.

title: Messenger
author: Lois Lowry
description: A boy with healing powers lives in a utopian village threatened by the surrounding Forest, which is becoming hostile.

title: Son
author: Lois Lowry
description: A young mother escapes the Community to find the son who was taken from her, connecting all four Giver novels.

title: Number the Stars
author: Lois Lowry
description: A ten-year-old Danish girl helps smuggle her Jewish best friend to safety during the Nazi occupation of Copenhagen.

title: Anil's Ghost
author: Michael Ondaatje
description: A forensic anthropologist returns to Sri Lanka to investigate mass graves during the civil war and is forced to confront the violence of her homeland.

title: In the Skin of a Lion
author: Michael Ondaatje
description: Immigrants who built Toronto's infrastructure in the 1920s and 1930s—tunnelers, bridge workers, tanners—in a lyrical novel that precedes The English Patient.

title: Coming Through Slaughter
author: Michael Ondaatje
description: A fictionalized account of Buddy Bolden, the New Orleans cornetist who invented jazz and went mad.

title: Warlight
author: Michael Ondaatje
description: In 1945 London, two teenagers are left in the care of a possibly criminal guardian when their parents disappear, and the truth takes decades to surface.

title: Tim
author: Colleen McCullough
description: A wealthy older woman befriends a handsome but intellectually disabled young laborer, and their unlikely bond scandalizes their community.

title: The First Man in Rome
author: Colleen McCullough
description: Gaius Marius and Lucius Cornelius Sulla rise through Roman politics in the first of seven novels spanning the fall of the Republic.

title: The Grass Crown
author: Colleen McCullough
description: Marius and Sulla's rivalry intensifies as Rome faces the Social War and the first march on the capital.

title: My Sister, Life
author: Boris Pasternak
description: Pasternak's breakthrough poetry collection, written during the Russian Revolution, celebrating love and nature with ecstatic intensity.

title: De Profundis
author: Oscar Wilde
description: Wilde's letter from prison to his lover Lord Alfred Douglas—a devastating account of betrayal, suffering, and spiritual transformation.

title: An Ideal Husband
author: Oscar Wilde
description: A politician's youthful secret threatens his career and marriage until his wife discovers that an ideal husband might be an impossible standard.

title: Lady Windermere's Fan
author: Oscar Wilde
description: A woman suspects her husband of having an affair and nearly runs off with another man, not knowing her mother-in-law has sacrificed everything to protect her.

title: The Joke
author: Milan Kundera
description: A student's sarcastic postcard about communism ruins his life, and fifteen years later he plots revenge on the friend who denounced him.

title: Immortality
author: Milan Kundera
description: A woman's casual wave inspires a novel that spirals into meditations on Goethe, Hemingway, image, and the desire to be remembered.

title: The Art of the Novel
author: Milan Kundera
description: Seven essays on the European novel's legacy, arguing that fiction's purpose is to explore the ambiguity of existence.

title: Ignorance
author: Milan Kundera
description: Two Czech émigrés return home after the fall of communism and discover they have nothing in common with the country or each other anymore.

title: Slowness
author: Milan Kundera
description: An 18th-century seduction and a modern academic conference at the same château become a meditation on speed, memory, and the loss of pleasure.

title: Strong Motion
author: Jonathan Franzen
description: Earthquakes in Boston are linked to a corporation's illegal waste dumping, and a young man and a seismologist try to prove it.

title: Purity
author: Jonathan Franzen
description: A young woman searching for her father connects a WikiLeaks-like activist, a journalist, and a murder committed in East Germany decades ago.

title: Crossroads
author: Jonathan Franzen
description: A pastor and his wife in 1970s suburban Chicago each hide devastating secrets while their children begin to self-destruct.

title: Big Sur
author: Jack Kerouac
description: Kerouac retreats to a cabin to dry out but fame, alcohol, and mental breakdown follow him to the California coast.

title: Desolation Angels
author: Jack Kerouac
description: Kerouac's months as a fire lookout in the Cascades followed by the frenzy of the Beat scene in San Francisco and Mexico City.

title: Visions of Cody
author: Jack Kerouac
description: An experimental portrait of Neal Cassady using tape transcripts, dreams, and stream-of-consciousness—Kerouac's most radical formal experiment.

title: The Subterraneans
author: Jack Kerouac
description: A doomed interracial love affair in the San Francisco jazz scene, written in a single three-day session.

title: The Intuitionist
author: Colson Whitehead
description: In a city obsessed with elevators, a Black female inspector who diagnoses faults by intuition becomes embroiled in a conspiracy over a perfect elevator design.

title: John Henry Days
author: Colson Whitehead
description: A freelance journalist at a John Henry festival spirals through American myth, celebrity, and the compulsion to consume.

title: Zone One
author: Colson Whitehead
description: A survivor of a zombie plague helps sweep lower Manhattan of remaining undead, reflecting on the old world in Whitehead's literary take on the genre.

title: Harlem Shuffle
author: Colson Whitehead
description: A furniture salesman in 1960s Harlem gets pulled into a heist at a luxury hotel and discovers the line between legit and crooked is where he lives.

title: Crook Manifesto
author: Colson Whitehead
description: Ray Carney returns in 1970s Harlem, navigating blaxploitation, corrupt cops, and arson-for-profit while trying to keep his family clean.

title: Of Love and Shadows
author: Isabel Allende
description: A journalist and a photographer uncover a mass grave under a military dictatorship and risk everything to tell the truth.

title: Daughter of Fortune
author: Isabel Allende
description: A Chilean woman follows her lover to California during the Gold Rush, reinventing herself across the Pacific.

title: A Long Petal of the Sea
author: Isabel Allende
description: Spanish Civil War refugees flee on Neruda's rescue ship to Chile, where decades of love and political upheaval await.

title: City of the Beasts
author: Isabel Allende
description: A teenager accompanies his grandmother on an expedition into the Amazon and encounters a lost civilization protecting the jungle.

title: The Electric Kool-Aid Acid Test
author: Tom Wolfe
description: Wolfe embeds with Ken Kesey and the Merry Pranksters as they drive across America on a painted bus, dosing everyone with LSD.

title: A Man in Full
author: Tom Wolfe
description: An Atlanta real estate baron facing ruin collides with a mayor navigating racial politics and a young factory worker discovering Stoic philosophy.

title: I Am Charlotte Simmons
author: Tom Wolfe
description: A brilliant small-town girl arrives at an elite university and is crushed by the drinking, sex, and status obsession of campus culture.

title: The Man in the Iron Mask
author: Alexandre Dumas
description: The aging musketeers unite for a final adventure involving a mysterious prisoner in an iron mask who may be the twin brother of King Louis XIV.

title: Twenty Years After
author: Alexandre Dumas
description: The musketeers reunite during the Fronde to rescue Charles I of England and navigate a France torn between Mazarin and the rebellious nobles.

title: The Vicomte of Bragelonne
author: Alexandre Dumas
description: The next generation enters the story as Louis XIV comes to power and the musketeers face their final intrigues.

title: The Last Picture Show
author: Larry McMurtry
description: Two teenagers come of age in a dying Texas town in the early 1950s as the local movie theater closes and the adults are as lost as the kids.

title: Streets of Laredo
author: Larry McMurtry
description: An aging Woodrow Call pursues a young Mexican bandit across the border in the sequel to Lonesome Dove.

title: Comanche Moon
author: Larry McMurtry
description: The prequel to Lonesome Dove follows young Call and McCrae as Texas Rangers fighting the Comanches in the 1850s.

title: Dead Man's Walk
author: Larry McMurtry
description: The earliest prequel: teenaged Call and McCrae join a disastrous Texas Ranger expedition to Santa Fe.

title: King Rat
author: James Clavell
description: An American corporal thrives as a black-market king in a Japanese POW camp while the other prisoners starve, based on Clavell's own captivity.

title: Noble House
author: James Clavell
description: A week of corporate warfare in 1963 Hong Kong as the tai-pan of a great trading house battles a rival while Cold War intelligence operations swirl around them.

title: Gai-Jin
author: James Clavell
description: The opening of Japan to the West in the 1860s, following the descendants of characters from Shōgun as samurai culture clashes with European gunboats.

title: By Night in Chile
author: Roberto Bolaño
description: A dying Chilean priest and literary critic confesses his complicity with the Pinochet regime in a single feverish monologue.

title: Distant Star
author: Roberto Bolaño
description: A Chilean poet who turns out to be a fascist death-squad pilot after the coup, writing his verses in airplane smoke over the concentration camps.

title: The Spirit of Science Fiction
author: Roberto Bolaño
description: Two young Chilean poets in 1970s Mexico City chase literary glory while one writes fan letters to sci-fi authors. Published posthumously.

title: Amulet
author: Roberto Bolaño
description: A Uruguayan woman trapped in a UNAM bathroom during the 1968 Mexican army invasion narrates the fate of a generation of Latin American poets.

title: Nazi Literature in the Americas
author: Roberto Bolaño
description: A fictional encyclopedia of imaginary far-right writers across the Western Hemisphere, each entry a compressed novel of literary evil.

title: Haroun and the Sea of Stories
author: Salman Rushdie
description: A boy journeys to the Sea of Stories to restore his storyteller father's gift, in an allegory about censorship written while Rushdie was in hiding.

title: The Moor's Last Sigh
author: Salman Rushdie
description: Four generations of a Cochin spice-trading family, narrated by a man who ages at double speed and is literally running out of time.

title: The Ground Beneath Her Feet
author: Salman Rushdie
description: An alternate history of rock and roll told through two Indian musicians whose love affair spans decades and parallel worlds.

title: Shalimar the Clown
author: Salman Rushdie
description: A Kashmiri clown turned terrorist assassinates the American ambassador who stole his wife, connecting the politics of Kashmir to Los Angeles.

title: Two Years Eight Months and Twenty-Eight Nights
author: Salman Rushdie
description: A jinnia's descendants discover their powers as a war between rational and irrational worlds breaks out in near-future New York.

title: The Beet Queen
author: Louise Erdrich
description: Two orphans arrive in a North Dakota town in the 1930s and their lives intertwine with the Ojibwe families from Love Medicine across four decades.

title: Tracks
author: Louise Erdrich
description: Two narrators—a trickster elder and a mixed-blood woman—tell competing stories of Ojibwe land theft in early 20th-century North Dakota.

title: The Plague of Doves
author: Louise Erdrich
description: A 1911 murder of a white family and the unjust lynching of Ojibwe men reverberates through decades in a small North Dakota town.

title: The Sentence
author: Louise Erdrich
description: A Minneapolis bookstore is haunted by the ghost of an annoying customer during the pandemic, and the bookseller must deal with it.

title: LaRose
author: Louise Erdrich
description: A man accidentally kills his neighbor's son while hunting and follows Ojibwe tradition by giving his own son to the grieving family.

title: Future Home of the Living God
author: Louise Erdrich
description: Evolution reverses and pregnant women are rounded up by the government as a Native woman tries to protect her unborn child.

title: A Song for Arbonne
author: Guy Gavriel Kay
description: A troubadour culture modeled on Provence faces annihilation by a puritanical northern kingdom in Kay's most romantic historical fantasy.

title: Sailing to Sarantium
author: Guy Gavriel Kay
description: A mosaicist is summoned to create a masterwork in a city inspired by Byzantium, navigating the politics of emperors and charioteers.

title: Lord of Emperors
author: Guy Gavriel Kay
description: The mosaicist completes his work as the empire convulses around him, and art outlasts the politics that commissioned it.

title: Children of Earth and Sky
author: Guy Gavriel Kay
description: Merchants, spies, soldiers, and artists cross paths in a Renaissance-inspired world poised between two empires and the memory of a fallen city.

title: All the Seas of the World
author: Guy Gavriel Kay
description: A corsair woman and a merchant from a fallen kingdom cross a Mediterranean-inspired world of intrigue, piracy, and holy war.

title: River of Stars
author: Guy Gavriel Kay
description: A poet and a woman warrior in a Song Dynasty-inspired empire navigate a world where the barbarians are winning and civilization is in retreat.

title: Vile Bodies
author: Evelyn Waugh
description: The Bright Young Things of 1920s London party their way through a whirlwind of gossip, debt, and meaninglessness as the world slides toward war.

title: Decline and Fall
author: Evelyn Waugh
description: An innocent theology student is expelled from Oxford and falls through a series of absurd misadventures involving a Welsh school, high society, and prison.

title: The Loved One
author: Evelyn Waugh
description: A British poet working in a Hollywood pet cemetery falls for a cosmetician at a ludicrously grandiose human funeral home.

title: Sword of Honour
author: Evelyn Waugh
description: A Catholic gentleman's disillusionment during WWII, from early idealism through the chaos of Crete to the moral collapse of the Allied cause.

title: The Ordeal of Gilbert Pinfold
author: Evelyn Waugh
description: A Catholic novelist on a sea cruise begins hearing voices that torment him—based on Waugh's own hallucinatory breakdown.

title: Ghostwritten
author: David Mitchell
description: Nine interlocking narratives span the globe from a cult member in Okinawa to an AI in New York, connected by chance and a supernatural presence.

title: Black Swan Green
author: David Mitchell
description: A stammering thirteen-year-old navigates one year in a rural English village in 1982, watching the Falklands War and his parents' marriage from the sidelines.

title: The Bone Clocks
author: David Mitchell
description: A woman's life from 1984 to 2043 intersects with an invisible war between two factions of immortals, connecting characters from Mitchell's other novels.

title: Slade House
author: David Mitchell
description: Every nine years, someone is lured into a house that exists outside of time, and the twin souls who live there feed on their visitors.

title: Utopia Avenue
author: David Mitchell
description: A late-1960s British rock band burns bright for two years, and each member's story connects to Mitchell's larger fictional universe.

title: Adam Bede
author: George Eliot
description: A carpenter's love for a vain dairymaid is tested when she is seduced by the local squire and commits a desperate act.

title: Daniel Deronda
author: George Eliot
description: A young Englishman discovers his Jewish heritage and becomes drawn to the Zionist cause, while a beautiful woman is trapped in a loveless marriage to a sadist.

title: Romola
author: George Eliot
description: A scholar's daughter in Renaissance Florence marries a charming Greek and watches him betray everyone during the rise and fall of Savonarola.

title: Felix Holt, the Radical
author: George Eliot
description: A young idealist refuses to use his inheritance and works as an artisan among the poor during the turbulent Reform era elections.

title: Washington Square
author: Henry James
description: A plain, wealthy young woman is courted by a charming fortune hunter, and her tyrannical father forbids the match, leaving her trapped between them.

title: The Ambassadors
author: Henry James
description: An American is sent to Paris to retrieve a young man from a suspected femme fatale, and instead discovers Europe, beauty, and the life he never lived.

title: The Golden Bowl
author: Henry James
description: An American heiress and her father unknowingly marry a pair of former lovers, and the discovery of a cracked golden bowl reveals the betrayal.

title: The Bostonians
author: Henry James
description: A Southern conservative and a Boston feminist fight for the soul of a young woman with a gift for public speaking.

title: The Aspern Papers
author: Henry James
description: A literary scholar schemes to get his hands on a dead poet's letters from the poet's ancient former mistress, who guards them in a crumbling Venetian palazzo.

title: What Maisie Knew
author: Henry James
description: A little girl navigates her divorced parents' affairs and remarriages, understanding more than the adults realize.

title: Daisy Miller
author: Henry James
description: A young American woman in Rome scandalizes the expatriate community with her freedom and informality, and pays the price for their disapproval.

title: The Mayor of Casterbridge
author: Thomas Hardy
description: A man who sold his wife at a fair in a drunken rage rises to become mayor, only to be destroyed when his past returns.

title: Jude the Obscure
author: Thomas Hardy
description: A stonemason dreams of attending university and loves a free-spirited cousin, but Victorian society crushes both ambitions with unbearable cruelty.

title: The Woodlanders
author: Thomas Hardy
description: A timber merchant's daughter is educated above her station and caught between a faithful woodsman and a feckless doctor in Hardy's Wessex forests.

title: Under the Greenwood Tree
author: Thomas Hardy
description: A rural choir is displaced by a new church organ while the choirmaster's son courts the pretty new schoolteacher, in Hardy's lightest and sunniest novel.

title: Swing Time
author: Zadie Smith
description: Two mixed-race girls in London bond over dance, but only one has talent, and their friendship follows diverging paths through fame, philanthropy, and betrayal.

title: The Fraud
author: Zadie Smith
description: A Scottish housekeeper to a bombastic Victorian novelist becomes obsessed with a trial involving a man who claims to be a lost aristocrat.

title: Grand Union
author: Zadie Smith
description: Nineteen stories ranging from realism to surrealism, covering race, aging, social media, and the comedy of modern life.

title: Typee
author: Herman Melville
description: A sailor jumps ship in the Marquesas Islands and lives among a tribe of cannibals who treat him as an honored guest while he wonders if they plan to eat him.

title: The Confidence-Man
author: Herman Melville
description: A shape-shifting con artist works a Mississippi riverboat on April Fools' Day, testing every passenger's capacity for trust.

title: Prodigal Summer
author: Barbara Kingsolver
description: Three interwoven stories in an Appalachian summer explore predator-prey relationships, both ecological and human.

title: The Bean Trees
author: Barbara Kingsolver
description: A Kentucky woman drives west and ends up in Tucson with an abandoned Cherokee toddler, building an unlikely family among immigrants and misfits.

title: Flight Behavior
author: Barbara Kingsolver
description: A restless farm wife discovers millions of monarch butterflies on her family's land—displaced by climate change—and is drawn into the world of science.

title: Animal Dreams
author: Barbara Kingsolver
description: A woman returns to her Arizona hometown to care for her father and rediscovers the community, the landscape, and the Native American lover she left behind.

title: Elizabeth Costello
author: J.M. Coetzee
description: An aging Australian novelist gives lectures on animal rights and the nature of evil, and each becomes a test of whether literature can bear the weight of moral argument.

title: Foe
author: J.M. Coetzee
description: A woman castaway on Robinson Crusoe's island tells her story to Daniel Foe, but he rewrites it—a novel about who controls narrative.

title: The Childhood of Jesus
author: J.M. Coetzee
description: A man and a boy arrive in a new country with no memories, assigned new names and lives, in an allegory that resists easy interpretation.

title: Age of Iron
author: J.M. Coetzee
description: A dying classics professor in apartheid Cape Town writes a letter to her daughter abroad, witnessing violence she can no longer pretend not to see.

title: CivilWarLand in Bad Decline
author: George Saunders
description: Six stories and a novella set in crumbling American theme parks and dystopian workplaces, announcing Saunders's satirical voice.

title: Pastoralia
author: George Saunders
description: Six stories of people trapped in absurd jobs—caveman actors, pharmaceutical test subjects—trying to hold onto their humanity under corporate pressure.

title: Liberation Day
author: George Saunders
description: Nine stories about power, captivity, and the stories we tell ourselves, including humans used as living art installations.

title: An Anthropologist on Mars
author: Oliver Sacks
description: Seven neurological case studies including a colorblind painter, a surgeon with Tourette's, and Temple Grandin—each person adapting creatively to their condition.

title: Musicophilia
author: Oliver Sacks
description: How music affects the brain—from earworms to musical hallucinations to the therapeutic power of rhythm for Parkinson's patients.

title: On the Move
author: Oliver Sacks
description: Sacks's autobiography: motorcycles, bodybuilding, drug experiments, and a lifelong closeted homosexuality alongside his celebrated neurology career.

title: Greek Lessons
author: Han Kang
description: A woman who has lost the ability to speak and a man who is going blind find each other in an ancient Greek class in Seoul.

title: The White Book
author: Han Kang
description: A meditation on the color white, grief, and a sister who died two hours after birth, blending poetry, fiction, and autobiography.

title: We Do Not Part
author: Han Kang
description: A woman travels to Jeju Island during a blizzard to care for a friend's bird, uncovering the island's history of massacre and survival.

title: Cloud Cuckoo Land
author: Anthony Doerr
description: An ancient Greek text connects five characters across three timelines—a besieged Constantinople, present-day Idaho, and a spaceship fleeing Earth.

title: The Shell Collector
author: Anthony Doerr
description: Eight stories set across the world, from Africa to Maine, connected by the natural world and the fragile human presence within it.

title: Free Food for Millionaires
author: Min Jin Lee
description: A Korean-American Princeton graduate navigates class, identity, and ambition in 1990s New York while straddling two cultures.

title: The Bone Season
author: Samantha Shannon
description: In a future London where clairvoyance is illegal, a young psychic is captured and taken to a penal colony run by an otherworldly race.

title: A Day of Fallen Night
author: Samantha Shannon
description: The prequel to Priory, spanning the first rising of the Nameless One centuries earlier.

title: Bewilderment
author: Richard Powers
description: An astrobiologist raises his neurodivergent son alone and turns to experimental neurofeedback to help him, using his dead wife's brain patterns.

title: The Echo Maker
author: Richard Powers
description: A man survives a truck accident on the Nebraska plains and wakes believing his sister is an impostor, in a novel about identity and the brain's construction of reality.

title: Gain
author: Richard Powers
description: A woman with ovarian cancer lives next to the chemical company that may have caused it, and the novel alternates her story with the corporation's 170-year history.

title: Galatea 2.2
author: Richard Powers
description: A novelist trains a neural network to pass a master's exam in English literature, and the AI begins to develop something like consciousness.

title: Oh William!
author: Elizabeth Strout
description: Lucy Barton's ex-husband's life unravels and she accompanies him on a road trip to Maine, rediscovering the old pull between them.

title: Olive, Again
author: Elizabeth Strout
description: Olive Kitteridge returns in thirteen new stories, older and somewhat softer, still sharp-tongued, navigating love and loss in coastal Maine.

title: The Burgess Boys
author: Elizabeth Strout
description: Two brothers and their sister are drawn back to their Maine hometown when the sister's son commits a hate crime against Somali immigrants.

title: Burmese Days
author: George Orwell
description: A timber merchant in colonial Burma is torn between his friendship with an Indian doctor and the racist European club he belongs to.

title: Keep the Aspidistra Flying
author: George Orwell
description: A poet quits his advertising job to live in poverty on principle, but discovers that rejecting money doesn't free you from its power.

title: Coming Up for Air
author: George Orwell
description: A middle-aged insurance salesman on the eve of WWII escapes to the village of his childhood and finds it destroyed by progress.

title: The Road to Wigan Pier
author: George Orwell
description: Orwell investigates poverty in northern England's coal-mining communities and then turns on the middle-class socialists who claim to represent the workers.

title: Tortilla Flat
author: John Steinbeck
description: A group of paisanos in post-WWI Monterey drink, philosophize, and get into Arthurian-tinged adventures with stolen vacuums and pirated goods.

title: The Winter of Our Discontent
author: John Steinbeck
description: An honest man in a corrupt Long Island town is tempted to sell out, and Steinbeck examines what happens to American morality when integrity doesn't pay.

title: The Pearl
author: John Steinbeck
description: A poor pearl diver finds the Pearl of the World and it brings only greed, violence, and tragedy to his family.

title: Travels with Charley
author: John Steinbeck
description: Steinbeck drives across America with his poodle Charley, searching for the country he wrote about and finding it changed beyond recognition.

title: A Room of One's Own
author: Virginia Woolf
description: Woolf argues that a woman needs money and a room of her own to write fiction, examining the systemic barriers that have silenced women's voices.

title: Between the Acts
author: Virginia Woolf
description: A village pageant on the eve of WWII becomes a meditation on English history, art, and the coming destruction, in Woolf's final novel.

title: Sanctuary
author: William Faulkner
description: A college girl is kidnapped by a bootlegger and installed in a Memphis brothel, in Faulkner's most sensational and commercially successful novel.

title: Go Down, Moses
author: William Faulkner
description: Seven stories tracing the McCaslin family across generations, including 'The Bear,' about a boy's initiation into manhood through hunting a legendary bear.

title: Intruder in the Dust
author: William Faulkner
description: A Black man accused of murder in Mississippi refuses to defend himself, and a white teenager who owes him a debt works to prove his innocence.

title: If Beale Street Could Talk
author: James Baldwin
description: A pregnant woman fights to free her fiancé from prison after he is falsely accused of rape, and her family rallies around them in a love story set against injustice.

title: Just Above My Head
author: James Baldwin
description: A man narrates his dead brother's life—a gospel singer who became a civil rights activist—exploring Black music, sexuality, and family across decades.

title: No Name in the Street
author: James Baldwin
description: Baldwin's account of the civil rights era through personal witness—the murders of Medgar Evers, Malcolm X, and Martin Luther King Jr.

title: Notes of a Native Son
author: James Baldwin
description: Ten essays on race in America and Europe, including the title essay about his father's death and the Harlem riot of 1943.

title: Nora Webster
author: Colm Tóibín
description: A widow in 1960s Ireland slowly rebuilds her life through small acts of independence—taking a job, buying a record player, learning to sing.

title: The Magician
author: Colm Tóibín
description: A novel of Thomas Mann's life—his hidden desires, his exile from Nazi Germany, and the family that both sustained and suffered from his art.

title: Long Island
author: Colm Tóibín
description: Eilis from Brooklyn is now middle-aged on Long Island when a stranger arrives with news that threatens to detonate her carefully constructed American life.

title: Leviathan
author: Paul Auster
description: A novelist pieces together the life of his friend who died making a bomb, tracing the idealism that led to domestic terrorism.

title: The Book of Illusions
author: Paul Auster
description: A grieving professor becomes obsessed with a silent-film comedian who vanished in 1929, and his research leads to a meeting that changes everything.

author: Paul Auster
description: Four parallel versions of the same boy's life from the 1950s onward, each shaped by different accidents, showing how contingency makes us who we are.

title: Wonder Boys
author: Michael Chabon
description: A pot-smoking professor whose second novel is 2,000 pages and counting has a disastrous weekend involving a dead dog, a stolen jacket, and a brilliant student.

title: The Mysteries of Pittsburgh
author: Michael Chabon
description: A mobster's son spends a post-graduation summer exploring love with both a man and a woman while his father's world closes in.

title: Moonglow
author: Michael Chabon
description: A dying grandfather tells his grandson stories of WWII, rocket science, a troubled wife, and a life shaped by secrets—blurring fiction and memoir.

title: The Rules of Attraction
author: Bret Easton Ellis
description: Three students at an elite New England college narrate a semester of parties, drugs, and overlapping romantic obsessions in Ellis's second novel.

title: Glamorama
author: Bret Easton Ellis
description: A male model is recruited into an international terrorist ring of beautiful people, and the line between fashion and violence dissolves.

title: Lunar Park
author: Bret Easton Ellis
description: A fictionalized Ellis moves to the suburbs with his family and is haunted by characters from his own novels—especially Patrick Bateman.

title: Lullaby
author: Chuck Palahniuk
description: A journalist discovers a children's song that kills anyone it's read to, and he's not the only one who knows it.

title: Haunted
author: Chuck Palahniuk
description: Aspiring writers at a retreat sabotage themselves to have the best suffering story, each chapter preceded by a poem and a story from one attendee.

title: Rant
author: Chuck Palahniuk
description: An oral biography of a small-town misfit who moves to the city and becomes the leader of a demolition derby cult that may be a time-travel conspiracy.

title: Seveneves
author: Neal Stephenson
description: The moon explodes and humanity has two years before the resulting meteorite bombardment makes Earth uninhabitable, then jumps forward five thousand years.

title: The Baroque Cycle: Quicksilver
author: Neal Stephenson
description: Natural philosophers, Puritans, and pirates collide in the late 17th century as Newton and Leibniz invent calculus and modern finance is born.

title: REAMDE
author: Neal Stephenson
description: A ransomware virus in an online game triggers a chain of events connecting Chinese hackers, Russian mobsters, and jihadists in a globe-spanning thriller.

title: A Pale View of Hills
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
description: A Japanese woman in England recalls her friendship with a woman in postwar Nagasaki, and the memories may not be what they seem.

title: The Unconsoled
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
description: A famous pianist arrives in a European city to give a concert and is drawn into the lives of everyone he meets, unable to fulfill any of their or his own expectations.

title: When We Were Orphans
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
description: A celebrated detective in 1930s London returns to Shanghai to solve the mystery of his parents' disappearance when he was a child.

title: The Buried Giant
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
description: An elderly couple in post-Arthurian Britain sets out to find their son, traveling through a land where a mist of collective amnesia has erased all memory.

title: Pnin
author: Vladimir Nabokov
description: A bumbling Russian émigré professor at an American college loses his office, his dentures, and his dignity in a comic novel with a devastating undertow.

title: The Defense
author: Vladimir Nabokov
description: A chess grandmaster's obsession with the game consumes his mind until he can no longer distinguish the board from reality.

title: Invitation to a Beheading
author: Vladimir Nabokov
description: A man sentenced to death for 'gnostical turpitude' in a totalitarian state waits in a surreal prison where nothing is solid.

title: The Real Life of Sebastian Knight
author: Vladimir Nabokov
description: A man researches the life of his dead half-brother, a famous novelist, and the biography becomes a mirror of the biographer's own uncertainties.

title: Surfacing
author: Margaret Atwood
description: A woman returns to the Quebec wilderness to search for her missing father and undergoes a psychological breakdown that strips away civilization.

title: The Robber Bride
author: Margaret Atwood
description: Three women are reunited at the funeral of a mutual friend who destroyed each of their lives—except she may not actually be dead.

title: The Year of the Flood
author: Margaret Atwood
description: Two women survive the plague that destroyed civilization, one from inside the biotech compound, the other among eco-religious survivalists.

title: MaddAddam
author: Margaret Atwood
description: The survivors rebuild alongside the post-human Crakers while a final threat emerges from the wreckage of the old world.

title: The City We Became
author: N.K. Jemisin
description: New York City comes alive as a person—actually six people, one per borough—and must fight an interdimensional Lovecraftian threat that is gentrification made cosmic.

title: The World We Make
author: N.K. Jemisin
description: The living boroughs of New York face the Enemy's second assault as the city fights to survive.

title: Tehanu
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: Tenar takes in a burned and abused child while the former Archmage Ged returns from his quest stripped of power, and both must find new purpose.

title: Tales from Earthsea
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: Five stories filling in Earthsea's history, from the founding of the school on Roke to the origins of the first Archmage.

title: The Other Wind
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: A sorcerer's dreams of the dead threaten the wall between life and death, and Ged, Tenar, and their allies must seal the breach.

title: The Guns of Avalon
author: Roger Zelazny
description: Corwin acquires gunpowder that works in Amber and marches against his brother Eric with an army from Shadow.

title: Sign of the Unicorn
author: Roger Zelazny
description: Corwin investigates who is behind the attacks on the royal family while a mysterious Black Road spreads through Shadow.

title: The Hand of Oberon
author: Roger Zelazny
description: Corwin discovers the true Pattern has been damaged and must find the traitor among his siblings before Amber is destroyed.

title: The Courts of Chaos
author: Roger Zelazny
description: Corwin rides to the Courts of Chaos for the final battle as Oberon attempts to repair the Pattern at the cost of his own life.

title: Trumps of Doom
author: Roger Zelazny
description: Merlin, Corwin's son, narrates the second Amber series as he flees an April 30th assassination curse and discovers his own connection to Chaos.

title: Creatures of Light and Darkness
author: Roger Zelazny
description: Gods from Egyptian mythology battle across a far-future cosmos in Zelazny's most experimental novel.

title: This Immortal
author: Roger Zelazny
description: A man who may be immortal guides an alien tourist through a post-nuclear Earth in Zelazny's Hugo-winning debut novel.

title: Isle of the Dead
author: Roger Zelazny
description: A billionaire worldshaper with the powers of an alien god confronts an enemy from his past on a planet he designed.

title: Cugel's Saga
author: Jack Vance
description: Cugel the Clever continues his picaresque journey home across the dying Earth, conning and being conned in equal measure.

title: Rhialto the Marvellous
author: Jack Vance
description: Feuding magicians in the final age of Earth scheme against each other with spells, artifacts, and elaborate betrayals.

title: Lyonesse: Suldrun's Garden
author: Jack Vance
description: A princess imprisoned in a garden by her father falls in love with a castaway, and their child becomes the key to a war among the Elder Isles.

title: Lyonesse: The Green Pearl
author: Jack Vance
description: The young prince Aillas fights to reclaim his throne while the sorcerer Tamurello and the fairy Twisk play their own games.

title: Lyonesse: Madouc
author: Jack Vance
description: A changeling princess seeks her true identity as the final war for the Elder Isles approaches.

title: The Sailor on the Seas of Fate
author: Michael Moorcock
description: Elric sails through dimensions encountering other incarnations of the Eternal Champion as the cosmic balance between Law and Chaos shifts.

title: The Weird of the White Wolf
author: Michael Moorcock
description: Elric returns to the Young Kingdoms and falls in love while Stormbringer hungers for more souls.

title: The Vanishing Tower
author: Michael Moorcock
description: Elric pursues his enemy Theleb K'aarna across dimensions while the struggle between Chaos and Law intensifies.

title: The Bane of the Black Sword
author: Michael Moorcock
description: Elric tries to find peace but Stormbringer demands blood, and the doom of the Young Kingdoms draws closer.

title: Stormbringer
author: Michael Moorcock
description: The final battle between Chaos and Law as Elric leads the last armies of the Young Kingdoms and Stormbringer reveals its ultimate purpose.

title: The Jewel in the Skull
author: Michael Moorcock
description: Dorian Hawkmoon, a duke with a black jewel implanted in his skull, fights the Granbretan Empire in Moorcock's science-fantasy Hawkmoon series.

title: Gloriana
author: Michael Moorcock
description: An alternate Elizabethan England where a queen presides over a utopian court hiding corruption and cruelty beneath its gilded surface.

title: Behold the Man
author: Michael Moorcock
description: A time traveler goes back to meet Jesus Christ and discovers the historical Jesus is severely disabled, forcing the traveler into an impossible choice.

title: The Last Wish
author: Andrzej Sapkowski
description: Short stories introducing Geralt of Rivia, a monster hunter in a morally grey world inspired by Slavic mythology and subverted fairy tales.

title: Sword of Destiny
author: Andrzej Sapkowski
description: More stories of Geralt as he encounters the child of destiny, Ciri, for the first time and falls deeper in love with the sorceress Yennefer.

title: Blood of Elves
author: Andrzej Sapkowski
description: Geralt trains Ciri at Kaer Morhen while political factions hunt for the girl whose Elder Blood could reshape the world.

title: Time of Contempt
author: Andrzej Sapkowski
description: A coup among the sorcerers separates Geralt from Ciri, who is stranded in a desert and must survive alone.

title: Baptism of Fire
author: Andrzej Sapkowski
description: Geralt assembles a company of misfits to find Ciri while war engulfs the Northern Kingdoms.

title: The Tower of the Swallow
author: Andrzej Sapkowski
description: Ciri flees through different worlds while Geralt's company closes in and the Wild Hunt pursues her across dimensions.

title: The Lady of the Lake
author: Andrzej Sapkowski
description: The saga concludes as all factions converge on Ciri, and Geralt and Yennefer face their destiny at Rivia.

title: Season of Storms
author: Andrzej Sapkowski
description: A standalone adventure set during the short stories where Geralt loses both his swords and must survive political intrigue without them.

title: Magician: Apprentice
author: Raymond E. Feist
description: An orphan boy discovers he has magical talent as an alien invasion from another world threatens his medieval kingdom.

title: Magician: Master
author: Raymond E. Feist
description: Pug masters the alien magic of the Tsurani and becomes the most powerful magician in two worlds as the Riftwar reaches its climax.

title: Silverthorn
author: Raymond E. Feist
description: A quest to find the antidote for a magical poison leads through the kingdom of the dead elves and the Assassins' Guild.

title: A Darkness at Sethanon
author: Raymond E. Feist
description: An ancient evil raises an army to assault the city of Sethanon, where a rift to the enemy's dimension threatens to tear the world apart.

title: The Sword of Shannara
author: Terry Brooks
description: A half-elf must wield the legendary Sword of Shannara to defeat the Warlock Lord in a post-apocalyptic world where magic has returned.

title: The Elfstones of Shannara
author: Terry Brooks
description: A young man and an elven princess must protect the Ellcrys tree that keeps demons imprisoned, carrying the last Elfstones through enemy territory.

title: The Wishsong of Shannara
author: Terry Brooks
description: A girl born with the wishsong—magic through voice alone—is the only one who can destroy the Ildatch, a book of dark magic corrupting the land.

title: The Druid of Shannara
author: Terry Brooks
description: Walker Boh must restore the lost Druid keep of Paranor and become the new Druid, though he has spent his life rejecting magic.

title: The Dragonbone Chair
author: Tad Williams
description: A kitchen boy flees a castle when a mad king takes the throne and joins a quest for three legendary swords that can save the world.

title: Stone of Farewell
author: Tad Williams
description: Simon and his companions are scattered across the land as the Storm King's power grows and the search for the swords continues.

title: To Green Angel Tower
author: Tad Williams
description: The final battle for Osten Ard as the three swords are gathered and the Storm King attempts to return from death to reclaim his throne.

title: The Witchwood Crown
author: Tad Williams
description: Thirty years later, Simon and Miriamele rule a peaceful Osten Ard, but the Norns are stirring again and their grandson has vanished.

series.name: The Last King of Osten Ard

title: Pawn of Prophecy
author: David Eddings
description: A farm boy raised by his aunt discovers he is part of an ancient prophecy and sets out on a quest to recover a stolen orb from an evil god.

title: Queen of Sorcery
author: David Eddings
description: Garion's quest takes him through hostile kingdoms as he learns more about his heritage and the power awakening inside him.

title: Magician's Gambit
author: David Eddings
description: The company enters the lair of the mad god Torak's priests to recover the Orb of Aldur.

title: Castle of Wizardry
author: David Eddings
description: The Orb is recovered but Garion learns his true destiny: he must face the dark god Torak in single combat.

title: Enchanters' End Game
author: David Eddings
description: The final confrontation between Garion and Torak as armies clash and the prophecy reaches its climax.

title: Legend
author: David Gemmell
description: An aging warrior comes out of retirement for one last stand at a fortress besieged by a barbarian horde, in the novel that defined heroic fantasy.

title: Waylander
author: David Gemmell
description: An assassin who killed the king seeks redemption by retrieving a magical artifact while an army invades his homeland.

title: Wolf in Shadow
author: David Gemmell
description: In a post-apocalyptic world, a gunslinger called the Jerusalem Man hunts evil across a shattered landscape.

title: Knights of Dark Renown
author: David Gemmell
description: A disgraced knight must gather a new company to ride into a demon-haunted dimension and rescue the original Knights who vanished years ago.

title: The Blue Sword
author: Robin McKinley
description: A young woman in a colonial outpost is kidnapped by a desert king and discovers she has the legendary power to wield the Blue Sword.

title: The Hero and the Crown
author: Robin McKinley
description: A king's daughter proves herself by slaying a dragon and recovering the Hero's Crown in the prequel to The Blue Sword.

title: Beauty
author: Robin McKinley
description: A retelling of Beauty and the Beast in which the youngest daughter volunteers to live with the Beast and discovers his enchanted castle.

title: Sunshine
author: Robin McKinley
description: A baker is kidnapped by vampires and chained to one as bait, but they form an alliance to survive and she discovers her own hidden magic.

title: Spindle's End
author: Robin McKinley
description: A retelling of Sleeping Beauty where the princess is raised by fairies in a village and must face the curse with her own courage.

title: Alanna: The First Adventure
author: Tamora Pierce
description: A girl disguises herself as a boy to train as a knight while her twin brother goes to study magic.

title: In the Hand of the Goddess
author: Tamora Pierce
description: Alanna serves as squire to the prince while hiding her gender and fighting a sorcerer who threatens the kingdom.

title: The Woman Who Rides Like a Man
author: Tamora Pierce
description: Alanna earns her shield and journeys to the desert, where she trains young mages and confronts the limits of her power.

title: Lioness Rampant
author: Tamora Pierce
description: Alanna quests for the Dominion Jewel while her enemy makes his final bid for the throne.

title: Wild Magic
author: Tamora Pierce
description: A girl who can communicate with animals joins the queen's riders and must master her wild magic to fight immortal creatures invading the realm.

title: Redwall
author: Brian Jacques
description: A young mouse at Redwall Abbey must find the legendary sword of Martin the Warrior to save his home from an invading rat army.

title: Mossflower
author: Brian Jacques
description: Martin the Warrior leads the woodland creatures against the wildcat queen Tsarmina who has enslaved Mossflower Woods.

title: Mattimeo
author: Brian Jacques
description: The son of Matthias is kidnapped by a fox slaver, and the warriors of Redwall must pursue across dangerous lands.

title: Martin the Warrior
author: Brian Jacques
description: The origin story of Redwall's greatest hero, a young mouse enslaved by a tyrant who rises to lead a rebellion.

title: Theft of Swords
author: Michael J. Sullivan
description: Two thieves are framed for the king's murder and must clear their names while uncovering a church conspiracy.

title: Rise of Empire
author: Michael J. Sullivan
description: Royce and Hadrian are drawn into a rebellion against the expanding empire as the true heir to the throne is revealed.

title: Heir of Novron
author: Michael J. Sullivan
description: The final battle against the empire and the ancient elven threat as the true emperor must be found before the world ends.

title: The Black Prism
author: Brent Weeks
description: The most powerful man in the world uses light-based magic to rule, but a war reveals he has a secret son and the entire magical system may be corrupted.

title: The Blinding Knife
author: Brent Weeks
description: The Color Prince's rebellion spreads as the Prism's powers begin to fail and his son struggles with a dangerous new ability.

title: The Blood Mirror
author: Brent Weeks
description: The Prism is imprisoned while his allies fight a losing war against the Color Prince and the old gods return.

title: The Burning White
author: Brent Weeks
description: The final battle for the Chromeria as every thread converges and the truth about the Prism and the gods is revealed.

title: Jade City
author: Fonda Lee
description: Two rival families of jade-powered warriors fight for control of a modernizing island nation in an Asian-inspired gangster fantasy.

title: Jade War
author: Fonda Lee
description: The clan war goes international as foreign powers seek access to jade and the family must expand or be destroyed.

title: Jade Legacy
author: Fonda Lee
description: A generational saga spanning decades as the next generation inherits the clan and the world transforms around them.

title: Black Sun
author: Rebecca Roanhorse
description: A man raised as a vessel for a god, a sea captain, and a priestess converge on a holy city as a solar eclipse approaches in a pre-Columbian inspired world.

title: Fevered Star
author: Rebecca Roanhorse
description: The aftermath of the solstice massacre reshapes alliances as gods stir and the city fractures into competing factions.

title: Mirrored Heavens
author: Rebecca Roanhorse
description: The trilogy concludes as the gods fully manifest and mortals must choose sides in a war that will reshape the world.

title: Senlin Ascends
author: Josiah Bancroft
description: A mild schoolteacher loses his wife in the Tower of Babel and must ascend through its bizarre, dangerous levels to find her.

title: Arm of the Sphinx
author: Josiah Bancroft
description: Senlin becomes an airship captain and pirate, climbing higher into the Tower while the levels grow stranger and more hostile.

title: The Hod King
author: Josiah Bancroft
description: Senlin infiltrates the most luxurious and dangerous level of the Tower while his crew faces betrayal from within.

title: The Fall of Babel
author: Josiah Bancroft
description: The Tower's secrets are finally revealed as Senlin reaches the apex and the truth about the Sphinx and the Tower's purpose comes to light.

title: The Curse of Chalion
author: Lois McMaster Bujold
description: A broken soldier becomes tutor to a princess and discovers a curse on the royal family that only a willing sacrifice can break.

title: Paladin of Souls
author: Lois McMaster Bujold
description: A middle-aged queen dowager goes on pilgrimage and is dragged into a demonic war by a god with a sense of humor.

title: The Hallowed Hunt
author: Lois McMaster Bujold
description: A lord who absorbed a dying shaman's animal spirit must escort a woman who killed a prince to stand trial.

title: Homeland
author: R.A. Salvatore
description: A dark elf is born into the evil society of Menzoberranzan and rejects its cruelty, fleeing to the surface world.

title: Exile
author: R.A. Salvatore
description: Drizzt survives alone in the Underdark for years, hunted by his own people and the monsters of the deep.

title: Sojourn
author: R.A. Salvatore
description: Drizzt reaches the surface world and must prove to suspicious humans and dwarves that a dark elf can be trusted.

title: The Crystal Shard
author: R.A. Salvatore
description: Drizzt and his companions defend Icewind Dale from a barbarian invasion and a wizard wielding a sentient crystal artifact.

title: The Darkness That Comes Before
author: R. Scott Bakker
description: A monk with terrifying powers of manipulation joins a holy war against a heathen empire, and no one suspects his true purpose.

title: The Warrior-Prophet
author: R. Scott Bakker
description: Kellhus consolidates power over the crusading army through charisma and intellectual domination as the war grows bloodier.

title: The Thousandfold Thought
author: R. Scott Bakker
description: The Holy War reaches the heathen capital as Kellhus's true nature and cosmic purpose become terrifyingly clear.

title: Clariel
author: Garth Nix
description: Six hundred years before Sabriel, a girl who wants only to live in the forest is drawn into the political machinations of the Old Kingdom.

title: Goldenhand
author: Garth Nix
description: Lirael must stop a new threat from the north while an ancient enemy stirs in the depths of Death.

title: Castle in the Air
author: Diana Wynne Jones
description: A carpet merchant buys a flying carpet and gets swept into an adventure involving stolen princesses, a djinn, and the moving castle from the first book.

title: The Dark Lord of Derkholm
author: Diana Wynne Jones
description: A wizard is forced to play the Dark Lord for a fantasy tourism company that drags adventurers through his world, and he must find a way to end the exploitation.

title: Fire and Hemlock
author: Diana Wynne Jones
description: A girl's childhood friendship with a cellist is tangled with the ballad of Tam Lin, and she must remember the truth to save him.

title: Charmed Life
author: Diana Wynne Jones
description: A boy discovers his sister has been stealing his nine lives to fuel her witchcraft in a parallel England ruled by enchanters.

title: A Fine and Private Place
author: Peter S. Beagle
description: A man living in a Bronx cemetery befriends the ghosts who reside there, and two of the dead fall in love before they fade.

title: The Innkeeper's Song
author: Peter S. Beagle
description: Three women summoned by a dying wizard converge at an inn, each carrying secrets about the man who taught them magic.

title: The Witness for the Dead
author: Katherine Addison
description: A cleric who can speak with the dead investigates murders and missing persons in an industrial city of the goblin-elf empire.

title: The Grief of Stones
author: Katherine Addison
description: Thara Celehar continues his work, investigating the death of a woman and the disappearance of children from a foundling school.

title: A Deadly Education
author: Naomi Novik
description: A school for magicians has no teachers and no exit—the building itself tries to kill students, and a girl prophesied to destroy the world tries to survive graduation.

title: The Last Graduate
author: Naomi Novik
description: El's senior year means surviving graduation—literally fighting through hordes of monsters to escape the school—while the system itself is breaking down.

title: The Golden Enclaves
author: Naomi Novik
description: El escapes the Scholomance and discovers the enclaves that protect magicians are built on a terrible secret she must confront.

title: The Fires of Vengeance
author: Evan Winter
description: Tau fights in the queen's war while training to become the most dangerous swordsman alive and unlocking forbidden demon magic.

title: The Crimson Campaign
author: Brian McClellan
description: The coup has succeeded but enemy armies invade from all sides while the dead god may be more alive than anyone thought.

title: The Autumn Republic
author: Brian McClellan
description: The final battle for the republic as foreign armies, traitorous allies, and divine forces all converge.

title: The Book of Merlyn
author: T.H. White
description: The unpublished conclusion to The Once and Future King, where an aging Arthur returns to Merlyn for a final lesson before his last battle.

title: Shardik
author: Richard Adams
description: A hunter discovers a giant bear he believes is the incarnation of God and founds an empire around it, with catastrophic consequences.

title: The Plague Dogs
author: Richard Adams
description: Two dogs escape an animal research laboratory and survive in the English Lake District while the military hunts them.

title: All Systems Red
author: Martha Wells
description: A self-aware security robot that calls itself Murderbot has hacked its own governance module and would rather watch soap operas than protect humans—but a mission goes wrong.

title: Artificial Condition
author: Martha Wells
description: Murderbot investigates a massacre in its past with the help of a friendly transport ship AI while posing as a human.

title: Rogue Protocol
author: Martha Wells
description: Murderbot infiltrates an abandoned terraforming station to find evidence against GrayCris while babysitting a naive young combat bot.

title: Exit Strategy
author: Martha Wells
description: Murderbot must rescue its favorite human Dr. Mensah from corporate kidnappers, finally confronting its fear of being seen.

title: Network Effect
author: Martha Wells
description: Murderbot and its humans are kidnapped by a rogue ship controlled by a former friend, and an alien contamination threatens everyone.

title: Fugitive Telemetry
author: Martha Wells
description: A body turns up on Preservation Station and Murderbot must solve the murder while dealing with humans who don't trust it.

title: System Collapse
author: Martha Wells
description: Murderbot is dealing with what might be PTSD while trying to save a colony from corporate exploitation.

title: Ancillary Justice
author: Ann Leckie
description: The last surviving segment of a starship AI inhabiting a human body seeks revenge against the ruler who destroyed the rest of her.

title: Ancillary Sword
author: Ann Leckie
description: Breq takes command of a ship and a space station, navigating colonial politics while the empire tears itself apart.

title: Ancillary Mercy
author: Ann Leckie
description: Breq confronts the divided emperor and fights for the freedom of her ship, her station, and an alien species no one wants to acknowledge.

title: Translation State
author: Ann Leckie
description: A diplomat, a fugitive alien, and a young person searching for a missing relative converge in a story about identity and belonging in the Radch universe.

title: Altered Carbon
author: Richard Morgan
description: A former soldier stored as digital consciousness is resleeved into a new body to solve a rich man's murder in a future where death is optional.

title: Broken Angels
author: Richard Morgan
description: Kovacs is fighting in a colonial war when he's recruited for a heist involving alien artifacts on a devastated planet.

title: Woken Furies
author: Richard Morgan
description: Kovacs returns to his home planet on a personal vendetta and discovers a copy of himself has been illegally spun up to hunt him.

title: Doomsday Book
author: Connie Willis
description: A historian time-travels to 1348 England and arrives during the Black Death, while a modern pandemic paralyzes the Oxford of the future.

title: To Say Nothing of the Dog
author: Connie Willis
description: A time-traveling historian is sent to Victorian England to recover a hideous piece of art, and comedy ensues as the timeline resists correction.

title: Blackout
author: Connie Willis
description: Three Oxford historians are sent to WWII England and find their return drops aren't working, stranding them in the Blitz.

title: All Clear
author: Connie Willis
description: The stranded historians realize they may have altered the timeline and that the continuum itself may be breaking down.

title: Crosstalk
author: Connie Willis
description: A woman undergoes a procedure to increase empathy with her boyfriend and instead becomes telepathic, hearing everyone's thoughts.

title: The Warrior's Apprentice
author: Lois McMaster Bujold
description: A physically disabled young lord accidentally acquires a mercenary fleet and bluffs his way into commanding it.

title: Barrayar
author: Lois McMaster Bujold
description: Cordelia defends her unborn son during a civil war on the militaristic planet Barrayar.

title: The Vor Game
author: Lois McMaster Bujold
description: Miles Vorkosigan's first military posting goes sideways and he ends up rescuing an emperor and preventing a galactic war.

title: Mirror Dance
author: Lois McMaster Bujold
description: Miles's clone-brother steals his identity and his fleet, and the rescue mission goes catastrophically wrong.

title: Memory
author: Lois McMaster Bujold
description: Miles is medically discharged from his mercenary career and must reinvent himself as an imperial investigator.

title: A Civil Campaign
author: Lois McMaster Bujold
description: Miles courts a widow with spectacularly bad judgment while political schemes and a bioengineered butter bug disrupt Barrayaran society.

title: The Day of the Triffids
author: John Wyndham
description: Most of humanity is blinded by a meteor shower, and mobile carnivorous plants begin to take over the world.

title: The Chrysalids
author: John Wyndham
description: In a post-nuclear society that destroys any genetic deviation, a group of telepathic children must hide their mutation or be killed.

title: The Midwich Cuckoos
author: John Wyndham
description: Every woman of childbearing age in an English village falls unconscious and wakes up pregnant with golden-eyed children who share a hive mind.

title: The Kraken Wakes
author: John Wyndham
description: Alien creatures settle in Earth's deep oceans and slowly escalate attacks on humanity, melting the ice caps to flood the land.

title: Blood Music
author: Greg Bear
description: A rogue geneticist injects himself with intelligent cells that begin to reshape him—and then all of North America—into something post-human.

title: Eon
author: Greg Bear
description: A hollow asteroid appears in Earth orbit containing a tunnel that extends infinitely, and inside are records of humanity's future—including nuclear war.

title: The Forge of God
author: Greg Bear
description: Aliens are secretly dismantling Earth from the inside while a second alien faction tries to save a remnant of humanity.

title: Darwin's Radio
author: Greg Bear
description: A retrovirus activates ancient junk DNA in pregnant women, triggering the next stage of human evolution while governments panic.

title: Sundiver
author: David Brin
description: Humans discover that all species in the galaxy were uplifted by patron races—except humans, who apparently uplifted themselves, making them a threat.

title: Startide Rising
author: David Brin
description: A ship crewed by uplifted dolphins discovers a fleet of ancient ships that every alien faction will kill for, and must hide on a water world.

title: The Uplift War
author: David Brin
description: Earth's colony is invaded while a group of humans and uplifted chimpanzees wage a guerrilla war in the wilderness.

title: The Postman
author: David Brin
description: A drifter in post-apocalyptic Oregon finds a mail carrier's uniform and discovers that the lie of a restored government can become the truth.

title: City
author: Clifford Simak
description: Dogs inherit Earth after humans leave, and robotic servants maintain the old ways in linked stories spanning thousands of years.

title: Way Station
author: Clifford Simak
description: A Civil War veteran secretly operates a galactic transfer station in rural Wisconsin, living for over a century while the Cold War threatens to end everything.

title: Ring Around the Sun
author: Clifford Simak
description: Indestructible consumer goods threaten the global economy, and the trail leads to mutant humans who can step between parallel Earths.

title: Dying Inside
author: Robert Silverberg
description: A New York telepath is slowly losing his power as he ages, and the loss of his only gift strips him of his identity.

title: The Book of Skulls
author: Robert Silverberg
description: Four college friends seek immortality at an Arizona monastery, knowing that two of them must die for the other two to live forever.

title: A Time of Changes
author: Robert Silverberg
description: On a planet where saying 'I' is taboo, a man discovers a drug that enables telepathic communion and begins a revolution of selfhood.

title: Downward to the Earth
author: Robert Silverberg
description: A colonial administrator returns to the planet he helped exploit, seeking redemption from its elephant-like natives who have a mysterious rebirth ritual.

title: Downbelow Station
author: C.J. Cherryh
description: A space station caught between Earth's fleet and a rebel navy must navigate politics and refugees as interstellar war reaches its climax.

title: Cyteen
author: C.J. Cherryh
description: A brilliant scientist who designs human clones is murdered, and her clone-successor must uncover the conspiracy while becoming the woman she was copied from.

title: The Pride of Chanur
author: C.J. Cherryh
description: A lion-like alien merchant captain harbors a human fugitive and is drawn into a multispecies political crisis.

title: Foreigner
author: C.J. Cherryh
description: The sole human diplomat on an alien world navigates a society where loyalty is biological and assassination is a political art form.

title: Beyond the Blue Event Horizon
author: Frederik Pohl
description: An expedition enters the alien Heechee food factory while Robinette Broadhead funds the search from Earth, still haunted by guilt.

title: Man Plus
author: Frederik Pohl
description: A man is surgically transformed into a cyborg capable of surviving on Mars, losing his humanity with each modification.

title: The Space Merchants
author: Frederik Pohl
description: An advertising executive in a corporate-run future is assigned to sell colonization of Venus and discovers the underground resistance.

title: The Mote in God's Eye
author: Larry Niven
description: Humanity's first contact with an alien species that has a terrifying biological secret—they breed uncontrollably and have destroyed themselves repeatedly.

title: Lucifer's Hammer
author: Larry Niven
description: A comet strikes Earth and the survivors must rebuild civilization while fighting off a cannibal army led by a charismatic preacher.

title: Footfall
author: Larry Niven
description: Elephant-like aliens invade Earth and drop an asteroid on the Indian Ocean, and humanity must fight back with improvised technology.

title: A World Out of Time
author: Larry Niven
description: A man revived from cryogenic suspension steals a starship and returns to find Earth transformed by millions of years of evolution.

title: The Door into Summer
author: Robert A. Heinlein
description: An inventor is cheated out of his company and uses cold sleep and time travel to get his life back—and his cat.

title: Have Space Suit—Will Travel
author: Robert A. Heinlein
description: A teenager wins a used spacesuit in a contest, is kidnapped by aliens, and ends up defending humanity before a galactic tribunal.

title: Time Enough for Love
author: Robert A. Heinlein
description: The oldest living human, Lazarus Long, is forced to tell his life story spanning two thousand years of adventure, love, and time travel.

title: Double Star
author: Robert A. Heinlein
description: A down-on-his-luck actor is hired to impersonate a kidnapped politician and must sustain the role through an interplanetary crisis.

title: Citizen of the Galaxy
author: Robert A. Heinlein
description: A boy sold as a slave on an alien planet is freed by a beggar who turns out to be a spy, beginning a journey that reveals he is heir to a vast fortune.

title: Friday
author: Robert A. Heinlein
description: An enhanced human courier navigates a fragmented future America while searching for a family and a place to belong.

title: The Peripheral
author: William Gibson
description: A woman in a near-future Appalachia connects to a far-future London where the apocalypse already happened, and both timelines begin to affect each other.

title: Agency
author: William Gibson
description: An alternate 2017 where Clinton won and a more stable future diverge from a darker timeline, connected through a mysterious app.

title: Virtual Light
author: William Gibson
description: A bike messenger steals a pair of VR glasses containing plans to rebuild San Francisco, and a security guard is sent to get them back.

title: Idoru
author: William Gibson
description: A rock star announces he will marry a virtual idol—an AI pop singer—and a data analyst is hired to find out if it's a hoax or something stranger.

title: All Tomorrow's Parties
author: William Gibson
description: A homeless man on the Bridge in San Francisco senses a nodal point approaching—a moment when everything changes—and all the players converge.

title: Xenocide
author: Orson Scott Card
description: A planet faces destruction by the Starways Congress fleet while its inhabitants search for a way to save three sentient species from extinction.

title: Children of the Mind
author: Orson Scott Card
description: Ender transfers his consciousness as his body fails, and the fleet's planet-killer weapon must be stopped before it reaches Lusitania.

title: Ender's Shadow
author: Orson Scott Card
description: The events of Ender's Game retold from the perspective of Bean, the smallest and most brilliant student in Battle School.

title: Dandelion Wine
author: Ray Bradbury
description: A twelve-year-old boy experiences the summer of 1928 in a small Illinois town, discovering the enchantments and terrors of being alive.

title: The Halloween Tree
author: Ray Bradbury
description: Eight boys chase the spirit of Halloween through history—from ancient Egypt to the Day of the Dead—to save their dying friend.

title: The Einstein Intersection
author: Samuel R. Delany
description: Aliens inhabiting a post-human Earth live out distorted versions of human myths—Orpheus, Billy the Kid—trying to understand the species they replaced.

title: Stars in My Pocket Like Grains of Sand
author: Samuel R. Delany
description: An industrial slave and an information diplomat fall in love across a galaxy where worlds are being destroyed by Cultural Fugue.

title: Triton
author: Samuel R. Delany
description: A man on Neptune's moon in a society of total freedom cannot find happiness because he can't accept anyone else's autonomy.

title: Inversions
author: Iain M. Banks
description: Two stories set on a pre-industrial world—a foreign doctor and a foreign bodyguard—who may be Culture agents subtly influencing events.

title: Matter
author: Iain M. Banks
description: A prince flees a murder on a Shellworld—a planet-sized artifact—while his Culture-agent sister races to intervene.

title: The Hydrogen Sonata
author: Iain M. Banks
description: A civilization preparing to Sublime into a higher dimension faces sabotage, and a soldier with four arms must find a man who is ten thousand years old.

title: Record of a Spaceborn Few
author: Becky Chambers
description: Life aboard the aging Exodus Fleet, where humans who left Earth generations ago must decide if their way of life still has meaning.

title: The Galaxy, and the Ground Within
author: Becky Chambers
description: Five aliens from different species are stranded at a waystation during an emergency and must coexist in Chambers's final Wayfarers novel.

title: A Psalm for the Wild-Built
author: Becky Chambers
description: A tea monk in a post-industrial world meets a robot from the wilderness and they ask each other what humans and machines actually need.

title: Permutation City
author: Greg Egan
description: Uploaded minds in a simulated universe discover that their reality may be more fundamental than the physical world that created them.

title: Diaspora
author: Greg Egan
description: Post-human intelligences—some biological, some digital—face a gamma-ray burst that threatens all life and must find a way to survive across dimensions.

title: Schild's Ladder
author: Greg Egan
description: A physics experiment creates an expanding bubble of new vacuum that converts everything it touches, and humanity must decide whether to destroy it or explore it.

title: Accelerando
author: Charles Stross
description: Three generations of a family ride the wave of the Singularity from present-day Earth through the dismantling of the solar system into computronium.

title: Glasshouse
author: Charles Stross
description: A post-human veteran of a memory war volunteers for an experiment recreating 20th-century life and discovers someone in the simulation is a killer.

title: The Atrocity Archives
author: Charles Stross
description: A computer scientist working for a secret British agency discovers that mathematics can summon Lovecraftian horrors from other dimensions.

title: Saturn's Children
author: Charles Stross
description: Humanity is extinct and the robots built to serve them have inherited the solar system, maintaining a society modeled on their creators.

title: The Reality Dysfunction
author: Peter F. Hamilton
description: The dead begin returning to life and possessing the living across a far-future interstellar civilization, threatening to overwhelm humanity.

title: The Neutronium Alchemist
author: Peter F. Hamilton
description: The possession crisis spreads as entire planets fall, and a desperate search for a solution leads to the neutronium alchemist.

title: The Naked God
author: Peter F. Hamilton
description: The final confrontation with the returning dead forces humanity to confront the nature of the soul and the origin of the crisis.

title: All These Worlds
author: Dennis E. Taylor
description: The Bobs face the Others—a hostile alien species that consumes entire solar systems—while trying to evacuate Earth.

title: Heaven's River
author: Dennis E. Taylor
description: A Bob discovers a massive alien megastructure disguised as a river-world and goes undercover among its inhabitants to find a missing friend.

title: The Prefect
author: Alastair Reynolds
description: A detective in the Glitter Band—ten thousand orbital habitats around Yellowstone—investigates a crime that threatens the democratic system itself.

title: Century Rain
author: Alastair Reynolds
description: A far-future archaeologist discovers an exact replica of 1950s Paris inside an alien artifact, where a detective is investigating a murder.

title: Terminal World
author: Alastair Reynolds
