description: A pathologist in a far-future city built on a massive spire discovers zones of different technology levels and a patient who shouldn't exist.

title: Guns of the Dawn
author: Adrian Tchaikovsky
description: A gentlewoman is conscripted to fight in a brutal swamp war when her country runs out of men, in a Napoleonic-era fantasy standalone.

title: The Doors of Eden
author: Adrian Tchaikovsky
description: Parallel Earths where different species evolved to dominance begin to collide, and a spy, a scientist, and two cryptozoologists must save all of them.

title: Iron Gold
author: Pierce Brown
description: Ten years after the revolution, Darrow fights an insurgency while new POV characters reveal the costs of the war he started.

title: Dark Age
author: Pierce Brown
description: The Republic is fracturing as enemies converge from all sides in the darkest and most brutal entry in the series.

title: Light Bringer
author: Pierce Brown
description: Darrow returns from captivity to lead a desperate counterattack as the war for the solar system reaches its climax.

title: Titan
author: John Varley
description: An expedition discovers that Saturn's moon is actually a living alien megastructure containing entire ecosystems and civilizations.

title: Wizard
author: John Varley
description: Two humans seek to prove themselves worthy to the living world Gaea, journeying through its bizarre landscapes on a heroic quest.

title: Demon
author: John Varley
description: Gaea has gone insane and declared war on humanity, modeling herself on the Wizard of Oz while nuclear war rages on Earth.

title: The Chronoliths
author: Robert Charles Wilson
description: Gigantic monuments from the future begin appearing around the world, commemorating victories in a war that hasn't happened yet.

title: Blind Lake
author: Robert Charles Wilson
description: A scientific facility using quantum technology to observe an alien civilization is suddenly quarantined, and no one knows why.

title: Forever Free
author: Joe Haldeman
description: Decades after the Forever War, Mandella leads an escape from a planet of veterans, but their ship's journey leads to a mind-bending revelation.

title: The Water Knife
author: Paolo Bacigalupi
description: In a drought-ravaged American Southwest, a water mercenary, a journalist, and a refugee fight over water rights as states go to war.

title: Ship Breaker
author: Paolo Bacigalupi
description: A boy scavenging wrecked oil tankers on a future Gulf Coast finds a wrecked clipper ship with a rich girl trapped inside.

title: A Study in Scarlet
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
description: Dr. Watson meets Sherlock Holmes and they investigate a murder in an empty house, tracing it back to a tale of love and revenge among the Mormons of Utah.

title: The Sign of the Four
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
description: Holmes investigates a missing treasure from India, a one-legged man, and a poisoned dart from a blowgun, while Watson falls in love.

title: The Hound of the Baskervilles
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
description: Holmes investigates whether a supernatural hound is killing the heirs of the Baskerville family on the moors of Devon.

title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
description: Twelve classic cases including A Scandal in Bohemia, The Red-Headed League, and The Speckled Band.

title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
description: Eleven stories culminating in The Final Problem, where Holmes and Moriarty plunge over the Reichenbach Falls.

title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
description: Holmes returns from the dead in The Empty House and solves thirteen more cases, including The Dancing Men and Charles Augustus Milverton.

title: The Valley of Fear
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
description: Holmes investigates a murder at a moated manor house, and the trail leads back to a secret society in an American mining valley.

title: Whose Body?
author: Dorothy L. Sayers
description: Lord Peter Wimsey investigates a naked corpse found in a bathtub wearing nothing but a pince-nez, in the first Wimsey novel.

title: Strong Poison
author: Dorothy L. Sayers
description: Wimsey falls in love with mystery writer Harriet Vane, who is on trial for poisoning her former lover.

title: The Nine Tailors
author: Dorothy L. Sayers
description: Wimsey investigates a corpse found in a grave in the Fens, with church bells and campanology at the heart of the mystery.

title: A Taste for Death
author: P.D. James
description: A baronet and a tramp are found with their throats cut in a church vestry, and Dalgliesh investigates connections reaching into high society.

title: The Children of Men
author: P.D. James
description: In a future where humanity has become infertile, a disillusioned academic is drawn into a conspiracy when a woman becomes pregnant.

title: An Unsuitable Job for a Woman
author: P.D. James
description: A young private detective investigates the apparent suicide of a Cambridge student and discovers his death was not what it seemed.

title: Faceless Killers
author: Henning Mankell
description: Inspector Wallander investigates the brutal murder of an elderly couple in rural Sweden, while anti-immigrant tensions rise around a dying woman's last word.

title: The Dogs of Riga
author: Henning Mankell
description: A life raft with two dead men washes up on the Swedish coast, and Wallander is drawn into the criminal underworld of post-Soviet Latvia.

title: Sidetracked
author: Henning Mankell
description: A serial killer scalps his victims while Wallander investigates, battling his own depression and a burning girl's death that haunts him.

title: The Three Evangelists
author: Fred Vargas
description: Three unemployed historians in Paris investigate when a mysterious tree appears overnight in a neighbor's garden, leading to murder.

title: Have Mercy on Us All
author: Fred Vargas
description: A town crier in Paris notices cryptic messages about the plague appearing on doors, and Commissaire Adamsberg investigates before the killing starts.

title: Case Histories
author: Kate Atkinson
description: Three cold cases converge around a private investigator in Cambridge: a missing child, a murdered girl, and a wife-killer who may be innocent.

title: Life After Life
author: Kate Atkinson
description: A woman is born, dies, and is born again, living through the 20th century in different versions as she tries to get her life right.

title: Behind the Scenes at the Museum
author: Kate Atkinson
description: A girl narrates the story of her Yorkshire family from her own conception through a century of secrets, loss, and black comedy.

title: "A" Is for Alibi
author: Sue Grafton
description: PI Kinsey Millhone reopens a poisoning case eight years cold when the convicted wife is released from prison and still claims innocence.

title: "B" Is for Burglar
author: Sue Grafton
description: Kinsey is hired to find a missing woman in Florida and discovers she may not want to be found—or may not be alive.

title: The Poet
author: Michael Connelly
description: A journalist investigates his detective brother's apparent suicide and uncovers a serial killer targeting homicide detectives who stages their deaths as suicides.

title: The Brass Verdict
author: Michael Connelly
description: Mickey Haller inherits a murdered lawyer's cases, including a Hollywood mogul accused of killing his wife, and discovers why the lawyer was killed.

title: A Noble Radiance
author: Donna Leon
description: Brunetti investigates when bones are found in a field near a Venetian villa, connecting to the disappearance of a count's son years ago.

title: Friends in High Places
author: Donna Leon
description: An official tells Brunetti his apartment has an illegal building permit, then turns up dead, leading to corruption in Venice's construction industry.

title: Malice
author: Keigo Higashino
description: A detective solves a murder quickly—too quickly—and the real mystery turns out to be not who killed the victim but why.

title: Newcomer
author: Keigo Higashino
description: A woman is murdered in a new neighborhood, and a detective investigates by learning the stories of everyone on the street.

title: Journey Under the Midnight Sun
author: Keigo Higashino
description: A pawnshop owner's unsolved murder casts a twenty-year shadow over two children connected to the crime, in Higashino's darkest novel.

title: The Haunting of Hill House
author: Shirley Jackson
description: Four people stay in a notoriously haunted mansion for a paranormal study, and one of them begins to lose the boundary between herself and the house.

title: We Have Always Lived in the Castle
author: Shirley Jackson
description: Two sisters live in isolation after most of their family was poisoned, and the arrival of a cousin threatens their fragile world.

title: The Lottery and Other Stories
author: Shirley Jackson
description: The collection containing The Lottery—a quiet town's annual ritual that reveals the violence underneath small-town conformity.

title: The Sundial
author: Shirley Jackson
description: A family in a country mansion becomes convinced the apocalypse is coming and they alone will survive, descending into paranoia and madness.

title: The Call of Cthulhu and Other Weird Stories
author: H.P. Lovecraft
description: The essential collection of Lovecraft's cosmic horror: Cthulhu, Dagon, The Colour Out of Space, and The Shadow Over Innsmouth.

title: At the Mountains of Madness
author: H.P. Lovecraft
description: An Antarctic expedition discovers the ruins of a city built by alien beings millions of years ago, and what they find in the tunnels beneath it.

title: The Dunwich Horror and Others
author: H.P. Lovecraft
description: More cosmic horror including The Dunwich Horror, The Whisperer in Darkness, and The Thing on the Doorstep.

title: Ghost Story
author: Peter Straub
description: Five elderly men in a small town share a terrible secret from fifty years ago, and a shape-shifting evil returns to destroy them one by one.

title: Koko
author: Peter Straub
description: Vietnam veterans track down a former platoon member who has become a serial killer, but none of them can trust their own memories of the war.

title: The Talisman
author: Peter Straub
description: A boy travels across America and a parallel world called the Territories to find a talisman that can save his dying mother. Co-written with Stephen King.

title: The Hellbound Heart
author: Clive Barker
description: A man opens a puzzle box that summons the Cenobites—angels to some, demons to others—and his resurrected body demands his brother's wife bring him victims.

title: The Books of Blood
author: Clive Barker
description: Barker's debut story collection that Stephen King called the future of horror—visceral, imaginative tales that redefined the genre.

title: Weaveworld
author: Clive Barker
description: A magical world woven into a carpet is threatened by a sorceress, and two ordinary people must protect it from being destroyed.

title: Imajica
author: Clive Barker
description: Five parallel Earths must be reconciled into one by a man who discovers he has lived before and his enemy is the God of the dominant religion.

title: The Damnation Game
author: Clive Barker
description: A gambler makes a Faustian deal with a man who learned the secrets of life and death in the ruins of Warsaw.

title: The Terror
author: Dan Simmons
description: The doomed 1847 Franklin Expedition is trapped in Arctic ice and hunted by something on the ice that is not a polar bear.

title: Song of Kali
author: Dan Simmons
description: An American writer travels to Calcutta to recover a lost manuscript and encounters a cult that worships the goddess of destruction.

title: Carrion Comfort
author: Dan Simmons
description: Psychic vampires who feed on violence have been manipulating history—including the Holocaust—and a small group discovers their existence.

title: The Pallbearers Club
author: Paul Tremblay
description: A memoir-like novel about a man's lifelong friendship with a woman who may be a vampire—annotated by the woman herself, who disagrees with everything.

title: Horror Movie
author: Paul Tremblay
description: An actor from a legendary unfinished horror film recounts what happened on set, where the line between the movie and reality dissolved.

title: The Good House
author: Tananarive Due
description: A woman returns to her grandmother's house in a Pacific Northwest town and awakens something her grandmother trapped there decades ago.

title: My Soul to Keep
author: Tananarive Due
description: A woman discovers her husband is an immortal Ethiopian who has lived for five hundred years—and his brotherhood will kill to keep the secret.

title: Her Body and Other Parties
author: Carmen Maria Machado
description: Eight stories blending horror, fairy tales, and erotica—a woman with a ribbon around her neck, a plague spread by sexual contact, a haunted Law & Order episode.

title: In the Dream House
author: Carmen Maria Machado
description: A memoir of an abusive same-sex relationship told through genre tropes—the haunted house, the choose-your-own-adventure, the unreliable narrator.

title: The Twisted Ones
author: T. Kingfisher
description: A woman cleaning out her dead grandmother's house in rural North Carolina discovers a journal describing something terrible in the woods—and the descriptions match what she's seeing.

title: The Hollow Places
author: T. Kingfisher
description: A woman discovers a portal in her uncle's taxidermy museum that leads to a place of willows and water where something is watching.

title: What Moves the Dead
author: T. Kingfisher
description: A retelling of Poe's Fall of the House of Usher in which a soldier visits dying friends and discovers something fungal and parasitic.

title: The Life Impossible
author: Matt Haig
description: A retired math teacher inherits a house in Ibiza and discovers it comes with a mystery, a connection to the sea, and something unexplainable.

title: The Good Lord Bird
author: James McBride
description: A young slave joins John Brown's raid on Harpers Ferry while disguised as a girl, narrating the abolitionist's holy war with dark humor.

title: Deacon King Kong
author: James McBride
description: An elderly church deacon shoots a drug dealer in a Brooklyn housing project in 1969, and the aftermath connects an entire community.

title: The Heaven & Earth Grocery Store
author: James McBride
description: In 1930s Pennsylvania, a skeleton found at the bottom of a well connects to the Black and Jewish communities who once shared a neighborhood.

title: The Color of Water
author: James McBride
description: A dual memoir of McBride and his white Jewish mother who married a Black man and raised twelve children in Brooklyn.

title: James
author: Percival Everett
description: Huck Finn retold from Jim's perspective—a literate, strategic man who plays dumb to survive, rewriting American literature's most famous friendship.

title: The Trees
author: Percival Everett
description: Bodies start turning up in Money, Mississippi—each with an extra corpse that looks like Emmett Till—in a satirical horror novel about racial justice.

title: The Flamethrowers
author: Rachel Kushner
description: A young artist and motorcycle racer moves to 1970s New York and is drawn into the Italian radical left through her boyfriend's family.

title: The Mars Room
author: Rachel Kushner
description: A woman serving two consecutive life sentences in a California prison for killing her stalker recounts the life that brought her there.

title: Creation Lake
author: Rachel Kushner
description: An American spy infiltrates a French commune of eco-radicals, but the group's philosopher may be more dangerous—or more right—than her handlers know.

title: The People in the Trees
author: Hanya Yanagihara
description: A disgraced Nobel laureate recounts his discovery of a remote Pacific island tribe with an apparent secret to immortality.

title: To Paradise
author: Hanya Yanagihara
description: Three stories set in the same New York townhouse in 1893, 1993, and 2093, each exploring love, freedom, and the meaning of utopia.

title: The Candy House
author: Jennifer Egan
description: A tech mogul invents a device that externalizes memory—you can access anyone's experiences—and the consequences ripple across interconnected lives.

title: Manhattan Beach
author: Jennifer Egan
description: A woman becomes the first female diver at the Brooklyn Navy Yard during WWII while searching for her father who vanished into the world of organized crime.

title: Time Is a Mother
author: Ocean Vuong
description: Poems written after the death of Vuong's mother, exploring grief, the body, queerness, and the Vietnamese diaspora.

title: Prophet Song
author: Paul Lynch
description: A Dublin mother watches Ireland slide into authoritarianism as her husband is taken by the secret police and she must decide whether to flee.

title: Orbital
author: Samantha Harvey
description: Six astronauts orbit Earth over twenty-four hours, watching hurricanes and wars from above while reflecting on the fragility of the world below.

title: Trust
author: Hernan Diaz
description: Four nested narratives reconstruct the life of a 1920s Wall Street tycoon and his wife, each version contradicting the last about who held the real power.

title: Magpie Murders
author: Anthony Horowitz
description: An editor reading a murder mystery manuscript realizes the author has hidden a real murder inside the fiction—and then the author dies.

title: The Sentence Is Death
author: Anthony Horowitz
description: A divorce lawyer is bludgeoned to death and the number 182 is painted at the crime scene, and Horowitz and his detective investigate.

title: The Seven Deaths of Evelyn Hardcastle
author: Stuart Turton
description: A man must solve a murder at a country house party by reliving the day eight times in eight different bodies.

title: The Devil and the Dark Water
author: Stuart Turton

title: The Mothers
author: Brit Bennett
description: A secret abortion in a Black church community in Southern California reverberates through three young people's lives over a decade.

title: The Power and the Glory
author: Graham Greene
description: The last Catholic priest in a Mexican state that has outlawed religion flees from a police lieutenant, sustained by whiskey and a faith he can barely hold.

title: The Heart of the Matter
author: Graham Greene
description: A police commissioner in colonial West Africa is trapped between his devout wife, his mistress, and his Catholic guilt, and every choice leads to damnation.

title: The End of the Affair
author: Graham Greene
description: A novelist investigates why his lover ended their affair during the Blitz and discovers a vow to God he never knew about.

title: The Quiet American
author: Graham Greene
description: A jaded British journalist and an idealistic American aid worker compete for a Vietnamese woman as covert operations reshape 1950s Saigon.

title: Brighton Rock
author: Graham Greene
description: A teenage gangster in Brighton commits murder and marries a waitress to prevent her testifying, and his casual evil collides with her stubborn goodness.

title: The Third Man
author: Graham Greene
description: A pulp novelist arrives in postwar Vienna to find his old friend Harry Lime has died—or has he?—in occupied Vienna's criminal underworld.

title: Our Man in Havana
author: Graham Greene
description: A vacuum cleaner salesman in Cuba is recruited as a spy and invents intelligence reports based on his product diagrams, until real agents start dying.

title: The Comedians
author: Graham Greene
description: Three men arrive in Haiti under Papa Doc Duvalier's terror—a hotelier, an arms dealer, and a con man—and their pretenses collapse under the regime's violence.

title: A Burnt-Out Case
author: Graham Greene
description: A famous architect fleeing his own reputation arrives at a Congo leper colony seeking anonymity, but the world follows him.

title: The Honorary Consul
author: Graham Greene
description: Paraguayan revolutionaries kidnap the wrong diplomat in a border town, and a doctor must negotiate for his friend's life.

title: Travels with My Aunt
author: Graham Greene
description: A retired bank manager's eccentric aunt drags him from suburban England into a life of smuggling, war criminals, and international intrigue.

title: The Adventures of Augie March
author: Saul Bellow
description: A Chicago boy from a poor immigrant family bounces through the Depression and WWII, trying on identities and refusing to be claimed by anyone.

title: Herzog
author: Saul Bellow
description: An intellectual in the middle of a nervous breakdown writes unsent letters to everyone from Nietzsche to Eisenhower while his second wife leaves him for his best friend.

title: Humboldt's Gift
author: Saul Bellow
description: A successful writer reflects on his friendship with a dead poet genius who was also a paranoid wreck, while a small-time mobster stalks him.

title: Henderson the Rain King
author: Saul Bellow
description: A restless American millionaire goes to Africa seeking meaning and becomes entangled with two tribes, a lion, and a rain-making ceremony.

title: Seize the Day
author: Saul Bellow
description: A man having the worst day of his life—fired, separated, fleeced by a con man, rejected by his father—collapses at a stranger's funeral.

title: Mr. Sammler's Planet
author: Saul Bellow
description: A Holocaust survivor in 1960s New York watches civilization unravel around him—student radicals, street crime, moon landings—with weary, luminous intelligence.

title: Ravelstein
author: Saul Bellow
description: A thinly veiled portrait of Allan Bloom by his closest friend, celebrating and mourning a great mind consumed by appetite and ideas.

title: The Dean's December
author: Saul Bellow
description: A Chicago dean visits his wife's dying mother in Ceaușescu's Romania, toggling between two kinds of decay—American and communist.

title: Rabbit, Run
author: John Updike
description: A former high school basketball star abandons his pregnant wife and runs, beginning a four-novel chronicle of American restlessness.

title: Rabbit Redux
author: John Updike
description: Rabbit's wife leaves him and he takes in a young runaway and a Black Vietnam veteran, and the 1960s crash into his living room.

title: Rabbit Is Rich
author: John Updike
description: Rabbit runs a Toyota dealership during the oil crisis, has money for the first time, and can't stop worrying about a girl who might be his illegitimate daughter.

title: Rabbit at Rest
author: John Updike
description: Rabbit is fat, retired in Florida, and his son is a cocaine addict, and America in the late Reagan era feels as bloated and exhausted as he does.

title: Couples
author: John Updike
description: Ten couples in a Massachusetts town swap partners in a web of adultery that defines suburban 1960s America.

title: The Witches of Eastwick
author: John Updike
description: Three divorced women in a Rhode Island town discover they have supernatural powers, and a mysterious man who moves in channels their magic toward destruction.

title: Terrorist
author: John Updike
description: An eighteen-year-old Muslim convert in New Jersey is recruited for a terrorist attack, and his high school guidance counselor tries to reach him.

title: Gertrude and Claudius
author: John Updike
description: The backstory of Hamlet's mother and uncle—their long-suppressed love affair—told with sympathy for the characters Shakespeare condemned.

title: The Sea, the Sea
author: Iris Murdoch
description: A retired theater director retreats to a coastal house and becomes obsessed with a childhood sweetheart, manipulating everyone around him while convinced of his own virtue.

title: Under the Net
author: Iris Murdoch
description: A translator and hack writer bounces around postwar London in a picaresque philosophical comedy involving stolen manuscripts, film sets, and a socialist mime troupe.

title: The Black Prince
author: Iris Murdoch
description: An aging writer falls obsessively in love with his rival's twenty-year-old daughter, and the novel is framed by competing postscripts from everyone involved.

title: The Bell
author: Iris Murdoch
description: A lay religious community near a convent is disrupted by a legendary bell in the lake and the sexual and spiritual confusion of its members.

title: A Severed Head
author: Iris Murdoch
description: A wine merchant's wife leaves him for his psychoanalyst, beginning a daisy chain of partner-swapping among six people who can't stop analyzing their own behavior.

title: The Good Apprentice
author: Iris Murdoch
description: A young man who accidentally drugged his friend searches for his absent father at a mysterious country house while his stepbrother pursues spiritual perfection.

title: A Clockwork Orange
author: Anthony Burgess
description: A teenage delinquent who speaks in invented slang and loves ultraviolence is subjected to aversion therapy that robs him of free will.

title: Earthly Powers
author: Anthony Burgess
description: An aging gay novelist recounts his friendship with a pope and a century of art, war, and moral compromise in Burgess's masterpiece.

title: The Malayan Trilogy
author: Anthony Burgess
description: Three novels of a British civil servant in Malaya as the empire collapses, told with linguistic exuberance and comic despair.

title: Nothing Like the Sun
author: Anthony Burgess
description: Shakespeare's love life reimagined—his marriage, his Dark Lady, his boy players—in prose that channels Elizabethan English.

title: Enderby
author: Anthony Burgess
description: A flatulent, reclusive poet who writes in the bathroom is dragged into the world by a woman who wants to make him respectable.

title: Ragtime
author: E.L. Doctorow
description: Three families—wealthy, immigrant, Black—intersect with Houdini, Emma Goldman, and Henry Ford in a kaleidoscopic portrait of early 20th-century America.

title: The Book of Daniel
author: E.L. Doctorow
description: The son of executed atomic spies (modeled on the Rosenbergs) tries to understand his parents' martyrdom while the 1960s rage around him.

title: Billy Bathgate
author: E.L. Doctorow
description: A Bronx teenager becomes the protégé of Dutch Schultz in the last days of the gangster's empire.

title: The March
author: E.L. Doctorow
description: Sherman's March to the Sea told through dozens of characters—soldiers, slaves, surgeons, refugees—in a panoramic Civil War novel.

title: World's Fair
author: E.L. Doctorow
description: A boy grows up in the Bronx in the 1930s, and the 1939 World's Fair becomes the luminous center of a Depression childhood.

title: Homer & Langley
author: E.L. Doctorow
description: The Collyer brothers—real-life hoarders found dead in their packed Manhattan brownstone—reimagined across a century of American history.

title: The Prime of Miss Jean Brodie
author: Muriel Spark
description: A charismatic Edinburgh schoolteacher molds her favorite students in her own image, with devastating consequences she never sees coming.

title: Memento Mori
author: Muriel Spark
description: Elderly Londoners receive anonymous phone calls saying 'Remember you must die,' and each reacts according to their character.

title: The Driver's Seat
author: Muriel Spark
description: A woman flies to a southern European city determined to be murdered, arranging her own death with terrifying precision.

title: The Girls of Slender Means
author: Muriel Spark
description: Young women in a London hostel in the last days of WWII gossip, share clothes, and survive a bombing that reveals who they really are.

title: A Far Cry from Kensington
author: Muriel Spark
description: A war widow in 1950s London publishing repeatedly calls a talentless writer a pisseur de copie and the consequences spiral outward.

title: The Blue Flower
author: Penelope Fitzgerald
description: The young poet Novalis falls in love with a plain twelve-year-old girl in 18th-century Germany, and his idealism collides with the messy real world.

title: Offshore
author: Penelope Fitzgerald
description: A community of people living on houseboats on the Thames—neither on land nor at sea—try to hold their unstable lives together.

title: The Bookshop
author: Penelope Fitzgerald
description: A widow opens a bookshop in a small English town and the entire establishment conspires to destroy her.

title: The Gate of Angels
author: Penelope Fitzgerald
description: A Cambridge physicist studying the nature of matter collides with a working-class nurse, and both must reconcile science with love.

title: Innocence
author: Penelope Fitzgerald
description: An Italian count's daughter falls for a neurologist in 1950s Florence, and her family's history of trying to help people has always ended in disaster.

title: The Elementary Particles
author: Michel Houellebecq
description: Two half-brothers—one a depressed biologist, the other a sex-obsessed drifter—embody the spiritual and physical emptiness of modern Western life.

title: Submission
author: Michel Houellebecq
description: A French academic watches as an Islamic party wins the presidency and he must decide whether to convert or lose everything.

title: The Map and the Territory
author: Michel Houellebecq
description: An artist becomes famous for photographing Michelin maps and painting portraits of professionals, and then Houellebecq himself is murdered in the novel.

title: Whatever
author: Michel Houellebecq
description: A depressed IT worker observes the sexual marketplace of modern France with clinical disgust in Houellebecq's debut provocation.

title: Platform
author: Michel Houellebecq
description: A civil servant falls in love on a Thai vacation and helps develop a sex tourism business that is destroyed by terrorism.

title: Serotonin
author: Michel Houellebecq
description: A depressed agronomist on antidepressants drifts through ruined French countryside, watching farmers destroy themselves while Europe rots.

title: Flights
author: Olga Tokarczuk
description: A constellation of stories about travel, anatomy, and human restlessness—pilgrims, anatomists, tourists—in the novel that won the International Booker.

title: Drive Your Plow Over the Bones of the Dead
author: Olga Tokarczuk
description: An eccentric older woman in a Polish village investigates the mysterious deaths of local hunters, convinced the animals are taking revenge.

title: The Books of Jacob
author: Olga Tokarczuk
description: A Jewish mystic in 18th-century Poland becomes a false messiah, converting thousands through Poland, Turkey, and Austria in a massive historical epic.

title: Satantango
author: László Krasznahorkai
description: Inhabitants of a ruined Hungarian collective farm are drawn to a charismatic con man in circular, hypnotic prose that captures the entropy of a dying world.

title: The Melancholy of Resistance
author: László Krasznahorkai
description: A traveling circus brings a giant stuffed whale to a Hungarian town, and its arrival triggers mob violence and the collapse of the social order.

title: Seiobo There Below
author: László Krasznahorkai
description: Seventeen chapters on encounters with beauty—a Japanese shrine, a Baroque altarpiece, the Alhambra—each numbered in the Fibonacci sequence.

title: Baron Wenckheim's Homecoming
author: László Krasznahorkai
description: An elderly gambler returns to his Hungarian hometown and the entire town descends into chaos preparing for his arrival.

title: A Heart So White
author: Javier Marías
description: A newlywed interpreter becomes obsessed with his father's secret—why did his first wife kill herself on their honeymoon?—in Marías's breakthrough novel.

title: Tomorrow in the Battle Think on Me
author: Javier Marías
description: A woman dies in a man's arms before they can consummate their affair, and he must leave her body for her husband and child to find.

title: Your Face Tomorrow: Fever and Spear
author: Javier Marías
description: A Spanish academic is recruited by British intelligence for his ability to read people's faces and predict their future actions.

title: The Infatuations
author: Javier Marías
description: A woman investigates the murder of a man she observed daily at a café, falling in love with his best friend while suspecting the worst.

title: Empire of the Sun
author: J.G. Ballard
description: A British boy survives the Japanese occupation of Shanghai, interned in a prison camp where he adapts with unsettling resourcefulness.

title: The Crystal World
author: J.G. Ballard
description: A doctor in West Africa discovers a jungle that is turning to crystal, and the transformed landscape offers a seductive alternative to time and death.

title: Cocaine Nights
author: J.G. Ballard
description: A man visits a Spanish resort to help his brother who confessed to arson, and discovers the community's paradise depends on manufactured crime.

title: Super-Cannes
author: J.G. Ballard
description: A man moves to a corporate estate on the Riviera and discovers its executives maintain their productivity through organized psychopathic violence.

title: The Kindness of Women
author: J.G. Ballard
description: The sequel to Empire of the Sun follows Jim through postwar England, medical school, and the making of the Spielberg film about his childhood.

title: A History of the World in 10½ Chapters
author: Julian Barnes
description: From Noah's Ark told by a stowaway woodworm to nuclear apocalypse, ten-and-a-half chapters explore how we mythologize history to survive.

title: England, England
author: Julian Barnes
description: A tycoon builds a theme park replica of England on the Isle of Wight, and the replica becomes more popular than the real thing.

title: The Noise of Time
author: Julian Barnes
description: Shostakovich waits through the night for the secret police, and the novel traces how the composer survived Stalin by compromising everything except his music.

title: Nothing to Be Frightened Of
author: Julian Barnes
description: Barnes confronts his terror of death through family history, philosophy, and literature in a memoir that is also an essay on mortality.

title: Cross Channel
author: Julian Barnes
description: Ten stories of English people in France across seven centuries, from medieval knights to modern tourists.

title: The Lemon Table
author: Julian Barnes
description: Eleven stories about aging, love, and death—a Swedish composer's last affair, a couple's fruit-buying ritual, an elderly man's letters to a dead author.

title: Elizabeth Finch
author: Julian Barnes
description: A student becomes obsessed with his university lecturer and her thesis about Julian the Apostate as the last chance to save civilization from Christianity.

title: A Sentimental Education
author: Gustave Flaubert
description: A young man's fruitless pursuit of a married woman spans the revolutions of 1848, as personal and political ideals dissolve into mediocrity.

title: Salammbô
author: Gustave Flaubert
description: A Carthaginian priestess and a mercenary leader are caught in a war of annihilation after the First Punic War, told with hallucinatory violence.

title: Bouvard and Pécuchet
author: Gustave Flaubert
description: Two retired clerks systematically attempt to master every field of human knowledge and fail at all of them in Flaubert's unfinished satire of stupidity.

title: The Broom of the System
author: David Foster Wallace
description: A switchboard operator searches for her great-grandmother who has escaped from a nursing home, in Wallace's Wittgenstein-inflected debut novel.

title: The Pale King
author: David Foster Wallace
description: IRS employees at a Midwest processing center confront boredom as a form of heroism, in Wallace's unfinished posthumous novel about attention.

title: Oblivion
author: David Foster Wallace
description: Eight stories of suffocating consciousness—a man who may be dreaming he snores, a boy's visit to his mother's office, a focus group that dissolves reality.

title: Girl with Curious Hair
author: David Foster Wallace
description: Stories ranging from a Keith Jarrett concert to a Jeopardy contestant to Lyndon Johnson, establishing Wallace's range and obsessions.

title: We Should All Be Feminists
author: Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie
description: Adichie's TED talk expanded into a book-length essay defining feminism for the 21st century through personal anecdotes and sharp argument.

title: The Outsider
author: Richard Wright
description: A man fakes his own death in a subway accident and reinvents himself, only to discover that freedom without accountability leads to nihilism.

title: Dust Tracks on a Road
author: Zora Neale Hurston
description: Hurston's autobiography from her childhood in the all-Black town of Eatonville through her anthropological work and literary career.

title: Moses, Man of the Mountain
author: Zora Neale Hurston
description: The Exodus retold through African American folklore and hoodoo, with Moses as a conjure man liberating his people through magic.

title: Intermezzo
author: Sally Rooney
description: Two brothers grieving their father's death navigate very different love stories—one with a woman twenty years older, one with his ex and a new flame.

title: David and Goliath
author: Malcolm Gladwell
description: Gladwell argues that disadvantages can become advantages and the powerful are not as strong as they seem.

title: Talking to Strangers
author: Malcolm Gladwell
description: Why we are so bad at reading other people's intentions, from Neville Chamberlain meeting Hitler to police traffic stops that end in death.

title: The Rainbow
author: D.H. Lawrence
description: Three generations of a Midlands family chart the changing relationship between men and women from Victorian stability to modern restlessness.

title: The Birds and Other Stories
author: Daphne du Maurier
description: Eight stories including The Birds—a coastal village besieged by murderous flocks—and Don't Look Now, a couple haunted in Venice.

title: Frenchman's Creek
author: Daphne du Maurier
description: A bored Restoration-era noblewoman escapes to Cornwall and falls in love with a French pirate.

title: A Perfect Spy
author: John le Carré
description: A British intelligence officer disappears and his memoir reveals a life of betrayal shaped by his con-man father, in le Carré's most autobiographical novel.

title: The Night Manager
author: John le Carré
description: A hotel manager infiltrates the inner circle of a billionaire arms dealer, risking his life in a post-Cold War thriller.

title: A Most Wanted Man
author: John le Carré
description: A half-Chechen, half-Russian immigrant in Hamburg becomes a pawn in the post-9/11 intelligence game between agencies that claim to be allies.

title: The Pigeon Tunnel
author: John le Carré
description: Le Carré's memoir of the real people and events behind his fiction—his con-man father, meetings with Arafat, Rupert Murdoch, and assorted spies.

title: The High Window
author: Raymond Chandler
description: Marlowe is hired to find a stolen coin and discovers a web of blackmail, murder, and a terrified secretary who knows too much.

title: The Lady in the Lake
author: Raymond Chandler
description: Marlowe searches for a missing wife and finds a body in a mountain lake, but the dead woman isn't who everyone thinks she is.

title: The Glass Key
author: Dashiell Hammett
description: A political fixer investigates a senator's son's murder to protect his boss, enduring brutal beatings and double-crosses.

title: The Dain Curse
author: Dashiell Hammett
description: The Continental Op investigates a diamond theft that leads through murder, a cult, and a woman believed to carry a family curse.

title: Pines
author: Blake Crouch
description: A Secret Service agent wakes up in a perfect Idaho town where no one can leave and the truth about its existence is terrifying.

title: Isaac's Storm
author: Erik Larson
description: The true story of the 1900 Galveston hurricane that killed eight thousand people, told through the weatherman who failed to predict it.

title: The Splendid and the Vile
author: Erik Larson
description: Churchill's first year as prime minister during the Blitz, told through diaries and letters that capture the terror and resilience of 1940-41 London.

title: The Diamond Eye
author: Kate Quinn
description: A Soviet woman becomes one of the deadliest snipers in history during WWII and is sent to America on a PR tour where she befriends Eleanor Roosevelt.

title: Veronika Decides to Die
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A young woman attempts suicide and wakes in a mental hospital where she is told she has days to live, and for the first time begins truly living.

title: Eleven Minutes
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A Brazilian prostitute in Geneva searches for love and meaning through sex, pain, and spiritual awakening.

title: Commonwealth
author: Ann Patchett
description: A kiss at a christening party merges two families, and the six children shuttle between households over fifty years of love, resentment, and loss.

title: State of Wonder
author: Ann Patchett
description: A pharmaceutical researcher is sent to the Amazon to check on a colleague who died and a drug trial that could end menopause, and finds her dead mentor alive.

title: Run
author: Ann Patchett
description: A former Boston mayor and his two adopted Black sons are involved in a car accident that reveals a woman who has been watching them for years.

title: Look at Me
author: Jennifer Egan
description: A model whose face is reconstructed after a car accident finds she is unrecognizable, and the novel explores identity in an image-obsessed culture.

title: The Last American Man
author: Elizabeth Gilbert
description: The true story of Eustace Conway, who left home at seventeen to live in the Appalachian wilderness and built a utopian community.

title: The Kreutzer Sonata
author: Leo Tolstoy
description: A man on a train confesses to murdering his wife, telling the story of their marriage, his jealousy, and his rage at a musician.

title: Childhood, Boyhood, Youth
author: Leo Tolstoy
description: Tolstoy's autobiographical trilogy tracing a Russian nobleman's son from his mother's death through university in Moscow.

title: The First Man
author: Albert Camus
description: An autobiographical novel found in Camus's briefcase after his fatal car crash, about a man returning to Algeria to learn about the father he never knew.

title: Exile and the Kingdom
author: Albert Camus
description: Six stories of people in North Africa and Europe caught between belonging and alienation.

title: Narcissus and Goldmund
author: Hermann Hesse
description: A scholar-monk and a wandering artist represent two modes of being—intellect and experience—in a medieval friendship that spans a lifetime.

title: Demian
author: Hermann Hesse
description: A boy's adolescence is transformed by a mysterious older student who teaches him to embrace both the light and dark sides of his nature.

title: A Clergyman's Daughter
author: George Orwell
description: A vicar's daughter loses her memory and drifts through hop-picking, homelessness, and teaching before returning to a life she can no longer believe in.

title: Amerika
author: Franz Kafka
description: A teenager shipped to America by his parents drifts through strange jobs and encounters in an unfinished novel that reimagines the land of opportunity as surreal bureaucracy.

title: Finnegans Wake
author: James Joyce
description: A Dublin publican's dream rendered in a language Joyce invented from dozens of tongues, cycling through all of human history in a single night.

title: Jacob's Room
author: Virginia Woolf
description: A young man's life is pieced together from impressions, letters, and the rooms he has inhabited, leading to his death in World War I.

title: The Garden of Eden
author: Ernest Hemingway
description: A young couple on honeymoon in the south of France experiment with gender and sexuality as a third person enters their relationship.

title: Islands in the Stream
author: Ernest Hemingway
description: A painter on a Caribbean island navigates loss, his sons, and submarine hunting in WWII, in Hemingway's posthumous novel.

title: After Dark
author: Haruki Murakami
description: A single night in Tokyo follows a girl reading in a Denny's, her sister in a mysterious sleep, a Chinese prostitute, and a love hotel with a secret.

title: Colorless Tsukuru Tazaki and His Years of Pilgrimage
author: Haruki Murakami
description: A man who was inexplicably expelled from his tight-knit friend group in high school finally confronts each of them to learn why.

title: Killing Commendatore
author: Haruki Murakami
description: A portrait painter moves into a famous artist's empty house, discovers a hidden painting, and opens a pit in the woods that connects to another world.

title: Tar Baby
author: Toni Morrison
description: A Black model raised by a white family on a Caribbean island falls for a fugitive who represents everything she was taught to reject.

title: A Mercy
author: Toni Morrison
description: In 1680s America before slavery was codified by race, a farmer takes a slave girl as payment for a debt, and her mother's sacrifice reverberates.

title: Home
author: Toni Morrison
description: A Korean War veteran returns to the racist Georgia hometown he fled to rescue his sister from a sadistic doctor.

title: God Help the Child
author: Toni Morrison
description: A successful woman whose very dark skin made her mother withhold love confronts the trauma of her childhood and the lie she told as a girl.

title: The Trouble with Peace
author: Joe Abercrombie
description: Revolution and counter-revolution tear the Union apart as the new generation discovers that overthrowing the old order is easier than building something better.

title: The Wisdom of Crowds
author: Joe Abercrombie
description: The final reckoning as populism, violence, and betrayal consume every character, and the sins of the First Law world come due.

title: The Heroes
author: Joe Abercrombie
description: A three-day battle between the Union and the North told from both sides—soldiers, commanders, cowards, and the old warrior who just wants to go home.

title: Red Country
author: Joe Abercrombie
description: A woman and a notorious killer track the bandits who stole her children across a frontier inspired by the American West.

title: Sharp Ends
author: Joe Abercrombie
description: Thirteen short stories set across the First Law world, filling in backstories for beloved characters from Shev and Javre to young Glokta.

title: The Broken Eye
author: Brent Weeks
description: Gavin Guile is imprisoned and blinded while his son Kip trains at the Chromeria and the Color Prince advances.

title: The Way of Shadows
author: Brent Weeks
description: A guild rat apprentices himself to the city's most feared assassin—a wetboy who uses magic to kill—and must become a weapon to survive.

title: Shadow's Edge
author: Brent Weeks
description: Kylar has retired from killing but a tyrant's invasion forces him to take up the black ka'kari one more time.

title: Beyond the Shadows
author: Brent Weeks
description: The final battle against the Godking as Kylar must choose between his love and his duty as the Night Angel.

title: Shadow Games
author: Glen Cook
description: The Company marches south toward Khatovar, the land of its origin, and Lady must rebuild her power from nothing.

title: Dreams of Steel
author: Glen Cook
description: Lady builds an army to reconquer the south while Croaker is lost, in the darkest and most personal Black Company novel.

title: Bleak Seasons
author: Glen Cook
description: Murgen is trapped in a besieged city while his consciousness slides between past and present in the Company's most experimental narrative.

title: She Is the Darkness
author: Glen Cook
description: The Company pushes toward its final confrontation with the Shadowmaster while Croaker's obsession with Khatovar drives them forward.

title: Water Sleeps
author: Glen Cook
description: A small remnant of the Company plots to free their trapped comrades from beneath the earth in a patient, methodical campaign.

title: Soldiers Live
author: Glen Cook
description: The final Black Company novel as the survivors return to Khatovar and the Annalist closes the last book.

title: The Lord of Demons
author: Evan Winter
description: Tau's power grows as the war escalates and the true nature of the demons he has been summoning becomes terrifyingly clear.

title: The Coming of Conan the Cimmerian
author: Robert E. Howard
description: The first thirteen Conan stories in their original versions—a barbarian wanders the Hyborian Age, fighting sorcerers, monsters, and civilized men who are worse.

title: The Bloody Crown of Conan
author: Robert E. Howard
description: Three novellas: The People of the Black Circle, The Hour of the Dragon, and A Witch Shall Be Born—Conan as king and captive.

title: The Conquering Sword of Conan
author: Robert E. Howard
description: The final Conan stories including Red Nails and Beyond the Black River—Howard's last and most mature sword-and-sorcery fiction.

title: Swords and Deviltry
author: Fritz Leiber
description: The origin stories of Fafhrd the barbarian and the Gray Mouser the thief—how they met and became the most famous duo in fantasy.

title: Swords Against Death
author: Fritz Leiber
description: Ten adventures of the duo across the world of Nehwon, from the thieves' guild of Lankhmar to the frozen north.

title: Swords in the Mist
author: Fritz Leiber
description: The duo faces supernatural threats including Lean Times in Lankhmar, where they take opposite sides of a religious war.

title: Swords Against Wizardry
author: Fritz Leiber
description: Four novellas pitting Fafhrd and the Mouser against sorcerers, including the mountain-climbing classic Stardock.

title: The Swords of Lankhmar
author: Fritz Leiber
description: The Mouser is shrunk to rat-size and enters the rat civilization beneath Lankhmar while Fafhrd defends the city above.

title: Nevernight
author: Jay Kristoff
description: A girl who can control shadows enrolls in a school for assassins, seeking revenge against the men who destroyed her family.

title: Godsgrave
author: Jay Kristoff
description: Mia infiltrates a gladiatorial arena to reach her target, fighting her way up from the sands.

title: Darkdawn
author: Jay Kristoff
description: Mia's final revenge and the revelation of what she truly is—a weapon forged by the gods themselves.

title: Empire of the Vampire
author: Jay Kristoff
description: The last silversaint tells his life story to a vampire captor—how he hunted the undead for decades as humanity's light slowly died.

title: Empire of the Damned
author: Jay Kristoff
description: Gabriel continues his story as the war against vampires reaches its most desperate hour.

title: Malice
author: John Gwynne
description: A young warrior discovers he may be the prophesied champion in a world where angels fell and their war continues through mortal proxies.

title: Valour
author: John Gwynne
description: War erupts across the Banished Lands as Corban gathers allies and enemies reveal their true allegiances.

title: Ruin
author: John Gwynne
description: The forces of light and dark converge as prophecy accelerates toward the final battle.

title: Wrath
author: John Gwynne
description: The final battle between the God-War's champions as the Faithful and the Fallen clash for the last time.

title: A Time of Dread
author: John Gwynne
description: A new generation in the Banished Lands faces the return of the Kadoshim demons over a hundred years after the God-War.

title: A Time of Blood
author: John Gwynne
description: The war against the Kadoshim intensifies as old prophecies repeat and new heroes rise.

title: A Time of Courage
author: John Gwynne
description: The final confrontation with the Kadoshim as every thread from both series converges.

title: Traitor's Blade
author: Sebastien de Castell
description: Three traveling magistrates—swordsmen without a country—fight to uphold the law in a kingdom that has outlawed justice.

title: Knight's Shadow
author: Sebastien de Castell
description: Falcio must protect a young girl who may be the rightful heir while the conspiracy against the Greatcoats deepens.

title: Saint's Blood
author: Sebastien de Castell
description: Someone is killing the saints of the kingdom, stripping away the divine protections that hold the realm together.

title: Tyrant's Throne
author: Sebastien de Castell
description: Falcio must put the rightful queen on the throne while every duke, knight, and assassin in the country tries to stop him.

title: The Thousand Names
author: Django Wexler
description: A new colonel arrives to lead a demoralized colonial regiment and turns out to be a sorcerer pursuing an ancient magical text.

title: The Shadow Throne
author: Django Wexler
description: Revolution comes home as Winter and Marcus fight to protect a young queen while supernatural forces gather.

title: The Price of Valor
author: Django Wexler
description: The revolution fractures into factions while an ancient order of sorcerers threatens everything the soldiers have fought for.

title: She Who Became the Sun
author: Shelley Parker-Chan
description: A peasant girl steals her dead brother's identity and destiny, rising from nothing to challenge the Mongol Yuan dynasty in a reimagined founding of the Ming.

title: He Who Drowned the World
author: Shelley Parker-Chan
description: Zhu Yuanzhang fights for the throne against rival warlords and the eunuch general who wants to reshape the world through suffering.

title: Daughter of the Empire
author: Raymond E. Feist
description: A young woman inherits her father's title in the Tsurani Empire and must navigate deadly political games to survive. Co-written with Janny Wurts.

title: Servant of the Empire
author: Raymond E. Feist
description: Mara of the Acoma expands her power through unconventional alliances, including a barbarian slave from the world across the Rift.

title: Mistress of the Empire
author: Raymond E. Feist
description: Mara challenges the most powerful institutions of the Tsurani Empire—the Assembly of Magicians and the Great Game itself.

title: Prince of the Blood
author: Raymond E. Feist
description: The twin sons of Prince Arutha travel to the enemy empire of Kesh and are caught in a succession crisis.

title: Shadow of a Dark Queen
author: Raymond E. Feist
description: A young man condemned to death is recruited for a suicide mission behind enemy lines as a new demon-backed invasion threatens Midkemia.

title: Empire of Grass
author: Tad Williams
description: The High Throne faces threats on every border while Simon and Miriamele's marriage strains and the Norns march south.

series.name: The Last King of Osten Ard

title: Into the Narrowdark
author: Tad Williams
description: Alliances crumble as the Norn Queen's plan becomes clear and the heroes are scattered across a continent at war.

series.name: The Last King of Osten Ard

title: The Republic of Thieves
author: Scott Lynch
description: Locke and Jean are pitted against Locke's childhood love in a political election, while flashbacks show the Gentlemen Bastards' origins in theater.

title: Red Sister
author: Mark Lawrence
description: A girl who killed a warrior twice her size is taken to a convent of warrior nuns on a dying world where the sun barely warms the equator.

title: Grey Sister
author: Mark Lawrence
description: Nona's enemies within and outside the convent close in as she masters the four martial paths and her own mixed blood.

title: Holy Sister
author: Mark Lawrence
description: The Scithrowl army invades and Nona must unite the warring factions of the empire to save her convent and her world.

title: The Crown Tower
author: Michael J. Sullivan
description: The prequel to Riyria Revelations showing how Royce and Hadrian first met and reluctantly became partners.

title: The Rose and the Thorn
author: Michael J. Sullivan
description: Royce and Hadrian's early partnership as they're hired for a job that puts them between a princess and an assassin.

title: Age of Myth
author: Michael J. Sullivan
description: In the prehistoric past of Sullivan's world, humans are ruled by god-like elves until a man kills one and shatters the myth of their divinity.

series.name: The Legends of the First Empire

title: The Mime Order
author: Samantha Shannon
description: Paige escapes the penal colony and returns to London's criminal underworld to challenge the ruling Underlord.

title: The Song Rising
author: Samantha Shannon
description: Paige leads the syndicate as Scion expands its surveillance state and the Rephaim tighten their grip.

title: Kraken
author: China Miéville
description: A giant squid specimen vanishes from the Natural History Museum and a curator is plunged into London's occult underworld of squid-worshipping cults.

title: Un Lun Dun
author: China Miéville
description: A girl falls into an alternate London built from the city's waste and forgotten things, where she's supposed to be the chosen one—but she's not.

title: His Majesty's Dragon
author: Naomi Novik
description: A British naval captain during the Napoleonic Wars captures a dragon egg and becomes an aviator, bonding with the dragon Temeraire.

title: Throne of Jade
author: Naomi Novik
description: China demands Temeraire's return as a stolen Celestial dragon, and Laurence must voyage east to negotiate.

title: Black Powder War
author: Naomi Novik
description: Laurence and Temeraire cross overland from China to Europe through the Ottoman Empire and arrive to face Napoleon's invasion of Prussia.

title: The Magicians' Guild
author: Trudi Canavan
description: A slum girl accidentally uses magic during the annual purge and must be found before her untrained power kills her or someone else.

title: The Novice
author: Trudi Canavan
description: Sonea enters the Magicians' Guild as the first lower-class student and faces bullying and a mystery about the High Lord's forbidden magic.

title: The High Lord
author: Trudi Canavan
description: Sonea discovers the High Lord is practicing black magic but learns his reasons may justify the forbidden power—if the Guild can survive the truth.

title: Dark Moon
author: David Gemmell
description: A dying wizard, a one-eyed swordsman, and a duke's daughter face an army of demonic warriors crossing through a magical pearl.

title: Sword in the Storm
author: David Gemmell
description: A young Celtic warrior battles to prove himself while a dark magic threatens his people in Gemmell's pseudo-historical Rigante series.

title: Midnight Falcon
author: David Gemmell
description: A Rigante fighter rises through the Roman-like Stone army's gladiatorial ranks while his people plot rebellion.

title: Penric's Demon
author: Lois McMaster Bujold
description: A young lord accidentally acquires a twelve-souled temple demon on his way to his betrothal, beginning an unlikely partnership.

title: Bloody Rose
author: Nicholas Eames
description: A new band of mercenaries led by the daughter of a legendary warrior takes on the most dangerous contract in the world.

title: Ilium
author: Dan Simmons
description: On a terraformed Mars, the Trojan War is being reenacted by post-humans while a robot from Jupiter's moons reads Proust and investigates the mystery.

title: Olympos
author: Dan Simmons
description: The Trojan War spirals out of control as time travel, quantum physics, and Shakespeare's Caliban converge on the fate of multiple worlds.

title: So Long, and Thanks for All the Fish
author: Douglas Adams
description: Arthur Dent returns to an inexplicably restored Earth and falls in love with a woman who knows why the dolphins left.

series.name: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy

title: Mostly Harmless
author: Douglas Adams
description: Arthur settles on a primitive planet as a sandwich maker while Ford discovers the Guide has been taken over by a sinister new edition from a parallel universe.

series.name: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy

title: The Futurological Congress
author: Stanisław Lem
description: Ijon Tichy attends a conference where hallucinogenic agents are released and reality dissolves into nested illusions of a drugged utopia.

title: The Invincible
author: Stanisław Lem
description: A space cruiser investigates a planet where a previous ship's crew died, and discovers an ecosystem of self-evolving micromachines.

title: The Star Diaries
author: Stanisław Lem
description: The voyages of Ijon Tichy—a cosmic Gulliver—through space, time, and philosophical paradox in Lem's most comic work.

title: A Perfect Vacuum
author: Stanisław Lem
description: Reviews of nonexistent books—each one a compressed novel—in Lem's most dazzling exercise in imaginary literature.

title: Fiasco
author: Stanisław Lem
description: A human expedition to contact an alien civilization goes catastrophically wrong because neither species can understand the other's intentions.

author: Kim Stanley Robinson
description: Three hundred years from now, humanity has colonized the solar system, and a mystery connects a designer of living asteroids to political upheaval across the planets.

title: Aurora
author: Kim Stanley Robinson
description: A generation ship approaches its target star after 170 years, and the colonists discover the planet is hostile—forcing a choice between staying and turning back.

title: New York 2140
author: Kim Stanley Robinson
description: Fifty feet of sea-level rise has turned Manhattan into a supervenice, and a cast of New Yorkers navigates finance, politics, and survival in the flooded city.

title: Wild Seed
author: Octavia Butler
description: An immortal shapeshifter and an immortal body-jumper clash across centuries, from 17th-century Africa to colonial America, in a battle over breeding and control.

title: Mind of My Mind
author: Octavia Butler
description: The body-jumper Doro has bred telepaths for millennia, but his latest creation—a young woman who can link minds into a network—may be powerful enough to destroy him.

title: Fledgling
author: Octavia Butler
description: A young Black woman with amnesia discovers she is a genetically modified vampire in a community where symbiosis between vampires and humans is under attack.

title: Binti
author: Nnedi Okorafor
description: A Himba girl is the first of her people accepted to an interstellar university, but her ship is attacked by aliens and she must use her cultural knowledge to survive.

title: Who Fears Death
author: Nnedi Okorafor
description: A woman born of rape in post-apocalyptic Africa discovers she has magical powers and sets out to confront the sorcerer who ordered her mother's violation.

title: Lagoon
author: Nnedi Okorafor
description: Aliens land in the waters off Lagos, Nigeria, and first contact is filtered through the chaotic, vibrant, myth-rich culture of the city.

title: Akata Witch
author: Nnedi Okorafor
description: A Nigerian-American albino girl discovers she has magical powers and enters the world of the Leopard People, where African mythology is real.

title: Ninefox Gambit
author: Yoon Ha Lee
description: A disgraced captain must use an undead traitor general's tactical genius to retake a fortress, but the general's madness may be part of his plan.

title: Raven Stratagem
author: Yoon Ha Lee
description: The undead general seizes a fleet and nobody knows whether he's saving the empire or destroying it.

title: Revenant Gun
author: Yoon Ha Lee
description: The general is resurrected with no memory of his crimes and must decide who he wants to be while the empire fractures.

title: The Calculating Stars
author: Mary Robinette Kowal
description: A meteorite destroys the eastern seaboard in 1952 and a mathematician fights to be included in the space program that may be humanity's only hope.

title: The Fated Sky
author: Mary Robinette Kowal
description: The first manned mission to Mars faces mechanical failures, racial tension, and the question of who gets saved when not everyone can.

title: The Light Brigade
author: Kameron Hurley
description: Soldiers are broken down into light and beamed to battlefields, but one soldier is experiencing the war out of order and realizes the timeline has been altered.

title: The Stars Are Legion
author: Kameron Hurley
description: Living world-ships orbit a dying star, and a woman who keeps losing her memory must fight her way to the center of a ship to save them all.

title: God's War
author: Kameron Hurley
description: A former government assassin on a planet of bug-based technology hunts a deserter while navigating a holy war between two nations.

title: Little Brother
author: Cory Doctorow
description: A seventeen-year-old in San Francisco is detained by Homeland Security after a terrorist attack and uses technology to fight back against the surveillance state.

title: Walkaway
author: Cory Doctorow
description: People abandon mainstream society to build a post-scarcity commune, and when they crack the secret of uploading consciousness, the billionaires come for them.

title: Down and Out in the Magic Kingdom
author: Cory Doctorow
description: In a post-scarcity future where death is reversible, a man living in Disney World fights to preserve a classic ride against a hostile faction.

title: All the Birds in the Sky
author: Charlie Jane Anders
description: A witch and a tech genius who were childhood friends reconnect as adults as the world ends, and their two approaches—magic and science—offer competing solutions.

title: The City in the Middle of the Night
author: Charlie Jane Anders
description: On a tidally locked planet, a student exiled to the frozen dark side bonds with the alien creatures there and discovers a new way to live.

title: Her Smoke Rose Up Forever
author: James Tiptree Jr.
description: The definitive collection of Tiptree's stories—The Women Men Don't See, Houston Houston Do You Read?, The Screwfly Solution—fierce, feminist, and decades ahead of their time.

title: Brightness Falls from the Air
author: James Tiptree Jr.
description: Tourists gather on a planet to watch the light from a nova reach them, but among the group are a killer, a torturer, and victims of an atrocity connected to the aliens they came to see.

title: The Rediscovery of Man
author: Cordwainer Smith
description: The complete short fiction of Cordwainer Smith—Scanners Live in Vain, The Ballad of Lost C'mell, Alpha Ralpha Boulevard—set in his Instrumentality of Mankind future history.

title: Norstrilia
author: Cordwainer Smith
description: The richest boy on the richest planet buys Earth on a whim and must escape the consequences, in Smith's only novel.

title: The Engines of God
author: Jack McDevitt
description: Mysterious alien monuments are found on multiple dead worlds, and an archaeologist races to decode them before humanity terraforms the last site.

title: Ancient Shores
author: Jack McDevitt
description: A farmer in North Dakota unearths a boat made of impossible material, and the discovery leads to a portal that changes everything.

title: The Shockwave Rider
author: John Brunner
description: A fugitive uses hacking skills to evade a surveillance state in the novel that invented the concept of the computer worm.

title: The Sheep Look Up
author: John Brunner
description: America drowns in pollution, poisoned food, and environmental collapse while an activist is blamed for the disasters he predicted.

title: The Jagged Orbit
author: John Brunner
description: A segregated near-future America is armed to the teeth by a weapons monopoly that profits from racial paranoia.

title: The Accidental Time Machine
author: Joe Haldeman
description: A graduate student accidentally builds a time machine that only goes forward, each jump multiplied by twelve, hurtling through increasingly strange futures.

title: Rainbows End
author: Vernor Vinge
description: A poet cured of Alzheimer's in a near-future of augmented reality must navigate a world where reality itself is layered with digital overlays.

title: The Wizard Knight
author: Gene Wolfe
description: A boy from our world is transported to a Norse-inspired realm of seven nested levels and must become a knight despite being trapped in a man's body with a child's mind.

title: Pirate Freedom
author: Gene Wolfe
description: A young man leaves a monastery and finds himself in the 17th-century Caribbean, becoming a pirate captain in a narrative that may or may not be fantasy.

title: Starfish
author: Peter Watts
description: Psychologically damaged workers are modified to live on the ocean floor at a geothermal rift, and the isolation and pressure bring out something dangerous.

title: The Doubt Factory
author: Paolo Bacigalupi
description: A teenage girl discovers her father's PR firm specializes in suppressing scientific evidence that products are killing people.

title: The Fall
author: Neal Stephenson
description: A tech mogul's brain is uploaded after death and he awakens as a god in a digital afterlife, while the real world fragments into bubbles of alternative truth.

title: Termination Shock
author: Neal Stephenson
description: A Texas billionaire launches a unilateral geoengineering project by shooting sulfur into the atmosphere, triggering a geopolitical crisis.

title: Blue Remembered Earth
author: Alastair Reynolds
description: In a near-future Africa-led space age, two siblings follow their grandmother's clues across the solar system to find what she hid on the Moon and Mars.

title: On the Steel Breeze
author: Alastair Reynolds
description: Generation ships approach a new star system while an AI intelligence and its human allies battle over humanity's future direction.

title: A Prayer for the Crown-Shy
author: Becky Chambers
description: The tea monk and the robot leave the wilderness and travel through human settlements, asking everyone what they need.

title: The Last Colony
author: John Scalzi
description: John Perry is appointed to lead a new colony and discovers it's a pawn in a galactic game between the Colonial Union and an alien alliance.

title: The Collapsing Empire
author: John Scalzi
description: The Flow—the network of interdimensional pathways connecting human colonies—is collapsing, and a scientist and an empress race to save civilization.

title: Halting State
author: Charles Stross
description: A bank robbery inside a massive multiplayer game turns out to be a cover for something far more dangerous involving espionage and quantum computing.

title: Neptune's Brood
author: Charles Stross
description: A post-human historian searches for her missing sister across the galaxy, following a trail of interstellar debt and financial fraud.

title: The Three Stigmata of Palmer Eldritch
author: Philip K. Dick
description: Colonists on Mars use a drug to share hallucinations, but a new drug offers something more—or traps them in the mind of its distributor forever.

title: Flow My Tears, the Policeman Said
author: Philip K. Dick
description: A celebrity wakes up in a world where no one has ever heard of him and no record of him exists, in a police state that tracks everyone.

title: Martian Time-Slip
author: Philip K. Dick
description: On a colonized Mars, a repairman, a union leader, and an autistic boy are connected by a time-slipping reality that may grant visions of the future.

title: The Penultimate Truth
author: Philip K. Dick
description: Millions live underground believing a nuclear war rages above, but the surface is actually a paradise controlled by the elites who keep them enslaved.

title: The Songs of Distant Earth
author: Arthur C. Clarke
description: A starship fleeing the destruction of Earth stops at a colony settled centuries earlier by seedships, and the two populations briefly connect before the ship moves on.

title: Against the Fall of Night
author: Arthur C. Clarke
description: An early version of The City and the Stars: a restless boy in a far-future sealed city ventures into the desert and finds the ruins of a greater civilization.

title: The First Men in the Moon
author: H.G. Wells
description: Two men use an anti-gravity substance to reach the Moon and discover a civilization of insect-like Selenites living beneath the surface.

title: Heechee Rendezvous
author: Frederik Pohl
description: Broadhead finally meets the Heechee—who have been hiding from something much worse—and the universe's deadliest threat is revealed.

title: The Puppet Masters
author: Robert A. Heinlein
description: Alien parasites that attach to human spines and control their minds invade the American Midwest, and an intelligence agent must stop them.

title: Methuselah's Children
author: Robert A. Heinlein
description: A secret group of long-lived humans is exposed and must flee Earth, encountering alien civilizations that make them question their own values.

title: Borne
author: Jeff VanderMeer
description: A scavenger in a ruined city finds a mysterious biotech creature and raises it, not knowing whether Borne is a pet, a weapon, or something else entirely.

title: Dead Astronauts
author: Jeff VanderMeer
description: Three characters cycle through versions of a devastated city, trying to destroy the Company that created the biotech horrors of Borne.

title: Fallen Dragon
author: Peter F. Hamilton
description: A corporate mercenary invading a colony planet for resources discovers the planet harbors an alien artifact that could change everything.

title: Great North Road
author: Peter F. Hamilton
description: A murder in Newcastle connects to an alien threat on a colony world reached through a gateway, and a detective and a military expedition converge on the truth.

title: Queen of Angels
author: Greg Bear
description: A nanotechnology-transformed future LA investigates a poet who murdered eight people, while on another planet an AI achieves consciousness.

title: Moving Mars
author: Greg Bear
description: A young Martian politician navigates independence from Earth while a physicist discovers a way to teleport entire planets.

title: The Southern Book Club's Guide to Slaying Vampires
author: Grady Hendrix
description: A suburban book club discovers their charming new neighbor is a vampire preying on the neighborhood's most vulnerable, and no one believes the housewives.

title: My Best Friend's Exorcism
author: Grady Hendrix
description: A teenage girl's best friend comes back from a night in the woods possessed by a demon, and 1980s friendship is the only weapon against it.

title: The Final Girl Support Group
author: Grady Hendrix
description: The real women who inspired slasher movies meet in a support group, and when someone starts killing them off, the final girls must fight back.

title: How to Sell a Haunted House
author: Grady Hendrix
description: Two siblings return home to sell their dead mother's house and discover her puppet collection is alive and the house doesn't want to be sold.

title: The Only Good Indians
author: Stephen Graham Jones
description: Four Native American men are hunted by an entity connected to an elk they killed on tribal land years ago, and it's picking them off one by one.

title: My Heart Is a Chainsaw
author: Stephen Graham Jones
description: A slasher-obsessed outcast in a small lake town recognizes the signs that a real slasher cycle is beginning and tries to survive by the rules of the genre.

title: Don't Fear the Reaper
author: Stephen Graham Jones
description: The sequel as a blizzard traps the town and a new killing spree begins, and Jade must face the slasher she thought she'd escaped.

title: Mongrels
author: Stephen Graham Jones
description: A boy growing up in a family of werewolves in the rural South waits to find out if he'll turn, in a coming-of-age horror about being different.

title: Bird Box
author: Josh Malerman
description: A mother and two children navigate a river blindfolded because whatever is outside—if you see it, you go insane and kill yourself.

title: Malorie
author: Josh Malerman
description: The sequel: the children are now teenagers and Malorie must face the creatures again when word comes of a safe haven.

title: Goblin
author: Josh Malerman
description: Six novellas set in the same eerie town on the same night—each house on the street hiding a different kind of terror.

title: Swan Song
author: Robert McCammon
description: After nuclear war, a young girl with the power to heal and an embodiment of evil cross a devastated America in a battle for humanity's soul.

title: Boy's Life
author: Robert McCammon
description: A boy in 1960s Alabama witnesses a murder and spends the year solving it while the magic and horror of small-town Southern life unfold around him.

title: The Wolf's Hour
author: Robert McCammon
description: A werewolf works as a British spy in World War II, parachuting into Nazi-occupied Paris on a mission to stop D-Day from failing.

title: Mine
author: Robert McCammon
description: A former 1960s radical kidnaps a baby and a mother pursues her across the country in a relentless thriller-horror hybrid.

title: The Hunger
author: Alma Katsu
description: The Donner Party's doomed journey west is reimagined with a supernatural evil stalking the wagon train before they ever reach the mountains.

title: The Deep
author: Alma Katsu
description: A woman who survived the Titanic serves as a nurse on the Britannic during WWI, where the ghosts of the drowned follow her.

title: The Fervor
author: Alma Katsu
description: A Japanese-American woman in a WWII internment camp fights a supernatural plague while the camp guards blame the inmates.

title: The Girl Next Door
author: Jack Ketchum
description: Based on the true story of Sylvia Likens: two orphaned girls are tortured by their caretaker and the neighborhood children, narrated by a boy who watches and does nothing.

title: Off Season
author: Jack Ketchum
description: Vacationers in rural Maine are hunted by a clan of feral cannibals in one of the most brutal horror novels ever written.

title: The Cellar
author: Richard Laymon
description: A woman flees her abusive ex-husband to a small town where the local tourist attraction—Beast House—contains real monsters.

title: Night in the Lonesome October
author: Richard Laymon
description: A college student roaming his town at night encounters increasingly disturbing situations that may be connected to a serial killer.

title: The Store
author: Bentley Little
description: A megastore opens in a small Arizona town and systematically destroys every local business, institution, and person who resists.

title: The Mailman
author: Bentley Little
description: A new mailman arrives in a small town and everyone starts receiving letters that destroy their lives—blackmail, pornography, secrets no one should know.

title: Thornhedge
author: T. Kingfisher
description: A retelling of Sleeping Beauty from the perspective of the fairy godmother who built the thorns—not to trap the princess, but to protect the world from her.

title: A House with Good Bones
author: T. Kingfisher
description: An entomologist visits her mother's house and finds her cheerful mom acting strange, the yard full of dead roses, and vultures circling.

title: The Other Boleyn Girl
author: Philippa Gregory
description: Anne Boleyn's sister Mary narrates the rivalry between the two Boleyn girls for Henry VIII's attention and the crown.

title: The White Queen
author: Philippa Gregory
