description: Elizabeth Woodville, the commoner who married Edward IV, fights for her sons' lives during the Wars of the Roses.

title: The Red Queen
author: Philippa Gregory
description: Margaret Beaufort schemes for decades to put her son Henry Tudor on the throne, sacrificing everything for the Tudor dynasty.

title: The Boleyn Inheritance
author: Philippa Gregory
description: Three women connected to Henry VIII—Anne of Cleves, Katherine Howard, and Jane Boleyn—narrate their own destruction.

title: The Constant Princess
author: Philippa Gregory
description: Katherine of Aragon's journey from a Spanish princess to Henry VIII's first wife, determined to hold her crown.

title: Sarum
author: Edward Rutherfurd
description: Five families in the Salisbury area from the Ice Age through the 20th century, spanning ten thousand years of English history.

title: London
author: Edward Rutherfurd
description: Two thousand years of London from Roman times through the Blitz, told through families whose fates intertwine with the city's.

title: Paris
author: Edward Rutherfurd
description: Eight centuries of Paris through interconnected families—from Notre-Dame's construction through Impressionism to the Occupation.

title: Genghis: Birth of an Empire
author: Conn Iggulden
description: The childhood and rise of Temüjin from an abandoned boy to the leader who united the Mongol tribes.

title: Genghis: Lords of the Bow
author: Conn Iggulden
description: Genghis Khan turns his united horde against the vast Xi Xia and Jin empires of China.

title: Genghis: Bones of the Hills
author: Conn Iggulden
description: Genghis invades the Khwarezmid Empire and destroys its cities in the most devastating military campaign of the medieval world.

title: Gods and Generals
author: Jeff Shaara
description: The prequel to The Killer Angels follows Lee, Jackson, Hancock, and Chamberlain from the start of the Civil War through Fredericksburg.

title: The Last Full Measure
author: Jeff Shaara
description: From Gettysburg to Appomattox: Grant and Lee, Sherman and Johnston, as the war grinds to its bloody conclusion.

title: The Killer Angels
author: Michael Shaara
description: The Battle of Gettysburg told through the eyes of Lee, Longstreet, Chamberlain, and Buford in the Pulitzer-winning novel that inspired the film.

title: The Rising Tide
author: Jeff Shaara
description: WWII from North Africa through Sicily as Eisenhower, Rommel, and Patton fight for control of the Mediterranean.

title: The Sunne in Splendour
author: Sharon Kay Penman
description: Richard III recast not as Shakespeare's villain but as a loyal brother and capable king destroyed by Tudor propaganda.

title: Here Be Dragons
author: Sharon Kay Penman
description: The marriage between the Welsh prince Llywelyn and King John's illegitimate daughter, caught between two nations at war.

title: When Christ and His Saints Slept
author: Sharon Kay Penman
description: The civil war between Stephen and Maude for the English throne, two decades of anarchy narrated through their partisans and enemies.

title: Hawaii
author: James Michener
description: The islands from volcanic creation through Polynesian settlement, missionary arrival, and statehood in Michener's sprawling multi-generational epic.

title: Centennial
author: James Michener
description: A Colorado town from prehistoric times through the 1970s, spanning Native Americans, trappers, cattlemen, farmers, and developers.

title: The Source
author: James Michener
description: An archaeological dig in Israel uncovers layers of civilization from prehistoric times through the founding of the modern state.

title: Chesapeake
author: James Michener
description: Four centuries of Maryland's Eastern Shore through families who settle, farm, fish, and fight from colonial times to the present.

title: Stalingrad
author: Antony Beevor
description: The definitive account of the Battle of Stalingrad—the turning point of WWII—from both German and Soviet perspectives.

title: D-Day
author: Antony Beevor
description: The invasion of Normandy told from all sides—Allied, German, and French civilian—in Beevor's signature narrative history style.

title: The Fall of Berlin 1945
author: Antony Beevor
description: The Soviet assault on Berlin and the final days of the Third Reich, including the mass atrocities committed during the conquest.

title: Cinder
author: Marissa Meyer
description: Cinderella is a cyborg mechanic in futuristic Beijing, and the ball she crashes is a political event where a lunar queen threatens Earth.

title: Scarlet
author: Marissa Meyer
description: A French girl searches for her missing grandmother and discovers a connection to the lunar threat, retelling Little Red Riding Hood.

title: Cress
author: Marissa Meyer
description: A Rapunzel figure—a hacker trapped on a satellite—joins the rebellion against the Lunar Queen.

title: Winter
author: Marissa Meyer
description: A lunar princess who refuses to use her mind-control powers joins the revolution to overthrow her stepmother the queen.

title: Shatter Me
author: Tahereh Mafi
description: A girl whose touch is lethal is imprisoned by a totalitarian regime that wants to weaponize her, until a rebel faction offers her a different path.

title: Unravel Me
author: Tahereh Mafi
description: Juliette joins the resistance and trains to control her powers while the enemy regime's leader pursues her relentlessly.

title: Ignite Me
author: Tahereh Mafi
description: Juliette fully embraces her power and leads the rebellion against the Reestablishment.

title: The Raven Boys
author: Maggie Stiefvater
description: A girl destined to kill her true love with a kiss joins four boys from a nearby prep school searching for a dead Welsh king on a ley line in Virginia.

title: The Dream Thieves
author: Maggie Stiefvater
description: Ronan can pull objects from his dreams into reality, and someone else knows his secret and will kill to control it.

title: Blue Lily, Lily Blue
author: Maggie Stiefvater
description: Blue's mother has vanished and the search for Glendower leads underground while new supernatural threats emerge.

title: The Raven King
author: Maggie Stiefvater
description: The search for Glendower reaches its conclusion as every character faces the cost of the quest and the prophecy comes true.

title: The Scorpio Races
author: Maggie Stiefvater
description: Every autumn, water horses emerge from the sea and the island's residents race them—a girl on a small mare against the defending champion on the most dangerous horse alive.

title: Daughter of Smoke & Bone
author: Laini Taylor
description: An art student in Prague discovers her foster father—a chimera who collects teeth—is connected to a war between angels and monsters in another world.

title: Days of Blood & Starlight
author: Laini Taylor
description: Karou struggles to resurrect the chimera army while Akiva tries to change his people from within, and the war consumes them both.

title: Dreams of Gods & Monsters
author: Laini Taylor
description: Angels and chimera must unite against a greater threat as the portals between worlds open and an ancient enemy returns.

title: Strange the Dreamer
author: Laini Taylor
description: A librarian obsessed with a mythical lost city discovers it is real and travels there to find it haunted by the children of murdered gods.

title: Muse of Nightmares
author: Laini Taylor
description: The godspawn and the humans of Weep must find peace while an ancient threat from beyond the sky approaches.

title: Just Listen
author: Sarah Dessen
description: A former model hiding a secret about the night that ruined her friendships slowly opens up to an angry music-obsessed boy.

title: The Truth About Forever
author: Sarah Dessen
description: A perfectionist grieving her father's death takes a job with a chaotic catering company and learns to let go.

title: Along for the Ride
author: Sarah Dessen
description: An insomniac girl spends the summer in a beach town and bonds with a boy who also can't sleep, exploring everything she missed by being the perfect student.

title: Saint Anything
author: Sarah Dessen
description: A girl overshadowed by her family's focus on her criminal brother finds herself and a new family at a pizza shop.

title: Carry On
author: Rainbow Rowell
description: The Chosen One at a magical school must defeat the Insidious Humdrum while falling for his vampire roommate, in a loving subversion of Harry Potter.

title: Wayward Son
author: Rainbow Rowell
description: Simon, Baz, and Penny road-trip across America and discover the American magical world is very different—and more dangerous—than Britain's.

title: History Is All You Left Me
author: Adam Silvera
description: A boy grieving his ex-boyfriend's death bonds with his ex's new boyfriend as they share memories and navigate loss together.

title: Nothing More to Tell
author: Karen M. McManus
description: A girl joins her school's true-crime TV show to investigate a teacher's murder that she has a personal connection to.

title: You'll Be the Death of Me
author: Karen M. McManus
description: Three former friends skip school together and witness a classmate's dead body, reigniting old friendships and suspicions.

title: The Unhoneymooners
author: Christina Lauren
description: A woman takes her twin sister's honeymoon trip with the best man she despises, and they must pretend to be the newlyweds.

title: In a Holidaze
author: Christina Lauren
description: A woman wishes for a do-over of the holiday where everything went wrong, and wakes up reliving the vacation from the start.

title: Something Wilder
author: Christina Lauren
description: A trail guide's ex shows up on her adventure tour, and a treasure hunt forces them to confront what went wrong.

title: The True Love Experiment
author: Christina Lauren
description: A romance novelist who's given up on love agrees to star in a dating show produced by a man who believes in data over feelings.

title: It Happened One Summer
author: Tessa Bailey
description: A party girl is cut off by her stepfather and sent to a small fishing town, where she clashes with a grumpy sea captain.

title: Hook, Line, and Sinker
author: Tessa Bailey
description: A flirtatious fisherman and an aspiring film score composer become friends with benefits, and the friendship part becomes the problem.

title: Fangirl Down
author: Tessa Bailey
description: A washed-up golfer hires his biggest fan as his caddie, and their professional relationship gets very unprofessional.

title: Get a Life, Chloe Brown
author: Talia Hibbert
description: A chronically ill woman makes a list of wild things to do and enlists her grumpy building superintendent to help her tick them off.

title: Take a Hint, Dani Brown
author: Talia Hibbert
description: A career-focused woman and a retired rugby player go viral in a fake relationship that she insists is just friends with benefits.

title: Act Your Age, Eve Brown
author: Talia Hibbert
description: A chaotic woman accidentally hits a grumpy B&B owner with her car and takes a job working for him to make amends.

title: The Wedding Date
author: Jasmine Guillory
description: A woman and a man who get stuck in an elevator together agree to be each other's wedding dates, kicking off a long-distance relationship.

title: The Proposal
author: Jasmine Guillory
description: A woman turns down a public proposal at a baseball game and is rescued by two strangers, one of whom becomes much more.

title: While We Were Dating
author: Jasmine Guillory
description: A movie star and an advertising executive begin a fling during a commercial shoot that both insist is temporary.

title: The Kiss Quotient
author: Helen Hoang
description: An autistic woman hires an escort to teach her about physical intimacy and falls in love with him instead.

title: The Bride Test
author: Helen Hoang
description: A Vietnamese woman comes to America for an arranged courtship with an autistic man who doesn't think he can love.

title: The Heart Principle
author: Helen Hoang
description: An autistic violinist in the midst of burnout starts a fake relationship with a man her family wants her to date.

title: Chump Change
author: Dan Fante
description: Bruno Dante, a drunk telemarketer and failed writer in LA, stumbles through blackouts, women, and self-destruction in the debut that channels his father's voice through a harder, angrier lens.

title: Mooch
author: Dan Fante
description: Bruno gets a job selling used cars in LA and tries to stay sober while his boss, his girlfriend, and his own rage conspire against him.

title: Spitting Off Tall Buildings
author: Dan Fante
description: Bruno works as a telemarketer in New York, drinks, fights, and ruins every relationship he touches while trying to write.

title: 86'd
author: Dan Fante
description: Bruno Dante in middle age, still drinking, now a limo driver in LA, confronting the wreckage of his life and his father's ghost.

title: Fante: A Family's Legacy of Writing, Drinking and Surviving
author: Dan Fante
description: Dan's memoir of growing up as John Fante's son—the alcoholism, the literary ambition, and the family curse of talent and self-destruction.

title: Short Dog
author: Dan Fante
description: Short stories from the same world as Bruno Dante—drunks, hustlers, and losers in LA and New York, told with brutal honesty.

title: Point Doom
author: Dan Fante
description: A drunk poet stumbles onto a murder conspiracy in Malibu and must survive long enough to figure out who's killing who.

title: Nightside the Long Sun
author: Gene Wolfe
description: A priest in a generation starship receives a revelation from a god and begins a quest through the ship's decaying cities to save his church.

series.name: The Book of the Long Sun

title: Lake of the Long Sun
author: Gene Wolfe
description: Patera Silk continues his mission as political conspiracies and the ship's ancient AI systems draw him deeper into the Whorl's secrets.

series.name: The Book of the Long Sun

title: Caldé of the Long Sun
author: Gene Wolfe
description: Revolution erupts in the city of Viron as Silk is declared caldé and must lead while the gods of the Whorl reveal their true nature.

series.name: The Book of the Long Sun

title: Exodus from the Long Sun
author: Gene Wolfe
description: The inhabitants of the Whorl finally leave the generation ship, and Silk's fate is stranger than anyone—including the reader—expects.

series.name: The Book of the Long Sun

title: On Blue's Waters
author: Gene Wolfe
description: A colonist on the planet Blue searches for the vanished Patera Silk, narrating his journey while unreliable memories and identities blur together.

series.name: The Book of the Short Sun

title: In Green's Jungles
author: Gene Wolfe
description: The narrator's search for Silk continues as he travels between Blue and the dangerous jungle planet Green, and his own identity becomes increasingly uncertain.

series.name: The Book of the Short Sun

title: Return to the Whorl
author: Gene Wolfe
description: The narrator returns to the generation ship Whorl and the truth about who he is—and who Silk became—is finally revealed in Wolfe's most challenging ending.

series.name: The Book of the Short Sun

title: Soldier of the Mist
author: Gene Wolfe
description: A Roman mercenary in ancient Greece loses his memory each night but gains the ability to see gods and spirits, recording his days in a scroll he reads each morning.

title: Soldier of Arete
author: Gene Wolfe
description: Latro continues his wanderings through the Greek world after the Persian Wars, encountering gods, heroes, and mysteries he can never remember the next day.

title: Soldier of Sidon
author: Gene Wolfe
description: Latro travels to Egypt and sails up the Nile, encountering the gods of the ancient kingdom in the long-awaited third volume.

title: The Sorcerer's House
author: Gene Wolfe
description: A recently released convict inherits a house that is larger inside than outside and contains doors to other worlds, narrated in letters to his twin brother.

title: An Evil Guest
author: Gene Wolfe
description: A struggling actress is transformed into a beauty by a mysterious benefactor in a near-future noir that hides Lovecraftian horrors beneath its pulp surface.

title: There Are Doors
author: Gene Wolfe
description: A man follows a woman he loves through a door into an alternate world where men die after sex and nothing works the way he expects.

title: Free Live Free
author: Gene Wolfe
description: Four misfits living in a condemned boarding house discover their landlord has a secret connected to a WWII time-travel experiment.

title: The Knight
author: Gene Wolfe
description: A boy from our world becomes a man in a Norse-inspired realm of seven nested levels, the first half of The Wizard Knight.

title: The Wizard
author: Gene Wolfe
description: Sir Able completes his quest through the seven worlds, confronting giants, dragons, and the god who rules the highest level.

title: The Little Sister
author: Raymond Chandler
description: A small-town girl hires Marlowe to find her missing brother, and the trail leads through Hollywood's movie industry and its connection to organized crime.

title: Playback
author: Raymond Chandler
description: Marlowe follows a mysterious woman to a coastal town in Chandler's final completed novel, lighter in tone but still sharp about wealth and deception.

title: Beneath the Wheel
author: Hermann Hesse
description: A gifted boy is crushed by the demands of a rigid seminary education that destroys his creativity and spirit, in Hesse's most autobiographical early novel.

title: Peter Camenzind
author: Hermann Hesse
description: A Swiss village boy leaves for the city to become a writer, fails, and returns home having learned that simplicity is its own kind of wisdom.

title: Rosshalde
author: Hermann Hesse
description: A painter trapped in a dead marriage finds his only joy in his young son, and must choose between art, love, and the child.

title: Knulp
author: Hermann Hesse
description: Three episodes from the life of a charming vagabond who drifts through southern Germany, welcome everywhere but belonging nowhere.

title: Journey to the East
author: Hermann Hesse
description: A member of a mystical league recounts a pilgrimage that dissolved into confusion, only to discover the journey's true meaning years later.

title: Last Exit to Brooklyn
author: Hubert Selby Jr.
description: Six brutal interconnected stories of prostitutes, union thugs, drag queens, and gang members in 1950s Brooklyn, written in a stream-of-consciousness style that was prosecuted for obscenity.

title: Requiem for a Dream
author: Hubert Selby Jr.
description: Four people in Brighton Beach pursue their dreams through drugs—heroin, diet pills, television—and each is destroyed by addiction in increasingly horrifying ways.

title: The Room
author: Hubert Selby Jr.
description: A man in a jail cell fantasizes increasingly sadistic revenge on the cops and lawyers who put him there, blurring the line between thought and madness.

title: The Demon
author: Hubert Selby Jr.
description: A successful executive is consumed by compulsive sex, theft, and violence that escalate beyond his control despite his perfect exterior.

title: Song of the Silent Snow
author: Hubert Selby Jr.
description: Short stories spanning Selby's career—junkies, loners, working-class dreamers—each finding or failing to find a moment of grace.

title: Jesus' Son
author: Denis Johnson
description: Eleven linked stories of a drifter called Fuckhead wandering through the American Midwest on drugs, witnessing car crashes, overdoses, and accidental miracles.

title: Tree of Smoke
author: Denis Johnson
description: A CIA officer in Vietnam runs a psychological operation that may be a double cross, in a sprawling novel about American delusion in Southeast Asia.

title: Angels
author: Denis Johnson
description: A woman fleeing her husband and a drifter meet on a Greyhound bus and spiral together through drugs, robbery, and prison in Johnson's debut novel.

title: Already Dead
author: Denis Johnson
description: A marijuana grower on the northern California coast is hired to kill a man, but the target may already be dead in a hallucinatory noir.

title: Train Dreams
author: Denis Johnson
description: A day laborer in the early 20th-century American West loses his family to a forest fire and lives out his years alone in the Idaho panhandle.

title: Nobody Move
author: Denis Johnson
description: A gambler on the run from a loan shark teams up with a woman stealing money from her ex-husband in a tight, pulpy crime novel.

title: A Sport and a Pastime
author: James Salter
description: An American drifter and a French girl have an affair in provincial France, narrated by an unreliable observer who may be inventing the sex scenes.

title: Light Years
author: James Salter
description: A marriage in the Hudson Valley dissolves slowly over decades as dinner parties, children, and affairs accumulate in prose of devastating beauty.

title: All That Is
author: James Salter
description: A book editor's life from WWII through the sexual revolution and into old age, told in Salter's luminous, elliptical style.

title: Solo Faces
author: James Salter
description: A man finds meaning only in mountain climbing—the Alps, the Dru, the hardest routes—and everything else in life falls away.

title: Burning the Days
author: James Salter
description: Salter's memoir of fighter pilot days in Korea, writing in Paris, climbing in the Alps, and the women and landscapes that shaped his prose.

title: The Man with the Golden Arm
author: Nelson Algren
description: A card dealer in Chicago's Polish slums returns from WWII with a morphine addiction and tries to go straight while the neighborhood drags him back.

title: A Walk on the Wild Side
author: Nelson Algren
description: A young Texan drifts to Depression-era New Orleans and falls into the world of prostitution, con men, and outcasts on the wrong side of everything.

title: The Neon Wilderness
author: Nelson Algren
description: Short stories of Chicago's underclass—drunks, thieves, fighters, and prostitutes—written with the compassion that made Hemingway call Algren America's best writer.

title: Never Come Morning
author: Nelson Algren
description: A Polish-American boxer in Chicago dreams of the ring while trapped by poverty, gang violence, and a crime he cannot undo.

title: Hunger
author: Knut Hamsun
description: A starving writer in Christiania wanders the streets in a fever of pride, humiliation, and delirium, refusing charity while his body shuts down.

title: Pan
author: Knut Hamsun
description: A lieutenant living alone in the northern Norwegian woods falls into an obsessive, destructive love affair during the endless summer daylight.

title: Mysteries
author: Knut Hamsun
description: A stranger arrives in a small coastal town and disrupts every relationship with his manic charm, cruelty, and inexplicable behavior.

title: Growth of the Soil
author: Knut Hamsun
description: A solitary man carves a farm from the Norwegian wilderness, and the Nobel Prize-winning novel celebrates the primal connection between man and earth.

title: Victoria
author: Knut Hamsun
description: A miller's son and a landowner's daughter fall in love as children but class separates them, and Hamsun turns their doomed romance into lyric prose.

title: Appointment in Samarra
author: John O'Hara
description: A car dealer in a Pennsylvania town destroys his life over three days of drinking, social humiliation, and bad decisions that feel inevitable.

title: BUtterfield 8
author: John O'Hara
description: A beautiful party girl in 1930s New York drifts through affairs and alcohol toward a fate she can see coming but can't stop.

title: Ten North Frederick
author: John O'Hara
description: A respected small-city lawyer's secret political ambitions and hidden love affair are revealed after his death, exposing the gap between reputation and truth.

title: Our Lady of the Flowers
author: Jean Genet
description: Written in prison on brown paper bags, a thief's fantasy of drag queens, murderers, and male prostitutes in the Montmartre underworld—transgressive, poetic, and sacred.

title: The Thief's Journal
author: Jean Genet
description: Genet's autobiography of begging, stealing, and prostitution across 1930s Europe, transforming degradation into a perverse sainthood.

title: Querelle of Brest
author: Jean Genet
description: A beautiful sailor commits murder in a port town and submits to homosexual sex as a form of expiation, in Genet's most mythological novel.

title: Miracle of the Rose
author: Jean Genet
description: Memories of a juvenile reformatory and an adult prison merge in a dream narrative where criminals become saints and chains become garlands.

title: Pimp
author: Iceberg Slim
description: The autobiography of a Black pimp on Chicago's South Side—brutal, manipulative, and unflinching in its portrayal of the hustling life.

title: Trick Baby
author: Iceberg Slim
description: A light-skinned Black con man and his mentor run grifts across Chicago, exploiting racial assumptions in both directions.

title: The Killer Inside Me
author: Jim Thompson
description: A small-town Texas deputy sheriff narrates his own psychopathic murders in a cheerful first person that is the most disturbing voice in American crime fiction.

title: Pop. 1280
author: Jim Thompson
description: A lazy Southern sheriff who everyone thinks is stupid turns out to be a manipulative killer who plays the entire town against itself.

title: The Grifters
author: Jim Thompson
description: A small-time con artist, his mother who works for the mob, and his girlfriend who's a better grifter than both of them destroy each other over money.

title: A Hell of a Woman
author: Jim Thompson
description: A door-to-door salesman starts stealing from his elderly customers and descends into madness, told in a narrative that literally splits in two.

title: Savage Night
author: Jim Thompson
description: A hitman sent to a small town to kill a witness falls for the target's wife, and the novel dissolves into surreal, violent fragmentation.

title: After Dark, My Sweet
author: Jim Thompson
description: A brain-damaged ex-boxer falls in with a scheming widow and a con man who plan to kidnap a rich child, and nobody in the triangle is what they seem.

title: The Getaway
author: Jim Thompson
description: A bank robber and his wife flee to Mexico after a heist, and their marriage unravels as violently as the crime.

title: A Swell-Looking Babe
author: Jim Thompson
description: A bellboy at a seedy hotel is drawn into a heist by a beautiful woman who may be setting him up.

title: The Postman Always Rings Twice
author: James M. Cain
description: A drifter and a roadside diner owner's wife murder her husband, and their guilt and lust destroy them both.

title: Double Indemnity
author: James M. Cain
description: An insurance salesman and a femme fatale plot her husband's murder for the insurance payout, narrated by the killer as the scheme collapses.

title: Mildred Pierce
author: James M. Cain
description: A Depression-era divorcee builds a restaurant empire to satisfy her ungrateful daughter's social ambitions, sacrificing everything for a child who despises her.

title: Serenade
author: James M. Cain
description: An opera singer who lost his voice in a homosexual affair flees to Mexico, recovers through a woman, and is destroyed when the past follows him.

title: The Talented Mr. Ripley
author: Patricia Highsmith
description: A charming sociopath is sent to Italy to retrieve a rich playboy and instead murders him and assumes his identity.

title: Ripley Under Ground
author: Patricia Highsmith
description: Ripley has settled in France and married money, but an art forgery scheme threatens to expose him and he must kill again to stay free.

title: Ripley's Game
author: Patricia Highsmith
description: Ripley manipulates a dying picture framer into becoming a hitman, partly for profit and partly because he's bored.

title: Strangers on a Train
author: Patricia Highsmith
description: Two men meet on a train and one proposes they swap murders—each killing the person the other wants dead so neither has a motive.

title: The Price of Salt
author: Patricia Highsmith
description: A young shop girl falls in love with an older married woman in 1950s New York, in the rare lesbian novel of its era with a happy ending.

title: Deep Water
author: Patricia Highsmith
description: A husband tolerates his wife's affairs until he starts murdering her lovers and the marriage becomes a game of psychological warfare.

title: If He Hollers Let Him Go
author: Chester Himes
description: A Black foreman in a WWII shipyard faces a racist coworker's accusation and four days of escalating rage in wartime Los Angeles.

title: Cotton Comes to Harlem
author: Chester Himes
description: Detectives Coffin Ed and Grave Digger Jones chase a bale of cotton stuffed with stolen money through Harlem in a wild, violent, darkly comic caper.

title: A Rage in Harlem
author: Chester Himes
description: A naive churchgoing man is conned by a beautiful woman and must navigate Harlem's criminal underworld to get his money back.

title: The Real Cool Killers
author: Chester Himes
description: A white man is murdered in Harlem and Coffin Ed and Grave Digger must solve it before a teenage gang takes the fall.

title: Cast the First Stone
author: Chester Himes
description: A white prisoner's homosexual relationship in an Ohio penitentiary, based on Himes's own seven years in prison.

title: The Underground Man
author: Ross Macdonald
description: A forest fire and a child's kidnapping lead Archer through layers of family dysfunction, buried corpses, and California's destruction of its own landscape.

title: The Chill
author: Ross Macdonald
description: A young man hires Archer to find his runaway bride, and the investigation reveals a murder committed twenty years ago by someone still walking free.

title: The Moving Target
author: Ross Macdonald
description: Archer's first case: a millionaire is kidnapped and his spoiled daughter, alcoholic wife, and shady associates all have reasons to want him gone.

title: Sleeping Beauty
author: Ross Macdonald
description: An oil spill off the California coast and a missing woman converge in Archer's investigation of a wealthy family destroying itself.

title: Rear Window and Other Stories
author: Cornell Woolrich
description: The collection containing the story Hitchcock filmed—a man with a broken leg watches his neighbors and sees a murder—plus other tales of urban paranoia and dread.

title: The Bride Wore Black
author: Cornell Woolrich
description: A woman systematically murders the men responsible for her husband's death on their wedding day, and a detective pieces together the pattern.

title: Phantom Lady
author: Cornell Woolrich
description: A man is convicted of murdering his wife and his only alibi is a woman he spent the evening with—but no one can find her and she may not exist.

title: Naked Lunch
author: William S. Burroughs
description: A junkie's hallucinatory journey through Interzone—a nightmarish city of addicts, agents, and talking insects—in the novel that broke obscenity law.

title: Junky
author: William S. Burroughs
description: Burroughs's first novel: a clinical, unsentimental account of heroin addiction in 1940s New York and Mexico.

title: Queer
author: William S. Burroughs
description: Written in 1953 but unpublished for decades: Lee searches for yagé in South America while pursuing a young man who doesn't want him.

title: The Soft Machine
author: William S. Burroughs
description: The first of the cut-up trilogy: fragments of narrative are spliced together as agents of control battle for dominance in a psychedelic dystopia.

title: The Ticket That Exploded
author: William S. Burroughs
description: The Nova Mob—parasitic alien entities who feed on conflict—are fought by agents using cut-up techniques to disrupt their control of language.

title: Nova Express
author: William S. Burroughs
description: The conclusion of the cut-up trilogy as Inspector Lee leads the final assault on the Nova Mob and their viral control systems.

title: The Wild Boys
author: William S. Burroughs
description: Roving gangs of feral boys in a post-apocalyptic landscape wage guerrilla war against civilization using sex, drugs, and time travel.

title: Cities of the Red Night
author: William S. Burroughs
description: Three intersecting narratives—a detective in present-day New York, 18th-century pirates, and an ancient plague city—form Burroughs's most ambitious late novel.

title: Light
author: M. John Harrison
description: Three narratives across centuries converge on a mysterious region of space called the Kefahuchi Tract, in Harrison's return to sci-fi.

title: Nova Swing
author: M. John Harrison
description: A tour guide smuggles people into the event zone—a patch of alien physics on a colony world—where reality itself is for sale.

title: Empty Space
author: M. John Harrison
description: The final Kefahuchi novel weaves a widow in present-day England, a far-future detective, and the alien Tract into Harrison's most elusive fiction.

title: Viriconium
author: M. John Harrison
description: Four linked works set in a dying-earth city that shifts and changes with each telling—Harrison's anti-fantasy that deconstructs worldbuilding itself.

title: Climbers
author: M. John Harrison
description: A man recovering from a breakdown takes up rock climbing in northern England, and the sport becomes a meditation on obsession, failure, and the body.

title: Wise Blood
author: Flannery O'Connor
description: A young war veteran founds the Church Without Christ in a Southern city, raging against faith while everyone around him embodies the grotesque grace he denies.

title: A Good Man Is Hard to Find
author: Flannery O'Connor
description: Ten stories of the American South where violence erupts from banality—a family meets the Misfit, a Bible salesman steals a wooden leg, a bull gores a woman into revelation.

title: The Violent Bear It Away
author: Flannery O'Connor
description: A teenager raised by his great-uncle to be a prophet resists his calling, but God—through violence and drowning—will not be refused.

title: Everything That Rises Must Converge
author: Flannery O'Connor
description: Nine posthumous stories of the South in transition—racial integration, generational warfare, and divine mercy arriving in the worst possible form.

title: Mystery and Manners
author: Flannery O'Connor
description: O'Connor's essays on writing, faith, and the grotesque—explaining why Southern writers are drawn to freaks and why violence is a vehicle for grace.

title: The Heart Is a Lonely Hunter
author: Carson McCullers
description: A deaf-mute in a small Georgia town becomes the confidant of four lonely people who pour their dreams into him, each seeing what they need to see.

title: The Member of the Wedding
author: Carson McCullers
description: A twelve-year-old tomboy in the South decides she will join her brother's wedding and go away with the couple, unable to bear being left behind.

title: The Ballad of the Sad Café
author: Carson McCullers
description: A tall, fierce woman who runs a café falls in love with a hunchback dwarf, and her ex-husband returns from prison to destroy them both.

title: Reflections in a Golden Eye
author: Carson McCullers
description: On a Southern army base, a repressed captain obsesses over a private who rides naked through the woods on a horse, and violence is inevitable.

title: Clock Without Hands
author: Carson McCullers
description: A dying pharmacist, a senile judge, a young Black man, and a lonely boy converge in a Georgia town during the civil rights era.

title: Waiting for Godot
author: Samuel Beckett
description: Two men wait by a tree for someone named Godot who never comes, and nothing happens—twice—in the play that changed theater forever.

title: Molloy
author: Samuel Beckett
description: A man in his mother's bed narrates his journey to find her, followed by a detective's report on finding him—both voices dissolving into uncertainty.

title: Malone Dies
author: Samuel Beckett
description: A man dying in bed tells stories to pass the time, but the stories keep collapsing and the narrator may already be dead.

title: The Unnamable
author: Samuel Beckett
description: A voice in a void speaks endlessly, unable to stop, unable to identify itself, in the final dissolution of narrative and self.

title: Murphy
author: Samuel Beckett
description: An Irishman in London ties himself to a rocking chair to achieve mental peace while his girlfriend searches for him across the city.

title: Watt
author: Samuel Beckett
description: A man goes to work as a servant in a house where nothing can be understood or named, and language itself becomes the problem.

title: Endgame
author: Samuel Beckett
description: A blind man in a wheelchair and his servant are the last people on earth, trapped in a room with two ashcans containing the blind man's legless parents.

title: Krapp's Last Tape
author: Samuel Beckett
description: An old man listens to tape recordings of his younger self and finds the person on the tapes as alien as a stranger.

title: A Confederacy of Dunces
author: John Kennedy Toole
description: A flatulent medievalist in New Orleans rages against modernity from his mother's house, accidentally causing chaos at every job he takes, published eleven years after the author's suicide.

title: The Neon Bible
author: John Kennedy Toole
description: A sixteen-year-old boy in a small Southern town recounts his childhood—his father's failures, his aunt's arrival, and the preacher who destroys everything.

title: Miss Lonelyhearts
author: Nathanael West
description: A newspaper advice columnist is crushed by the genuine suffering in the letters he receives and spirals into a Christ complex that destroys him.

title: The Day of the Locust
author: Nathanael West
description: A painter in 1930s Hollywood observes the desperate people who came to California to die, building toward an apocalyptic riot at a movie premiere.

title: A Cool Million
author: Nathanael West
description: A naive young American is systematically dismembered—literally—as he pursues the American Dream, in West's savage Candide.

title: Devil in a Blue Dress
author: Walter Mosley
description: A Black WWII veteran in 1948 LA is hired to find a white woman who frequents jazz clubs, and the search takes him through the city's racial underworld.

title: A Red Death
author: Walter Mosley
description: Easy is blackmailed by the FBI into spying on a union organizer during the McCarthy era while a murder complicates everything.

title: White Butterfly
author: Walter Mosley
description: A serial killer targeting Black women is ignored by the LAPD until a white victim appears, and Easy must solve it while his marriage falls apart.

title: Black Betty
author: Walter Mosley
description: Easy searches for a legendary beauty from his Texas past who has vanished from a wealthy white family's household.

title: Always Outnumbered, Always Outgunned
author: Walter Mosley
description: Socrates Fortlow, an ex-convict living in a Watts shack, tries to live a moral life through fourteen connected stories.

title: Delta of Venus
author: Anaïs Nin
description: Fifteen erotic stories originally written for a private collector, blending sensuality with psychological depth in prose that reclaimed female desire.

title: Henry and June
author: Anaïs Nin
description: Nin's diary of her affair with Henry Miller and his wife June in 1930s Paris, the ménage that fueled both writers' greatest work.

title: A Spy in the House of Love
author: Anaïs Nin
description: A woman conducts multiple affairs across New York, each lover drawing out a different version of herself, while guilt and freedom war within her.

title: House of Incest
author: Anaïs Nin
description: A prose poem of erotic obsession, dream imagery, and self-imprisonment—Nin's most experimental work.

title: The Loser
author: Thomas Bernhard
description: Three piano students study under Horowitz—one becomes Glenn Gould, one kills himself, and the narrator quits, destroyed by genius he cannot match.

title: Woodcutters
author: Thomas Bernhard
description: A writer attends a dinner party in Vienna and silently eviscerates every guest, the hostess, Austrian culture, and himself in one sustained interior monologue.

title: Correction
author: Thomas Bernhard
description: A man examines his dead friend's papers—a genius who built a perfect cone-shaped house for his sister—and the obsessive thinking that destroyed him.

title: Wittgenstein's Nephew
author: Thomas Bernhard
description: Bernhard's memoir of his friendship with Paul Wittgenstein, the philosopher's nephew, two men bonded by illness and the mutual hatred of Austrian society.

title: Extinction
author: Thomas Bernhard
description: A man in Rome learns his parents and brother have died in a car crash and returns to the family estate he despises, raging against everything Austrian.

title: Old Masters
author: Thomas Bernhard
description: An 82-year-old music critic sits in front of the same Tintoretto painting every other day for thirty years, demolishing every cultural institution in Europe.

title: Concrete
author: Thomas Bernhard
description: A man has been trying to write a study of Mendelssohn for ten years but cannot write the first sentence, blaming his sister, his illness, and the world.

title: Frost
author: Thomas Bernhard
description: A medical student is sent to observe a reclusive painter in an Alpine village and is consumed by the man's nihilistic worldview in Bernhard's debut novel.

title: Miami Blues
author: Charles Willeford
description: A psychopath steals a detective's badge and gun and starts impersonating a cop in Miami, and Sergeant Hoke Moseley must stop him.

title: New Hope for the Dead
author: Charles Willeford
description: Hoke investigates the death of a young woman while dealing with his ex-wife dumping their daughters on him and his partner moving in.

title: Sideswipe
author: Charles Willeford
description: Hoke has a breakdown and tries to disappear while an elderly con man runs an insurance scam that turns murderous.

title: The Way We Die Now
author: Charles Willeford
description: Hoke investigates a murdered Haitian immigrant and uncovers a network of exploitation in Miami's immigrant community.

title: Pick-Up
author: Charles Willeford
description: Two alcoholics meet in a San Francisco bar and spiral into a suicide pact that one of them doesn't mean.

title: Dark Passage
author: David Goodis
description: A man convicted of murdering his wife escapes prison and gets plastic surgery to change his face while hunting the real killer.

title: Down There
author: David Goodis
description: A former concert pianist hiding in a dive bar as a honky-tonk player is dragged back into violence when his brother shows up running from criminals.

title: Nightfall
author: David Goodis
description: A man framed for murder and pursued by both cops and criminals tries to prove his innocence while barely holding himself together.

title: Street of No Return
author: David Goodis
description: A former singer living on Skid Row is drawn into a race riot by a woman from his past in Goodis's most ambitious novel.

title: The Neon Rain
author: James Lee Burke
description: A New Orleans homicide detective pulls a dead woman from the bayou and follows the trail into CIA operations in Central America.

title: Heaven's Prisoners
author: James Lee Burke
description: Robicheaux rescues a child from a plane crash in the Gulf and the investigation brings the mob to his door and costs him everything.

title: A Morning for Flamingos
author: James Lee Burke
description: Robicheaux goes undercover in the New Orleans drug trade after being shot and left for dead by a prisoner he was transporting.

title: In the Electric Mist with Confederate Dead
author: James Lee Burke
description: Robicheaux investigates a serial killer in the bayou while the ghost of a Confederate general offers counsel and a Hollywood production films nearby.

title: The Hunter
author: Richard Stark
description: Parker, a professional thief, is double-crossed by his partner and left for dead, then methodically destroys everyone who cheated him to get his money back.

title: The Man with the Getaway Face
author: Richard Stark
description: Parker gets plastic surgery and plans a new heist, but someone from the surgeon's past threatens to blow the job.

title: The Outfit
author: Richard Stark
description: Parker declares war on the Outfit—the organized crime syndicate that put a contract on him—by encouraging freelance thieves to hit their operations.

title: The Score
author: Richard Stark
description: Parker's most ambitious job: robbing an entire mining town in one night by cutting off all communication with the outside world.

title: The Big Blowdown
author: George Pelecanos
description: Two boys from a 1930s DC diner grow up on opposite sides of the law, and the Greek-American community becomes the backdrop for a crime epic.

title: Hard Revolution
author: George Pelecanos
description: A young Black cop in 1968 DC investigates his brother's murder as the city erupts in riots after Martin Luther King Jr.'s assassination.

title: The Night Gardener
author: George Pelecanos
description: A serial killer in DC targets teenagers in a community garden, and three detectives—past and present—try to stop him.

title: Drama City
author: George Pelecanos
description: An ex-con working for an animal shelter and his parole officer navigate the streets of DC where temptation and violence never stop pulling.

title: Dare Me
author: Megan Abbott
description: A new cheerleading coach disrupts the power dynamics of a high school squad, and an affair and a death push the girls toward the edge.

title: The Fever
author: Megan Abbott
description: A mysterious illness strikes the girls at a high school one by one, and the town spirals into panic, conspiracy theories, and blame.

title: You Will Know Me
author: Megan Abbott
description: A gymnast's Olympic dreams collide with a murder in the gym community, and her mother discovers how far she'll go to protect her daughter's career.

title: Give Me Your Hand
author: Megan Abbott
description: Two women competing for the same prestigious lab position share a secret from their past that one of them is willing to kill to keep.

title: The Turnout
author: Megan Abbott
description: Two sisters who run a ballet studio are disrupted by a contractor renovating their building who sees through their carefully maintained world.

title: He Died with His Eyes Open
author: Derek Raymond
description: An unnamed detective in London's Department of Unexplained Deaths investigates a murdered drifter through his tape-recorded diaries.

title: The Devil's Home on Leave
author: Derek Raymond
description: A man is found boiled down to his bones in a dustbin, and the detective's investigation leads into the heart of London's criminal establishment.

title: How the Dead Live
author: Derek Raymond
description: A woman dies at a French country house and the detective discovers a conspiracy of silence surrounding her death and her marriage.

title: I Was Dora Suarez
author: Derek Raymond
description: The most extreme Factory novel: a dying woman's diary and a brutal double murder push the detective past the limits of what he can bear to know.

title: The Last Good Kiss
author: James Crumley
description: A PI follows a missing writer's trail from a Montana bar to San Francisco, accompanied by a bulldog, in the novel with the greatest opening line in crime fiction.

title: The Wrong Case
author: James Crumley
description: A drunk PI in a Montana college town investigates the disappearance of a young woman and discovers the case connects to a drug operation.

title: The Mexican Tree Duck
author: James Crumley
description: Sughrue is hired to find a woman who vanished from a bombing and the search takes him through the Southwest's criminal underground.

title: Bordersnakes
author: James Crumley
description: Crumley's two PIs—Milodragovitch and Sughrue—finally team up to pursue a con man across the Texas-Mexico border.

title: The Basketball Diaries
author: Jim Carroll
description: A teenage basketball prodigy on the Lower East Side documents his descent into heroin addiction in diary entries of raw, lyrical desperation.

title: Forced Entries
author: Jim Carroll
description: The sequel diary covering Carroll's years in the downtown New York art scene, punk rock, and recovery.

title: Bad Behavior
author: Mary Gaitskill
description: Nine stories of power, sex, and sadomasochism in 1980s New York—strippers, secretaries, and damaged people seeking connection through domination.

title: Veronica
author: Mary Gaitskill
description: A former model remembers her friendship with an older woman who died of AIDS, and the glamour and cruelty of the 1980s fashion world.

title: Two Girls, Fat and Thin
author: Mary Gaitskill
description: A journalist and a follower of an Ayn Rand-like philosopher are connected by an interview that dredges up both women's histories of abuse.

title: The Mare
author: Mary Gaitskill
description: A Dominican girl from Brooklyn joins a Fresh Air Fund program and bonds with a horse and her white host mother, and the relationship tests everyone.

title: Blood and Guts in High School
author: Kathy Acker
description: A girl sold into slavery by her father wanders through a nightmare New York of sex, violence, and plagiarized texts in Acker's most notorious novel.

title: Empire of the Senseless
author: Kathy Acker
description: A half-robot woman and a pirate navigate a post-revolutionary Paris, stealing from Neuromancer and Huckleberry Finn in Acker's post-punk literary terrorism.

title: Great Expectations
author: Kathy Acker
description: Not Dickens—Acker's version splices her own childhood abuse with plagiarized classic texts to create a new form of autobiography through theft.

title: Closer
author: Dennis Cooper
description: A group of teenage boys in LA orbit around violence and desire in the first of Cooper's George Miles Cycle—transgressive, clinical, and devastating.

title: Frisk
author: Dennis Cooper
description: A man may or may not be murdering young men in Amsterdam, and the reader cannot determine what is real and what is fantasy.

title: Guide
author: Dennis Cooper
description: A pornographer, a boy obsessed with Disneyland, and a serial killer intersect in LA in the cycle's most structurally complex entry.

title: The Painted Bird
author: Jerzy Kosinski
description: A dark-haired boy wanders through Eastern Europe during WWII, passed from one peasant household to another, each inflicting a new form of cruelty.

title: Being There
author: Jerzy Kosinski
description: A simple gardener whose only knowledge comes from television is mistaken for a political genius and rises to the highest levels of power.

title: Steps
author: Jerzy Kosinski
description: Fragmentary episodes of sex, violence, and manipulation told in cold, precise prose—each scene a power exchange between predator and prey.

title: Too Loud a Solitude
author: Bohumil Hrabal
description: A man who has spent thirty-five years compacting wastepaper—rescuing books from the crusher—narrates his education in beauty through garbage.

title: Closely Watched Trains
author: Bohumil Hrabal
description: A young railway apprentice in Nazi-occupied Czechoslovakia is more concerned with losing his virginity than the Resistance, until the two collide.

title: I Served the King of England
author: Bohumil Hrabal
description: A small waiter rises through the grandest hotels of pre-war Czechoslovakia, marries a Nazi, and loses everything after the communist takeover.

title: Dancing Lessons for the Advanced in Age
author: Bohumil Hrabal
description: An old man's monologue—one unbroken sentence for the entire novel—rambles through memory, sex, war, and shoe-making in Hrabal's most formally radical work.

title: Ferdydurke
author: Witold Gombrowicz
description: A thirty-year-old writer is kidnapped back to school by a professor and forced into adolescence, in a surreal satire of immaturity, form, and cultural pretension.

title: Cosmos
author: Witold Gombrowicz
description: Two men on vacation become obsessed with a series of possibly connected signs—a hanged sparrow, a crack in the ceiling—and create meaning from coincidence until it kills.

title: Pornografia
author: Witold Gombrowicz
description: Two older men in occupied Poland become obsessed with manipulating two teenagers into a sexual relationship, and the project becomes monstrous.

title: Trans-Atlantyk
author: Witold Gombrowicz
description: A Polish writer stranded in Buenos Aires in 1939 must choose between patriotic duty and the freedom of exile, narrated in mock-baroque Polish.

title: The Book of Disquiet
author: Fernando Pessoa
description: A Lisbon bookkeeper's diary of dreams, observations, and despair—a fragmentary masterpiece written under a pseudonym and assembled from a trunk of manuscripts after Pessoa's death.

title: Selected Poems
author: Fernando Pessoa
description: Poetry written under four different identities—each with a distinct biography and style—by the man who was the entire Portuguese avant-garde.

title: Gravity's Rainbow
author: Thomas Pynchon
description: A WWII soldier whose erections predict V-2 rocket strikes becomes the center of a paranoid conspiracy spanning Europe, physics, and the occult in the most ambitious American novel of the 20th century.

title: The Crying of Lot 49
author: Thomas Pynchon
description: A woman executing her ex-lover's estate discovers an underground postal system that may connect every conspiracy in American history—or may be a joke.

title: V.
author: Thomas Pynchon
description: A discharged sailor drifts through 1950s New York while a historian traces a mysterious woman called V. through every violent upheaval of the 20th century.

title: Mason & Dixon
author: Thomas Pynchon
description: The two surveyors who drew the line dividing North and South travel through a magical 18th-century America of talking dogs, mechanical ducks, and Jesuits.

title: Inherent Vice
author: Thomas Pynchon
description: A stoned PI in 1970 LA searches for his missing ex-girlfriend and stumbles into a conspiracy involving dentists, Nazis, and the death of the counterculture.

title: Against the Day
author: Thomas Pynchon
description: A thousand-page epic spanning 1893 to WWI, featuring anarchists, balloonists, mathematicians, and the weaponization of light across the entire planet.

title: Bleeding Edge
author: Thomas Pynchon
description: A fraud investigator in pre-9/11 Manhattan follows dirty money into the Deep Web and discovers a conspiracy connecting Silicon Alley to the attacks.

title: Vineland
author: Thomas Pynchon
description: A zonked-out hippie in Reagan's America tries to protect his daughter from a fascist DEA agent who's been pursuing his ex-wife since the 1960s.

title: The Recognitions
author: William Gaddis
description: A painter of perfect Flemish forgeries is caught in a world where everything—religion, money, love, art—is a counterfeit, in the great American novel nobody read.

title: JR
author: William Gaddis
description: An eleven-year-old builds a paper empire from a phone booth using only capitalist logic, told almost entirely in unattributed dialogue.

title: A Frolic of His Own
author: William Gaddis
description: A college professor sues a Hollywood studio for stealing his play, and the lawsuit spawns lawsuits in a satire of American litigiousness.

title: Carpenter's Gothic
author: William Gaddis
description: A woman, her con-man husband, and a geologist collide in a rented house as televangelism, mineral rights, and CIA operations in Africa converge.

title: A Feast of Snakes
author: Harry Crews
description: A former football star in a dying Georgia town hosts the annual rattlesnake roundup as his life collapses into violence, sex, and madness.

title: Car
author: Harry Crews
description: A man eats an entire Ford Maverick piece by piece in a Jacksonville showroom, and the spectacle becomes a media event in Crews's most absurd novel.

title: Body
author: Harry Crews
description: A female bodybuilder at a Florida competition pushes her body past every limit while the men around her disintegrate.

title: A Childhood: The Biography of a Place
author: Harry Crews
description: Growing up poor in rural Georgia—hookworms, polio, boiling water, and hog-killing—in one of the great American memoirs.

title: The Gospel Singer
author: Harry Crews
description: A beautiful gospel singer returns to his hometown where a Black man is about to be lynched and the singer's fame is built on fraud.

title: Ray
author: Barry Hannah
description: A doctor, pilot, and Civil War obsessive narrates his chaotic life in short bursts of manic Southern prose—funny, violent, and barely held together.

title: Airships
author: Barry Hannah
description: Twenty stories of the American South—Civil War ghosts, jazz musicians, Vietnam vets, drunks—in prose that reads like a shotgun blast.

title: Geronimo Rex
author: Barry Hannah
description: A young man in Mississippi discovers music, violence, and sex while idolizing the Apache chief and hating the boredom of Southern respectability.

title: The Tennis Handsome
author: Barry Hannah
description: A brain-damaged tennis player and his sociopathic manager careen through the South in a hallucinated sports novel.

title: A Fan's Notes
author: Frederick Exley
description: A man obsessed with football and Frank Gifford drinks himself through institutions, bars, and breakdowns in the great American novel of failure and spectatorship.

title: Pages from a Cold Island
author: Frederick Exley
description: Exley tries to write about Edmund Wilson's funeral and his own nervous breakdown, producing a memoir of literary ambition and alcoholic collapse.

title: Trout Fishing in America
author: Richard Brautigan
description: A series of short chapters that use trout fishing as a metaphor for everything—America, sex, nature, poverty—in the hippie generation's favorite novel.

title: In Watermelon Sugar
author: Richard Brautigan
description: A commune called iDEATH lives gently in watermelon sugar while the violent outsiders of the Forgotten Works threaten their fragile peace.

title: The Abortion: An Historical Romance 1966
author: Richard Brautigan
description: A librarian who shelves unpublished manuscripts falls for a beautiful woman and accompanies her to Tijuana for an abortion in a gentle, strange love story.

title: A Confederate General from Big Sur
author: Richard Brautigan
description: Two drifters in Big Sur argue over a possibly fictional Confederate ancestor while dropping acid and living on nothing.

title: The Hawkline Monster
author: Richard Brautigan
description: Two gunslingers are hired by an Indian woman to kill a monster in the basement of a Gothic mansion in 1902 Oregon, in Brautigan's genre parody.

title: Snow White
author: Donald Barthelme
description: The fairy tale retold as a postmodern satire: Snow White lives with seven men in Greenwich Village, waiting for a prince who never comes.

title: Sixty Stories
author: Donald Barthelme
description: The definitive Barthelme collection—The Balloon, The School, The Indian Uprising—fragmentary, hilarious fictions that reinvented the American short story.

title: The Dead Father
author: Donald Barthelme
description: A massive dead father is dragged across the countryside by his children toward burial, still talking, still demanding, in an allegory about patriarchy.

title: Paradise
author: Donald Barthelme
description: A fifty-three-year-old architect lives with three lingerie models in his New York apartment for months, and the arrangement is sadder than it sounds.

title: Omensetter's Luck
author: William Gass
description: A natural man moves into an Ohio river town and his effortless goodness destroys the preacher who envies and hates him.

title: In the Heart of the Heart of the Country
author: William Gass
description: Five stories including the title piece—a poet in a dying Indiana town catalogs his surroundings in sections that become an anatomy of American emptiness.

title: The Tunnel
author: William Gass
description: A historian writing the introduction to his book about Nazi guilt instead tunnels into his own life, marriage, and the fascism of everyday American thought.

title: Malcolm
author: James Purdy
description: A beautiful, vacant teenager sitting on a bench is passed between eccentric mentors who each consume a piece of him until nothing is left.

title: Cabot Wright Begins
author: James Purdy
description: A Wall Street broker becomes a rapist and everyone wants to write his story, in Purdy's savage satire of American media and sexual hypocrisy.

title: In a Shallow Grave
author: James Purdy
description: A Vietnam veteran horribly disfigured by wounds hires a series of messengers to court the woman he loves in a Gothic Southern love story.

title: The Quick and the Dead
author: Joy Williams
description: Three teenage girls in the Arizona desert—an eco-radical, an heiress, and a ghost—orbit around death, nature, and the adults who have failed them.

title: The Changeling
author: Joy Williams
description: A woman marries into a wealthy island family whose children may not be entirely human, in Williams's most unsettling novel.

title: Harrow
author: Joy Williams
description: A girl wanders through an ecologically collapsed America searching for her mother among a community of aging radicals waiting for the end.

title: State of Grace
author: Joy Williams
description: A young woman's relationship with her preacher father and her first love fractures under the weight of his religious fervor and her own desire.

title: Austerlitz
author: W.G. Sebald
description: A man raised in Wales discovers he was a Kindertransport child from Prague, and his search for his past merges architecture, photography, and memory into Sebald's masterpiece.

title: The Rings of Saturn
author: W.G. Sebald
description: A walking tour of Suffolk becomes a meditation on decay, destruction, and history—silk worms, the Congo, Thomas Browne—as landscape and memory merge.

title: The Emigrants
author: W.G. Sebald
description: Four stories of exiles and emigrants whose lives have been shaped by the Holocaust, told through fragmentary narratives and grainy photographs.

title: Vertigo
author: W.G. Sebald
description: Four linked narratives—Stendhal in Italy, Kafka in Riva, Sebald returning to his Bavarian hometown—haunted by déjà vu and the persistence of the past.

title: Missing Person
author: Patrick Modiano
description: An amnesiac private detective investigates his own identity through the streets of Paris, finding traces of a life lived during the Occupation.

title: Dora Bruder
author: Patrick Modiano
description: Modiano searches for a Jewish girl who disappeared from a Paris boarding school in 1941, and the gaps in the record become the story.

title: In the Café of Lost Youth
author: Patrick Modiano
description: Four narrators circle a young woman who appears in a Left Bank café—each knowing a piece of her story, none knowing the whole.

title: So You Don't Get Lost in the Neighborhood
author: Patrick Modiano
description: A reclusive novelist receives a phone call about a long-forgotten address that reopens a childhood he has spent his life suppressing.

title: Blindness
author: José Saramago
description: A city is struck by an epidemic of white blindness and the government quarantines the infected in an asylum where civilization collapses—except for one woman who can still see.

title: The Year of the Death of Ricardo Reis
author: José Saramago
description: One of Fernando Pessoa's heteronyms arrives in Lisbon in 1936, visits Pessoa's ghost, and watches Portugal slide toward fascism.

title: The Gospel According to Jesus Christ
author: José Saramago
description: Jesus grows up knowing God has terrible plans for him and tries to refuse his destiny, aided by a sympathetic Devil.

title: All the Names
author: José Saramago
description: A clerk in a vast registry office becomes obsessed with an unknown woman's record and searches for her through a bureaucratic labyrinth and a cemetery without maps.

title: The Cave
author: José Saramago
description: A potter whose livelihood is destroyed by a shopping mall called the Center moves into it and discovers Plato's cave beneath the building.

title: Death with Interruptions
author: José Saramago
description: Death stops working in one country and no one can die, causing chaos—then Death falls in love with a cellist who is somehow immune to her.

title: The Goalie's Anxiety at the Penalty Kick
author: Peter Handke
description: A goalkeeper sent off in a match wanders Vienna and strangles a woman, then drifts through the countryside in a state of detached observation.

title: A Sorrow Beyond Dreams
author: Peter Handke
description: Handke's memoir of his mother's suicide—a working-class Austrian woman whose life was a slow erasure—written in deliberately restrained prose.

title: Slow Homecoming
author: Peter Handke
description: A geologist in Alaska experiences a crisis of perception and journeys home to Austria, relearning how to see landscape and language.

title: The Piano Teacher
author: Elfriede Jelinek
description: A Vienna Conservatory piano teacher trapped by her domineering mother enters a sadomasochistic relationship with a student, and control becomes the only language either speaks.

title: Women as Lovers
author: Elfriede Jelinek
description: Two Austrian working-class women pursue marriage as an escape from factory labor, and Jelinek dissects their delusions with merciless precision.

title: Lust
author: Elfriede Jelinek
description: A factory owner rapes his wife nightly while she seeks an affair as escape, and Jelinek's prose makes the violence of patriarchy inescapable.

title: Fatelessness
author: Imre Kertész
description: A fourteen-year-old Hungarian Jewish boy narrates his experience in Auschwitz and Buchenwald with a detachment that refuses to grant the Holocaust meaning.

title: Kaddish for an Unborn Child
author: Imre Kertész
description: A Holocaust survivor explains to his unborn child why he refuses to bring a life into a world that produced Auschwitz.

title: The Blind Owl
author: Sadegh Hedayat
description: A pen-case painter addicted to opium narrates his obsession with a mysterious woman in a feverish, looping narrative—Iran's greatest modern novel.

title: The Tartar Steppe
author: Dino Buzzati
description: A young officer is posted to a remote fortress on the edge of a desert, waiting decades for an enemy attack that may never come, in an allegory of wasted life.

title: Pietr the Latvian
author: Georges Simenon
description: Inspector Maigret's first case: a dead man on a train turns out not to be who anyone thinks, and Maigret patiently unravels an identity fraud spanning continents.

title: Maigret Sets a Trap
author: Georges Simenon
description: Maigret uses a decoy to catch a serial killer terrorizing Montmartre, but the killer's identity reveals the real monster is closer to home.

title: Maigret and the Madwoman
author: Georges Simenon
description: An old woman tells Maigret someone is moving things in her apartment, and when she is murdered, he must find who killed her and why no one listened.

title: The Man Who Watched Trains Go By
author: Georges Simenon
description: A respectable Dutch clerk discovers his boss is a fraud and snaps, fleeing to Paris where he commits murder and descends into madness.

title: Dirty Snow
author: Georges Simenon
description: A young man in an occupied city commits murder to prove something to himself and is methodically destroyed by the system he thought he could exploit.

title: The Strangers in the House
author: Georges Simenon
description: A reclusive alcoholic lawyer discovers a body in his attic and investigates the murder himself, rediscovering his sharpness and his humanity.

title: Act of Passion
author: Georges Simenon
description: A respectable doctor writes a letter to his judge explaining why he murdered his mistress, and the confession is more disturbing than the crime.

title: The Seven Madmen
author: Roberto Arlt
description: An embezzler in Buenos Aires falls in with a secret society planning to finance a revolution through a chain of brothels, in Argentina's great expressionist novel.

title: The Flamethrowers
author: Roberto Arlt
description: The sequel follows the revolutionary cell as their schemes collapse into paranoia, betrayal, and apocalyptic violence.

title: No Longer Human
author: Osamu Dazai
description: A man's notebooks record his lifelong inability to connect with other people, performing clownishness to hide his alienation, in Japan's darkest confession novel.

title: The Setting Sun
author: Osamu Dazai
description: An aristocratic family disintegrates in postwar Japan as the mother dies, the son drinks himself to death, and the daughter refuses to give up.

title: Schoolgirl
author: Osamu Dazai
description: A day in the life of a teenage girl, narrated with startling intimacy, as she navigates school, family, and her own restless intelligence.

title: Almost Transparent Blue
author: Ryū Murakami
description: A group of young Japanese people near an American military base drift through drug-fueled orgies and violence in a debut that scandalized Japan.

title: In the Miso Soup
author: Ryū Murakami
description: A Tokyo sex-industry tour guide suspects his American client is a serial killer as they cruise through Kabukichō over three nights.

title: Coin Locker Babies
author: Ryū Murakami
description: Two boys abandoned in coin lockers as infants grow up and seek revenge on the city that rejected them—one through music, the other through a chemical weapon.

title: Audition
author: Ryū Murakami
description: A widower holds fake movie auditions to find a new wife, and the woman he chooses is hiding something unimaginably violent.

title: Out
author: Natsuo Kirino
description: Four women working the night shift at a Tokyo boxed-lunch factory dispose of a coworker's murdered husband and enter the criminal underworld.

title: Grotesque
author: Natsuo Kirino
description: Two women from an elite Tokyo school become prostitutes, and their murders reveal the beauty obsession and class warfare beneath Japanese society.

title: Real World
author: Natsuo Kirino
description: Four teenage girls help a neighbor who murdered his mother escape, each drawn to the killer for different reasons.

title: Hopscotch
author: Julio Cortázar
description: A novel that can be read in two different orders—following an Argentine intellectual from Paris to Buenos Aires—reinventing fiction as a game the reader plays.

title: Blow-Up and Other Stories
author: Julio Cortázar
description: Fifteen stories including the one Antonioni filmed—a photographer enlarges a picture and discovers a murder—plus axolotls, invaded houses, and time loops.

title: Cronopios and Famas
author: Julio Cortázar
description: Tiny absurdist sketches dividing humanity into three types: the free-spirited cronopios, the rigid famas, and the conformist esperanzas.

title: 62: A Model Kit
author: Julio Cortázar
description: Characters from Chapter 62 of Hopscotch live in a fluid space between Paris, London, and Vienna, their stories assembling like a model the reader builds.

title: A Certain Lucas
author: Julio Cortázar
description: Short pieces about a man named Lucas who fights against routine, language, and the tyranny of reasonable behavior.

title: The War of the End of the World
author: Mario Vargas Llosa
description: A millennial religious community in 19th-century Brazil resists the new republic and is annihilated by the army, in Vargas Llosa's most ambitious historical novel.

title: Conversation in the Cathedral
author: Mario Vargas Llosa
description: A newspaper man and his father's former chauffeur talk in a Lima bar, and their conversation unravels decades of Peruvian dictatorship, corruption, and personal ruin.

title: The Feast of the Goat
author: Mario Vargas Llosa
description: The last days of Dominican dictator Trujillo, told through the tyrant, his assassins, and a woman returning decades later to confront what was done to her.

title: Aunt Julia and the Scriptwriter
author: Mario Vargas Llosa
description: A young radio journalist falls for his aunt by marriage while a soap opera writer's plots go increasingly insane, in Vargas Llosa's funniest novel.

title: The Green House
author: Mario Vargas Llosa
description: Five interweaving stories set in the Peruvian jungle and desert converge on a brothel called the Green House, spanning decades of exploitation and myth.

title: The Time of the Hero
author: Mario Vargas Llosa
description: Cadets at a Lima military academy cover up a theft that leads to murder, and the institution protects itself at any cost—the novel that got burned by the army.

title: The Decapitated Chicken and Other Stories
author: Horacio Quiroga
description: Stories of the South American jungle, madness, and death—a man eaten by ants, children raised by animals, a feather pillow that drains a woman's blood—the Poe of Latin America.

title: A Grain of Wheat
author: Ngũgĩ wa Thiong'o
description: Four days before Kenya's independence, a village prepares to celebrate and the betrayals of the Mau Mau uprising surface—who was the traitor?

title: Petals of Blood
author: Ngũgĩ wa Thiong'o
description: Four suspects are arrested for arson-murder in a Kenyan village, and their stories reveal how colonialism and capitalism devoured the country's independence.

title: Weep Not, Child
author: Ngũgĩ wa Thiong'o
description: A Kenyan boy's dream of education is shattered by the Mau Mau emergency and his father's dispossession by white settlers.

title: Wizard of the Crow
author: Ngũgĩ wa Thiong'o
description: A massive satirical novel about an African dictator who wants to build a tower to heaven, told through a beggar who accidentally becomes a wizard.

title: The House of Hunger
author: Dambudzo Marechera
description: A young Zimbabwean writer rages against the township, the university, and colonial Rhodesia in a collection of explosive, hallucinatory prose.

title: Black Sunlight
author: Dambudzo Marechera
description: A photographer joins a guerrilla group and descends into violence and madness in Marechera's most experimental novel.

title: Dead Souls
author: Nikolai Gogol
description: A con man travels provincial Russia buying the ownership rights to dead serfs—who still count on the census—to mortgage them for profit, in Russia's greatest comic novel.

title: The Overcoat
author: Nikolai Gogol
description: A poor clerk scrapes together money for a new overcoat that transforms his life, then it is stolen and he dies—and his ghost haunts St. Petersburg.

title: The Nose
author: Nikolai Gogol
description: A bureaucrat wakes to find his nose has left his face and is walking around St. Petersburg in a higher-ranking uniform.

title: Taras Bulba
author: Nikolai Gogol
description: A Cossack chieftain takes his two sons to war against the Poles, and one son betrays him for love of a Polish woman.

title: The Inspector General
author: Nikolai Gogol
description: A broke traveler is mistaken for a government inspector and an entire corrupt town falls over itself to bribe and flatter him.

title: The Cherry Orchard
author: Anton Chekhov
description: An aristocratic family returns to their estate and cannot prevent its sale and the chopping down of their beloved cherry orchard as the old world gives way to the new.

title: Three Sisters
author: Anton Chekhov
description: Three sisters in a provincial town dream of returning to Moscow, but inertia, bad marriages, and the passage of time make the dream impossible.

title: Uncle Vanya
author: Anton Chekhov
description: A man who has wasted his life managing an estate for his brother-in-law discovers the professor is a fraud and nearly shoots him.

title: The Seagull
author: Anton Chekhov
description: A young writer and an aspiring actress are destroyed by the older generation's selfishness on a lakeside estate, and a seagull is shot as a symbol.

title: Selected Stories
author: Anton Chekhov
description: The essential Chekhov stories—The Lady with the Dog, Ward No. 6, The Darling, Gooseberries—the short fiction that made the modern short story.

title: Fathers and Sons
author: Ivan Turgenev
description: A nihilist student clashes with his friend's liberal father on a provincial estate, and the generational war between Russian idealism and materialism begins.

title: First Love
author: Ivan Turgenev
description: A sixteen-year-old boy falls in love with his neighbor's daughter and discovers his father is his rival.

title: A Sportsman's Sketches
author: Ivan Turgenev
description: A hunter's observations of Russian peasant life—portraits so compassionate they helped persuade the tsar to free the serfs.

title: On the Eve
author: Ivan Turgenev
description: A young Russian woman falls for a Bulgarian revolutionary and follows him to fight for his country's independence, choosing passion over security.

title: Eugene Onegin
author: Alexander Pushkin
description: A bored aristocrat rejects a country girl's love, kills his best friend in a duel, and years later realizes she was the one—Russia's founding novel, written in verse.

title: The Captain's Daughter
author: Alexander Pushkin
description: A young officer during the Pugachev Rebellion falls in love with a captain's daughter and must navigate loyalty, honor, and a peasant insurgency.

title: The Queen of Spades
author: Alexander Pushkin
description: An officer obsesses over a countess who knows the secret of winning at cards, and his pursuit of the secret destroys him.

title: Oblomov
author: Ivan Goncharov
description: A Russian nobleman cannot get out of bed, and his inability to act—on love, on business, on life itself—becomes the definitive portrait of aristocratic paralysis.

title: A Hero of Our Time
author: Mikhail Lermontov
description: A Byronic officer in the Caucasus manipulates everyone around him out of boredom and self-loathing, in Russia's first psychological novel.

title: Chess Story
author: Stefan Zweig
description: A man who survived Gestapo isolation by playing chess against himself faces a world champion on an ocean liner, and the game threatens his sanity.

title: Beware of Pity
author: Stefan Zweig
description: A young officer's pity for a crippled girl is mistaken for love, and his cowardice destroys them both in Zweig's only novel.

title: The World of Yesterday
author: Stefan Zweig
description: Zweig's memoir of the golden age of European culture destroyed by two world wars, written in exile before his suicide.

title: Letter from an Unknown Woman
author: Stefan Zweig
description: A dying woman writes to the famous author she has loved since girlhood—he never recognized her once across all their encounters.

title: Amok
author: Stefan Zweig
description: A doctor in the Dutch East Indies is asked by a woman to perform an abortion and his obsession with her drives him to madness.

title: The Post-Office Girl
author: Stefan Zweig
