description: A poor Austrian post-office clerk tastes luxury at a Swiss resort and is destroyed by the return to poverty, in Zweig's posthumous novel.

title: The Radetzky March
author: Joseph Roth
description: Three generations of an Austro-Hungarian family decline in parallel with the empire itself, from the Battle of Solferino to World War I.

title: The Legend of the Holy Drinker
author: Joseph Roth
description: A Parisian clochard is lent money by a stranger and tries to repay it at a church, but drink and life keep intervening in Roth's last, gentlest work.

title: Job
author: Joseph Roth
description: A pious Jewish teacher in a Russian shtetl loses everything—home, family, faith—when his children emigrate to America.

title: Hotel Savoy
author: Joseph Roth
description: A soldier returns from a POW camp to a decaying hotel in a Polish border town where every floor represents a different rung of postwar society.

title: The Man Without Qualities
author: Robert Musil
description: A mathematician in 1913 Vienna is recruited for a committee to celebrate the emperor's jubilee, and the novel becomes a vast meditation on ideas, desire, and the death of a civilization.

title: The Confusions of Young Törless
author: Robert Musil
description: A student at a military academy watches two classmates torture a weaker boy and is fascinated rather than repelled, exploring the psychology of cruelty.

title: Zeno's Conscience
author: Italo Svevo
description: A Trieste businessman writes his memoirs for his psychoanalyst, lying constantly, in the funniest and most unreliable confession in European fiction.

title: At Swim-Two-Birds
author: Flann O'Brien
description: A lazy Dublin student writes a novel whose characters revolt against their author, creating a three-level metafiction that out-postmoderns postmodernism.

title: The Third Policeman
author: Flann O'Brien
description: A murderer in rural Ireland encounters a police station where the officers are obsessed with bicycles and the laws of physics don't apply—because he is already dead.

title: The Poor Mouth
author: Flann O'Brien
description: A savage parody of Irish-language autobiographies: a man in the rainiest village in Ireland lives in poverty so extreme it becomes surreal.

title: The Dalkey Archive
author: Flann O'Brien
description: A man discovers that James Joyce is alive and working as a barman, and De Selby has invented a substance that destroys time.

title: The Magician of Lublin
author: Isaac Bashevis Singer
description: A traveling magician in 19th-century Poland juggles five women and two religions until his duplicity collapses and he walls himself into a room to pray.

title: Enemies, A Love Story
author: Isaac Bashevis Singer
description: A Holocaust survivor in 1940s New York hides three women—the wife who saved him, the wife he married in America, and a mistress—from each other.

title: The Slave
author: Isaac Bashevis Singer
description: A Jewish man enslaved by Polish peasants after the Chmielnicki massacres falls in love with his master's daughter and must choose between faith and desire.

title: Shosha
author: Isaac Bashevis Singer
description: A writer in 1930s Warsaw marries his childhood sweetheart—a woman with the mind of a child—as the Nazi shadow falls over Poland.

title: Gimpel the Fool
author: Isaac Bashevis Singer
description: Stories of the vanished world of Polish Jewry, beginning with the title story of a man everyone deceives who chooses belief over cynicism.

title: Palace Walk
author: Naguib Mahfouz
description: A tyrannical Cairo patriarch rules his family with iron control while secretly carousing at night, as Egypt convulses under British occupation.

title: Palace of Desire
author: Naguib Mahfouz
description: The patriarch's sons pursue desire—one through politics, one through women—as the family fractures and Egypt modernizes.

title: Sugar Street
author: Naguib Mahfouz
description: The third generation comes of age between communism and Islamic fundamentalism as the family's saga reaches WWII.

title: Midaq Alley
author: Naguib Mahfouz
description: The residents of a Cairo alley—a pimp, a dentist, a baker's wife, a young woman dreaming of escape—live through WWII as the old world crumbles.

title: Children of the Alley
author: Naguib Mahfouz
description: The history of an alley in Cairo retells the stories of Adam, Moses, Jesus, and Muhammad as neighborhood strongmen, in the novel that got Mahfouz stabbed.

title: My Name Is Red
author: Orhan Pamuk
description: A murder among Ottoman miniature painters in 1591 Istanbul becomes a meditation on art, Western influence, and the tension between representation and idolatry.

title: Snow
author: Orhan Pamuk
description: A poet returns to a remote Turkish city during a blizzard and a military coup, caught between secularists and Islamists while a wave of suicides among girls goes unexplained.

title: The Museum of Innocence
author: Orhan Pamuk
description: A wealthy man becomes obsessed with a shopgirl in 1970s Istanbul and collects objects associated with her over decades, eventually building a real museum.

title: The New Life
author: Orhan Pamuk
description: A student reads a book that changes his life and sets out across Turkey searching for the new existence it promises.

title: The Black Book
author: Orhan Pamuk
description: A lawyer searches for his missing wife through the labyrinthine streets and stories of Istanbul, where identity is as unstable as narrative.

title: A Personal Matter
author: Kenzaburō Ōe
description: A man's son is born with a brain hernia and he must decide whether to let the baby die or accept his life as a father of a disabled child.

title: The Silent Cry
author: Kenzaburō Ōe
description: Two brothers return to their ancestral village where one reenacts a 19th-century peasant uprising while the other retreats into alcoholism and despair.

title: Teach Us to Outgrow Our Madness
author: Kenzaburō Ōe
description: Four novellas about fathers and disabled sons, the atomic bomb, and the search for meaning in postwar Japan.

title: The Interpreters
author: Wole Soyinka
description: Young Nigerian intellectuals try to make sense of their corrupt, post-independence society through art, journalism, and argument.

title: Death and the King's Horseman
author: Wole Soyinka
description: A Yoruba chief must follow his king into death by ritual suicide, but the British colonial officer intervenes, triggering a catastrophe for both cultures.

title: Season of Anomy
author: Wole Soyinka
description: A marketing executive in a corrupt African state joins the resistance and journeys through an increasingly nightmarish landscape of state violence.

title: Aké: The Years of Childhood
author: Wole Soyinka
description: Soyinka's memoir of growing up in colonial Nigeria—the parsonage, the markets, the uprising—written with the wonder and wit of the child he was.

title: Braiding Sweetgrass
author: Robin Wall Kimmerer
description: A botanist and Potawatomi woman weaves indigenous wisdom and scientific knowledge into essays about reciprocity between humans and the natural world.

title: Gathering Moss
author: Robin Wall Kimmerer
description: A bryologist's love letter to mosses—tiny plants most people never see—combining Native American traditions with botanical science.

title: Crying in H Mart
author: Michelle Zauner
description: A Korean-American musician grieves her mother's death through food—learning to cook Korean dishes as a way to hold onto her identity and her loss.

title: Me Talk Pretty One Day
author: David Sedaris
description: Sedaris moves to France, butchers the language, and recounts his childhood, his family, and his misadventures in essays that are among the funniest ever written.

title: Naked
author: David Sedaris
description: Essays about Sedaris's childhood—his OCD, his nudist neighbors, his stint as a hitchhiker—each one funnier and sadder than the last.

title: Dress Your Family in Corduroy and Denim
author: David Sedaris
description: Sedaris at his best: essays on his father's jazz obsession, his brother's roofing company, and life with Hugh in Normandy.

title: Calypso
author: David Sedaris
description: Sedaris confronts aging, his sister's suicide, and his family's dysfunction from his beach house in a darker, more tender collection.

title: When You Are Engulfed in Flames
author: David Sedaris
description: Moving to Tokyo to quit smoking, living with Hugh, and the absurdity of everyday life in Sedaris's most peripatetic collection.

title: Bad Feminist
author: Roxane Gay
description: Essays on feminism, race, pop culture, and the imperfection of trying to be a good person in a flawed world.

title: Hunger
author: Roxane Gay
description: Gay's memoir of her body—a gang rape at twelve, the weight she gained as armor, and the world's cruelty toward fat people.

title: I Feel Bad About My Neck
author: Nora Ephron
description: Essays on aging, maintenance, and the small indignities of being a woman over sixty, written with the wit that made Ephron a cultural icon.

title: Heartburn
author: Nora Ephron
description: A food writer's husband leaves her for another woman while she's pregnant, and she writes a cookbook/revenge novel about it.

title: Running with Scissors
author: Augusten Burroughs
description: A boy is handed over to his mother's insane psychiatrist and raised in a household of medicated chaos, sexual abuse, and zero boundaries.

title: Dry
author: Augusten Burroughs
description: Burroughs's memoir of his alcoholism in the New York advertising world and his attempt at sobriety as his best friend dies of AIDS.

title: The Sixth Extinction
author: Elizabeth Kolbert
description: Kolbert travels the world documenting species going extinct right now and argues humanity is causing a mass extinction event comparable to the one that killed the dinosaurs.

title: Under a White Sky
author: Elizabeth Kolbert
description: Humans have so altered the natural world that now we must intervene further to save it—gene editing, geoengineering, and the paradox of controlling nature to preserve it.

title: Men Explain Things to Me
author: Rebecca Solnit
description: The essay that coined 'mansplaining' plus six more on feminism, silence, and violence against women.

title: A Field Guide to Getting Lost
author: Rebecca Solnit
description: Essays on getting lost—in the wilderness, in memory, in art—as a creative and philosophical practice.

title: Wanderlust
author: Rebecca Solnit
description: A history of walking—from pilgrims to protesters to poets—as a political, spiritual, and creative act.

title: Daring Greatly
author: Brené Brown
description: Brown argues that vulnerability is not weakness but the source of courage, connection, and creativity.

title: The Gifts of Imperfection
author: Brené Brown
description: Ten guideposts for wholehearted living: letting go of perfectionism, cultivating self-compassion, and embracing authenticity.

title: Atlas of the Heart
author: Brené Brown
description: A guide to eighty-seven emotions and experiences, mapping the language of human feeling to build connection.

title: Being Mortal
author: Atul Gawande
description: A surgeon confronts medicine's failure to deal with aging and death, arguing for quality of life over quantity.

title: Complications
author: Atul Gawande
description: A surgical resident's honest account of medical uncertainty—what doctors don't know, the mistakes they make, and how they learn.

title: How to Be an Antiracist
author: Ibram X. Kendi
description: Kendi argues there is no such thing as being 'not racist'—you are either actively antiracist or passively supporting racist systems.

title: Stamped from the Beginning
author: Ibram X. Kendi
description: A history of racist ideas in America from Cotton Mather to the present, showing how they were created to justify racist policies, not the other way around.

title: Behave
author: Robert Sapolsky
description: Why humans do what they do—from neuroscience to evolutionary biology to culture—in the seconds, hours, years, and millennia before a behavior occurs.

title: A Primate's Memoir
author: Robert Sapolsky
description: A neuroscientist's account of twenty years studying baboons in Kenya, mixing primatology with African politics and dark comedy.

title: The Divine Comedy
author: Dante Alighieri
description: A poet journeys through Hell, Purgatory, and Paradise guided by Virgil and then Beatrice, in the poem that created the Italian language and mapped the afterlife.

title: The Aeneid
author: Virgil
description: A Trojan prince flees the burning city and journeys to Italy to found Rome, losing his lover Dido and fighting a war for his destined homeland.

title: Oedipus Rex
author: Sophocles
description: A king investigating a plague discovers he is the source—he killed his father and married his mother, and every step toward truth is a step toward destruction.

title: Antigone
author: Sophocles
description: A woman defies the king to bury her brother's body, choosing divine law over human law, and both are destroyed.

title: Metamorphoses
author: Ovid
description: Two hundred and fifty myths of transformation—gods into animals, mortals into trees, humans into stars—woven into a single epic from the creation of the world to the death of Julius Caesar.

title: Medea
author: Euripides
description: A foreign sorceress abandoned by her Greek husband takes the most devastating revenge possible—killing their children to destroy him.

title: The Bacchae
author: Euripides
description: Dionysus returns to Thebes to punish the king who denied his divinity, and the women of the city, driven mad, tear the king apart.

title: The Oresteia
author: Aeschylus
description: Three plays—Agamemnon returns from Troy and is murdered by his wife, their son kills his mother in revenge, and Athena invents the jury trial to end the cycle of blood.

title: Lysistrata
author: Aristophanes
description: The women of Greece withhold sex until their husbands agree to end the Peloponnesian War, in the world's first antiwar comedy.

title: What We Talk About When We Talk About Love
author: Raymond Carver
description: Seventeen stories stripped to the bone—alcoholics, waitresses, unemployed men—where what is not said is more devastating than what is.

title: Cathedral
author: Raymond Carver
description: Twelve stories including the title piece—a man draws a cathedral with a blind visitor and transcends his own limitations—Carver's most generous collection.

title: Will You Please Be Quiet, Please?
author: Raymond Carver
description: Carver's debut collection: twenty-two stories of blue-collar America where marriages crack, jobs are lost, and small cruelties accumulate.

title: Where I'm Calling From
author: Raymond Carver
description: The definitive selected stories, spanning Carver's career from the early minimalism to the later, more expansive work.

title: Interpreter of Maladies
author: Jhumpa Lahiri
description: Nine stories of Indian-Americans navigating two cultures—a tour guide who translates pain, a couple drifting apart over a shared loss, a woman's secret revealed by a child.

title: The Namesake
author: Jhumpa Lahiri
description: A boy named Gogol after the Russian writer by his Bengali parents struggles with his name, his identity, and the distance between immigrant expectation and American life.

title: Unaccustomed Earth
author: Jhumpa Lahiri
description: Eight stories of second-generation Bengali-Americans whose parents' sacrifices haunt their pursuit of love and independence.

title: The Lowland
author: Jhumpa Lahiri
description: Two brothers in Calcutta take different paths—one becomes a revolutionary, the other emigrates to America—and a single act of violence shapes both families for decades.

title: The Joy Luck Club
author: Amy Tan
description: Four Chinese immigrant mothers and their American-born daughters tell interlocking stories of sacrifice, misunderstanding, and the fierce love that survives cultural translation.

title: The Kitchen God's Wife
author: Amy Tan
description: A Chinese-American woman finally hears her mother's story—wartime China, an abusive first marriage, and the secret she has kept for fifty years.

title: The Bonesetter's Daughter
author: Amy Tan
description: A woman discovers her mother's memoir written in Chinese, revealing a past in rural China connected to oracle bones and a family curse.

title: The House on Mango Street
author: Sandra Cisneros
description: A Chicana girl in Chicago narrates her year on Mango Street in lyrical vignettes about poverty, machismo, and the dream of a house of her own.

title: Woman Hollering Creek
author: Sandra Cisneros
description: Twenty-two stories of Mexican-American women on both sides of the border—a girl who won't speak English, a woman who hears the creek calling her name.

title: Caramelo
author: Sandra Cisneros
description: A Mexican-American family's annual road trip from Chicago to Mexico City becomes an epic multigenerational saga narrated by the granddaughter and her dead grandmother.

title: Annie John
author: Jamaica Kincaid
description: A girl grows up in Antigua, bonded to and then violently separating from her mother, in crystalline prose about colonial childhood and female becoming.

title: Lucy
author: Jamaica Kincaid
description: A young Caribbean woman works as an au pair in New York and refuses every version of herself that others try to impose.

title: A Small Place
author: Jamaica Kincaid
description: Kincaid addresses the tourist directly: you who come to Antigua see paradise, but the people who live here see the ruins of colonialism everywhere.

title: The Autobiography of My Mother
author: Jamaica Kincaid
description: A Dominican woman narrates her life of refusal—of love, of motherhood, of the colonial world—in Kincaid's coldest and most radical novel.

title: Breath, Eyes, Memory
author: Edwidge Danticat
description: A girl raised by her aunt in Haiti is sent to her mother in New York, and the generational trauma of rape and testing connects them.

title: The Dew Breaker
author: Edwidge Danticat
description: Connected stories reveal that a quiet Brooklyn barber was once a torturer in Haiti's Duvalier regime, and his past reaches everyone around him.

title: Krik? Krak!
author: Edwidge Danticat
description: Nine stories of Haitian life—boat people, political prisoners, market women—each beginning with the call-and-response that starts Haitian storytelling.

title: The Farming of Bones
author: Edwidge Danticat
description: A Haitian servant in the Dominican Republic witnesses the 1937 massacre of Haitian workers ordered by Trujillo.

title: The Golden Notebook
author: Doris Lessing
description: A woman keeps four notebooks—black for Africa, red for politics, yellow for fiction, blue for diary—and the novel is about what happens when she tries to combine them.

title: The Grass Is Singing
author: Doris Lessing
description: A white farmer's wife in Rhodesia is murdered by her Black servant, and the novel traces the racial and sexual dynamics that made the killing inevitable.

title: The Good Terrorist
author: Doris Lessing
description: A middle-class woman joins a squatter commune of revolutionary poseurs in London and accidentally enables real terrorism.

title: The Fifth Child
author: Doris Lessing
description: A happy English family is destroyed by their fifth child—a violent, possibly inhuman throwback who cannot be loved or controlled.

title: July's People
author: Nadine Gordimer
description: A white South African family flees revolution and is sheltered by their Black servant in his village, and every power dynamic inverts.

title: The Conservationist
author: Nadine Gordimer
description: A wealthy white farmer finds a Black man's body on his land and it keeps resurfacing—literally and symbolically—as apartheid South Africa denies what it has buried.

title: Burger's Daughter
author: Nadine Gordimer
description: The daughter of a martyred anti-apartheid activist must decide whether to continue her father's fight or live her own life.

title: Possession
author: A.S. Byatt
description: Two modern scholars discover a secret love affair between two Victorian poets, and the academic investigation becomes its own love story.

title: The Children's Book
author: A.S. Byatt
description: An Edwardian children's author's family and circle navigate art, socialism, sex, and the approach of WWI in Byatt's most ambitious novel.

title: Birds of America
author: Lorrie Moore
description: Twelve stories of American loneliness—a woman caring for a dying baby, a wife stuck at a conference, a mother joke-cracking through catastrophe—Moore at her devastating best.

title: Self-Help
author: Lorrie Moore
description: Nine stories, several written in second person as mock self-help instructions—how to be a writer, how to have an affair, how to lose everything.

title: A Gate at the Stairs
author: Lorrie Moore
description: A college student babysits for a couple adopting a biracial child in post-9/11 America, and race, grief, and war converge on a small Midwestern town.

title: Who Will Run the Frog Hospital?
author: Lorrie Moore
description: A woman on vacation with her distant husband remembers the summer she was fifteen and her best friend was the most beautiful girl in town.

title: Matrix
author: Lauren Groff
description: Marie de France, exiled to a crumbling English abbey, transforms it into a wealthy, powerful female community through sheer force of will.

title: Fates and Furies
author: Lauren Groff
description: A marriage told in two halves—his version is a golden success story, hers reveals the fury and sacrifice underneath.

title: Florida
author: Lauren Groff
description: Eleven stories set in Florida where the subtropical landscape—snakes, hurricanes, sinkholes—mirrors the danger beneath domestic surfaces.

title: The Vaster Wilds
author: Lauren Groff
description: A servant girl flees the Jamestown colony into the wilderness and must survive alone as winter approaches.

title: Luster
author: Raven Leilani
description: A young Black woman begins an affair with a white man in an open marriage and moves in with his family, including his adopted Black daughter.

title: Real Life
author: Brandon Taylor
description: A Black gay biochemistry student from Alabama navigates a white Midwestern university over one weekend of microaggressions, desire, and quiet rage.

title: Filthy Animals
author: Brandon Taylor
description: Connected stories of young people in a Midwestern college town—dancers, grad students, lovers—trying to feel something real through their defenses.

title: The Late Americans
author: Brandon Taylor
description: A group of MFA students and their orbits in Iowa City over a year of workshops, relationships, and the question of whether art matters.

title: Detransition, Baby
author: Torrey Peters
description: A trans woman, her ex who detransitioned, and his pregnant boss negotiate whether three people can raise a baby together in the most talked-about debut of its year.

title: Interview with the Vampire
author: Anne Rice
description: A vampire tells a reporter the story of his making—by the charismatic Lestat in 18th-century New Orleans—and the centuries of beauty and horror that followed.

title: The Vampire Lestat
author: Anne Rice
description: Lestat tells his own side of the story—from 18th-century France to ancient Egypt—and becomes a rock star to provoke the vampire world into revealing itself.

title: The Queen of the Damned
author: Anne Rice
description: The mother of all vampires awakens and plans to rule the world, and only Lestat's bond with her can stop the annihilation of his kind.

title: The Witching Hour
author: Anne Rice
description: A neurosurgeon returns to New Orleans and discovers she is the heir to a dynasty of witches haunted by a powerful spirit named Lasher.

title: Storm Front
author: Jim Butcher
description: Chicago's only professional wizard investigates a double murder committed with dark magic while the White Council watches for any excuse to execute him.

title: Fool Moon
author: Jim Butcher
description: Harry Dresden faces werewolves—four different kinds—as a serial killer with supernatural connections terrorizes Chicago.

title: Grave Peril
author: Jim Butcher
description: A ghost uprising in Chicago leads Harry into a trap set by the Red Court vampires, and his rescue of a friend starts a war.

title: Dead Beat
author: Jim Butcher
description: A necromancer's convention in Chicago forces Harry to raise a dinosaur skeleton and ride it into battle on Halloween night.

title: Changes
author: Jim Butcher
description: Harry discovers he has a daughter who has been kidnapped by the Red Court, and he tears down everything to save her—literally everything.

title: Battle Ground
author: Jim Butcher
description: The Fomor and their Titan attack Chicago in the most devastating battle the Dresden Files has ever seen.

title: Charlie and the Chocolate Factory
author: Roald Dahl
description: A poor boy wins a golden ticket to tour Willy Wonka's magical chocolate factory, where bratty children are eliminated one by one.

title: Matilda
author: Roald Dahl
description: A brilliant girl with telekinetic powers is neglected by her awful parents and terrorized by a monstrous headmistress, until she fights back.

title: James and the Giant Peach
author: Roald Dahl
description: An orphan boy escapes his cruel aunts inside a magical giant peach that rolls to the sea and floats to New York with a crew of giant insects.

title: The BFG
author: Roald Dahl
description: A Big Friendly Giant who catches dreams befriends a girl and together they convince the Queen to stop the other giants from eating children.

title: The Witches
author: Roald Dahl
description: A boy discovers that the women at a seaside hotel are actually witches plotting to turn all the children of England into mice.

title: Danny, the Champion of the World
author: Roald Dahl
description: A boy and his father live in a caravan behind a filling station and their poaching adventures become a war against a cruel landowner.

title: Boy
author: Roald Dahl
description: Dahl's memoir of his childhood—boarding school cruelty, chocolate testing for Cadbury, and the experiences that became his fiction.

title: Kiss Kiss
author: Roald Dahl
description: Eleven adult short stories: a woman murders her husband with a frozen leg of lamb, a man bets his finger in a lighter game, a sound machine hears flowers screaming.

title: Someone Like You
author: Roald Dahl
description: Eighteen macabre stories including the tattoo collector who skins his prize from a living man and the landlady who taxidermies her guests.

title: The Bad Beginning
author: Lemony Snicket
description: Three orphans are placed in the care of the villainous Count Olaf, who wants their fortune, in the first of thirteen unfortunate events.

title: The Reptile Room
author: Lemony Snicket
description: The Baudelaires find a kind guardian in a herpetologist, but Count Olaf arrives in disguise and murder follows.

title: The Wide Window
author: Lemony Snicket
description: The orphans live with a fearful aunt on a lake where Count Olaf's schemes involve leeches, hurricanes, and grammar.

title: The Penultimate Peril
author: Lemony Snicket
description: Every villain and ally converges at a hotel where the Baudelaires must finally decide whose side they are on.

title: The End
author: Lemony Snicket
description: The Baudelaires wash up on a desert island where a utopian community hides the final secrets of VFD.

title: A Wrinkle in Time
author: Madeleine L'Engle
description: A girl, her brother, and a friend tesseract through space and time to rescue her father from a planet controlled by a disembodied brain.

title: A Wind in the Door
author: Madeleine L'Engle
description: Charles Wallace is dying and Meg must journey inside his cells to fight creatures destroying him from within.

title: A Swiftly Tilting Planet
author: Madeleine L'Engle
description: Charles Wallace travels through time on a unicorn to change the past and prevent nuclear war.

title: The Essential Rumi
author: Rumi
description: Coleman Barks's translations of Rumi's ecstatic Sufi poetry—about love, loss, and the divine—that made a 13th-century Persian poet a 21st-century bestseller.

title: Twenty Love Poems and a Song of Despair
author: Pablo Neruda
description: The collection that made Neruda famous at nineteen—erotic, melancholy poems of desire and loss set against the Chilean landscape.

title: Canto General
author: Pablo Neruda
description: An epic poem covering the entire history of the Americas, from Machu Picchu to the copper miners, in Neruda's most politically ambitious work.

title: The Waste Land
author: T.S. Eliot
description: The poem that defined modernism—fragments of myth, conversation, and allusion depicting a civilization in spiritual collapse after World War I.

title: Four Quartets
author: T.S. Eliot
description: Four meditations on time, history, and eternity set in places meaningful to Eliot—an English garden, a Massachusetts coast, a London church, a Somerset village.

title: Collected Poems 1909-1962
author: T.S. Eliot
description: The complete poems including Prufrock, The Waste Land, The Hollow Men, Ash Wednesday, and Four Quartets.

title: Leaves of Grass
author: Walt Whitman
description: The collection Whitman revised his entire life—Song of Myself, I Sing the Body Electric, Crossing Brooklyn Ferry—inventing American poetry from the body up.

title: The Complete Poems
author: Emily Dickinson
description: Nearly 1,800 poems written in a bedroom in Amherst—about death, nature, God, and ecstasy—most unpublished in her lifetime, all essential.

title: The Poetry of Robert Frost
author: Robert Frost
description: The complete poems: The Road Not Taken, Stopping by Woods, Mending Wall, Fire and Ice—deceptively simple surfaces hiding dark complexity.

title: The Collected Poems of W.B. Yeats
author: W.B. Yeats
description: From the Celtic Twilight to the fierce late poems—Easter 1916, The Second Coming, Sailing to Byzantium, Leda and the Swan—Ireland's greatest poet.

title: The Collected Poems of Langston Hughes
author: Langston Hughes
description: Every poem by the poet laureate of the Harlem Renaissance—The Negro Speaks of Rivers, Harlem, Theme for English B—jazz, blues, and Black American life.

title: Not Without Laughter
author: Langston Hughes
description: A Black boy grows up in small-town Kansas with his grandmother, his blues-singing aunt, and his ambitious mother in Hughes's only novel.

title: Red Sorghum
author: Mo Yan
description: A family saga set among sorghum fields in northeast China during the Japanese occupation, told in a blood-red mythic voice.

title: Life and Death Are Wearing Me Out
author: Mo Yan
description: A landlord executed during land reform is reincarnated as a donkey, an ox, a pig, a dog, and a monkey, watching Chinese history from animal eyes.

title: The Garlic Ballads
author: Mo Yan
description: Peasant farmers riot when corrupt officials let their garlic crop rot, and the novel intercuts the uprising with a love story and folk songs.

title: The General of the Dead Army
author: Ismail Kadare
description: An Italian general travels through Albania twenty years after WWII to recover the bones of his fallen soldiers, and the landscape refuses to give them up.

title: Broken April
author: Ismail Kadare
description: A young man in the Albanian highlands is caught in a blood feud that demands he kill and then be killed, and a honeymooning couple stumbles into his world.

title: The Palace of Dreams
author: Ismail Kadare
description: A man joins a government ministry that collects and interprets the dreams of every citizen to find the Master Dream that will guide the empire.

title: Chronicle in Stone
author: Ismail Kadare
description: A boy in an Albanian city watches WWII unfold—Italian occupation, Greek invasion, German bombardment—from the ancient stone streets of his hometown.

title: A Tale of Love and Darkness
author: Amos Oz
description: Oz's memoir of growing up in Jerusalem during the founding of Israel, his mother's suicide, and his escape to a kibbutz to reinvent himself.

title: My Michael
author: Amos Oz
description: A Jerusalem woman's marriage to a mild geology student suffocates her, and she retreats into fantasies about two Arab twins from her childhood.

title: Black Box
author: Amos Oz
description: A divorced couple's correspondence about their troubled son becomes a battle over Israel itself—secular vs. religious, Ashkenazi vs. Sephardi.

title: Voss
author: Patrick White
description: A German explorer leads a doomed expedition across the Australian desert while a woman in Sydney forms a telepathic bond with him across the continent.

title: The Tree of Man
author: Patrick White
description: A man clears a patch of Australian bush and builds a life with his wife over decades, and White makes the ordinary luminous and strange.

title: Riders in the Chariot
author: Patrick White
description: Four outcasts in suburban Sydney—a Holocaust survivor, an Aboriginal painter, a spinster, and a washerwoman—share a mystical vision of Ezekiel's chariot.

title: Kitchen
author: Banana Yoshimoto
description: A grieving young woman finds comfort in the kitchen of a transgender mother and her son, in the gentle novella that made Yoshimoto famous worldwide.

title: Goodbye Tsugumi
author: Banana Yoshimoto
description: A girl spends a final summer with her beautiful, selfish, chronically ill cousin at a seaside inn before the family moves away.

title: N.P.
author: Banana Yoshimoto
description: A circle of young people in Tokyo are connected by a dead American writer's untranslated story that drives its translators to suicide.

title: Three Act Tragedy
author: Agatha Christie
description: A clergyman drops dead at a dinner party with no apparent poison, and Poirot investigates when it happens again at another gathering.

title: Cards on the Table
author: Agatha Christie
description: A collector of murderers invites four suspects and four detectives to a bridge party, and the host is stabbed during the game.

title: Dumb Witness
author: Agatha Christie
description: An elderly woman writes to Poirot about a suspicious fall on the stairs, but by the time he reads the letter she is dead.

title: One, Two, Buckle My Shoe
author: Agatha Christie
description: Poirot's dentist is found shot dead and the case connects to international finance and political assassination.

title: N or M?
author: Agatha Christie
description: Tommy and Tuppence Beresford go undercover at a seaside guesthouse to find a Nazi spy among the residents during WWII.

title: The Moving Finger
author: Agatha Christie
description: Poison-pen letters terrorize a village and then someone dies, and Miss Marple arrives to sort truth from malice.

title: Taken at the Flood
author: Agatha Christie
description: A wealthy man dies and his young widow inherits, then a stranger claims to be a relative thought killed in the war.

title: They Do It with Mirrors
author: Agatha Christie
description: Miss Marple visits a friend living in a house converted to a rehabilitation center for young criminals, and murder follows.

title: Destination Unknown
author: Agatha Christie
description: A woman assumed to be suicidal is recruited by intelligence to impersonate a dead woman and infiltrate a mysterious organization kidnapping scientists.

title: Ordeal by Innocence
author: Agatha Christie
description: A man provides an alibi that exonerates a convicted murderer posthumously—but if he didn't do it, someone in the family did.

title: The Mirror Crack'd from Side to Side
author: Agatha Christie
description: A famous actress moves to Miss Marple's village and a woman dies at her party, poisoned by a cocktail meant for someone else.

title: At Bertram's Hotel
author: Agatha Christie
description: Miss Marple stays at a London hotel that is suspiciously perfect—too perfect, as if the Edwardian charm is a front for something criminal.

title: Endless Night
author: Agatha Christie
description: A chauffeur marries a rich American heiress and builds his dream house on cursed land, in Christie's darkest and most psychological novel.

title: Elephants Can Remember
author: Agatha Christie
description: Poirot and Ariadne Oliver investigate a decades-old double death—was it murder-suicide or a double murder?

title: Postern of Fate
author: Agatha Christie
description: Tommy and Tuppence find a coded message in a children's book in their new house, connecting to an old murder and wartime espionage.

title: Angels Flight
author: Michael Connelly
description: A prominent civil rights attorney is murdered on the Angels Flight funicular in downtown LA, and Bosch must solve it before the city erupts.

title: A Darkness More Than Night
author: Michael Connelly
description: Terry McCaleb from Blood Work investigates whether Harry Bosch committed a murder, putting two of Connelly's protagonists against each other.

title: The Narrows
author: Michael Connelly
description: The Poet killer from Connelly's standalone has returned, and retired FBI agent Rachel Walling recruits Bosch to help find him.

title: Echo Park
author: Michael Connelly
description: A serial killer offers to reveal where he buried his victims, including the girl from Bosch's oldest unsolved case, but something doesn't add up.

title: Nine Dragons
author: Michael Connelly
description: A shopkeeper is murdered in Bosch's territory and the trail leads to Hong Kong, where his daughter has been kidnapped.

title: The Wrong Side of Goodbye
author: Michael Connelly
description: Bosch works two cases: a dying billionaire's search for a lost heir and a serial rapist terrorizing San Fernando Valley.

title: The Law of Innocence
author: Michael Connelly
description: Mickey Haller is arrested for murder and must defend himself from behind bars while his team investigates who framed him.

title: The Chamber
author: John Grisham
description: A young lawyer takes on the case of his Klan-member grandfather on death row for a bombing that killed two children.

title: The Brethren
author: John Grisham
description: Three imprisoned judges run a blackmail scam from their cells and accidentally target a CIA-backed presidential candidate.

title: The King of Torts
author: John Grisham
description: A public defender stumbles onto a mass tort case that makes him rich overnight, but the fortune is built on sand.

title: The Confession
author: John Grisham
description: A dying man confesses to a murder for which an innocent man is about to be executed, and the race against the clock begins.

title: Sycamore Row
author: John Grisham
description: Jake Brigance from A Time to Kill returns to defend a will that leaves a fortune to a Black housekeeper, outraging the white family.

title: The Reckoning
author: John Grisham
description: A decorated WWII hero walks into a church and kills the pastor, then refuses to explain why.

title: A Time for Mercy
author: John Grisham
description: Jake Brigance defends a sixteen-year-old boy who killed a deputy sheriff, and the case splits the town of Clanton again.

title: No Second Chance
author: Harlan Coben
description: A doctor is shot, his wife is killed, and their baby daughter vanishes—then he receives a ransom demand six months later.

title: Just One Look
author: Harlan Coben
description: A woman finds an old photograph in her husband's things and he disappears the moment she shows it to him.

title: Caught
author: Harlan Coben
description: A social worker who catches online predators discovers her latest target may be connected to a missing teenager and a web of lies.

title: Six Years
author: Harlan Coben
description: A man who promised to stay away from the woman he loved for six years searches for her and discovers she doesn't exist.

title: Run Away
author: Harlan Coben
description: A father finds his drug-addicted daughter playing guitar in Central Park and his attempt to bring her home sets off a chain of violence and revelations.

title: Echo Burning
author: Lee Child
description: Reacher hitchhikes into a Texas ranching family's domestic war and must figure out who's trying to kill whom before a murdered child appears.

title: Without Fail
author: Lee Child
description: Reacher is hired by the Secret Service to find weaknesses in the Vice President-elect's security by trying to assassinate him.

title: Persuader
author: Lee Child
description: Reacher goes undercover inside a drug smuggler's compound on the Maine coast to rescue a DEA agent and settle an old score.

title: The Enemy
author: Lee Child
description: A prequel: Reacher as an MP in 1990 investigates a general's death on New Year's Eve as the Cold War ends and the army reorganizes.

title: Bad Luck and Trouble
author: Lee Child
description: Members of Reacher's old MP unit are being killed, and the survivors reunite to find out who's targeting them and why.

title: Nothing to Lose
author: Lee Child
description: Reacher walks into a town called Despair and is immediately thrown out, so naturally he goes right back.

title: Gone Tomorrow
author: Lee Child
description: Reacher spots a suicide bomber on the New York subway and his intervention pulls him into a conspiracy involving a senator and the Pentagon.

title: A Wanted Man
author: Lee Child
description: Reacher hitches a ride with three people and realizes two of them just killed the third person's colleague.

title: Make Me
author: Lee Child
description: Reacher gets off a train in a town called Mother's Rest to find out why it's called that, and the answer is worse than he imagined.

title: The Midnight Line
author: Lee Child
description: Reacher finds a West Point class ring in a pawnshop and tracks its owner across the Midwest, uncovering an opioid operation.

title: Blue Moon
author: Lee Child
description: Reacher helps an elderly couple being squeezed by a loan shark and gets caught between two rival criminal organizations.

title: Blindsighted
author: Karin Slaughter
description: A college professor is found gutted in a diner, and police chief Jeffrey Tolliver and medical examiner Sara Linton investigate their small Georgia town's secrets.

title: A Faint Cold Fear
author: Karin Slaughter
description: A series of apparent suicides on a college campus turn out to be murders, and Sara and Jeffrey must untangle a conspiracy of silence.

title: The Kept Woman
author: Karin Slaughter
description: Will Trent investigates a murder at an abandoned construction site that connects to a cold case and threatens to expose Sara's past.

title: The Silent Wife
author: Karin Slaughter
description: Will Trent investigates a prisoner's claims of innocence that connect to a current series of disappearances and a secret from Jeffrey's past.

title: After That Night
author: Karin Slaughter
description: Sara is attacked at a hospital fundraiser and the investigation connects to a fifteen-year-old case she thought was closed.

title: The Brutal Telling
author: Louise Penny
description: A stranger's body is found in the local bistro in Three Pines, and no one knows who he is or how he got there.

title: A Trick of the Light
author: Louise Penny
description: A woman is found dead in Clara's garden the morning after her triumphant art show, and Gamache investigates the art world's jealousies.

title: The Beautiful Mystery
author: Louise Penny
description: A monk is murdered in a remote monastery famous for its Gregorian chants, and Gamache investigates while his own department conspires against him.

title: How the Light Gets In
author: Louise Penny
description: Gamache's investigation of corruption within the Sûreté reaches its climax as he risks everything to expose the rot at the top.

title: The Long Way Home
author: Louise Penny
description: Gamache, newly retired, helps Clara search for her missing husband and the trail leads through the Quebec art world.

title: The Nature of the Beast
author: Louise Penny
description: A boy who cries wolf is found dead in the woods, and his wild stories about a monster turn out to be connected to a real weapon of mass destruction.

title: Glass Houses
author: Louise Penny
description: A mysterious robed figure appears on the Three Pines village green and stands motionless, and Gamache knows exactly who it is—and what's coming.

title: Kingdom of the Blind
author: Louise Penny
description: Gamache is named executor of a bizarre will by a woman he's never met while investigating a deadly opioid ring.

title: All the Devils Are Here
author: Louise Penny
description: Gamache's godfather is attacked in Paris and the investigation leads to a multinational engineering firm and the secrets of WWII.

title: A World of Curiosities
author: Louise Penny
description: A cold case from Gamache's first year as a homicide detective resurfaces when a mysterious map arrives in Three Pines.

title: Cockroaches
author: Jo Nesbø
description: Harry investigates the murder of a Norwegian ambassador in a Bangkok brothel in the second Hole novel.

title: Nemesis
author: Jo Nesbø
description: Harry investigates a series of bank robberies connected to a murder while battling alcoholism and a woman from his past.

title: The Redeemer
author: Jo Nesbø
description: A man is shot at a Salvation Army concert and Harry must find the killer among the city's underworld and aid organizations.

title: Phantom
author: Jo Nesbø
description: Harry returns to Oslo when his ex's son is arrested for murder, and discovers the drug trade that connects to the highest levels of power.

title: Police
author: Jo Nesbø
description: Someone is killing police officers at the scenes of their unsolved murders, and the suspects include Harry himself.

title: The Thirst
author: Jo Nesbø
description: A Tinder killer is targeting women in Oslo and Harry is dragged out of retirement to hunt a murderer who may be a vampire.

title: Killing Moon
author: Jo Nesbø
description: Harry, disgraced and living in Los Angeles, is lured back by a case that connects to his past and a new kind of serial killer.

title: The Duke and I
author: Julia Quinn
description: Daphne Bridgerton and the Duke of Hastings fake a courtship to solve their respective problems with the marriage mart, and the pretense becomes real.

title: The Viscount Who Loved Me
author: Julia Quinn
description: Anthony Bridgerton decides to marry for duty, but his chosen bride's older sister stands in his way—and becomes impossible to resist.

title: An Offer from a Gentleman
author: Julia Quinn
description: A Cinderella story: Benedict Bridgerton falls for a mysterious woman at a masquerade ball and spends years searching for her.

title: Romancing Mister Bridgerton
author: Julia Quinn
description: Colin Bridgerton finally notices Penelope Featherington, who has loved him since childhood—and who is secretly the notorious gossip columnist Lady Whistledown.

title: Devil in Winter
author: Lisa Kleypas
description: A shy wallflower proposes a marriage of convenience to the most notorious rake in London to escape her abusive family.

title: It Happened One Autumn
author: Lisa Kleypas
description: A brash American heiress clashes with a proper British lord, and their mutual disdain turns to passion.

title: Dreaming of You
author: Lisa Kleypas
description: A country author researching London's underworld falls for the dangerous owner of a gambling club.

title: The Duchess Deal
author: Tessa Dare
description: A scarred war-hero duke needs an heir and marries his ex-fiancée's seamstress, expecting nothing but duty—and getting everything.

title: A Week to Be Wicked
author: Tessa Dare
description: A prim geologist and a rakish viscount fake an elopement so she can attend a scientific symposium, and the road trip changes everything.

title: The Wallflower Wager
author: Tessa Dare
description: A duke who buys a house to flip must deal with the animal-hoarding spinster next door who refuses to sell.

title: Say Yes to the Marquess
author: Tessa Dare
description: A woman engaged to a marquess for eight years falls for his brother, a retired bare-knuckle boxer, while planning the wedding.

title: When You Are Mine
author: Kennedy Ryan
description: Best friends fall in love while one is married to an abusive man, and they must fight through guilt, loyalty, and trauma.

title: Before I Let Go
author: Kennedy Ryan
description: A divorced couple reunites when he emerges from a coma and doesn't remember why they separated.

title: The Bromance Book Club
author: Lyssa Kay Adams
description: A baseball player whose wife kicked him out joins a secret book club of men who read romance novels to save their marriages.

title: Nine Rules to Break When Romancing a Rake
author: Sarah MacLean
description: A wallflower makes a list of scandalous things she's never done and enlists a notorious rake to help her check them off.

title: A Rogue by Any Other Name
author: Sarah MacLean
description: A man who lost everything to gambling is given a chance to reclaim his fortune—by marrying his childhood friend in a marriage of convenience.

title: Vision in White
author: Nora Roberts
description: A wedding photographer at a Connecticut venue falls for the best friend of a groom, in the first of the Bride Quartet.

title: The Search
author: Nora Roberts
description: A search-and-rescue dog trainer on a Pacific Northwest island becomes the target of a serial killer while falling for a new neighbor.

title: Hideaway
author: Nora Roberts
description: A woman hiding from the man who murdered her family must decide whether to run again or fight when he finds her.

title: Without Merit
author: Colleen Hoover
description: A family of secrets lives in a converted church, and the daughter who sees through everyone's lies can't face her own.

title: Too Late
author: Colleen Hoover
description: A woman trapped in a relationship with a drug dealer is drawn to an undercover cop, and all three lives are at risk.

title: Safe Haven
author: Nicholas Sparks
description: A woman arrives in a small North Carolina town carrying secrets and a new identity, and falls for a widowed shopkeeper while danger follows.

title: The Lucky One
author: Nicholas Sparks
description: A Marine who survived three tours in Iraq walks across the country to find the woman in a photograph he believes saved his life.

title: See Me
author: Nicholas Sparks
description: A man with a violent past falls for a lawyer who is being stalked, and both must face what they've been hiding.

title: Two by Two
author: Nicholas Sparks
description: A man's marriage collapses and he must raise his daughter alone while rebuilding everything he thought he had.

title: 21 Lessons for the 21st Century
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: Harari tackles the urgent questions of the present: terrorism, fake news, AI, and the crisis of liberal democracy.

title: Why We Sleep
author: Matthew Walker
description: A neuroscientist explains why sleep is the most important thing you do and how modern life is destroying it, with devastating consequences.

title: Think Again
author: Adam Grant
description: An organizational psychologist argues that the ability to rethink and unlearn is more important than raw intelligence.

title: Give and Take
author: Adam Grant
description: Grant shows that the most successful people are givers—not takers or matchers—and explains why generosity is a competitive advantage.

title: Grit
author: Angela Duckworth
description: A psychologist argues that passion and perseverance matter more than talent, backed by research on West Point cadets, spelling bee champions, and Green Berets.

title: Start with Why
author: Simon Sinek
description: Leaders who inspire action all communicate the same way—they start with why, not what—and Sinek explains the biology behind it.

title: Leaders Eat Last
author: Simon Sinek
description: Great leaders create environments of trust where people feel safe, using biology and military examples to show how.

title: The Black Swan
author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb
description: Rare, unpredictable events drive history and markets, and we are terrible at seeing them coming because our brains crave narrative and certainty.

title: Antifragile
author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb
description: Some things benefit from shock and volatility—Taleb argues we should build systems that get stronger from disorder rather than merely surviving it.

title: The Better Angels of Our Nature
author: Steven Pinker
description: Violence has declined dramatically over human history, and Pinker marshals data from centuries of warfare, murder, and cruelty to prove it.

title: Enlightenment Now
author: Steven Pinker
description: Life, health, prosperity, and peace are improving by almost every measure, and the values of the Enlightenment are the reason.

title: What the Dog Saw
author: Malcolm Gladwell
description: Collected New Yorker pieces on ketchup, hair dye, plagiarism, criminal profiling, and why some people choke while others panic.

title: The Omnivore's Dilemma
author: Michael Pollan
description: Pollan traces four meals from source to plate—industrial, organic, local, and foraged—to answer the question: what should we eat?

title: In Defense of Food
author: Michael Pollan
description: Eat food. Not too much. Mostly plants. Seven words that became a manifesto against the Western diet.

title: How to Change Your Mind
author: Michael Pollan
description: Pollan explores the new science of psychedelics—psilocybin, LSD, DMT—and their potential to treat depression, addiction, and fear of death.

title: Stiff
author: Mary Roach
description: What happens to bodies after death—crash testing, decomposition research, crucifixion experiments—told with irreverent curiosity and dark humor.

title: Bonk
author: Mary Roach
description: The science of sex: from Masters and Johnson to penis cameras to artificial insemination in pigs, Roach investigates what researchers have learned about human arousal.

title: Gulp
author: Mary Roach
description: The alimentary canal from mouth to exit: chewing, saliva, stomach acid, and everything that happens between swallowing and the inevitable conclusion.

title: So You've Been Publicly Shamed
author: Jon Ronson
description: Ronson investigates people whose lives were destroyed by social media pile-ons and the strange new era of public shaming.

title: The Psychopath Test
author: Jon Ronson
description: Ronson learns to spot psychopaths using a clinical checklist and discovers they may be running the world.

title: Four Thousand Weeks
author: Oliver Burkeman
description: Time management is a losing game—you have roughly four thousand weeks to live, and the only sane response is to stop trying to do everything.

title: Greenlights
author: Matthew McConaughey
description: The actor's unconventional memoir built from decades of journals: part philosophy, part adventure story, part self-help from a man who has lived fully.

title: Spare
author: Prince Harry
description: The Duke of Sussex's memoir of growing up in the royal family, losing his mother, serving in Afghanistan, and the rift that led him to leave.

title: Will
author: Will Smith
description: Smith's memoir of his childhood, his father's violence, his rise to fame, and the internal struggles behind the world's biggest smile.

title: Open
author: Andre Agassi
description: The tennis champion confesses he hated tennis from childhood, was forced into it by his father, and used crystal meth during his career.

title: Shoe Dog
author: Phil Knight
description: Nike's founder recounts the company's scrappy early years—importing Japanese running shoes, near-constant bankruptcy, and the team that built a global brand from nothing.

title: Père Goriot
author: Honoré de Balzac
description: A father bankrupts himself for his ungrateful daughters in a Paris boarding house, while a young law student watches and learns how power works.

title: Lost Illusions
author: Honoré de Balzac
description: A provincial poet comes to Paris seeking literary fame and is devoured by journalism, debt, and ambition.

title: Cousin Bette
author: Honoré de Balzac
description: A plain, bitter spinster orchestrates the ruin of her wealthy relatives through patience and a beautiful courtesan.

title: Eugénie Grandet
author: Honoré de Balzac
description: A miser's daughter falls for her bankrupt cousin and her father's greed destroys every possibility of love.

title: Germinal
author: Émile Zola
description: A young man arrives at a northern French coal mine and leads the starving miners in a strike that becomes a violent uprising.

title: Nana
author: Émile Zola
description: A beautiful actress with no talent rises to become the most expensive courtesan in Paris, destroying every man who touches her.

title: L'Assommoir
author: Émile Zola
description: A washerwoman in working-class Paris rises through hard work, marries, is destroyed by her husband's alcoholism, and dies in poverty.

title: Thérèse Raquin
author: Émile Zola
description: A woman and her lover murder her husband and are consumed by guilt that manifests as the dead man's presence in their bed.

title: La Bête Humaine
author: Émile Zola
description: A train engineer with homicidal urges becomes entangled in a murder on the Paris-Le Havre line, and the railway itself becomes a character.

title: The Red and the Black
author: Stendhal
description: An ambitious young man from the provinces seduces his way upward through Restoration France, torn between the red of the army and the black of the clergy.

title: The Charterhouse of Parma
author: Stendhal
description: A young Italian nobleman stumbles into Waterloo, flees to the court of Parma, is imprisoned in a tower, and falls in love with the jailer's daughter.

title: Tristram Shandy
author: Laurence Sterne
description: A man tries to tell the story of his life but can't get past his own birth because every detail spawns a digression, inventing the postmodern novel 200 years early.

title: Tom Jones
author: Henry Fielding
description: A foundling's picaresque journey through 18th-century England—brawls, love affairs, mistaken identities—in the first great English comic novel.

title: Robinson Crusoe
author: Daniel Defoe
description: A shipwrecked man survives alone on a Caribbean island for twenty-eight years, building a civilization from nothing.

title: Moll Flanders
author: Daniel Defoe
description: A woman born in Newgate Prison narrates her life of crime, marriage, prostitution, and eventual redemption in colonial America.

title: Gulliver's Travels
author: Jonathan Swift
description: A ship's surgeon visits tiny people, giants, a flying island of mad scientists, and intelligent horses, each voyage a savage satire of humanity.

title: Candide
author: Voltaire
description: An optimistic young man is expelled from paradise and endures every disaster the 18th century can inflict—war, earthquake, the Inquisition—while his tutor insists this is the best of all possible worlds.

title: The Lifted Veil
author: George Eliot
description: A man cursed with the ability to read minds and see the future marries a woman whose thoughts he cannot penetrate—until blood transfusion reveals her murderous intent.

title: A Pair of Blue Eyes
author: Thomas Hardy
description: Two men love the same woman on the Cornish coast, and the famous cliff-hanging scene Hardy invented became a literal cliffhanger.

title: The Woman in White
author: Wilkie Collins
description: A drawing teacher encounters a mysterious woman dressed in white on a moonlit road, and the meeting draws him into a conspiracy of identity theft, madness, and murder.

title: The Moonstone
author: Wilkie Collins
description: A sacred Indian diamond is stolen from an English country house and the investigation—told by multiple narrators—invented the detective novel.

title: No Name
author: Wilkie Collins
description: Two sisters discover they are illegitimate and penniless, and the elder wages a ruthless campaign of disguise and fraud to reclaim their inheritance.

title: North and South
author: Elizabeth Gaskell
description: A clergyman's daughter moves from the rural south to an industrial northern city and clashes with a cotton mill owner over workers' rights—and slowly falls for him.

title: Wives and Daughters
author: Elizabeth Gaskell
description: A doctor's daughter navigates her stepmother's scheming, her stepsister's scandals, and her own quiet love in Gaskell's masterpiece, left unfinished at her death.

title: Cranford
author: Elizabeth Gaskell
description: The gentle, impoverished ladies of a small English town manage their dignity, their economies, and their friendships with comic grace.

title: The Age of Innocence
author: Edith Wharton
description: A New York aristocrat is torn between his conventional fiancée and her scandalous cousin, and 1870s society crushes every impulse toward freedom.

title: The House of Mirth
author: Edith Wharton
description: A beautiful woman without money navigates Gilded Age New York, gambling on marriage to maintain her status until one wrong step destroys her.

title: Ethan Frome
author: Edith Wharton
description: A poor farmer in bleak Massachusetts is trapped between his sickly wife and her young cousin, and their desperate escape attempt ends in horror.

title: The Custom of the Country
author: Edith Wharton
description: A Midwestern social climber marries and discards husbands across New York and Europe in Wharton's most savage portrait of American ambition.

title: Summer
author: Edith Wharton
description: A young woman in a stifling New England village has a passionate affair with an architect from the city, with consequences she cannot escape.

title: My Ántonia
author: Willa Cather
description: A lawyer remembers growing up on the Nebraska prairie alongside a Bohemian immigrant girl whose vitality defined his understanding of America.

title: O Pioneers!
author: Willa Cather
description: A Swedish immigrant woman tames the Nebraska prairie through sheer will while her brothers resent her success and a forbidden love affair ends in violence.

title: The Professor's House
author: Willa Cather
description: A history professor refuses to move into his new house, clinging to the old one where he wrote his life's work, as a student's story of discovering Mesa Verde interrupts his crisis.

title: Death Comes for the Archbishop
author: Willa Cather
description: A French priest builds a Catholic diocese in 1850s New Mexico, and the landscape, the Native Americans, and the frontier become the real subject.

title: A Lost Lady
author: Willa Cather
description: A boy watches a railroad baron's beautiful wife slowly decline after her husband's death, as the Old West gives way to a meaner, smaller age.

title: Sister Carrie
author: Theodore Dreiser
description: A country girl comes to Chicago and rises in the world through two men and the stage, while her first lover sinks into poverty.

title: An American Tragedy
author: Theodore Dreiser
description: A poor young man drowns his pregnant girlfriend to marry a rich woman, and the machinery of American justice and ambition grinds him to dust.

title: Main Street
author: Sinclair Lewis
description: A cultured woman marries a small-town doctor and tries to improve the stultifying conformity of Gopher Prairie, Minnesota, and the town fights back.

title: Babbitt
author: Sinclair Lewis
description: A middle-class real estate agent in a Midwestern city briefly rebels against conformity and is crushed back into respectability.

title: Elmer Gantry
author: Sinclair Lewis
description: A charming, corrupt evangelist rises through American religion through charisma and fraud, in the novel that infuriated the clergy.

title: Arrowsmith
author: Sinclair Lewis
description: A doctor torn between pure science and commercial medicine fights corruption and plague in Lewis's most idealistic novel.

title: It Can't Happen Here
author: Sinclair Lewis
description: A populist senator becomes a fascist president of the United States and a small-town editor leads the resistance.

title: The Jungle
author: Upton Sinclair
description: A Lithuanian immigrant family is destroyed by the Chicago meatpacking industry—the conditions so revolting Congress passed food safety laws within months.

title: Winesburg, Ohio
author: Sherwood Anderson
description: Linked stories of grotesques in a small Ohio town—each person defined by a single truth they clutch until it deforms them.

title: The Victim
author: Saul Bellow
description: A man is confronted by a stranger who claims his life was ruined by him years ago, and their claustrophobic relationship escalates through a sweltering New York summer.

title: U.S.A.
author: John Dos Passos
description: Three novels—The 42nd Parallel, 1919, The Big Money—use newsreels, biographies, and stream of consciousness to capture America from 1900 to 1930.

title: From Here to Eternity
author: James Jones
description: Soldiers at Schofield Barracks in Hawaii in the months before Pearl Harbor—boxing, affairs, brutality, and the peacetime army that isn't ready for war.

title: The Thin Red Line
author: James Jones
description: A rifle company fights on Guadalcanal and Jones strips combat of every romance, showing men reduced to animal survival and accidental heroism.

title: Revolutionary Road
author: Richard Yates
description: A couple in 1950s Connecticut believe they are too special for suburban life and plan to move to Paris, but their marriage and their illusions collapse.

title: Eleven Kinds of Loneliness
author: Richard Yates
description: Eleven stories of American loneliness—a schoolteacher, a TB patient, soldiers, office workers—each one a perfect miniature of disappointment.

title: Disturbing the Peace
author: Richard Yates
description: A businessman has a breakdown, is institutionalized, and tries to rebuild while his marriage, his ambitions, and his sanity keep slipping.

title: The Easter Parade
author: Richard Yates
description: Two sisters' lives diverge—one marries badly, the other doesn't marry at all—and neither finds what she's looking for in postwar America.

title: The Wapshot Chronicle
author: John Cheever
description: An eccentric Massachusetts family declines as the father's seafaring past gives way to the sons' suburban futures.

title: Falconer
author: John Cheever
description: A professor imprisoned for murdering his brother finds love, heroin withdrawal, and redemption in a federal penitentiary.

title: The Stories of John Cheever
author: John Cheever
description: Sixty-one stories of suburban America—The Swimmer, The Enormous Radio, Goodbye My Brother—the definitive portrait of postwar middle-class anxiety.

title: The Centaur
author: John Updike
description: A high school teacher in Pennsylvania is reimagined as the centaur Chiron, and his son narrates the mythology of an ordinary man's sacrifice.

title: Ninety-Two in the Shade
author: Thomas McGuane
description: A fishing guide in Key West enters a territorial war with another guide that can only end in someone's death.

title: The Sporting Club
author: Thomas McGuane
description: Two friends at an exclusive Michigan fishing club declare war on each other, and the club—and the wilderness—are destroyed.

title: Panama
author: Thomas McGuane
description: A failed rock musician in Key West tries to win back his girlfriend while descending into drugs, paranoia, and the Florida heat.

title: Dog Soldiers
author: Robert Stone
description: A journalist in Vietnam sends heroin to his wife in California, and a burned-out Marine carries it into a nightmare of paranoia and violence across the American West.

title: A Flag for Sunrise
author: Robert Stone
description: A nun, an anthropologist, and a drug-running sailor converge in a Central American revolution in Stone's most ambitious novel about American complicity.

title: Damascus Gate
author: Robert Stone
description: A journalist in Jerusalem is drawn into a plot to blow up the Temple Mount as religious fanatics from three faiths pursue the apocalypse.

title: Ironweed
author: William Kennedy
description: A Depression-era bum returns to Albany haunted by the ghosts of everyone he has failed—his dropped baby, his abandoned family—in Kennedy's Pulitzer winner.

title: The Sheltering Sky
author: Paul Bowles
description: An American couple travels deep into the Sahara to save their marriage and are destroyed by the desert—one by disease, the other by absorption into a world that erases identity.

title: Let It Come Down
author: Paul Bowles
description: An American bank clerk flees to Tangier seeking excitement and spirals into drugs, gambling, and murder.

title: The Passenger
author: Cormac McCarthy
description: A salvage diver haunted by his dead sister's genius investigates a submerged plane with a missing passenger and a missing black box.

title: Stella Maris
author: Cormac McCarthy
description: The companion to The Passenger: the sister, a mathematical prodigy, checks herself into a psychiatric hospital and argues with her doctor about reality, language, and death.

title: Jack
author: Marilynne Robinson
description: The prodigal son from Gilead falls in love with a Black teacher in 1950s St. Louis, and their interracial relationship tests every boundary.

title: Wizard's First Rule
author: Terry Goodkind
description: A woods guide discovers he is the Seeker of Truth and must wield the Sword of Truth to stop a tyrant from crossing the boundary between lands.

title: Stone of Tears
author: Terry Goodkind
description: Richard is captured by the Sisters of the Light while Kahlan fights to hold the Midlands together against the Imperial Order.

title: Blood of the Fold
author: Terry Goodkind
description: Richard must unite three lands against the Blood of the Fold—zealots who hunt magic users—while learning to master his own gift.

title: Temple of the Winds
author: Terry Goodkind
description: A plague unleashed by the dream walker forces Richard to enter the Temple of the Winds, but the price demanded is devastating.

title: Soul of the Fire
author: Terry Goodkind
description: The chimes are unleashed and magic itself begins to die across the world while Richard and Kahlan are trapped in a land without magic.

title: Faith of the Fallen
author: Terry Goodkind
description: Richard is captured and forced to live under the Imperial Order's communist-like system, and must inspire a revolution through art.

title: On Basilisk Station
author: David Weber
description: Honor Harrington is given a ship and an impossible assignment guarding a backwater station, where she uncovers an invasion plot.

title: The Honor of the Queen
author: David Weber
description: Honor escorts a diplomatic mission to a religious fundamentalist planet and must fight a battle against overwhelming odds.

title: The Short Victorious War
author: David Weber
description: The People's Republic of Haven launches a short victorious war against Manticore, and Honor commands the fleet that must stop them.

title: Field of Dishonor
author: David Weber
description: Honor faces a political enemy who arranges the murder of someone she loves, and she must choose between law and personal vengeance.

title: Arrows of the Queen
author: Mercedes Lackey
description: A girl fleeing an arranged marriage is Chosen by a magical Companion horse and brought to the capital to train as a Herald.

title: Arrow's Flight
author: Mercedes Lackey
description: Talia's first assignment as a Herald goes wrong when her empathic Gift spirals out of control in the frozen north.

title: Arrow's Fall
author: Mercedes Lackey
description: Talia faces political intrigue and torture as she negotiates a crucial alliance and proves her worth as Queen's Own Herald.

title: Magic's Pawn
author: Mercedes Lackey
description: Vanyel, a young nobleman rejected by his father, discovers he has the most powerful magical Gift in Valdemar's history—and it nearly kills him.

title: Rise of a Merchant Prince
author: Raymond E. Feist
description: A young thief becomes a merchant lord in the rebuilding kingdom while a sorcerous threat grows in the south.

title: Rage of a Demon King
author: Raymond E. Feist
description: The demon-backed invasion reaches the Kingdom and only the combined might of every ally can hold the line.

title: Shards of a Broken Crown
author: Raymond E. Feist
description: The aftermath of the demon war as the Kingdom rebuilds and the final betrayal is revealed.

title: The Scions of Shannara
author: Terry Brooks
description: Par Ohmsford is haunted by the shade of Allanon who sends him on a quest to recover the Sword of Shannara and restore the Druids.

title: The Elf Queen of Shannara
author: Terry Brooks
description: Wren Ohmsford journeys to a volcanic island to find the lost Elves and bring them back to the Four Lands.

title: The Talismans of Shannara
author: Terry Brooks
description: The four quests converge as the Shadowen threat reaches its peak and the fate of the Four Lands hangs on the scions.

title: The Word for World Is Forest
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: Human colonists enslave the peaceful forest-dwellers of Athshe, and the natives learn violence from their oppressors—Le Guin's Vietnam allegory.

title: Rocannon's World
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: An ethnologist stranded on a medieval planet joins a quest across its surface to destroy an enemy base with a device that sends a message faster than light.

title: Clay's Ark
author: Octavia Butler
description: A returning astronaut carries an alien organism that compels its hosts to spread the infection, and a family is captured by the infected community.

title: Time Out of Joint
author: Philip K. Dick
description: A man who wins a newspaper contest every day begins to suspect his entire small-town life is a constructed reality hiding something much darker.

title: Dr. Bloodmoney
author: Philip K. Dick
description: After nuclear war, survivors in Marin County rebuild while a man in an orbiting satellite and a physicist who may have caused the war watch from above.

title: Clans of the Alphane Moon
author: Philip K. Dick
description: A mental hospital on a moon has been abandoned and the patients have formed a society based on their diagnoses—manics run the military, paranoids handle intelligence.

title: 2061: Odyssey Three
author: Arthur C. Clarke
description: Heywood Floyd, now over a hundred, visits Halley's Comet and is diverted to the forbidden surface of Europa.

title: The Hollow Man
author: Dan Simmons
description: A telepath whose wife has died is drowning in the thoughts of everyone around him and flees across America seeking silence.

title: The Years of Rice and Salt
author: Kim Stanley Robinson
description: The Black Death kills 99% of Europe's population, and the next seven centuries of world history unfold under Chinese, Islamic, and Indian dominance.

title: The Integral Trees
author: Larry Niven
description: Humans have adapted to life in a gas torus orbiting a neutron star, living on mile-long trees in freefall, having forgotten they ever came from a planet.

title: Protector
author: Larry Niven
description: An alien arrives on Earth and reveals that humanity was seeded by his species—and the next stage of human evolution is a terrifying armored warrior.

title: The Number of the Beast
author: Robert A. Heinlein
description: Four people in a car with a device that travels between parallel universes visit fictional worlds—Oz, Barsoom, Wonderland—in Heinlein's most playful late novel.

title: Job: A Comedy of Justice
author: Robert A. Heinlein
description: A man and a woman keep being shifted between parallel worlds and must find each other again in each new reality while the apocalypse approaches.

title: Quarantine
author: Greg Egan
description: Earth has been sealed inside a bubble by aliens, and a private investigator discovers the reason involves quantum mechanics and the nature of consciousness.

title: The Children of the Sky
author: Vernor Vinge
description: The survivors on the Tines' world face political scheming among the pack-mind aliens while the threat of the Blight looms beyond the zone boundaries.

title: The Dreaming Void
author: Peter F. Hamilton
description: A man's dreams of a medieval world inside a dangerous galactic void inspire a pilgrimage that could destroy the galaxy.

title: Brightness Reef
author: David Brin
description: Six illegal alien species hiding on a forbidden planet must deal with the arrival of starships that could expose or destroy them all.

title: Earth
author: David Brin
description: A micro black hole is dropped into the Earth's core and scientists race to extract it before it consumes the planet, while the internet becomes sentient.

title: Kiln People
author: David Brin
description: People can make clay copies of themselves that last one day, and a detective sends his copies to investigate a case while his original stays home.

title: The State of the Art
author: Iain M. Banks
description: A Culture ship visits Earth in 1977 and the crew debates whether to contact, help, or leave humanity alone—plus other Culture stories.

title: A Man of the People
author: Chinua Achebe
description: A young teacher challenges a corrupt politician in newly independent Nigeria, and the novel predicted the military coup that followed its publication.

title: Anthills of the Savannah
author: Chinua Achebe
description: Three childhood friends—a dictator, an editor, and a poet—navigate a fictional West African coup in Achebe's final novel.

title: The Famished Road
author: Ben Okri
description: A spirit child in a Nigerian slum keeps dying and being reborn, torn between the spirit world and the poverty of the living in Okri's Booker winner.

title: Songs of Enchantment
author: Ben Okri
description: Azaro continues navigating between the spirit world and political chaos as Nigeria approaches independence.

title: Nervous Conditions
author: Tsitsi Dangarembga
description: A Zimbabwean girl escapes rural poverty through education at her uncle's mission school, but education itself becomes a form of colonization.

title: This Mournable Body
author: Tsitsi Dangarembga
description: Tambudzai from Nervous Conditions is now middle-aged, unemployed in Harare, and unraveling as post-independence Zimbabwe fails its promises.

title: Maps
author: Nuruddin Farah
description: A Somali boy raised by an Ethiopian woman during the Ogaden War questions borders, identity, and the body that maps he belongs to.

title: From a Crooked Rib
author: Nuruddin Farah
description: A Somali nomad girl flees an arranged marriage and navigates Mogadishu's patriarchy, discovering that the city offers different cages.

title: Links
author: Nuruddin Farah
description: A Somali exile returns to Mogadishu during the civil war to find the city destroyed and his family scattered among warring clans.

title: Open City
author: Teju Cole
description: A Nigerian-German psychiatrist walks through New York and Brussels, observing the city's layered histories while suppressing a terrible memory of his own.

title: Every Day Is for the Thief
author: Teju Cole
description: A Nigerian living in New York returns to Lagos and confronts the corruption, beauty, and disorientation of a homeland that is and isn't his.

title: The Memory of Love
author: Aminatta Forna
description: A British psychologist in postwar Sierra Leone treats a dying professor whose memories of the country's intellectual life before the war illuminate what was lost.

title: The Book of Memory
author: Petina Gappah
