description: An albino woman on death row in a Zimbabwean prison writes the story of how she got there—adopted by a white man, accused of his murder.

title: The Guide
author: R.K. Narayan
description: A con man released from prison becomes a holy man by accident in a drought-stricken village and must decide whether to fast to death or confess.

title: The English Teacher
author: R.K. Narayan
description: A teacher's idyllic life is shattered by his wife's death, and he turns to spiritualism to communicate with her.

title: Swami and Friends
author: R.K. Narayan
description: A boy in the fictional town of Malgudi navigates school, cricket, and the stirrings of Indian independence in Narayan's beloved debut.

title: The Vendor of Sweets
author: R.K. Narayan
description: A Gandhian candy maker clashes with his Americanized son who returns from abroad with a Korean wife and a novel-writing machine.

title: The Home and the World
author: Rabindranath Tagore
description: A progressive landlord encourages his wife to engage with the world, and she falls for a charismatic nationalist whose politics mask selfishness.

title: Gitanjali
author: Rabindranath Tagore
description: The prose poems that won Tagore the Nobel Prize—devotional songs offering the self to God in language of startling intimacy.

title: A Suitable Boy
author: Vikram Seth
description: A mother in newly independent India searches for a husband for her daughter across four families, in one of the longest novels in English.

title: The Golden Gate
author: Vikram Seth
description: A novel in verse about young professionals in 1980s San Francisco—love, technology, nuclear protest—written entirely in Pushkin's Onegin stanzas.

title: The Shadow Lines
author: Amitav Ghosh
description: A narrator in Calcutta and London traces the invisible borders that divide families and nations across the India-Bangladesh partition.

title: The Glass Palace
author: Amitav Ghosh
description: A boy watches the British depose the Burmese king in 1885 and the novel follows his descendants through a century of colonial upheaval across Southeast Asia.

title: Sea of Poppies
author: Amitav Ghosh
description: A ship carries indentured workers and convicts across the Indian Ocean during the Opium Wars, and their fates intertwine on the voyage.

title: The Inheritance of Loss
author: Kiran Desai
description: A retired judge in the Himalayas and his granddaughter are caught in a Nepalese insurgency while the cook's son struggles as an illegal immigrant in New York.

title: The Swallows of Kabul
author: Yasmina Khadra
description: Two couples in Taliban-controlled Kabul—one privileged, one poor—are destroyed by the regime's cruelty in a slim, devastating novel.

title: The Attack
author: Yasmina Khadra
description: An Arab-Israeli surgeon discovers his wife was the suicide bomber who killed nineteen people in a Tel Aviv restaurant.

title: The Story of Zahra
author: Hanan al-Shaykh
description: A Lebanese woman trapped between patriarchal family and civil war finds freedom only through a sniper—a novel about female desire under siege.

title: Women of Sand and Myrrh
author: Hanan al-Shaykh
description: Four women in an unnamed Gulf state—two Arab, two Western—suffocate under restrictions that shape every aspect of their lives.

title: Gate of the Sun
author: Elias Khoury
description: A man tells stories to a comatose Palestinian fighter, and the stories become the collective memory of the Nakba and sixty years of exile.

title: The Rock of Tanios
author: Amin Maalouf
description: A boy in 19th-century Lebanon becomes entangled in the political intrigues between Druze lords and Egyptian occupiers.

title: Leo Africanus
author: Amin Maalouf
description: The fictional autobiography of a real 16th-century Arab geographer who traveled from Granada to Fez to Rome, witnessing the collision of Islam and Christianity.

title: Samarkand
author: Amin Maalouf
description: The manuscript of Omar Khayyam's Rubaiyat travels through centuries of Persian history and sinks with the Titanic.

title: The Sound of the Mountain
author: Yasunari Kawabata
description: An aging businessman in postwar Japan watches his family disintegrate while he is haunted by the sound of the mountain that signals death.

title: Beauty and Sadness
author: Yasunari Kawabata
description: An aging novelist visits the woman he seduced decades ago, and her young female protégée plans a devastating revenge.

title: Thousand Cranes
author: Yasunari Kawabata
description: A young man is drawn into the world of tea ceremony and the entanglements of his dead father's mistresses.

title: The Makioka Sisters
author: Jun'ichirō Tanizaki
description: Four sisters in a declining Osaka merchant family navigate marriage prospects, scandal, and tradition as Japan drifts toward war.

title: Some Prefer Nettles
author: Jun'ichirō Tanizaki
description: A man in a dying marriage is torn between his Westernized mistress and the traditional Japanese beauty his father-in-law promotes.

title: The Key
author: Jun'ichirō Tanizaki
description: A husband and wife keep separate diaries about their sexual experiments, each knowing the other reads them, in a game of erotic escalation.

title: In Praise of Shadows
author: Jun'ichirō Tanizaki
description: An essay on Japanese aesthetics arguing that beauty lives in shadow, patina, and darkness—not the harsh illumination the West prefers.

title: Kokoro
author: Natsume Sōseki
description: A young man befriends a reclusive older man called Sensei whose long-hidden guilt over a betrayal in his youth has defined his life.

title: Botchan
author: Natsume Sōseki
description: A hot-tempered young man from Tokyo takes a teaching job in the provinces and wages comic war against the school's corrupt faculty.

title: I Am a Cat
author: Natsume Sōseki
description: A cat observes Meiji-era Japanese society from its owner's house, offering sardonic commentary on intellectuals, Westernization, and human vanity.

title: Memoirs of a Polar Bear
author: Yoko Tawada
description: Three generations of polar bears—a Soviet circus performer, a zoo bear in Germany, and her cub—narrate their lives among humans in surreal, tender prose.

title: Cursed Bunny
author: Bora Chung
description: Ten stories of Korean horror and surrealism—a woman gives birth to her apartment's plumbing, a cursed lamp ruins a family, a head grows from a fetus.

title: Gabriela, Clove and Cinnamon
author: Jorge Amado
description: A sensual mulata woman from the backlands transforms a Brazilian cacao town with her cooking and her body, in Amado's most beloved novel.

title: Dona Flor and Her Two Husbands
author: Jorge Amado
description: A cooking teacher's dead first husband—a gambler and womanizer—returns as a ghost after she remarries a boring pharmacist, and she keeps both.

title: Água Viva
author: Clarice Lispector
description: A painter writes a stream-of-consciousness letter to a former lover about the act of living in the present instant—Lispector's most experimental work.

title: A Breath of Life
author: Clarice Lispector
description: An author creates a character named Ângela Pralini and they write to each other as the boundary between creator and creation dissolves.

title: The Posthumous Memoirs of Brás Cubas
author: Machado de Assis
description: A dead man narrates his own life story with ironic detachment, inventing the modern unreliable narrator fifty years before anyone else.

title: Dom Casmurro
author: Machado de Assis
description: A man recounts his marriage and his suspicion that his wife cheated on him with his best friend—but he may be the most unreliable narrator in literature.

title: Quincas Borba
author: Machado de Assis
description: A naive man inherits a fortune and a dog named Quincas Borba and is systematically stripped of both by the schemers around him.

title: The Death of Artemio Cruz
author: Carlos Fuentes
description: A dying Mexican tycoon relives his life in three persons—I, you, he—from the Revolution through the corruption of power.

title: Aura
author: Carlos Fuentes
description: A historian takes a job cataloging a dead general's papers and falls for the old widow's young niece, who may be the same person.

title: Terra Nostra
author: Carlos Fuentes
description: A massive novel spanning Spanish and Mexican history from Philip II to the year 1999, mixing myth, history, and hallucinatory fiction.

title: The Obscene Bird of Night
author: José Donoso
description: A deaf-mute servant in a Chilean convent tells—or hallucinates—a story of aristocrats, monsters, and the collapse of identity in Latin America's most nightmarish novel.

title: Kiss of the Spider Woman
author: Manuel Puig
description: A Marxist revolutionary and a gay window dresser share a cell in an Argentine prison, and the window dresser retells old movies while a political conspiracy unfolds.

title: Heartbreak Tango
author: Manuel Puig
description: A handsome man dies of tuberculosis in a small Argentine town and the women he used recall their obsession through letters, diaries, and radio serials.

title: Senselessness
author: Horacio Castellanos Moya
description: A paranoid writer edits testimonies of indigenous massacre survivors in a Central American country and begins to lose his mind.

title: The She-Devil in the Mirror
author: Horacio Castellanos Moya
description: A socialite's friend is murdered in San Salvador and her investigation reveals the corruption connecting her own class to the crime.

title: Fever Dream
author: Samanta Schweblin
description: A dying woman in an Argentine clinic tells a boy the story of what happened to her daughter—something involving pesticides, metempsychosis, and rural horror.

title: Mouthful of Birds
author: Samanta Schweblin
description: Disturbing stories from Argentina: a woman's daughter eats live birds, a couple guards a mysterious mound of dirt, digestion becomes an act of love.

title: Little Eyes
author: Samanta Schweblin
description: People around the world buy cute surveillance devices called kentukis and use them to watch strangers—or be watched—in a novel about digital voyeurism.

title: The Dangers of Smoking in Bed
author: Mariana Enriquez
description: Twelve Argentine horror stories where the ghosts of the Dirty War, poverty, and urban decay produce monsters as real as the political violence that created them.

title: Things We Lost in the Fire
author: Mariana Enriquez
description: Twelve stories of Buenos Aires horror: women set themselves on fire in protest, a slum witch curses a social worker, and the disappeared return.

title: Our Share of Night
author: Mariana Enriquez
description: A medium and his son flee a secret society that uses dark rituals connected to Argentina's Dirty War, spanning decades of national horror.

title: A Tomb for Boris Davidovich
author: Danilo Kiš
description: Seven stories of revolutionaries destroyed by the Soviet system they served—each based on real cases of Stalinist persecution.

title: Garden, Ashes
author: Danilo Kiš
description: A boy remembers his father—a railway inspector who compiled a fantastical Bus, Ship, Rail, and Air Travel Guide—before the Holocaust took him.

title: The Encyclopedia of the Dead
author: Danilo Kiš
description: Nine stories ranging from a secret library that records every ordinary life to a medieval legend to a woman dreaming her father's entire biography.

title: The Museum of Unconditional Surrender
author: Dubravka Ugrešić
description: A Croatian exile in Berlin assembles fragments of memory, photographs, and lists into a novel about displacement and the impossibility of holding onto a vanished country.

title: The Foundation Pit
author: Andrei Platonov
description: Workers dig an enormous pit for a building that will house the entire proletariat, and the pit keeps growing as the workers die—Soviet utopianism as absurdist horror.

title: Soul
author: Andrei Platonov
description: A man returns to his Central Asian homeland to save his people from extinction, only to find them dissolving into the desert.

title: The White Guard
author: Mikhail Bulgakov
description: A family in Kiev survives the chaos of the Russian Civil War as armies sweep through the city and the old world disintegrates.

title: The Fish Can Sing
author: Halldór Laxness
description: A boy raised by his grandparents in Reykjavik harbor is obsessed with a famous singer who may not exist, in Laxness's gentlest comedy.

title: World Light
author: Halldór Laxness
description: An orphan poet in Iceland endures brutal foster parents and grinding poverty while clinging to beauty as the only thing worth living for.

title: Out Stealing Horses
author: Per Petterson
description: A man in a Norwegian cabin remembers the summer of 1948 when his fifteen-year-old world broke apart through an accident, a disappearance, and a war secret.

title: I Refuse
author: Per Petterson
description: Two childhood friends meet by chance on a bridge thirty-five years after the event that separated them, and their parallel lives unspool.

title: My Struggle: Book 1
author: Karl Ove Knausgård
description: A man writes about his father's death, his childhood, and the task of cleaning his father's filthy house in the autobiographical novel that scandalized Norway.

title: My Struggle: Book 2
author: Karl Ove Knausgård
description: Marriage, fatherhood, writer's block, and children's birthday parties in Stockholm—Knausgård turns domestic life into existential crisis.

title: My Struggle: Book 3
author: Karl Ove Knausgård
description: Childhood on a Norwegian island—the cruelty of his father, school humiliations, and the strange intensity of being a boy.

title: My Struggle: Book 4
author: Karl Ove Knausgård
description: An eighteen-year-old Knausgård takes a teaching job in northern Norway and discovers alcohol, sex, and his own inadequacy.

title: The Summer Book
author: Tove Jansson
description: A grandmother and her granddaughter spend a summer on a tiny Finnish island, and every small adventure contains the whole of life.

title: The True Deceiver
author: Tove Jansson
description: A cold, manipulative woman insinuates herself into the life of an elderly children's book illustrator in a snowbound Finnish village.

title: The Lover
author: Marguerite Duras
description: A fifteen-year-old French girl in 1930s Saigon begins an affair with a wealthy Chinese man, and the power dynamics of colonialism, race, and desire play out in their bed.

title: The Ravishing of Lol Stein
author: Marguerite Duras
description: A woman watches her fiancé dance all night with another woman at a ball, and the trauma defines her life—but the narrator telling the story may be unreliable.

title: Moderato Cantabile
author: Marguerite Duras
description: A wealthy woman and a working-class man bond over a crime of passion they both witnessed, meeting daily at a café to reconstruct the murder.

title: The Years
author: Annie Ernaux
description: Ernaux's life from 1941 to 2006 told in the collective 'we'—using photographs, songs, and products to capture how an era shaped a generation.

title: A Woman's Story
author: Annie Ernaux
description: Ernaux's memoir of her mother—an ambitious shopkeeper who dragged herself from poverty—written with clinical restraint after her death from Alzheimer's.

title: Shame
author: Annie Ernaux
description: Ernaux reconstructs the day her father tried to kill her mother when she was twelve, and the class shame that defined her afterwards.

title: A Man's Place
author: Annie Ernaux
description: Ernaux's memoir of her father—a café owner who left school at twelve—written in flat prose that refuses to beautify or betray his class.

title: Life: A User's Manual
author: Georges Perec
description: Every room in a Parisian apartment building at one moment in time, each containing a story—a puzzle maker's lifelong project, a millionaire's revenge, and hundreds of other lives.

title: W, or the Memory of Childhood
author: Georges Perec
description: Alternating chapters: a boy's childhood during the Holocaust with no memories before age five, and a fictional island where an Olympic society becomes a concentration camp.

title: A Void
author: Georges Perec
description: A full-length novel written without using the letter E—not as a stunt but as a meditation on loss, absence, and the disappeared.

title: Nausea
author: Jean-Paul Sartre
description: A historian in a provincial French town is overwhelmed by the sheer existence of objects and his own contingency, in the novel that launched existentialism.

title: No Exit
author: Jean-Paul Sartre
description: Three people are locked in a room for eternity and discover that hell is other people.

title: The Mandarins
author: Simone de Beauvoir
description: French intellectuals after WWII navigate politics, affairs, and the Cold War—a roman à clef featuring Sartre, Camus, and Nelson Algren.

title: She Came to Stay
author: Simone de Beauvoir
description: A couple's open relationship is destroyed when a young woman moves in and the existentialist theory of freedom collides with jealousy.

title: Mr. Palomar
author: Italo Calvino
description: A man tries to observe the world one thing at a time—a wave, a cheese shop, a gecko—and each observation spirals into philosophical vertigo.

title: The Path to the Nest of Spiders
author: Italo Calvino
description: A street urchin joins the Italian partisans during WWII in Calvino's debut—gritty neorealism before he turned to fantasy.

title: If This Is a Man
author: Primo Levi
description: Levi's account of Auschwitz—not a cry of rage but a chemist's precise observation of how the camp system reduced humans to objects.

title: The Truce
author: Primo Levi
description: Levi's picaresque journey home from Auschwitz through the chaos of postwar Europe—nine months of wandering before he reaches Turin.

title: The Periodic Table
author: Primo Levi
description: Twenty-one chapters named for chemical elements, each a story from Levi's life as a chemist, partisan, and survivor.

title: If Not Now, When?
author: Primo Levi
description: A band of Jewish partisans fights across Eastern Europe from 1943 to 1945 in Levi's only novel.

title: Family Lexicon
author: Natalia Ginzburg
description: Ginzburg's memoir-novel of her antifascist Jewish family in Turin, told through the phrases and habits that defined them.

title: The Little Virtues
author: Natalia Ginzburg
description: Eleven essays on children, writing, England, and silence—including the title essay arguing we should teach children great virtues, not small ones.

title: The Leopard
author: Giuseppe Tomasi di Lampedusa
description: A Sicilian prince watches his world dissolve during the Risorgimento—'everything must change so that everything can remain the same.'

title: The Moon and the Bonfires
author: Cesare Pavese
description: A man returns to his Piedmont village after making money in America and finds that everyone he knew has been destroyed by war, poverty, and fascism.

title: A Love Affair
author: Dino Buzzati
description: A middle-aged architect becomes obsessed with a young woman who may be a prostitute, and his love destroys his dignity without touching hers.

title: The Bears' Famous Invasion of Sicily
author: Dino Buzzati
description: Bears driven from the mountains by hunger invade Sicily, defeat the humans, but are corrupted by civilization—a fable illustrated by Buzzati himself.

title: Under the Autumn Star
author: Knut Hamsun
description: A middle-aged man abandons his comfortable life to work as a laborer in the Norwegian countryside, finding peace in anonymity and physical work.

title: A Wanderer Plays on Muted Strings
author: Knut Hamsun
description: The wanderer returns to the same district and the same people, older and more resigned, watching the woman he loved from afar move on without him.

title: The Whore's Child
author: Richard Russo
description: Seven stories of small-town America—a nun in a writing workshop, a man reunited with his college roommate, a boy watching his parents fail—Russo's only story collection.

title: Elsewhere
author: Richard Russo
description: Russo's memoir of his mother—anxious, dependent, impossible to please—and the small upstate New York towns they moved through together.

title: The Water-Method Man
author: John Irving
description: A man with a urinary tract problem and a failing marriage stumbles through academia, fatherhood, and filmmaking in Irving's wildly comic second novel.

title: The 158-Pound Marriage
author: John Irving
description: Two couples swap partners—a wrestler-turned-professor and a writer—and the arrangement destroys all four of them in Irving's shortest, darkest novel.

title: A Son of the Circus
author: John Irving
description: An Indian-born Canadian doctor returns to Bombay and is drawn into a murder investigation involving circus dwarfs, transsexual prostitutes, and a serial killer.

title: Until I Find You
author: John Irving
description: A boy and his mother search Europe for his tattooed father, and the unreliable memories of childhood are revised in adulthood with devastating consequences.

title: The Last Chairlift
author: John Irving
description: A screenwriter haunted by ghosts—literally—traces his family history from a 1940s ski lodge to the present, in Irving's return to sprawling, multigenerational storytelling.

title: Fevre Dream
author: George R.R. Martin
description: A steamboat captain on the Mississippi partners with a mysterious aristocrat who turns out to be a vampire trying to reform his kind—Anne Rice meets Mark Twain.

title: Tuf Voyaging
author: George R.R. Martin
description: A cat-loving trader stumbles onto a lost seedship that can create any life form and becomes the most powerful man in the galaxy, dispensing ecological justice.

title: Nightflyers
author: George R.R. Martin
description: A research team on a starship discovers something is killing them one by one—the ship itself, or its reclusive captain, or something they brought aboard.

title: Dreamsongs: Volume I
author: George R.R. Martin
description: Martin's collected stories from the 1970s and 1980s—science fiction, horror, fantasy—with autobiographical commentary showing the writer he was before Westeros.

title: Dreamsongs: Volume II
author: George R.R. Martin
description: More pre-Thrones work including The Skin Trade, Sandkings, and the Hollywood stories that came from his years writing for television.

title: The Ice Dragon
author: George R.R. Martin
description: A girl born during the coldest winter befriends an ice dragon and must ride it to save her family from enemy soldiers.

title: Skyward
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: A girl on a planet under constant alien attack defies the government to become a fighter pilot and discovers the truth about the enemy and her father's legacy.

title: Starsight
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: Spensa infiltrates an alien space station to learn the enemy's secrets and discovers the war is far more complicated than humans were told.

title: Cytonic
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: Spensa enters the nowhere—a dimension between worlds—to find the weapon that can end the war, but the nowhere has its own dangers.

title: Defiant
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: The final Skyward novel as Spensa returns to lead humanity's last stand against the Superiority.

title: Steelheart
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: Superhumans called Epics have conquered the world, and a teenage boy joins a resistance cell to kill the invincible Epic who murdered his father.

title: Firefight
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: David travels to a flooded New York ruled by a seemingly benevolent Epic and must decide whether Epics can be redeemed or only destroyed.

title: Calamity
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: The Reckoners discover the source of Epic powers—a red star called Calamity—and David must confront what it means that he might be one of them.

title: The Rithmatist
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: In an alternate America where chalk drawings come to life as defensive magic, a boy without Rithmatic powers investigates the disappearance of students from his academy.

title: The Sunlit Man
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: A man fleeing across the cosmere crashes on a planet where the sun burns everything it touches and the inhabitants must keep moving to survive.

title: The Frugal Wizard's Handbook for Surviving Medieval England
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: A man wakes in a medieval world with no memory and a guidebook for interdimensional tourists, and must figure out who sent him and why.

title: My Struggle: Book 5
author: Karl Ove Knausgård
description: Knausgård's years in Bergen as a young writing student—workshops, alcoholic blackouts, failed relationships, and the desperate need to write something real.

title: My Struggle: Book 6
author: Karl Ove Knausgård
description: The 1,160-page finale: the fallout of publishing the earlier books, a 400-page essay on Hitler and evil, and the end of the project that consumed his life.

title: A Time for Everything
author: Karl Ove Knausgård
description: Angels appear throughout history—at the Flood, in the Renaissance, in 17th-century Norway—and gradually withdraw from the world in Knausgård's pre-Struggle novel.

title: The Morning Star
author: Karl Ove Knausgård
description: A new star appears in the sky over Norway and strange things begin happening—the dead return, crabs swarm the shore—told through nine interconnected narrators.

title: The Wolves of Eternity
author: Karl Ove Knausgård
description: The companion to The Morning Star shifts between 1986 Soviet Russia and present-day Scandinavia as the supernatural events intensify.

title: The Ministry of Fear
author: Graham Greene
description: A man who mercy-killed his wife wins a cake at a charity fete and is plunged into a Nazi spy ring in wartime London.

title: The Human Factor
author: Graham Greene
description: A mole in British intelligence is leaking secrets to Moscow, and the investigation targets the wrong man while the real spy agonizes over loyalty.

title: Stamboul Train
author: Graham Greene
description: Passengers on the Orient Express—a chorus girl, a Jewish businessman, a revolutionary, a journalist—cross Europe toward Istanbul as their fates intertwine.

title: England Made Me
author: Graham Greene
description: A charming English drifter joins his twin sister in Stockholm working for a Swedish financier, and his amateurish decency threatens the professional fraud.

title: It's a Battlefield
author: Graham Greene
description: A bus driver is condemned to death for killing a policeman at a political rally, and his wife, his brother, and the establishment all pursue their own agendas.

title: The Confidential Agent
author: Graham Greene
description: An agent for a foreign government comes to England to buy coal and prevent the enemy from getting it, hunted by rivals and betrayed by everyone.

title: Loser Takes All
author: Graham Greene
description: A meek accountant's honeymoon in Monte Carlo is derailed when his boss strands him and he discovers a system for winning at roulette.

title: Doctor Fischer of Geneva
author: Graham Greene
description: A toothpaste millionaire humiliates his rich friends with increasingly cruel dinner party games to test how much greed they'll endure for gifts.

title: The Captain and the Enemy
author: Graham Greene
description: A boy is won from his father in a backgammon game by a mysterious man and raised in a world of petty crime and uncertain love, in Greene's last novel.

title: Monsignor Quixote
author: Graham Greene
description: A village priest and a communist ex-mayor travel across Spain like Don Quixote and Sancho Panza, debating faith and politics over wine.

title: The Tenth Man
author: Graham Greene
description: A wealthy prisoner during WWII pays a poor man to die in his place, then returns after the war disguised as a stranger to the dead man's family.

title: The Fallen Idol
author: Graham Greene
description: A boy idolizes his parents' butler and accidentally provides false testimony that could convict the man of murder.

title: A Christmas Carol
author: Charles Dickens
description: A miser is visited by three ghosts on Christmas Eve who show him his past, present, and future, transforming him overnight.

title: The Pickwick Papers
author: Charles Dickens
description: A gentleman and his friends travel the English countryside encountering comic misadventures, con men, and the debtor's prison.

title: Nicholas Nickleby
author: Charles Dickens
description: A young man supports his family after his father's death, confronting a cruel uncle and the horrors of a Yorkshire boarding school.

title: Our Mutual Friend
author: Charles Dickens
description: A man presumed drowned in the Thames returns in disguise to test his fiancée's love in Dickens's last completed novel, centered on money, dust heaps, and the river.

title: Little Dorrit
author: Charles Dickens
description: A woman born in the Marshalsea debtors' prison cares for her imprisoned father while a returned traveler investigates a mystery connecting their families.

title: Dombey and Son
author: Charles Dickens
description: A proud merchant values his son above everything—including his neglected daughter—and his empire and family both collapse.

title: Martin Chuzzlewit
author: Charles Dickens
description: A young man travels to America to make his fortune and finds it a land of swindlers, while back in England his grandfather's inheritance attracts predators.

title: The Old Curiosity Shop
author: Charles Dickens
description: Little Nell and her gambling-addicted grandfather flee London and wander the countryside pursued by the monstrous dwarf Quilp.

title: The Mystery of Edwin Drood
author: Charles Dickens
description: A young man disappears and his uncle may have murdered him—but Dickens died before finishing, leaving literature's most famous unsolved mystery.

title: Hard Times
author: Charles Dickens
description: A factory town ruled by utilitarian philosophy crushes the imagination of its children and workers in Dickens's shortest and angriest novel.

title: The Hamlet
author: William Faulkner
description: The Snopes family—poor, ruthless, endlessly multiplying—infiltrates a Mississippi hamlet and begins their takeover, starting with Flem Snopes's first con.

title: The Town
author: William Faulkner
description: Flem Snopes moves to Jefferson and schemes his way to respectability while three narrators—including the lawyer who opposes him—watch helplessly.

title: The Mansion
author: William Faulkner
description: Mink Snopes is released from prison after forty years and walks across Mississippi to kill his cousin Flem, completing the Snopes saga.

title: The Reivers
author: William Faulkner
description: An eleven-year-old boy, a family retainer, and a horse thief steal a car and drive to Memphis for a weekend of brothels and horse racing in Faulkner's last, gentlest novel.

title: Pylon
author: William Faulkner
description: A journalist covers an air show in a city modeled on New Orleans and becomes obsessed with the barnstormers—a pilot, his wife, and her other lover.

title: Dance Dance Dance
author: Haruki Murakami
description: The narrator from A Wild Sheep Chase returns to the Dolphin Hotel searching for a vanished woman and finds the hotel replaced by a gleaming tower hiding the same dark room.

title: South of the Border, West of the Sun
author: Haruki Murakami
description: A jazz bar owner whose life is finally settled is destabilized by the reappearance of his childhood sweetheart, who may not be who she seems.

title: Sputnik Sweetheart
author: Haruki Murakami
description: A man loves a woman who loves another woman, and when one vanishes on a Greek island, the triangle collapses into mystery.

title: What I Talk About When I Talk About Running
author: Haruki Murakami
description: Murakami's memoir about running marathons and how the discipline of distance running connects to the discipline of writing novels.

title: First Person Singular
author: Haruki Murakami
description: Eight stories about music, baseball, aging, and the strangeness of memory, in Murakami's most recent collection.

title: Anansi Boys
author: Neil Gaiman
description: A man discovers his recently dead father was the spider god Anansi, and his long-lost brother shows up to make his life a living hell.

title: Fragile Things
author: Neil Gaiman
description: Short stories and poems including A Study in Emerald (Sherlock Holmes meets Lovecraft) and The Monarch of the Glen (a Shadow sequel).

title: Smoke and Mirrors
author: Neil Gaiman
description: Short fiction including Snow Glass Apples (Snow White from the queen's perspective), Chivalry, and Murder Mysteries.

title: Trigger Warning
author: Neil Gaiman
description: Stories and poems including The Truth Is a Cave in the Black Mountains, Black Dog (Shadow in England), and a Doctor Who story.

title: The View from the Cheap Seats
author: Neil Gaiman
description: Gaiman's collected nonfiction—speeches, introductions, essays on Pratchett, Bradbury, bookshops, and why we need stories.

title: Boyhood
author: J.M. Coetzee
description: Coetzee's childhood in 1950s South Africa told in third person—a boy caught between Afrikaner and English cultures, already an outsider.

title: Youth
author: J.M. Coetzee
description: Coetzee in 1960s London—programming computers, failing at relationships, wanting to be a writer but unable to live enough to write.

title: Summertime
author: J.M. Coetzee
description: A biographer interviews five people who knew the late 'John Coetzee' and each reveals a different version of the man in Coetzee's most audacious autofiction.

title: Slow Man
author: J.M. Coetzee
description: A cyclist who loses his leg in an accident becomes obsessed with his Croatian nurse, then the novelist Elizabeth Costello walks into the novel to take over.

title: Diary of a Bad Year
author: J.M. Coetzee
description: Three parallel narratives run down each page: an aging writer's opinions on politics, his infatuation with a young neighbor, and her own perspective.

title: The Schooldays of Jesus
author: J.M. Coetzee
description: The boy from Childhood of Jesus enrolls in a dance academy run by a mysterious woman, in an allegory that resists every attempt at interpretation.

title: Fury
author: Salman Rushdie
description: A dollmaker flees London for New York after nearly killing his family and finds Manhattan a furnace of celebrity, rage, and post-millennial madness.

title: The Enchantress of Florence
author: Salman Rushdie
description: A stranger arrives at the Mughal court claiming to be the emperor's uncle, with a story connecting Renaissance Florence to the harem of Akbar the Great.

title: Quichotte
author: Salman Rushdie
description: An aging traveling salesman obsessed with television falls for a TV star and drives across America with his imaginary son in Rushdie's Don Quixote riff.

title: Victory City
author: Salman Rushdie
description: A girl creates a city that becomes an empire over 250 years, narrating its rise and fall in a novel about the power of storytelling.

title: Knife
author: Salman Rushdie
description: Rushdie's account of the 2022 stabbing attack that nearly killed him, and his recovery and reflections on violence, art, and survival.

title: Adulthood Rites
author: Octavia Butler
description: The first human-Oankali hybrid child is kidnapped by resisters who refuse to merge with the aliens, and he must bridge both species.

title: Imago
author: Octavia Butler
description: The first ooloi construct—neither male nor female but the genetic mixer—must prove it can be trusted not to destroy the new human-Oankali species.

title: Patternmaster
author: Octavia Butler
description: In a future where telepaths form a mental network dominated by a Patternmaster, two brothers fight for control while mute humans serve and die.

title: Northanger Abbey
author: Jane Austen
description: A naive young woman addicted to Gothic novels visits a country house and imagines murders everywhere, in Austen's parody of the genre.

title: Love
author: Toni Morrison
description: Two women who were childhood friends are now bitter enemies fighting over the estate of the man they both loved—a hotel owner whose legacy is poisoned.

title: To Have and Have Not
author: Ernest Hemingway
description: A fishing boat captain in Key West is forced into smuggling by the Depression, and the novel contrasts his desperate vitality with the emptiness of the rich.

title: Across the River and into the Trees
author: Ernest Hemingway
description: An aging colonel in postwar Venice falls for a nineteen-year-old countess and reflects on war, death, and the things that matter as his heart fails.

title: True at First Light
author: Ernest Hemingway
description: Hemingway's posthumous African memoir-novel of a 1953 safari, lion hunts, and his relationship with a Wakamba woman.

title: The Voyage Out
author: Virginia Woolf
description: A sheltered young woman sails to South America with her aunt and uncle, falls in love, and dies of fever in Woolf's first novel.

title: Night and Day
author: Virginia Woolf
description: A young woman torn between a safe engagement and a passionate admirer navigates London's suffrage movement in Woolf's most conventional novel.

title: The Cossacks
author: Leo Tolstoy
description: A young Moscow aristocrat flees to the Caucasus seeking authenticity among Cossack warriors and falls for a village girl.

title: Family Happiness
author: Leo Tolstoy
description: A young woman's romantic marriage cools into something quieter and more real in Tolstoy's short, perfect early novel.

title: The Deer Park
author: Norman Mailer
description: A war veteran in a Palm Springs-like resort town watches a blacklisted director, a movie star, and a call girl destroy each other in Hollywood's moral wasteland.

title: Tough Guys Don't Dance
author: Norman Mailer
description: A writer in Provincetown wakes with blood on his car and a woman's head in his marijuana stash and can't remember what happened.

title: The Fight
author: Norman Mailer
description: The Rumble in the Jungle—Ali versus Foreman in Zaire—told by Mailer as a cosmic event where boxing, Africa, and American mythology collide.

title: Of a Fire on the Moon
author: Norman Mailer
description: Mailer covers the Apollo 11 moon landing as only he could—mixing engineering detail with existential dread about technology replacing the human spirit.

title: The Body Artist
author: Don DeLillo
description: A woman alone in a rented house after her husband's suicide finds a strange man who can replay conversations and inhabit other people's voices.

title: Point Omega
author: Don DeLillo
description: A filmmaker visits a war theorist in the desert to make a documentary, and someone disappears into the landscape.

title: The Silence
author: Don DeLillo
description: Five people gather for Super Bowl Sunday and every screen goes dark—a catastrophic systems failure that reduces civilization to silence.

title: The Confusion
author: Neal Stephenson
description: Jack Shaftoe escapes slavery and circles the globe while Eliza maneuvers through the courts of Europe in the second Baroque Cycle novel.

title: The System of the World
author: Neal Stephenson
description: Newton, Leibniz, and the invention of modern finance converge as Daniel Waterhouse returns to London in the Baroque Cycle's conclusion.

title: Zodiac
author: Neal Stephenson
description: An environmental activist in Boston investigates toxic waste dumping in the harbor, mixing hard science with gonzo investigation.

title: The Town and the City
author: Jack Kerouac
description: A large family in a small Massachusetts mill town scatters to New York and the wider world in Kerouac's Wolfe-influenced debut before he invented Beat prose.

title: Tristessa
author: Jack Kerouac
description: Kerouac watches a beautiful Mexican prostitute destroy herself with morphine in Mexico City and falls in love with her suffering.

title: Satori in Paris
author: Jack Kerouac
description: Kerouac goes to France and Brittany to research his ancestry, gets drunk everywhere, and achieves a sudden illumination in a taxi.

title: Maggie Cassidy
author: Jack Kerouac
description: A teenager in Lowell, Massachusetts falls for a beautiful girl in the 1930s and must choose between hometown love and the wider world.

title: The Ionian Mission
author: Patrick O'Brian
description: Aubrey is given a ship too rotten for the line and sent to blockade duty in the Mediterranean, where diplomacy and a daring raid break the monotony.

title: Treason's Harbour
author: Patrick O'Brian
description: Maturin's intelligence work in Malta is compromised by a French spy, and the voyage to the Red Sea nearly ends in disaster.

title: The Far Side of the World
author: Patrick O'Brian
description: Aubrey pursues an American frigate around Cape Horn into the Pacific in the novel that inspired the film Master and Commander.

title: The Reverse of the Medal
author: Patrick O'Brian
description: Aubrey is framed for stock fraud and struck from the navy list, and the trial scene is the most devastating in the series.

title: The Letter of Marque
author: Patrick O'Brian
description: Aubrey sails as a privateer to redeem himself, capturing a French frigate in a brilliant action that restores his reputation.

title: The Thirteen-Gun Salute
author: Patrick O'Brian
description: Aubrey and Maturin sail to the South China Sea on a diplomatic mission to a Malay sultanate while French agents work against them.

title: The Nutmeg of Consolation
author: Patrick O'Brian
description: Shipwrecked on a desert island, Aubrey builds a vessel from the wreck and sails to Australia, where a new ship and new orders await.

title: The Wine-Dark Sea
author: Patrick O'Brian
description: Aubrey pursues a privateer across the Pacific to South America while Maturin becomes entangled in Peruvian revolutionary politics.

title: Blue at the Mizzen
author: Patrick O'Brian
description: Aubrey sails to newly independent Chile and finally achieves his flag—rear admiral of the blue—in the last novel O'Brian completed.

title: The Innocents Abroad
author: Mark Twain
description: Twain's comic account of an American tour group in Europe and the Holy Land, mocking both Old World pretension and American ignorance.

title: The Mysterious Stranger
author: Mark Twain
description: Satan's nephew visits an Austrian village and demonstrates that human existence is a dream and morality an illusion, in Twain's darkest work.

title: Two on a Tower
author: Thomas Hardy
description: A young astronomer and an older noblewoman fall in love against the backdrop of the universe's indifference, in Hardy's most cosmic romance.

title: The Last Report on the Miracles at Little No Horse
author: Louise Erdrich
description: A woman who has spent decades disguised as a priest on an Ojibwe reservation must confess her secret when a visiting priest investigates a nun's sainthood.

title: The Master Butchers Singing Club
author: Louise Erdrich
description: A German sniper from WWI becomes a butcher in North Dakota and starts a men's singing club, and his family's secrets unfold across decades.

title: Shadow Tag
author: Louise Erdrich
description: A painter's wife discovers he reads her diary and begins a second, fake diary to manipulate him, and the marriage warfare escalates toward destruction.

title: The Antelope Wife
author: Louise Erdrich
description: A Minneapolis man kidnaps an Ojibwe woman at a powwow, connecting two families across a century of love, loss, and spiritual transformation.

title: The Last Thing He Wanted
author: Joan Didion
description: A journalist covering the 1984 election leaves the campaign to help her dying father and is drawn into an arms-dealing operation in Central America.

title: South and West
author: Joan Didion
description: Two previously unpublished notebooks: a 1970 road trip through the Gulf States and notes on the Patty Hearst trial, revealing Didion's method.

title: The Lair of the White Worm
author: Bram Stoker
description: An ancient evil in the form of a giant white worm lurks beneath an English estate, and a young man must destroy it.

title: The Samurai
author: Shusaku Endo
description: A Japanese samurai is sent to Mexico and Spain to negotiate trade, forced to convert to Christianity, and returns to find his country has closed its borders to the faith.

title: Deep River
author: Shusaku Endo
description: Japanese tourists travel to India for different reasons—faith, grief, birdwatching—and each confronts the Ganges and what it means to believe.

title: The Sea and Poison
author: Shusaku Endo
description: Japanese doctors perform vivisection on captured American airmen during WWII, and the novel asks how ordinary people become complicit in atrocity.

title: Ultramarine
author: Malcolm Lowry
description: A Cambridge student ships out as a deckhand to prove himself among working-class sailors and is humiliated and transformed.

title: Hear Us O Lord from Heaven Thy Dwelling Place
author: Malcolm Lowry
description: Stories and novellas set in British Columbia where the Lowry-like narrator lives in a squatter's cabin on the beach, finding paradise and losing it.

title: Stalingrad
author: Vasily Grossman
description: The prequel to Life and Fate: the German advance to Stalingrad through the eyes of scientists, soldiers, and civilians.

title: Everything Flows
author: Vasily Grossman
description: A man released from the gulag returns to Moscow and reflects on the nature of Russian unfreedom from Ivan the Terrible to Stalin.

title: Momo
author: Michael Ende
description: A homeless girl who listens so well she heals people discovers that gray men are stealing everyone's time, and she must defeat them with help from a tortoise.

title: Cat and Mouse
author: Günter Grass
description: A boy with an enormous Adam's apple in wartime Danzig becomes a war hero to compensate, in the novella bridging The Tin Drum and Dog Years.

title: Dog Years
author: Günter Grass
description: Three narrators trace German history from the 1920s through the postwar miracle through the story of a dog descended from Hitler's German Shepherd.

title: Ariel
author: Sylvia Plath
description: The poems Plath wrote in the months before her suicide—Daddy, Lady Lazarus, Ariel—fierce, controlled, and incandescent with rage and beauty.

title: M Train
author: Patti Smith
description: Smith's meditation on loss, coffee, detective shows, and the objects and places that anchor a creative life—the companion to Just Kids.

title: Old School
author: Tobias Wolff
description: A scholarship boy at a prep school plagiarizes a story to win a contest judged by Hemingway, and the consequences haunt him.

title: In Pharaoh's Army
author: Tobias Wolff
description: Wolff's memoir of his year as an Army adviser in Vietnam—not combat heroics but the surreal boredom and moral rot of the war.

title: The Barracks Thief
author: Tobias Wolff
description: Three paratroopers at Fort Bragg—one steals from the others—and the betrayal reveals what the army does to young men.

title: Cherry
author: Mary Karr
description: The sequel to The Liars' Club: Karr's teenage years of drugs, sex, and surfing in 1970s Texas and California.

title: Lit
author: Mary Karr
description: Karr's memoir of alcoholism, marriage to a blue-blood, motherhood, and the conversion to Catholicism that saved her life.

title: Mountains Beyond Mountains
author: Tracy Kidder
description: The story of Paul Farmer, the doctor who built hospitals in Haiti and fought to make modern medicine available to the world's poorest people.

title: House
author: Tracy Kidder
description: The construction of a single house from foundation to roof, revealing the tensions between architect, builder, and homeowner.

title: On the Come Up
author: Angie Thomas
description: A sixteen-year-old rapper's song goes viral for the wrong reasons and she must navigate fame, poverty, and censorship.

title: Concrete Rose
author: Angie Thomas
description: The prequel to The Hate U Give follows Starr's father Maverick as a teenager dealing with gang life and unexpected fatherhood.

title: A Beautifully Foolish Endeavor
author: Hank Green
description: The sequel as the Carls disappear and a tech billionaire exploits the alien technology while the original group fights to stop him.

title: The Heart's Invisible Furies
author: John Boyne
description: A man born to an unwed mother in 1940s Ireland is adopted and lives through seven decades of Irish history, from repression to liberation.

title: A Ladder to the Sky
author: John Boyne
description: A talentless but beautiful young writer steals other people's stories and lives to fuel his career, destroying everyone he touches.

title: What Are You Going Through
author: Sigrid Nunez
description: A woman accompanies a friend who has chosen to end her life, and their last days together become a meditation on suffering and connection.

title: The Vulnerables
author: Sigrid Nunez
description: A writer pandemic-sits a parrot and reflects on loneliness, aging, and the strange intimacies of quarantine.

title: Shirley
author: Charlotte Brontë
description: Two women navigate love and independence during the Luddite uprisings in Yorkshire, in Brontë's most political and socially engaged novel.

title: The Collector
author: John Fowles
description: A lonely clerk kidnaps a beautiful art student and imprisons her in his cellar, told from both perspectives—his deluded love and her desperate captivity.

title: The Whole Truth
author: David Baldacci
description: A perception management firm manufactures a global crisis between Russia and the West, and two people discover the truth behind the fake war.

title: Memory Man
author: David Baldacci
description: A former football player with hyperthymesia—he can forget nothing—investigates a massacre at his old high school connected to his family's murder.

title: Long Road to Mercy
author: David Baldacci
description: An FBI agent investigating a mule's mysterious death in the Grand Canyon uncovers a conspiracy involving national security.

title: The Gold Coast
author: Nelson DeMille
description: A WASP lawyer on Long Island's Gold Coast becomes neighbors with a Mafia don and their families entangle in ways neither expected.

title: The General's Daughter
author: Nelson DeMille
description: A female captain is found murdered on an army base and the investigation reveals she was living a secret life that implicates the military establishment.

title: By the Rivers of Babylon
author: Nelson DeMille
description: Two Israeli Concordes are forced down in the Iraqi desert and the passengers must fight off Palestinian guerrillas while awaiting rescue.

title: Erasure
author: Percival Everett
description: A Black experimental novelist writes a parody of ghetto fiction that becomes a massive bestseller, and no one realizes it's satire.

title: I Am Not Sidney Poitier
author: Percival Everett
description: A man named Not Sidney Poitier who looks exactly like Sidney Poitier keeps living through the plots of Sidney Poitier movies.

title: Telephone
author: Percival Everett
description: A geologist discovers a code in geological data while his daughter deteriorates from a rare disease—published in three different versions with three different endings.

title: The Vanishing Act of Esme Lennox
author: Maggie O'Farrell
description: A woman discovers she has a great-aunt who has been locked in a psychiatric hospital for sixty years, and the reason why upends everything she knows.

title: Instructions for a Heatwave
author: Maggie O'Farrell
description: A father disappears during the 1976 London heatwave and his three adult children return home, each carrying secrets the heat brings to the surface.

title: This Must Be the Place
author: Maggie O'Farrell
description: A reclusive former actress hiding in rural Ireland is found by the past she fled, told in chapters that jump across decades and continents.

title: The Feral Detective
author: Jonathan Lethem
description: A woman hires a detective to find her missing friend's daughter in the California desert, and the search leads to a commune of runaways.

title: Chronic City
author: Jonathan Lethem
description: A former child actor and a cultural critic drift through a surreal Manhattan where a tiger roams underground and reality is negotiable.

title: Gun, with Occasional Music
author: Jonathan Lethem
description: A hardboiled detective in a future where evolved animals hold jobs and asking questions is illegal investigates a murder with a kangaroo as prime suspect.

title: Starter for Ten
author: David Nicholls
description: A working-class boy at Bristol University joins the University Challenge team to impress a girl and humiliates himself spectacularly.

title: Sweet Sorrow
author: David Nicholls
description: A boy with a depressed father is roped into a summer production of Romeo and Juliet and falls for the girl playing Juliet.

title: Tall Oaks
author: Chris Whitaker
description: A boy vanishes in a small town and every resident becomes a suspect in Whitaker's debut about community, secrets, and the American dream gone wrong.

title: The Fury
author: Alex Michaelides
description: A famous actress invites friends to her private Greek island and a murder follows, narrated by someone who may not be telling the truth.

title: His & Hers
author: Alice Feeney
description: A BBC newsreader and a detective are both connected to a murder in a small English village, and both are lying about why.

title: Four Aunties and a Wedding
author: Jesse Sutanto
description: The aunties are back, this time accidentally killing a guest at a destination wedding and hiding the body while the bride panics.

title: 10 Blind Dates
author: Ashley Elston
description: A heartbroken girl's large Southern family sets her up on ten blind dates over the holidays to get her over her ex.

title: Anastasia Krupnik
author: Lois Lowry
description: A ten-year-old girl keeps lists of things she loves and hates and navigates her parents' announcement that she's getting a sibling.

title: Gossamer
author: Lois Lowry
description: Tiny dream-givers touch sleeping people to bestow good dreams while dark creatures bring nightmares, and a young dream-giver must protect a troubled boy.

title: Hummingbird Salamander
author: Jeff VanderMeer
description: A woman receives a mysterious key from a dead eco-activist and follows a trail of clues involving a stuffed hummingbird and a stuffed salamander into a conspiracy.

title: Big Sky
author: Kate Atkinson
description: Jackson Brodie has retired to a Yorkshire seaside town and is drawn into a case involving sex trafficking, corrupt developers, and a silver-haired vigilante.

title: Transcription
author: Kate Atkinson
description: A young woman recruited by MI5 during WWII to transcribe conversations with fascist sympathizers is haunted by the work decades later.

title: A God in Ruins
author: Kate Atkinson
description: The companion to Life After Life follows Teddy, Ursula's brother, through his life as a bomber pilot and into peaceful, disappointing old age.

title: The Voice of the Violin
author: Andrea Camilleri
description: Montalbano investigates the murder of a beautiful young woman and the case connects to a missing Vermeer painting.

title: Excursion to Tindari
author: Andrea Camilleri
description: An elderly couple vanishes after booking a trip to the ancient Greek ruins, and a young man is found dead in front of their building.

title: The Patience of the Spider
author: Andrea Camilleri
description: Montalbano recovers from a gunshot wound while investigating a kidnapping that doesn't add up.

title: Kill Joy
author: Holly Jackson
description: A prequel novella where Pip attends a murder mystery party that turns into a real investigation.

title: Five Survive
author: Holly Jackson
description: Six friends on a road trip are stranded when a sniper shoots out their tires, and the shooter demands they reveal which one of them is hiding a secret.

title: Secretly Yours
author: Tessa Bailey
description: A free-spirited gardener and a stuffy wine professor are forced together when she's hired to tend his late grandmother's garden.

title: Unfortunately Yours
author: Tessa Bailey
description: Two enemies agree to a fake marriage so he can save his vineyard and she can access her trust fund.

title: The Hundred Secret Senses
author: Amy Tan
description: A woman's Chinese half-sister claims to remember a past life that connects them, and a trip to China forces a reckoning with belief and family.

title: Saving Fish from Drowning
author: Amy Tan
description: A dead socialite narrates the story of a group of her friends who go missing in Burma, kidnapped by a tribe that believes one of them is a god.

title: Far Tortuga
author: Peter Matthiessen
description: A doomed turtle-hunting expedition in the Caribbean told through dialogue, ship logs, and white space—Matthiessen's most experimental novel.

title: At Play in the Fields of the Lord
author: Peter Matthiessen
description: A mercenary pilot and missionaries converge on an uncontacted Amazon tribe, each pursuing their own salvation at the tribe's expense.

title: Shadow Country
author: Peter Matthiessen
description: The life of a Florida sugar planter and outlaw across three perspectives, revised from a trilogy into a single National Book Award-winning novel.

title: The Path to Power
author: Robert Caro
description: LBJ's rise from poverty in the Texas Hill Country through his first congressional race, in the first volume of Caro's masterwork.

title: Means of Ascent
author: Robert Caro
description: Johnson steals the 1948 Senate election by 87 votes and the methods reveal the ruthlessness that would define his career.

title: Master of the Senate
author: Robert Caro
description: Johnson transforms the Senate from a graveyard into an instrument of his will and passes the first civil rights bill since Reconstruction.

title: The Passage of Power
author: Robert Caro
description: The vice presidency, the assassination, and the first weeks of Johnson's presidency as he masters the transition and passes JFK's stalled agenda.

title: The Water Dancer
author: Ta-Nehisi Coates
description: An enslaved man with a supernatural memory power escapes through the Underground Railroad in Coates's debut novel.

title: Beatrice and Virgil
author: Yann Martel
description: A novelist receives a manuscript about a donkey and a monkey who are Holocaust survivors, and the relationship with the mysterious author turns sinister.

title: The High Mountains of Portugal
author: Yann Martel
description: Three stories connected across a century in Portugal: a man walking backward in grief, a pathologist examining a body, and a senator who adopts a chimpanzee.

title: The Full Cupboard of Life
author: Alexander McCall Smith
description: Mma Ramotswe investigates a wealthy man's fiancées while planning her own wedding to Mr. J.L.B. Matekoni.

title: Morality for Beautiful Girls
author: Alexander McCall Smith
description: Mma Ramotswe and Mma Makutsi investigate a poisoning at a government farm while navigating a beauty contest and an engagement.

title: The Weed That Strings the Hangman's Bag
author: Alan Bradley
description: Flavia investigates when a traveling puppet show comes to town and a puppeteer is murdered in the middle of Jack and the Beanstalk.

title: A Red Herring Without Mustard
author: Alan Bradley
description: A Gypsy woman attacked on the grounds of Buckshaw draws Flavia into a mystery involving a decades-old disappearance.

title: The Apprentice
author: Tess Gerritsen
description: A copycat of a jailed serial killer is operating in Boston, and Rizzoli must work with the imprisoned original to stop the apprentice.

title: The Sinner
author: Tess Gerritsen
description: A nun is found murdered in a convent and a pregnant woman is attacked, and the cases connect through a secret both institutions are protecting.

title: The Mephisto Club
author: Tess Gerritsen
description: A murder scene bearing satanic symbols leads Rizzoli to a secret society that studies evil and believes the Devil walks among us.

title: The Silent Girl
author: Tess Gerritsen
description: A severed hand found in Chinatown connects to a nineteen-year-old massacre and the Monkey King legend.

title: Looking Good Dead
author: Peter James
description: A man finds a USB stick on a train and watches a snuff video, and the killers know he has it.

title: Not Dead Enough
author: Peter James
description: A man is seen at a business dinner while his wife is being murdered at home, and Grace must figure out how he could be in two places at once.

title: Teaching a Stone to Talk
author: Annie Dillard
description: Fourteen essays on nature and the numinous—an eclipse, a weasel attack, a church in the Galápagos—Dillard looking at the world until it gives up its secrets.

title: An American Childhood
author: Annie Dillard
description: Growing up in 1950s Pittsburgh—a mother who loved pranks, a father who tried to sail to New Orleans, and the awakening of a girl's fierce attention to the world.

title: Under the Banner of Heaven
author: Jon Krakauer
description: A double murder by Mormon fundamentalists becomes an investigation into the violent history of the LDS Church and the nature of religious extremism.

title: Where Men Win Glory
author: Jon Krakauer
description: The story of Pat Tillman—the NFL player who enlisted after 9/11 and was killed by friendly fire, then covered up by the military.

title: The Rum Diary
author: Hunter S. Thompson
description: A young journalist takes a job at a failing newspaper in 1960s San Juan and discovers corruption, cockfighting, and his own dissolute appetites.

title: Fear and Loathing on the Campaign Trail '72
author: Hunter S. Thompson
description: Thompson covers the 1972 presidential campaign with manic energy, skewering Nixon, McGovern, and the American political machine.

title: Savage Son
author: Jack Carr
description: Reece is hunted by a Russian oligarch who collects human prey on his Siberian estate.

title: The Devil's Hand
author: Jack Carr
description: A bioweapon threatens the United States and Reece must track its source through the Middle East.

title: In the Blood
author: Jack Carr
description: Reece hunts a sniper targeting CIA operatives across three continents.

title: Only the Dead
author: Jack Carr
description: Reece confronts a conspiracy reaching the highest levels of government in the series' darkest entry.

title: Kill Shot
author: Vince Flynn
description: Young Rapp's third mission goes wrong in Paris when an informant betrays him and he must survive both the enemy and his own agency.

title: The Third Option
author: Vince Flynn
description: Rapp is sent to kill a target in Germany and is double-crossed by someone inside the CIA who wants him dead.

title: Separation of Power
author: Vince Flynn
description: Rapp must destroy an Iraqi nuclear weapons lab while his boss faces a confirmation hearing that could expose the entire covert operation.

title: Executive Power
author: Vince Flynn
description: A hostage rescue in the Philippines triggers a chain of events that puts Rapp in the crosshairs of a terrorist with a personal vendetta.

title: Act of Treason
author: Vince Flynn
description: An assassination attempt on the president-elect during the campaign is connected to someone inside the incoming administration.

title: The Sailor Who Fell from Grace with the Sea
author: Yukio Mishima
description: A boy spies on his widowed mother's affair with a sailor and, when the sailor settles into domesticity, the boy and his nihilistic gang take action.

title: Spring Snow
author: Yukio Mishima
description: A young aristocrat in Taisho-era Japan pursues a woman betrothed to a prince, and their doomed affair echoes through three more incarnations.

title: Runaway Horses
author: Yukio Mishima
description: A young ultranationalist in 1930s Japan plots to assassinate industrialists and restore the emperor, in the second Sea of Fertility novel.

title: The Temple of Dawn
author: Yukio Mishima
description: A lawyer encounters what may be the latest reincarnation—a Thai princess—and follows her from Bangkok to postwar Tokyo.

title: The Decay of the Angel
author: Yukio Mishima
description: The final reincarnation—or is it?—as the aging lawyer adopts a boy who may be the last soul, and the novel ends in devastating ambiguity.

title: Maurice
author: E.M. Forster
description: A young Edwardian man realizes he is homosexual and must navigate desire, class, and secrecy in a novel Forster wrote in 1913 but could not publish in his lifetime.

title: Where Angels Fear to Tread
author: E.M. Forster
description: An English widow marries an Italian man half her age in Tuscany, and her horrified in-laws send a rescue mission that ends in tragedy.

title: Raise High the Roof Beam, Carpenters and Seymour: An Introduction
author: J.D. Salinger
description: Two novellas about Seymour Glass: his wedding day told by Buddy, and Buddy's attempt to describe who Seymour really was.

title: Of Love and Other Demons
author: Gabriel García Márquez
description: A girl bitten by a rabid dog is suspected of demonic possession and locked in a convent, where a young priest falls in love with her.

title: Leaf Storm
author: Gabriel García Márquez
description: Three generations watch over a dead man's body in Macondo as the town refuses to bury him, in García Márquez's first novella set in his mythical town.

title: No One Writes to the Colonel
author: Gabriel García Márquez
description: An aging colonel waits for a pension that never comes while caring for a fighting cock that may be his last hope, in a perfect novella of dignity under poverty.

title: The Story of a Shipwrecked Sailor
author: Gabriel García Márquez
description: A sailor survives ten days on a raft after falling off a Colombian navy ship, and García Márquez's journalism reveals the government tried to suppress the story.

title: The Island of the Colorblind
author: Oliver Sacks
description: Sacks visits a Pacific island where total colorblindness is endemic and a Guam where a mysterious neurological disease has devastated the population.

title: Hallucinations
author: Oliver Sacks
description: The many forms of hallucination—from Charles Bonnet syndrome to sleep paralysis to drug-induced visions—examined with Sacks's trademark empathy.

title: Gratitude
author: Oliver Sacks
description: Four essays written in the months before Sacks's death, reflecting on aging, mortality, and the privilege of having lived.

title: The Ballad of Reading Gaol
author: Oscar Wilde
description: Wilde's poem about a condemned man in the prison where Wilde himself served two years, meditating on cruelty, guilt, and the way each man kills the thing he loves.

title: The Happy Prince and Other Tales
author: Oscar Wilde
description: Fairy tales for adults: a golden statue gives away its jewels, a nightingale dies for love, a selfish giant builds a wall—each one a parable of sacrifice.

title: Benito Cereno
author: Herman Melville
description: An American captain boards a distressed Spanish slave ship and cannot understand why the captain seems so nervous—the truth is far more terrifying than he imagines.

title: The Piazza Tales
author: Herman Melville
description: Six stories including Bartleby and Benito Cereno, plus The Encantadas—sketches of the Galápagos—and The Lightning-Rod Man.

title: A Universal History of Infamy
author: Jorge Luis Borges
description: Borges's first fiction: retold stories of real villains—pirates, outlaws, impostors—each compressed into a few dazzling pages.

title: Memoirs of a Dutiful Daughter
author: Simone de Beauvoir
description: De Beauvoir's memoir of her bourgeois Catholic childhood and her intellectual awakening at the Sorbonne where she met Sartre.

title: Eden
author: Stanisław Lem
description: A crashed ship's crew explores an alien planet and discovers a civilization so different that communication is impossible and observation is misinterpretation.

title: Return from the Stars
author: Stanisław Lem
description: An astronaut returns to Earth after a relativistic journey to find humanity has eliminated all aggression and risk, creating a safe but stifling paradise.

title: Memoirs Found in a Bathtub
author: Stanisław Lem
description: A man trapped in an underground Pentagon-like building tries to find his mission but every person he meets sends him to someone else in an infinite loop of paranoia.

title: The Lost World
author: Michael Crichton
description: A second island of dinosaurs is discovered and a team returns to study them, finding the animals have evolved in unexpected and dangerous ways.

title: Eaters of the Dead
author: Michael Crichton
description: An Arab diplomat joins a band of Vikings to fight a mysterious enemy in Scandinavia, retelling Beowulf as a historical thriller.

title: Congo
author: Michael Crichton
description: An expedition to the Congo rainforest searches for a lost city's diamond mines and encounters a population of killer gray gorillas.

title: Rising Sun
author: Michael Crichton
description: A murder in the boardroom of a Japanese corporation's LA headquarters exposes the economic tensions between America and Japan.

title: The October Country
author: Ray Bradbury
description: Nineteen stories of the dark and strange—a skeleton that wants to leave its owner, a man who inherits a jar of preserved specimens, a scythe that controls death.

title: I Sing the Body Electric!
author: Ray Bradbury
description: Eighteen stories including the title piece about a robot grandmother and The Kilimanjaro Device about rescuing Hemingway from his death.

title: The Toynbee Convector
author: Ray Bradbury
description: Twenty-three stories including the title piece about a man who faked time travel to give humanity hope—and it worked.

title: Long After Midnight
author: Ray Bradbury
description: Twenty-two stories ranging from horror to nostalgia to science fiction, including some of Bradbury's finest late work.

title: Lush Life
author: Richard Price
description: A mugging-turned-murder on the Lower East Side entangles a bartender, a detective, and the neighborhood itself in a novel about gentrifying New York.

title: The Whites
author: Richard Price
description: A night-watch detective in Manhattan is haunted by the one criminal he could never catch, and when someone starts killing his colleagues' white whales, he becomes a suspect.

title: The Snow Queen
author: Michael Cunningham
description: A man sees a celestial light in the sky over Central Park and it changes him, while his brother prepares to marry his dying partner in a novel about faith and love.

title: By Nightfall
author: Michael Cunningham
description: A middle-aged art dealer becomes obsessed with his wife's beautiful younger brother and the desire threatens to blow up his comfortable life.

title: My Own Country
author: Abraham Verghese
description: An Indian doctor in rural Tennessee treats the first AIDS patients in the area and becomes the town's reluctant expert on a disease no one wants to acknowledge.

title: An Indecent Obsession
author: Colleen McCullough
description: A nurse on a psychiatric ward for soldiers at the end of WWII struggles with her attraction to a patient and the arrival of a dangerous new inmate.

title: Presumed Innocent
author: Scott Turow
description: A prosecutor is accused of murdering his colleague and former lover, and the trial reveals that everyone in the system has something to hide.

title: The Burden of Proof
author: Scott Turow
description: A defense attorney investigates why his wife killed herself and discovers her death connects to a federal investigation of his brother-in-law.

title: Personal Injuries
author: Scott Turow
description: A personal injury lawyer is caught in a sting operation and must wear a wire to trap corrupt judges while protecting his own secrets.

title: Coma
author: Robin Cook
description: A medical student discovers that healthy patients are being put into comas at a Boston hospital and their organs harvested for profit.

title: Outbreak
author: Robin Cook
description: An epidemiologist investigates an Ebola-like outbreak and discovers the virus is being deliberately spread.

title: Even Cowgirls Get the Blues
author: Tom Robbins
description: A woman born with enormous thumbs becomes the world's greatest hitchhiker and joins a rancho of cowgirls who are hiding whooping cranes from the government.

title: Still Life with Woodpecker
author: Tom Robbins
description: A princess in exile falls for a radical bomber, and the novel asks how to make love stay while being typed on a Remington SL3.

title: Jitterbug Perfume
author: Tom Robbins
description: An immortal king, a Seattle waitress, a New Orleans perfumer, and the god Pan converge in a novel about immortality, beets, and the perfect scent.

title: Another Roadside Attraction
author: Tom Robbins
description: A couple running a roadside zoo acquires the mummified body of Jesus Christ and must decide what to do with it.

title: Death in a Strange Country
author: Donna Leon
description: An American soldier is found dead in a Venice canal and Brunetti's investigation leads to toxic waste dumping by the US military.

title: A Sea of Troubles
author: Donna Leon
description: Two clam fishermen are murdered on the island of Pellestrina and Brunetti sends Signorina Elettra undercover to the island.

title: Path of the Assassin
author: Brad Thor
description: Harvath must stop a terrorist plot targeting the Pope during a Middle Eastern peace summit.

title: Blowback
author: Brad Thor
description: An ancient weapon is discovered in the Alps and multiple factions race to claim it before it can be used.

title: Hidden Order
author: Brad Thor
description: Five candidates to lead the Federal Reserve are kidnapped and Harvath must find them before the economy collapses.

title: Here I Am
author: Jonathan Safran Foer
description: A family in Washington DC faces a divorce and an earthquake that destroys Israel simultaneously, and the personal and political collapse mirror each other.

title: Wash This Blood Clean from My Hand
author: Fred Vargas
description: Adamsberg pursues a killer who has been murdering for thirty years along the path of a medieval plague trail.

title: This Night's Foul Work
author: Fred Vargas
description: Two severed stag heads lead Adamsberg to a case involving a nurse, a cemetery, and the ghost of a medieval angel of death.

title: The Ghost Riders of Ordebec
author: Fred Vargas
description: A woman in Normandy sees the ghost riders—a medieval procession of the damned—and the men she saw start dying.

title: Attachments
author: Rainbow Rowell
description: A man hired to monitor company emails falls in love with a woman through reading her messages to her best friend.

title: Landline
author: Rainbow Rowell
description: A TV writer discovers her childhood phone connects to the past, letting her talk to her husband before they were married.

title: Slow Dance
author: Rainbow Rowell
description: Two best friends from high school reconnect at a wedding decades later and must decide if it's too late for what they always wanted.

title: The Pairing
author: Casey McQuiston
description: Two exes end up on the same European food and wine tour and compete to see who can have more flings before admitting they still want each other.

title: Royal Holiday
author: Jasmine Guillory
description: A woman accompanies her daughter to style a duchess and falls for the Queen's private secretary during a holiday at Sandringham.

title: By the Book
author: Jasmine Guillory
description: An editorial assistant and a reclusive bestselling author clash over his overdue manuscript and discover they have more in common than stubbornness.

title: "C" Is for Corpse
author: Sue Grafton
description: Kinsey befriends a young man recovering from a car accident that killed his friend, and when he's murdered, she investigates.

title: "D" Is for Deadbeat
author: Sue Grafton
description: A man hires Kinsey to deliver a check to a young man, then the man turns up drowned and the check bounces.

title: "E" Is for Evidence
author: Sue Grafton
description: Kinsey is framed for insurance fraud and must clear her name while someone methodically destroys her life.

title: "F" Is for Fugitive
author: Sue Grafton
description: A man convicted of murder seventeen years ago hires Kinsey after new evidence surfaces, but the small town doesn't want the case reopened.

title: Kentukis
author: Samanta Schweblin
description: People around the world buy cute surveillance devices and use them to watch strangers—or be watched—in a novel about digital voyeurism.

title: A Sunny Place for Shady People
author: Mariana Enriquez
description: New stories of Argentine horror: a cursed hotel, teenage Satanists, and the ghosts that haunt a continent built on violence.

title: Moth Smoke
author: Mohsin Hamid
description: A banker in Lahore loses his job, starts dealing drugs, and begins an affair with his best friend's wife as Pakistan tests nuclear weapons.

title: The Terracotta Dog
author: Andrea Camilleri
description: Montalbano discovers two mummified lovers in a cave alongside weapons from WWII and must solve a fifty-year-old mystery.

title: Rounding the Mark
author: Andrea Camilleri
description: A body washes ashore and Montalbano discovers a ring of child trafficking connected to illegal immigration.

title: The Old Gringo
author: Carlos Fuentes
description: Ambrose Bierce crosses into Mexico during the Revolution to die and instead becomes entangled with a schoolteacher and a general.

title: Philosopher or Dog?
author: Machado de Assis
description: A man inherits a fortune and a dog from an eccentric philosopher, and the schemers of Rio de Janeiro circle both, in a companion to Brás Cubas.

title: The Complete Stories
author: Clarice Lispector
description: All 85 of Lispector's stories in English for the first time—from her earliest work to her final pieces, each one an encounter with the mystery of consciousness.

title: Naomi
author: Jun'ichirō Tanizaki
description: A salaryman grooms a young café waitress to be his Westernized ideal woman, and she outgrows him in ways he never intended.

title: The Diary of a Mad Old Man
author: Jun'ichirō Tanizaki
description: A seventy-seven-year-old man's diary records his obsessive desire for his daughter-in-law's feet as his body fails.

title: The Gate
author: Natsume Sōseki
description: A married couple live in quiet contentment shadowed by the guilt of how they came together—he stole her from his best friend.

title: And Then
author: Natsume Sōseki
description: An idle young man supported by his father falls in love with his best friend's wife and must choose between duty and desire.

title: The Princess Casamassima
author: Henry James
description: A bookbinder in London is drawn into an anarchist conspiracy and pledges to commit an assassination, then falls in love with the aristocratic world he promised to destroy.

title: The Spoils of Poynton
author: Henry James
description: A mother and her son's fiancée battle over a house full of beautiful objects, and the woman who could resolve everything refuses to act.

title: Hold Tight
author: Harlan Coben
