description: Parents install spyware on their son's computer and discover he's connected to a suicide, setting off a chain reaction across several families.

title: Fool Me Once
author: Harlan Coben
description: A former army pilot sees her dead husband on a nanny cam and must figure out how a man she buried is walking through her home.

title: I Found You
author: Lisa Jewell
description: A man with no memory washes up on a beach in Yorkshire and a woman in London reports her new husband missing—and the stories converge on a terrible truth.

title: One by One
author: Freida McFadden
description: Guests at a vacation rental are murdered one by one during a blizzard and the survivors must figure out who among them is the killer.

title: The Locked Door
author: Freida McFadden
description: A surgeon discovers evidence that her serial-killer father may not have acted alone, and the accomplice may still be active.

title: The Lying Game
author: Ruth Ware
description: Four women who played a lying game at boarding school are summoned by one of them: a body has been found in the estuary near their old school.

title: My Sister's Keeper
author: Jodi Picoult
description: A girl conceived to be a donor for her leukemia-stricken sister sues her parents for the rights to her own body.

title: Small Great Things
author: Jodi Picoult
description: A Black labor and delivery nurse is forbidden from touching a white supremacist's baby, and when the baby dies, she is charged with murder.

title: The Book of Two Ways
author: Jodi Picoult
description: A woman surviving a plane crash sees two possible lives—one with her husband, one with the Egyptologist she loved—and must choose.

title: Nineteen Minutes
author: Jodi Picoult
description: A school shooting in a small New Hampshire town is examined from every angle—the shooter, the judge, the victims, and the parents who didn't see it coming.

title: Wish You Were Here
author: Jodi Picoult
description: A woman stranded alone in the Galápagos during COVID questions everything about her life as reality and hallucination blur.

title: Watchers
author: Dean Koontz
description: A man adopts a super-intelligent golden retriever escaped from a lab, hunted by both the government and a monstrous creature created in the same experiment.

title: Intensity
author: Dean Koontz
description: A young woman hides in a serial killer's motor home as he drives to his rural lair, and she must escape before she becomes his next victim.

title: The Bad Place
author: Dean Koontz
description: A man who wakes up covered in blood with no memory hires private detectives to find out what happens when he sleeps.

title: Phantoms
author: Dean Koontz
description: Two sisters arrive in a mountain town to find everyone dead or missing, and something ancient beneath the earth is responsible.

title: Odd Thomas
author: Dean Koontz
description: A short-order cook who sees dead people must stop a catastrophe when bodachs—invisible predators—swarm his small California town.

title: Strangers
author: Dean Koontz
description: People across America are gripped by unexplained phobias and sleepwalking, all connected to something that happened at a Nevada motel.

title: Practical Magic
author: Alice Hoffman
description: Two sisters from a family of witches try to escape their legacy of doomed love affairs, but magic keeps pulling them back.

title: The Rules of Magic
author: Alice Hoffman
description: The prequel: three siblings in 1960s New York discover their family's magical gifts and the curse that comes with them.

title: The Dovekeepers
author: Alice Hoffman
description: Four women at Masada during the Roman siege of 73 CE tell their stories of survival, magic, and defiance.

title: The Museum of Extraordinary Things
author: Alice Hoffman
description: A Coney Island sideshow performer and a Ukrainian immigrant photographer cross paths during the Triangle Shirtwaist Factory fire era.

title: The World That We Knew
author: Alice Hoffman
description: A mother in 1940s Berlin creates a golem to protect her daughter as she flees the Nazis into occupied France.

title: Fried Green Tomatoes at the Whistle Stop Cafe
author: Fannie Flagg
description: An elderly woman in a nursing home tells stories of 1930s Alabama—the café, the railroad, and the fierce friendship between two women that defied every convention.

title: Standing in the Rainbow
author: Fannie Flagg
description: A small Missouri town from the 1940s through the 1990s, told through the community that holds together through change.

title: Plainsong
author: Kent Haruf
description: A pregnant teenager, two elderly bachelor ranchers, and a schoolteacher converge in a Colorado plains town in a novel of quiet, devastating compassion.

title: Eventide
author: Kent Haruf
description: The McPheron brothers and Victoria Roubideaux return as the town of Holt faces domestic violence, child neglect, and the small kindnesses that hold a community together.

title: Our Souls at Night
author: Kent Haruf
description: A widow proposes to her widower neighbor that they spend their nights together to ease the loneliness, and the town talks while they find unexpected love.

title: Benediction
author: Kent Haruf
description: A dying hardware store owner reflects on his life and his estranged son while the women of Holt care for a girl in trouble.

title: True Grit
author: Charles Portis
description: A fourteen-year-old girl hires a one-eyed drunk of a marshal to hunt the man who killed her father across Indian Territory.

title: Norwood
author: Charles Portis
description: A gas station attendant drives from Arkansas to New York to collect a debt and picks up the most American road trip companions imaginable.

title: The Dog of the South
author: Charles Portis
description: A man pursues his wife and her lover to Mexico and Honduras in a car chase that is also a picaresque comedy of American incompetence abroad.

title: Masters of Atlantis
author: Charles Portis
description: A WWI veteran buys the secret knowledge of a lost civilization from a con man and spends his life running the Gnomon Society, a fraternal order with no real secrets.

title: Gringos
author: Charles Portis
description: An American expat in Mexico dodges New Age seekers, fugitives, and hippies while searching for a missing archaeological site.

title: Winter's Bone
author: Daniel Woodrell
description: A sixteen-year-old Ozark girl must find her meth-cooking father—dead or alive—before the family loses their house, because he put it up for bail.

title: Tomato Red
author: Daniel Woodrell
description: A drifter falls in with a poor Ozark family scheming to escape their circumstances, and violence is the only currency anyone accepts.

title: The Death of Sweet Mister
author: Daniel Woodrell
description: A fat, gentle boy is forced by his stepfather to rob pharmacies for pills in the Missouri Ozarks in Woodrell's most heartbreaking novel.

title: Give Us a Kiss
author: Daniel Woodrell
description: A writer returns to the Ozarks and gets pulled into his family's marijuana crop, a feud with meth dealers, and an affair with a dangerous woman.

title: Woe to Live On
author: Daniel Woodrell
description: Missouri guerrillas ride with Quantrill during the Civil War in a novel that became the film Ride with the Devil.

title: Fay
author: Larry Brown
description: A seventeen-year-old girl walks away from her abusive family in rural Mississippi and drifts into stripping, crime, and the arms of men who are no better.

title: Joe
author: Larry Brown
description: A hard-drinking ex-con who runs a tree-poisoning crew takes a beaten boy under his wing, and their bond becomes a lifeline in a world of violence.

title: Dirty Work
author: Larry Brown
description: Two Vietnam veterans—one Black, one white, both wrecked—share a VA hospital room and talk through the night about what the war did to them.

title: Father and Son
author: Larry Brown
description: A man released from prison returns to his Mississippi hometown and the collision with his sheriff father builds toward inevitable violence.

title: The Sportswriter
author: Richard Ford
description: A former novelist turned sportswriter navigates Easter weekend in suburban New Jersey—divorce, death, and the dreaminess of a life deliberately uncommitted.

title: Independence Day
author: Richard Ford
description: Frank Bascombe sells real estate and takes his troubled son on a Fourth of July road trip to the baseball Hall of Fame, in the first novel to win both the Pulitzer and PEN/Faulkner.

title: The Lay of the Land
author: Richard Ford
description: Frank at fifty-five faces prostate cancer, a wife who left him for her first husband, and Thanksgiving in post-9/11 New Jersey.

title: Let Me Be Frank with You
author: Richard Ford
description: Four novellas of Frank Bascombe in the aftermath of Hurricane Sandy, dealing with destroyed homes and the permanence of impermanence.

title: Rock Springs
author: Richard Ford
description: Ten stories of the American West—men failing at jobs, marriages, and fatherhood across Montana, Wyoming, and the Great Plains.

title: Wildlife
author: Richard Ford
description: A sixteen-year-old boy watches his parents' marriage disintegrate when his father goes to fight a forest fire and his mother begins an affair.

title: Canada
author: Richard Ford
description: A boy's parents rob a bank, and he is sent across the border to Saskatchewan where a charming expatriate may be a murderer.

title: Beautiful Ruins
author: Jess Walter
description: An Italian innkeeper falls for an actress in 1962 and the story jumps to modern Hollywood, connecting Fellini, Cleopatra, and fifty years of longing.

title: The Cold Millions
author: Jess Walter
description: Two orphaned brothers in 1909 Spokane are caught between labor organizers and Pinkerton thugs during the free speech fights.

title: The Angel of Rome
author: Jess Walter
description: Stories spanning decades and continents—a woman meeting her Tinder date's mother, a man tracking his stolen bicycle, an Italian con man's last grift.

title: The Financial Lives of the Poets
author: Jess Walter
description: A journalist who quit to start a financial poetry website is losing his house and his wife, so he starts dealing pot.

title: Affliction
author: Russell Banks
description: A small-town cop in New Hampshire, abused as a child, investigates a hunting death while his own rage and his father's violence consume him.

title: The Sweet Hereafter
author: Russell Banks
description: A school bus accident kills fourteen children in a small town, and four narrators tell the story of the aftermath—grief, lawsuits, and the impossibility of justice.

title: Continental Drift
author: Russell Banks
description: A New Hampshire oil burner repairman moves to Florida seeking a new life, and a Haitian woman flees to America by boat, and their stories converge.

title: Cloudsplitter
author: Russell Banks
description: John Brown's son narrates the abolitionist's obsessive quest to free the slaves, from the Kansas wars to Harpers Ferry.

title: Rule of the Bone
author: Russell Banks
description: A homeless teenager in upstate New York drifts through drugs and petty crime before following a Rastafarian to Jamaica in a Huck Finn for the 1990s.

title: Jayber Crow
author: Wendell Berry
description: The barber of Port William, Kentucky narrates his life and his silent, unrequited love for a woman married to a man who is destroying the land.

title: Hannah Coulter
author: Wendell Berry
description: A widow in her eighties looks back on two marriages, farming, and the loss of her children to the modern world that pulls them from the land.

title: The Memory of Old Jack
author: Wendell Berry
description: An old farmer spends his last day remembering a life of work, marriage, and the Kentucky land he loved more than anything.

title: That Distant Land
author: Wendell Berry
description: The collected Port William stories spanning a century of Kentucky farming life—birth, death, seasons, and the community that holds.

title: The Nix
author: Nathan Hill
description: A professor's mother—who abandoned him as a child—reappears on the news attacking a presidential candidate, and he must uncover her 1968 secrets to save her.

title: Wellness
author: Nathan Hill
description: A couple who fell in love in 1990s Chicago face a marriage in crisis twenty years later as placebos, algorithms, and self-deception test what's real.

title: Euphoria
author: Lily King
description: A love triangle among three anthropologists in 1930s New Guinea, inspired by Margaret Mead, in a novel about the thrill of discovery and the violence of possession.

title: Writers & Lovers
author: Lily King
description: A broke waitress writing her first novel in her thirties must choose between two men—a widowed father and a famous novelist—while her mother is dying.

title: Lost Children Archive
author: Valeria Luiselli
description: A family drives from New York to Arizona as the parents' marriage frays and the border crisis of migrant children echoes through the landscape.

title: The Story of My Teeth
author: Valeria Luiselli
description: An auctioneer who replaces his teeth with those of famous people tells his life story through the objects he's sold.

title: Tell Me How It Ends
author: Valeria Luiselli
description: Luiselli volunteers as a translator for migrant children in immigration court and confronts the bureaucratic horror of the forty questions they must answer.

title: Signs Preceding the End of the World
author: Yuri Herrera
description: A woman crosses the Mexican border to find her brother, and the journey becomes a mythological descent into the underworld.

title: Kingdom Cons
author: Yuri Herrera
description: A songwriter becomes the court bard of a Mexican drug lord, and his ballads shape the violence they celebrate.

title: When We Cease to Understand the World
author: Benjamín Labatut
description: True stories of scientists—Fritz Haber, Schwarzschild, Heisenberg, Grothendieck—whose discoveries pushed knowledge past the edge of sanity.

title: The MANIAC
author: Benjamín Labatut
description: The life of John von Neumann—from Budapest to Los Alamos to the invention of the computer—told by the people around him as AI approaches.

title: Go, Went, Gone
author: Jenny Erpenbeck
description: A retired classics professor in Berlin becomes involved with African refugees and discovers how little he knows about the world outside his books.

title: Kairos
author: Jenny Erpenbeck
description: A love affair in 1980s East Berlin parallels the collapse of the GDR—both the relationship and the state built on lies.

title: Visitation
author: Jenny Erpenbeck
description: A house by a Brandenburg lake passes through owners across the 20th century—each displaced by history—in a compressed masterpiece.

title: Time Shelter
author: Georgi Gospodinov
description: A clinic in Zurich recreates past decades for dementia patients, and the idea spreads until entire nations vote to live permanently in the past.

title: Hurricane Season
author: Fernanda Melchor
description: A witch's body is found in a canal in rural Mexico and the town's voices spiral through poverty, violence, machismo, and narco terror in torrential prose.

title: Paradais
author: Fernanda Melchor
description: A fat teenager and a garden boy in a gated Mexican community bond over beer and shared resentment that escalates toward a horrifying act.

title: Alexander Hamilton
author: Ron Chernow
description: The biography that inspired the musical—Hamilton's rise from Caribbean orphan to founding father, and the duel that killed him.

title: Washington: A Life
author: Ron Chernow
description: Washington from surveyor to general to president, revealing the man behind the monument—his temper, his teeth, his slaves, and his ambition.

title: Grant
author: Ron Chernow
description: Ulysses S. Grant rehabilitated—from failed farmer to Civil War genius to president who fought the Klan, then ruined by trusting the wrong people.

title: Titan
author: Ron Chernow
description: John D. Rockefeller from devout bookkeeper to the richest American who ever lived—ruthless, brilliant, and ultimately a revolutionary philanthropist.

title: John Adams
author: David McCullough
description: The overlooked founding father—stubborn, brilliant, vain—and his fifty-year marriage to Abigail, told with McCullough's narrative gift.

author: David McCullough
description: The year that decided the Revolution—Washington's defeats, the crossing of the Delaware, and the amateur army that somehow survived.

title: The Wright Brothers
author: David McCullough
description: Two bicycle mechanics from Dayton, Ohio solve the problem of powered flight through stubbornness, ingenuity, and a willingness to crash.

title: Truman
author: David McCullough
description: The haberdasher who became president—the bomb, the Cold War, Korea—told as the story of an ordinary man in extraordinary circumstances.

title: Team of Rivals
author: Doris Kearns Goodwin
description: Lincoln puts his three rivals for the Republican nomination in his cabinet and manages their egos to win the Civil War and end slavery.

title: No Ordinary Time
author: Doris Kearns Goodwin
description: Franklin and Eleanor Roosevelt during WWII—their marriage, their separate lives, and the home front that transformed America.

title: The Bully Pulpit
author: Doris Kearns Goodwin
description: Theodore Roosevelt, William Howard Taft, and the muckraking journalists who together defined the Progressive Era.

title: Just Mercy
author: Bryan Stevenson
description: A young lawyer represents a man wrongly condemned to death in Alabama and confronts the racism embedded in America's criminal justice system.

title: Evicted
author: Matthew Desmond
description: Eight families in Milwaukee face eviction and a sociologist shows how losing your home is not a symptom of poverty but a cause of it.

title: Poverty, by America
author: Matthew Desmond
description: Desmond argues that poverty persists in America not despite the affluent but because of them—through exploitation, tax policy, and willful ignorance.

title: On Tyranny
author: Timothy Snyder
description: Twenty lessons from the 20th century on how to resist authoritarianism, written in the months after the 2016 election.

title: Bloodlands
author: Timothy Snyder
description: The fourteen million people killed by Hitler and Stalin in the lands between them—Ukraine, Poland, Belarus—in a history that refuses to let either regime escape comparison.

title: The Perfect Storm
author: Sebastian Junger
description: The 1991 nor'easter that sank the Andrea Gail and its six-man crew, reconstructed from weather data, radio transmissions, and the science of giant waves.

title: War
author: Sebastian Junger
description: A year embedded with a platoon in Afghanistan's Korengal Valley—the most dangerous posting in the US military—and what combat does to men.

title: Tribe
author: Sebastian Junger
description: Junger argues that modern society has lost the tribal bonds that sustained humans for millennia, and that's why veterans can't come home.

title: Ghost Soldiers
author: Hampton Sides
description: The raid on the Cabanatuan POW camp in the Philippines—the greatest rescue mission of WWII—told hour by hour.

title: Blood and Thunder
author: Hampton Sides
description: Kit Carson and the conquest of the American West, from the fur trade through the Navajo Long Walk, in a revisionist history of Manifest Destiny.

title: In the Kingdom of Ice
author: Hampton Sides
description: The doomed 1879 voyage of the USS Jeannette to reach the North Pole through the ice, and the crew's desperate march across Siberia.

title: Humankind
author: Rutger Bregman
description: A Dutch historian argues that humans are fundamentally decent—debunking the Stanford Prison Experiment, Lord of the Flies, and other pessimistic myths.

title: Utopia for Realists
author: Rutger Bregman
description: Universal basic income, open borders, and a fifteen-hour workweek are not utopian fantasies but practical policies supported by evidence.

title: Stolen Focus
author: Johann Hari
description: Why you can't pay attention anymore—it's not your fault, it's a crisis caused by technology, pollution, stress, and a food system that impairs cognition.

title: Lost Connections
author: Johann Hari
description: Depression and anxiety are not just brain chemistry but responses to disconnection—from meaningful work, nature, other people, and the future.

title: Chasing the Scream
author: Johann Hari
description: The hundred-year war on drugs has failed, and Hari travels the world finding alternatives that actually work.

title: Dead Until Dark
author: Charlaine Harris
description: A telepathic waitress in Louisiana falls for a vampire and gets pulled into a murder investigation when women who date vampires start dying.

title: Living Dead in Dallas
author: Charlaine Harris
description: Sookie is loaned to the Dallas vampires to find a missing colleague using her telepathy, and discovers a church that hunts the undead.

title: Club Dead
author: Charlaine Harris
description: Sookie travels to Mississippi to rescue her vampire boyfriend from a powerful vampire king who has taken him prisoner.

title: Dead to the World
author: Charlaine Harris
description: Eric Northman shows up on Sookie's doorstep with amnesia, and a coven of witches is responsible for cursing him.

title: Moon Called
author: Patricia Briggs
description: A VW mechanic who can shift into a coyote is drawn into werewolf pack politics when a newly turned teenager shows up at her shop.

title: Blood Bound
author: Patricia Briggs
description: Mercy owes a favor to a vampire and must help hunt a sorcerer-vampire hybrid that is slaughtering people in the Tri-Cities.

title: Iron Kissed
author: Patricia Briggs
description: The fae ask Mercy to investigate murders on their reservation, and the case exposes dangerous secrets the fae have been hiding.

title: Rivers of London
author: Ben Aaronovitch
description: A London cop discovers magic is real when he interviews a ghost at a crime scene and is apprenticed to the last wizard in the Met Police.

title: Moon Over Soho
author: Ben Aaronovitch
description: Peter Grant investigates jazz musicians dying under mysterious circumstances while a supernatural tiger stalks London's streets.

title: Whispers Under Ground
author: Ben Aaronovitch
description: A murder in the London Underground leads Peter to an underground community of magical potters and a connection to the spirit of the city's sewers.

title: Broken Homes
author: Ben Aaronovitch
description: A brutalist tower block on a South London estate was designed to channel magic, and Peter must stop someone from using it as a weapon.

title: Magic Bites
author: Ilona Andrews
description: In a post-apocalyptic Atlanta where magic and technology alternate, a mercenary investigates her guardian's death and gets caught between the city's vampire and shapeshifter factions.

title: Magic Burns
author: Ilona Andrews
description: Kate takes on a case involving a missing map and a teenage girl while ancient Celtic powers stir during a magical flare.

title: Magic Strikes
author: Ilona Andrews
description: Kate enters an underground fighting tournament to save a friend and discovers a conspiracy within the shapeshifter pack.

title: The Tale of the Body Thief
author: Anne Rice
description: Lestat trades bodies with a mortal and discovers that being human again is horrifying in its vulnerability and ordinariness.

title: Brown Girl Dreaming
author: Jacqueline Woodson
description: Woodson's childhood in verse—growing up Black in South Carolina and Brooklyn in the 1960s and '70s, between the civil rights movement and the world of stories.

title: Another Brooklyn
author: Jacqueline Woodson
description: Four girls in 1970s Brooklyn form a fierce bond as they navigate adolescence, beauty, and the dangers lurking at the edges of their neighborhood.

title: Red at the Bone
author: Jacqueline Woodson
description: A teenage pregnancy connects two Brooklyn families across generations, and a daughter's coming-of-age ceremony becomes a reckoning with class and ambition.

title: The Crossover
author: Kwame Alexander
description: Twin brothers who are basketball stars navigate their father's health crisis and their changing relationship in a novel written in verse.

title: Booked
author: Kwame Alexander
description: A soccer-playing boy who hates reading discovers the power of words as his parents' marriage falls apart.

title: Stargirl
author: Jerry Spinelli
description: A nonconformist girl arrives at a conformist Arizona high school and her joyful weirdness first enchants then threatens the student body.

title: Maniac Magee
author: Jerry Spinelli
description: A homeless boy becomes a legend in a racially divided Pennsylvania town by running, reading, and refusing to accept that the East End and West End can't get along.

title: Wringer
author: Jerry Spinelli
description: A boy dreads the day he must participate in his town's annual pigeon shoot, especially after he secretly befriends a pigeon.

title: Monster
author: Walter Dean Myers
description: A Black teenager on trial for murder writes the experience as a screenplay, questioning whether the jury can see him as human.

title: Fallen Angels
author: Walter Dean Myers
description: A Harlem teenager serves in Vietnam and watches the war strip away every illusion about heroism, patriotism, and survival.

title: Scorpions
author: Walter Dean Myers
description: A twelve-year-old boy in Harlem is pressured to lead his brother's gang and given a gun that changes everything.

title: Out of My Mind
author: Sharon Draper
description: A brilliant girl with cerebral palsy who cannot speak or move on her own gets a device that lets her communicate for the first time.

title: Stella by Starlight
author: Sharon Draper
description: A Black girl in 1930s North Carolina witnesses a Klan burning and must find the courage to speak out as her community fights for the right to vote.

title: Diary of a Wimpy Kid
author: Jeff Kinney
description: A scrawny middle schooler documents his misadventures in a journal filled with cartoons as he tries to survive the social minefield of sixth grade.

title: Rodrick Rules
author: Jeff Kinney
description: Greg's older brother Rodrick torments him while Mom tries to make them bond, and a secret from a pool party threatens to ruin Greg's life.

title: The Last Straw
author: Jeff Kinney
description: Dad tries to toughen Greg up by getting him into sports, and Greg must find a way to avoid military school.

title: Smile
author: Raina Telgemeier
description: A girl knocks out her front teeth in sixth grade and spends years in braces and dental surgery while navigating the cruelty of middle school.

title: Sisters
author: Raina Telgemeier
description: A road trip from San Francisco to Colorado with an annoying younger sister tests their bond and reveals the cracks in their parents' marriage.

title: Drama
author: Raina Telgemeier
description: A theater-loving girl navigates middle school crushes and backstage drama while designing sets for the school musical.

title: Guts
author: Raina Telgemeier
description: A girl with severe stomach problems and anxiety learns that her body and her fears are connected, and therapy helps.

title: Priest
author: Sierra Simone
description: A Catholic priest falls for a woman in his congregation and must choose between his vocation and the love that is consuming him.

title: Any Duchess Will Do
author: Tessa Dare
description: A duke's mother demands he choose a bride, so he picks the least suitable woman in England—a barmaid—assuming his mother will give up.

title: The Governess Game
author: Tessa Dare
description: A rake hires a governess for his two wards and their battle over the children's education becomes an irresistible attraction.

title: Again the Magic
author: Lisa Kleypas
description: A nobleman's daughter and a servant's son are separated by her father and reunite years later when he has become a self-made millionaire.

title: Marrying Winterborne
author: Lisa Kleypas
description: A Welsh department store owner and a sheltered aristocrat's daughter navigate class differences and family opposition.

title: To Sir Phillip, With Love
author: Julia Quinn
description: Eloise Bridgerton corresponds with a widowed botanist and shows up at his door, discovering he is nothing like his letters.

title: When He Was Wicked
author: Julia Quinn
description: Francesca Bridgerton's husband's cousin has been secretly in love with her for years, and when she is widowed, guilt and desire collide.

title: It's in His Kiss
author: Julia Quinn
description: Hyacinth Bridgerton teams up with a reluctant garnet-hunting rake and their bickering becomes the best Bridgerton courtship.

title: On the Way to the Wedding
author: Julia Quinn
description: Gregory Bridgerton falls in love at first sight—with the wrong woman—while the right one has been beside him all along.

title: Born in Fire
author: Nora Roberts
description: An Irish glass artist clashes with a Dublin gallery owner who wants to represent her, and their professional relationship becomes intensely personal.

title: The Liar
author: Nora Roberts
description: A widow discovers her husband was a con man with multiple identities and debts to dangerous people, and she must rebuild in the Tennessee mountains.

title: Come Sundown
author: Nora Roberts
description: A woman who was kidnapped and held captive for twenty-five years escapes, and her return forces a Montana ranching family to confront the evil among them.

title: Finding Cinderella
author: Colleen Hoover
description: Two strangers share a random encounter in a dark room and the connection is instant—but meeting again is complicated.

title: Lightning
author: Dean Koontz
description: A woman has been protected by a mysterious stranger since birth—appearing at key moments to save her life—and the reason connects to time travel and Nazi Germany.

title: Midnight
author: Dean Koontz
description: Residents of a California coastal town are being transformed into something inhuman by a rogue scientist's experiments with microwave technology.

title: From the Corner of His Eye
author: Dean Koontz
description: A blind boy who can see through rain, a woman who survives tragedy through grace, and a psychopath converge in a novel about the interconnectedness of lives.

title: The Face
author: Dean Koontz
description: A bodyguard protects a movie star's son from a deranged anarchist sending increasingly bizarre threats, while the boy's own abilities defy explanation.

title: Gone South
author: Robert McCammon
description: A Vietnam vet on the run for murder flees through the Louisiana bayous with a young woman searching for a faith healer.

title: Speaks the Nightbird
author: Robert McCammon
description: A young magistrate's clerk in 1699 Carolina investigates a woman accused of witchcraft and discovers the real evil in the colony.

title: Survivor Song
author: Paul Tremblay
description: A pregnant woman bitten during a fast-spreading rabies-like epidemic must reach a hospital before she turns, accompanied by her pediatrician friend.

title: Pearl
author: Josh Malerman
description: A man discovers something monstrous growing in his yard—a creature made of discarded things that becomes increasingly intelligent and demanding.

title: Daphne
author: Josh Malerman
description: A high school basketball team begins dying one by one and the surviving girls suspect a figure from their past called Daphne.

title: Tales of the South Pacific
author: James Michener
description: Linked stories of Americans in the Pacific during WWII—the boredom, the beauty, the terror—that became the musical South Pacific.

title: Alaska
author: James Michener
description: From mammoths crossing the land bridge to Russian fur traders to the oil pipeline, Alaska's history through the people who shaped it.

title: Poland
author: James Michener
description: Three Polish families across eight centuries of invasion, partition, and resistance, from the Tatar hordes to Solidarity.

title: Caravans
author: James Michener
description: An American diplomat in 1946 Afghanistan searches for a woman who married a nomad and vanished into the tribal lands.

title: The King's Curse
author: Philippa Gregory
description: Margaret Pole navigates the reigns of three Tudor monarchs while carrying the Plantagenet blood that makes her both valuable and dangerous.

title: The Lady of the Rivers
author: Philippa Gregory
description: Jacquetta of Luxembourg uses her gift of foresight to navigate the Wars of the Roses and position her daughter to become queen.

title: The Kingmaker's Daughter
author: Philippa Gregory
description: Anne Neville is married to two rival claimants to the throne as her father the Kingmaker plays his deadly game.

title: Khan: Empire of Silver
author: Conn Iggulden
description: Ogedei Khan rules the Mongol Empire while his brothers and nephews scheme for power after Genghis's death.

title: Conqueror
author: Conn Iggulden
description: Kublai Khan rises to power over his brothers and extends the empire to China, completing the Conqueror series.

title: The Gift
author: Danielle Steel
description: A teenage girl in the 1950s faces an unwanted pregnancy and gives up her baby, and the decision haunts her for decades.

title: Safe Harbour
author: Danielle Steel
description: A widowed photographer and a recently separated man meet on a Cape Cod beach and help each other rebuild from loss.

title: The House
author: Danielle Steel
description: A woman buys a ruined San Francisco mansion after a divorce and restoring it becomes a metaphor for rebuilding her life.

title: Daddy
author: Danielle Steel
description: When his wife leaves, a suburban father must raise three children alone and discovers strengths he never knew he had.

title: One for the Money
author: Janet Evanovich
description: A newly divorced woman becomes a bounty hunter in Trenton, New Jersey to pay her rent, and her first skip is the cop who took her virginity.

title: Two for the Dough
author: Janet Evanovich
description: Stephanie hunts a bail jumper while stolen caskets of ammunition circulate through Trenton's underworld.

title: Three to Get Deadly
author: Janet Evanovich
description: Stephanie must bring in the town's most beloved citizen—an elderly candy store owner—and no one can believe he's done anything wrong.

title: Four to Score
author: Janet Evanovich
description: Stephanie chases a woman who stole her ex's car and left a coded message, while a psycho stalker targets Stephanie herself.

title: High Five
author: Janet Evanovich
description: Stephanie searches for her missing uncle Fred while juggling two love interests and accidentally blowing up multiple cars.

title: Raise the Titanic!
author: Clive Cussler
description: Dirk Pitt leads an expedition to raise the Titanic from the ocean floor because its cargo holds a rare mineral needed for a Cold War defense system.

title: Inca Gold
author: Clive Cussler
description: Pitt hunts for a legendary Inca treasure in the Andes while an antiquities smuggling ring tries to stop him.

title: Cyclops
author: Clive Cussler
description: Pitt discovers a secret colony on the moon and a sunken treasure ship while Cuba and the Soviet Union threaten to start WWIII.

title: Seeing Red
author: Sandra Brown
description: A man investigating his firefighter father's death in a hotel fire clashes with the woman whose father was accused of setting it.

title: Outfox
author: Sandra Brown
description: An FBI agent hunts a con man who seduces women and then vanishes—or kills them—and he's been one step ahead for years.

title: Mean Streak
author: Sandra Brown
description: A doctor goes missing during a mountain run and wakes in an isolated cabin with a stranger who won't let her leave.

title: Envy
author: Sandra Brown
description: A book editor receives a mysterious manuscript that appears to describe a real unsolved crime, and the author wants revenge.

title: Where Are the Children?
author: Mary Higgins Clark
description: A woman whose two children from her first marriage disappeared years ago faces the nightmare again when her current children vanish.

title: A Stranger Is Watching
author: Mary Higgins Clark
description: A journalist's wife was murdered and the man convicted may be innocent—someone else is watching, and has taken the journalist's son.

title: The Cradle Will Fall
author: Mary Higgins Clark
description: A prosecutor sees a doctor loading a body into his car from a hospital window, and the doctor realizes she saw him.

title: I'll Walk Alone
author: Mary Higgins Clark
description: A woman whose toddler was kidnapped two years ago discovers that someone has been stealing her identity—and it may be connected to the kidnapping.

title: The Other Side of Midnight
author: Sidney Sheldon
description: A beautiful actress and a war hero's wife discover they share the same man, and the revenge spans continents.

title: Master of the Game
author: Sidney Sheldon
description: Four generations of a South African diamond dynasty, from a young Scotsman who walked to the diamond fields to his great-granddaughters who inherit his ruthlessness.

title: If Tomorrow Comes
author: Sidney Sheldon
description: A woman framed and imprisoned becomes the world's greatest con artist, pulling heists across Europe while a detective pursues her.

title: Rage of Angels
author: Sidney Sheldon
description: A young female attorney is caught between a DA and a mob boss who both want her, and the choice defines—and nearly destroys—her career.

title: Hollywood Wives
author: Jackie Collins
description: The wives of Hollywood's most powerful men scheme, cheat, and claw their way through Beverly Hills in Collins's breakout blockbuster.

title: Lucky
author: Jackie Collins
description: Lucky Santangelo—beautiful, ruthless, and the daughter of a mob boss—builds her own Las Vegas empire and takes on every man who underestimates her.

title: Chances
author: Jackie Collins
description: Gino Santangelo rises from the streets to control Las Vegas, and his daughter Lucky inherits both his empire and his enemies.

title: River God
author: Wilbur Smith
description: A eunuch slave in ancient Egypt narrates the fall of a dynasty and the flight up the Nile as invaders conquer the kingdom.

title: When the Lion Feeds
author: Wilbur Smith
description: Twin brothers in 19th-century South Africa take different paths—one seeks gold, the other becomes a farmer—as the Zulu Wars reshape the land.

title: Birds of Prey
author: Wilbur Smith
description: An English privateer and his son fight the Dutch East India Company off the coast of 17th-century Africa.

title: Shout at the Devil
author: Wilbur Smith
description: An American con man and an Irish big-game hunter wage a private war against a German commissioner in East Africa during WWI.

title: House of Sand and Fog
author: Andre Dubus III
description: A recovering addict loses her house in a tax dispute and an Iranian immigrant buys it, and both will destroy each other to keep it.

title: Townie
author: Andre Dubus III
description: Growing up poor and violent in Massachusetts mill towns with a famous writer father who wasn't around—Dubus's raw memoir of fists and survival.

title: Gone So Long
author: Andre Dubus III
description: A man released from prison for killing his wife forty years ago seeks out the daughter who was three when it happened.

title: Deadwood
author: Pete Dexter
description: Wild Bill Hickok's last days in Deadwood, South Dakota told through the drunks, prostitutes, and killers of the lawless camp.

title: Paris Trout
author: Pete Dexter
description: A merchant in a small Georgia town shoots a Black girl over a debt and the town must decide whether to hold him accountable.

title: The Paperboy
author: Pete Dexter
description: Two Miami journalists investigate a death row case in the Florida swamps and the investigation destroys everyone involved.

title: Train
author: Pete Dexter
description: A Black caddy in 1950s Los Angeles gets entangled with a cop and a rape victim in a noir about race and violence in the city of dreams.

title: Winter in the Blood
author: James Welch
description: A young Blackfeet man on a Montana reservation drifts through bars and memories, searching for his identity in a landscape stripped of meaning.

title: Fools Crow
author: James Welch
description: A Blackfeet warrior in the 1870s rises in his band as the white Napikwans close in and the old way of life faces extinction.

title: The Death of Jim Loney
author: James Welch
description: A half-Indian, half-white man in a Montana town drinks and drifts toward a death he has chosen but cannot explain.

title: The Art of Fielding
author: Chad Harbach
description: A shortstop at a small Wisconsin college suddenly can't throw to first base, and the crisis ripples through five lives connected to the team.

title: City on Fire
author: Garth Risk Hallberg

title: Then We Came to the End
author: Joshua Ferris
description: An advertising agency during the dot-com bust narrated in first-person plural—'we'—as employees are laid off one by one and the collective voice thins.

title: The Unnamed
author: Joshua Ferris
description: A lawyer is afflicted by an uncontrollable compulsion to walk—he simply cannot stop—and the disease destroys his career, his marriage, and his body.

title: To Rise Again at a Decent Hour
author: Joshua Ferris
description: A dentist discovers someone has stolen his identity online and is posting things he secretly agrees with, forcing him to confront his own emptiness.

title: The Ask
author: Sam Lipsyte
description: A failed painter working in a university development office must squeeze a donation from his old college roommate, who has become a billionaire and a monster.

title: Home Land
author: Sam Lipsyte
description: A man writes increasingly unhinged letters to his high school alumni newsletter about his failed life, in Lipsyte's funniest novel.

title: The Fun Parts
author: Sam Lipsyte
description: Stories of American men failing at everything—parenthood, marriage, ambition—with savage wit and surprising tenderness.

title: Baker Towers
author: Jennifer Haigh
description: Five siblings in a 1940s Pennsylvania coal town scatter across America as the mines close and the postwar world offers different fates by gender and birth order.

title: Heat and Light
author: Jennifer Haigh
description: Fracking comes to a Pennsylvania farm town and divides the community between those who lease their mineral rights and those who fight to stop the drilling.

title: Mercy Street
author: Jennifer Haigh
description: A counselor at a women's health clinic in Boston navigates anti-abortion protesters, opioid-addicted patients, and her own complicated past.

title: The Consuming Fire
author: John Scalzi
description: The empress races to prove the Flow is collapsing while political enemies try to use the crisis to seize power.

title: The Last Emperox
author: John Scalzi
description: The Flow collapses and the empire's star systems are cut off from each other, and the empress must outmaneuver a final conspiracy to save civilization.

title: Starter Villain
author: John Scalzi
description: A substitute teacher inherits his uncle's supervillain business, complete with volcano lair, sentient cats, and a war with other villains.

title: Lock In
author: John Scalzi
description: A virus locks millions of people inside their bodies, and they use robot bodies and virtual spaces to participate in society—until someone is murdered.

title: Wayward
author: Blake Crouch
description: The sheriff of Wayward Pines discovers the truth about the town and must decide whether to maintain the lie to protect its residents.

title: The Last Town
author: Blake Crouch
description: The residents of Wayward Pines learn the truth and the town descends into chaos as the creatures outside the fence close in.

title: The Things We Leave Unfinished
author: Rebecca Yarros
description: A romance novelist must finish her grandmother's WWII love story while falling for the ghostwriter her publisher hired to do it.

title: Great and Precious Things
author: Rebecca Yarros
description: A prodigal son returns to a small Colorado mountain town to care for his father with Alzheimer's and confronts the brother he left behind.

title: Pillars of Creation
author: Terry Goodkind
description: A young woman discovered to be ungifted in a world of magic becomes a tool of the Imperial Order against Richard.

title: Naked Empire
author: Terry Goodkind
description: Richard and Kahlan are captured by a pacifist nation that refuses to fight the invaders destroying them.

title: Chainfire
author: Terry Goodkind
description: Richard wakes to find no one remembers Kahlan—she has been erased from existence—and he must find her while everyone thinks he's insane.

title: Phantom
author: Terry Goodkind
description: Kahlan has been taken by the enemy and has no memory of her identity while Richard races to free her before the world ends.

title: Confessor
author: Terry Goodkind
description: The final Sword of Truth novel as Richard confronts the Imperial Order in the last battle for the New World.

title: Guardians of the West
author: David Eddings
description: Years after defeating Torak, Garion is a king raising his son when the Prophecy stirs again and a new quest begins.

title: King of the Murgos
author: David Eddings
description: Garion's quest to rescue his kidnapped son takes him through the Murgo kingdoms and into the deadly southern continent.

title: Demon Lord of Karanda
author: David Eddings
description: The quest continues through demon-infested Karanda as the ultimate confrontation with the Dark Prophecy approaches.

title: Sorceress of Darshiva
author: David Eddings
description: Garion closes in on the new Child of Dark while the opposing Prophecies maneuver their champions into position.

title: The Seeress of Kell
author: David Eddings
description: The final confrontation between the two Prophecies at the Place Which Is No More determines the fate of the universe.

title: Streams of Silver
author: R.A. Salvatore
description: Drizzt and his companions journey to reclaim the dwarven homeland of Mithral Hall from the shadow dragon that conquered it.

title: The Halfling's Gem
author: R.A. Salvatore
description: Drizzt races to Calimport to rescue Regis from the assassin Artemis Entreri in the conclusion of the Icewind Dale Trilogy.

title: The Legacy
author: R.A. Salvatore
description: Drizzt's past catches up as his dark elf family sends a hunting party to Mithral Hall to kill him.

title: Starless Night
author: R.A. Salvatore
description: Drizzt returns to Menzoberranzan alone to end the threat against his friends, walking back into the city that wants him dead.

title: The Privileges
author: Jonathan Dee
description: A golden couple on Park Avenue enriches themselves through insider trading and charm, and the novel refuses to punish them for it.

title: A Thousand Pardons
author: Jonathan Dee
description: A woman discovers a talent for public apology—coaching disgraced men through press conferences—while her husband sits in jail.

title: Leave the World Behind
author: Rumaan Alam
description: A family's vacation rental is interrupted when the homeowners return claiming a blackout has hit the city, and no one knows what's happening outside.

title: That Kind of Mother
author: Rumaan Alam
description: A white poet adopts a Black child and must confront the gap between her good intentions and the reality of transracial motherhood.

title: Intimacies
author: Katie Kitamura
description: An interpreter at The Hague translates for a former president on trial for war crimes and begins an affair with a man whose wife hasn't left.

title: A Separation
author: Katie Kitamura
description: A woman travels to a Greek village to find her estranged husband who has gone missing, and a body turns up.

title: Still Born
author: Guadalupe Nettel
description: Two women—one carrying a terminally ill fetus, the other ambivalent about motherhood—form a friendship that redefines what it means to give life.

title: The Body Where I Was Born
author: Guadalupe Nettel
description: A woman tells her psychoanalyst about her childhood—a cataract that divided her vision, an eccentric mother, a brother who stopped speaking.

title: Solenoid
author: Mircea Cărtărescu
description: A failed poet teaching at a Bucharest school discovers his house is built over a giant solenoid and reality begins to warp in Romania's most ambitious novel.

title: Blinding
author: Mircea Cărtărescu
description: A Bucharest writer's memoir spirals into hallucination as the city, his body, and his mother's past merge into a visionary fever dream.

title: The Discomfort of Evening
author: Marieke Lucas Rijneveld
description: A girl on a Dutch dairy farm prays for her brother to die instead of her rabbit, and when he drowns, the family disintegrates into grief and delusion.

title: My Heavenly Favourite
author: Marieke Lucas Rijneveld
description: A veterinarian's relationship with a teenage girl crosses every boundary as a cow disease sweeps the countryside.

title: Asymmetry
author: Lisa Halliday
description: Two seemingly unconnected stories—a young editor's affair with a famous older writer and an Iraqi-American detained at Heathrow—mirror each other in surprising ways.

title: The River of Doubt
author: Candice Millard
description: Theodore Roosevelt nearly dies exploring an unmapped tributary of the Amazon after losing the 1912 election.

title: Destiny of the Republic
author: Candice Millard
description: President Garfield is shot by a lunatic and killed not by the bullet but by his doctors, who refuse to accept germ theory.

title: Hero of the Empire
author: Candice Millard
description: A young Winston Churchill is captured during the Boer War and escapes in a breakout that launched his political career.

title: In the Heart of the Sea
author: Nathaniel Philbrick
description: The true story of the Essex—the whaling ship sunk by a sperm whale that inspired Moby-Dick—and the crew's horrifying survival in open boats.

title: Mayflower
author: Nathaniel Philbrick
description: The Pilgrims from the Mayflower voyage through King Philip's War—fifty years that transformed cooperation between colonists and Native Americans into genocide.

title: Valiant Ambition
author: Nathaniel Philbrick
description: George Washington and Benedict Arnold—how the Revolution's greatest general and its greatest traitor were shaped by the same war.

title: American Lion
author: Jon Meacham
description: Andrew Jackson in the White House—the first populist president who expanded democracy, defied the Supreme Court, and almost started a civil war over nullification.

title: Thomas Jefferson: The Art of Power
author: Jon Meacham
description: Jefferson as a political operator—not just the philosopher of liberty but the ruthless tactician who knew how to get and keep power.

title: His Truth Is Marching On
author: Jon Meacham
description: John Lewis's life from the Alabama cotton fields through the Freedom Rides to Congress, as told through his friendship with Meacham.

title: Relic
author: Douglas Preston
description: Something is killing people in the basement of the New York Museum of Natural History, and a museum researcher and an FBI agent hunt it through the tunnels.

title: The Cabinet of Curiosities
author: Douglas Preston
description: A 19th-century serial killer's lair is discovered during construction in Lower Manhattan and the murders resume in the present.

title: Still Life with Crows
author: Douglas Preston
description: Agent Pendergast investigates bizarre murders in a Kansas corn town where the victims are arranged in elaborate tableaux.

title: Before the Frost
author: Henning Mankell
description: Wallander's daughter Linda joins the Ystad police and investigates a case involving religious fanaticism and a decades-old disappearance.

title: August Heat
author: Andrea Camilleri
description: Montalbano discovers a hidden room while renovating a house and the room contains a dead body and a living girl.

title: The Track of Sand
author: Andrea Camilleri
description: A dead horse appears on the beach outside Montalbano's house, and the investigation leads to horse racing fraud and murder.

title: An Uncertain Place
author: Fred Vargas
description: Severed feet in shoes are found outside Highgate Cemetery in London, and Adamsberg connects them to a Serbian vampire legend.

title: Earthly Remains
author: Donna Leon
description: Brunetti takes a break on a lagoon island and befriends a rowing guide who turns up dead, possibly connected to pollution in the lagoon.

title: Transient Desires
author: Donna Leon
description: Two American tourists are seriously injured in a boating accident and Brunetti investigates the speedboat culture that endangers Venice's waters.

title: Sandstorm
author: James Rollins
description: Sigma Force investigates the lost city of Ubar in the Arabian desert while a curator is murdered over a meteorite with impossible properties.

title: Map of Bones
author: James Rollins
description: A massacre in a Cologne cathedral leads Sigma Force across Europe following clues left by the Magi and connected to a doomsday weapon.

title: Black Order
author: James Rollins
description: Sigma Force investigates a Nazi experiment in quantum evolution that has been revived in a Himalayan monastery.

title: The Doomsday Key
author: James Rollins
description: A threat to the world's food supply connects Viking history, genetic engineering, and a Vatican conspiracy.

title: The Fallen
author: David Baldacci
description: Amos Decker investigates a series of murders in a dying Pennsylvania coal town where every victim seems unconnected.

title: Redemption
author: David Baldacci
description: Decker's very first case comes back to haunt him when a convicted killer appears and claims he didn't commit the murders.

title: A Gambling Man
author: David Baldacci
description: Aloysius Archer arrives in 1950s California to work as a PI and immediately stumbles onto corruption and murder.

title: One Good Deed
author: David Baldacci
description: A WWII veteran just out of prison becomes entangled in a murder case in a small 1949 Southern town where everyone has something to hide.

title: Wild
author: Cheryl Strayed
description: A woman with no hiking experience walks 1,100 miles of the Pacific Crest Trail alone to recover from her mother's death, a divorce, and heroin.

title: Tiny Beautiful Things
author: Cheryl Strayed
description: Strayed's collected Dear Sugar advice columns—raw, compassionate, and profane—on love, loss, sex, and how to live.

title: Packing for Mars
author: Mary Roach
description: The human body in space: how astronauts eat, sleep, go to the bathroom, and have sex (or don't) in zero gravity.

title: Into the Uncut Grass
author: Trevor Noah
description: A boy who refuses to cut the lawn escapes into a magical world inside the tall grass, in Noah's first picture-book-for-adults fable.

title: I'm Glad My Mom Died
author: Jennette McCurdy
description: A former child star recounts her mother's abuse, her eating disorders, and her recovery in the memoir that shocked Hollywood.

title: It's a Long Story
author: Willie Nelson
description: Willie tells his own story—from Abbott, Texas to Nashville rejection to outlaw country legend—in the voice of a man who has seen everything.

title: Life
author: Keith Richards
description: The Rolling Stones guitarist's autobiography—drugs, riffs, Mick, and the miracle of surviving sixty years of rock and roll.

title: Born to Run
author: Bruce Springsteen
description: Springsteen's memoir of growing up in New Jersey, forming the E Street Band, battling depression, and the music that saved him.

title: Chronicles: Volume One
author: Bob Dylan
description: Dylan's memoir of arriving in New York, his early folk years, and his creative rebirths—elliptical, poetic, and deliberately unreliable.

title: Saga: Volume One
author: Brian K. Vaughan
description: Two soldiers from opposite sides of a galactic war fall in love, have a baby, and are hunted by everyone in the most acclaimed comic of the 2010s.

title: Y: The Last Man Volume 1
author: Brian K. Vaughan
description: Every male mammal on Earth dies simultaneously except one man and his monkey, and he must survive a world of grieving, scheming women.

title: Blankets
author: Craig Thompson
description: A boy raised in a strict Christian household falls in love during a Wisconsin winter and must reconcile faith, desire, and art in a stunning graphic memoir.

title: Shortcomings
author: Adrian Tomine
description: A Japanese-American man in Berkeley sabotages his relationship while obsessing over white women in Tomine's sharpest examination of identity and desire.

title: Jimmy Corrigan: The Smartest Kid on Earth
author: Chris Ware
description: A lonely middle-aged man meets the father who abandoned him as a child, and four generations of male failure are rendered in impossibly intricate panels.

title: Building Stories
author: Chris Ware
description: A box containing fourteen different printed pieces—books, newspapers, boards—that can be read in any order, telling the story of a woman in a Chicago apartment building.

title: The Talisman
author: Stephen King
description: A boy travels across America and a parallel world called the Territories to find a talisman that can save his dying mother. Co-written with Peter Straub.

title: Black House
author: Stephen King
description: The sequel to The Talisman: a retired detective in Wisconsin investigates child murders connected to the Dark Tower and the Territories.

title: The Last Hero
author: Terry Pratchett
description: Cohen the Barbarian leads a band of aged heroes to return fire to the gods—by blowing up their mountain—and a wizard must stop them to save the Disc.

title: Murder Is Easy
author: Agatha Christie
description: An old woman tells a stranger on a train that a series of deaths in her village are murders—and is killed before she can reach the police.

title: Sparkling Cyanide
author: Agatha Christie
description: A woman who died at a dinner party a year ago—supposedly suicide—is echoed when another dinner is arranged at the same table with the same guests.

title: Siracusa
author: Delia Ephron
description: Two couples vacation together in Sicily and their marriages unravel as an unsettling child observes everything.

title: Open House
author: Elizabeth Berg
description: A newly divorced woman takes in boarders and rebuilds her life through small connections and the courage to start over.

title: The Story of Arthur Truluv
author: Elizabeth Berg
description: An elderly widower who visits his wife's grave daily befriends a pregnant teenager and a reclusive neighbor in a quiet novel about found family.

title: She's Come Undone
author: Wally Lamb
description: A woman narrates her life from an obese, depressed teenager through therapy and self-destruction to hard-won stability.

title: I Know This Much Is True
author: Wally Lamb
description: A house painter struggles to free his paranoid schizophrenic twin brother from a maximum-security asylum while confronting their family's buried Italian immigrant history.

title: The Lovely Bones
author: Alice Sebold
description: A murdered fourteen-year-old girl narrates from heaven as her family grieves and her killer lives next door.

title: Still Alice
author: Lisa Genova
description: A Harvard psychology professor is diagnosed with early-onset Alzheimer's and narrates her own cognitive decline with devastating clarity.

title: Every Note Played
author: Lisa Genova
description: A concert pianist with ALS must rely on his ex-wife for care as his body fails and their old grievances surface.

title: The Book of Lost Names
author: Kristin Harmel
description: A young woman in occupied France forges identity documents to save Jewish children, encoding their real names in a book of maps.

title: The Forest of Vanishing Stars
author: Kristin Harmel
description: A woman raised in the wilderness by a kidnapper uses her survival skills to save a group of Jews hiding in a Polish forest during WWII.

title: The Orphan's Tale
author: Pam Jenoff
description: A Jewish woman hiding as a circus performer during WWII and the German teenager who joins the act form a bond that is tested by betrayal and survival.

title: The Lost Girls of Paris
author: Pam Jenoff
description: A woman finds a suitcase of photographs in Grand Central Station and discovers the story of female spies sent into occupied France.

title: The Woman with the Blue Star
author: Pam Jenoff
description: A Jewish girl hiding in the sewers beneath Kraków and a Polish girl who discovers her become connected across an impossible divide.

title: Before We Were Yours
author: Lisa Wingate
description: Based on a real Tennessee orphanage that kidnapped poor children and sold them to wealthy families, told through a girl in the 1930s and a woman in the present.

title: The Book of Lost Friends
author: Lisa Wingate
description: A teacher in Louisiana discovers the Lost Friends column—ads placed by freed slaves searching for family—and connects them to her own community.

title: Range
author: David Epstein
description: Generalists triumph over specialists in most fields—Epstein argues against early specialization using Roger Federer, Darwin, and Duke Ellington.

title: The Sports Gene
author: David Epstein
description: Nature vs. nurture in athletics: why Jamaicans sprint, Kenyans run distance, and the 10,000-hour rule is more complicated than Gladwell said.

title: Breath
author: James Nestor
description: How modern humans have lost the ability to breathe correctly and the science of what happens when you relearn—through your nose, slowly, deeply.

title: Outlive
author: Peter Attia
description: A physician argues that medicine focuses on treating disease instead of preventing it, and lays out a science-based strategy for living longer and better.

title: The Extended Mind
author: Annie Murphy Paul
description: Thinking doesn't happen only in the brain—we think with our bodies, our spaces, and other people, and understanding this makes us smarter.

title: Everything Is F*cked
author: Mark Manson
description: A follow-up about hope: why we need it, why it keeps failing us, and what Nietzsche, Buddhism, and Kant can teach us about meaning.

title: Beyond Order
author: Jordan Peterson
description: Twelve more rules for life, focused on the dangers of too much order and the need to venture into chaos to grow.

title: Heartbreaker
author: Sarah MacLean
description: A duchess and a newspaper magnate face off over her reputation and his empire in MacLean's latest Regency.

title: Bombshell
author: Sarah MacLean
description: A society spinster who secretly runs the gossip sheets and a duke's heir who needs her silence find that blackmail makes a terrible foundation for attraction.

title: This Could Be Us
author: Kennedy Ryan
description: A single mother and her ex-husband's best friend have been fighting their attraction for years, and a crisis brings them together.

title: Reel
author: Kennedy Ryan
description: A filmmaker and an actress navigate Hollywood, race, and ambition while falling for each other on set.

title: The Soulmate Equation
author: Christina Lauren
description: A single mom skeptical of a DNA-based matchmaking company discovers she's a 98% match with its annoyingly handsome founder.

title: Josh and Hazel's Guide to Not Dating
author: Christina Lauren
description: Two friends set each other up on double dates that keep going wrong, because the real match is each other.

title: Hopeless
author: Elsie Silver
description: A former bull rider turned rancher and a woman on the run find each other in a small town where everyone minds everyone's business.

title: Wild Love
author: Elsie Silver
description: A music mogul returns to her hometown and clashes with the single dad running her family's lakeside resort.

title: The Angel of Indian Lake
author: Stephen Graham Jones
description: The third Indian Lake novel: Jade returns for the final confrontation with the slasher cycle that has haunted her town.

title: Horrorstör
author: Grady Hendrix
description: Employees at a furniture store modeled on IKEA work the night shift and discover the building was constructed on the site of a prison that used torture as therapy.

title: We Sold Our Souls
author: Grady Hendrix
description: A former metal guitarist discovers her old bandmate sold their souls to a demon for fame, and she must reunite the band to fight back.

title: Nettle & Bone
author: T. Kingfisher
description: A shy princess must build a dog from bones, capture moonlight in a jar, and sew a cloak of nettles to save her sister from a murderous prince.

title: Red Widow
author: Alma Katsu
description: Two CIA women—one a spy handler, one an analyst—discover a mole in the agency while navigating the boys' club of intelligence.

title: City of Ghosts
author: Victoria Schwab
description: A girl who can see ghosts travels to Edinburgh with her ghost-hunting parents and must cross the Veil to save her best friend—who is already dead.

title: Tunnel of Bones
author: Victoria Schwab
description: Cassidy and her ghost best friend explore the Paris catacombs where something ancient and powerful has been waiting.

title: Bridge of Souls
author: Victoria Schwab
description: Cassidy visits New Orleans where voodoo and haunted history make the Veil between living and dead dangerously thin.

title: Heartless
author: Marissa Meyer
description: The origin story of the Queen of Hearts—a girl who dreams of opening a bakery but is destined to become the Red Queen of Wonderland.

title: Restore Me
author: Tahereh Mafi
description: Juliette is now the leader of Sector 45 but discovers that everything she thought she knew about her abilities and the Reestablishment was wrong.

title: Defy Me
author: Tahereh Mafi
description: Juliette's world shatters when she learns the truth about her parents and the experiments that created her.

title: Imagine Me
author: Tahereh Mafi
description: The final battle against the Reestablishment as Juliette must embrace who she really is to save everyone she loves.

title: Lady Midnight
author: Cassandra Clare
description: A Shadowhunter in the Los Angeles Institute investigates murders connected to the Wild Hunt and a faerie conspiracy.

title: Lord of Shadows
author: Cassandra Clare
description: The Shadowhunters face political upheaval as the faerie courts maneuver for war and Emma and Julian's forbidden love threatens to destroy them.

title: Queen of Air and Darkness
author: Cassandra Clare
description: The Dark Artifices trilogy concludes with the faerie queen's assault on the Shadowhunters and sacrifices that reshape the shadow world.

title: Once and for All
author: Sarah Dessen
description: A girl who works for her mother's wedding planning business doesn't believe in love after a tragedy, until a charming boy challenges her cynicism.

title: Lock and Key
author: Sarah Dessen
description: A girl whose mother has abandoned her is taken in by the older sister she hasn't seen in years and must learn to trust again.

title: Dreamland
author: Sarah Dessen
description: A girl falls for a boy whose charm masks an escalating pattern of abuse in Dessen's darkest and most important novel.

title: This Lullaby
author: Sarah Dessen
description: A girl who doesn't believe in love because her songwriter father abandoned her meets a boy who refuses to be pushed away.

title: The Good Guy
author: Dean Koontz
description: A bricklayer sitting in a bar is mistaken for a hitman and given a target's photo, and must protect the woman while fleeing the real killer.

title: Velocity
author: Dean Koontz
description: A bartender finds a note on his car: someone will die, and he must choose who. Then it happens again. And again.

title: Flowers in the Attic
author: V.C. Andrews
description: Four siblings are hidden in their grandmother's attic for years by a mother seeking an inheritance, and the confinement breeds incest and murder.

title: Petals on the Wind
author: V.C. Andrews
description: The surviving children escape the attic and grow up, but the damage follows them and Cathy plots revenge against their mother.

title: If There Be Thorns
author: V.C. Andrews
description: Cathy's sons discover the family secret and the younger one is corrupted by the grandmother's twisted religion.

title: My Sweet Audrina
author: V.C. Andrews
description: A girl with no memory of certain days discovers her family has been manipulating her perception of reality her entire life.

title: A Dog's Purpose
author: W. Bruce Cameron
description: A dog is reincarnated through four lives and four owners, each time learning more about what it means to be a good dog.

title: A Dog's Journey
author: W. Bruce Cameron
description: The dog returns in a new life to watch over his original owner's granddaughter through her tumultuous adolescence.

title: A Dog's Way Home
author: W. Bruce Cameron
description: A dog separated from her owner travels 400 miles through the Colorado wilderness to find her way back.

title: Big Stone Gap
author: Adriana Trigiani
description: A self-proclaimed spinster in a Virginia mountain town discovers a family secret that changes her identity and opens her to love.

title: The Shoemaker's Wife
author: Adriana Trigiani
description: Two Italian immigrants who met as children in the Alps are separated and reunited across decades in early 20th-century New York.

title: The Secret Between Us
author: Barbara Delinsky
description: A mother covers up the truth about a car accident to protect her daughter, and the lie metastasizes through their small town.

title: Blueprints
author: Barbara Delinsky
description: A mother and daughter who co-host a home renovation show must rebuild their own relationship when secrets surface.

title: The Probable Future
author: Alice Hoffman
description: Women in a Massachusetts family each develop a magical gift on their thirteenth birthday, and a girl who can see how people will die must save her father.

title: Magic Lessons
author: Alice Hoffman
description: The origin of the Owens family curse: a woman in 1600s Salem discovers her power and the price it exacts on everyone she loves.

title: The Pact
author: Jodi Picoult
description: Two families are shattered when their teenage children are found in a suicide pact—but only the girl died, and her boyfriend is charged with murder.

title: Handle with Care
author: Jodi Picoult
description: A mother sues her obstetrician for wrongful birth to pay for her daughter's brittle bone disease treatment—meaning she must argue her daughter should never have been born.

title: Leaving Time
author: Jodi Picoult
description: A girl searching for her missing mother—an elephant researcher who vanished after a death at the sanctuary—hires a psychic and a private detective.

title: Can't Wait to Get to Heaven
author: Fannie Flagg
description: An elderly woman falls out of a fig tree, dies briefly, visits heaven, and returns to find her small Missouri town in an uproar.

title: The All-Girl Filling Station's Last Reunion
author: Fannie Flagg
description: A woman discovers she was adopted and her real mother was one of the WASPs—female pilots who flew military planes in WWII.

title: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People
author: Stephen Covey
description: The foundational self-help book: be proactive, begin with the end in mind, put first things first, and four more principles that shaped a generation of management.

title: How to Win Friends and Influence People
author: Dale Carnegie
description: The original self-improvement classic: smile, remember names, be a good listener, and make the other person feel important.

title: Rich Dad Poor Dad
author: Robert Kiyosaki
description: Two fathers—one educated and poor, one uneducated and rich—teach different lessons about money, assets, and financial independence.

title: The Power of Habit
author: Charles Duhigg
description: Why we do what we do and how to change it—the science of habit loops in individuals, organizations, and societies.

title: Supercommunicators
author: Charles Duhigg
description: The science of conversation: why some people connect effortlessly and how matching the type of conversation transforms relationships.

title: Mindset
author: Carol Dweck
description: Fixed vs. growth mindset: how believing your abilities can develop through effort changes everything from parenting to business to sports.

title: Drive
author: Daniel Pink
description: What motivates us is not money but autonomy, mastery, and purpose—and the carrot-and-stick approach actually kills performance.

title: When
author: Daniel Pink
description: The science of timing: when to start a project, take a nap, get married, or quit your job based on chronobiology and psychology.

title: Quiet
author: Susan Cain
description: A third of people are introverts, and Cain argues that society's bias toward extroversion wastes their creativity, empathy, and leadership.

title: Emotional Intelligence
author: Daniel Goleman
description: EQ matters more than IQ: self-awareness, empathy, and social skills predict success better than raw intelligence.

title: Flow
author: Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi
description: The psychology of optimal experience: when challenge matches skill, you enter a state of flow where time disappears and performance peaks.

title: Arnhem
author: Antony Beevor
description: Operation Market Garden—the largest airborne assault in history—and why the bold plan to end WWII in 1944 failed catastrophically.

title: The Second World War
author: Antony Beevor
description: The entire war from China to Stalingrad to the Pacific in a single volume, connecting every theater into one narrative.

title: Russka
author: Edward Rutherfurd
description: Russia from the first Slavic settlements through the Soviet era, told through families that witness every upheaval.

title: The Forest
author: Edward Rutherfurd
description: The New Forest from William the Conqueror to the 20th century through families connected to England's ancient woodland.

title: New York
author: Edward Rutherfurd
description: Manhattan from a Dutch fur trader in 1664 through 9/11, spanning four centuries of the city's families.

title: Falls the Shadow
author: Sharon Kay Penman
description: Simon de Montfort leads the barons' rebellion against Henry III in the sequel to Here Be Dragons.

title: The Reckoning
author: Sharon Kay Penman
description: Edward I conquers Wales and the last native prince, Llywelyn ap Gruffudd, fights a doomed war for independence.

title: The Steel Wave
author: Jeff Shaara
description: D-Day through the liberation of Paris as Eisenhower, Rommel, and the soldiers on the beaches experience the invasion.

title: No Less Than Victory
author: Jeff Shaara
description: The Battle of the Bulge through V-E Day as the Allies push into Germany and discover the concentration camps.

title: To the Last Man
author: Jeff Shaara
description: WWI from the trenches through Belleau Wood, told through soldiers from both sides—American, British, German.

title: The Man from St. Petersburg
author: Ken Follett
description: A Russian anarchist comes to London in 1914 to assassinate a prince negotiating Britain's alliance with Russia, and the woman hiding him has secrets of her own.

title: Jackdaws
author: Ken Follett
description: A female SOE agent assembles an all-woman team to sabotage a Nazi telephone exchange in occupied France before D-Day.

title: Triple
author: Ken Follett
description: An Israeli agent steals uranium from a European nuclear plant to build Israel's bomb, based on a real theft.

title: Emperor: The Gates of Rome
author: Conn Iggulden
description: Young Julius Caesar and Marcus Brutus grow up together on a Roman estate, and their friendship is tested as Rome's republic begins to crumble.

title: Emperor: The Death of Kings
author: Conn Iggulden
description: Caesar is captured by pirates and Brutus rises through the legions as Rome faces threats from every direction.

title: Sharpe's Tiger
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Private Sharpe volunteers for a suicide mission in India to escape a flogging and ends up infiltrating the Tiger of Mysore's palace.

title: Sharpe's Rifles
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Sharpe leads a band of riflemen through the Galician mountains during the retreat to Corunna and earns his commission.

title: Sharpe's Waterloo
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: The battle that ended Napoleon, told through Sharpe in the thick of the fighting at Hougoumont and La Haye Sainte.

title: The Bicentennial Man
author: Isaac Asimov
description: A robot who develops creativity and emotions petitions over two hundred years to be declared legally human.

title: Fantastic Voyage
author: Isaac Asimov
description: A submarine and its crew are miniaturized and injected into a dying scientist's body to destroy a blood clot in his brain.

title: The White Plague
author: Frank Herbert
description: A molecular biologist whose family is killed by an IRA bomb creates a plague that kills only women and releases it on the world.

title: The Food of the Gods
author: H.G. Wells
description: Scientists create a substance that makes everything grow enormous—babies, wasps, chickens—and the giant children threaten to overthrow normal humanity.

title: When the Sleeper Wakes
author: H.G. Wells
description: A man wakes after two hundred years of sleep to find he owns half the world and a revolution has been launched in his name.

title: Now Wait for Last Year
author: Philip K. Dick
description: A doctor's wife becomes addicted to a drug that sends her through time while Earth is trapped in an alien war it cannot win.

title: The Transmigration of Timothy Archer
author: Philip K. Dick
description: A bishop's daughter-in-law narrates his obsessive quest for Dead Sea Scrolls that prove Jesus used a hallucinogenic mushroom, in Dick's last and most realistic novel.

title: 3001: The Final Odyssey
author: Arthur C. Clarke
description: Frank Poole is revived a thousand years after being killed by HAL and discovers humanity has colonized the solar system—but the monolith has a final message.

title: Farnham's Freehold
author: Robert A. Heinlein
description: A family survives a nuclear attack in their bomb shelter and emerges in a future where the racial power structure has been inverted.

title: The Cat Who Walks Through Walls
author: Robert A. Heinlein
description: A writer is drawn into a conspiracy involving time travel, parallel universes, and all of Heinlein's previous characters.

title: Spook Country
author: William Gibson
description: A journalist, an addict, and a Cuban-Chinese intelligence agent converge on a shipping container whose contents could change everything.

title: Zero History
author: William Gibson
description: A former rock star and an ex-addict are hired by a marketing mogul to find the designer of a secret brand of jeans.

title: Life Before Man
author: Margaret Atwood
description: A love triangle in Toronto between a museum worker, her husband, and her dead lover's colleague, set against the dinosaur exhibits that mirror their extinctions.

title: The Edible Woman
author: Margaret Atwood
