description: A young woman engaged to a boring lawyer gradually loses the ability to eat as her body rebels against the consumption her fiancé represents.

title: Lady Oracle
author: Margaret Atwood
description: A woman who writes Gothic romances under a pseudonym fakes her own death and hides in Italy when her secret identity is about to be exposed.

title: Bodily Harm
author: Margaret Atwood
description: A journalist recovering from a mastectomy travels to a Caribbean island and stumbles into a political coup.

title: The Penelopiad
author: Margaret Atwood
description: Penelope tells her side of the Odyssey from the afterlife—waiting for Odysseus while the suitors ate her food—and her twelve hanged maids demand justice.

title: The Choice
author: Nicholas Sparks
description: A veterinarian and his new neighbor fall in love, and years later he faces a devastating choice about keeping his wife on life support.

title: Nights in Rodanthe
author: Nicholas Sparks
description: A woman running a friend's inn and a doctor escaping his mistakes meet during a storm on the Outer Banks.

title: The Return
author: Nicholas Sparks
description: A Navy doctor recovering from injuries takes up beekeeping in a small town and falls for a deputy sheriff investigating a series of fires.

title: Every Breath
author: Nicholas Sparks
description: A woman at a crossroads meets a man from Zimbabwe on a North Carolina beach and their connection spans years and oceans.

title: Hot Six
author: Janet Evanovich
description: Stephanie's mentor Ranger is wanted for murder and she must find him while dodging a homicidal arms dealer and her own grandmother.

title: Seven Up
author: Janet Evanovich
description: Stephanie hunts an elderly mobster who keeps escaping, Grandma Mazur moves in, and a kidnapper targets Stephanie's boyfriend.

title: The Wedding
author: Danielle Steel
description: A Hollywood producer plans his daughter's wedding while his own marriage crumbles and his mother hides a wartime secret.

title: Impossible
author: Danielle Steel
description: A widowed art dealer falls for a younger artist and must navigate the disapproval of her adult children and her own doubts.

title: Worthy Opponents
author: Danielle Steel
description: A woman running a legendary department store fights a corporate raider who wants to buy it—and falls for him in the process.

title: Rules of Prey
author: John Sandford
description: A Minneapolis detective hunts a serial killer who leaves notes with rules for the perfect murder at each crime scene.

title: Shadow Prey
author: John Sandford
description: A pair of Native American assassins target politicians in Minneapolis and Davenport must track them through both white and Indian worlds.

title: Eyes of Prey
author: John Sandford
description: A psychopathic ophthalmologist and a deranged actor team up for a murder, and Davenport investigates while battling his own depression.

title: Silent Prey
author: John Sandford
description: Davenport goes to New York to hunt a killer he let escape in Minneapolis, now murdering homeless men in Manhattan.

title: Winter Prey
author: John Sandford
description: A killer in a frozen Wisconsin town is eliminating everyone who knows about a child pornography ring, and Davenport faces the cold.

title: Phantom Prey
author: John Sandford
description: A young woman vanishes from a locked house and Davenport follows a trail through Minneapolis's Goth subculture.

title: Innocent
author: Scott Turow
description: Twenty years after Presumed Innocent, Rusty Sabich is again suspected of murdering a woman—this time his wife.

title: The Last Trial
author: Scott Turow
description: An eighty-five-year-old defense attorney takes one final case: a friend accused of fraud and murder involving a cancer drug.

title: Prisoner's Dilemma
author: Richard Powers
description: A dying father tells his family stories about WWII and Disney's propaganda films while his children try to understand him before it's too late.

title: The Testament of Mary
author: Colm Tóibín
description: An aging Mary, mother of Jesus, tells her version of events: she never wanted her son to die and the disciples who write the gospels are liars.

title: The Heather Blazing
author: Colm Tóibín
description: An Irish high court judge spends a summer at the coast as his wife dies and memories of his childhood under De Valera's Ireland surface.

title: Abide with Me
author: Elizabeth Strout
description: A minister in a small Maine town tries to care for his traumatized daughter after his wife's death while his congregation turns against him.

title: Tell Me Everything
author: Elizabeth Strout
description: Lucy Barton and Bob Burgess share stories in this latest interweaving of Strout's Crosby, Maine universe.

title: The Patron Saint of Liars
author: Ann Patchett
description: A pregnant woman leaves her husband and checks into a home for unwed mothers in Kentucky, where she reinvents herself.

title: The Magician's Assistant
author: Ann Patchett
description: A magician's assistant discovers after his death that her partner had a family in Nebraska he never mentioned, and she goes to find them.

title: The Running Grave
author: Robert Galbraith
description: Robin goes undercover inside a cult to rescue a young woman, and the investigation becomes the most dangerous of Strike's career.

title: The Ink Black Heart
author: Robert Galbraith
description: The creator of a popular cartoon is murdered and Strike must navigate the toxic world of online fandom and trolling to find the killer.

title: Salvation of a Saint
author: Keigo Higashino
description: A woman poisons her husband with a method so impossible that Detective Galileo must solve a locked-room mystery of chemistry and time.

title: A Midsummer's Equation
author: Keigo Higashino
description: A physicist on vacation helps a boy with homework while a body is found on the beach and an old case resurfaces.

title: "G" Is for Gumshoe
author: Sue Grafton
description: Kinsey is targeted by a hitman while investigating the disappearance of an elderly woman from a desert commune.

title: "H" Is for Homicide
author: Sue Grafton
description: Kinsey goes undercover inside an insurance fraud ring run by a charismatic criminal and nearly loses herself in the role.

title: "K" Is for Killer
author: Sue Grafton
description: A mother hires Kinsey to investigate her daughter's unsolved murder ten months after the police gave up.

title: "O" Is for Outlaw
author: Sue Grafton
description: Kinsey discovers new evidence about her first ex-husband that makes her question their entire marriage and his possible involvement in a murder.

title: Tears of the Giraffe
author: Alexander McCall Smith
description: Mma Ramotswe searches for an American boy who disappeared in the Kalahari years ago while adjusting to her engagement.

title: The Kalahari Typing School for Men
author: Alexander McCall Smith
description: A rival detective agency opens in Gaborone and Mma Ramotswe must prove the No. 1 agency's worth while a man seeks typing lessons as a cover for his real need.

title: Moonflower Murders
author: Anthony Horowitz
description: The sequel to Magpie Murders: a hotel guest disappears after reading the same mystery novel, and Susan must decode a new hidden crime.

title: A Line to Kill
author: Anthony Horowitz
description: Horowitz and Hawthorne attend a literary festival on Alderney and a real murder interrupts the fiction.

title: The Twist of a Knife
author: Anthony Horowitz
description: A theater critic who savaged Horowitz's play is murdered with a knife from the prop table and Horowitz is arrested.

title: Close to Death
author: Anthony Horowitz
description: A hated homeowner in a gated community is killed and all eight neighbors had motive in the latest Hawthorne and Horowitz mystery.

title: The Bellmaker
author: Brian Jacques
description: Joseph the Bellmaker sails south with the crew of the Pearl Queen to rescue his daughter Mariel from a tyrannical fox.

title: Outcast of Redwall
author: Brian Jacques
description: A ferret raised by badgers at Redwall must prove whether nature or nurture determines a creature's destiny.

title: The Long Patrol
author: Brian Jacques
description: The Long Patrol of hares marches to Redwall's defense against a Rapscallion army led by the weasel Damug Warfang.

title: Lord Brocktree
author: Brian Jacques
description: The legendary badger lord assembles an army to liberate the mountain fortress of Salamandastron from a wildcat tyrant.

title: Magic's Promise
author: Mercedes Lackey
description: Vanyel, exhausted from border wars, returns home to find his family still can't accept him and a mystery involving dead children and dark magic.

title: Magic's Price
author: Mercedes Lackey
description: Vanyel faces his final battle against the dark mage Leareth and must make the ultimate sacrifice to save Valdemar.

title: By the Sword
author: Mercedes Lackey
description: A noblewoman becomes a mercenary swordswoman and eventually a Herald of Valdemar, connecting the Vows and Honor series to the Heralds.

title: Flag in Exile
author: David Weber
description: Honor is exiled to Grayson where she must defend her adopted planet against both political enemies and a Havenite invasion.

title: Honor Among Enemies
author: David Weber
description: Honor commands a squadron of armed merchant ships against pirates while her political enemies hope she'll be killed.

title: In Enemy Hands
author: David Weber
description: Honor is captured by the Peeps and sentenced to execution, and must escape from a prison planet.

title: Ashes of Victory
author: David Weber
description: Honor returns from the dead to lead Manticore's forces as the war with Haven reaches a turning point.

title: Deerskin
author: Robin McKinley
description: A princess flees her father's incestuous assault and rebuilds herself with the help of a dog and her own growing strength, retelling Donkeyskin.

title: Chalice
author: Robin McKinley
description: A beekeeper becomes the Chalice—the land's second most important officeholder—and must heal a Master who was transformed by fire priests.

title: Pegasus
author: Robin McKinley
description: A princess bonds with a pegasus in a way that hasn't happened in a thousand years, and their connection threatens the political order.

title: Empire of Ivory
author: Naomi Novik
description: A plague strikes Britain's dragons and Laurence and Temeraire must find a cure in Africa while the Napoleonic Wars rage on.

title: Victory of Eagles
author: Naomi Novik
description: Napoleon invades Britain and Temeraire leads a feral dragon resistance while Laurence faces execution for treason.

title: Tongues of Serpents
author: Naomi Novik
description: Laurence and Temeraire are transported to the Australian penal colony with dragon eggs that hatch on the voyage.

title: Port of Shadows
author: Glen Cook
description: Set between the first two novels, Croaker and the Company face a mysterious sorceress while Lady's past threatens to surface.

title: Full Throttle
author: Joe Hill
description: Thirteen stories including two co-written with his father Stephen King—haunted roads, sentient trees, and a woman who discovers she can fly.

title: The Black Phone
author: Joe Hill
description: Stories including the title piece about a kidnapped boy and a disconnected phone in the basement that rings with calls from previous victims.

title: Memnoch the Devil
author: Anne Rice
description: The Devil himself takes Lestat on a tour of Heaven and Hell and asks him to judge whether God is just.

title: The Vampire Armand
author: Anne Rice
description: Armand tells his story: a Renaissance boy captured by a vampire coven in Venice and shaped into a creature of the night.

title: Hangsaman
author: Shirley Jackson
description: A college girl's grip on reality loosens as she navigates a hostile campus and an imaginary—or perhaps real—friend who leads her into the woods.

title: The Bird's Nest
author: Shirley Jackson
description: A quiet young woman develops multiple personalities and her psychiatrist tries to integrate them, but which personality is the real one?

title: Life Among the Savages
author: Shirley Jackson
description: Jackson's hilarious domestic memoir of raising four children in Vermont—the same woman who wrote The Lottery cooking dinner and herding kids.

title: Thunderstruck
author: Erik Larson
description: The invention of wireless telegraphy and a London murder converge when Marconi's technology helps catch Dr. Crippen fleeing across the Atlantic.

title: Notes from a Small Island
author: Bill Bryson
description: Bryson's farewell tour of Britain before moving back to America—riding trains, visiting towns, and marveling at British eccentricity.

title: At Home
author: Bill Bryson
description: A room-by-room history of domestic life—why forks have four tines, why houses have hallways, and how Thomas Edison electrocuted an elephant.

title: One Summer: America, 1927
author: Bill Bryson
description: Lindbergh flies the Atlantic, Babe Ruth hits sixty homers, and the Mississippi floods—all in the summer that made modern America.

title: Liar's Poker
author: Michael Lewis
description: Lewis's memoir of working at Salomon Brothers in the 1980s—the bond trading floor, the insanity, and how Wall Street ran on greed and testosterone.

title: The Undoing Project
author: Michael Lewis
description: The friendship between Kahneman and Tversky—two Israeli psychologists who invented behavioral economics and changed how we understand human judgment.

title: Going Infinite
author: Michael Lewis
description: Lewis embeds with Sam Bankman-Fried during the rise and spectacular collapse of FTX, the crypto exchange.

title: Benjamin Franklin
author: Walter Isaacson
description: The printer, scientist, diplomat, and founding father who was the most American of Americans—practical, curious, and endlessly self-improving.

title: The Innovators
author: Walter Isaacson
description: From Ada Lovelace to Google: the collaborative geniuses who created the computer and the internet.

title: The Wager
author: David Grann
description: An 18th-century British warship is wrecked on a desolate island and the survivors split into factions, each telling a different story of what happened when they return.

title: Triptych
author: Karin Slaughter
description: Will Trent's first case: a serial killer is copying the methods of a man already in prison, and the investigation reveals Will's own orphanage past.

title: Fractured
author: Karin Slaughter
description: A home invasion in an Atlanta mansion exposes secrets between the victim's family, and Will and Sara work together for the first time.

title: Undone
author: Karin Slaughter
description: A woman is found brutalized in the woods and the investigation connects Will Trent to Sara Linton as their cases merge.

title: Criminal
author: Karin Slaughter
description: A 1975 case and a present-day murder connect as Will discovers the truth about his parents and the crime that created him.

title: Survive the Night
author: Riley Sager
description: A college student gets a ride home with a stranger who might be the serial killer terrorizing campus, and she can't tell what's real and what's her movie-obsessed imagination.

title: The Only One Left
author: Riley Sager
description: A caretaker is hired to tend an elderly woman in a decaying mansion who was accused of murdering her family decades ago and wants to tell her story.

title: Middle of the Night
author: Riley Sager
description: A man returns to his childhood home next door to where his best friend vanished from a tent in the backyard thirty years ago.

title: Dead Man's Footsteps
author: Peter James
description: A con man uses 9/11 to fake his death and start over, but Grace connects him to a body found in a storm drain years later.

title: Dead Tomorrow
author: Peter James
description: A desperate mother needs a liver transplant for her daughter and Grace investigates an organ trafficking ring operating in Brighton.

title: When She Was Good
author: Michael Robotham
description: A woman who can detect lies has been hiding for six years and the people who want her dead have finally found her location.

title: Lying Beside You
author: Michael Robotham
description: A psychologist's brother is released from a psychiatric hospital and a woman is found murdered in almost the same way his brother killed before.

title: When You Are Mine
author: Michael Robotham
description: A police constable intervenes in a domestic violence case and becomes obsessed with protecting the victim, risking her own career and life.

title: Trust Your Eyes
author: Linwood Barclay
description: A schizophrenic man obsessively browsing street-view maps spots what looks like a murder in progress in a New York apartment window.

title: Elevator Pitch
author: Linwood Barclay
description: Elevators in New York high-rises start plummeting and a journalist and a detective race to find who is sabotaging the city.

title: Take Your Breath Away
author: Linwood Barclay
description: A man whose wife vanished six years ago has just started a new relationship when his wife walks into her old hair salon.

title: The Doomsday Book
author: Connie Willis
description: A historian time-travels to 1348 England during the Black Death while a modern pandemic paralyzes Oxford.

title: Passage
author: Connie Willis
description: A psychologist studying near-death experiences discovers they all involve the Titanic, and the connection may reveal what death actually is.

title: The Faded Sun: Kesrith
author: C.J. Cherryh
description: An alien warrior race faces extinction after losing a war, and a human soldier becomes entangled with the last survivors.

title: Rimrunners
author: C.J. Cherryh
description: A former military spacer hiding on a merchant ship must keep her identity secret while the crew faces mutiny and piracy.

title: Existence
author: David Brin
description: An astronaut finds an alien artifact in orbit and it contains a message—but so do other artifacts, and the messages contradict each other.

title: Glory Season
author: David Brin
description: On a planet where women clone themselves and men are marginalized, a young variant girl discovers a secret that could change everything.

title: Hull Zero Three
author: Greg Bear
description: A man wakes naked on a damaged generation ship with no memory and must survive monstrous creatures while figuring out who he is and what went wrong.

title: Shaman
author: Kim Stanley Robinson
description: A boy in Ice Age Europe trains to become his tribe's shaman while dealing with rival tribes, cave paintings, and the first stirrings of civilization.

title: Galileo's Dream
author: Kim Stanley Robinson
description: Galileo is visited by a time traveler from Jupiter's moons who shows him the future his telescope will create, and he must choose between knowledge and safety.

title: Maelstrom
author: Peter Watts
description: Lenie Clarke escapes the rift and carries a doomsday microbe to the surface while the internet evolves into a predatory ecosystem.

title: Wolf-Speaker
author: Tamora Pierce
description: Daine must defend the wolves who saved her from a mining operation that threatens their valley and the kingdom.

title: Emperor Mage
author: Tamora Pierce
description: Daine visits the emperor of Carthak to negotiate peace and discovers the emperor plans treachery, but her own power has grown terrifying.

title: The Realms of the Gods
author: Tamora Pierce
description: Daine and Numair are cast into the Divine Realms and must find their way home while Tortall faces an invasion of immortals.

title: First Test
author: Tamora Pierce
description: The first girl to openly train as a knight in Tortall faces hazing, prejudice, and enemies who want her to fail.

title: Page
author: Tamora Pierce
description: Kel continues her training as a page, standing up to bullies and discovering that leadership means protecting everyone.

title: Squire
author: Tamora Pierce
description: Kel becomes a squire and rides to war, proving herself in battle while a killing machine terrorizes the border.

title: Lady Knight
author: Tamora Pierce
description: Kel earns her shield and is given command of a refugee camp, but her true mission is to destroy the machines that steal children's souls.

title: Mister Monday
author: Garth Nix
description: A boy near death is given a key by a mysterious figure, and it pulls him into the House—a cosmic structure at the center of the universe.

title: Grim Tuesday
author: Garth Nix
description: Arthur must claim the Second Key from a miser who is mining Nothing from the void and selling it for profit.

title: Drowned Wednesday
author: Garth Nix
description: The Third Part of the House is a vast sea where a whale-sized Trustee has eaten everything, and Arthur must navigate pirate ships and sunken treasures.

title: Siege of Darkness
author: R.A. Salvatore
description: The drow army marches on Mithral Hall in a massive assault, and Drizzt must defend his home against his own people.

title: Passage to Dawn
author: R.A. Salvatore
description: Drizzt sails as a privateer and discovers that the evil Errtu has captured his friend Wulfgar's soul.

title: The Omen Machine
author: Terry Goodkind
description: A machine beneath the People's Palace begins issuing prophecies that come true, and Richard must decide whether prophecy serves freedom or destroys it.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: Genes, not organisms, are the true unit of natural selection, and this insight reframes all of evolution—and invented the word 'meme.'

title: The God Delusion
author: Richard Dawkins
description: Dawkins argues that God almost certainly does not exist and that religion is a dangerous delusion that humanity should outgrow.

title: The Blind Watchmaker
author: Richard Dawkins
description: Natural selection is the blind watchmaker—it has no purpose, no foresight, and yet produces the illusion of design.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: From the Big Bang to black holes, Hawking explains the universe to non-scientists in the bestselling science book of all time.

title: The Universe in a Nutshell
author: Stephen Hawking
description: The sequel to A Brief History: string theory, branes, and the cutting edge of physics explained with Hawking's trademark clarity.

title: Astrophysics for People in a Hurry
author: Neil deGrasse Tyson
description: The essentials of astrophysics—dark matter, the Big Bang, cosmic expansion—compressed into a book you can read in a sitting.

title: Death by Black Hole
author: Neil deGrasse Tyson
description: Essays on the most violent events in the universe, from gamma-ray bursts to asteroids, told with Tyson's trademark enthusiasm.

title: Wonders of the Universe
author: Brian Cox
description: The physicist and BBC presenter explains the fundamental forces of the universe through stunning imagery and accessible prose.

title: The Future of Humanity
author: Michio Kaku
description: How we will colonize Mars, travel to the stars, and achieve immortality according to the physics that makes it possible.

title: Physics of the Impossible
author: Michio Kaku
description: A physicist evaluates the science fiction technologies—force fields, teleportation, time travel—and rates how impossible each actually is.

title: The Elegant Universe
author: Brian Greene
description: String theory explained for non-physicists: how vibrating strings may unite quantum mechanics and gravity into a theory of everything.

title: The Fabric of the Cosmos
author: Brian Greene
description: Space, time, and the texture of reality itself—from Newton to Einstein to quantum mechanics to the multiverse.

title: Guns, Germs, and Steel
author: Jared Diamond
description: Why did Europeans conquer everyone else? Not because of racial superiority but geography, crops, and domesticable animals that gave some civilizations a head start.

title: Collapse
author: Jared Diamond
description: Why civilizations fail: the Maya, Easter Island, the Norse in Greenland, and what their collapses tell us about our environmental future.

title: On Human Nature
author: E.O. Wilson
description: Wilson applies sociobiology to humans—arguing that genes shape aggression, religion, and altruism—in the book that launched decades of debate.

title: Angela's Ashes
author: Frank McCourt
description: An Irish-American childhood of staggering poverty in Limerick—dead siblings, a drunk father, a suffering mother—told with dark humor that makes the misery bearable.

title: 'Tis
author: Frank McCourt
description: McCourt arrives in New York at nineteen and works his way from the docks to a teaching career while the ghosts of Limerick follow him.

title: Teacher Man
author: Frank McCourt
description: Thirty years in New York City public schools—how McCourt found his teaching voice through storytelling, honesty, and immigrant grit.

title: A Long Way Gone
author: Ishmael Beah
description: A boy soldier in Sierra Leone's civil war recounts being drugged, armed, and forced to kill before being rescued and rehabilitated by UNICEF.

title: I Am Malala
author: Malala Yousafzai
description: A Pakistani girl shot by the Taliban for advocating girls' education tells her story and becomes the youngest Nobel Peace Prize laureate.

title: Three Wishes
author: Liane Moriarty
description: Triplet sisters in Sydney—one pregnant, one trying to get pregnant, one with a terrible secret—celebrate their 34th birthday as their lives unravel.

title: On Mystic Lake
author: Kristin Hannah
description: A woman whose husband asks for a divorce returns to her hometown and falls for a widowed father struggling to reach his silent daughter.

title: The Wild
author: Kristin Hannah
description: A family moves to the Alaskan wilderness to escape civilization and must survive when nature turns hostile.

title: Maybe Not
author: Colleen Hoover
description: A companion novella to Maybe Someday told from the male roommate's perspective as he falls for his unexpected new roommate.

title: Mary, Mary
author: James Patterson
description: Cross investigates a killer targeting Hollywood's biggest female stars, each murder staged as a screenplay.

title: Double Cross
author: James Patterson
description: Cross faces a copycat of his old nemesis Kyle Craig while a second killer uses the internet to broadcast murders live.

title: The Women's Murder Club: 1st to Die
author: James Patterson
description: Four women—a detective, a medical examiner, a journalist, and an attorney—form a club to solve murders the system can't handle.

title: Dark Witch
author: Nora Roberts
description: A woman travels to County Mayo to explore her roots and discovers she is heir to a powerful magical legacy that binds three cousins.

title: Shelter in Place
author: Nora Roberts
description: Survivors of a mall shooting are connected by the tragedy years later when someone starts killing them one by one.

title: The Girls in the Garden
author: Lisa Jewell
description: A thirteen-year-old girl is found unconscious in the communal garden of a London housing complex and every neighbor is hiding something.

title: The Housemaid Is Watching
author: Freida McFadden
description: Millie has started a new life in the suburbs but her perfect neighbor is watching her family through the windows.

title: Missing You
author: Harlan Coben
description: A detective finds her ex-fiancé on an online dating site ten years after he vanished and discovers his profile is connected to missing persons.

title: Win
author: Harlan Coben
description: Windsor Horne Lockwood III—impossibly rich, impossibly skilled—investigates when a suitcase from a decades-old kidnapping turns up in a dead man's closet.

title: The Seven Moons of Maali Almeida
author: Shehan Karunatilaka
description: A dead war photographer in 1990 Sri Lanka has seven moons to solve his own murder from the afterlife before he's recycled.

title: Sunset Park
author: Paul Auster
description: A young man hiding from his past squats in a Brooklyn house with three others, each running from something, as the 2008 financial crisis reshapes New York.

title: Timbuktu
author: Paul Auster
description: A dog narrates his life with a homeless poet dying on the streets of Baltimore, and must find a new home before winter.

title: Telegraph Avenue
author: Michael Chabon
description: Two men run a used-record store on the Oakland-Berkeley border as a mega-retailer threatens to destroy them and a football star complicates everything.

title: Summerland
author: Michael Chabon
description: A boy must save the world through baseball in Chabon's portal fantasy for young readers, spanning parallel worlds held together by the game.

title: The Twenty-Seventh City
author: Jonathan Franzen
description: An Indian woman becomes St. Louis's police chief and orchestrates a conspiracy to reshape the city through surveillance, real estate, and manipulation.

title: The Autograph Man
author: Zadie Smith
description: A Chinese-Jewish autograph dealer in London obsesses over a reclusive 1950s movie star and the difference between the sacred and the profane.

title: Band of Brothers
author: Stephen Ambrose
description: Easy Company of the 101st Airborne from D-Day through the Eagle's Nest—the book that became the legendary HBO series.

title: D-Day: June 6, 1944
author: Stephen Ambrose
description: The invasion of Normandy from every angle—paratroopers, beach landings, commanders, German defenders—in Ambrose's defining work.

title: Undaunted Courage
author: Stephen Ambrose
description: Lewis and Clark's expedition across America told as the adventure story it was—grizzlies, rapids, Sacagawea, and the vast unknown.

title: Citizen Soldiers
author: Stephen Ambrose
description: The GIs who fought from Normandy to the Bulge to the crossing of the Rhine—ordinary men doing extraordinary things.

title: An Army at Dawn
author: Rick Atkinson
description: The North Africa campaign from Operation Torch through Tunisia—America's first battles and first defeats in WWII.

title: The Day of Battle
author: Rick Atkinson
description: The invasion of Sicily and Italy—Anzio, Cassino, the liberation of Rome—in the bloodiest and most frustrating theater of the war.

title: The Guns at Last Light
author: Rick Atkinson
description: D-Day through V-E Day: the final year of the war in Europe told through soldiers, generals, and civilians.

title: The Battle of the Bulge
author: Antony Beevor
description: Hitler's last gamble in the Ardennes—the frozen forests, the massacres, and the American resilience that turned near-defeat into the beginning of the end.

title: Overlord: D-Day and the Battle for Normandy
author: Max Hastings
description: A revisionist account of D-Day arguing the Allies won through firepower rather than fighting quality, and that the Germans were better soldiers.

title: Inferno: The World at War, 1939-1945
author: Max Hastings
description: WWII told through the experiences of ordinary people on all sides—soldiers, civilians, prisoners—rather than through strategy and politics.

title: Vietnam: An Epic Tragedy
author: Max Hastings
description: The entire Vietnam War from Dien Bien Phu to the fall of Saigon, told through Vietnamese, French, and American perspectives.

title: The Longest Day
author: Cornelius Ryan
description: D-Day hour by hour from all sides—the paratroopers, the beach landings, the German defenders, the French civilians—the definitive account.

title: A Bridge Too Far
author: Cornelius Ryan
description: Operation Market Garden—the airborne assault on the bridges at Arnhem—and why the boldest Allied plan of the war failed.

title: Agent Zigzag
author: Ben Macintyre
description: Eddie Chapman—a safecracker recruited by the Nazis as a spy who became a British double agent—the most colorful spy of WWII.

title: Operation Mincemeat
author: Ben Macintyre
description: British intelligence plants fake invasion plans on a dead man's body and floats it to Spain to deceive the Nazis about the invasion of Sicily.

title: The Spy and the Traitor
author: Ben Macintyre
description: Oleg Gordievsky, the KGB officer who spied for MI6 during the Cold War's most dangerous moment, and the escape that almost failed.

title: A Spy Among Friends
author: Ben Macintyre
description: Kim Philby—the most devastating double agent in history—and the friend who never suspected him until the betrayal destroyed everything.

title: Romancing the Duke
author: Tessa Dare
description: A woman inherits a castle occupied by a blind, bitter duke, and their battle over the property becomes a love story.

title: Do You Want to Start a Scandal
author: Tessa Dare
description: A woman caught in a compromising position at a ball must marry the last man she'd choose—a spy masquerading as a lord.

title: The Day of the Duchess
author: Sarah MacLean
description: A duke's estranged wife returns to demand a divorce, and he has twelve days to win her back or lose her forever.

title: Chasing Cassandra
author: Lisa Kleypas
description: A ruthless railway magnate falls for an idealistic young woman who is everything he doesn't believe in.

title: Hello Stranger
author: Lisa Kleypas
description: One of the first female doctors in Victorian London clashes with a charming detective while investigating a case that endangers them both.

title: Because of Miss Bridgerton
author: Julia Quinn
description: A Bridgerton neighbor and a Rokesby heir have been childhood enemies for years, until a twist of fate forces them to see each other differently.

title: Breathless
author: Beverly Jenkins
description: A Black woman doctor in 1880s Arizona Territory and a half-Seminole rancher clash over a saloon and fall in love across the frontier.

title: Forbidden
author: Beverly Jenkins
description: A Black female newspaper editor in 1870s Virginia and a white Union Army officer navigate Reconstruction and forbidden love.

title: Tempest
author: Beverly Jenkins
description: A physician and a spring-wagon driver are forced into a marriage of convenience in 1850s Michigan, and neither expects to fall in love.

title: The Duchess War
author: Courtney Milan
description: A duke with radical politics and a woman hiding a scandal in her past are forced together in a small town where everyone is watching.

title: The Governess Affair
author: Courtney Milan
description: A man hired to make a ruined woman go away falls for her instead in Milan's Regency novella about power and justice.

title: An Extraordinary Union
author: Alyssa Cole
description: A Black woman spy with a photographic memory and a white Pinkerton detective go undercover in a Confederate senator's household during the Civil War.

title: A Princess in Theory
author: Alyssa Cole
description: A grad student keeps deleting emails from a supposed African prince who turns out to be real—and she's his betrothed.

title: Someone Like You
author: Sarah Dessen
description: A girl supports her best friend through a teenage pregnancy while falling for a boy her mother disapproves of.

title: The Reappearance of Rachel Price
author: Holly Jackson
description: A girl filming a documentary about her mother's disappearance is stunned when her mother walks through the door alive after eight years.

title: The Cousins
author: Karen M. McManus
description: Three cousins are invited to their estranged grandmother's island resort and discover why she disowned their parents decades ago.

title: The Naturals
author: Jennifer Lynn Barnes
description: A girl with an uncanny ability to read people is recruited into an FBI program for teenagers with natural crime-solving gifts.

title: The First to Die at the End
author: Adam Silvera
description: The prequel to They Both Die at the End: the first day the Death-Cast system goes live and two boys discover they're on the list.

title: The House of Hades
author: Rick Riordan
description: Percy and Annabeth fight through Tartarus itself while their friends race to the Doors of Death.

title: The Blood of Olympus
author: Rick Riordan
description: The Greek and Roman demigods unite for the final battle against Gaea as the giants rise and the Earth Mother wakes.

title: Persepolis 2
author: Marjane Satrapi
description: Satrapi's years in Vienna as a teenager and her return to Iran, navigating depression, identity, and the impossibility of belonging in either world.

title: Essex County
author: Jeff Lemire
description: Three interlocking stories set in rural Ontario—a boy who loves superheroes, an aging hockey player, and a traveling nurse—connected by loss and landscape.

title: American Born Chinese
author: Gene Luen Yang
description: Three stories converge: a Chinese-American boy trying to fit in, the Monkey King from Chinese mythology, and a sitcom nightmare of racial stereotypes.

title: Spinning
author: Tillie Walden
description: A memoir of competitive figure skating, coming out as gay in Texas, and the slow process of quitting the thing that defined you.

title: My Favorite Thing Is Monsters
author: Emil Ferris
description: A girl in 1960s Chicago who draws herself as a werewolf investigates her neighbor's murder in a pen-drawn graphic novel that looks like a kid's notebook.

title: Whirlwind
author: James Clavell
description: The Iranian Revolution of 1979 traps a helicopter company's pilots and their families between the Shah's fall and the Ayatollah's rise.

title: The Little Drummer Girl
author: John le Carré
description: An English actress is recruited by Israeli intelligence to infiltrate a Palestinian bomb-making network by becoming the lover of a terrorist she's never met.

title: Our Kind of Traitor
author: John le Carré
description: A couple on holiday befriends a Russian money launderer who wants to defect, and MI6 uses them as go-betweens.

title: Agent Running in the Field
author: John le Carré
description: A middle-aged spy running a minor London station befriends a young man at a badminton club who has connections to Russian intelligence.

title: Red Storm Rising
author: Tom Clancy
description: World War III erupts between NATO and the Soviet Union in a conventional war fought across Europe, the Atlantic, and Iceland.

title: Without Remorse
author: Tom Clancy
description: The origin story of John Clark: a Navy SEAL hunts the drug dealers who killed his girlfriend while running a covert mission in Vietnam.

title: The Cardinal of the Kremlin
author: Tom Clancy
description: A CIA mole inside the Kremlin is in danger of exposure while both superpowers race to build a Star Wars missile defense system.

title: Debt of Honor
author: Tom Clancy
description: Japan launches an economic and military attack on the United States and Jack Ryan must prevent a Pacific war.

title: Executive Orders
author: Tom Clancy
description: Ryan becomes president after a kamikaze attack on the Capitol and faces a biological weapons attack from Iran while rebuilding the government.

title: Swag
author: Elmore Leonard
description: Two guys write ten rules for armed robbery and follow them perfectly—until they don't.

title: Stick
author: Elmore Leonard
description: An ex-con in Miami plans one last score involving drug dealers and millionaires on the Gold Coast.

title: Freaky Deaky
author: Elmore Leonard
description: Two former 1960s radicals use their bomb-making skills to extort a drunken millionaire in Detroit.

title: Glitz
author: Elmore Leonard
description: A Miami cop follows a psychopath to Atlantic City where the casinos provide cover for revenge.

title: Maximum Bob
author: Elmore Leonard
description: A hanging judge in Palm Beach whose wife channels a 12-year-old slave girl becomes the target of an alligator assassination.

title: Mr. Majestyk
author: Elmore Leonard
description: A melon farmer tangles with a hitman and the mob when he refuses to let them use his migrant workers as a front.

title: Be Cool
author: Elmore Leonard
description: Chili Palmer moves from movies to the music business and discovers the record industry is even more crooked than Hollywood.

title: Tishomingo Blues
author: Elmore Leonard
description: A high diver at a Mississippi casino witnesses a murder from his platform and gets caught between the Dixie Mafia and a Detroit hustler.

title: Raylan
author: Elmore Leonard
description: U.S. Marshal Raylan Givens deals with organ harvesters, a coal company femme fatale, and poker cheats in backwoods Kentucky.

title: The Closers
author: Michael Connelly
description: Bosch returns to the LAPD to work cold cases and reopens a 1988 murder of a mixed-race teenager that was originally dismissed as gang-related.

title: The Overlook
author: Michael Connelly
description: A murder on a Mulholland overlook leads Bosch into a case involving stolen radioactive materials and Homeland Security turf wars.

title: The Black Box
author: Michael Connelly
description: Bosch reopens the case of a Danish journalist killed during the 1992 LA riots and discovers a connection to the Gulf War.

title: Two Kinds of Truth
author: Michael Connelly
description: Bosch goes undercover inside a pill mill while an old conviction is challenged and a killer he put away thirty years ago may walk free.

title: Desert Star
author: Michael Connelly
description: Bosch and Ballard team up again to hunt a serial killer who has been operating undetected for years.

title: Resurrection Walk
author: Michael Connelly
description: Mickey Haller and Harry Bosch team up to free a woman convicted of killing her husband, and the real killer is watching.

title: Ender in Exile
author: Orson Scott Card
description: The story between Ender's Game and Speaker for the Dead: Ender's first colony mission and the political maneuvering to control him.

title: The Dosadi Experiment
author: Frank Herbert
description: A hidden planet serves as a social experiment where billions are crammed into a tiny area, breeding the most cunning humans and aliens in the universe.

title: The Dragon in the Sea
author: Frank Herbert
description: A submarine crew steals oil from undersea wells in enemy territory while a psychologist aboard tries to identify which of them is a saboteur.

title: The Ones Who Walk Away from Omelas
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: A perfect city's happiness depends on the suffering of one child locked in a basement, and some citizens, upon learning this, walk away.

title: Always Coming Home
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: An anthropological account of a future California people called the Kesh—their stories, songs, customs, and conflicts—as if Le Guin had lived among them.

title: The Telling
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: An Earth observer on a planet that has suppressed its traditional storytelling culture seeks out the banned Telling—the oral tradition that connects people to meaning.

title: Nemesis
author: Isaac Asimov
description: A secret settlement orbiting a nearby star discovers their sun is on a collision course with Earth, and a girl can telepathically sense the star's alien intelligence.

title: R Is for Rocket
author: Ray Bradbury
description: Seventeen stories about space, boyhood, and wonder—including The Fog Horn, about a sea monster drawn to a lighthouse it mistakes for a mate.

title: The Golden Apples of the Sun
author: Ray Bradbury
description: Twenty-two stories including A Sound of Thunder—the butterfly effect before it had a name—and The Fog Horn.

title: Norse Mythology
author: Neil Gaiman
description: Gaiman retells the Norse myths from the creation to Ragnarok—Odin, Thor, Loki—in his own voice, faithful to the originals and crackling with life.

title: The Carpet People
author: Terry Pratchett
description: Tiny people living in a carpet face a devastating Fray (vacuum cleaner) and must journey across the carpet to find allies. Pratchett's first novel, revised in 1992.

title: The Nome Trilogy: Truckers
author: Terry Pratchett
description: Tiny people called nomes live under a department store and must evacuate when the store is demolished, in the first of Pratchett's YA trilogy.

title: The Emperor's Soul
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: A forger who can rewrite the history of objects is imprisoned and tasked with forging a new soul for the comatose emperor in 100 days.

title: Edgedancer
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: A Stormlight novella following Lift as she infiltrates Azir and confronts a herald of the Almighty who has gone mad.

title: Dawnshard
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: A Stormlight novella: Rysn leads an expedition to a mysterious island holding a weapon that predates the Shards.

title: Clarissa Oakes
author: Patrick O'Brian
description: A woman stowaway is discovered aboard ship and her presence disrupts the crew as Aubrey sails through the Pacific.

title: The Commodore
author: Patrick O'Brian
description: Aubrey is promoted to commodore and sent to blockade the French coast while Maturin's intelligence operations grow more dangerous.

title: The Yellow Admiral
author: Patrick O'Brian
description: Aubrey faces the threat of being made a yellow admiral—promoted to a rank with no command—while Napoleon returns from Elba.

title: The Hundred Days
author: Patrick O'Brian
description: Aubrey and Maturin race to prevent Napoleon from receiving a shipment of gold during the Hundred Days before Waterloo.

title: Fatal Remedies
author: Donna Leon
description: Brunetti's wife is arrested for vandalizing a factory she believes is poisoning Venice, and he must investigate while protecting her.

title: The Man Who Smiled
author: Henning Mankell
description: Wallander emerges from depression to investigate a lawyer's death that connects to a billionaire businessman with dark secrets.

title: Firewall
author: Henning Mankell
description: A blackout and a dead body in an ATM vestibule lead Wallander into a conspiracy targeting Sweden's power grid and financial systems.

title: The Pyramid
author: Henning Mankell
description: Five stories spanning Wallander's career from his first case as a young cop to the events just before Faceless Killers.

title: The Wings of the Sphinx
author: Andrea Camilleri
description: A dead woman with a butterfly tattoo is found at a garbage dump and Montalbano investigates the illegal immigration network that brought her to Sicily.

title: The Age of Doubt
author: Andrea Camilleri
description: A yacht arrives in Vigàta's port with suspicious cargo and Montalbano falls for one of its passengers while investigating.

title: The Safety Net
author: Andrea Camilleri
description: Montalbano investigates the filming of a TV show that disturbs the town while strange surveillance drones appear overhead.

title: Riccardino
author: Andrea Camilleri
description: Montalbano's final case, published after Camilleri's death: a man is murdered after calling the inspector by name, and the author himself appears in the story.

title: The Path Between the Seas
author: David McCullough
description: The building of the Panama Canal—the French disaster, the American triumph, the diseases, the politics—told as a grand adventure.

title: The Great Bridge
author: David McCullough
description: The building of the Brooklyn Bridge: the Roeblings father and son, the caissons, the bends, and the fourteen years it took to connect Brooklyn to Manhattan.

title: Brave Companions
author: David McCullough
description: Profiles of remarkable people—Alexander von Humboldt, Harriet Beecher Stowe, Louis Agassiz, the builders of the Brooklyn Bridge—McCullough's portrait gallery.

title: No One Goes Alone
author: Erik Larson
description: Larson's brief account of a séance he attended to investigate the world of Victorian spiritualism.

title: Hellhound on His Trail
author: Hampton Sides
description: The assassination of Martin Luther King Jr. and the two-month manhunt for James Earl Ray across three continents.

title: On Desperate Ground
author: Hampton Sides
description: The Battle of Chosin Reservoir in Korea, where US Marines fought their way out of a Chinese trap in temperatures below zero.

title: Rogue Heroes
author: Ben Macintyre
description: The founding of the SAS during WWII—a band of eccentrics who invented modern special forces through audacity, sabotage, and desert raids.

title: Double Cross
author: Ben Macintyre
description: The five spies who ran D-Day's deception campaign, convincing Hitler the invasion would come at Calais while it hit Normandy.

title: Coldheart Canyon
author: Clive Barker
description: A movie star recovering from plastic surgery hides in a Hollywood mansion that contains a tile mosaic leading to a supernatural hunting ground.

title: The Great and Secret Show
author: Clive Barker
description: Two postal workers in a dead-letter office discover the Art—a reality-altering power—and their war over it spans generations in a California town.

title: The Five
author: Robert McCammon
description: A small rock band on tour is hunted by a sniper with a grudge, and each member's backstory reveals what they're fighting to save.

title: The Listener
author: Robert McCammon
description: A man who can hear people's inner pain uses his gift to save lives while a serial killer targets the same vulnerable people.

title: The Shadow Out of Time
author: H.P. Lovecraft
description: A professor blacks out for five years and discovers his body was inhabited by a being from a race that existed before humanity and will exist after.

title: The Case of Charles Dexter Ward
author: H.P. Lovecraft
description: A young man researches his ancestor—an 18th-century necromancer—and the ancestor comes back to life and takes over.

title: The Lion's Game
author: Nelson DeMille
description: A Libyan assassin arrives in New York to avenge the 1986 bombing of Tripoli and an anti-terrorism agent must stop him.

title: Wild Fire
author: Nelson DeMille
description: A secret cabal of billionaires plans to trigger nuclear war by detonating a suitcase nuke on American soil and blaming it on Islam.

title: The Quest
author: Nelson DeMille
description: Three journalists in war-torn Ethiopia hear about the Holy Grail hidden in a remote monastery and set out to find it.

title: The Winner
author: David Baldacci
description: A woman in a trailer park is offered a fixed lottery win in exchange for doing exactly what a mysterious fixer demands.

title: The Simple Truth
author: David Baldacci
description: A Supreme Court clerk discovers a petition from a military prisoner that could expose a decades-old miscarriage of justice.

title: The Judas Strain
author: James Rollins
description: A plague breaks out in the Indian Ocean and Sigma Force traces it to Marco Polo's lost voyage and an ancient bioweapon.

title: The Last Oracle
author: James Rollins
description: Autistic children are being used as biological weapons and the trail leads from Washington to the Oracle at Delphi.

title: The Devil Colony
author: James Rollins
description: A massacre at a Native American cave connected to Thomas Jefferson and Lewis and Clark threatens to start a new Indian war.

title: White Hot
author: Sandra Brown
description: A woman returns to her Louisiana hometown for a family funeral and discovers her brother may be responsible for a series of hate crimes.

title: Tailspin
author: Sandra Brown
description: A pilot crash-lands in fog-shrouded Appalachia while transporting a mysterious package, and the locals aren't friendly.

title: The Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-Time
author: Mark Haddon
description: A fifteen-year-old with autism investigates who killed his neighbor's dog and the investigation leads him to London and the truth about his family.

title: A Spot of Bother
author: Mark Haddon
description: A retired man convinced he has cancer, his daughter's disastrous wedding, and a family that can't talk to each other converge in dark British comedy.

title: By the River Piedra I Sat Down and Wept
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A woman reunites with a childhood friend who has become a spiritual leader and must choose between the safe life she knows and the love that transforms her.

title: The Witch of Portobello
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A woman's life and mysterious death are reconstructed through interviews with the people who knew her—a tale of the divine feminine and unconventional faith.

title: Tuesdays with Morrie
author: Mitch Albom
description: A sportswriter reconnects with his dying college professor for fourteen Tuesday visits about the meaning of life.

title: The Five People You Meet in Heaven
author: Mitch Albom
description: An elderly amusement park worker dies and meets five people in heaven who explain how his life mattered.

title: For One More Day
author: Mitch Albom
description: A man attempts suicide and gets one more day with his dead mother, learning the sacrifices she made that he never knew about.

title: The Messenger
author: Markus Zusak
description: An aimless cabdriver starts receiving playing cards with addresses, each sending him on a mission to help—or confront—a stranger.

title: Silver Girl
author: Elin Hilderbrand
description: A woman whose husband is arrested for a Ponzi scheme retreats to Nantucket and rebuilds with the help of her college roommate.

title: Summer of '69
author: Elin Hilderbrand
description: A Nantucket family navigates Vietnam, the moon landing, Woodstock, and Chappaquiddick during the most tumultuous summer of the century.

title: The Five-Star Weekend
author: Elin Hilderbrand
description: A newly widowed food blogger invites her best friend from each era of her life to Nantucket, and the women clash.

title: After I Do
author: Taylor Jenkins Reid
description: A married couple agrees to separate for one year with no contact to decide if they want to stay together.

title: The Deal of a Lifetime
author: Fredrik Backman
description: A father on Christmas Eve tells his estranged son about the deal he made to save a dying girl's life.

title: The Botany of Desire
author: Michael Pollan
description: Four plants—apple, tulip, marijuana, potato—have used human desire to spread across the world, and we may be the bees.

title: Cooked
author: Michael Pollan
description: Four transformations of food—fire, water, air, earth—and what we lose when we outsource cooking to corporations.

title: Let's Explore Diabetes with Owls
author: David Sedaris
description: Essays from the years Sedaris spent on a world book tour—dentists in Paris, gun culture in America, and the wonders of taxidermy.

title: Happy-Go-Lucky
author: David Sedaris
description: Sedaris navigates the pandemic, his father's decline, and the aftermath of his sister's death with characteristic dark humor.

title: The Life and Times of the Thunderbolt Kid
author: Bill Bryson
description: Bryson's memoir of growing up in 1950s Des Moines—nuclear anxiety, TV dinners, and the last golden age of American innocence.

title: Shakespeare: The World as Stage
author: Bill Bryson
description: What we know and don't know about Shakespeare—which turns out to be almost nothing—told with Bryson's gift for making gaps in knowledge entertaining.

title: Determined
author: Robert Sapolsky
description: Sapolsky argues there is no free will—not even a little—and that understanding this should transform criminal justice, morality, and how we treat each other.

title: When a Scot Ties the Knot
author: Tessa Dare
description: A woman who invented a fake fiancé in letters is stunned when a very real Scottish soldier arrives claiming to be him.

title: Cold-Hearted Rake
author: Lisa Kleypas
description: A rake inherits an earldom and must decide whether to evict the tenants—including his beautiful widowed cousin-in-law.

title: Devil in Disguise
author: Lisa Kleypas
description: A wealthy Scottish whisky magnate and a London widow find each other irresistible despite every sensible reason to stay apart.

title: Twice in a Blue Moon
author: Christina Lauren
description: A movie star runs into the man who sold her secret to the tabloids years ago—now he's directing her next film.

title: Love and Other Words
author: Christina Lauren
description: Childhood best friends who were once in love reconnect after eleven years and one devastating secret.

title: If You Would Have Told Me
author: Abby Jimenez
description: A woman inherits a mansion from a stranger on the condition she live in it for a year—with the man who was supposed to inherit it.

title: Joyland
author: Stephen King
description: A college student takes a summer job at a North Carolina amusement park haunted by the ghost of a murdered girl.

title: Elevation
author: Stephen King
description: A man is inexplicably losing weight while his mass stays the same, and his impossible condition changes his relationship with his bigoted neighbors.

title: The Eyes of Darkness
author: Dean Koontz
description: A mother investigates her son's death in a camping accident and discovers he may still be alive in a government laboratory.

title: Cold Fire
author: Dean Koontz
description: A journalist investigates a man who keeps appearing at disasters just in time to save lives—driven by a force even he doesn't understand.

title: Dark Rivers of the Heart
author: Dean Koontz
description: A man searching for a woman he met once is hunted by a sociopathic government agent with unlimited surveillance technology.

title: The Final Girls Support Group
author: Grady Hendrix
description: The real women who inspired slasher movies meet in a support group, and when someone starts killing them off, the final girls must fight back.

title: Inspection
author: Josh Malerman
description: Boys and girls raised in separate towers with no knowledge of the opposite sex discover each other's existence and the system that separated them.

title: Tithe
author: Holly Black
description: A sixteen-year-old girl discovers she is connected to the faerie world when she finds a wounded knight on her doorstep.

title: Valiant
author: Holly Black
description: A runaway in New York City discovers a world of faeries living in the subway tunnels and becomes addicted to a magical drug.

title: Ironside
author: Holly Black
description: Kaye must complete an impossible faerie quest or lose the boy she loves—and the Unseelie Court wants her to fail.

title: Rebel
author: Marie Lu
description: Ten years after Champion, a new threat emerges and Day's younger brother must step out of his shadow.

title: Warcross
author: Marie Lu
description: A teenage hacker bounty hunter is recruited by the creator of a VR game to find a security threat from inside.

title: Wildcard
author: Marie Lu
description: Emika goes undercover to stop Zero from using Warcross to control people's minds worldwide.

title: All the Crooked Saints
author: Maggie Stiefvater
description: A family of saints in a Colorado desert who can perform miracles—but the miracles always come with a cost—face a crisis when a radio DJ arrives.

title: Call Down the Hawk
author: Maggie Stiefvater
description: Ronan Lynch from the Raven Cycle struggles with his dreampower while a visionary hunts dreamers who are destroying the ley line.

title: Mister Impossible
author: Maggie Stiefvater
description: Ronan and his brothers race to save the ley line while Hennessy must confront her own dreams or be consumed.

title: Greywaren
author: Maggie Stiefvater
description: The Dreamer Trilogy concludes as Ronan faces the final choice between the dream world and the waking one.

title: Stars Above
author: Marissa Meyer
description: Nine stories set in the Lunar Chronicles universe—the backstories of Cinder, Scarlet, Cress, and Winter before the series began.

title: Renegades
author: Marissa Meyer
description: In a world of superheroes and villains, a girl from the villain side infiltrates the heroic Renegades and falls for one of them.

title: Archenemies
author: Marissa Meyer
description: Nova's undercover mission deepens as she and Adrian get closer and the line between hero and villain blurs.

title: Supernova
author: Marissa Meyer
description: The truth about Nova's identity threatens to destroy everything as the final battle between Renegades and Anarchists approaches.

title: Skinny Legs and All
author: Tom Robbins
description: A belly dancer, a painting, and five objects journeying to Jerusalem converge as art, religion, and the Middle East collide.

title: Half Asleep in Frog Pajamas
author: Tom Robbins
description: A stockbroker loses everything in a market crash over Easter weekend and wanders through Seattle encountering a monkey trainer and a fortune teller.

title: Fierce Invalids Home from Hot Climates
author: Tom Robbins
description: A CIA agent cursed to never touch the ground by a Peruvian shaman hides in a convent and falls for a nun.

title: Airframe
author: Michael Crichton
description: A quality assurance VP at an aircraft manufacturer investigates a near-disaster on a transpacific flight while the media circles for a scandal.

title: Disclosure
author: Michael Crichton
description: A man is sexually harassed by his new female boss and when he reports it, the corporation turns against him.

title: State of Fear
author: Michael Crichton
description: Eco-terrorists plan disasters to create fear about global warming while a lawyer discovers the environmental movement may be manufacturing its own crises.

title: Body Double
author: Tess Gerritsen
description: Maura Isles finds a woman murdered on her doorstep who looks exactly like her—a twin she never knew existed.

title: Vanish
author: Tess Gerritsen
description: A dead woman in the morgue wakes up, takes a hostage, and Rizzoli must negotiate while Isles discovers the woman was a victim of sex trafficking.

title: The Keepsake
author: Tess Gerritsen
description: A mummy donated to a museum turns out to be a modern murder victim, and the killer collects and preserves his victims.

title: Ice Cold
author: Tess Gerritsen
description: Maura is stranded in a mountain town that appears to have been abandoned overnight, and the residents may not have left voluntarily.

title: Last to Die
author: Tess Gerritsen
description: Three orphaned children whose families were murdered are sent to a special school, and someone is killing the adults around them.

title: Dead Like You
author: Peter James
description: A shoe fetishist is raping women in Brighton and the attacks mirror an unsolved case from 1997 that Grace investigated as a rookie.

title: Want You Dead
author: Peter James
description: A stalker ex-boyfriend who has been released from prison begins a campaign of terror against the woman who put him there.

title: Love You Dead
author: Peter James
description: A black widow who marries wealthy men and kills them targets a Brighton criminal, and Grace must stop her before she strikes again.

title: Night Prey
author: John Sandford
description: A serial killer targeting overweight women has returned after years and Davenport must find him before he finishes his list.

title: Mind Prey
author: John Sandford
description: A psychiatrist and her daughters are kidnapped by one of her former patients, and Davenport races to find them before the man kills.

title: Sudden Prey
author: John Sandford
description: A bank robber's wife is killed by police and he targets the cops' families in revenge, including Davenport's.

title: Chosen Prey
author: John Sandford
description: An art history professor is a serial killer who buries his victims in the Minnesota countryside, and Davenport finds the connection through a missing woman.

title: Naked Prey
author: John Sandford
description: A Black man and a white woman are found hanged in a tree in rural Minnesota and Davenport investigates a community with secrets to protect.

title: Broken Prey
author: John Sandford
description: A serial killer is recreating the murders of another killer who is locked in a maximum-security hospital, and the institutionalized man may be directing it.

title: Storm Prey
author: John Sandford
description: A pharmacy robbery at a hospital goes wrong and the robbers start killing witnesses while Davenport's wife operates on a baby upstairs.

title: Field of Prey
author: John Sandford
description: A buried cistern full of bodies is discovered in southern Minnesota and Davenport must find a killer who has been operating for decades.

title: Ocean Prey
author: John Sandford
description: Davenport and Virgil Flowers go to Florida to investigate a drug ring operating from offshore boats.

title: Open Season
author: C.J. Box
description: A Wyoming game warden finds a dead man with an endangered species in his cooler and the investigation threatens his family and career.

title: Savage Run
author: C.J. Box
description: An environmental activist is blown up and Joe Pickett investigates despite being told to stay out of it.

title: Winterkill
author: C.J. Box
description: A body in the forest on Christmas Eve leads Joe to a sovereign citizen compound and a conspiracy involving federal land management.

title: Trophy Hunt
author: C.J. Box
description: Mutilated animals and a human body are found near Joe's district and the cases connect to a serial killer and his mysterious friend Nate Romanowski.

title: Blue Heaven
author: C.J. Box
description: Two children witness a murder by retired LAPD cops who have settled in a corrupt Idaho town, and they must run through the wilderness to survive.

title: The Highway
author: C.J. Box
description: Two sisters vanish on a Montana highway and an investigator discovers a long-haul trucker has been hunting women for years.

title: Cold Granite
author: Stuart MacBride
description: A child's body is found in Aberdeen during a brutal winter and DS Logan McRae investigates while the city freezes and more children go missing.

title: Dying Light
author: Stuart MacBride
description: Prostitutes are being murdered in Aberdeen and Logan must navigate police politics and a media frenzy to find the killer.

title: Knots and Crosses
author: Ian Rankin
description: Edinburgh detective John Rebus investigates the kidnapping of girls while receiving mysterious anonymous letters connected to his SAS past.

title: Black and Blue
author: Ian Rankin
description: Rebus hunts a copycat of a real 1960s serial killer while investigating corrupt cops, oil money, and Scottish politics.

title: The Falls
author: Ian Rankin
description: A student vanishes and a doll in a tiny coffin is found, echoing the mysterious miniature coffins discovered on Arthur's Seat in 1836.

title: Resurrection Men
author: Ian Rankin
description: Rebus is sent to a police retraining course and goes undercover among fellow officers suspected of corruption.

title: A Song for the Dark Times
author: Ian Rankin
description: Rebus's daughter's partner disappears and Rebus investigates a murder in an isolated Scottish community while confronting his own past.

title: The Mermaids Singing
author: Val McDermid
description: A serial killer is targeting men in northern England and profiler Tony Hill must decode the killer's psychology while fighting his own demons.

title: The Wire in the Blood
author: Val McDermid
description: Tony Hill trains profiling recruits while a killer is abducting and murdering teenagers and the investigation brings Hill close to breaking.

title: A Place of Execution
author: Val McDermid
description: A girl vanishes from an isolated Derbyshire village in 1963 and a detective spends his career on the case until a journalist reopens it decades later.

title: The Distant Echo
author: Val McDermid
description: Four students who found a dying woman in 1978 Scotland are reunited twenty-five years later when someone starts killing them.

title: Broken Ground
author: Val McDermid
description: A body from WWII is discovered in a peat bog and Chief Inspector Karen Pirie connects it to a present-day disappearance.

title: Gallows View
author: Peter Robinson
description: Inspector Banks arrives in a Yorkshire market town and investigates a Peeping Tom, burglaries, and a brutal assault that may all be connected.

title: In a Dry Season
author: Peter Robinson
description: A drought reveals a submerged village and a skeleton, and Banks investigates a fifty-year-old murder connected to wartime secrets.

title: Aftermath
author: Peter Robinson
description: Police responding to a domestic violence call discover a serial killer's house of horrors, and Banks must find out if the wife knew.

title: Andy Catlett: Early Travels
author: Wendell Berry
description: A nine-year-old boy visits his grandparents' farms during Christmas 1943 and the memory becomes a meditation on the vanishing world of small-scale agriculture.

title: Horseman, Pass By
author: Larry McMurtry
description: A boy on a Texas ranch watches his grandfather's cattle destroyed by disease and his stepuncle's cruelty escalate in McMurtry's first novel, filmed as Hud.

title: Leaving Cheyenne
author: Larry McMurtry
description: Two men and the woman they both love tell the story of their lives in three parts across forty years of Texas ranching.

title: Anything for Billy
author: Larry McMurtry
description: A dime novelist travels west and befriends Billy the Kid, whose real story is nothing like the legends.

title: Buffalo Girls
author: Larry McMurtry
description: Calamity Jane writes letters to her daughter while the Wild West show circles the globe and the frontier vanishes.

title: SPQR
author: Mary Beard
description: A thousand years of Roman history from Romulus to Caracalla, told with wit and scholarship that demolishes every cliché about the ancient world.

title: Women & Power
author: Mary Beard
description: Two lectures on how Western civilization has silenced women for three thousand years, from Homer's Telemachus telling Penelope to shut up to modern Twitter.

title: Fooled by Randomness
author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb
description: Humans are terrible at understanding probability and mistake luck for skill, especially on Wall Street.

title: Skin in the Game
author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb
description: People who don't bear the consequences of their decisions make bad decisions—Taleb argues skin in the game is the foundation of fairness.

title: The Daily Stoic
author: Ryan Holiday
description: 366 meditations on Stoic philosophy—one for each day—drawn from Marcus Aurelius, Seneca, and Epictetus.

title: Discipline Is Destiny
author: Ryan Holiday
description: Self-control is the key to greatness, illustrated through Queen Elizabeth II, Lou Gehrig, Eisenhower, and other masters of temperance.

title: Courage Is Calling
author: Ryan Holiday
description: The Stoic virtue of courage examined through Florence Nightingale, Martin Luther King Jr., and Charles de Gaulle.

title: The Laws of Human Nature
author: Robert Greene
description: Eighteen laws governing human behavior—narcissism, envy, grandiosity—each illustrated through historical figures and actionable strategies.

title: The 33 Strategies of War
author: Robert Greene
description: Military strategy applied to everyday life: Napoleon's speed, Sun Tzu's deception, guerrilla flexibility—each principle illustrated through history.

title: So Good They Can't Ignore You
author: Cal Newport
description: 'Follow your passion' is terrible advice—instead build rare and valuable skills and the passion will follow.

title: A World Without Email
author: Cal Newport
description: The hyperactive hive mind of constant email is destroying productivity and Newport proposes radical alternatives.

title: Slow Productivity
author: Cal Newport
description: Do fewer things, work at a natural pace, and obsess over quality—Newport's antidote to burnout culture.

title: The Taming of the Queen
author: Philippa Gregory
description: Katherine Parr, Henry VIII's sixth and final wife, must hide her Protestant beliefs and her love for Thomas Seymour to survive.

title: The Last Tudor
author: Philippa Gregory
description: The three Grey sisters after Lady Jane Grey's execution—each imprisoned, exiled, or forced to marry by the Tudor crown.

title: Sharpe's Triumph
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Sharpe fights at the Battle of Assaye in India—Wellington's greatest victory—while a traitor in British ranks feeds information to the enemy.

title: Sharpe's Fortress
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Sharpe must storm a seemingly impregnable Indian fortress while dealing with a treacherous officer who wants him dead.

title: Sharpe's Trafalgar
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Sharpe sails home from India and finds himself aboard HMS Pucelle at the Battle of Trafalgar.

title: Sharpe's Havoc
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Sharpe is stranded behind French lines in Portugal and must lead a handful of riflemen through enemy territory to rejoin Wellington.

title: Sharpe's Escape
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: The French invade Portugal and Sharpe must fight his way out of a besieged town while protecting a fortune in gold.

title: Sharpe's Fury
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Sharpe races to protect compromising letters while the siege of Cádiz rages and a beautiful spy complicates everything.

title: Sharpe's Battle
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Sharpe must whip a useless regiment of Irish soldiers into shape before the Battle of Fuentes de Oñoro.

title: Sharpe's Company
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Sharpe leads the forlorn hope at the storming of Badajoz—the bloodiest and most controversial siege of the Peninsular War.

title: Sharpe's Sword
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Sharpe hunts a French spy in Salamanca while Wellington prepares for the battle that will break Napoleon's hold on Spain.

title: Sharpe's Enemy
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Deserters from both armies hold hostages in a mountain fortress, and Sharpe must rescue them through a Christmas blizzard.

title: Sharpe's Honour
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Sharpe is framed for murder and sentenced to hang while Napoleon's spy network tries to turn Spain against Wellington.

title: Sharpe's Regiment
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Sharpe returns to England to find where his regiment's replacements are disappearing and uncovers a criminal conspiracy in the army.

title: Sharpe's Siege
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Sharpe leads a raid on a French fortress in the Gironde and is trapped behind enemy lines as Napoleon's empire crumbles.

title: Sharpe's Revenge
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Sharpe is accused of stealing Napoleon's treasure after the Battle of Toulouse and must clear his name across France.

title: Sharpe's Devil
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: Sharpe travels to Chile to find a missing friend and crosses paths with Napoleon on St. Helena in the final Sharpe novel.

title: Sleepyhead
author: Mark Billingham
description: A killer deliberately induces locked-in syndrome in his victims, and DI Tom Thorne must catch him before the next woman is left conscious but paralyzed.

title: Scaredy Cat
author: Mark Billingham
description: Two killers are working together—one dominant, one submissive—and Thorne must separate them before the weaker one breaks free.

title: Lazybones
author: Mark Billingham
description: Released sex offenders are being murdered and Thorne faces the question of whether catching the killer is really the right thing to do.

title: The Blackhouse
author: Peter May
description: A detective returns to the Isle of Lewis to investigate a murder identical to one from his childhood and must confront the secrets he left behind.

title: The Lewis Man
author: Peter May
description: A body preserved in a peat bog is identified as a murdered man and the investigation connects to a case of dementia and lost identity.

title: The Chessmen
author: Peter May
description: A plane wreck in a loch reveals a body and Fin must solve the mystery while his own past on Lewis threatens to consume him.

title: Raven Black
author: Ann Cleeves
description: A teenage girl is found dead in the Shetland snow near the home of an outsider accused of murder decades ago, and Inspector Perez investigates.

title: White Nights
author: Ann Cleeves
description: A stranger hangs himself in a Shetland boathouse during a midsummer arts festival and Perez discovers no one knows who the dead man was.

title: Red Bones
author: Ann Cleeves
description: An archaeological dig on Whalsay unearths old bones and a new shooting death, connecting Viking history to modern island feuds.

title: Blue Lightning
author: Ann Cleeves
description: Perez takes his fiancée to Fair Isle to meet his parents and a researcher at the bird observatory is murdered during a storm.

title: The Crow Trap
author: Ann Cleeves
description: Three women working at a nature survey in Northumberland discover a body and DI Vera Stanhope investigates the remote community.

title: Telling Tales
author: Ann Cleeves
description: A woman convicted of murder is exonerated by DNA evidence and the real killer is still in the village where Vera grew up.

title: Hidden Depths
author: Ann Cleeves
description: Bodies decorated with flowers are found in pools of water across Northumberland and Vera must connect the artistic murders to a suspect.

title: The Crossing Places
author: Elly Griffiths
description: A forensic archaeologist on the Norfolk coast is asked by a detective to examine bones found in a marsh—they may belong to a missing child.

title: The Janus Stone
author: Elly Griffiths
description: A child's skeleton is found under a doorway during demolition and Ruth must determine if the burial is Roman or modern.

title: The House at Sea's End
author: Elly Griffiths
description: Coastal erosion reveals WWII bodies on the Norfolk shore and Ruth investigates while a storm threatens to wash away the evidence.

title: A Dying Fall
author: Elly Griffiths
description: Ruth inherits the research of a dead colleague who claimed to have found King Arthur's bones in Blackpool.

title: The Puppet Show
author: M.W. Craven
description: A serial killer is burning people alive in stone circles across Cumbria, and disgraced detective Washington Poe discovers he's on the victim list.

title: Black Summer
author: M.W. Craven
description: A man Poe sent to prison for murder is released when his alleged victim turns up alive—but she's been dead for a year.

title: The Botanist
author: M.W. Craven
description: A killer leaves pressed flowers at each crime scene and Poe and Tilly must decode the botanical messages before the next victim dies.

title: Fatherland
author: Robert Harris
description: A detective in a Berlin where Nazi Germany won WWII investigates a murder that leads to the regime's greatest secret: the Holocaust.

title: Enigma
author: Robert Harris
description: A Bletchley Park codebreaker races to crack a new U-boat cipher while a colleague's disappearance suggests a Soviet mole.

title: An Officer and a Spy
author: Robert Harris
description: The Dreyfus Affair told from the perspective of the intelligence officer who discovered the real traitor and was punished for it.

title: The Ghost
author: Robert Harris
description: A ghostwriter hired by a former British prime minister discovers the man's memoirs conceal a connection to the CIA.

title: Conclave
author: Robert Harris
description: The dean of the College of Cardinals manages the election of a new pope while secrets and conspiracies threaten to tear the Church apart.

title: Imperium
author: Robert Harris
description: Cicero's rise in Roman politics narrated by his slave secretary Tiro, from his first case to his election as consul.

title: Lustrum
author: Robert Harris
