description: Cicero is consul and must navigate Caesar, Pompey, and Crassus while the Catiline conspiracy threatens to destroy the Republic.

title: Dictator
author: Robert Harris
description: Cicero's final years as Caesar crosses the Rubicon and the Republic dies, ending in the orator's assassination on Antony's orders.

title: Every Dead Thing
author: John Connolly
description: A former NYPD detective whose wife and daughter were murdered hunts a serial killer called the Traveling Man across the American South.

title: Dark Hollow
author: John Connolly
description: Parker returns to Maine where a woman's body is found in the woods and the investigation connects to a century-old evil.

title: The Killing Kind
author: John Connolly
description: Parker investigates the suicide of a lawyer connected to a religious community and discovers a burial ground of children.

title: Jar City
author: Arnaldur Indriðason
description: An Icelandic detective investigates a murder connected to a decades-old rape case and the country's genetic database.

title: Silence of the Grave
author: Arnaldur Indriðason
description: Bones found on a Reykjavik hillside lead Erlendur to a WWII-era family destroyed by domestic violence.

title: Voices
author: Arnaldur Indriðason
description: A hotel doorman is stabbed to death during the Christmas season and Erlendur discovers the man was hiding from a terrible past.

title: Hypothermia
author: Arnaldur Indriðason
description: A woman found hanged at a summer cottage may have been investigating a drowning from thirty years ago.

title: The Ice Princess
author: Camilla Läckberg
description: A writer returns to her small Swedish town and discovers her childhood friend dead in a bathtub, the water frozen solid around her body.

title: The Preacher
author: Camilla Läckberg
description: Two skeletons are found alongside a fresh body in a forest clearing, and the investigation reaches back to a preacher's family thirty years ago.

title: The Stonecutter
author: Camilla Läckberg
description: A little girl is found drowned with ashes in her stomach, and the investigation uncovers a web of secrets in the close-knit community.

title: The Keeper of Lost Causes
author: Jussi Adler-Olsen
description: A Copenhagen detective banished to the basement cold case department reopens the disappearance of a politician and discovers she may still be alive.

title: The Absent One
author: Jussi Adler-Olsen
description: Carl Mørck investigates a twenty-year-old double murder connected to a group of boarding school elites who have been covering for each other.

title: A Conspiracy of Faith
author: Jussi Adler-Olsen

title: The Secret
author: Lee Child
description: A prequel: Reacher in 1992 investigates the suspicious deaths of scientists connected to a classified Cold War project.

title: Walk the Wire
author: David Baldacci
description: Decker investigates a murdered woman in a fracking boomtown and discovers the death connects to a military weapons facility.

title: Simply Lies
author: David Baldacci
description: A former detective turned data analyst for a PI firm is manipulated by a con woman who may be the most brilliant criminal she's ever faced.

title: The 6:20 Man
author: David Baldacci
description: A commuter who takes the 6:20 train to his Wall Street job discovers his boss murdered and the investigation connects to cybercrime and corruption.

title: The Match
author: Harlan Coben
description: A man who was abandoned as a baby uses a DNA testing service and discovers a match that leads to a decades-old kidnapping.

title: I Will Find You
author: Harlan Coben
description: A man in prison for his son's murder sees a photo suggesting his son is alive and escapes to find the truth.

title: Think Twice
author: Harlan Coben
description: A sports agent's DNA is found at a crime scene—but he's been dead for three years.

title: Girl, Forgotten
author: Karin Slaughter
description: A US Marshal investigates a forty-year-old murder of a teenage girl and discovers the case connects to powerful people who will kill to keep the secret.

title: Let Me In
author: Karin Slaughter
description: Sara and Will investigate a brutal crime in a wealthy community where everyone has something to hide.

title: Before I Met You
author: Lisa Jewell
description: A young woman inherits a house in London and discovers her grandmother led a wild life in 1920s Soho.

title: One Perfect Couple
author: Ruth Ware
description: Five couples are stranded on a reality TV island during a storm and start dying one by one.

title: Inheritance
author: Dani Shapiro
description: A DNA test reveals Shapiro's father was not her biological father—she was conceived via sperm donor—and she must reconstruct her entire identity.

title: Devotion
author: Dani Shapiro
description: A secular Jewish woman raised between orthodoxy and assimilation searches for spiritual meaning through yoga, Buddhism, and her own tradition.

title: Outline
author: Rachel Cusk
description: A writer teaches a course in Athens and listens to the stories of the people around her, effacing herself until only they remain.

title: Transit
author: Rachel Cusk
description: The writer renovates a London flat while encounters with builders, neighbors, and friends produce monologues about marriage, family, and change.

title: Kudos
author: Rachel Cusk
description: The writer travels to a European literary festival and listens to more stories—about feminism, art, national identity—each one reflecting something she won't say.

title: Antarctica
author: Claire Keegan
description: Fifteen stories set in rural Ireland—a woman marries a man for his farm, a priest questions his vocation, a girl discovers her father's affair—each one flawless.

title: Last Things
author: Jenny Offill
description: A girl raised by her naturalist mother in rural New England recounts their increasingly unstable life as her mother's mental health deteriorates.

title: Life Ceremony
author: Sayaka Murata
description: Twelve stories where Japanese society's norms are pushed to surreal extremes—eating the dead, making friends from human hair, living without gender.

title: Moshi Moshi
author: Banana Yoshimoto
description: A woman moves to Shimokitazawa after her father's death—possibly murder—and heals through the neighborhood's food, music, and quiet kindness.

title: Blue Bamboo
author: Osamu Dazai
description: A collection of Dazai's short fiction including fairy tale retellings and autobiographical pieces from his final years.

title: Black Earth
author: Timothy Snyder
description: The Holocaust as an ecological panic: Hitler believed Germans needed fertile land in the East and the Jews were obstacles to that vision.

title: Our Malady
author: Timothy Snyder
description: Snyder nearly dies from a botched surgery and uses the experience to argue that American healthcare is a symptom of a failing state.

title: The Language Instinct
author: Steven Pinker
description: Language is not culture but biology—a human instinct as natural as a spider's web—and Pinker proves it through children, pidgins, and brain injuries.

title: How the Mind Works
author: Steven Pinker
description: The mind is a system of computational organs designed by natural selection to solve the problems of our ancestors.

title: Rationality
author: Steven Pinker
description: Why smart people believe stupid things and how the tools of rational thinking can save us from conspiracy theories and tribal thinking.

title: Noise
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: Unwanted variability in human judgment—why two judges give different sentences for the same crime—is as damaging as bias and easier to fix.

title: The Bed of Procrustes
author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb
description: Philosophical aphorisms: Taleb at his most compressed, one-liners about knowledge, luck, modernity, and the difference between nerds and practitioners.

title: The Checklist Manifesto
author: Atul Gawande
description: Simple checklists can prevent catastrophic errors in surgery, aviation, and construction—Gawande shows why and how.

title: Better
author: Atul Gawande
description: What makes some doctors and hospitals better than others: performance, diligence, and the willingness to measure and improve.

title: Kingdom of the Cursed
author: Kerri Maniscalco
description: Emilia descends into the underworld to find her sister's killer among the demon princes and falls deeper into the Prince of Wrath's orbit.

title: Kingdom of the Feared
author: Kerri Maniscalco
description: Emilia discovers the truth about her identity and must choose between the demon prince she loves and the witches she was born to protect.

title: A Curse for True Love
author: Stephanie Garber
description: Evangeline must break a curse on the Prince of Hearts before it destroys him—and their love story—in the final Once Upon a Broken Heart novel.

title: The Ballad of Never After
author: Stephanie Garber
description: Evangeline hunts for the four items needed to break the curse while Jacks's motives become more mysterious and more dangerous.

title: The Ashes and the Star-Cursed King
author: Carissa Broadbent
description: Oraya and Raihn must navigate a civil war among the vampire houses while their marriage of political convenience becomes something real.

title: The Songbird and the Heart of Stone
author: Carissa Broadbent
description: A human musician enters a vampire tournament and discovers her magic connects to the very foundations of the vampire world.

title: Nightbane
author: Alex Aster
description: Isla must navigate the Wildling realm while her powers grow and the Nightshade curse threatens everyone she loves.

title: Skyshade
author: Alex Aster
description: The third Lightlark novel as Isla faces the consequences of her choices and the true threat to the island realm.

title: Somewhere Beyond the Sea
author: TJ Klune
description: The sequel to Cerulean Sea: Arthur and Linus face the government that wants to take the children away.

title: Leadership
author: Doris Kearns Goodwin
description: Lincoln, Teddy Roosevelt, FDR, and LBJ: four presidents examined for the qualities that made them effective leaders.

title: An Unfinished Love Story
author: Doris Kearns Goodwin
description: Goodwin and her dying husband Richard go through his boxes of papers from the Kennedy and Johnson years, reliving the 1960s together.

title: If You Come Softly
author: Jacqueline Woodson
description: A Black boy and a white Jewish girl fall in love at a Manhattan prep school, and the outside world threatens what they've found.

title: Miracle's Boys
author: Jacqueline Woodson
description: Three orphaned brothers in New York struggle to stay together and stay out of trouble after their mother's death and their oldest brother's return from juvenile detention.

title: The Comfort Book
author: Matt Haig
description: Short passages of comfort, wisdom, and reassurance for hard days—drawn from Haig's experience with depression and his reading.

title: Every Morning the Way Home Gets Longer and Longer
author: Fredrik Backman
description: A grandfather with dementia sits on a park bench with his grandson, and the bench shrinks as his memories fade.

title: The Book of Magic
author: Alice Hoffman
description: The final Practical Magic novel: the Owens family curse must be broken before it claims another generation, and the family gathers for one last fight.

title: These Precious Days
author: Ann Patchett
description: Essays on friendship, reading, lockdown, and the bookshop she opened in Nashville—Patchett's most personal writing.

title: When Will There Be Good News?
author: Kate Atkinson
description: A girl who survived her family's murder thirty years ago is connected to Jackson Brodie through a train crash and a missing woman.

title: Started Early, Took My Dog
author: Kate Atkinson
description: A retired policewoman impulsively buys a child from a sex worker, and Brodie investigates a cold case that connects to her decision.

title: Shrines of Gaiety
author: Kate Atkinson
description: A nightclub queen in 1926 London emerges from prison to reclaim her empire while a librarian and a detective pursue missing girls in Soho.

title: Man and His Symbols
author: Carl Jung
description: Jung's most accessible work, written for a general audience: the unconscious speaks through symbols in dreams, art, and myth.

title: Memories, Dreams, Reflections
author: Carl Jung
description: Jung's autobiography: his childhood visions, his break with Freud, his confrontation with the unconscious, and the development of his ideas.

title: The Red Book
author: Carl Jung
description: Jung's private journal of active imagination—paintings, calligraphy, and dialogues with figures from his unconscious—kept secret for nearly a century.

title: Modern Man in Search of a Soul
author: Carl Jung
description: Essays on dream analysis, the stages of life, psychology and literature, and the spiritual crisis of modern humanity.

title: The Archetypes and the Collective Unconscious
author: Carl Jung
description: Jung's theory of universal patterns in the human psyche—the Shadow, the Anima, the Self—drawn from myth, religion, and clinical experience.

title: On the Genealogy of Morality
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
description: Three essays tracing how 'good' and 'evil' were invented by the weak to restrain the strong, and how guilt became internalized as bad conscience.

title: The Birth of Tragedy
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
description: Greek tragedy was born from the tension between Apollonian order and Dionysian chaos, and its death killed Western culture's vitality.

title: The Gay Science
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
description: The book that first declares God is dead and introduces the eternal recurrence—Nietzsche at his most joyful and aphoristic.

title: Twilight of the Idols
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
description: A lightning summary of Nietzsche's entire philosophy—destroying every false idol from Socrates to Christianity in his most concentrated work.

title: Ecce Homo
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
description: Nietzsche's autobiography with chapter titles like 'Why I Am So Wise' and 'Why I Write Such Good Books'—written months before his collapse into madness.

title: Civilization and Its Discontents
author: Sigmund Freud
description: Happiness is impossible because civilization requires us to suppress our drives—Freud's bleakest and most brilliant cultural critique.

title: The Psychopathology of Everyday Life
author: Sigmund Freud
description: Slips of the tongue, forgotten names, and lost objects are not accidents but messages from the unconscious—the book that gave us 'Freudian slip.'

title: Beyond the Pleasure Principle
author: Sigmund Freud
description: Freud introduces the death drive: humans are drawn not only toward pleasure but toward destruction, repetition, and the inorganic state.

title: Fear and Trembling
author: Søren Kierkegaard
description: Abraham's willingness to sacrifice Isaac becomes the framework for examining faith as something beyond reason—the 'leap of faith' that defined existentialism.

title: Either/Or
author: Søren Kierkegaard
description: Two life views—the aesthetic (pleasure-seeking) and the ethical (duty-bound)—presented through the papers of two fictional authors, with no resolution.

title: The Sickness Unto Death
author: Søren Kierkegaard
description: Despair is the universal human condition—not knowing you have a self, not wanting the self you have, or not being the self you want to be.

title: The World as Will and Representation
author: Arthur Schopenhauer
description: Reality is blind, purposeless Will, and everything we see is representation—Schopenhauer's pessimistic masterpiece that influenced Nietzsche, Freud, and Wittgenstein.

title: Essays and Aphorisms
author: Arthur Schopenhauer
description: Selected writings on suffering, religion, women, suicide, and the art of being right—Schopenhauer at his most readable and most savage.

title: Discipline and Punish
author: Michel Foucault
description: How modern society shifted from public torture to prisons, schools, and hospitals that control bodies through surveillance—the panopticon as metaphor for everything.

title: The History of Sexuality, Volume 1
author: Michel Foucault
description: Sex was not repressed by the Victorians—it was produced as a discourse of power, and talking about it was always a form of control.

title: Madness and Civilization
author: Michel Foucault
description: How the West invented madness by separating 'reason' from 'unreason' and locking up everyone who didn't fit—the book that launched Foucault's career.

title: Tractatus Logico-Philosophicus
author: Ludwig Wittgenstein
description: Seven numbered propositions that attempt to show the limits of language and thought—'Whereof one cannot speak, thereof one must be silent.'

title: Philosophical Investigations
author: Ludwig Wittgenstein
description: Wittgenstein destroys his own earlier work and argues that meaning comes from use—language games, family resemblances, and the beetle in the box.

title: A History of Western Philosophy
author: Bertrand Russell
description: Every major philosopher from Thales to logical positivism, told with wit, clarity, and strong opinions by the man who helped invent modern logic.

title: The Problems of Philosophy
author: Bertrand Russell
description: A short, brilliant introduction to epistemology: how do we know anything? What is real? Is the table really there?

title: Why I Am Not a Christian
author: Bertrand Russell
description: Russell's famous lecture plus essays dismantling the arguments for God's existence and the moral authority of religion.

title: The Hero with a Thousand Faces
author: Joseph Campbell
description: All myths follow the same pattern—the hero's journey from departure through initiation to return—and Campbell maps the monomyth that influenced Star Wars and everything after.

title: The Power of Myth
author: Joseph Campbell
description: Campbell's conversations with Bill Moyers about mythology, meaning, and the need for stories in modern life—the companion to the PBS series.

title: The Masks of God: Primitive Mythology
author: Joseph Campbell
description: The first volume of Campbell's four-part study of world mythology, tracing the origins of myth in human biology and psychology.

title: The Way of Zen
author: Alan Watts
description: The history and practice of Zen Buddhism explained for Western readers by the man who made Eastern philosophy accessible to a generation.

title: The Book: On the Taboo Against Knowing Who You Are
author: Alan Watts
description: You are not a skin-encapsulated ego separate from the universe—you ARE the universe—and Watts explains why this isn't mystical but obvious.

title: The Wisdom of Insecurity
author: Alan Watts
description: The search for security is the source of anxiety—real peace comes from embracing uncertainty and living in the present.

title: The Art of Loving
author: Erich Fromm
description: Love is not a feeling but a practice requiring knowledge, effort, and discipline—and modern society makes it nearly impossible.

title: Escape from Freedom
author: Erich Fromm
description: Freedom is terrifying and people flee it into authoritarianism, conformity, or destructiveness—written as fascism consumed Europe.

title: To Have or to Be?
author: Erich Fromm
description: Two modes of existence—having (possessing, consuming) and being (experiencing, creating)—and why modern capitalism traps us in the first.

title: The Trouble with Being Born
author: Emil Cioran
description: Aphorisms on the catastrophe of existence—birth as the original sin, consciousness as a disease—written with a beauty that contradicts the despair.

title: A Short History of Decay
author: Emil Cioran
description: Cioran's first book in French: Western civilization is decaying and the only honest response is lucid despair, delivered in gorgeous prose.

title: On the Heights of Despair
author: Emil Cioran
description: Written at 22 during a period of insomnia: agonized meditations on suffering, death, and the failure of reason that established Cioran's voice.

title: Letters from a Stoic
author: Seneca
description: One hundred and twenty-four letters to a friend covering how to live, how to die, and how to endure everything in between—the most practical Stoic text.

title: On the Shortness of Life
author: Seneca
description: Life is long enough if you stop wasting it—Seneca's essay on time management, written two thousand years before productivity culture.

title: Discourses and Selected Writings
author: Epictetus
description: A former slave's teachings on what we can and cannot control—the foundation of Stoic practice that influenced Marcus Aurelius and modern CBT.

title: Tao Te Ching
author: Lao Tzu
description: Eighty-one brief chapters on the Way—governing, living, and being—in the foundational text of Taoism that has never stopped being relevant.

title: The Art of War
author: Sun Tzu
description: The oldest military treatise in the world: deception, terrain, espionage, and the supreme art of winning without fighting.

title: The Analects
author: Confucius
description: Sayings and dialogues of Confucius on virtue, governance, education, and the proper relationships between people—the text that shaped East Asian civilization.

title: The Book of Five Rings
author: Miyamoto Musashi
description: A samurai who fought sixty duels and never lost writes five chapters on strategy, timing, and the Way of the warrior.

title: Gravity and Grace
author: Simone Weil
description: Notebooks of a mystic philosopher who starved herself to death in solidarity with occupied France—on attention, affliction, and the void where God enters.

title: The Need for Roots
author: Simone Weil
description: Written for the Free French government: what a just society needs—community, truth, order, and the obligations that connect people to place.

title: The Consolations of Philosophy
author: Alain de Botton
description: Six philosophers—Socrates, Epicurus, Seneca, Montaigne, Schopenhauer, Nietzsche—applied to everyday problems: unpopularity, poverty, heartbreak.

title: How Proust Can Change Your Life
author: Alain de Botton
description: Practical lessons from Proust on friendship, suffering, reading, and paying attention, extracted from the longest novel ever written.

title: The Architecture of Happiness
author: Alain de Botton
description: Buildings affect how we feel and who we are, and de Botton examines why we find some spaces beautiful and others oppressive.

title: Status Anxiety
author: Alain de Botton
description: Why we care so much about what others think of us and how philosophy, art, Christianity, and bohemia offer cures.

title: Manufacturing Consent
author: Noam Chomsky
description: The mass media serves as propaganda for powerful interests through five filters that determine what becomes news—Chomsky and Herman's foundational media critique.

title: Who Rules the World?
author: Noam Chomsky
description: American foreign policy from the Cold War to the War on Terror examined as a system of global domination.

title: Understanding Power
author: Noam Chomsky
description: Edited transcripts of Chomsky's discussions on politics, media, and activism—his most accessible and wide-ranging work.

title: The Burnout Society
author: Byung-Chul Han
description: Modern society has shifted from discipline to achievement, and the result is depression, ADHD, and burnout—we exploit ourselves.

title: The Transparency Society
author: Byung-Chul Han
description: The demand for total transparency—in politics, relationships, and the self—destroys trust, mystery, and the possibility of genuine communication.

title: Saving Beauty
author: Byung-Chul Han
description: Beauty is disappearing from art and life, replaced by the smooth and the polished, and only negativity and otherness can restore it.

title: Eichmann in Jerusalem
author: Hannah Arendt
description: Arendt covers the trial of Adolf Eichmann and coins 'the banality of evil'—he was not a monster but a bureaucrat who didn't think.

title: Between Past and Future
author: Hannah Arendt
description: Eight exercises in political thought on authority, freedom, education, and truth—Arendt at her most accessible.

title: The Sublime Object of Ideology
author: Slavoj Žižek
description: Žižek uses Lacan and Hegel to explain how ideology works through enjoyment—we know capitalism is broken and we do it anyway because it feels good.

title: Violence
author: Slavoj Žižek
description: The violence we see on the news is nothing compared to the systemic violence built into capitalism and language itself.

title: Living in the End Times
author: Slavoj Žižek
description: Global capitalism is approaching its end—and Žižek maps the five stages of grief (denial, anger, bargaining, depression, acceptance) onto our political moment.

title: Imperial Bedrooms
author: Bret Easton Ellis
description: Clay returns to Los Angeles twenty-five years after Less Than Zero and finds the same people doing the same things—only now the violence is real and aimed at him.

title: White
author: Bret Easton Ellis
description: Ellis's essay collection on living through the Trump era, the death of transgression, and why his generation's freedom is being policed by millennials.

title: The Shards
author: Bret Easton Ellis
description: A seventeen-year-old Ellis in 1981 LA becomes obsessed with a new student at his school while a serial killer called the Trawler stalks the city.

title: The Prague Orgy
author: Philip Roth
description: Zuckerman travels to Communist Prague to retrieve a dead writer's Yiddish manuscript and is caught in a trap of Czech absurdity and state surveillance.

title: The Great American Novel
author: Philip Roth
description: A fictional third major baseball league is erased from history in Roth's wildest, most Swiftian satire of American mythology.

title: In Dubious Battle
author: John Steinbeck
description: Communist organizers lead a fruit pickers' strike in California and idealism collides with violence in Steinbeck's most politically charged novel.

title: Sweet Thursday
author: John Steinbeck
description: Doc returns to Cannery Row after the war and the bums scheme to set him up with a woman in the comic sequel.

title: The Log from the Sea of Cortez
author: John Steinbeck
description: Steinbeck and marine biologist Ed Ricketts collect specimens in the Gulf of California, and the journey becomes a meditation on ecology, philosophy, and friendship.

title: The City and Its Uncertain Walls
author: Haruki Murakami
description: A man who lost a woman to a walled city of dreams becomes a librarian in a small town where the boundary between real and imaginary blurs—Murakami's latest and most personal novel.

title: Men Without Women
author: Haruki Murakami
description: Seven stories of men who have lost women—through death, breakup, or misunderstanding—each one a quiet study of loneliness and memory.

title: Underground
author: Haruki Murakami
description: Interviews with survivors of the 1995 Tokyo subway sarin gas attack, plus members of the Aum Shinrikyo cult—Murakami's most important nonfiction.

title: Hear the Wind Sing
author: Haruki Murakami
description: A college student recalls a summer of beer, jazz, and a girl with nine fingers in Murakami's debut novella.

title: Diary
author: Chuck Palahniuk
description: A waitress on a resort island discovers her comatose husband's construction projects are bricked-up rooms containing messages, and the island's history is darker than she knew.

title: Damned
author: Chuck Palahniuk
description: A thirteen-year-old girl in Hell—think The Breakfast Club meets Dante—navigates the afterlife while her celebrity parents hold séances.

title: Pygmy
author: Chuck Palahniuk
description: A foreign exchange student is actually a terrorist sleeper agent, and his fractured English narration of suburban America is both hilarious and disturbing.

title: Tell-All
author: Chuck Palahniuk
description: A loyal personal assistant to a fading Hollywood star tries to protect her from a younger man who may be after her memoirs—or her life.

title: The Bee Sting
author: Paul Murray
description: An Irish family falls apart as the father's business fails, the mother gambles online, the daughter applies to college, and the son radicalized online—a tragicomedy of modern ruin.

title: Martyr!
author: Kaveh Akbar
description: An Iranian-American orphan obsessed with martyrdom searches for meaning through art, addiction, and a dying painter at the Brooklyn Museum.

title: Night Watch
author: Jayne Anne Phillips
description: A Civil War nurse and her patients at a West Virginia asylum, and the woman who sheltered her—spanning the war and its aftermath in Phillips's Pulitzer winner.

title: Holly
author: Stephen King
description: Private investigator Holly Gibney takes a missing persons case that leads to a retired couple with a monstrous secret.

title: Birnam Wood
author: Eleanor Catton
description: A guerrilla gardening collective in New Zealand encounters a billionaire doomsday prepper, and their idealism collides with his ruthlessness.

title: The Luminaries
author: Eleanor Catton
description: Twelve men meet in a New Zealand goldfields hotel in 1866 and their stories interlock through an astrological structure spanning a year.

title: Western Lane
author: Chetna Maroo
description: A grieving girl finds solace in squash—the rhythm of the ball, the geometry of the court—as her family struggles after her mother's death.

title: Old God's Time
author: Sebastian Barry
description: A retired Irish detective is drawn back into a case involving clerical abuse and must confront the truth about his own family.

title: North Woods
author: Daniel Mason
description: A single house in New England passes through centuries of inhabitants—Puritans, Revolutionary soldiers, transcendentalists, artists—as the forest reclaims the land.

title: All Fours
author: Miranda July
description: A woman drives from LA to New York and instead checks into a motel thirty minutes from home and redesigns the room while having an affair that rewires her understanding of desire and aging.

title: The Anxious Generation
author: Jonathan Haidt
description: Smartphones and social media rewired childhood after 2012, causing a mental health crisis among teens—and Haidt proposes concrete solutions.

title: Doppelganger
author: Naomi Klein
description: Klein is constantly confused with Naomi Wolf, who has gone down a conspiracy rabbit hole, and the mix-up becomes a meditation on doubles, mirrors, and the far right.

title: Nightbitch
author: Rachel Yoder
description: A stay-at-home mother suspects she is literally turning into a dog—growing fur, craving raw meat—and the transformation may be the most honest thing that's happened to her.

title: Vladimir
author: Julia May Jonas
description: A college professor whose husband is accused of sexual misconduct becomes obsessed with a younger male colleague in a campus novel about desire and power.

title: Slow Horses
author: Mick Herron
description: MI5 agents who screwed up badly enough to be banished to Slough House—a dumping ground for failures—accidentally uncover a real plot.

title: Dead Lions
author: Mick Herron
description: A Cold War spy is found dead on a bus and the Slow Horses investigate while their boss Lamb plays his own game.

title: Real Tigers
author: Mick Herron
description: A Slow Horse is kidnapped and the ransom is a classified file—someone is using the failures for a power play inside MI5.

title: Spook Street
author: Mick Herron
description: A bombing in a London shopping center connects to Lamb's past and the Slow Horses must stop a conspiracy within the intelligence services.

title: London Rules
author: Mick Herron
description: A serial killer is targeting random Londoners and the Slow Horses are dragged into a case that reaches 10 Downing Street.

title: Joe Country
author: Mick Herron
description: A former Slow Horse has gone rogue and is assassinating intelligence agents across Europe.

title: Slough House
author: Mick Herron
description: The past catches up with every Slow Horse as old secrets surface and new threats emerge from within the Service.

title: Bad Actors
author: Mick Herron
description: A think tank advisor disappears and Lamb must navigate Downing Street politics while one of his agents is undercover inside Number 10.

title: The Secret Hours
author: Mick Herron
description: An inquiry into intelligence service failures from 1994 Berlin to the present connects to Lamb's earliest days and the Slow Horses' origins.

title: Same Bed Different Dreams
author: Ed Park
description: A Korean-American editor discovers his publishing house may be a front for a secret organization founded in 1919 to restore Korean independence.

title: The Ministry of Time
author: Kaliane Bradley
description: A government project plucks people from the past and assigns them handlers in the present—and a woman falls for an 1847 Arctic explorer.

title: Colored Television
author: Danzy Senna
description: A mixed-race novelist's career is stalling and her marriage is fraying when she gets a chance to sell a TV show—if she'll perform her racial identity for Hollywood.

title: My Friends
author: Hisham Matar
description: Three Libyan students in 1980s London are forever changed by a protest at their embassy where police and diplomats open fire.

title: Playground
author: Richard Powers
description: Three lives connected across decades converge on an atoll in the Pacific where AI, climate change, and the ocean's mysteries meet.

title: Help Wanted
author: Adelle Waldman
description: Workers at a big-box store navigate the gig economy, student debt, and corporate indifference in a novel about labor and dignity.

title: The God of the Woods
author: Liz Moore
description: A girl vanishes from an Adirondacks summer camp in 1975 and the investigation connects to her brother's disappearance fourteen years earlier.

title: There's Always This Year
author: Hanif Abdurraqib
description: Basketball, Ohio, and Black American life woven through the metaphor of LeBron James and the pull of home.

title: Patriot
author: Alexei Navalny
description: The Russian opposition leader's memoir, completed before his death in prison—his fight against Putin and his vision for a free Russia.

title: Sunrise on the Reaping
author: Suzanne Collins
description: The 50th Hunger Games—the Second Quarter Quell that sent Haymitch into the arena.

title: Eruption
author: Michael Crichton
description: Crichton's unfinished novel about a Hawaiian volcanic eruption completed by James Patterson, mixing science and catastrophe.

title: How to Fail at Flirting
author: Denise Williams
description: A tenure-track professor takes a chance on an exciting stranger, navigating past trauma and new desire.

title: The Fastest Way to Fall
author: Denise Williams
description: A plus-size journalist reviews a fitness app and falls for its CEO, finding love beyond body expectations.

title: Do You Take This Man
author: Denise Williams
description: Two wedding planners with opposite styles are forced to work together after their clients ceremony goes off the rails.

title: Technically Yours
author: Denise Williams
description: A woman starting over after prison and a tech CEO cross paths in a romance about second chances.

title: The Queer Principles of Kit Webb
author: Cat Sebastian
description: A retired highwayman is drawn into one last heist by a charming aristocrat in Georgian London.

title: We Could Be So Good
author: Cat Sebastian
description: Two newspapermen in 1950s New York navigate careers, family expectations, and a forbidden romance.

title: You Should Be So Lucky
author: Cat Sebastian
description: A washed-up journalist assigned to cover a struggling baseball player in 1960 finds love amid professional failure.

title: The Magpie Lord
author: KJ Charles
description: A disgraced earl returns from Shanghai to find someone is trying to kill him with magic.

title: A Case of Possession
author: KJ Charles
description: Lord Crane and Stephen Day face a rat infestation with dark magical roots and their growing feelings.

title: Flight of Magpies
author: KJ Charles
description: Stephen and Lucien confront a rogue practitioner threatening London while protecting their relationship.

title: Think of England
author: KJ Charles
description: An injured veteran investigates treason at a country house party and finds an unlikely lover.

title: Proper English
