title: The Aquitaine Progression
author: Robert Ludlum
description: A military conspiracy to take over Western democracies—one lawyer stumbles onto the plot and runs.

title: The Day of the Jackal
author: Frederick Forsyth
description: A professional assassin is hired to kill Charles de Gaulle—the police race to identify him before it's too late.

title: The Odessa File
author: Frederick Forsyth
description: A journalist hunts a former SS officer protected by a secret organization of ex-Nazis—based on real events.

title: The Dogs of War
author: Frederick Forsyth
description: A mining corporation hires mercenaries to overthrow an African government—meticulous, clinical, terrifying.

title: The Fourth Protocol
author: Frederick Forsyth
description: The Soviets plan to detonate a nuclear bomb in England to swing an election—one MI5 agent figures it out.

title: The Fist of God
author: Frederick Forsyth
description: A spy behind Iraqi lines during the Gulf War discovers Saddam has a supergun—and a nuclear weapon.

title: The Godwulf Manuscript
author: Robert B. Parker
description: Spenser's first case—a stolen medieval manuscript at a Boston university leads to murder and the counterculture.

title: God Save the Child
author: Robert B. Parker
description: A missing teenager in the suburbs—Spenser discovers the boy's disappearance is connected to organized crime.

title: Mortal Stakes
author: Robert B. Parker
description: A Red Sox pitcher may be throwing games—Spenser investigates and finds a wife with a dark past.

title: Promised Land
author: Robert B. Parker
description: A woman walks out on her husband—Spenser finds her mixed up with radical feminists and loan sharks.

title: The Judas Goat
author: Robert B. Parker
description: Spenser and Hawk hunt terrorists across Europe—a bombing survivor wants revenge.

title: Looking for Rachel Wallace
author: Robert B. Parker
description: Spenser is hired to protect a feminist author from death threats—she fires him, then she vanishes.

title: Early Autumn
author: Robert B. Parker
description: A divorced couple uses their son as a weapon—Spenser takes the boy and teaches him to be a man.

title: A Catskill Eagle
author: Robert B. Parker
description: Hawk is in prison and Susan is held captive—Spenser wages war against an arms dealer to free them both.

title: The Burglar in the Closet
author: Lawrence Block
description: Burglar Bernie Rhodenbarr breaks into an apartment and finds a dead body—now he has to solve the murder to clear himself.

title: The Burglar Who Liked to Quote Kipling
author: Lawrence Block
description: Bernie steals a rare Kipling book and ends up framed for murder—again.

title: Eight Million Ways to Die
author: Lawrence Block
description: A prostitute asks Matt Scudder for help—two days later she's dead. Scudder hunts her killer while battling alcoholism.

title: When the Sacred Ginmill Closes
author: Lawrence Block
description: Scudder investigates a robbery at a bar where he used to drink—the past and present blur in the midnight world.

title: A Dance at the Slaughterhouse
author: Lawrence Block
description: Scudder hunts a couple who make snuff films—the darkest Scudder novel.

title: A Walk Among the Tombstones
author: Lawrence Block
description: A drug dealer's wife is kidnapped and murdered—Scudder tracks the killers through Brooklyn's underworld.

title: A Long Line of Dead Men
author: Lawrence Block
description: Members of a men's club are dying at an alarming rate—Scudder investigates a pattern spanning decades.

title: Hit Man
author: Lawrence Block
description: Keller is a professional assassin who collects stamps and sees a therapist—darkly funny hitman stories.

title: Indemnity Only
author: Sara Paretsky
description: V.I. Warshawski's first case—a missing woman, a dead man, and a union fraud in Chicago.

title: Deadlock
author: Sara Paretsky
description: Warshawski investigates her cousin's death at a Great Lakes shipping company—corporate murder.

title: Killing Orders
author: Sara Paretsky
description: A nun asks Warshawski to investigate stock fraud—the trail leads to the Vatican and the mob.

title: Bitter Medicine
author: Sara Paretsky
description: A teenage girl dies after being turned away from a hospital—Warshawski takes on the medical establishment.

title: Blood Shot
author: Sara Paretsky
description: Warshawski returns to her South Chicago roots to investigate a childhood friend's mother's death—toxic waste and coverups.

title: Fer-de-Lance
author: Rex Stout
description: Nero Wolfe's first case—an orchid-growing genius detective and his legman Archie Goodwin investigate a poisoning.

title: The League of Frightened Men
author: Rex Stout
description: A group of men who caused a college accident are being murdered one by one—Wolfe solves it from his brownstone.

title: Too Many Cooks
author: Rex Stout
description: Wolfe attends a gourmet chef competition—when a chef is murdered, he must actually leave his chair to investigate.

title: Some Buried Caesar
author: Rex Stout
description: A prize bull, a beautiful woman, and a murder at a country estate—Archie narrates with his usual wisecracking charm.

title: The Doorbell Rang
author: Rex Stout
description: A woman hires Wolfe to stop the FBI from harassing her—Nero Wolfe takes on J. Edgar Hoover.

title: The Girl You Left Behind
author: Jojo Moyes
description: A painting connects two women across a century—one in WWI France, one in modern London.

title: Someone Else's Shoes
author: Jojo Moyes
description: Two women accidentally swap gym bags—a wealthy trophy wife and a middle-aged woman losing everything.

title: Only Time Will Tell
author: Jeffrey Archer
description: A boy from the Bristol docks discovers his father may not be who he thinks—the first Clifton Chronicles.

title: The Sins of the Father
author: Jeffrey Archer
description: Harry Clifton travels to America under a dead man's name—the truth about his father haunts him.

title: Best Kept Secret
author: Jeffrey Archer
description: Harry returns to England as his wife builds a business empire—enemies from the past conspire against them.

title: Be Careful What You Wish For
author: Jeffrey Archer
description: The Cliftons face a hostile takeover of their shipping company—business war meets family drama.

title: Mightier Than the Sword
author: Jeffrey Archer
description: Harry's new novel is a bestseller—but a secret from the past could destroy everything he's built.

title: A Judgement in Stone
author: Ruth Rendell
description: The first sentence tells you who dies and who did it—the genius is in the why. A housekeeper's illiteracy leads to mass murder.

title: A Demon in My View
author: Ruth Rendell
description: A seemingly mild man keeps a mannequin in his cellar—when a new tenant takes the basement, the killing starts.

title: From Doon with Death
author: Ruth Rendell
description: Inspector Wexford's first case—a housewife is murdered, and love letters from a mysterious admirer surface.

title: A Guilty Thing Surprised
author: Ruth Rendell
description: A wealthy woman is found murdered in the woods—Wexford suspects her husband, her brother, or the gardener.

title: No More Dying Then
author: Ruth Rendell
description: Two children go missing—one is found dead, the other might still be alive. Wexford races against time.

title: A Great Deliverance
author: Elizabeth George
description: Inspector Lynley and Sergeant Havers investigate a decapitation in a Yorkshire village—class tension between the two detectives.

title: Payment in Blood
author: Elizabeth George
description: A playwright is stabbed with a knife from the kitchen—everyone in the Scottish country house is a suspect.

title: Well-Schooled in Murder
author: Elizabeth George
description: A boy from an elite boarding school is found dead—Lynley and Havers confront institutional abuse.

title: A Suitable Vengeance
author: Elizabeth George
description: Lynley brings his fiancée to his family estate in Cornwall—a journalist is murdered and old family secrets surface.

title: For the Sake of Elena
author: Elizabeth George
description: A deaf Cambridge student is murdered during her morning run—the investigation reveals hidden lives.

title: The Dirty Duck
author: Martha Grimes
description: Superintendent Jury investigates murders near Stratford-upon-Avon—each victim connected to a Shakespeare play.

title: The Man with a Load of Mischief
author: Martha Grimes
description: Bodies appear at pubs in a Yorkshire village—Jury investigates while the locals drink and gossip.

title: The Old Fox Deceiv'd
author: Martha Grimes
description: A woman claiming to be a long-lost heiress is murdered—Jury travels to a fishing village on the Yorkshire coast.

title: The Anodyne Necklace
author: Martha Grimes
description: A severed finger leads Jury to a village where a woman was murdered—and everyone has a secret.

title: Last Bus to Woodstock
author: Colin Dexter
description: A girl is murdered after hitching a ride—Inspector Morse's first case in Oxford.

title: Last Seen Wearing
author: Colin Dexter
description: A schoolgirl vanishes—two years later, Morse thinks he knows what happened, but the evidence keeps shifting.

title: The Silent World of Nicholas Quinn
author: Colin Dexter
description: A deaf examiner is poisoned during a foreign exam syndicate meeting—Morse investigates academic corruption.

title: Service of All the Dead
author: Colin Dexter
description: Two murders in an Oxford church—Morse suspects the vicar knows more than he's telling.

title: The Dead of Jericho
author: Colin Dexter
description: A woman Morse fancies is found dead in her Jericho home—suicide or murder?

title: The Riddle of the Third Mile
author: Colin Dexter
description: A headless body in the Oxford canal and a missing don—Morse connects them through a decades-old grudge.

title: The Cater Street Hangman
author: Anne Perry
description: Women are being strangled in Victorian London—Charlotte Pitt and her police inspector husband investigate.

title: Callander Square
author: Anne Perry
description: Two dead babies are found buried in a fashionable London square—Pitt investigates the upper class.

title: The Face of a Stranger
author: Anne Perry
description: William Monk wakes in hospital with no memory—and discovers he's a detective investigating a murder.

title: A Dangerous Mourning
author: Anne Perry
description: A woman is stabbed in her own bed—the family insists a burglar did it, but Monk suspects one of them.

title: A Morbid Taste for Bones
author: Ellis Peters
description: Brother Cadfael investigates a murder during a quest for relics in 12th-century Wales—the first medieval mystery.

title: One Corpse Too Many
author: Ellis Peters
description: After the siege of Shrewsbury, Cadfael finds an extra body among the executed prisoners.

title: Monk's-Hood
author: Ellis Peters
description: A wealthy donor to the abbey is poisoned with monk's-hood—Cadfael investigates while tending his herb garden.

title: The Leper of Saint Giles
author: Ellis Peters
description: A forced marriage and a murder at a leper hospital—Cadfael champions the helpless.

title: The Virgin in the Ice
author: Ellis Peters
description: A frozen body in the woods and two missing children—Cadfael searches through a harsh winter.

title: A Man Lay Dead
author: Ngaio Marsh
description: A game of murder at an English country house turns real—Inspector Alleyn's first case.

title: Artists in Crime
author: Ngaio Marsh
description: A model is murdered at an artists' colony—Alleyn falls for a painter who may be involved.

title: Death in a White Tie
author: Ngaio Marsh
description: A blackmailer is strangled at a London ball—Alleyn investigates high society at its most murderous.

title: The Franchise Affair
author: Josephine Tey
description: Two women are accused of kidnapping a girl—a lawyer believes them, but the evidence is damning.

title: The Daughter of Time
author: Josephine Tey
description: A bedridden detective investigates whether Richard III really murdered the princes in the Tower—from his hospital bed.

title: The Singing Sands
author: Josephine Tey
description: Inspector Grant finds a dead man on a train—a poem in his pocket leads to the Scottish Highlands.

title: Brat Farrar
author: Josephine Tey
description: An impostor claims to be the long-lost heir to an estate—but the real heir may have been murdered.

title: The Moon-Spinners
author: Mary Stewart
description: A young woman on holiday in Crete stumbles onto a murder—romantic suspense in the Greek islands.

title: Nine Coaches Waiting
author: Mary Stewart
description: A governess at a French chateau suspects someone is trying to kill her young charge.

title: The Crystal Cave
author: Mary Stewart
description: Merlin's story from boyhood—raised as a bastard in Dark Age Wales, discovering his prophetic gifts.

title: The Hollow Hills
author: Mary Stewart
description: Merlin guides the young Arthur to the throne—hiding him, teaching him, preparing him for Camelot.

title: The Last Enchantment
author: Mary Stewart
description: Arthur rules and Merlin's power wanes—betrayal, magic, and the building of Camelot.

title: Circle of Friends
author: Maeve Binchy
description: Two girls from a small Irish town go to Dublin for university—friendship, love, and betrayal.

title: Tara Road
author: Maeve Binchy
description: An Irish woman and an American swap homes—each finds clarity in the other's life.

title: Evening Class
author: Maeve Binchy
description: Students in a Dublin Italian class—each brings a story, a problem, and a reason for being there.

title: A Week in Winter
author: Maeve Binchy
description: A restored hotel on the wild Irish coast—each guest arrives carrying something they need to leave behind.

title: Deadheads
author: Reginald Hill
description: A mild accountant's enemies keep dying—Dalziel and Pascoe investigate the most polite serial killer in Yorkshire.

title: A Clubbable Woman
author: Reginald Hill
description: A rugby player's wife is found bludgeoned—the first Dalziel and Pascoe, introducing the fat bully and the sensitive graduate.

title: An Advancement of Learning
author: Reginald Hill
description: A skeleton under a college statue—Dalziel and Pascoe investigate academia's dirty laundry.

title: On Beulah Height
author: Reginald Hill
description: A drought reveals a drowned village—and a child who vanished fifteen years ago is linked to new disappearances.

title: Bones and Silence
author: Reginald Hill
description: Dalziel witnesses a killing but can't prove it happened—meanwhile a mystery play becomes a real-life drama.

title: The Ice House
author: Minette Walters
description: A decomposed body in an ice house—three women in the manor are the obvious suspects, but nothing is obvious.

title: The Sculptress
author: Minette Walters
description: A journalist visits a woman in prison for a double murder—the convicted woman may be innocent, or she may be manipulating.

title: The Scold's Bridle
author: Minette Walters
description: An elderly woman is found dead in her bath wearing a medieval torture device—three women inherit and all are suspects.

title: The Echo
author: Minette Walters
description: A homeless man dies in a garage—a journalist discovers he was once a wealthy businessman. Who destroyed him?

title: Roseanna
author: Maj Sjöwall
description: A woman's body is found in a canal lock—Martin Beck's first case. The book that invented Scandinavian noir.

title: The Man Who Went Up in Smoke
author: Maj Sjöwall
description: A journalist vanishes in Budapest—Beck investigates behind the Iron Curtain during a heatwave.

title: The Man on the Balcony
author: Maj Sjöwall
description: A child killer stalks Stockholm's parks—Beck races to stop him before the next victim.

title: The Laughing Policeman
author: Maj Sjöwall
description: Eight people are shot dead on a Stockholm bus—including one of Beck's detectives. Who was the target?

title: The Fire Engine That Disappeared
author: Maj Sjöwall
description: A building explodes and a man is found dead—Beck investigates a case that connects to a drug ring.

title: The Abominable Man
author: Maj Sjöwall
description: A police captain is butchered in his hospital bed—the killer is targeting cops, and Beck is next.

title: The Name of the Game Is a Kidnapping
author: Keigo Higashino
description: An ad executive kidnaps a CEO's daughter for ransom—but nothing about the crime goes as planned.

title: Six Four
author: Hideo Yokoyama
description: A PR officer in a Japanese police department investigates a cold kidnapping case—bureaucracy as thriller.

title: The Tokyo Zodiac Murders
author: Soji Shimada
description: An alchemist's will demands his daughters be dismembered to create the perfect woman—forty years later, a detective solves it.

title: The Lady Killer
author: Masako Togawa
description: Women in a Tokyo apartment building are connected to the same serial killer—Japanese noir from a transgender author.

title: Alex
author: Pierre Lemaitre
description: A woman is kidnapped and locked in a cage—Commandant Verhoeven investigates, but the victim has her own secrets.

title: Irène
author: Pierre Lemaitre
description: A serial killer recreates murders from famous crime novels—Verhoeven investigates until the case destroys him.

title: Camille
author: Pierre Lemaitre
description: Verhoeven's partner is attacked by a robber—the detective goes rogue to find him before the justice system lets him escape.

title: Au revoir là-haut
author: Pierre Lemaitre
description: Two WWI survivors pull off a massive fraud on war memorials—Goncourt Prize winner.

title: Crocodile on the Sandbank
author: Elizabeth Peters
description: A Victorian Egyptologist and her companion investigate a mummy walking at night in the desert—the first Amelia Peabody.

title: The Curse of the Pharaohs
author: Elizabeth Peters
description: A tomb excavation is plagued by a curse—Amelia suspects murder rather than supernatural vengeance.

title: The Mummy Case
author: Elizabeth Peters
description: Amelia and Emerson investigate thefts at an archaeological dig—someone is after a painted mummy case.

title: Lion in the Valley
author: Elizabeth Peters
description: Amelia's nemesis Sethos returns—murders, disguises, and romance along the Nile.

title: The Deeds of the Disturber
author: Elizabeth Peters
description: A night watchman dies at the British Museum—Amelia investigates Egyptian artifacts and London intrigue.

title: A Room Full of Bones
author: Elly Griffiths
description: A museum curator dies during a ceremony—Ruth examines bones that connect to slavery and Indigenous remains.

title: Dying Fall
author: Elly Griffiths
description: Ruth's old friend dies—his final letter warns of a dangerous discovery at an archaeological dig in Lancashire.

title: The Word Is Murder
author: Anthony Horowitz
description: A woman arranges her own funeral—six hours later she's murdered. Horowitz writes himself into the detective story.

title: A Necessary End
author: Peter Robinson
description: A policeman is stabbed during an anti-nuclear protest—Banks must find the killer among the demonstrators.

title: The Hanging Valley
author: Peter Robinson
description: A body is found in a hidden valley—the dead man was from the village but had been missing for years.

title: Past Reason Hated
author: Peter Robinson
description: A young woman is found dead on Christmas Eve—Banks discovers she had recently left her husband for a woman.

title: Piece of My Heart
author: Peter Robinson
description: A body at a 1969 rock festival connects to a modern murder—Banks investigates across two timelines.

title: Voice of the Violin
author: Andrea Camilleri
description: A beautiful woman is murdered in her home—Montalbano falls for the dead woman's image while hunting the killer.

title: Death at La Fenice
author: Donna Leon
description: A famous conductor is poisoned during intermission at Venice's opera house—Commissario Brunetti investigates.

title: Dressed for Death
author: Donna Leon
description: A man in women's clothing is found dead in a field outside Venice—Brunetti investigates the world of transvestites and power.

title: Death and Judgment
author: Donna Leon
description: A truck crash and a lawyer's murder in Venice—Brunetti connects them to a sex trafficking ring.

title: The Winter Queen
author: Boris Akunin
description: A student shoots himself in a Moscow park—young detective Fandorin discovers a conspiracy behind the suicide.

title: The Turkish Gambit
author: Boris Akunin
description: Fandorin investigates a spy during the Russo-Turkish War—chess, espionage, and 19th-century adventure.

title: Murder on the Leviathan
author: Boris Akunin
description: Ten suspects on a steamship—Fandorin must identify which one stole a golden shawl and committed murder.

title: The Death of Achilles
author: Boris Akunin
description: A war hero dies in a Moscow hotel—natural causes or assassination? Fandorin investigates.

title: Special Assignments
author: Boris Akunin
description: Two novellas—Fandorin takes on a Jack the Ripper copycat and a master confidence trickster.

title: Mistress of Mellyn
author: Victoria Holt
description: A governess at a Cornish estate falls for the brooding master—his first wife's death may not have been an accident.

title: The King of the Castle
author: Victoria Holt
description: A woman hired to catalog paintings at a French chateau discovers the count has a dark history with wives.

title: The Legend of the Seventh Virgin
author: Victoria Holt
description: A woman returns to Cornwall and discovers seven virgins were walled up in a convent—and one may still haunt it.

title: The Shivering Sands
author: Victoria Holt
description: A woman's sister vanishes near the treacherous Kent coast—the quicksands hold secrets from Roman times.

title: A Woman of Substance
author: Barbara Taylor Bradford
description: A servant girl builds a retail empire from nothing—the ultimate rags-to-riches saga spanning decades.

title: Hold the Dream
author: Barbara Taylor Bradford
description: Emma Harte's granddaughter inherits the empire—and must fight the same enemies that plagued her grandmother.

title: To Be the Best
author: Barbara Taylor Bradford
description: Paula fights to protect the Harte dynasty against corporate raiders and family betrayal.

title: The Shell Seekers
author: Rosamunde Pilcher
description: An elderly woman's valuable painting brings her selfish children circling—a story of memory, art, and what really matters.

title: Coming Home
author: Rosamunde Pilcher
description: A girl is taken in by a wealthy family in Cornwall—their world is shattered by WWII.

title: September
author: Rosamunde Pilcher
description: A Scottish estate prepares for a September dance—old loves, family secrets, and the changing of seasons.

title: Tau Zero
author: Poul Anderson
description: A starship's drive malfunctions and it can't decelerate—the crew watches the universe age and die as they accelerate forever.

title: The High Crusade
author: Poul Anderson
description: Medieval English knights capture an alien spaceship—and conquer the galaxy with swords, faith, and cunning.

title: Brain Wave
author: Poul Anderson
description: Earth moves out of a cosmic field that suppressed intelligence—overnight, every creature on the planet becomes a genius.

title: The Boat of a Million Years
author: Poul Anderson
description: A handful of immortals born throughout history find each other across millennia—from ancient Phoenicia to the stars.

title: To Your Scattered Bodies Go
author: Philip José Farmer
description: Every human who ever lived wakes up along an endless river—Richard Burton the explorer tries to find who resurrected them.

title: The Fabulous Riverboat
author: Philip José Farmer
description: Mark Twain builds a riverboat on the Riverworld to sail to the source—with a crew of historical figures.

title: The Dark Design
author: Philip José Farmer
description: Multiple expeditions converge on the tower at the end of the river—the truth about the Riverworld is near.

title: Night of Light
author: Philip José Farmer
description: A planet where a cosmic event makes your subconscious manifest physically—a priest faces his own darkness.

title: The Lovers
author: Philip José Farmer
description: A man on a repressed theocratic world falls for an alien woman—the first SF novel to deal openly with sex.

title: Mission of Gravity
author: Hal Clement
description: A disc-shaped planet with 700x Earth gravity at the poles—tiny centipede-like aliens help a human recover a probe.

title: Needle
author: Hal Clement
description: An alien symbiont hides inside a teenage boy on a Pacific island—together they hunt a fugitive alien killer.

title: Close to Critical
author: Hal Clement
description: A planet where water exists near its critical point—the surface shifts between liquid and gas unpredictably.

title: Jirel of Joiry
author: C.L. Moore
description: The first female sword-and-sorcery hero—a medieval warrior woman ventures into otherworldly dimensions.

title: Shambleau and Others
author: C.L. Moore
description: Northwest Smith stories—a space adventurer encounters alien horrors across the solar system. Pioneering science fantasy.

title: The Long Tomorrow
author: Leigh Brackett
description: After nuclear war, America bans cities—two boys search for a hidden scientific community.

title: The Sword of Rhiannon
author: Leigh Brackett
description: A thief on Mars is hurled back in time to the planet's lush past—planetary romance at its best.

title: The Ginger Star
author: Leigh Brackett
description: Eric John Stark returns—searching for his mentor on a dying world beyond the stars.

title: Schismatrix Plus
author: Bruce Sterling
description: Posthumanity splits into Shapers (genetic) and Mechanists (cybernetic)—a diplomat plays both sides across centuries.

title: Islands in the Net
author: Bruce Sterling
description: A corporate negotiator in a networked near-future is kidnapped by data pirates—cyberpunk meets geopolitics.

title: Axiomatic
author: Greg Egan
description: Eighteen stories of identity, consciousness, and reality—if you can edit your own brain, who are you?

title: Infidel
author: Kameron Hurley
description: Nyx returns from exile to take on a shape-shifter assassination—the war between nations escalates.

title: Rapture
author: Kameron Hurley
description: Nyx is sent to negotiate peace—but someone is building a weapon that could end the world.

title: Provenance
author: Ann Leckie
description: A woman steals artifacts to impress her politician mother—and stumbles into an interstellar diplomatic crisis.

title: Deepsix
author: Jack McDevitt
description: A planet is about to be destroyed by a gas giant—a team has days to rescue artifacts from a dying world.

title: Omega
author: Jack McDevitt
description: Mysterious planet-destroying clouds are heading toward a world of sentient beings—humanity must save them without revealing itself.

title: Seeker
author: Jack McDevitt
description: A cup from a legendary colony ship appears on the antiquities market—two dealers hunt for the lost world.

title: The Relentless Moon
author: Mary Robinette Kowal
description: While Mars colonization proceeds, a saboteur threatens the lunar colony—Nicole must find the traitor.

title: Homeland
author: Cory Doctorow
description: Marcus gets a USB drive of government secrets at Burning Man—publish or protect the people in the files?

title: Red Team Blues
author: Cory Doctorow
description: A retired forensic accountant gets one last case—a crypto key that could bring down the financial system.

title: Armada
author: Ernest Cline
description: A teenager discovers the government has been using video games to train pilots for an alien invasion—it's really happening.

title: Victories Greater Than Death
author: Charlie Jane Anders
description: A teenager discovers she's a clone of an alien war hero—and the galaxy needs her to lead a crew of misfits.

title: Dragonflight
author: Anne McCaffrey
description: Threadfall menaces the planet Pern—a young woman bonds with a golden queen dragon and must save her world.

title: Dragonquest
author: Anne McCaffrey
description: F'lar and Lessa discover the origins of Thread—and face rebellion among the dragonriders.

title: The White Dragon
author: Anne McCaffrey
description: Young Jaxom and his runt white dragon Ruth prove their worth on a secret mission to the Southern Continent.

title: Dragonsong
author: Anne McCaffrey
description: A girl forbidden to make music runs away and discovers fire lizards—Harper Hall's first apprentice.

title: Dragonsinger
author: Anne McCaffrey
description: Menolly arrives at Harper Hall with her fire lizards—she must prove herself against jealous rivals.

title: The Ship Who Sang
author: Anne McCaffrey
description: A woman's body is too damaged to survive—her brain is installed in a spaceship. She sings and she flies.

title: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea
author: Jules Verne
description: Captain Nemo and his submarine Nautilus—a voyage through the world's oceans encountering wonders and horrors.

title: Journey to the Center of the Earth
author: Jules Verne
description: A professor and his nephew descend through an Icelandic volcano into a prehistoric underground world.

title: Around the World in Eighty Days
author: Jules Verne
description: Phileas Fogg bets he can circumnavigate the globe in 80 days—trains, steamships, and an elephant across India.

title: From the Earth to the Moon
author: Jules Verne
description: A gun club fires three men at the moon in a giant cannon—Verne predicted many details of the Apollo missions.

title: The Mysterious Island
author: Jules Verne
description: Civil War prisoners escape by balloon and crash on a Pacific island—someone is secretly helping them survive.

title: The Time Ships
author: Stephen Baxter
description: The authorized sequel to H.G. Wells's The Time Machine—the time traveler returns to the far future and finds it changed.

title: Voyage
author: Stephen Baxter
description: JFK survives Dallas—NASA goes to Mars in the 1980s. Alternate history hard SF.

title: Raft
author: Stephen Baxter
description: Humans survive in a universe where gravity is a billion times stronger—civilizations built on compressed stars.

title: Coalescent
author: Stephen Baxter
description: A man researching his family discovers a colony of humans who've evolved into a hive mind beneath Rome.

title: Evolution
author: Stephen Baxter
description: The story of human evolution from 65 million years ago to 500 million in the future—epic deep-time SF.

title: Witch World
author: Andre Norton
description: A man escapes through a portal to a world of witches and medieval warfare—the first Witch World novel.

title: Star Man's Son
author: Andre Norton
description: A mutant boy in post-apocalyptic America searches for the Star Men—Norton's breakthrough.

title: The Beast Master
author: Andre Norton
description: A Navajo soldier and his telepathically linked animals settle a frontier planet after Earth is destroyed.

title: Forerunner Foray
author: Andre Norton
description: A psychic woman touches an alien artifact and is hurled across time—Norton's signature blend of ESP and adventure.

title: The Mists of Avalon
author: Marion Zimmer Bradley
description: The Arthurian legend told by the women—Morgaine, Viviane, Gwenhwyfar. Feminism meets mythology.

title: The Heritage of Hastur
author: Marion Zimmer Bradley
description: A young man discovers his telepathic powers on the planet Darkover—caught between the Comyn lords and the Terran Empire.

title: The Shattered Chain
author: Marion Zimmer Bradley
description: A Terran woman is rescued by the Free Amazons of Darkover—a novel about women's freedom across two cultures.

title: The World of Null-A
author: A.E. van Vogt
description: A man wakes with no memory in a future ruled by non-Aristotelian logic—conspiracies and identity crises abound.

title: Slan
author: A.E. van Vogt
description: A telepathic mutant boy hides among humans who want to exterminate his kind—Golden Age paranoid SF.

title: The Voyage of the Space Beagle
author: A.E. van Vogt
description: A starship encounters alien creatures—including the inspiration for Alien. Fix-up novel of First Contact.

title: Cities in Flight
author: James Blish
description: Entire cities take flight with anti-gravity engines—New York wanders the galaxy as a migrant worker among the stars.

title: A Case of Conscience
author: James Blish
description: A Jesuit biologist discovers a planet of sinless aliens—are they proof of God's grace or the Devil's trap?

title: Timescape
author: Gregory Benford
description: Scientists in 1998 send tachyon messages to 1963 to prevent ecological disaster—hard SF time travel.

title: In the Ocean of Night
author: Gregory Benford
description: An astronaut discovers alien machinery orbiting Earth—the first contact begins a saga spanning millennia.

title: Across the Sea of Suns
author: Gregory Benford
description: Humanity's first interstellar expedition encounters machine intelligence civilizations among the stars.

title: Great Sky River
author: Gregory Benford
description: In the far future, humans are hunted by machine intelligences near the galactic center—survival as a species.

title: The Dark Eidolon and Other Fantasies
author: Clark Ashton Smith
description: The collected weird fiction—Zothique, Hyperborea, Averoigne. Lovecraft's peer and the most lyrical pulp writer.

title: A Rendezvous in Averoigne
author: Clark Ashton Smith
description: Stories of medieval France where vampires, sorcerers, and demons lurk—Smith's most atmospheric cycle.

title: The Anubis Gates
author: Tim Powers
description: A literature professor travels to 1810 London and gets trapped—Egyptian sorcery, body-swapping, and time travel.

title: Declare
author: Tim Powers
description: A Cold War spy thriller where the secret war is against djinn on Mount Ararat—le Carré meets the supernatural.

title: Last Call
author: Tim Powers
description: A one-eyed poker player must win back his soul from the Fisher King of Las Vegas—tarot, Arthurian myth, and gambling.

title: On Stranger Tides
author: Tim Powers
description: Pirates and voodoo in the 18th-century Caribbean—Blackbeard hunts the Fountain of Youth. Inspired the Pirates of the Caribbean film.

title: Stations of the Tide
author: Michael Swanwick
description: A bureaucrat hunts a magician on a planet about to be submerged—technology and magic are indistinguishable.

title: The Iron Dragon's Daughter
author: Michael Swanwick
description: A changeling girl in a faerie world works in an iron dragon factory—industrial fantasy as social criticism.

title: Bones of the Earth
author: Michael Swanwick
description: Time-traveling paleontologists study dinosaurs firsthand—but changing the past has consequences.

title: The Speed of Dark
author: Elizabeth Moon
description: An autistic man in the near future is offered a cure—a profound novel about identity, normalcy, and choice.

title: Sheepfarmer's Daughter
author: Elizabeth Moon
description: A farmer's daughter joins a mercenary company and discovers she has a paladin's gift—military fantasy.

title: Divided Allegiance
author: Elizabeth Moon
description: Paks leaves the company to train as a paladin—but the gods have a harder road planned for her.

title: Oath of Gold
author: Elizabeth Moon
description: Paks faces her darkest trial—stripped of her gifts, she must choose to rise or fall.

title: The Reluctant Swordsman
author: Dave Duncan
description: A dying Earth man wakes in the body of a swordsman on a fantasy world—and must fight to survive.

title: Magic Casement
author: Dave Duncan
description: A stableman discovers he may be the prophesied hero—the first Man of His Word, a fun portal/quest fantasy.

title: Faery Lands Forlorn
author: Dave Duncan
description: Inos is kidnapped to a desert empire while Rap crosses the frozen north—parallel quests.

title: Transformation
author: Carol Berg
description: An enslaved sorcerer is bought by a barbarian prince—together they must fight demons possessing the prince's soul.

title: Revelation
author: Carol Berg
description: Seyonne is free but his power is damaged—the demons he fought are coming back, and his former master needs him.

title: Restoration
author: Carol Berg
description: The demon war reaches its climax—Seyonne must sacrifice everything to seal the portal between worlds.

title: Range of Ghosts
author: Elizabeth Bear
description: A steppe warrior and a wizard princess flee across a continent where each kingdom has its own sky—Silk Road epic fantasy.

title: Shattered Pillars
author: Elizabeth Bear
description: Temur and Samarkar continue their quest as a dark cult threatens to remake the world.

title: Steles of the Sky
author: Elizabeth Bear
description: The epic conclusion—armies, magic, and the fate of the Eternal Sky converge in the final battle.

title: The Ten Thousand Doors of January
author: Alix E. Harrow
description: A girl discovers that doors to other worlds are real—and her missing father may have opened one.

title: The Once and Future Witches
author: Alix E. Harrow
description: Three sisters in 1890s New Salem fight for suffrage and witchcraft—feminism, magic, and resistance.

title: A Mirror Mended
author: Alix E. Harrow
description: A fairy-tale scholar gets stuck inside Sleeping Beauty—and the story is more dangerous than she expected.

title: Starling House
author: Alix E. Harrow
description: A haunted house in Kentucky needs a new warden—a young woman with nothing to lose takes the job.

title: When the Tiger Came Down the Mountain
author: Nghi Vo
description: A cleric is surrounded by tigers—she buys time by telling them the love story between a tiger and a woman.

title: Into the Riverlands
author: Nghi Vo
description: Chih encounters three legendary martial artists at an inn—stories of violence, honor, and regret.

title: Mammoths at the Gates
author: Nghi Vo
description: Chih returns home to find mammoth riders at the abbey gates—demanding an impossible debt.

title: A Master of Djinn
author: P. Djèlí Clark
description: A government agent in 1912 Cairo investigates a murder at a secret brotherhood—someone is summoning djinn.

title: The Dead Cat Tail Assassins
author: P. Djèlí Clark
description: An assassin discovers her latest mark is already dead—and the wrong people want to know why.

title: Iron Widow
author: Xiran Jay Zhao
description: In a world where women are sacrificed to pilot giant mechs, an eighteen-year-old kills her co-pilot and takes his place.

title: Iron Heart
author: Xiran Jay Zhao
description: Zetian controls the most powerful mech but the system that oppresses women is bigger than one machine.

title: The Bone Shard Daughter
author: Andrea Stewart
description: An emperor controls his subjects with bone shards implanted at birth—his daughter discovers the system's dark truth.

title: The Bone Shard Emperor
author: Andrea Stewart
description: The new empress must unite sinking islands while a revolutionary army and ancient constructs threaten her rule.

title: The Bone Shard War
author: Andrea Stewart
description: The conclusion—a war on two fronts as the empire's islands sink and the constructs awaken.

title: The Book Eaters
author: Sunyi Dean
description: A woman from a family that literally eats books must find a way to feed her son—who eats minds.

title: Godkiller
author: Hannah Kaner
description: A god killer, a cursed noble, and a hidden god travel together—the gods are real and they're hungry.

title: Sunbringer
author: Hannah Kaner
description: The god of war is rising and Kissen must stop him—the second Fallen Gods novel.

title: The Shadow of the Gods
author: John Gwynne
description: In a Norse-inspired world where the gods are dead, three warriors hunt for power among the bones of fallen deities.

title: The Hunger of the Gods
author: John Gwynne
description: Dragon-born warriors clash as the dead gods stir—the bloodsworn oaths demand sacrifice.

title: The Fury of the Gods
author: John Gwynne
description: The epic conclusion—the final battle as gods rise from death and the bloodsworn meet their fates.

title: Winter's Orbit
author: Everina Maxwell
description: A prince is forced into an arranged marriage after his husband dies—the new match must investigate the murder.

title: A Marvellous Light
author: Freya Marske
description: An Edwardian bureaucrat stumbles into a world of magic—and falls for the prickly magician investigating him.

title: A Restless Truth
author: Freya Marske
description: A murder on an ocean liner and a magical contract—two women investigate while falling for each other.

title: A Power Unbound
author: Freya Marske
description: The final binding—enemies to lovers as two men race to save English magic from destruction.

title: The Unbroken
author: C.L. Clark
description: A soldier conscripted by the empire returns to her colonized homeland—revolution, magic, and impossible choices.

title: The Faithless
author: C.L. Clark
description: Touraine navigates court politics while Luca schemes to hold power—the colonizer and the rebel circle each other.

title: The Adventures of Amina al-Sirafi
author: Shannon Chakraborty
description: A retired pirate queen comes back for one last job—to rescue a friend's kidnapped granddaughter across the medieval Indian Ocean.

title: The Spear Cuts Through Water
author: Simon Jimenez
description: Two warriors escort an ancient goddess across a cursed land—told as a theater performance within a dream.

title: Paladin's Grace
author: T. Kingfisher
description: A disgraced paladin and a perfumer stumble into a murder investigation—cozy romance meets fantasy mystery.

title: Paladin's Strength
author: T. Kingfisher
description: A paladin escorts a nun across dangerous territory—she's hiding something inhuman, and he doesn't care.

title: Paladin's Hope
author: T. Kingfisher
description: A paladin and a forensic sorcerer investigate bodies in the sewers—banter, bones, and unexpected romance.

title: Sorcery of Thorns
author: Margaret Rogerson
description: A librarian's apprentice accidentally unleashes a dangerous grimoire—and must ally with a sorcerer she's been taught to hate.

title: Daughter of the Moon Goddess
author: Sue Lynn Tan
description: The daughter of Chang'e trains as a warrior to free her mother—Chinese mythology meets epic fantasy.

title: Heart of the Sun Warrior
author: Sue Lynn Tan
description: Xingyin must save the realms from an ancient evil—the Celestial Kingdom duology concludes.

title: The Saint of Bright Doors
author: Vajra Chandrasekera
description: A man raised to kill a god moves to a city of impossible doors—Sri Lankan-inspired fantasy about radicalization and escape.

title: The Water Outlaws
author: S.L. Huang
description: A retelling of Water Margin—a woman mathematician joins a band of outlaws fighting a corrupt empire in Song Dynasty China.

title: The Foxglove King
author: Hannah Whitten
description: A woman who can raise the dead is recruited as a spy in a decadent court—poison, necromancy, and two love interests.

title: Regeneration
author: Pat Barker
description: WWI poets Sassoon and Owen meet at a psychiatric hospital—trauma, masculinity, and the madness of war.

title: The Eye in the Door
author: Pat Barker
description: A working-class soldier-spy faces his own divided loyalty—pacifists and patriots in 1918 London.

title: The Ghost Road
author: Pat Barker
description: The final months of WWI—Rivers treats patients while Billy Prior returns to the trenches. Booker winner.

title: Noonday
author: Pat Barker
description: The Blitz—an artist, a war correspondent, and their tangled past survive London's bombardment.

title: The Silence of the Girls
author: Pat Barker
description: The Iliad retold by Briseis—the captive queen who watched Achilles rage while women suffered in silence.

title: Paddy Clarke Ha Ha Ha
author: Roddy Doyle
description: A ten-year-old boy in 1960s Dublin—his world shrinks as his parents' marriage falls apart. Booker winner.

title: The Commitments
author: Roddy Doyle
description: A Dublin soul band forms, fights, and breaks up—working-class music and maximum craic.

title: The Snapper
author: Roddy Doyle
description: A young Dublin woman gets pregnant and won't say who the father is—her family copes in hilarious fashion.

title: The Van
author: Roddy Doyle
description: Two unemployed men buy a chip van during the 1990 World Cup—friendship tested by deep fat and desperation.

title: A Star Called Henry
author: Roddy Doyle
description: A Dublin street urchin joins the 1916 Easter Rising—the Irish revolution through one life of poverty and violence.

title: The Sea
author: John Banville
description: A man returns to the seaside town of his childhood after his wife's death—memory, loss, and a long-ago summer. Booker winner.

title: The Book of Evidence
author: John Banville
description: A dissolute man confesses to murder—not remorsefully, but as an aesthetic exercise. Chilling and beautiful.

title: Ancient Light
author: John Banville
description: An aging actor remembers his teenage affair with his friend's mother—Banville at his most luminous.

title: The Gathering
author: Anne Enright
description: A woman confronts her family at her brother's funeral—Irish dysfunction, memory, and the wreckage of childhood. Booker winner.

title: The Green Road
author: Anne Enright
description: An Irish mother's four children scatter across the world—they return for Christmas as she sells the family home.

title: Actress
author: Anne Enright
description: A daughter reconstructs her famous mother's life—an Irish actress's glamour, secrets, and self-destruction.

title: Room
author: Emma Donoghue
description: A five-year-old boy and his mother are held captive in a single room—told from the child's perspective. Devastating.

title: The Pull of the Stars
author: Emma Donoghue
description: Three days in a Dublin maternity ward during the 1918 flu pandemic—birth, death, and connection.

title: Frog Music
author: Emma Donoghue
description: A burlesque dancer investigates her friend's murder in 1876 San Francisco—based on a true unsolved killing.

title: Birdsong
author: Sebastian Faulks
description: A love affair in pre-war France and the horrors of the Somme—one of the great WWI novels.

title: Charlotte Gray
author: Sebastian Faulks
description: A Scottish woman joins the French Resistance to find her missing RAF lover—WWII espionage and love.

title: Engleby
author: Sebastian Faulks
description: A Cambridge student's unreliable memoir—blackouts, obsession, and a missing girl. Something is very wrong with Engleby.

title: Money
author: Martin Amis
description: A self-destructing film director binges across New York and London—Amis's savage comic masterpiece about greed.

title: London Fields
author: Martin Amis
description: A woman engineers her own murder—a darts player and a yuppie compete to be her killer. Apocalyptic London noir.

title: Time's Arrow
author: Martin Amis
description: A life told backwards—a doctor's career in reverse reveals he was a Nazi death camp physician. Brilliant and horrifying.

title: The Information
author: Martin Amis
description: Two novelists—one successful, one failed—and the failed one's elaborate plan to destroy his friend.

title: Girl, Woman, Other
author: Bernardine Evaristo
description: Twelve Black British women across generations—intersecting lives of race, gender, and identity. Joint Booker winner.

title: Mr Loverman
author: Bernardine Evaristo
description: A 74-year-old Caribbean man in Hackney has been hiding a secret lover—his best friend—for decades.

title: The Bloody Chamber
author: Angela Carter
description: Fairy tales rewritten as feminist gothic—Bluebeard, Beauty and the Beast, Red Riding Hood, all dripping with desire and danger.

title: Nights at the Circus
author: Angela Carter
description: A winged aerialist in 1890s London—is she really part bird? A journalist follows her circus across Siberia.

title: Wise Children
author: Angela Carter
description: Twin showgirls tell their family's theatrical history—Shakespeare, illegitimacy, and the wrong side of the tracks.

title: Waterland
author: Graham Swift
description: A history teacher in the Fens tells his students about his own past—incest, murder, and the landscape of memory.

title: Last Orders
author: Graham Swift
description: Four men drive from London to Margate to scatter a friend's ashes—a Booker-winning Canterbury Tales for the working class.

title: Wish You Were Here
author: Graham Swift
description: A farmer on the Isle of Wight receives his brother's body from Iraq—grief, landscape, and the cost of war.

title: Moon Tiger
author: Penelope Lively
description: A dying historian reconstructs her life—wartime Cairo, love affairs, and the writing of history itself. Booker winner.

title: The Road to Lichfield
author: Penelope Lively
description: A woman visiting her dying father discovers he had a secret mistress—everything she knew shifts.

title: Consequences
author: Penelope Lively
description: A wartime love leads to three generations of women—choices echo across decades.

title: Prep
author: Curtis Sittenfeld
description: A Midwestern girl at an elite boarding school—class, insecurity, and the desperate need to belong.

title: American Wife
author: Curtis Sittenfeld
description: A librarian marries into a political dynasty—loosely based on Laura Bush's life before and during the presidency.

title: Eligible
author: Curtis Sittenfeld
description: Pride and Prejudice set in modern Cincinnati—a magazine writer and a neurosurgeon clash at a family barbecue.

title: Romantic Comedy
author: Curtis Sittenfeld
description: A comedy writer at a SNL-like show falls for a pop star guest host—can real romance match the script?

title: The Interestings
author: Meg Wolitzer
description: Six teenagers at an arts camp—their lives diverge over thirty years as talent, luck, and jealousy shape their fates.

title: The Female Persuasion
author: Meg Wolitzer
description: A college student is mentored by a famous feminist—power, compromise, and what the movement costs.

title: The Wife
author: Meg Wolitzer
description: A famous novelist's wife has been keeping a secret for decades—she wrote his books.

title: English Creek
author: Ivan Doig
description: A fourteen-year-old in 1939 Montana—one summer of ranch work, family tension, and the last days of the old West.

title: Dancing at the Rascal Fair
author: Ivan Doig
description: Scottish immigrants homestead in 1890s Montana—friendship, love triangles, and the brutal beauty of the frontier.

title: This House of Sky
author: Ivan Doig
description: Doig's memoir of growing up motherless in Montana ranch country—landscape as character, memory as survival.

title: The Whistling Season
author: Ivan Doig
description: A housekeeper and her brother transform a one-room schoolhouse in 1909 Montana—before Halley's Comet changes everything.

title: Peace Like a River
author: Leif Enger
description: An asthmatic boy narrates his family's pursuit of his fugitive brother across the 1960s Badlands—miracles happen.

title: Virgil Wander
author: Leif Enger
description: A man drives off a cliff into Lake Superior and survives—he rebuilds his life and his dying Minnesota town.

title: Montana 1948
author: Larry Watson
description: A twelve-year-old watches his father investigate his uncle for assaulting Native American women—family, law, and silence.

title: Let Him Go
author: Larry Watson
description: Retired parents drive across the Dakotas to rescue their grandson from a dangerous new family—quiet Western thriller.

title: Where the Sea Used to Be
author: Rick Bass
description: Two geologists in Montana's Yaak Valley—one seeks oil, the other seeks wilderness. The land decides.

title: The Watch
author: Rick Bass
description: Stories of the American West—a man who runs with wolves, a town that floods, a bear hunter's obsession.

title: For a Little While
author: Rick Bass
description: Selected stories spanning thirty years—Montana, Mississippi, and the wild places between.

title: Breathing Lessons
author: Anne Tyler
description: A married couple drives to a funeral—one day reveals the accumulated disappointments and devotions of a lifetime. Pulitzer winner.

title: The Accidental Tourist
author: Anne Tyler
description: A travel writer who hates travel loses his son—a dog trainer slowly brings him back to life.

title: Dinner at the Homesick Restaurant
author: Anne Tyler
description: Three siblings and their difficult mother—a restaurant where no meal is ever finished. Tyler's masterpiece.

title: A Spool of Blue Thread
author: Anne Tyler
description: Four generations of a Baltimore family in the house they built—the last Tyler novel to be shortlisted for the Booker.

title: Saint Maybe
author: Anne Tyler
description: A teenager's careless words cause a tragedy—he spends the rest of his life trying to atone.

title: Back When We Were Grownups
author: Anne Tyler
description: A grandmother wonders if she became the wrong person—what if she'd made different choices?

title: Redhead by the Side of the Road
author: Anne Tyler
description: A tech support man with rigid routines is disrupted by a woman from his past—and a teenager who might be his son.

title: Of Human Bondage
author: Somerset Maugham
description: A club-footed orphan's obsessive love for a vulgar waitress—Maugham's autobiographical epic of suffering and art.

title: The Razor's Edge
author: Somerset Maugham
description: A WWI veteran abandons success to search for meaning—from Chicago to Paris to India.

title: The Moon and Sixpence
author: Somerset Maugham
description: A London stockbroker abandons his family to become a painter in Tahiti—loosely based on Gauguin.

title: Cakes and Ale
author: Somerset Maugham
description: A writer's widow controls his literary legacy—but his first wife, a barmaid, was the real muse.

title: The Painted Veil
author: Somerset Maugham
description: A bacteriologist takes his unfaithful wife to a cholera epidemic in China—punishment becomes redemption.

title: The Good Soldier
author: Ford Madox Ford
description: The saddest story ever told—two couples, infidelity, madness, and the narrator who refuses to see the truth.

title: Parade's End
author: Ford Madox Ford
description: A tetralogy of WWI—an honorable Tory, his vicious wife, and the suffragette he loves. England before and after the deluge.

title: Restoration
author: Rose Tremain
description: A doctor in Charles II's court—plague, fire, and the pursuit of love in 1660s London.

title: The Gustav Sonata
author: Rose Tremain
description: A Swiss boy and his friend—one a refugee, the other repressed—a lifetime of quiet devotion.

title: The Colour
author: Rose Tremain
description: A couple emigrates to 1860s New Zealand for gold—the husband's obsession destroys them both.

title: Sacred Country
author: Rose Tremain
description: A girl in 1950s Suffolk knows she's really a boy—a transgender odyssey from farm to Nashville.

title: Hotel du Lac
author: Anita Brookner
description: A romance novelist hides at a Swiss hotel after a disgrace—quiet, devastatingly precise about loneliness. Booker winner.

title: Brief Lives
author: Anita Brookner
description: A widow reflects on her friendship with a glamorous woman—Brookner's anatomy of female solitude.

title: Fraud
author: Anita Brookner
description: A woman disappears from her London flat—was she ever really there? A mystery of invisibility.

title: Captain Corelli's Mandolin
author: Louis de Bernières
description: An Italian officer and a Greek girl fall in love during the WWII occupation of Cephalonia—war, music, and heartbreak.

title: Birds Without Wings
author: Louis de Bernières
description: A Turkish village is destroyed by nationalism and WWI—Christians and Muslims who lived together are torn apart.

title: The Unlikely Pilgrimage of Harold Fry
author: Rachel Joyce
description: A retired man sets out to walk 600 miles across England to save a dying friend—one foot in front of the other.

title: The Love Song of Miss Queenie Hennessy
author: Rachel Joyce
description: The companion to Harold Fry—the dying woman he's walking to tells her own story of secret love.

title: Miss Benson's Beetle
author: Rachel Joyce
description: A teacher and an unlikely companion travel to New Caledonia in 1950 to find a golden beetle—adventure and friendship.

title: The Elegance of the Hedgehog
author: Muriel Barbery
description: A Parisian concierge hides her brilliance—a suicidal twelve-year-old and a Japanese neighbor change everything.

title: A Tale for the Time Being
author: Ruth Ozeki
description: A writer in Canada finds a diary washed ashore from the Japanese tsunami—a teenage girl's story reaches across the Pacific.

title: The Book of Form and Emptiness
author: Ruth Ozeki
description: A boy hears the voices of objects after his father dies—a novel about hoarding, loss, and the life of things.

title: The Buddha in the Attic
author: Julie Otsuka
description: Japanese picture brides arrive in San Francisco—told in collective first person, their American dream turns to internment.

title: The Swimmers
author: Julie Otsuka
description: Lap swimmers at a community pool—a crack appears in the bottom and reality fractures. Memory and dementia.

title: Waiting
author: Ha Jin
description: A Chinese army doctor waits eighteen years for a divorce—bureaucracy, patience, and love deferred.

title: War Trash
author: Ha Jin
description: A Chinese POW in the Korean War—caught between repatriation to China and defection to Taiwan.

title: A Free Life
author: Ha Jin
description: A Chinese immigrant family in Atlanta after Tiananmen—the American Dream as daily grind.

title: The Vagrants
author: Yiyun Li
description: A young woman is executed in a Chinese provincial town—the ripples touch every life in the community.

title: A Thousand Years of Good Prayers
author: Yiyun Li
description: Stories of Chinese and Chinese-American lives—immigration, loneliness, and the distance between generations.

title: Where Reasons End
author: Yiyun Li
description: A mother has imaginary conversations with her dead son—grief as dialogue, impossible and necessary.

title: Brick Lane
author: Monica Ali
description: A Bangladeshi woman in London's East End—an arranged marriage, a sewing machine, and the slow discovery of herself.

title: Small Island
author: Andrea Levy
description: Jamaican immigrants and their English landlady in 1948 London—race, war, and the myth of the mother country.

title: The Long Song
author: Andrea Levy
description: A slave woman in 19th-century Jamaica tells her story—wit, survival, and the last days of slavery.

title: The Woman Upstairs
author: Claire Messud
description: A schoolteacher's rage—she befriends an exotic family and is consumed by desire, envy, and fury.

title: The Emperor's Children
author: Claire Messud
description: Three friends in New York before and after 9/11—privilege, ambition, and the end of innocence.

title: The Book of Unknown Americans
author: Cristina Henríquez
description: Immigrant families in a Delaware apartment complex—a Panamanian boy and a Mexican girl fall in love.

title: The Moor's Account
author: Laila Lalami
description: The true story of the first Black explorer of America—a Moroccan slave on the 1527 Spanish expedition, told from his perspective.

title: The Other Americans
author: Laila Lalami
description: A Moroccan immigrant is killed in a hit-and-run in California—his daughter investigates as the community fractures.

title: Call Me by Your Name
author: Andre Aciman
description: A seventeen-year-old falls for his father's summer guest in 1980s Italy—desire, intellect, and heartbreak.

title: Find Me
author: Andre Aciman
description: The sequel—Elio and Oliver years later, each searching for love in different corners of the world.

title: Enigma Variations
author: Andre Aciman
description: A man's lifelong obsessions—from a cabinet maker in Italy to a woman on a tennis court. Love as constant reinvention.

title: Ellen Foster
author: Kaye Gibbons
description: An eleven-year-old girl in rural North Carolina narrates her escape from an abusive home—fierce, funny, and heartbreaking.

title: A Virtuous Woman
author: Kaye Gibbons
description: A farmer and his dying wife tell their love story in alternating voices—quiet, devastating Southern fiction.

title: Charms for the Easy Life
author: Kaye Gibbons
description: Three generations of North Carolina women—a grandmother who practices medicine, a mother who reads, a daughter who observes.

title: Fair and Tender Ladies
author: Lee Smith
description: An Appalachian woman's life told through letters—from girlhood to old age in the Virginia mountains.

title: Oral History
author: Lee Smith
description: A college student records her mountain family's stories—generations of love, violence, and Appalachian ghosts.

title: On Agate Hill
author: Lee Smith
description: A girl's diary from Reconstruction-era North Carolina—orphaned, wild, and determined to survive.

title: Guests on Earth
author: Lee Smith
description: A patient at a psychiatric hospital in Asheville encounters Zelda Fitzgerald—art, madness, and the 1948 fire.

title: Walking Across Egypt
author: Clyde Edgerton
description: A seventy-eight-year-old Baptist woman adopts a juvenile delinquent—warm, hilarious Southern fiction.

title: Raney
author: Clyde Edgerton
description: A Baptist woman marries a liberal—their first year of marriage is a culture war fought over fried chicken and race.

title: Killer Diller
author: Clyde Edgerton
description: A delinquent in a halfway house forms a blues band—religion, music, and the meaning of redemption.

title: The Missing
author: Tim Gautreaux
description: A floorwalker in 1920s New Orleans searches for a kidnapped child—steamboats, jazz, and the river underworld.

title: The Next Step in the Dance
author: Tim Gautreaux
description: A Cajun couple divorces and separately moves to California—then both come home to Louisiana.

title: The Clearing
author: Tim Gautreaux
description: Two brothers in a 1920s Louisiana lumber camp—one an alcoholic, one running from the mob.

title: Same Place, Same Things
author: Tim Gautreaux
description: Stories of Cajun Louisiana—pump repairmen, factory workers, and people making the best of hard lives.

title: The Good Mother
author: Sue Miller
description: A divorced mother loses custody of her daughter after her new lover is accused—the system punishes women who desire.

title: While I Was Gone
author: Sue Miller
description: A veterinarian's perfect life is disrupted when a man from a commune where someone was murdered reappears.

title: The Arsonist
author: Sue Miller

title: The Tempest
author: William Shakespeare

author: William Shakespeare

title: Who Moved My Cheese?

author: Roxana Robinson
description: A Marine returns from Iraq to his family—PTSD, rage, and the impossibility of coming home. One of the great homecoming novels.

title: Fobbit
author: David Abrams
description: A Catch-22 for Iraq—a public affairs officer in the Green Zone writes press releases while war rages outside the wire.

title: The Lotus Eaters
author: Tatjana Soli
description: A female combat photographer in Vietnam from 1963 to the fall of Saigon—war through the camera's eye.

title: Here, Bullet
author: Brian Turner
description: Poems from an infantry team leader in Iraq—the most acclaimed poetry to come out of the Iraq War.

title: Milkman
author: Anna Burns
description: A young woman in 1970s Belfast is stalked by a paramilitary commander everyone calls the milkman—Troubles fiction as suffocating comedy. Booker winner.

title: The Sellout
author: Paul Beatty
description: A Black farmer in Los Angeles reinstates slavery and segregation in his neighborhood—the most savage racial satire since Swift. Booker winner.

title: The Narrow Road to the Deep North
author: Richard Flanagan
description: An Australian POW on the Burma Railway—love, war, and a haiku that haunts him for sixty years. Booker winner.

title: The Unknown Terrorist
author: Richard Flanagan
description: A lap dancer in Sydney is mistakenly identified as a terrorist—media, fear, and the destruction of an innocent life.

title: The Promise
author: Damon Galgut
description: A white South African family gathers for funerals over thirty years—a promise to their Black housekeeper is never kept. Booker winner.

title: The Good Doctor
author: Damon Galgut
description: Two doctors at a rural South African hospital—one an idealist, one a cynic—post-apartheid disillusionment.

title: In a Strange Room
author: Damon Galgut
description: Three journeys—a man travels through Greece, India, and Africa, searching for connection and finding solitude.

title: True History of the Kelly Gang
author: Peter Carey
description: Ned Kelly writes his story for his unborn daughter—Australian outlaw history in wild, unpunctuated prose. Booker winner.

title: Oscar and Lucinda
author: Peter Carey
description: Two gamblers in colonial Australia bet on whether a glass church can be transported across the outback. Booker winner.

title: Vernon God Little
author: DBC Pierre
description: A Texas teenager is blamed for a school shooting he didn't commit—media circus, death row, and savage satire. Booker winner.

title: The Finkler Question
author: Howard Jacobson
description: Three old friends—two Jewish, one not—navigate love, death, and anti-Semitism in modern London. Booker winner.

title: The Netanyahus
author: Joshua Cohen
description: A Jewish historian at a 1960s American college hosts the Netanyahu family for a job interview—the visit from hell. Pulitzer winner.

title: The Book of Numbers
author: Joshua Cohen
description: A failed novelist ghostwrites the memoir of a tech billionaire—the internet, language, and surveillance.

title: Let the Great World Spin
author: Colum McCann
description: A tightrope walker crosses between the Twin Towers in 1974—below, a dozen New Yorkers' lives intersect. National Book Award.

title: Apeirogon
author: Colum McCann
description: An Israeli and a Palestinian who each lost a daughter to the conflict meet and forge an impossible friendship.

title: TransAtlantic
author: Colum McCann
description: Three crossings of the Atlantic—Frederick Douglass, Alcock and Brown, George Mitchell—connected across centuries.

title: Europe Central
author: William T. Vollmann
description: Germany and Russia at war—Shostakovich, Stalingrad, the Eastern Front told through interlocking fictions. National Book Award.

title: The News from Paraguay
author: Lily Tuck
description: An Irish courtesan becomes the mistress of Paraguay's dictator—the War of the Triple Alliance destroys the country. National Book Award.

title: Three Junes
author: Julia Glass
description: A Scottish family across three Junes—a widowed father, a gay son, and the complicated love between them. National Book Award.

title: Lord of Misrule
author: Jaimy Gordon
description: A down-at-heel racetrack in West Virginia—four horses, their handlers, and the scams that keep them running. National Book Award.

title: The Sparsholt Affair
author: Alan Hollinghurst
description: A scandal at wartime Oxford echoes through four decades of a family—beauty, art, and changing attitudes to homosexuality.

title: Dear Life
author: Alice Munro
description: Fourteen stories—Munro's final collection. Small-town Ontario, women's choices, and the devastating clarity of a lifetime.

title: Runaway
author: Alice Munro
description: Eight stories of women escaping—or trying to—from marriages, families, and their own natures.

title: The Love of a Good Woman
author: Alice Munro
description: Stories of love gone wrong, right, and sideways—Ontario Gothic at its most precise.

title: Open Secrets
author: Alice Munro
description: Eight stories—a girl vanishes on a hike, a woman's secret is exposed, a town's past erupts.

title: Lives of Girls and Women
author: Alice Munro
description: A girl grows up in small-town Ontario—religion, sex, death, and the making of a writer. Munro's breakthrough.

title: Too Much Happiness
author: Alice Munro
description: Ten stories—a woman mathematician in 19th-century Russia, a man who kills a child, a wife who dismembers.

title: Hateship, Friendship, Courtship, Loveship, Marriage
author: Alice Munro
description: Nine stories—a housekeeper's mail-order romance, a woman's hidden pregnancy, the patterns of a lifetime.

title: Septology
author: Jon Fosse
description: A painter in western Norway thinks about his life, his dead wife, and God—written in one unbroken sentence across seven parts. Nobel laureate.

title: A New Name
author: Jon Fosse
description: The continuation of Septology—Asle paints, prays, and remembers. Fosse's hypnotic prose approaches silence.

title: Melancholy
author: Jon Fosse
description: A 19th-century Norwegian painter loses his mind—Fosse's portrait of Lars Hertervig's descent into madness.

title: Soul Mountain
author: Gao Xingjian
description: A man travels through rural China to escape a cancer diagnosis—and discovers the country's suppressed folklore and spirituality. Nobel laureate.

title: Desert
author: J.M.G. Le Clézio
description: A Tuareg girl crosses the Sahara in 1912—a civilization destroyed by French colonialism. Nobel laureate.

title: The Prospector
author: J.M.G. Le Clézio
description: A young man searches Mauritius for pirate treasure—obsession, nature, and the end of colonial paradise.

title: The Birthday Party
author: Harold Pinter
description: Two strangers arrive at a seaside boarding house and terrorize the only guest—menace, silence, and the Pinter pause. Nobel laureate.

title: The Homecoming
author: Harold Pinter
description: A man brings his wife home to meet his family—they decide to keep her. Power, sex, and the most threatening hospitality in theater.

title: Autumn
author: Ali Smith
description: Post-Brexit England—a woman visits her dying friend in a care home. The first of Smith's seasonal quartet.

title: Winter
author: Ali Smith
description: Christmas with a dysfunctional family—a severed head appears in a room, and a stranger arrives.

title: Spring
author: Ali Smith
description: Immigration detention, Shakespeare, and a magical child who opens locked doors—Smith at her most urgent.

title: Summer
author: Ali Smith
description: Lockdown summer 2020—the quartet concludes as all four seasons' characters converge.

title: May We Be Forgiven
author: A.M. Homes
description: A man sleeps with his brother's wife, then his brother kills her—a darkly comic spiral into American dysfunction. Women's Prize winner.

title: This Book Will Save Your Life
author: A.M. Homes
description: A rich recluse in Los Angeles starts engaging with the world—a sinkhole opens in his yard, a donut shop saves him.

title: Celestial Bodies
author: Jokha Alharthi
description: Three Omani sisters navigate tradition and modernity—the first Arabic novel to win the International Booker.

title: At Night All Blood Is Black
author: David Diop
description: A Senegalese soldier in WWI collects German hands—madness and colonialism in the trenches. International Booker winner.

title: A Girl Is a Half-Formed Thing
author: Eimear McBride
description: A girl's life from before birth—abuse, Catholicism, and stream of consciousness so raw it invented a new way to write. Women's Prize winner.

title: The Glorious Heresies
author: Lisa McInerney
description: A grandmother kills an intruder in Cork and five lives collide—drug dealing, prostitution, and the Catholic Church. Women's Prize winner.

title: Brotherless Night
author: V.V. Ganeshananthan
description: A Tamil medical student in Sri Lanka's civil war—she tries to heal while her brothers fight. Women's Prize 2024.

title: Your House Will Pay
author: Steph Cha
description: A Korean American family and a Black family in Los Angeles—connected by a 1991 shooting, the riots, and the present. Edgar winner.

title: Clark and Division
author: Naomi Hirahara
description: A Japanese American woman leaves an internment camp for Chicago in 1944—her sister dies the day she arrives. Edgar winner.

title: The Expats
author: Chris Pavone
description: A CIA wife in Luxembourg realizes her husband is hiding something—espionage, marriage, and lies. Edgar winner.

title: Defending Jacob
author: William Landay
description: A prosecutor's fourteen-year-old son is accused of murder—is he covering for his child or blind to the truth? Edgar winner.

title: Long and Faraway Gone
author: Lou Berney
description: Two survivors of 1986 Oklahoma City tragedies—one a theater shooting, one a disappearance—search for answers decades later. Edgar winner.

title: November Road
author: Lou Berney
description: After the JFK assassination, a mob fixer flees New Orleans with a woman escaping her marriage—both running from violent men.

title: Secret Identity
author: Alex Segura
description: A Cuban American secretary at a 1970s comic book company investigates her colleague's murder—noir meets comics history. Edgar winner.

title: Opened Ground: Selected Poems 1966-1996
author: Seamus Heaney
description: The essential Heaney—from Death of a Naturalist to The Spirit Level. Ireland's greatest poet since Yeats. Nobel laureate.

title: North
author: Seamus Heaney
description: Bog bodies, Viking Dublin, and the Troubles—Heaney finds ancient violence under Irish soil.

title: Station Island
author: Seamus Heaney
description: A pilgrimage on Lough Derg—Heaney meets ghosts including Joyce, who tells him to fill the element with signatures of your own frequency.

title: The Collected Stories
author: William Trevor
description: The greatest short story writer in English after Chekhov—Irish lives of quiet desperation, betrayal, and unexpected grace.

title: Felicia's Journey
author: William Trevor
description: An Irish girl travels to England searching for her boyfriend—a kindly caterer offers to help her. He's a serial killer.

title: Love and Summer
author: William Trevor
description: A young wife in rural Ireland is tempted by a stranger—the whole town watches and waits for her to fall.

title: That They May Face the Rising Sun
author: John McGahern
description: A year in a rural Irish lakeside community—McGahern's final, most serene novel about ordinary life lived fully.

title: Amongst Women
author: John McGahern
description: An IRA veteran rules his family like a platoon—his daughters both adore and resist him. Ireland's postcolonial patriarch.

title: The Country Girls
author: Edna O'Brien
description: Two Irish girls escape convent school for Dublin—O'Brien's banned debut that scandalized Ireland.

title: The Love Object
author: Edna O'Brien
description: Stories of women loving badly—married men, impossible situations, and the wreckage of desire.

title: Girl
author: Edna O'Brien
description: A Nigerian girl is kidnapped by Boko Haram—O'Brien at 88, writing her most harrowing novel.

title: The Woman Warrior
author: Maxine Hong Kingston
description: A Chinese American girl grows up between two cultures—myth, memory, and the stories her mother told.

title: China Men
author: Maxine Hong Kingston
description: The men of Kingston's family—building railroads, working sugarcane, fighting in Vietnam. The male counterpart to The Woman Warrior.

title: Fences
author: August Wilson
description: A garbage collector and former Negro League player battles his family and his bitterness in 1950s Pittsburgh. Pulitzer winner.

title: The Piano Lesson
author: August Wilson
description: A brother and sister fight over a piano carved with their family's slave history—sell it or keep it? Pulitzer winner.

title: Ma Rainey's Black Bottom
author: August Wilson
description: A recording session in 1920s Chicago—the Mother of the Blues vs. her white management, and a trumpeter's rage.

title: Joe Turner's Come and Gone
author: August Wilson
description: A boardinghouse in 1911 Pittsburgh—a man searches for his wife who left while he was imprisoned by Joe Turner.

title: A Raisin in the Sun
author: Lorraine Hansberry
description: A Black family in 1950s Chicago gets an insurance check—each member has a different dream for the money. The first play by a Black woman on Broadway.

title: Omeros
author: Derek Walcott
description: Homer's epics reimagined in the Caribbean—fishermen, colonial history, and the sea. Nobel laureate.

title: View with a Grain of Sand
author: Wisława Szymborska
description: Selected poems—deceptively simple, quietly devastating observations on existence. Nobel laureate.

title: The Captive Mind
author: Czesław Miłosz
description: How intellectuals surrender to totalitarianism—Miłosz's diagnosis of the communist seduction of the mind. Nobel laureate.

title: New and Collected Poems 1931-2001
author: Czesław Miłosz
description: A lifetime of poetry from exile—witness to the century's worst horrors, rendered in luminous Polish-American verse.

title: The Group Portrait with Lady
author: Heinrich Böll
description: A woman's life in wartime and postwar Germany—told through interviews with everyone who knew her. Nobel laureate.

title: The Lost Honour of Katharina Blum
author: Heinrich Böll
description: A woman shelters a suspected terrorist—the tabloid press destroys her. Böll's furious indictment of media power.

title: Auto-da-Fé
author: Elias Canetti
description: A scholar's library obsession drives him mad—the books consume him literally and figuratively. Nobel laureate.

title: The Collected Stories
author: Grace Paley
description: Three slim collections in one volume—Faith, her neighbors, and the women of New York. Among the greatest American story writers.

title: Tell Me a Riddle
author: Tillie Olsen
description: Four stories about women's silenced lives—the title novella of a dying grandmother's suppressed rage is devastating.

title: The Shawl
author: Cynthia Ozick
description: Two stories—a mother in a concentration camp hides her baby in a shawl, and the aftermath thirty years later.

title: The Puttermesser Papers
author: Cynthia Ozick
description: A female lawyer in New York creates a golem—Ozick's comic, theological novel about power and creation.

title: Brown Girl, Brownstones
author: Paule Marshall
description: A Barbadian girl grows up in Brooklyn—her mother's ambition and her father's dreams tear the family apart. The first great Caribbean American novel.

title: Maud Martha
author: Gwendolyn Brooks
description: A Black woman's life in Chicago—small moments of beauty and racism rendered in poetic prose. Brooks's only novel.

title: The Collected Stories
author: Mavis Gallant
description: Fifty-two stories of exile and displacement—Canadians in Paris, refugees in transit, lives lived between cultures. The greatest Canadian short story writer.

title: According to Queeney
author: Beryl Bainbridge
description: Samuel Johnson through the eyes of his friend's daughter—Bainbridge's precise, witty historical fiction.

title: Every Man for Himself
author: Beryl Bainbridge
description: The Titanic sinks—told by a young man dining, flirting, and philosophizing as the ship goes down.

title: Master Georgie
author: Beryl Bainbridge
description: The Crimean War through three perspectives—photography, surgery, and the chaos of Victorian ambition.

title: The Tortilla Curtain
author: T.C. Boyle
description: A liberal LA couple and Mexican immigrants collide over a canyon wall—immigration, class, and the American Dream at its cruelest.

title: World's End
author: T.C. Boyle
