description: Three centuries of two families in the Hudson Valley—the Dutch, the English, the hippies. PEN/Faulkner winner.

title: The Road to Wellville
author: T.C. Boyle
description: John Harvey Kellogg's sanatorium in 1907 Michigan—colonic irrigation, cornflakes, and American health madness.

title: Drop City
author: T.C. Boyle
description: A hippie commune in 1970s California moves to the Alaskan wilderness—back-to-the-land idealism meets brutal reality.

title: The Women
author: T.C. Boyle
description: Frank Lloyd Wright told through the four women in his life—genius, ego, and the destruction it leaves behind.

title: Talk Talk
author: T.C. Boyle
description: A deaf woman's identity is stolen—she chases the thief across America in this Boyle road thriller.

title: The Harder They Come
author: T.C. Boyle
description: A Vietnam vet, his unhinged son, and a gun—sovereign citizen ideology meets family catastrophe.

title: Blue Skies
author: T.C. Boyle
description: Climate collapse hits one family—a daughter keeps a python, a son farms insects, and Florida is drowning.

title: For the Relief of Unbearable Urges
author: Nathan Englander
description: Stories of Jewish identity—a rabbi prescribes prostitutes, a Holocaust survivor hides, a Hasid goes to therapy.

title: The Ministry of Special Cases
author: Nathan Englander
description: A father in 1970s Buenos Aires searches for his son who was disappeared by the junta—Jewish Argentina meets state terror.

title: What We Talk About When We Talk About Anne Frank
author: Nathan Englander
description: Stories about being Jewish in the modern world—survivors, settlers, and a couple in Florida playing the Anne Frank game.

title: Dinner at the Center of the Earth
author: Nathan Englander
description: A spy imprisoned in the Negev desert—Israel, Palestine, and the endless war told through one man's captivity.

title: Rubbernecker
author: Belinda Bauer
description: An autistic student dissecting a body in anatomy class realizes it was murdered—no one believes him. CWA Gold Dagger.

title: The Shut Eye
author: Belinda Bauer
description: A mother whose child was snatched visits a psychic—the police think she's crazy, but the psychic knows things.

title: Snap
author: Belinda Bauer
description: Three children are abandoned by the roadside—their mother is murdered. Eleven-year-old Jack becomes a burglar to survive.

title: Exit
author: Belinda Bauer
description: A retired man volunteers for an assisted suicide group—then walks into the wrong room and finds a murder.

title: The Necessary Death of Lewis Winter
author: Malcolm Mackay
description: A Glasgow hitman's first contract—clinical, procedural, and the darkest Scottish noir. CWA Dagger for best first novel.

title: How a Gunman Says Goodbye
author: Malcolm Mackay
description: An aging hitman losing his edge—the organization sends his replacement. CWA Gold Dagger.

title: The Sudden Arrival of Violence
author: Malcolm Mackay
description: The Glasgow underworld reaches its reckoning—detectives and criminals converge. The trilogy concludes.

title: The Lie Tree
author: Frances Hardinge
description: A Victorian girl discovers a tree that feeds on lies and bears fruit of truth—Gothic feminist YA. Costa Book of the Year.

title: Fly by Night
author: Frances Hardinge
description: A girl and her goose travel through a land where words are controlled—Dickensian adventure meets Terry Pratchett.

title: A Face Like Glass
author: Frances Hardinge
description: An underground city where faces must be learned—a girl with natural expressions is dangerous.

title: Cuckoo Song
author: Frances Hardinge
description: A girl wakes after falling into a river and realizes she isn't herself—a changeling story set in 1920s England.

title: The Shock of the Fall
author: Nathan Filer
description: A young man with schizophrenia writes his brother's story—guilt, grief, and the unreliable narrator you root for. Costa Book of the Year.

title: The Mermaid of Black Conch
author: Monique Roffey
description: A fisherman catches a mermaid off Trinidad—she transforms into a woman, and the island must decide what to do. Costa Book of the Year.

title: Magpie Lane
author: Elizabeth Day
description: A nanny's relationship with a mute child becomes dangerously obsessive—Oxford Gothic. Costa winner.

title: A Song for a New Day
author: Sarah Pinsker
description: After a pandemic bans public gatherings, a musician discovers underground concerts—prescient pre-COVID novel. Nebula winner.

title: Christine Falls
author: Benjamin Black
description: A pathologist in 1950s Dublin investigates a woman's death—Catholic institutions, adoption, and John Banville writing noir under a pseudonym.

title: The Silver Swan
author: Benjamin Black
description: A beautiful woman dies of an overdose—Quirke suspects the grieving husband and investigates Dublin's dark side.

title: Hyena
author: David Means
description: Stories of American ruin—fires, violence, and the Rust Belt's slow collapse. Among the finest living short story writers.

title: Instructions for a Funeral
author: David Means
description: Stories of Michigan, New York, and the damaged American male—Means at his most precise and devastating.

title: Wench
author: Dolen Perkins-Valdez
description: Four enslaved women at an Ohio resort—their masters bring them every summer. PEN/Hemingway winner.

title: A Burning
author: Megha Majumdar
description: Three lives in contemporary India—a Muslim girl accused of terrorism, an aspiring actress, and a gym teacher who must choose sides.

title: The Great Man
author: Kate Christensen
description: A famous painter dies—his two mistresses and his wife each claim to know the real man. PEN/Faulkner winner.

title: The Caprices
author: Sabina Murray
description: Stories of WWII in the Pacific—Japanese soldiers, Filipino guerrillas, and American POWs. PEN/Faulkner winner.

title: The Group
author: Mary McCarthy
description: Eight Vassar graduates face the 1930s—sex, politics, careers, and the gap between idealism and reality. The original smart-women novel.

title: Memories of a Catholic Girlhood
author: Mary McCarthy
description: McCarthy's memoir of being orphaned and raised by cruel relatives—then escaping to Seattle and discovering her mind.

title: The Stones of Florence
author: Mary McCarthy
description: Florence as seen by a merciless intellect—art, politics, and the Florentine character dissected with precision.

title: Harvest
author: Jim Crace
description: A medieval village is enclosed and destroyed—Crace's spare, devastating fable about the end of a way of life. Booker shortlisted.

title: Being Dead
author: Jim Crace
description: A couple is murdered on a beach—their bodies decompose forward while their love story is told backward. Whitbread winner.

title: Quarantine
author: Jim Crace
description: Jesus's forty days in the desert—told by an atheist novelist who puts the miracle in doubt. Whitbread winner.

title: The Pesthouse
author: Jim Crace
description: America has collapsed—two people cross the ruined continent eastward, reversing the pioneer journey.

title: Hot Milk
author: Deborah Levy
description: A daughter takes her ailing mother to a clinic in Spain—desire, jellyfish, and the pull of maternal dependency. Booker shortlisted.

title: The Man Who Saw Everything
author: Deborah Levy
description: A historian is hit by a car in 1988 and 2016—two timelines blur as the Berlin Wall falls and rises. Booker shortlisted.

title: Real Estate
author: Deborah Levy
description: The final Living Autobiography—what does it mean to own a house, a body, a life? Levy at her most luminous.

title: The Cost of Living
author: Deborah Levy
description: Levy's divorce, her shed, Simone de Beauvoir—a feminist memoir about building a new life from rubble.

title: Late in the Day
author: Tessa Hadley
description: Two couples whose lives are intertwined—one husband dies suddenly, and everything shifts. Exquisite domestic fiction.

title: Free Love
author: Tessa Hadley
description: 1967 London—a diplomat's wife has an affair with a young radical and blows up her family. The sixties as personal earthquake.

title: The Past
author: Tessa Hadley
description: Four siblings spend three weeks at their grandparents' house—old tensions, new affairs, and whether to sell.

title: After the Funeral
author: Tessa Hadley
description: Stories spanning decades—a woman's shoe, a childhood friend, a marriage undone by what's not said.

title: Remainder
author: Tom McCarthy
description: A man receives a huge settlement after an accident—he uses it to reconstruct and re-enact moments of reality. Obsessively.

title: Satin Island
author: Tom McCarthy
description: A corporate anthropologist tries to write the Great Report on contemporary life—information overload as novel. Booker shortlisted.

title: C
author: Tom McCarthy
description: A boy born with the radio grows up amid signals—WWI, silk moths, and early wireless. Booker shortlisted.

title: Motel Chronicles
author: Sam Shepard
description: Prose fragments, poems, and photographs—the American road through Shepard's eyes: motels, horses, and nowhere to go.

title: Cruising Paradise
author: Sam Shepard
description: Stories of the West—drifters, rodeo riders, and men who can't stop moving. Shepard's finest fiction.

title: The One Inside
author: Sam Shepard
description: Shepard's final work—written as ALS took his body. Fragments of memory, identity dissolving, the desert.

title: Speedboat
author: Renata Adler
description: A journalist in 1970s New York—fragments of observation that feel like the novel reinvented. No plot, all perception.

title: Pitch Dark
author: Renata Adler
description: A woman leaves her married lover—Ireland, a stolen car, and sentences that break open like riddles.

title: Sleepless Nights
author: Elizabeth Hardwick
description: A woman's memories of New York, Kentucky, and a dissolving marriage—Hardwick's novel that reads like dreaming.

title: Reservoir 13
author: Jon McGregor
description: A girl vanishes from an English village—the novel follows the village for thirteen years as nature cycles on. Costa winner.

title: Darkmans
author: Nicola Barker
description: A town in Kent haunted by its medieval past—a jester, a chiropodist, and language that keeps slipping. Booker shortlisted.

title: Ingenious Pain
author: Andrew Miller
description: An 18th-century man born without the ability to feel pain—he becomes a surgeon, then must learn to feel. James Tait Black winner.

title: Pure
author: Andrew Miller
description: A young engineer is sent to clear a Paris cemetery in 1785—the stench of the dead and the coming revolution. Costa winner.

title: The Dark Room
author: Rachel Seiffert
description: Three stories of ordinary Germans during and after WWII—complicity, denial, and inherited guilt. Booker shortlisted.

title: The Electric Michelangelo
author: Sarah Hall
description: A tattoo artist from the English Lake District to Coney Island—the body as canvas. Booker shortlisted.

title: The Wolf Border
author: Sarah Hall
description: A zoologist reintroduces wolves to the English countryside—rewilding the land and herself.

title: When Rain Clouds Gather
author: Bessie Head
description: A South African political refugee settles in rural Botswana—community, farming, and the struggle to belong.

title: A Question of Power
author: Bessie Head
description: A woman in Botswana suffers a mental breakdown—racism, exile, and evil narrated from inside madness.

title: Measuring the World
author: Daniel Kehlmann
description: Humboldt the explorer and Gauss the mathematician—two geniuses who measured everything and understood nothing about people.

title: If We Were Villains
author: M.L. Rio
description: Seven Shakespeare students at an elite conservatory—one of them kills another on opening night. Dark academia meets The Secret History.

title: Unsouled
author: Will Wight
description: A boy born without power in a world of martial artists trains obsessively to prove everyone wrong—the start of Cradle, the biggest self-pub fantasy series.

title: Soulsmith
author: Will Wight
description: Lindon enters a sacred valley tournament to earn the right to leave—Cradle's power system deepens.

title: Blackflame
author: Will Wight
description: Lindon trains in destructive black flame techniques—the power scaling accelerates.

title: Skysworn
author: Will Wight
description: Lindon joins an elite organization and faces his first real threats—Cradle hits its stride.

title: Ghostwater
author: Will Wight
description: Lindon enters a pocket dimension of a dead Monarch—the fan-favorite volume of Cradle.

title: Underlord
author: Will Wight
description: Lindon advances to Underlord—the power system opens up and the stakes go global.

title: Waybound
author: Will Wight
description: The conclusion—Lindon faces the ultimate enemy and decides the fate of the Cradle universe.

title: Sufficiently Advanced Magic
author: Andrew Rowe
description: A boy enters a magical tower to find his brother—hard magic system, puzzle-solving, and academic fantasy.

title: On the Shoulders of Titans
author: Andrew Rowe
description: Corin enters the university and discovers deeper conspiracies within the tower system.

title: He Who Fights with Monsters
author: Shirtaloon
description: An Australian is transported to a world with a video game-like system—snarky, smart LitRPG. Massive on Royal Road.

title: He Who Fights with Monsters 2
author: Shirtaloon
description: Jason continues leveling up and navigating political intrigue in the magical world—the series deepens.

title: Dungeon Born
author: Dakota Krout
description: A dungeon becomes sentient and must grow to survive—told from the dungeon's perspective. Genre-defining LitRPG.

title: The Court of Broken Knives
author: Anna Smith Spark
description: A squad of mercenaries is hired to kill the emperor—grimdark with poetic prose and no heroes.

title: The Tower of Living and Dying
author: Anna Smith Spark
description: The conqueror's army marches—death, addiction, and the seductiveness of power. Grimdark at its most literary.

title: Blackwing
author: Ed McDonald
description: A bounty hunter in a post-apocalyptic wasteland investigates a conspiracy—dark, gritty secondary world fantasy.

title: Ravencry
author: Ed McDonald
description: The wasteland is changing—Galharrow faces a new threat from the deep kings as the Misery expands.

title: Crowfall
author: Ed McDonald
description: The conclusion—Galharrow must end the war between light and dark or lose everyone he loves.

title: Never Die
author: Rob Hayes
description: A boy resurrects dead warriors to kill the emperor—wuxia meets grimdark in this SPFBO winner.

title: Air Awakens
author: Elise Kova
description: A librarian discovers she has wind magic and is conscripted into the war—romantasy with political intrigue.

title: Pestilence
author: Laura Thalassa
description: The first horseman of the apocalypse rides—a woman is chained to him and forced to travel as he destroys the world.

title: War
author: Laura Thalassa
description: The second horseman is pure brutality—a woman soldier is captured and becomes his reluctant companion.

title: Famine
author: Laura Thalassa
description: The third horseman kills crops—a botanist is immune to his power and he wants to know why.

title: Death
author: Laura Thalassa
description: The final horseman—a woman who keeps dying and resurrecting catches Death's attention.

title: Iron Prince
author: Bryce O'Connor
description: A boy with the lowest combat rating enters a military academy—mech suits, progression, and underdog triumph.

title: The Wild Iris
author: Louise Glück
description: Poems spoken by flowers, a gardener, and God—each voice addresses the others across a single growing season. Pulitzer winner. Nobel laureate.

title: Faithful and Virtuous Night
author: Louise Glück
description: A painter reflects on art, childhood, and death—Glück's most dreamlike collection. National Book Award.

title: The Half-Finished Heaven
author: Tomas Tranströmer
description: Selected poems of the Swedish Nobel laureate—precise images from a man who communicated through poems after a stroke silenced him.

title: I, Claudius
author: Robert Graves
description: The stammering fool of the Roman imperial family secretly records its history—from Augustus to Caligula. One of the greatest historical novels ever written.

title: Claudius the God
author: Robert Graves
description: Claudius becomes emperor despite himself—governing Rome while his wife Messalina and then Agrippina plot behind his back.

title: Goodbye to All That
author: Robert Graves
description: Graves's WWI memoir—the trenches, the shattered nerves, and the savage farewell to England that made him an exile.

title: Wide Sargasso Sea
author: Jean Rhys
description: The madwoman in the attic speaks—Jane Eyre's Bertha is a Creole heiress destroyed by her English husband and colonial violence.

title: Good Morning, Midnight
author: Jean Rhys
description: A woman alone in 1930s Paris—drinking, remembering, and dissolving. Rhys's masterpiece of female desolation.

title: Darkness at Noon
author: Arthur Koestler
description: An old Bolshevik is arrested by the Party he helped create—interrogated into confessing crimes he didn't commit. The definitive novel of totalitarianism.

title: All the King's Men
author: Robert Penn Warren
description: A populist Southern governor rises and corrupts everything around him—the Pulitzer-winning novel of American political power.

title: Deliverance
author: James Dickey
description: Four Atlanta businessmen canoe a wild Georgia river—the trip becomes a nightmare of survival and violence.

title: The Moviegoer
author: Walker Percy
description: A New Orleans stockbroker searches for meaning at the movies—existential malaise in the suburbs. First novel to win the National Book Award.

title: The Last Gentleman
author: Walker Percy
description: A confused Southerner with a telescope follows a dying man's family across America—Percy's comic odyssey.

title: The Bridge of San Luis Rey
author: Thornton Wilder
description: Five people die when a bridge collapses in 18th-century Peru—a monk investigates whether their deaths had meaning. Pulitzer winner.

title: Tobacco Road
author: Erskine Caldwell
description: A Georgia sharecropper family in extreme poverty—grotesque, funny, and the novel that shocked Depression-era America.

title: A Dance to the Music of Time: A Question of Upbringing
author: Anthony Powell
description: The first of twelve novels following English upper-class life from WWI to the 1970s—Proust meets the Establishment.

series.name: A Dance to the Music of Time

title: A Buyer's Market
author: Anthony Powell
description: Nick Jenkins enters 1920s London society—parties, debutantes, and the eccentric Widmerpool.

series.name: A Dance to the Music of Time

title: The Acceptance World
author: Anthony Powell
description: The 1930s—Nick falls in love, Widmerpool rises, and the group navigates marriage and ambition.

series.name: A Dance to the Music of Time

title: Loving
author: Henry Green
description: Servants in an Irish castle during WWII—gossip, theft, desire, and Green's luminous dialogue. The greatest English novel you haven't read.

title: Party Going
author: Henry Green
description: Rich young people are trapped in a fog-bound London railway station—class, boredom, and the comedy of manners at its purest.

title: Zuleika Dobson
author: Max Beerbohm
description: A beautiful woman visits Oxford and every undergraduate falls in love with her—they all drown themselves. The wittiest satire of Oxford ever written.

title: Kim Ji-young, Born 1982
author: Cho Nam-Joo
description: A Korean everywoman's life from birth to motherhood—systemic sexism rendered in clinical, devastating prose. Sold a million copies in Korea.

title: The Old Garden
author: Hwang Sok-yong
description: A political prisoner is released after 18 years—his lover is dead, Korea has changed. The great novel of the democratization movement.

title: At Dusk
author: Hwang Sok-yong
description: A successful architect receives messages from a woman he wronged decades ago—guilt, capitalism, and modern Seoul.

title: Please Look After Mom
author: Shin Kyung-sook
description: A mother vanishes in the Seoul subway—her family searches and realizes how little they knew her. International bestseller.

title: I Have the Right to Destroy Myself
author: Kim Young-ha
description: A man helps people commit suicide in Seoul—two brothers love the same woman. Korean nihilism as cold precision.

title: History: A Novel
author: Elsa Morante
description: A woman and her son survive WWII in Rome—the war seen from the absolute bottom of society. Italy's greatest postwar novel.

title: Arturo's Island
author: Elsa Morante
description: A boy grows up wild on a Mediterranean island—his father is absent, and a young stepmother changes everything.

title: The Conformist
author: Alberto Moravia
description: A man joins the fascists to feel normal—then is ordered to assassinate his former professor. Conformity as moral suicide.

title: Contempt
author: Alberto Moravia
description: A screenwriter's wife despises him—is it because he prostituted himself to a producer? The novel Godard filmed.

title: Boredom
author: Alberto Moravia
description: A painter is bored by everything except his young mistress—obsession with a girl who feels nothing.

title: Six Characters in Search of an Author
author: Luigi Pirandello
description: Six fictional characters interrupt a rehearsal demanding their story be performed—reality vs. illusion. Nobel laureate.

title: Confessions
author: Kanae Minato
description: A teacher announces her daughter was killed by two students in the class—then reveals her revenge. Japanese psychological thriller phenomenon.

title: The Thief
author: Fuminori Nakamura
description: A Tokyo pickpocket targets a yakuza wallet—his perfect skill draws him into a conspiracy. Japanese noir at its most elegant.

title: Evil and the Mask
author: Fuminori Nakamura
description: A boy is told he was born to be a vessel of evil—he undergoes plastic surgery to become someone new. Dark philosophical thriller.

title: House Made of Dawn
author: N. Scott Momaday
description: A Native American man returns from WWII to his Pueblo—caught between worlds, lost in both. The first Pulitzer for a Native writer.

title: The Way to Rainy Mountain
author: N. Scott Momaday
description: The Kiowa migration from Montana to Oklahoma—myth, history, and memoir braided into seventy pages of beauty.

title: Suddenly, a Knock at the Door
author: Etgar Keret
description: Flash fiction from Israel's most beloved writer—a man orders a story at gunpoint, a woman swallows her husband. Surreal, funny, heartbreaking.

title: The Bus Driver Who Wanted to Be God
author: Etgar Keret
description: Stories of Israeli life—soldiers, angels, goldfish, and people who can't connect. Keret at his purest.

title: Fly Already
author: Etgar Keret
description: A father, a son, a dying man who won't die—Keret's darkest, warmest collection.

title: Stay with Me
author: Ayọ̀bámi Adébáyọ̀
description: A Nigerian couple's marriage is tested when she can't conceive—polygamy, tradition, and a devastating secret.

title: The Girl with the Louding Voice
author: Abi Daré
description: A Nigerian girl sold into domestic service fights for an education—her broken English is her weapon and her poetry.

title: Our Lady of the Nile
author: Scholastique Mukasonga
description: A Catholic girls' school in 1970s Rwanda—ethnic tension simmers years before the genocide. By a genocide survivor.

title: Black Moses
author: Alain Mabanckou
description: An orphan in 1970s Congo-Brazzaville survives through cunning—Dickens meets African childhood. France's most prominent African writer.

title: African Psycho
author: Alain Mabanckou
description: A petty criminal in Brazzaville tries to commit his first murder—he keeps failing. American Psycho's African cousin.

title: Kintu
author: Jennifer Nansubuga Makumbi
description: A Ugandan family over 250 years—a curse from the founding patriarch echoes through every generation. Africa's One Hundred Years of Solitude.

title: The Tunnel
author: Ernesto Sabato
description: A painter murders the only woman who understood his art—he tells us why from prison. Argentine existentialism at its most claustrophobic.

title: Trick
author: Domenico Starnone
description: A grandfather babysits his grandchildren—one locks him in a room. A novella about aging, family, and captivity. Rumored to be Elena Ferrante.

title: Ties
author: Domenico Starnone
description: A husband leaves his wife for a younger woman—decades later, the wife's fury resurfaces. Translated by Jhumpa Lahiri.

title: The Eight Mountains
author: Paolo Cognetti
description: Two boys—one from Milan, one from the Alps—grow up and apart on a mountain. Italian Stoner. International bestseller.

title: Mornings in Jenin
author: Susan Abulhawa
description: A Palestinian family across four generations—from 1948 to the present, through every war and displacement.

title: Ragazzi di Vita
author: Pier Paolo Pasolini
description: Boys in the Roman slums—theft, sex, and survival in postwar Italy. Pasolini's scandalous debut that got him prosecuted.

title: Chronicle of a Last Summer
author: Yasmine El Rashidi
description: A Cairo girl grows up through Mubarak's Egypt to the Arab Spring—each section a different summer, a different revolution.

title: Women Without Men
author: Shahrnush Parsipur
description: Five Iranian women escape their lives to a garden—magical realism meets feminist resistance. Banned in Iran.

title: Super Sad True Love Story
author: Gary Shteyngart
description: A middle-aged Russian American and a young Korean American fall in love as America collapses into debt and surveillance—prescient and hilarious.

title: Absurdistan
author: Gary Shteyngart
description: A 325-pound Russian oligarch's son is stranded in a Central Asian republic during a civil war—satire of post-Soviet chaos.

title: The Russian Debutante's Handbook
author: Gary Shteyngart
description: A Russian immigrant in New York gets involved in a scam in Eastern Europe—Shteyngart's manic debut.

title: Our Country Friends
author: Gary Shteyngart
description: Eight friends quarantine at a country house during COVID—Chekhov meets the pandemic.

title: Three Lives
author: Gertrude Stein
description: Three portraits of working-class women—Stein's most accessible work and the beginning of literary modernism.

title: The Autobiography of Alice B. Toklas
author: Gertrude Stein
description: Stein writes her own memoir in the voice of her partner—Paris, Picasso, Hemingway, and the invention of modern art.

title: Goodbye to Berlin
author: Christopher Isherwood
description: I am a camera—Weimar Berlin's nightlife, Sally Bowles, and the Nazis rising. The book that became Cabaret.

title: A Single Man
author: Christopher Isherwood
description: One day in the life of a gay English professor in 1960s LA—grief, desire, and being a minority of one.

title: The Berlin Stories
author: Christopher Isherwood
description: Mr Norris Changes Trains and Goodbye to Berlin combined—the definitive portrait of the Weimar Republic's last days.

title: The Death of the Heart
author: Elizabeth Bowen
description: A sixteen-year-old orphan disrupts a London household—innocence against sophisticated cruelty. Bowen's masterpiece.

title: The Heat of the Day
author: Elizabeth Bowen
description: A woman in wartime London discovers her lover may be a spy—the Blitz as emotional landscape.

title: The Last September
author: Elizabeth Bowen
description: An Anglo-Irish big house during the Troubles of 1920—tennis, parties, and the IRA in the shrubbery.

title: Red Cavalry
author: Isaac Babel
description: Stories of the 1920 Polish-Soviet War—a Jewish intellectual rides with Cossacks. Violence and beauty in equal measure.

title: Odessa Stories
author: Isaac Babel
description: The Jewish gangsters of Odessa—Benya Krik the King, his gang, and the life of the street. Babel at his most exuberant.

title: The Spinning Heart
author: Donal Ryan
description: Twenty-one voices in a ruined Irish village after the Celtic Tiger crash—each monologue a life in fragments.

title: Strange Flowers
author: Donal Ryan
description: A girl vanishes from rural Ireland in 1973 and returns five years later with a mixed-race child—silence, love, and small-town Ireland.

title: All We Shall Know
author: Donal Ryan
description: A pregnant schoolteacher and a Traveller woman form an unlikely bond—two outcasts in small-town Ireland.

title: The Mambo Kings Play Songs of Love
author: Oscar Hijuelos
description: Two Cuban brothers in 1950s New York play mambo—music, sex, and the immigrant dream. First Hispanic Pulitzer winner.

title: Exciting Times
author: Naoise Dolan
description: An Irish woman in Hong Kong has affairs with a British banker and a local lawyer—Sally Rooney meets expat malaise.

title: Selected Poems
author: Federico García Lorca
description: Spain's greatest poet—Gypsy Ballads, Poet in New York, and the duende. Murdered by fascists at 38.

title: At Night We Walk in Circles
author: Daniel Alarcón
description: An actor joins a theater troupe touring the Peruvian countryside—the play about a disappeared man becomes dangerously real.

title: Lost City Radio
author: Daniel Alarcón
description: A radio host in an unnamed South American country reunites families separated by civil war—a boy arrives with a list of names.

title: Typical American
author: Gish Jen
description: A Chinese immigrant family in postwar America chases success—the Changs become typical Americans, for better and worse.

title: Mona in the Promised Land
author: Gish Jen
description: A Chinese American teenager in 1960s suburbia converts to Judaism—identity as infinite choice.

title: The Queue
author: Vladimir Sorokin
description: An entire novel set in a Soviet queue—told entirely through dialogue. What are they waiting for? No one knows.

title: Day of the Oprichnik
author: Vladimir Sorokin
description: Russia in 2028—a tsar rules behind a new Great Wall, and a secret policeman's day of violence and perversion. Satirical dystopia.

title: Ice Trilogy
author: Vladimir Sorokin
description: A meteorite in Siberia contains ice that awakens chosen humans—Sorokin's most ambitious and disturbing work.

title: Medea and Her Children
author: Ludmila Ulitskaya
description: A Crimean matriarch holds her sprawling family together across the Soviet century—Ulitskaya's breakthrough.

title: The Big Green Tent
author: Ludmila Ulitskaya
description: Three friends from school through the Soviet era—dissidence, compromise, and the samizdat underground.

title: The Light and the Dark
author: Mikhail Shishkin
description: Love letters between a woman and a soldier—but they're in different times. Shishkin's masterpiece of collapsing chronology.

title: The Aviator
author: Eugene Vodolazkin
description: A man wakes from cryogenic sleep in 1990s St. Petersburg—he fell asleep in the Gulag in the 1930s. Memory as resurrection.

title: Laurus
author: Eugene Vodolazkin
description: A medieval Russian healer's journey from hermit to holy fool—the life of a saint told with postmodern playfulness.

title: Dreams of My Russian Summers
author: Andrei Makine
description: A Russian boy's French grandmother tells him stories of her youth—two cultures, two languages, one beautiful novel. Prix Goncourt winner.

title: The Sot-Weed Factor
author: John Barth
description: A picaresque epic of colonial Maryland—a poet laureate's virginity is under constant threat. The funniest postmodern novel.

title: The Universal Baseball Association, Inc., J. Henry Waugh, Prop.
author: Robert Coover
description: An accountant creates a tabletop baseball game so complex it becomes more real than his life—metafiction meets sports obsession.

title: Dreaming in Cuban
author: Cristina García
description: Three generations of Cuban women—one stays loyal to Castro, one flees to Brooklyn, one paints. The Revolution as family schism.

title: Natasha and Other Stories
author: David Bezmozgis
description: A Latvian Jewish boy grows up in Toronto—immigration, identity, and the Soviet past in seven perfect stories.

title: A Replacement Life
author: Boris Fishman
description: A Russian Jewish grandson forges Holocaust restitution claims for his immigrant community—love and fraud in Brooklyn.

title: Still Here
author: Lara Vapnyar
description: Russian immigrants in Staten Island create an app that predicts your death date—Silicon Valley meets Soviet nostalgia.

title: Young Skins
author: Colin Barrett
description: Stories of young men in a small Irish town—drink, fights, boredom, and flashes of tenderness. The Irish Raymond Carver.

title: Wild Houses
author: Colin Barrett
description: A kidnapping in a Mayo town—two brothers, a hostage, and a woman caught between them. Barrett's debut novel.

title: The Shelters of Stone
author: Jean Auel
description: Ayla and Jondalar arrive at his home—she must win acceptance from his people while introducing revolutionary ideas.

title: The Land of Painted Caves
author: Jean Auel
description: Ayla trains as a spiritual leader—visiting the painted caves of prehistoric France. The final Earth's Children.

title: A Shadow in the Ember
author: Jennifer L. Armentrout
description: The prequel to From Blood and Ash—Sera is trained to kill a god, but falls for him instead.

title: Until November
author: Åsa Larsson
description: Rebecka investigates a murdered woman found under the ice of a frozen lake—Kiruna's darkest secrets.

title: Rizzoli & Isles: The Silent Girl
author: Tess Gerritsen
description: A Chinatown massacre connects to a decades-old murder—Rizzoli and Isles navigate Boston's most secretive community.

title: The Veiled Threat
author: Abir Mukherjee
description: Wyndham and Banerjee investigate a murder at a maharaja's palace during partition—the final Sam Wyndham.

title: Before
author: Anna Todd
description: Hardin's perspective—the events of After told from the man who destroyed everything.

title: Verity and Vengeance
author: Aleatha Romig
description: The final Consequences—the full truth about Tony and Claire's tangled web of power and obsession.

title: Dragondrums
author: Anne McCaffrey
description: Piemur, a drum apprentice, is caught up in political intrigue as his voice breaks—the third Harper Hall.

title: Cometh the Hour
author: Jeffrey Archer
description: Harry Clifton faces his greatest challenge as secrets from the past threaten to destroy everything.

title: This Was a Man
author: Jeffrey Archer
description: The final Clifton Chronicles—decades of rivalry, love, and ambition reach their conclusion.

title: The Attic on Queen Street
author: Karen White
description: Melanie and Jack face one final haunting on Tradd Street—ghosts, family, and the house that holds them.

title: Dreams of Falling
author: Karen White
description: A woman returns to South Carolina after her mother collapses—family secrets from the 1950s surface.

title: Swords and Ice Magic
author: Fritz Leiber
description: Fafhrd and the Mouser face aging, gods, and ice magic—the sixth collection of their adventures.

title: The Knight and Knave of Swords
author: Fritz Leiber
description: The final Fafhrd and Mouser—the heroes settle down, sort of, but adventure won't leave them alone.

title: Flavia de Luce: I Am Half-Sick of Shadows
author: Alan Bradley
description: A film crew arrives at Buckshaw for Christmas—then an actress is found strangled. Flavia investigates.

title: The Grave's a Fine and Private Place
author: Alan Bradley
description: Flavia finds a body in a river during a punt ride—the corpse has a communion wafer on its tongue.

title: The Golden Tresses of the Dead
author: Alan Bradley
description: A finger appears in a wedding cake—Flavia launches a detective agency to investigate.

title: Isn't It Bromantic?
author: Lyssa Kay Adams
description: A Russian former hockey player writes romance novels in secret—and falls for the woman helping him improve his English.

title: Shadow Campaigns: The Infernal Battalion
author: Django Wexler
description: The final Shadow Campaigns—Winter and Marcus face the Beast and the fate of the revolution.

title: The Guns of Empire
author: Django Wexler
description: The armies march on the heartland of the enemy—Winter commands while the supernatural threat grows.

title: Crucible of Gold
author: Naomi Novik
description: Laurence and Temeraire are sent to South America—Inca dragon politics and Napoleon's schemes.

title: Blood of Tyrants
author: Naomi Novik
description: Laurence loses his memory in Japan—Temeraire must convince him they're partners while war looms.

title: League of Dragons
author: Naomi Novik
description: The final Temeraire—Napoleon's last campaign, the Congress of Vienna, and dragon rights.

title: Across the Green Grass Fields
author: Seanan McGuire
description: A girl who doesn't fit in falls into a world of centaurs—the Wayward Children explore the Hooflands.

title: Where the Drowned Girls Go
author: Seanan McGuire
description: A student transfers to a sinister school that suppresses portal world memories—darker than Eleanor West's.

title: Mislaid in Parts Half-Known
author: Seanan McGuire
description: A student must return to a portal world to save a friend—the Wayward Children continue.

title: Caesar
author: Colleen McCullough
description: Caesar crosses the Rubicon, conquers Gaul, and falls for Cleopatra—the climax of the Masters of Rome.

title: The October Horse
author: Colleen McCullough
description: Caesar's assassination and its aftermath—Brutus, Cassius, and the end of the Republic.

title: Antony and Cleopatra
author: Colleen McCullough
description: Octavian rises as Antony and Cleopatra fall—the final Masters of Rome.

title: First Among Sequels
author: Jasper Fforde
description: Thursday Next is a middle-aged librarian—but the BookWorld still needs her, and her fictional self is out of control.

title: One of Our Thursdays Is Missing
author: Jasper Fforde
description: The written Thursday Next searches for the real one—metafiction goes full recursive.

title: The Woman Who Died a Lot
author: Jasper Fforde
description: Thursday recovers from assassination while preventing the smiting of Swindon by an angry deity—Fforde at peak absurdity.

title: Many Waters
author: Madeleine L'Engle
description: Sandy and Dennys are transported to biblical times—Noah's family, nephilim, and the coming flood.

title: An Acceptable Time
author: Madeleine L'Engle
description: Polly travels three thousand years into the past—druids, sacrifice, and whether to change history.

title: The Wicked Day
author: Mary Stewart
description: Mordred's story—Arthur's illegitimate son is not the villain of legend. Stewart's most sympathetic portrait.

title: The Prince and the Pilgrim
author: Mary Stewart
description: Two young people in Arthur's world—a prince seeking vengeance and a girl on a pilgrimage.

title: The Magic Labyrinth
author: Philip José Farmer
description: The Riverworld's purpose is revealed—who resurrected humanity and why?

title: Gods of Riverworld
author: Philip José Farmer
description: The heroes reach the tower at the source—and must decide what to do with ultimate power.

title: Paladin's Faith
author: T. Kingfisher
description: A paladin escorts a spy across enemy territory—trust issues, attraction, and secret identities.

title: Paladin's Prayer
author: T. Kingfisher
description: The final Saint of Steel—a paladin and a scholar must stop a magical plague.

title: Ride with Me, Mariah Montana
author: Ivan Doig
description: A rancher and his journalist daughter travel Montana during the centennial—Doig's farewell to the McCaskill family.

title: The Veiled Throne
author: Ken Liu
description: The third Dandelion Dynasty—occupied lands revolt and new technologies reshape warfare.

title: Crown of Stars: The Gathering Storm
author: Kate Elliott
description: The gathering storm approaches as Sanglant and Liath's fates converge—the fifth Crown of Stars.

title: In the Ruins
author: Kate Elliott
description: The cataclysm strikes—civilizations fall as ancient magic reshapes the world.

title: Crown of Stars
author: Kate Elliott
description: The epic conclusion—Sanglant and Liath face the final battle for the fate of all kingdoms.

title: The Assassin's Blade
author: Sarah J. Maas
description: Five prequel novellas—Celaena's missions before the competition. Essential backstory for the Throne of Glass series.

title: The Navigator's Children
author: Tad Williams
description: The conclusion of The Last King of Osten Ard—the final battle for the Hayholt.

series.name: The Last King of Osten Ard

title: The Keeper
author: David Baldacci
description: Archer's latest case—Hollywood noir in the 1950s.

title: Broken and Beloved
author: Lyla Sage
description: The final Rebel Blue Ranch—the last brother finds love.

title: The Playbook
author: Liz Tomforde
description: The final Windy City—a football player and a woman who hates the spotlight.

title: Be With Me
author: Becka Mack
description: The final Playing for Keeps—the last hockey player finds his match.

title: The Mistress of Bhatia House
author: Sujata Massey
description: Perveen investigates a case involving an expectant mother and a threatening employer—1920s Bombay.

title: The Queen of Bone and Ash
author: Vaseem Khan
description: Persis Wadia investigates a case connected to India's most powerful family—the fourth Malabar House.

title: Where You Once Belonged
author: Kent Haruf
description: A football star returns to Holt after embezzling from the town—everyone watches and nobody acts.

title: Emily Wilde's Compendium of Lost Tales
author: Heather Fawcett
description: The third Emily Wilde—the prickly scholar faces her most dangerous faerie encounter yet.

title: Dawnbringer
author: Hannah Kaner
description: The final Fallen Gods—Kissen and Inara face the war god in the trilogy's conclusion.

title: The House of Sundering Flames
author: Aliette de Bodard
description: Paris burns as Houses go to war—the final Dominion of the Fallen.

title: Lavender and Lightning
author: Brynne Weaver
description: The final Ruinous Love Trilogy—the last killer finds their match.

title: Sovereignty
author: Tracy Deonn
description: The Legendborn Cycle concludes—Bree must claim her full power to save both worlds.

title: The Sacred Band
author: David Anthony Durham
description: The Akarans must unite the Known World against the Lothan Aklun—the Acacia trilogy concludes.

title: Artifact Space
author: Miles Cameron
description: A young officer on humanity's sole interstellar trading ship discovers a conspiracy—military sci-fi from the Traitor Son author.

title: The Dread Wyrm
author: Miles Cameron
description: The Red Knight faces the ultimate dragon—the third Traitor Son Cycle.

title: The Third Kingdom
author: Terry Goodkind
description: Richard faces the undead walking—the Sword of Truth continues in a new trilogy.

title: Severed Souls
author: Terry Goodkind
description: Richard and Kahlan are poisoned by the undead—they must reach help before it's too late.

title: When You See Me
author: Lisa Gardner
description: D.D. Warren, Flora Dane, and Kimberly Quincy investigate a corpse found on a Georgia mountain—connected to a decade of kidnappings.

title: Before She Disappeared
author: Lisa Gardner
description: Frankie Elkin searches for missing people nobody else cares about—a Haitian girl in Boston.

title: Seeds of Yesterday
author: V.C. Andrews
description: The Dollanganger children are old—Foxworth Hall burns as the cycle of family horror repeats.

title: Garden of Shadows
author: V.C. Andrews
description: The prequel—how the grandmother who locked children in the attic became a monster.

title: The Summer Tree
author: Guy Gavriel Kay
description: Five university students are transported to Fionavar, the first of all worlds—sacrifice, prophecy, and the Unraveller.

title: The Wandering Fire
author: Guy Gavriel Kay
description: Winter locks Fionavar—Arthur Pendragon is summoned as the war against Rakoth Maugrim escalates.

title: The Darkest Road
author: Guy Gavriel Kay
description: The final battle for Fionavar—all the threads of myth and sacrifice converge.

title: The Killing Moon
author: N.K. Jemisin
description: A priest who harvests dreams discovers corruption in his city—Egyptian-inspired fantasy from the three-time Hugo winner.

title: The Shadowed Sun
author: N.K. Jemisin
description: An occupied city, a healer, and a prince—the Dreamblood duology concludes.

title: Poseidon's Wake
author: Alastair Reynolds
description: Humanity reaches another star—and finds something waiting. The Poseidon's Children conclusion.

title: Dead Man Upright
author: Derek Raymond
description: The final Factory novel—the unnamed sergeant faces the most depraved killer of the series.

title: The Awakening
author: Nora Roberts
description: A young woman discovers she's the key to saving a magical realm—the first Dragon Heart Legacy.

title: The Becoming
author: Nora Roberts
description: Breen returns to Talamh and trains for war—the second Dragon Heart Legacy.

title: The Choice
author: Nora Roberts
description: Breen faces the dark god in the final battle—the Dragon Heart Legacy concludes.

title: Lasher
author: Anne Rice
description: The spirit becomes flesh—Rowan Mayfair's child is something ancient and dangerous.

title: Taltos
author: Anne Rice
description: Ashlar, the last Taltos, emerges—the Mayfair Witches saga reaches its explosive conclusion.

title: River of Smoke
author: Amitav Ghosh
description: The opium trade reaches Canton—Indian merchants, Chinese officials, and the storm building toward the Opium Wars.

title: Flood of Fire
author: Amitav Ghosh
description: The First Opium War erupts—the Ibis trilogy concludes as empires collide over drug profits.

title: Passage of Power
author: Robert Caro
description: LBJ from political exile to the presidency—JFK's assassination and the transition of power. The fourth Lyndon Johnson volume.

title: Night's Sorceries
author: Tanith Lee
description: The fifth Flat Earth—new tales of gods and mortals in the world where death and desire rule.

title: βehemoth
author: Peter Watts
description: The catastrophe spreads from the ocean floor to the surface—the Rifters trilogy concludes with global biological warfare.

title: Binti: Home
author: Nnedi Okorafor
description: Binti returns home to the Himba people—but she's changed, and so has her relationship with her culture.

title: Binti: The Night Masquerade
author: Nnedi Okorafor
description: War comes to Earth—Binti must unite humans and Meduse in the trilogy's conclusion.

title: The Broken Kingdoms
author: N.K. Jemisin
description: A blind artist in Shadow takes in a homeless man—he's a godling, and someone is murdering his kind.

title: The Kingdom of Gods
author: N.K. Jemisin
description: The trickster god Sieh is cursed with mortality—the Inheritance trilogy concludes with divine politics.

title: Of Blood and Bone
author: Nora Roberts
description: Fallon Swift grows into her powers—the Chosen One must unite the light against the dark. Chronicles of The One continues.

title: The Rise of Magicks
author: Nora Roberts
description: Fallon leads the final battle between good and evil—the Chronicles of The One concludes.

title: Born in Ice
author: Nora Roberts
description: The practical sister runs a B&B—an American writer staying there challenges her ordered life.

title: Born in Shame
author: Nora Roberts
description: A woman discovers she's the illegitimate daughter of an Irish artist—she travels to Ireland and finds family.

title: The Boy Who Followed Ripley
author: Patricia Highsmith
description: A teenage boy seeks out Ripley—he's killed his father, and only Tom understands. Highsmith's most sympathetic Ripley novel.

title: Ripley Under Water
author: Patricia Highsmith
description: Ripley's neighbors suspect him—the final Ripley novel, as Tom fights to protect his manufactured paradise.

title: The Stricken Field
author: Dave Duncan
description: Rap faces the evil dwarf Zinixo—the third Man of His Word.

title: Emperor and Clown
author: Dave Duncan
description: The final confrontation—Rap must save the world using every word of power he's gathered.

title: Ravenheart
author: David Gemmell
description: A young Rigante man defies the occupying Varlish empire—three centuries after the first Rigante novels.

title: Stormrider
author: David Gemmell
description: The final Rigante—the spirit of the Rigante rises against tyranny in an explosive conclusion.

title: Eagles in the Storm
author: Ben Kane
description: The Romans return to Germany seeking vengeance for Teutoburg—Arminius faces Rome's fury.

title: Hunting the Eagles
author: Ben Kane
description: A Roman centurion hunts for the lost eagles of the Teutoburg Forest—deep behind German lines.

title: Wings of the Storm
author: Giles Kristian
description: Sigurd gathers a war band and a ship—the second Rise of Sigurd.

title: Winter's Fire
author: Giles Kristian
description: Sigurd faces his greatest battle—the Rise of Sigurd trilogy concludes in blood and glory.

title: The Fix
author: David Baldacci
description: Decker witnesses a man shoot a woman outside the FBI—the victim has no connection to the killer. Or does she?

title: The Last Mile
author: David Baldacci
description: Decker investigates a death row case—a man convicted of murder may have been framed by someone with a bigger secret.

title: The Tie That Binds
author: Kent Haruf
description: An old man narrates his neighbor's life—a woman who sacrificed everything for her family's farm in Holt, Colorado.

title: The Great Ordeal
author: R. Scott Bakker
description: The Great Ordeal marches north—the Aspect-Emperor faces the Consult as madness spreads through the army.

title: The Unholy Consult
author: R. Scott Bakker
description: The Ordeal reaches Golgotterath—Kellhus faces the No-God in the most devastating fantasy finale ever written.

title: The Ghost Ship
author: Kate Mosse
description: The third Burning Chambers—pirates, the Dutch Golden Age, and a family scattered across the 17th century.

title: The House of Sacrifice
author: Anna Smith Spark
description: Marith the Ansikanderakesis Amrakane reaches the height of his power—and it destroys everything.

title: Diamantine
author: Andrew Rowe
description: Corin enters the tournament—higher-level magic, new enemies, and the tower's deeper secrets.

title: The Death of Dulgath
author: Michael J. Sullivan
description: Royce and Hadrian are hired to protect a woman marked for death—the Riyria Chronicles continue.

title: The Disappearance of Winter's Daughter
author: Michael J. Sullivan
description: A nobleman's wife vanishes in the city of Rochelle—Royce and Hadrian investigate religious persecution.

title: Infinity's Shore
author: David Brin
description: The fugitive races on Jijo face galactic authorities—secrets of uplift technology threaten everything.

title: Heaven's Reach
author: David Brin
description: The galaxy itself is transforming—the Uplift saga concludes as civilizations face cosmic change.

title: A Beautiful Wedding
author: Jamie McGuire
description: The Vegas wedding from Beautiful Disaster—Travis and Abby's wild night told in full.

title: Period
author: Dennis Cooper
description: The George Miles Cycle concludes—a filmmaker's obsession with a missing boy reaches its nihilistic endpoint.

title: Try
author: Dennis Cooper
description: Two teenagers in LA—abuse, desire, and Cooper's most accessible novel about damaged youth.

title: Rejoice
author: Karen Kingsbury
description: The Baxter family faces new challenges—faith, forgiveness, and a prodigal's return.

title: Reunion
author: Karen Kingsbury
description: The final Redemption—the Baxter family comes together for a reunion that heals old wounds.

title: Beautiful Burn
author: Jamie McGuire
description: The final Maddox brother—a firefighter and a woman running from her past.

title: Beautiful Chaos
author: Jamie McGuire
description: The youngest Maddox—a tornado hunter falls for a storm chaser.

title: Happily Ever Ninja
author: Penny Reid
description: A married couple's love is tested when she goes on a dangerous mission—marriage as adventure.

title: Dating-ish
author: Penny Reid
description: A journalist investigates a sex robot company and falls for the engineer who built them.

title: Working
author: Robert Caro
description: Caro on research and writing—the methods behind his LBJ and Robert Moses books. Not the 5th LBJ volume (still unfinished) but essential Caro.

title: The Temporal Void
author: Peter F. Hamilton
description: The Living Dream pilgrimage advances toward the Void—the inner story of Edeard the Waterwalker deepens.

title: The Evolutionary Void
author: Peter F. Hamilton
description: The final confrontation with the Void—the fate of the galaxy depends on understanding what it really is.

title: Close Quarters
author: William Golding
description: The voyage continues—the ship is becalmed and the passengers descend into boredom and madness.

series.name: To the Ends of the Earth

title: Fire Down Below
author: William Golding
description: The ship reaches Australia at last—the trilogy's conclusion as Talbot confronts colonial reality.

series.name: To the Ends of the Earth

title: The Faded Sun: Shon'jir
author: C.J. Cherryh
description: Sten and the mri journey across space—a human must become alien to save an alien species.

title: The Faded Sun: Kutath
author: C.J. Cherryh
description: The mri homeworld Kutath—humanity and the regul converge for a final reckoning with the warrior race.

title: Shadow Spell
author: Nora Roberts
description: The second O'Dwyer cousin confronts the dark sorcerer—horseback riding, magic, and Irish romance.

title: Blood Magick
author: Nora Roberts
description: The third cousin faces the demon—the O'Dwyer trilogy's magical conclusion.

title: Red Lily
author: Nora Roberts
description: The final In the Garden—a single mother and an ex-soldier fight the Harper Bride's violent ghost.

title: The Last Boyfriend
author: Nora Roberts
description: The second brother and a fitness trainer—the inn is haunted by a friendly ghost named Eliza.

title: The Perfect Hope
author: Nora Roberts
description: The final brother and the inn's manager—Eliza's ghost story is finally resolved.

title: The Reprieve
author: Jean-Paul Sartre
description: Munich 1938—dozens of characters across Europe wait for war as Chamberlain negotiates. The second Roads to Freedom.

title: Troubled Sleep
author: Jean-Paul Sartre
description: France falls in 1940—prisoners, refugees, and soldiers face defeat. The third Roads to Freedom.

title: The Destiny of the Sword
author: Dave Duncan
description: Wallie must fulfill his goddess's mission—the Seventh Sword trilogy concludes with war and sacrifice.

title: The Coming of Wisdom
author: Dave Duncan
description: Wallie trains in the sword—the middle Seventh Sword as he gathers allies.

title: The Hounds of Skaith
author: Leigh Brackett
description: Stark battles the Lords Protector on the dying world of Skaith—the second Book of Skaith.

title: The Reavers of Skaith
author: Leigh Brackett
description: Stark leads the exodus from Skaith—the final Brackett space adventure.

title: Zoe's Tale
author: John Scalzi
description: The Last Colony retold from the teenager's perspective—Zoe's alien heritage makes her the key to everything.

title: The Human Division
author: John Scalzi
description: Earth and the Colonial Union are cut off—diplomatic incidents and sabotage threaten humanity's unity.

title: The End of All Things
author: John Scalzi
description: The Old Man's War saga concludes—brainjacked pilots, alien conspiracies, and the future of the Colonial Union.

title: The Claw of the Conciliator
author: Gene Wolfe
description: Severian continues north with the Claw—theater, revolution, and the Autarch's war. Nebula winner.

series.name: The Book of the New Sun

title: The Sword of the Lictor
author: Gene Wolfe
description: Severian reaches Thrax and is exiled again—the mountains, the alzabo, and a boy who may be more than human.

series.name: The Book of the New Sun

title: The Citadel of the Autarch
author: Gene Wolfe
description: Severian reaches the war and the Autarch—the truth about the New Sun and Urth's dying future.

series.name: The Book of the New Sun

title: Blood of Amber
author: Roger Zelazny
description: Merlin navigates between Amber and the Courts of Chaos—a living maze, a sentient computer, and family politics.

title: Sign of Chaos
author: Roger Zelazny
description: Merlin is pulled between powers—the Logrus and the Pattern both want him, and someone is manipulating events.

title: Knight of Shadows
author: Roger Zelazny
description: Merlin discovers the truth about the Pattern ghost and the Logrus—Amber's reality is more complex than anyone knew.

title: Prince of Chaos
author: Roger Zelazny
description: The final Amber—Merlin must choose between Amber and Chaos as the universe's fundamental powers collide.

title: Magic Shifts
author: Ilona Andrews
description: Kate and Curran leave the Pack—then a djinn starts killing in Atlanta and only they can stop it.

title: Magic Binds
author: Ilona Andrews
description: Kate's father is coming—the god who wants to remake the world in his image, and she's the only one who can stop him.

title: Magic Triumphs
author: Ilona Andrews
description: The final Kate Daniels—Roland attacks Atlanta and Kate must embrace her full power to save everyone she loves.

title: Cop Killer
author: Maj Sjöwall
description: A woman is murdered in the countryside—Martin Beck investigates as a cop killer stalks Stockholm.

title: The Terrorists
author: Maj Sjöwall
description: The final Martin Beck—an assassination attempt during a state visit as Sweden's welfare state crumbles.

title: The Locked Room
author: Maj Sjöwall
description: A body in a locked room and a wave of bank robberies—two cases that may be connected.

title: Murder at the Savoy
author: Maj Sjöwall
description: An arms dealer is shot at a banquet—Beck investigates Sweden's military-industrial complex.

title: Dead Water
author: Ann Cleeves
description: A journalist's body is found on a beach—Perez investigates a media scandal connecting Shetland to the outside world.

title: Thin Air
author: Ann Cleeves
description: A woman vanishes during a hamefarin—Shetlanders returning from around the world for a festival.

title: Cold Earth
author: Ann Cleeves
description: A landslide reveals a woman's body—she was last seen at a party. Perez investigates as another storm approaches.

title: Wild Fire
author: Ann Cleeves
description: The final Shetland—a family moves to the islands and is targeted with hate. Perez's last case.

title: Indelible
author: Karin Slaughter
description: A hostage situation at a police station—intercut with a flashback revealing how Sara and Jeffrey met.

title: Faithless
author: Karin Slaughter
description: A body is found buried alive in the woods—Grant County's darkest case.

title: Beyond Reach
author: Karin Slaughter
description: Sara and Jeffrey investigate a case in his hometown—a place with ugly secrets that could destroy them both. The final Grant County.

title: Rama II
author: Arthur C. Clarke
description: A second Rama spacecraft enters the solar system—humanity sends a team to explore it. Co-written with Gentry Lee.

title: The Garden of Rama
author: Arthur C. Clarke
description: The explorers are trapped inside Rama as it travels to a distant star—they must build a society inside the ship.

title: Rama Revealed
author: Arthur C. Clarke
description: The final Rama—the spacecraft reaches its destination and humanity discovers the purpose of the Raman expedition.

title: The Throat
author: Peter Straub
description: A writer returns to his hometown where a serial killer is active—the Blue Rose trilogy concludes. Straub's most ambitious work.

title: Hunted
author: Kevin Hearne
description: Atticus is on the run from every pantheon—Artemis hunts him across Europe while dark elves close in.

title: Shattered
author: Kevin Hearne
description: Atticus faces Loki's schemes—the trickster god is building toward Ragnarok and using Atticus's friends as weapons.

title: Staked
author: Kevin Hearne
description: Atticus hunts the last remaining vampire while his apprentice Granuaile faces her own trial.

title: Besieged
author: Kevin Hearne
description: Stories bridging the gap—Atticus, Granuaile, and Owen face threats between the main novels.

title: Scourged
author: Kevin Hearne
description: Ragnarok arrives—the final Iron Druid as Atticus, Granuaile, and Owen face the end of the world.

title: Selfies
author: Jussi Adler-Olsen
description: Department Q investigates social services fraud—women are dying and a ruthless bureaucrat is connected.

title: Victim 2117
author: Jussi Adler-Olsen
description: A drowned refugee's photo goes viral—Assad's past explodes as Department Q confronts his secret history.

title: The Shadow Murders
author: Jussi Adler-Olsen
description: The final Department Q—Carl Mørck faces a case spanning decades as the team's fates are decided.

title: The Annals of the Heechee
author: Frederik Pohl
description: Broadhead is now a computer program—the Heechee's ancient enemy approaches and humanity must choose sides.

title: The Gateway Trip
author: Frederik Pohl
description: Tales from the Heechee universe—prospectors, aliens, and the dangers of faster-than-light gambling.

title: Doctor Thorne
author: Anthony Trollope
description: A country doctor's niece loves the heir to the local estate—illegitimacy, inheritance, and Victorian class warfare.

title: Framley Parsonage
author: Anthony Trollope
description: A young clergyman gets into financial trouble—Trollope's most popular serial, written for the Cornhill Magazine.

title: The Small House at Allington
author: Anthony Trollope
description: Lily Dale is jilted by a cad—she refuses to love again. Trollope's most heartbreaking heroine.

title: The Last Chronicle of Barset
author: Anthony Trollope
description: A clergyman is accused of theft—Trollope's own favorite novel and the Barsetshire finale.

title: The Eustace Diamonds
author: Anthony Trollope
description: A widow refuses to return her dead husband's diamonds—Lizzie Eustace manipulates everyone around her.

title: The Prime Minister
author: Anthony Trollope
description: Plantagenet Palliser becomes PM—his wife disrupts his government while a con man destroys a woman.

title: The Duke's Children
author: Anthony Trollope
description: The final Palliser—the Duke's children choose their own partners against his wishes. Trollope's farewell.

title: The Mask Falling
author: Samantha Shannon
description: Paige goes undercover in Scion Paris—spies, revolution, and the Rephaim's true plans revealed.

title: Foxglove Summer
author: Ben Aaronovitch
description: Peter Grant investigates missing children in the countryside—Herefordshire has its own kind of magic.

title: The Hanging Tree
author: Ben Aaronovitch
description: A murder at a party in One Hyde Park—Peter's investigation leads to the Third Principia and Lady Ty.

title: Lies Sleeping
author: Ben Aaronovitch
description: The Faceless Man is finally identified—Peter and Nightingale prepare for the final confrontation.

title: False Value
author: Ben Aaronovitch
description: Peter goes undercover at a tech company—AI, magic, and a genius who might have cracked the secret of eternity.

title: Amongst Our Weapons
author: Ben Aaronovitch
description: A man dies clutching a silver ring—the Angels of Death are in London, and nobody expected the Spanish Inquisition.

title: The Sun Sister
author: Lucinda Riley
description: Electra d'Aplièse discovers her roots connect to a Maasai tribe in Kenya—fame, addiction, and African heritage.

title: The Missing Sister
author: Lucinda Riley
description: The search for the seventh sister—a mystery spanning Ireland, New Zealand, and the family's deepest secret.

title: Atlas: The Story of Pa Salt
author: Lucinda Riley
description: Pa Salt's own story—who was he, where did he come from, and why did he adopt seven girls? The saga concludes.

title: Blue Dahlia
author: Nora Roberts
description: A widow moves to Tennessee with her sons—the gardening center next door is haunted by a ghost called the Harper Bride.

title: Phineas Redux
author: Anthony Trollope
description: Phineas returns to Parliament and is accused of murder—the darkest Palliser novel.

title: Rupture
author: Ragnar Jónasson
description: A young woman is shot in Siglufjörður—Ari Thór investigates as dark secrets from WWII surface.

title: Whiteout
author: Ragnar Jónasson
description: An author is found dead in Siglufjörður during a blizzard—her memoir threatens powerful people.

title: Winterkill
author: Ragnar Jónasson
description: A girl dies at Easter—the final Dark Iceland as Ari Thór faces the town's last secret.

title: Empress of Forever
author: Max Gladstone
description: A tech mogul is thrown into the far future—she must cross the galaxy with aliens, monks, and a pirate queen to get home.

title: Last First Snow
author: Max Gladstone
description: A protest against redevelopment turns violent—the Craft Sequence's most political novel. Occupy meets necromancy.

title: Four Roads Cross
author: Max Gladstone
description: Tara Abernathy returns to Alt Coulumb—a god is on trial and the whole city's faith is at stake.

title: The Ruin of Angels
author: Max Gladstone
description: A smuggler in a conquered city—two versions of reality overlap, and Craft practitioners exploit the gap.

title: Sir Thursday
author: Garth Nix
description: Arthur battles the Piper's army in the Great Maze—the fourth Key to the Kingdom.

title: Lady Friday
author: Garth Nix
description: Arthur enters the Middle House—Lady Friday's domain of experience, where every sense is traded.

title: Superior Saturday
author: Garth Nix
description: The Upper House is falling—Saturday attacks as Arthur races to claim the sixth Key.

title: Lord Sunday
author: Garth Nix
description: The final Key to the Kingdom—Arthur faces Sunday and the Incomparable Gardens as the House collapses.

title: Thursday's Children
author: Nicci French
description: Frieda Klein investigates a teacher's abuse—her own childhood trauma surfaces as the case escalates.

title: Friday on My Mind
author: Nicci French
description: Frieda is suspected of murder—her ex-husband is dead and she goes on the run to prove her innocence.

title: Saturday Requiem
author: Nicci French
description: A woman has been in a coma for years—Frieda suspects she was poisoned, not suicidal.

title: Sunday Silence
author: Nicci French
description: Frieda is in hiding—someone is killing people connected to her, one by one. The day of reckoning approaches.

title: Day of the Dead
author: Nicci French
description: The final Frieda Klein—she returns from exile to face Dean Reeve once and for all.

title: Back on Blossom Street
author: Debbie Macomber
description: The knitting circle returns—new projects, new problems, and the community that holds women together.

title: Twenty Wishes
author: Debbie Macomber
description: A widow's friends convince her to make twenty wishes—fulfilling them changes her life.

title: Summer on Blossom Street
author: Debbie Macomber
description: A summer knitting class brings together women facing crossroads—love, loss, and starting over.

title: Hannah's List
author: Debbie Macomber
description: A widowed doctor finds his wife left a list of three women he should consider marrying—each one surprises him.

title: A Minute to Midnight
author: David Baldacci
description: Pine returns to her Georgia hometown to investigate her sister's abduction thirty years ago—and finds fresh bodies.

title: Daylight
author: David Baldacci
description: Pine's hunt for her sister leads her to a human trafficking ring—and a connection to John Puller's family.

title: Love Letters
author: Debbie Macomber
description: A woman receives love letters that transform her understanding of her grandmother's past—Rose Harbor Inn.

title: Silver Linings
author: Debbie Macomber
description: The final Rose Harbor—Jo Marie's husband may still be alive, and the inn's guests need her more than ever.

title: The Dare
author: Elle Kennedy
description: A hockey player and his best friend's little sister—he's dared to seduce her. He's not supposed to fall.

title: The Play
author: Elle Kennedy
description: A football player and a film student—she casts him in her senior project and sparks fly on set.

title: The Legacy
author: Elle Kennedy
description: The final Briar U—a hockey player's reputation precedes him, but the girl next door sees who he really is.

title: Magic Gifts
author: Ilona Andrews
description: A short novella—Kate and Curran investigate a cursed boy. Bridging Magic Bleeds and Magic Slays.

title: Susannah's Garden
author: Debbie Macomber
description: A woman returns to her childhood home after her father dies—digging in the garden reveals her mother's secrets.

title: Kisscut
author: Karin Slaughter
description: A teenage girl is shot at the skating rink—Sara Linton discovers the girl was hiding a terrible secret.

title: Mercy
author: David Baldacci
description: Atlee Pine finally learns the truth about her twin sister—the Atlee Pine series concludes.

title: Die Again
author: Tess Gerritsen
description: A murder in Boston connects to a safari massacre in Africa—Rizzoli investigates a killer who hunts humans.

title: I Know a Secret
author: Tess Gerritsen
description: Women who were childhood friends are dying—each killed in the manner of a Catholic saint. The final Rizzoli & Isles.

title: Wintersteel
author: Will Wight
description: Lindon enters the Uncrowned King tournament—the most acclaimed Cradle volume. Power-scaling meets emotional stakes.

title: Bloodline
author: Will Wight
description: Lindon returns to Sacred Valley—but he's no longer the weakling who left. Cradle at its most satisfying.

title: Reaper
author: Will Wight
description: Lindon advances to Monarch territory—the endgame of the Cradle power system begins.

title: Dreadgod
author: Will Wight
description: Lindon hunts the Dreadgods—world-ending monsters that define Cradle's power ceiling.

title: Uncrowned
author: Will Wight
description: The Uncrowned King tournament begins—Lindon faces the best young sacred artists in the world.

title: Flying Colours
author: C.S. Forester
description: Hornblower escapes from French captivity and makes his way back to England—a Napoleonic road novel.

title: Commodore Hornblower
author: C.S. Forester
description: Hornblower commands a squadron in the Baltic—diplomacy, sea battles, and Napoleon's Russian campaign.

title: Lord Hornblower
author: C.S. Forester
description: Hornblower deals with mutiny, Napoleon's escape from Elba, and the Hundred Days.

title: Admiral Hornblower in the West Indies
author: C.S. Forester
description: Hornblower as admiral—piracy, revolution, and his final years in the Caribbean.

title: Hornblower and the Crisis
author: C.S. Forester
description: Forester's unfinished final Hornblower—published posthumously. Napoleon's invasion plan.

title: Tides of Light
author: Gregory Benford
description: Humans flee through the Galactic Center—hunted by machine intelligences in a universe hostile to organic life.

title: Furious Gulf
author: Gregory Benford
description: The survivors approach the black hole at the galaxy's heart—relativistic physics and ancient alien artifacts.

title: Sailing Bright Eternity
author: Gregory Benford
description: The Galactic Center saga concludes—humanity's remnant makes a final stand at the Eater of All Things.

title: Sandman: Dream Country
author: Neil Gaiman
description: Four standalone stories—Calliope imprisoned by a writer, a cat who dreams of changing the world, Shakespeare's A Midsummer Night's Dream.

title: Sandman: A Game of You
author: Neil Gaiman
description: Barbie enters a fantasy land she dreamed as a child—but the Cuckoo is destroying it from within.

title: Sandman: Fables & Reflections
author: Neil Gaiman
description: Stories spanning centuries—Emperor Norton, Orpheus, Marco Polo, and the French Revolution. Dream touches everything.

title: Sandman: World's End
author: Neil Gaiman
description: Travelers stranded at a reality-nexus inn tell stories—each tale is stranger than the last. A funeral procession passes.

title: Sandman: The Kindly Ones
author: Neil Gaiman
description: The Furies come for Dream—he made a choice that demands punishment. The longest, darkest Sandman arc.

title: The Sleepwalker
author: Chris Bohjalian
description: A woman who sleepwalks vanishes one night—her daughters and husband each suspect the others.

title: Close Your Eyes, Hold Hands
author: Chris Bohjalian
description: A teenage girl on the run after a Vermont nuclear meltdown—her father was the plant operator.

title: The Double Bind
author: Chris Bohjalian
description: A social worker inherits a homeless man's photographs—they connect to the Gatsby era and her own trauma.

title: Then Came You
author: Jennifer Weiner
description: Four women connected by one baby—a donor, a surrogate, a stepmother, and a mother who lost everything.

title: Alone in the Dark
author: Karen Rose
description: An investigative journalist and a detective hunt a trafficking ring—the killer is closer than they think.

title: Edge of Darkness
author: Karen Rose
description: A professor's students are being targeted—the detective investigating falls for the woman at the center.

title: Into the Dark
author: Karen Rose
description: A doctor witnesses a child abuse case and is targeted for what she knows—dark, propulsive.

title: Sisterland
author: Curtis Sittenfeld
description: Twin sisters with psychic abilities—one embraces her gift, the other suppresses it. Then one predicts an earthquake.

title: You Think It, I'll Say It
author: Curtis Sittenfeld
description: Ten stories about the gap between what we think and what we say—gender, class, and the comedy of American manners.

title: Hostage
author: Clare Mackintosh
description: A flight attendant on a twenty-hour flight receives a note: crash the plane or your daughter dies.

title: Find You First
author: Linwood Barclay
description: A tech millionaire is dying—his anonymous sperm donations produced nine children, and someone is killing them.

title: Dark Horse
author: Tami Hoag
description: An ex-cop investigates the kidnapping of a girl from a horse farm—the equestrian world's dark side.

title: Prior Bad Acts
author: Tami Hoag
description: A judge's lenient ruling frees a killer—she's targeted for assassination while covering the case.

title: Cold Cold Heart
author: Tami Hoag
description: A journalist returns home after being kidnapped—brain damage has changed her, but she remembers a cold case.

title: Dark Harbor
author: Stuart Woods
description: An FBI agent investigates murders on a Maine island—Stone Barrington gets involved.

title: Iron Orchid
author: Stuart Woods
description: Stone investigates an art theft that connects to terrorism—the wealthy world of Manhattan.

title: Two Dollar Bill
author: Stuart Woods
description: A charismatic con man hires Stone—is he a client or a mark?

title: The Identicals
author: Elin Hilderbrand
description: Identical twins on different islands swap lives for the summer—sisterhood, secrets, and Nantucket.

title: The Family Remains
author: Lisa Jewell
description: The sequel to The Family Upstairs—a body in the Thames connects to the dark house where three children were imprisoned.

title: A Spark of Light
author: Jodi Picoult
description: A hostage situation at an abortion clinic—told in reverse chronology, every character has a reason to be there.

title: The Storyteller
author: Jodi Picoult
description: A baker befriends an old man who confesses to being a Nazi—should she help him die or turn him in?

title: The Marriage of Opposites
author: Alice Hoffman
description: Camille Pissarro's mother—a forbidden love on the island of St. Thomas. Art, race, and paradise.

title: Thick as Thieves
author: Sandra Brown
description: Twenty years after a heist, the stolen money surfaces—the four thieves' children must find it first.

title: Taking Eve
author: Iris Johansen
description: Eve Duncan is kidnapped—Bonnie's ghost and Joe Quinn race to find her before it's too late.

title: Bombshell
author: Catherine Coulter
description: Savich and Sherlock investigate a bombing—the evidence points to a dead man. FBI Thriller continues.

title: Power Play
author: Catherine Coulter
description: A senator and a British spy are targeted by the same killer—Savich connects the cases.

title: Insidious
author: Catherine Coulter
description: A Venus flytrap killer targeting wealthy women—Savich and Sherlock pursue across California and DC.

title: Left Neglected
author: Lisa Genova
description: A high-powered executive suffers a brain injury—she can no longer perceive the left side of anything. Based on real neuroscience.

title: Love Anthony
author: Lisa Genova
description: Two women on Nantucket—one has an autistic son, the other finds his story in the sand. Connection through different minds.

title: A Flicker in the Dark
author: Stacy Willingham
description: A psychologist's father was a serial killer—now women are disappearing again and the pattern matches.

title: Only If You're Lucky
author: Stacy Willingham
description: A college student befriends a magnetic girl—then someone on campus is found dead.

title: Finding Ashley
author: Danielle Steel
description: Two estranged sisters reunite to search for a child given up decades ago—family, forgiveness, and second chances.

title: The Affair
author: Danielle Steel
description: A professor's affair with a student goes public—the fallout destroys two families across the Atlantic.

title: Flying Angels
author: Danielle Steel
description: Six nurses serving in WWII—from Pearl Harbor to the liberation of Europe, their courage and sacrifice.

title: The Whittiers
author: Danielle Steel
description: A family patriarch dies and his children must decide what to do with the Manhattan mansion—each has a secret.

title: Without a Trace
author: Danielle Steel
description: A man vanishes during a business trip—his wife discovers he had an entire second life.

title: The Silent Corner
author: Dean Koontz
description: An FBI agent investigates a wave of suicides—they're connected to a mind-control technology. Jane Hawk begins.

title: The Whispering Room
author: Dean Koontz
description: Jane Hawk goes underground—the conspiracy runs deeper than any government agency.

title: The Crooked Staircase
author: Dean Koontz
description: Jane hunts the architects of the brain implant technology—while they hunt her son.

title: The Forbidden Door
author: Dean Koontz
description: Jane is the most wanted fugitive in America—the people controlling minds want her silenced.

title: The Night Window
author: Dean Koontz
description: The final Jane Hawk—she confronts the mastermind behind the conspiracy to end free will.

title: Blue Smoke
author: Nora Roberts
description: An arsonist stalked her as a child—twenty years later, the fires start again. Romantic suspense.

title: Violets Are Blue
author: James Patterson
description: Vampire killers stalk victims across America—Cross hunts them from San Francisco to the Deep South.

title: First Family
author: David Baldacci
