description: The president's niece is kidnapped—Sean King and Michelle Maxwell investigate.

title: Zero Day
author: David Baldacci
description: John Puller investigates a murder in a West Virginia mining town—the victims connect to a military conspiracy.

title: Unseen
author: Karin Slaughter
description: Will goes undercover in a biker gang—Sara discovers his secret life and the case threatens to consume them both.

title: Catch Me
author: Lisa Gardner
description: A woman believes she'll be murdered on January 21st—she goes to D.D. Warren for help before it happens.

title: The Promise
author: Danielle Steel
description: A woman is disfigured in a car crash—her fiancé's mother drives her away, and she must build a new life.

title: Palomino
author: Danielle Steel
description: A woman recovering from heartbreak finds love on a California ranch—horses, healing, and second chances.

title: Zoya
author: Danielle Steel
description: A Russian countess escapes the Revolution and rebuilds her life in Paris and New York—from Romanov to immigrant.

title: B Is for Burglar
author: Sue Grafton
description: Kinsey Millhone investigates a missing woman—the neighbors don't want her found. The second alphabet mystery.

title: C Is for Corpse
author: Sue Grafton
description: A hit-and-run survivor hires Kinsey—then he's murdered before she can find who wanted him dead.

title: D Is for Deadbeat
author: Sue Grafton
description: An ex-con hires Kinsey to deliver a check—he's found dead before it clears. Who did he owe?

title: E Is for Evidence
author: Sue Grafton
description: Kinsey is framed for insurance fraud—someone planted evidence in her files. She must clear her own name.

title: F Is for Fugitive
author: Sue Grafton
description: A man convicted of murder seventeen years ago insists he's innocent—Kinsey reopens the case in a small coastal town.

title: The Cruelest Month
author: Louise Penny
description: A séance in the old Hadley house goes wrong—someone dies, and Three Pines confronts its haunted past.

title: Next to Last Stand
author: Craig Johnson
description: Longmire investigates a painting worth millions—it was supposed to have been destroyed. Someone is killing for it.

title: Daughter of the Morning Star
author: Craig Johnson
description: A girl vanishes from the Northern Cheyenne reservation—Longmire and Henry Standing Bear search as tensions rise.

title: Hell and Back
author: Craig Johnson
description: Longmire wakes in a ghost town with no memory—the dead are walking and nothing is what it seems.

title: The Highwayman
author: Craig Johnson
description: A novella—Longmire's father investigates a phantom vehicle on the deadliest highway in Wyoming.

title: Simple Genius
author: David Baldacci
description: Sean King and Michelle Maxwell investigate murders near a CIA facility and a code-breaking think tank.

title: Hour Game
author: David Baldacci
description: A serial killer mimics famous murderers—King and Maxwell must find the pattern before the next strike.

title: The Crossing
author: Michael Connelly
description: Bosch crosses to the defense side—helping his half-brother Mickey Haller prove a client's innocence.

title: Block Shot
author: Kennedy Ryan
description: A basketball agent and the woman who broke his heart cross paths again—second chance sports romance.

title: To Catch a Rogue
author: Beverly Jenkins
description: A female sharpshooter and a saloon owner in 1880s Colorado—sparks fly over a stolen diamond.

title: One Good Earl Deserves a Lover
author: Sarah MacLean
description: A brilliant scientist asks a notorious rake to teach her about passion before her wedding—he falls instead.

title: Devil in Spring
author: Lisa Kleypas
description: A devil-may-care lord is trapped in a conservatory with a brilliant woman—marriage or scandal. Kleypas's modern classic.

title: The Stranger Beside Me
author: Ann Rule
description: Ann Rule worked alongside Ted Bundy at a crisis hotline—the definitive account of America's most infamous serial killer by the woman who knew him.

title: Small Sacrifices
author: Ann Rule
description: A mother shoots her three children and claims a stranger did it—Rule's investigation exposes a sociopath.

title: Dead by Sunset
author: Ann Rule
description: A charming man marries woman after woman—each relationship ends in violence. Rule follows the trail of bodies.

title: I'll Be Gone in the Dark
author: Michelle McNamara
description: The hunt for the Golden State Killer—McNamara's obsessive investigation, published after her death, helped catch him.

title: Mindhunter
author: John Douglas
description: The FBI agent who invented criminal profiling—face-to-face interviews with serial killers from Manson to Gacy.

title: The Killer Across the Table
author: John Douglas
description: Four new cases of murderers Douglas interviewed—what he learned about evil after forty years of profiling.

title: Zodiac
author: Robert Graysmith
description: A cartoonist obsessively investigates the Zodiac Killer—the unsolved case that terrorized San Francisco.

title: Helter Skelter
author: Vincent Bugliosi
description: The Manson murders and the trial—told by the prosecutor who put Charles Manson away. The bestselling true crime book ever.

title: Midnight in the Garden of Good and Evil
author: John Berendt
description: A murder in Savannah's eccentric high society—drag queens, voodoo, and a trial that captivated America.

title: Mastering the Art of French Cooking
author: Julia Child
description: The cookbook that taught America to cook French—Julia Child's definitive guide to boeuf bourguignon, soufflés, and everything else.

title: My Life in France
author: Julia Child
description: Julia's memoir of discovering France and food—the years that transformed a government worker into a culinary legend.

title: Plenty
author: Yotam Ottolenghi
description: Vibrant vegetable cooking from the chef who changed how the world eats plants—Middle Eastern flavors, London energy.

title: Jerusalem
author: Yotam Ottolenghi
description: Recipes from the holy city—Palestinian and Israeli cuisines side by side. Food as common ground.

title: The Food Lab
author: J. Kenji Lopez-Alt
description: The science of cooking perfected—why does a burger need a smash? How do you make the best scrambled eggs? Science answers.

title: How to Eat
author: Nigella Lawson
description: The pleasures of eating—not a cookbook but a philosophy of food, from midnight toast to Christmas dinner.

title: The Art of Eating
author: M.F.K. Fisher
description: Five books in one—Fisher on hunger, love, war, France, and why we eat. The greatest American food writer.

title: Tender at the Bone
author: Ruth Reichl
description: The New York Times food critic's memoir—growing up with a mother who was a terrible cook, and finding food as salvation.

title: Garlic and Sapphires
author: Ruth Reichl
description: Reichl reviews restaurants in disguise—wigs, accents, and the power of the anonymous critic.

title: Heat
author: Bill Buford
description: A New Yorker editor abandons his desk to work in Mario Batali's kitchen—then apprentices with an Italian butcher.

title: A Time of Gifts
author: Patrick Leigh Fermor
description: A teenager walks from London to Constantinople in 1933—the greatest travel book ever written. Pre-war Europe on foot.

title: Between the Woods and the Water
author: Patrick Leigh Fermor
description: The walk continues through Hungary and Romania—castles, peasants, and a world about to vanish in war.

title: The Broken Road
author: Patrick Leigh Fermor
description: The final leg—Bulgaria and Constantinople. Published posthumously, completing Fermor's epic journey.

title: The Global Soul
author: Pico Iyer
description: What it means to belong nowhere—airports, Olympics, Los Angeles, and the new rootlessness.

title: The Art of Stillness
author: Pico Iyer
description: Going nowhere as the ultimate luxury—why sitting still is a radical act in the age of movement.

title: Shadow of the Silk Road
author: Colin Thubron
description: Seven thousand miles from China to Turkey on the ancient trade route—Thubron at his most epic.

title: Among the Russians
author: Colin Thubron
description: Driving alone through Soviet Russia—Thubron's quiet, penetrating portrait of a closed empire.

title: Trieste and the Meaning of Nowhere
author: Jan Morris
description: A meditation on Trieste—the city where Europe meets itself, and Morris confronts identity, loss, and belonging.

title: Venice
author: Jan Morris
description: The definitive portrait of the floating city—its glories, decay, and the melancholy of beauty.

title: Confederates in the Attic
author: Tony Horwitz
description: A journalist travels the South to understand why Americans still refight the Civil War—reenactors, battlefields, and race.

title: Blue Latitudes
author: Tony Horwitz
description: Retracing Captain Cook's voyages—from Tahiti to Alaska, following the explorer who mapped the Pacific.

title: A Short Walk in the Hindu Kush
author: Eric Newby
description: A fashion buyer and a diplomat attempt to climb a mountain in Afghanistan—incompetent, hilarious, and immortal.

title: Full Tilt
author: Dervla Murphy
description: An Irishwoman cycles from Dublin to India alone—the original adventure travel classic.

title: The Boys in the Boat
author: Daniel James Brown
description: Nine working-class boys from the University of Washington win Olympic gold in 1936 Berlin—rowing, grit, and defying Hitler.

title: Season of the Witch
author: David Talbot
description: San Francisco from 1967 to 1982—Manson, Jonestown, Harvey Milk, and the AIDS epidemic in one city's crucible.

title: A Season on the Brink
author: John Feinstein
description: A year inside Bobby Knight's Indiana basketball program—the most famous sports book about coaching.

title: A Good Walk Spoiled
author: John Feinstein
description: A year on the PGA Tour—Feinstein's inside look at golf's professional circuit.

title: The Summer of '49
author: David Halberstam
description: The pennant race between the Yankees and Red Sox—DiMaggio vs. Williams in baseball's golden age.

title: The Boys of Summer
author: Roger Kahn
description: The Brooklyn Dodgers of the 1950s—Jackie Robinson, the integration of baseball, and what happened after the glory years.

title: The Valleys of the Assassins
author: Freya Stark
description: A woman travels alone through Persia and Iraq in the 1930s—mountains, tribesmen, and the ruins of ancient civilizations.

title: This Old Man
author: Roger Angell
description: The legendary New Yorker baseball writer and editor on aging, writing, love, and the game—his final collection.

title: The Awakening
author: Kate Chopin
description: A woman in New Orleans discovers desire and independence—then drowns. The novel that scandalized America and invented feminist fiction.

title: The Storm and Other Stories
author: Kate Chopin
description: Chopin's collected stories including The Story of an Hour—women trapped in marriages, freed by death or desire.

title: The Yellow Wallpaper
author: Charlotte Perkins Gilman
description: A woman confined to a room by her doctor husband slowly goes mad—feminist horror that predicted a century of protest.

title: Passing
author: Nella Larsen
description: Two light-skinned Black women in 1920s New York—one passes as white, the other is drawn to her dangerous secret. Harlem Renaissance masterpiece.

title: Quicksand
author: Nella Larsen
description: A biracial woman moves between Black and white worlds—Denmark, Harlem, the South—finding belonging nowhere.

title: Cane
author: Jean Toomer
description: Poems, stories, and a play about Black life in the South and North—the most experimental work of the Harlem Renaissance.

title: Gorilla, My Love
author: Toni Cade Bambara
description: Stories of Black women and girls in Brooklyn and the rural South—fierce, funny, and revolutionary.

title: The Salt Eaters
author: Toni Cade Bambara
description: A civil rights activist attempts suicide—a faith healer tries to save her. Black community as cosmos.

title: Corregidora
author: Gayl Jones
description: A blues singer carries the memory of her enslaved ancestors—her great-grandmother was raped by a Portuguese slave owner. Toni Morrison edited this debut.

title: Eva's Man
author: Gayl Jones
description: A woman in prison for a horrific crime tells her story—sexual violence, madness, and Black female rage.

title: Middle Passage
author: Charles Johnson
description: A freed slave stows away on a ship—it turns out to be a slave trader. A philosophical adventure novel. National Book Award.

title: A Lesson Before Dying
author: Ernest Gaines
description: A teacher visits a condemned man in jail—to teach him to die with dignity. 1940s Louisiana, race, and what it means to be a man.

title: The Autobiography of Miss Jane Pittman
author: Ernest Gaines
description: A 110-year-old Black woman narrates her life from slavery through the civil rights movement—an American epic.

title: A Gathering of Old Men
author: Ernest Gaines
description: A white man is killed in rural Louisiana—eighteen old Black men each confess to the crime. Solidarity and defiance.

title: Brothers and Keepers
author: John Edgar Wideman
description: Two brothers—one a professor, one in prison for life. Wideman confronts race, family, and the America that made them.

title: Philadelphia Fire
author: John Edgar Wideman
description: The MOVE bombing in Philadelphia—Wideman weaves the 1985 disaster with Shakespeare and his own family's tragedy.

title: Sent for You Yesterday
author: John Edgar Wideman
description: Three generations in Homewood, Pittsburgh—jazz, heroin, and the ghosts of a neighborhood. PEN/Faulkner winner.

title: A Visitation of Spirits
author: Randall Kenan
description: A gay Black teenager in rural North Carolina tries to transform himself—magic, religion, and the cost of not belonging.

title: Let the Dead Bury Their Dead
author: Randall Kenan
description: Stories of Tims Creek, North Carolina—a community where the supernatural lives alongside prejudice and love.

title: Drinking Coffee Elsewhere
author: ZZ Packer
description: Stories of young Black Americans—a girl at Yale, Brownies at camp, a man in Japan. Debut that announced a major voice.

title: Bless Me, Ultima
author: Rudolfo Anaya
description: A boy in 1940s New Mexico is guided by a curandera—Mexican American identity, Catholicism vs. indigenous faith.

title: Under the Feet of Jesus
author: Helena María Viramontes
description: A thirteen-year-old girl in a migrant worker family—pesticides, poverty, and the fierce will to survive.

title: Their Dogs Came with Them
author: Helena María Viramontes
description: East Los Angeles in the 1960s—freeways tear through the barrio as four women fight displacement.

title: So Far from God
author: Ana Castillo
description: Four sisters in New Mexico—one rises from the dead, one becomes a saint. Magical realism in the Chicano Southwest.

title: The Friends of Eddie Coyle
author: George V. Higgins
description: Boston criminals talk—gun deals, bank robberies, and betrayal in Higgins's debut that invented the crime dialogue novel.

title: Cogan's Trade
author: George V. Higgins
description: A fixer is hired to deal with two men who robbed a mob card game—the novel behind Killing Them Softly.

title: The Dick Gibson Show
author: Stanley Elkin
description: A late-night radio host collects America's confessions—Elkin's manic prose makes talk radio an art form.

title: The Magic Kingdom
author: Stanley Elkin
description: A man takes terminally ill children to Disney World—dark comedy about death, wish fulfillment, and the American way.

title: A Summons to Memphis
author: Peter Taylor
description: A man's elderly father wants to remarry—his children conspire to stop him. Genteel Southern family as battlefield. Pulitzer winner.

title: In the Tennessee Country
author: Peter Taylor
description: A senator's cousin tells the family's story—Tennessee aristocracy fading into the modern world.

title: A Long and Happy Life
author: Reynolds Price
description: A young North Carolina woman loves a man who won't commit—her pregnancy forces a reckoning with what she wants.

title: Kate Vaiden
author: Reynolds Price
description: A woman at fifty-seven tells the story of leaving her son—the South, men, and the cost of freedom. National Book Critics Circle Award.

title: Shiloh
author: Shelby Foote
description: The Battle of Shiloh from both sides—soldiers' voices in alternating chapters. The novel that made Foote before the Civil War trilogy.

title: The Home Place
author: Wright Morris
description: A man returns to his Nebraska family farm—photographs and prose alternate in this hybrid novel of the plains.

title: Plains Song
author: Wright Morris
description: Three generations of Nebraska women—silence, endurance, and the prairie that shaped them. American Book Award.

title: Women and Men
author: Joseph McElroy
description: A journalist and a New Age guru in New York—the most ambitious and difficult American novel, spanning everything.

title: Lookout Cartridge
author: Joseph McElroy
description: A filmmaker's footage is stolen—a paranoid investigation through New York, London, and Stonehenge. DeLillo before DeLillo.

title: Cloudstreet
author: Tim Winton
description: Two working-class families share a house in Perth for twenty years—the great Australian novel of postwar life.

title: Breath
author: Tim Winton
description: Two boys learn to surf the dangerous coast of Western Australia—risk, obsession, and the thrill of breathing.

title: The Riders
author: Tim Winton
description: A man arrives in Ireland to meet his wife—she's not on the plane. He searches across Europe with their daughter.

title: Dirt Music
author: Tim Winton
description: A poacher and a fisherman's wife—the Australian coast, music, and escape into the wilderness.

title: Remembering Babylon
author: David Malouf
description: A white boy raised by Aboriginals returns to the settlement—he belongs to neither world. Booker shortlisted.

title: An Imaginary Life
author: David Malouf
description: The poet Ovid in exile on the Black Sea—he encounters a feral child and discovers the wild.

title: Ransom
author: David Malouf
description: Priam goes to Achilles to ransom Hector's body—the Iliad distilled to its most human moment.

title: Schindler's List
author: Thomas Keneally
description: Oskar Schindler saves 1,200 Jews during the Holocaust—the novel that became Spielberg's film. Booker winner.

title: The Chant of Jimmie Blacksmith
author: Thomas Keneally
description: A half-Aboriginal man in 1900s Australia erupts into violence against the white world that won't accept him.

title: The Man Who Loved Children
author: Christina Stead
description: A monstrous, charismatic father and a bitter mother destroy each other—the most devastating family novel in English.

title: The Bone People
author: Keri Hulme
description: A reclusive artist, a Maori man, and his mute foster son form a violent, healing bond—New Zealand's Booker winner.

title: Owls Do Cry
author: Janet Frame
description: Four children in a New Zealand family—one dies, one goes mad, one conforms. Frame's hallucinatory debut.

title: Faces in the Water
author: Janet Frame
description: A woman in a psychiatric hospital—Frame's own experience of misdiagnosis and near-lobotomy.

title: An Angel at My Table
author: Janet Frame
description: Frame's three-volume autobiography—poverty, institutionalization, and becoming a writer against all odds.

title: The Slap
author: Christos Tsiolkas
description: A man slaps someone else's child at a barbecue—the repercussions tear through eight lives in multicultural Melbourne.

title: Burial Rites
author: Hannah Kent
description: The last woman executed in Iceland—a servant condemned for murder is sent to a farm to await her beheading.

title: Carpentaria
author: Alexis Wright
description: An Aboriginal community in Northern Australia fights a mining company—mythic, sprawling, and the Miles Franklin winner.

title: The Manticore
author: Robertson Davies
description: A man goes to Jungian analysis after his father's death—the second Deptford novel, told from the analysand's chair.

title: World of Wonders
author: Robertson Davies
description: A magician tells his life story—kidnapped as a boy, he traveled with a carnival and learned the art of illusion.

title: The Apprenticeship of Duddy Kravitz
author: Mordecai Richler
description: A Jewish hustler in Montreal will do anything to own land—ambition as self-destruction. Canada's Gatsby.

title: Barney's Version
author: Mordecai Richler
description: An unreliable memoirist may or may not have murdered his best friend—Richler's comic masterpiece.

title: Solomon Gursky Was Here
author: Mordecai Richler
description: A Jewish dynasty from the 1800s to the present—bootlegging, fraud, and the mythology of Canadian wealth.

title: The Stone Angel
author: Margaret Laurence
description: A ninety-year-old woman refuses to go gently—she escapes her family to face her past on the Manitoba prairie.

title: The Diviners
author: Margaret Laurence
description: A writer raises her daughter alone in a Manitoba river town—the past of settlers and Métis people flows through everything.

title: Green Grass, Running Water
author: Thomas King
description: Four old Indians escape from a mental institution to fix the world—Coyote, God, and the Canadian Rockies. Indigenous postmodern comedy.

title: The Inconvenient Indian
author: Thomas King
description: A provocative, funny history of Indigenous-White relations in North America—from Columbus to Standing Rock.

title: The Book of Negroes
author: Lawrence Hill
description: An African woman kidnapped into slavery survives the Middle Passage, the Revolution, and Sierra Leone—Canada's most acclaimed historical novel.

title: Perfume: The Story of a Murderer
author: Patrick Süskind
description: A man born with no scent but a supernatural sense of smell murders young women to capture their essence—18th-century France.

title: The Reader
author: Bernhard Schlink
description: A teenager has an affair with an older woman—years later she appears on trial for war crimes. Guilt, literacy, and the Holocaust.

title: Excellent Women
author: Barbara Pym
description: A spinster in 1950s London observes her neighbors' romantic entanglements—Pym's comedy of Anglican manners. Jane Austen's heir.

title: Quartet in Autumn
author: Barbara Pym
description: Four single office workers approach retirement—loneliness, routine, and the terror of becoming invisible. Booker shortlisted.

title: A Glass of Blessings
author: Barbara Pym
description: A bored housewife develops a crush on a priest—Pym at her most wickedly observant.

title: Mrs. Palfrey at the Claremont
author: Elizabeth Taylor
description: An elderly widow checks into a London residential hotel—she pretends a young writer is her grandson. Dignity and deception.

title: Angel
author: Elizabeth Taylor
description: A working-class girl writes terrible romantic novels and becomes famous—Taylor's devastating satire of literary delusion.

title: The Return of the Soldier
author: Rebecca West
description: A shell-shocked soldier returns from WWI having forgotten the last fifteen years—his wife, his mistress, and a girl from his past.

title: The Fountain Overflows
author: Rebecca West
description: A musical family in Edwardian London—poverty, genius, and a mother who holds everything together with art.

title: The Quest for Christa T.
author: Christa Wolf
description: A narrator reconstructs her dead friend's life in East Germany—the individual vs. the state. The novel that challenged the DDR.

title: Cassandra
author: Christa Wolf
description: The Trojan princess who could see the future retells the fall of Troy—a feminist, anti-war masterpiece.

title: Malina
author: Ingeborg Bachmann
description: A woman between two men in Vienna—one real, one imagined. She disappears into the wall. Austrian postwar literature's greatest novel.

title: Dream Story
author: Arthur Schnitzler
description: A Viennese doctor's wife confesses a sexual fantasy—he wanders the night into an orgy and a conspiracy. The novel behind Eyes Wide Shut.

title: Lieutenant Gustl
author: Arthur Schnitzler
description: An Austrian officer is insulted by a baker—he must fight a duel or kill himself. Interior monologue that predated Ulysses.

title: Kristin Lavransdatter
author: Sigrid Undset
description: A woman's life in 14th-century Norway—love, marriage, faith, and plague across three volumes. Nobel laureate.

title: Uncle Tom's Cabin
author: Harriet Beecher Stowe
description: The novel that started the Civil War—a slave's journey through cruelty and kindness. The most important American novel of the 19th century.

title: Self-Reliance and Other Essays
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
description: Trust thyself—Emerson's essays on individualism, nature, and the American spirit that defined Transcendentalism.

title: Nature
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
description: The founding document of American Transcendentalism—the transparent eyeball, the oversoul, and the divine in every leaf.

title: The Conjure Woman
author: Charles Chesnutt
description: An ex-slave tells stories about conjure magic on a plantation—trickster tales that subvert the master's power. The first major African American fiction.

title: The Marrow of Tradition
author: Charles Chesnutt
description: A fictionalized account of the 1898 Wilmington massacre—the most important Black novel before the Harlem Renaissance.

title: The Country of the Pointed Firs
author: Sarah Orne Jewett
description: A summer in a Maine coastal village—Jewett's masterpiece of New England regionalism. Willa Cather called it one of three American books that would endure.

title: Look Homeward, Angel
author: Thomas Wolfe
description: Eugene Gant grows up in Asheville—everything is too big, too passionate, too alive. The most lyrical American novel.

title: Of Time and the River
author: Thomas Wolfe
description: Eugene at Harvard and abroad—Wolfe's torrential prose floods across 900 pages. A young man trying to devour the world.

title: You Can't Go Home Again
author: Thomas Wolfe
description: A novelist's success estranges him from his hometown—America in the Depression, and the knowledge that the past is gone.

title: Call It Sleep
author: Henry Roth
description: A Jewish immigrant boy in the Lower East Side—terror, Oedipal love, and the most brilliant stream-of-consciousness novel of the 1930s. Neglected for thirty years, then rediscovered.

title: Chilly Scenes of Winter
author: Ann Beattie
description: A man can't get over his married ex-girlfriend—the novel that defined the 1970s: aimlessness, irony, and pop culture as emotional language.

title: The Burning House
author: Ann Beattie
description: Stories of the educated, the lost, the post-counterculture—Beattie as the chronicler of the boomer malaise.

title: Picturing Will
author: Ann Beattie
description: A photographer, her son, and the men in their lives—Beattie's most emotionally generous novel.

title: In Country
author: Bobbie Ann Mason
description: A Kentucky teenager searches for the truth about her father killed in Vietnam—Bruce Springsteen, M*A*S*H, and the Wall.

title: Shiloh and Other Stories
author: Bobbie Ann Mason
description: Working-class Kentucky in transition—WalMart, TV, and the rural South becoming suburban. The first great minimalist collection.

title: Why Did I Ever
author: Mary Robison
description: A Hollywood script doctor's life in 536 fragments—pills, her damaged son, and the chaos of thought. Minimalism pushed to the edge.

title: How Much of These Hills Is Gold
author: C Pam Zhang
description: Two Chinese American children bury their father in Gold Rush California—a nation built on bones and myth.

title: Land of Milk and Honey
author: C Pam Zhang
description: A chef is hired to cook for the ultra-rich on a mountaintop while the world below starves—food, class, and apocalypse.

title: The Secret Lives of Church Ladies
author: Deesha Philyaw
description: Black women in church communities—desire, secrecy, and the bodies underneath the Sunday dresses. National Book Award finalist.

title: The Office of Historical Corrections
author: Danielle Evans
description: Stories about race, history, and who controls the narrative—a woman whose job is correcting the historical record.

title: Before You Suffocate Your Own Fool Self
author: Danielle Evans
description: Debut stories of young Black Americans navigating racial identity—sharp, funny, and devastating.

title: Afterparties
author: Anthony Veasna So
description: Stories of Cambodian Americans in Central California—queer, hilarious, and haunted by the Khmer Rouge. Published after the author's death at 28.

title: Sabrina & Corina
author: Kali Fajardo-Anstine
description: Stories of Latina women in the American West—Denver, the mountains, indigenous and mestiza identity.

title: Woman of Light
author: Kali Fajardo-Anstine
description: A Chicana woman in 1930s Denver sees the future—her family's history from 1860s New Mexico forward.

title: Milk Blood Heat
author: Dantiel W. Moniz
description: Stories of Florida—Black women, heat, bodies, and the ferocity of girlhood.

title: Sharks in the Time of Saviors
author: Kawai Strong Washburn
description: A Hawaiian boy is saved by sharks—his family believes he's blessed. A novel about indigenous myth and modern displacement.

title: Disappearing Earth
author: Julia Phillips
description: Two girls vanish from a beach in Kamchatka—each chapter follows a different woman in the community over the next year.

title: A Lucky Man
author: Jamel Brinkley
description: Stories of Black men and boys in New York—masculinity, desire, and the neighborhoods that shape them.

title: Witness
author: Jamel Brinkley
description: More stories—gentrification, family, and what it means to watch from the margins.

title: Heads of the Colored People
author: Nafissa Thompson-Spires
description: Stories of Black middle-class life—academic parents, microaggressions, and the pressure to perform identity.

title: The Five Wounds
author: Kirstin Valdez Quade
description: A man in New Mexico plays Jesus in the Good Friday procession—real nails, real blood, and his pregnant teenage daughter watching.

title: Night at the Fiestas
author: Kirstin Valdez Quade
description: Stories of New Mexico—Catholic faith, Mexican American families, and the landscape that holds them.

title: The Young Lions
author: Irwin Shaw
description: Three soldiers in WWII—two Americans and a German—whose paths converge in the final days of the war. The great American WWII novel alongside The Naked and the Dead.

title: The Brothers
author: Frederick Barthelme
description: Two brothers and a woman on the Mississippi Gulf Coast—the suburbs as existential wasteland. Minimalism's other Barthelme.

title: Bob the Gambler
author: Frederick Barthelme
description: A couple loses everything gambling on the Gulf Coast—Barthelme's most autobiographical novel about addiction and marriage.

title: The Pugilist at Rest
author: Thom Jones
description: Stories of boxers, Marines, and people barely hanging on—Jones's debut collection of desperate, brilliant prose. National Book Award finalist.

title: An Occurrence at Owl Creek Bridge
author: Ambrose Bierce
description: Civil War stories and supernatural tales—the most bitter, sardonic American writer of the 19th century.

title: The Stories of Richard Bausch
author: Richard Bausch
description: Forty-two stories spanning three decades—marriages, families, and the small catastrophes of ordinary American life.

title: The Sport of the Gods
author: Paul Laurence Dunbar
description: A Black family is destroyed by a false accusation—they flee to New York and are destroyed again. The first African American naturalist novel.

title: A New England Nun
author: Mary Wilkins Freeman
description: Stories of New England women choosing solitude over bad marriages—proto-feminist regionalism.

title: Up from Slavery
author: Booker T. Washington
description: Washington's autobiography—from slavery to founding Tuskegee. The most influential Black memoir before Malcolm X.

title: Christ Stopped at Eboli
author: Carlo Levi
description: An anti-fascist intellectual is exiled to a remote Italian village—peasant life so poor that even Christ passed it by.

title: The Luck of Roaring Camp
author: Bret Harte
description: Stories of Gold Rush California—miners, gamblers, and the rough tenderness of frontier life. Harte invented the Western.

title: Machine Dreams
author: Jayne Anne Phillips
description: A West Virginia family from WWII to Vietnam—the American century told through the Hampsons' hopes and losses.

title: Feather Crowns
author: Bobbie Ann Mason
description: A Kentucky farm wife gives birth to quintuplets in 1900—fame devours the family. Mason's epic.

title: Summer in Williamsburg
author: Daniel Fuchs
description: A young man in a Brooklyn tenement—the first Williamsburg novel, working-class Jewish life in the Depression.

title: Christ in Concrete
author: Pietro di Donato
description: An Italian bricklayer is killed on Good Friday—his twelve-year-old son takes his place. The immigrant worker's novel.

title: The Girl
author: Meridel Le Sueur
description: A girl in Depression-era Minnesota—hunger, solidarity, and the radical hope of working-class women. Written in the 1930s, suppressed for decades.

title: Last Notes from Home
author: Frederick Exley
description: Exley's brother dies—the third fictional memoir of failure, Florida, and the American male on the edge.

title: Our House in the Last World
author: Oscar Hijuelos
description: A Cuban family in 1940s New York—the father's drinking, the mother's disappointment, and two boys caught between cultures.

title: The Long Night of White Chickens
author: Francisco Goldman
description: A Guatemalan American investigates his childhood nanny's murder—the Guatemalan civil war and the diaspora.

title: The Ordinary Seaman
author: Francisco Goldman
description: Central American sailors are stranded on a derelict ship in Brooklyn—Goldman's Kafkaesque immigrant novel.

title: World and Town
author: Gish Jen
description: A Chinese American woman and a Cambodian refugee family in a New England town—immigration, faith, and small-town America.

title: Native Speaker
author: Chang-rae Lee
description: A Korean American spy infiltrates a Korean American politician—identity as performance, belonging as betrayal.

title: A Gesture Life
author: Chang-rae Lee
description: A retired Japanese American man in a suburb hides his past—comfort women, war, and the price of assimilation.

title: On Such a Full Sea
author: Chang-rae Lee
description: A dystopian America divided into labor settlements—a young Chinese American woman leaves her community to find her lover.

title: Whereabouts
author: Jhumpa Lahiri
description: A woman walks through an unnamed Italian city—forty-six short chapters of solitude, observation, and the self. Lahiri's first novel written in Italian.

title: The Collected Stories of Lydia Davis
author: Lydia Davis
description: Seven hundred pages of stories—some are one sentence long. Davis reinvented the short story form. MacArthur genius, Man Booker International winner.

title: Can't and Won't
author: Lydia Davis
description: One hundred and twenty-two stories—some are dreams, some are complaints, one is a letter to a frozen peas company.

title: The End of the Story
author: Lydia Davis
description: A woman writes a novel about her last affair—the writing changes the memory. Davis's only novel.

title: The Dog Stars
author: Peter Heller
description: A pilot and his dog survive in a post-flu America—he hears a radio signal and flies toward it. The most lyrical apocalypse novel.

title: The River
author: Peter Heller
description: Two college friends canoe through the Canadian wilderness—a forest fire and a man's scream change everything.

title: The Guide
author: Peter Heller
description: A fly-fishing guide at an exclusive Colorado lodge discovers the resort is hiding something in the cliffs above the river.

title: Chemistry
author: Weike Wang
description: A Chinese American graduate student in chemistry can't decide whether to stay in her PhD or her relationship—anxiety as element.

title: Joan Is Okay
author: Weike Wang
description: A Chinese American ICU doctor during COVID—her mother visits, her colleagues worry, and Joan insists she's fine.

title: The Particular Sadness of Lemon Cake
author: Aimee Bender
description: A girl can taste emotions in food—her mother's lemon cake tastes of despair. Magic realism as family pathology.

title: The Girl in the Flammable Skirt
author: Aimee Bender
description: Surreal stories—a woman dates a man made of ice, a girl's skirt catches fire, a boy born with keys for fingers.

title: The Seas
author: Samantha Hunt
description: A girl in a coastal town believes she's a mermaid—her missing father, a war vet, and the ocean that connects them.

title: Mr. Splitfoot
author: Samantha Hunt
description: Two timelines—foster children in upstate New York communicate with the dead, and a pregnant woman follows a silent guide through the wilderness.

title: The Third Hotel
author: Laura van den Berg
description: A woman attends a film festival in Havana—she sees her dead husband alive. Horror, grief, and tropical surrealism.

title: I Hold a Wolf by the Ears
author: Laura van den Berg
description: Eleven stories of women in extremis—plagues, doubles, sinkholes, and the uncanny lurking in Florida.

title: Something New Under the Sun
author: Alexandra Kleeman
description: A novelist works on a film adaptation in LA—synthetic water, wildfires, and the end of California as ecological horror.

title: You Too Can Have a Body Like Mine
author: Alexandra Kleeman
description: A woman, her roommate, and her boyfriend—eating, watching TV, and losing the boundaries between bodies. DeLillo for millennials.

title: Refuge
author: Terry Tempest Williams
description: The Great Salt Lake rises as her mother dies of cancer—nuclear testing, birds, and the landscape of grief.

title: Sour Heart
author: Jenny Zhang
description: Stories of Chinese immigrant families in New York—daughters growing up between two worlds, all bodily fluids included.

title: The Beautiful Bureaucrat
author: Helen Phillips
description: A woman starts a data entry job—the numbers she enters control something terrible. Kafka meets the temp economy.

title: The Need
author: Helen Phillips
description: A paleobotanist finds an intruder in her house—the intruder looks exactly like her. Motherhood as body horror.

title: The Collected Stories
author: Diane Williams
description: Three decades of flash fiction—stories that are sometimes one paragraph, always a detonation. The most radical American miniaturist.

title: Our Endless Numbered Days
author: Claire Fuller
description: A father takes his daughter to a forest hut and tells her the rest of the world has been destroyed—she believes him for years.

title: Swimming Lessons
author: Claire Fuller
description: A famous novelist's wife vanishes—she left letters hidden inside his books. He's never read them.

title: Unsettled Ground
author: Claire Fuller
description: Twins who've never left home discover their dead mother's secrets—the cottage, the debts, the lies.

title: Preparation for the Next Life
author: Atticus Lish
description: An undocumented Uighur woman and an Iraq War vet with PTSD in Queens—the rawest love story of the century.

title: Gutshot
author: Amelia Gray
description: Stories where women swallow objects, hair grows from walls, and a man's wife is a sinkhole—Southern Gothic surrealism.

title: Isadora
author: Amelia Gray
description: The life of Isadora Duncan—the revolutionary dancer who lost her children, her art, and her neck in a scarf.

title: My Date with Satan
author: Stacey Richter
description: Stories of the American West gone wrong—a woman dates a sasquatch, a girl joins a gang, a neighborhood collapses.

title: Ugly Girls
author: Lindsay Hunter
description: Two teenage girls in a Florida trailer park—joyriding, catfishing, and the men who prey on them. Feral and furious.

title: The New and Improved Romie Futch
author: Julia Elliott
description: A taxidermist signs up for a brain enhancement experiment—he becomes a genius and goes hunting for Bigfoot. Southern sci-fi comedy.

title: One Hundred Apocalypses and Other Apocalypses
author: Lucy Corin
description: One hundred flash fictions about the end of things—each apocalypse is a different kind of ending, comic and terrible.

title: Swim for the Little One First
author: Noy Holland
description: Stories of women in danger—Holland's compressed, violent prose reads like poetry dictated under duress.

title: Further Adventures in the Restless Universe
author: Dawn Raffel
description: Stories pared to the bone—Raffel's minimalism makes Carver look verbose. Every word earns its place.

title: Florida
author: Christine Schutt
description: A girl grows up in the care of strangers—each chapter is a different foster home. Schutt's lyric precision is devastating. National Book Award finalist.

title: The Zookeeper's Wife
author: Diane Ackerman
description: The Warsaw Zoo during WWII—a zookeeper and her husband hide Jews in animal cages. A true story of courage and nature.

title: The Complete Tales of Ketzia Gold
author: Kate Bernheimer
description: A Jewish girl obsessed with fairy tales—Bernheimer blurs the line between magic and dysfunction.

title: Nice Big American Baby
author: Judy Budnitz
description: Surreal stories of immigration—a woman's pregnancy lasts years, a border is made of human bodies.

title: Half Life
author: Shelley Jackson
description: Conjoined twins in a world where twofers are common—one wants to kill the other. Postmodern body horror.

title: The Solace of Open Spaces
author: Gretel Ehrlich
description: Wyoming ranch life—Ehrlich moved west after her partner's death and found the landscape healed her. Nature writing as grief memoir.

title: Youngblood
author: Matt Gallagher
description: An Iraq War lieutenant navigates the surge—soldiers, sheikhs, and the moral impossibility of counterinsurgency. Written by a veteran.

title: Sand Queen
author: Helen Benedict
description: A female soldier and an Iraqi woman—war from both sides, sexual harassment, and the cost of occupation.

title: Battleborn
author: Claire Vaye Watkins
description: Stories of the Nevada desert—the Manson Family, mining towns, and the American West as myth and ruin. Debut collection.

title: The Diver's Clothes Lie Empty
author: Vendela Vida
description: A woman's passport and wallet are stolen in Casablanca—she assumes a new identity and likes it better. Second-person identity crisis.

title: The Agüero Sisters
author: Cristina García
description: Two Cuban sisters—one in Havana, one in Miami—connected by their father's terrible secret about their mother's death.

title: Eleven Days
author: Lea Carpenter
description: A mother waits for her son—a Navy SEAL who hasn't returned from a mission that looks a lot like the Bin Laden raid.

title: Say Her Name
author: Francisco Goldman
description: Goldman's wife died in a body-surfing accident two years after their wedding—this novel is his attempt to keep her alive.

title: The Last of Her Kind
author: Sigrid Nunez
description: Two college roommates in the 1960s—one becomes a radical who kills a cop. Nunez's most ambitious novel about America's broken idealism.

title: Emily, Alone
author: Stewart O'Nan
description: Emily Maxwell is eighty and widowed in Pittsburgh—she goes to the grocery store, sees the doctor, and faces another day. The most beautiful novel about aging.

title: The Longest Silence
author: Tom McGuane
description: McGuane on fishing—from Montana to the Keys, the philosophy of angling as a way of being in the world.

title: The Music of Chance
author: Paul Auster
description: Two men lose a poker game and must build a wall from a medieval castle—Kafka meets the American road.

title: The Third Life of Grange Copeland
author: Alice Walker
description: Three generations of a Black sharecropping family—violence, poverty, and the possibility of redemption. Walker's debut.

title: Linden Hills
author: Gloria Naylor
description: A prosperous Black neighborhood modeled on Dante's Inferno—each circle of success hides a deeper damnation.

title: Tell Me How Long the Train's Been Gone
author: James Baldwin
description: A famous Black actor has a heart attack—flashbacks reveal his brother, his white lover, and a young Black revolutionary.

title: Mules and Men
author: Zora Neale Hurston
description: Hurston collects Black folklore in Florida and hoodoo in New Orleans—the first great work of African American anthropology.

title: The Earth Transformed
author: Peter Frankopan
description: How climate has shaped civilization—from ice ages to global warming, the environment as history's hidden driver.

title: The Dawn of Everything
author: David Graeber
description: Human history rewritten—inequality wasn't inevitable, cities didn't need kings, and everything you learned about the past is wrong.

title: How Democracies Die
author: Steven Levitsky
description: How elected leaders dismantle democracy from within—historical parallels from Venezuela to Turkey to the United States.

title: The Age of Surveillance Capitalism
author: Shoshana Zuboff
description: Google and Facebook mine human experience for profit—the most important book about tech since 1984.

title: The Anthropocene Reviewed
author: John Green
description: Reviews of the human-centered planet—from scratch-and-sniff stickers to plague to sunsets. Each rated on a five-star scale.

title: The Only Story
author: Julian Barnes
description: A nineteen-year-old falls for a forty-eight-year-old married woman—their love story told across a lifetime.

title: The Enigma of Arrival
author: V.S. Naipaul
description: A Trinidadian writer settles in rural Wiltshire—the English countryside as colonial mirror. Naipaul's most personal novel.

title: In a Free State
author: V.S. Naipaul
description: Three stories of displacement—an Indian servant in Washington, an African revolution, an Egyptian hustler. Booker winner.

title: The Master of Petersburg
author: J.M. Coetzee
description: Dostoevsky in 1869 St. Petersburg—his stepson is dead and the police want to use Dostoevsky as a spy.

title: Paula
author: Isabel Allende
description: Allende's daughter falls into a coma—this memoir is written at her bedside, telling the family's story to keep her alive.

title: How to Be Both
author: Ali Smith
description: Two stories—a Renaissance painter and a modern teenager—printed in random order. Every copy reads differently. Booker shortlisted.

title: Your Face Tomorrow: Dance and Dream
author: Javier Marías
description: The narrator witnesses shocking violence—Marías's long sentences spiral through memory, betrayal, and the ethics of watching.

title: Your Face Tomorrow: Poison, Shadow, and Farewell
author: Javier Marías
description: The trilogy concludes—the narrator must decide whether to use the violence he's been taught. Marías's greatest achievement.

title: Nostalgia
author: Mircea Cărtărescu
description: Five stories of Bucharest—a boy who flies, twins separated at birth, and a city dreaming itself. Romanian magical realism.

title: Villa Triste
author: Patrick Modiano
description: A man remembers a summer in a lake town during the Algerian War—everyone was hiding something. Nobel laureate.

title: The Successor
author: Ismail Kadare
description: Albania's designated successor to the dictator is found dead—suicide or assassination? Kadare's political mystery.

title: The River Between
author: Ngũgĩ wa Thiong'o
description: Two ridges divided by a river—one Christian, one traditional. A young man tries to bridge them as Kenya fights for independence.

title: Devil on the Cross
author: Ngũgĩ wa Thiong'o
description: The first novel written in Gĩkũyũ—thieves and robbers compete for the title of greatest exploiter. Written on toilet paper in prison.

title: Notes on Grief
author: Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie
description: Adichie's father dies during COVID—she can't travel to Nigeria. A brief, devastating meditation on loss.

title: Night Sky with Exit Wounds
author: Ocean Vuong
description: Poems of war, immigration, and desire—Vuong's debut that announced a major voice. T.S. Eliot Prize winner.

title: Heart Berries
author: Terese Marie Mailhot
description: A Seabird Island woman writes her way through bipolar disorder, motherhood, and the reservation—memoir as survival.

title: The Removed
author: Brandon Hobson
description: A Cherokee family gathers fifteen years after a son's death—the Trail of Tears and modern grief merge through visions.

title: Where the Dead Sit Talking
author: Brandon Hobson
description: A Cherokee teenager in a foster home bonds with another troubled kid—Indigenous youth and the system that fails them.

title: The Heartbeat of Wounded Knee
author: David Treuer
description: Native American history from 1890 to the present—not a story of decline but of survival, resistance, and reinvention. National Book Award finalist.

title: The Marrow Thieves
author: Cherie Dimaline
description: In a future where only Indigenous people can still dream, they're hunted for their bone marrow—dystopian resistance.

title: White Magic
author: Elissa Washuta
description: A Cowlitz woman examines her relationship with the land through tarot, astrology, and the colonization of magic.

title: Whereas
author: Layli Long Soldier
description: Poetry responding to the Congressional Apology to Native Peoples—Lakota language, legal language, and the space between. National Book Award finalist.

title: Postcolonial Love Poem
author: Natalie Diaz
description: Mojave desire, the Colorado River, and the body as colonized land—Diaz's Pulitzer-winning poems burn.

title: An American Sunrise
author: Joy Harjo
description: The first Native American Poet Laureate—Muscogee Creek memory, jazz, and a sunrise that's been coming since 1830.

title: Crazy Brave
author: Joy Harjo
description: Harjo's memoir—growing up in Tulsa, finding music and poetry, and the journey to becoming a voice for Indigenous America.

title: Nothing Ever Dies
author: Viet Thanh Nguyen
description: How wars are remembered and forgotten—Vietnam, Korea, and the machinery of memory across nations.

title: Don't Let Me Be Lonely
author: Claudia Rankine
description: An American lyric about depression, death, and media—Rankine's first experiment in the form she perfected with Citizen.

title: Minor Feelings
author: Cathy Park Hong
description: An Asian American reckoning—the minor feelings of shame, melancholy, and rage that come from being a model minority.

title: The Best We Could Do
author: Thi Bui
description: A Vietnamese American woman's graphic memoir—her parents' escape from Saigon, immigration, and the weight of inherited trauma.

title: Maus II
author: Art Spiegelman
description: The Holocaust continues—Vladek in Auschwitz, Art struggling to draw it. The second volume, completing the masterwork.

title: Persepolis: The Story of a Childhood
author: Marjane Satrapi
description: An Iranian girl grows up during the Islamic Revolution—Marxist uncle, contraband Iron Maiden tapes, and the veil.

title: Are You My Mother?
author: Alison Bechdel
description: Bechdel follows Fun Home with her mother's story—psychoanalysis, Virginia Woolf, and the harder parent to understand.

title: Twenties Girl
author: Sophie Kinsella
description: A great-aunt's ghost appears at her own funeral—she needs her necklace found. Kinsella's funniest standalone.

title: I Owe You One
author: Sophie Kinsella
description: A woman saves a stranger's laptop in a coffee shop—he owes her one. The favor exchange spirals out of control.

title: Finding Audrey
author: Sophie Kinsella
description: A girl with severe anxiety starts filming a documentary about her family—then she meets the boy next door.

title: The Woman Who Stole My Life
author: Marian Keyes
description: A woman is paralyzed by Guillain-Barré syndrome—her recovery makes her famous, but her old life won't take her back.

title: The Mystery of Mercy Close
author: Marian Keyes
description: A private detective with depression searches for a missing man—the Walsh family returns.

title: When I Was a Child I Read Books
author: Marilynne Robinson
description: Essays on faith, science, and American democracy—Robinson's fierce intelligence applied to the crisis of public discourse.

title: The Beginner's Goodbye
author: Anne Tyler
description: A man's wife dies—then she starts appearing to him. Tyler's gentlest ghost story.

title: Like Life
author: Lorrie Moore
description: Eight stories—a woman's baby is sick, a couple drifts, a woman moves to the country. Moore at her sharpest.

title: Bark
author: Lorrie Moore
description: Eight stories after a long silence—older, darker, funnier. Moore on aging, war, and the comedy of devastation.

title: Breaking and Entering
author: Joy Williams
description: A couple breaks into vacant Florida homes—they're not stealing, just inhabiting. Williams on dislocation.

title: Rodham
author: Curtis Sittenfeld
description: What if Hillary didn't marry Bill? An alternate history of the most famous political marriage in America.

title: Arcadia
author: Lauren Groff
description: A boy grows up on a commune in 1970s New York—the dream of utopia and its inevitable collapse.

title: The Monsters of Templeton
author: Lauren Groff
description: A woman returns to Cooperstown pregnant—a monster surfaces in the lake and her family's secrets rise with it.

title: A Life's Work
author: Rachel Cusk
description: Cusk on motherhood—brutal, funny, and honest about the obliteration of self that comes with having a baby.

title: Homesick for Another World
author: Ottessa Moshfegh
description: Fourteen stories of repulsive, lonely people—each one indelible. Moshfegh's short fiction is as strong as her novels.

title: Fine Just the Way It Is
author: Annie Proulx
description: Stories of Wyoming—cowboys, wind, and people crushed by landscape. The third Wyoming collection.

title: Winter in Paradise
author: Elin Hilderbrand
description: A woman's husband dies in the Caribbean—she discovers he had a secret life in St. John.

title: What Happens in Paradise
author: Elin Hilderbrand
description: The family returns to St. John to unravel more of the husband's secrets—Caribbean mystery continues.

title: Troubles in Paradise
author: Elin Hilderbrand
description: The Paradise trilogy concludes—truth, justice, and the tropical sun reveal everything.

title: Let It Bleed
author: Ian Rankin
description: Rebus investigates the death of a councilman's daughter—Edinburgh's politics are as corrupt as its criminals.

title: Spirit of Steamboat
author: Craig Johnson
description: A Christmas Eve novella—a young Longmire flies a WWII bomber through a blizzard to save a burned girl.

title: G Is for Gumshoe
author: Sue Grafton
description: Kinsey turns 33 and someone puts a hit on her—she hires a bodyguard she can't stand.

title: H Is for Homicide
author: Sue Grafton
description: Kinsey goes undercover in an insurance fraud ring—she ends up living with a violent con man.

title: I Is for Innocent
author: Sue Grafton
description: A murdered investigator's case lands in Kinsey's lap—she reconstructs his work and finds what killed him.

title: J Is for Judgment
author: Sue Grafton
description: A dead man appears alive in Mexico—Kinsey must find him while her own family past catches up.

title: K Is for Killer
author: Sue Grafton
description: A woman was murdered months ago and no one investigated—her mother hires Kinsey. The night world of Santa Teresa.

title: Taming a Sea-Horse
author: Robert B. Parker
description: Spenser searches for a missing prostitute—she's in danger from a porn king and a real estate mogul.

title: Pale Kings and Princes
author: Robert B. Parker
description: A reporter investigating cocaine trafficking is murdered—Spenser goes to the source.

title: Crimson Joy
author: Robert B. Parker
description: A serial killer targets Black women—the evidence points at Susan's new patient. Spenser's most personal case.

title: A Ticket to the Boneyard
author: Lawrence Block
description: A man Scudder helped convict is released from prison—he's coming to kill everyone Scudder cares about.

title: The Devil Knows You're Dead
author: Lawrence Block
description: A street person is accused of murder—Scudder investigates the gentrification of Hell's Kitchen.

title: Even the Wicked
author: Lawrence Block
description: A vigilante killer is executing people who escaped justice—Scudder must stop him while questioning whether he should.

title: Walkin' the Dog
author: Walter Mosley
description: Socrates Fortlow—an ex-con in Watts tries to live right. Connected stories of redemption in South Central.

title: The Man in My Basement
author: Walter Mosley
description: A Black man rents his basement to a white man who wants to be imprisoned—race, guilt, and power.

title: Wayfaring Stranger
author: James Lee Burke
description: Two Texas soldiers survive the liberation of a concentration camp—they come home to fight the oil industry.

title: The New Iberia Blues
author: James Lee Burke
description: Bodies posed like tarot cards appear in New Iberia—Robicheaux faces an artist-killer and his own ghosts.

title: The Cut
author: George Pelecanos
description: An Iraq vet becomes a PI in DC—his first case connects to a sex trafficking ring.

title: The Double
author: George Pelecanos
description: Spero Lucas hunts a stolen painting—Pelecanos's modern DC meets the underground art world.

title: LaBrava
author: Elmore Leonard
description: A former Secret Service agent photographs Miami Beach—a faded movie star draws him into a con.

title: The Temptation of Forgiveness
author: Donna Leon
description: A woman falls from a bridge—Brunetti suspects her husband's debts connect to a larger fraud.

title: A Beam of Light
author: Andrea Camilleri
description: A beam of light through a window reveals a crime—Montalbano investigates while his love life crumbles.

title: The Dance of the Seagull
author: Andrea Camilleri
description: Montalbano's loyal deputy Fazio is kidnapped—the inspector races to find him before the seagulls reveal the worst.

title: The Shadow District
author: Arnaldur Indriðason
description: A retired detective reopens a WWII-era murder in Reykjavík—American soldiers, Icelandic women, and a body behind a theater.

title: Touch & Go
author: Lisa Gardner
description: A family vanishes from their Boston home—the ransom demand is unusual. They want the husband to pay for what he did.

title: Fear Nothing
author: Lisa Gardner
description: D.D. Warren and Flora Dane team up—a serial killer targets women who can't feel pain.

title: Saving Faith
author: David Baldacci
description: A lobbyist knows too much about both sides of a political conspiracy—the CIA and FBI both want her dead.

title: Stay Close
author: Harlan Coben
description: Three people in Atlantic City—a suburban mom, a detective, and a photographer—are connected by a disappearance seventeen years ago.

title: Worth Dying For
author: Lee Child
description: Reacher takes on a family of Nebraska cattle barons—they've been running the county for generations, and a child went missing.

title: Never Go Back
author: Lee Child
description: Reacher returns to his old command—the new CO has been arrested and he's been framed for a murder.

title: Hope to Die
author: James Patterson
description: Cross's family is kidnapped—the killer demands he solve impossible cases to keep them alive.

title: Cross the Line
author: James Patterson
description: A sniper is targeting the powerful—Cross and Sampson hunt a killer with a political agenda.

title: Wanderlust
author: Danielle Steel
description: A woman photographer travels the world between the wars—Shanghai, London, Africa, and the men who try to pin her down.

title: The Ring
author: Danielle Steel
