title: The Boy
author: Tami Hoag
description: A seven-year-old is murdered in Cajun Louisiana—his mother is lying, his teacher is hiding, and detective Nick Fourcade hunts the truth.

title: The 9th Girl
author: Tami Hoag
description: A girl's body is found in a frozen Minneapolis lake—she's the ninth in a series connected to a shock jock.

title: 1st to Die
author: James Patterson
description: Four professional women form the Women's Murder Club to catch a serial killer targeting newlyweds—Patterson's biggest series after Cross.

title: 2nd Chance
author: James Patterson
description: A sniper targets Black victims in San Francisco—the Women's Murder Club hunts a killer with a racial agenda.

title: Birthright
author: Nora Roberts
description: An archaeologist discovers she was a stolen baby—her dig in the Appalachians unearths more than ancient bones.

title: Angels Fall
author: Nora Roberts
description: A woman who survived a mass shooting witnesses a murder in Wyoming—nobody believes her. She must prove she's sane.

title: Northern Lights
author: Nora Roberts
description: A burned-out Baltimore cop becomes police chief of a small Alaska town—the frozen wilderness and a bush pilot heal him.

title: The Good Fight
author: Danielle Steel
description: A young lawyer in the 1960s fights for civil rights—from Freedom Riders to Vietnam protests.

title: All That Glitters
author: Danielle Steel
description: A journalist's perfect life shatters on 9/11—rebuilding from the worst day in American history.

title: The Husband
author: Dean Koontz
description: A landscaper's wife is kidnapped—the ransom is $2 million and he has sixty hours. He earns $45,000 a year.

title: Doctored Evidence
author: Donna Leon
description: An elderly woman is found murdered in her apartment—her Romanian maid is blamed but Brunetti suspects the victim's own family.

title: Through a Glass, Darkly
author: Donna Leon
description: A Murano glass factory is dumping toxic waste—Brunetti investigates when whistleblowers start dying.

title: Beastly Things
author: Donna Leon
description: A body at a Venice slaughterhouse—Brunetti discovers the illegal trade in animals and the people who profit from cruelty.

title: Give unto Others
author: Donna Leon
description: A veterinarian asks Brunetti for help—his daughter is being followed. The investigation leads to financial fraud.

title: The Overnight Kidnapper
author: Andrea Camilleri
description: Two women are kidnapped and returned unharmed—Montalbano suspects it's connected to construction contracts and corruption.

title: Abattoir Blues
author: Peter Robinson
description: A tractor is stolen from a farm—Banks discovers it's connected to a migrant worker's death and human trafficking.

title: How the Dead Speak
author: Val McDermid
description: Bodies are found at a convent—Tony Hill and Carol Jordan investigate deaths spanning decades.

title: The Rising Tide
author: Ann Cleeves
description: Former classmates reunite every five years on Holy Island—one is found dead at low tide. Vera's penultimate case.

title: Wait for Signs
author: Craig Johnson
description: Twelve Longmire stories—one for each month. Christmas in Absaroka County, a missing horse, and Henry Standing Bear's wisdom.

title: The Complaints
author: Ian Rankin
description: Internal affairs detective Malcolm Fox investigates a dirty cop—then his own life starts falling apart. Rankin without Rebus.

title: Hombre
author: Elmore Leonard
description: A white man raised by Apaches must decide whether to save the racists who despise him—Leonard's best Western.

title: Pronto
author: Elmore Leonard
description: A bookie flees to Italy—the mob, the FBI, and Raylan Givens are all looking for him. The novel that launched Justified.

title: The Way Home
author: George Pelecanos
description: A juvenile offender gets a construction job—his partner finds cash in a wall and everything goes wrong.

title: Bluebird, Bluebird
author: Attica Locke
description: A Black Texas Ranger investigates two murders in a small East Texas town—one Black victim, one white. Everyone's lying.

title: Heaven, My Home
author: Attica Locke
description: Ranger Darren Mathews searches for a missing white supremacist's son—on a lake where an entire Black town was flooded.

title: The Winter of Frankie Machine
author: Don Winslow
description: A retired San Diego hit man is targeted—he must figure out which of his many old jobs is getting him killed.

title: Savages
author: Don Winslow
description: Two marijuana growers and their shared girlfriend face a Mexican cartel—SoCal beach noir at maximum velocity.

title: Darkness, Take My Hand
author: Dennis Lehane
description: A serial killer from the 1970s is connected to a current crime—Kenzie must protect a woman from her own past.

title: Sacred
author: Dennis Lehane
description: A dying billionaire hires Kenzie to find his missing daughter—she doesn't want to be found.

title: Prayers for Rain
author: Dennis Lehane
description: A woman's life is systematically destroyed—Kenzie hunts the stalker who's dismantling her from within.

title: Moonlight Mile
author: Dennis Lehane
description: Amanda McCready is missing again—twelve years after Gone Baby Gone, Kenzie must find her once more.

title: Black Water Rising
author: Attica Locke
description: A Houston lawyer rescues a woman from a bayou—the act connects him to an oil company's conspiracy. Locke's debut.

title: The Cutting Season
author: Attica Locke
description: A body is found on a Louisiana plantation—the manager must face the property's slave history to solve the murder.

title: Pleasantville
author: Attica Locke
description: A girl vanishes during a Houston election—the historically Black neighborhood of Pleasantville hides the truth.

title: Cinnamon Kiss
author: Walter Mosley
description: Easy Rawlins needs money for his daughter's medical treatment—he takes a job that leads to Nazi gold and murder.

title: Blonde Faith
author: Walter Mosley
description: Easy searches for a friend and a missing girl in 1967 LA—racial tensions, Vietnam, and the Watts riots aftermath.

title: Little Green
author: Walter Mosley
description: Easy Rawlins survives—he wakes from a coma to find LA has changed. A young man has gone missing in the hippie scene.

title: Dixie City Jam
author: James Lee Burke
description: A sunken Nazi submarine in the Gulf—Robicheaux faces white supremacists and a con man preacher.

title: Burning Angel
author: James Lee Burke
description: A Civil War-era murder connects to modern drug trafficking—Robicheaux fights for a Black family's land.

title: Cadillac Jukebox
author: James Lee Burke
description: A governor candidate's past includes murder—Robicheaux must choose between justice and politics.

title: All the Flowers Are Dying
author: Lawrence Block
description: A serial killer taunts Scudder—the most dangerous adversary he's faced. The final full-length Scudder novel.

title: Hope to Die
author: Lawrence Block
description: A family is murdered and Scudder knows the official story is wrong—the killer is still out there.

title: Blue-Eyed Devil
author: Robert B. Parker
description: Virgil Cole and Everett Hitch face a corrupt lawman in the West—Parker's final novel, published posthumously.

title: Sixkill
author: Robert B. Parker
description: Spenser takes on a movie star accused of killing a young woman—his last published novel.

title: The Impossible Dead
author: Ian Rankin
description: Malcolm Fox investigates a cop who may have been framed—the trail leads to 1980s Scotland and a radical disappearance.

title: An Event in Autumn
author: Henning Mankell
description: Wallander finds a skeleton in his new garden—a novella that serves as the last glimpse of the detective.

title: Reykjavik Nights
author: Arnaldur Indriðason
description: Young Erlendur as a beat cop—a homeless man drowns and the rookie detective senses foul play. Prequel.

title: In the Darkness
author: Karin Fossum
description: A woman's body floats in a lake—Inspector Sejer's first case, and Fossum's debut. Norwegian noir begins.

title: A Climate of Fear
author: Fred Vargas
description: Members of an Icelandic expedition are being murdered—Adamsberg investigates a curse that began in volcanic fog.

title: The Sicilian Method
author: Andrea Camilleri
description: A famous actor is murdered—Montalbano suspects the killing mirrors a play being performed in Vigàta.

title: Trace Elements
author: Donna Leon
description: A dying woman's confession—she says her husband was poisoned by someone at the company he worked for. Brunetti investigates.

title: A Song for the Dying
author: Stuart MacBride
description: Ash Henderson is out of prison—the Inside Man serial killer has started again and only Ash can stop him. MacBride standalone.

title: The Dying Hours
author: Mark Billingham
description: A pensioner dies in a suspicious fire—Thorne connects it to other elderly deaths and a pattern of silenced witnesses.

title: The Locked Room
author: Elly Griffiths
description: COVID lockdown in Norfolk—Ruth finds bones in a garden and a friend receives threatening letters. Galloway investigates from home.

title: Mrs McGinty's Dead
author: Agatha Christie
description: A charwoman is murdered for thirty pounds—Poirot investigates because the convicted man's solicitor says he's innocent.

title: Lord Edgware Dies
author: Agatha Christie
description: An actress asks Poirot to help her divorce Lord Edgware—then Lord Edgware is stabbed. She has the perfect alibi.

title: Bag of Bones
author: Stephen King
description: A writer moves to his lake house after his wife dies—the house is haunted and the town has a terrible secret about race and murder.

title: The Colorado Kid
author: Stephen King
description: Two old editors tell a young reporter about an unsolved mystery—a dead man on an island with no explanation. The mystery stays unsolved.

title: Black Hills
author: Nora Roberts
description: A woman returns to the Black Hills of South Dakota—a childhood friend has become a man worth loving, but a killer stalks the wilderness.

title: Carnal Innocence
author: Nora Roberts
description: A concert violinist flees to rural Mississippi—a serial killer is targeting local women. Roberts's darkest standalone.

title: The Witness
author: Sandra Brown
description: A woman in witness protection falls for the local sheriff—the man she's hiding from is a corrupt judge.

title: Jewels
author: Danielle Steel
description: A woman builds a jewelry empire from war-torn Europe to modern Paris—love, loss, and diamonds across six decades.

title: Message from Nam
author: Danielle Steel
description: A journalist covers the Vietnam War—the soldiers, the protests, and the love that survives it all.

title: Private
author: James Patterson
description: A PI firm investigates the NFL's most dangerous secrets—Jack Morgan runs Private, the world's most elite detective agency.

title: NYPD Red
author: James Patterson
description: An elite NYPD squad protects the rich and famous—a killer is targeting Hollywood stars during a film festival.

title: The Partner
author: John Grisham
description: A lawyer fakes his death and steals $90 million—then they find him in Brazil. The trial becomes the real story.

title: The Testament
author: John Grisham
description: A billionaire leaves his fortune to an unknown missionary in the Brazilian jungle—the family sues, a drunk lawyer is sent to find her.

title: Total Control
author: David Baldacci
description: A man appears to die in a plane crash—his wife discovers he may have been involved in technology theft.

title: Live Wire
author: Harlan Coben
description: Myron's client's husband has vanished—the search leads to a decades-old conspiracy involving Myron's own family.

title: Home
author: Harlan Coben
description: Two boys kidnapped ten years ago—one is found wandering London. Myron Bolitar searches for answers.

title: Lost Light
author: Michael Connelly
description: Bosch retires from the LAPD and reopens a cold case—a production assistant was murdered and $2 million vanished.

title: Of the Farm
author: John Updike
description: A man visits his mother's farm with his new wife—three people and a cow negotiate the past.

title: The Castle in the Forest
author: Norman Mailer
description: A demon narrates the childhood of Adolf Hitler—evil as supernatural intervention. Mailer's final novel.

title: The Last Tycoon
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A Hollywood producer modeled on Irving Thalberg—Fitzgerald's unfinished final novel about power and art in the movies.

title: After Many a Summer
author: Aldous Huxley
description: A California millionaire seeks immortality—Huxley's Hollywood satire and his first American novel.

title: Ape and Essence
author: Aldous Huxley
description: Post-nuclear Los Angeles—mutants worship Belial and reverse all values. Huxley's darkest vision after Brave New World.

title: Barnaby Rudge
author: Charles Dickens
description: The Gordon Riots of 1780—mob violence, a simpleton with a raven, and Dickens's first historical novel.

title: He Knew He Was Right
author: Anthony Trollope
description: A husband's jealousy destroys him—Trollope's psychological masterpiece about obsession and the damage of suspicion.

title: Mary Barton
author: Elizabeth Gaskell
description: A working-class girl in 1840s Manchester—factory strikes, murder, and the gap between rich and poor. Gaskell's industrial debut.

title: The Master of Ballantrae
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
description: Two Scottish brothers—one joins Bonnie Prince Charlie, the other stays loyal. Their rivalry spans decades and continents.

title: Towards Zero
author: Agatha Christie
description: A house party at a cliff-top estate—someone has been planning murder for months. Christie's most meticulously constructed plot.

title: The Man in the Brown Suit
author: Agatha Christie
description: A girl witnesses a death at a Tube station—she follows the clues to South Africa. Christie's early adventure thriller.

title: Death in the Clouds
author: Agatha Christie
description: A woman dies on a plane from Paris to London—everyone in first class is a suspect, including Poirot himself.

title: Dolores Claiborne
author: Stephen King
description: A housekeeper confesses—not to the murder she's accused of, but to the one she actually committed decades ago. King's most feminist novel.

title: The Girl Who Loved Tom Gordon
author: Stephen King
description: A nine-year-old girl gets lost in the woods—she walks for days, listening to Red Sox games on her Walkman. Something is following her.

title: Public Secrets
author: Nora Roberts
description: A rock star's daughter witnesses her brother's murder as a child—twenty years later, the killer returns. Roberts's darkest early thriller.

title: Genuine Lies
author: Nora Roberts
description: A Hollywood legend hires a writer for her memoir—the secrets she reveals are worth killing for.

title: Montana Sky
author: Nora Roberts
description: Three sisters inherit a Montana ranch—they must live on it together for a year or lose everything. A killer stalks the land.

title: A Is for Alibi
author: Sue Grafton
description: Kinsey Millhone's first case—a woman convicted of poisoning her husband is released and hires Kinsey to find the real killer. The alphabet begins.

title: Miracle
author: Danielle Steel
description: Three people's lives intersect after a devastating earthquake—faith, survival, and second chances.

title: Sunset in St. Tropez
author: Danielle Steel
description: Three couples vacation together—affairs, illness, and the truth about thirty-year marriages.

title: Sisters
author: Danielle Steel
description: Four sisters face life after their parents' accident—each must find her own way to survive and love.

title: Cross My Heart
author: James Patterson
description: Cross receives a terrifying threat—a genius psychopath targets his entire family one by one.

title: Merry Christmas, Alex Cross
author: James Patterson
description: Cross serves Christmas dinner at a homeless shelter—then a hostage situation erupts across town.

title: Ford County
author: John Grisham
description: Seven stories of rural Mississippi—Grisham's first short fiction. Meth labs, snakebite preachers, and Southern desperation.

title: Deliver Us from Evil
author: David Baldacci
description: A man hunts the world's most brutal criminals—the CIA operative Evan Waller has left a trail of destruction across continents.

title: Seconds Away
author: Harlan Coben
description: Mickey Bolitar investigates a shooting—his teacher was targeted and the clues connect to a mysterious organization.

title: Found
author: Harlan Coben
description: The conclusion—Mickey finally learns what happened to his father and the Abeona Shelter's true purpose.

title: Fat Tuesday
author: Sandra Brown
description: A cop kidnaps a drug lord's wife on Mardi Gras—the only way to bring down the untouchable criminal.

title: The Switch
author: Sandra Brown
description: Twin sisters swap identities—one ends up in a kidnapper's crosshairs meant for the other.

title: Pandora's Daughter
author: Iris Johansen
description: A woman discovers she has psychic abilities—a shadowy organization wants to weaponize her gift.

title: Bonnie
author: Iris Johansen
description: Eve Duncan's journey reaches its climax—she finally learns the truth about her daughter Bonnie's fate.

title: Vortex
author: Catherine Coulter
description: Savich and Sherlock investigate a CIA agent's murder—a painting connects to a congressman and a decades-old secret.

title: Life Expectancy
author: Dean Koontz
description: A man is told the five dates he'll face crisis—he lives his life around them, and on each date, terror arrives.

title: Brother Odd
author: Dean Koontz
description: Odd Thomas takes shelter at a monastery—the dead are appearing and something dark is coming for the children there.

title: Keeping the Dead
author: Tess Gerritsen
description: A mummy in a museum is actually a recent murder victim—Rizzoli and Isles investigate when the killer starts recreating history.

title: Chosen to Die
author: Lisa Jackson
description: A serial killer in the Montana wilderness leaves victims to freeze—detective Pescoli is his next target.

title: Born to Die
author: Lisa Jackson
description: A doctor's patients are dying—someone is killing people connected to her clinic and the secrets they share.

title: Skeleton Crew
author: Stephen King
description: Twenty-two stories including The Mist—a supermarket besieged by creatures from another dimension. King's best collection.

title: Night Shift
author: Stephen King
description: Twenty stories including Children of the Corn and Jerusalem's Lot—King's first collection, raw and terrifying.

title: Nightmares & Dreamscapes
author: Stephen King
description: Twenty-three stories—a possessed finger, a homicidal toy soldier, and Dolan's Cadillac. King's most varied collection.

title: Everything's Eventual
author: Stephen King
description: Fourteen stories including 1408 and The Road Virus Heads North—King's 21st century short fiction begins.

title: Whiskey Beach
author: Nora Roberts
description: A man accused of murder hides in his family's beach house—a local woman helps him search for pirate treasure.

title: The Reef
author: Nora Roberts
description: A marine archaeologist hunts for a legendary necklace—her partner's father may have killed for it decades ago.

title: Country
author: Danielle Steel
description: A widow at a concert meets a country music star—Nashville, motherhood, and starting over at fifty.

title: Prodigal Son
author: Danielle Steel
description: Twin brothers reunite after twenty years—one is rich and lost, the other found meaning in Africa.

title: A Perfect Life
author: Danielle Steel
description: A news anchor's perfect life is disrupted—a stalker and a secret from her past threaten everything.

title: The Women's War
author: James Patterson
description: Women around the world begin fighting back against oppression—Patterson's most topical thriller.

title: 12th of Never
author: James Patterson
description: Lindsay Boxer is in labor—but a serial killer won't wait. The Women's Murder Club races against biology.

title: The Racketeer
author: John Grisham
description: A lawyer in prison knows who killed a federal judge—he'll trade the information for his freedom.

title: Gray Mountain
author: John Grisham
description: A laid-off lawyer takes a job in Appalachian coal country—she discovers Big Coal is poisoning everything.

title: The Hit
author: David Baldacci
description: Will Robie is the government's best assassin—then he's ordered to kill a fellow operative. The target is better than he is.

title: The Target
author: David Baldacci
description: Robie and Reel must infiltrate North Korea—the mission is impossible and the stakes are nuclear.

title: End Game
author: David Baldacci
description: Robie goes off the grid searching for his handler—the trail leads to a small Colorado town with a deadly secret.

title: The Drop
author: Michael Connelly
description: DNA links a rape case to a suspect who was eight at the time—Bosch investigates while the department wants him retired.

title: In Too Deep
author: Lee Child
description: Reacher goes undercover in a small town controlled by a single family—nothing is what it seems.

title: Chill Factor
author: Sandra Brown
description: A woman is stranded in a mountain cabin during a blizzard—with a man who might be a serial killer. Or her savior.

title: Ricochet
author: Sandra Brown
description: A detective's wife shoots an intruder—the case is clean. Too clean. He investigates his own wife.

title: The Alibi Man
author: Tami Hoag
description: A body in a canal wears a saddle—Elena Estes investigates Palm Beach's equestrian world and its dangerous men.

title: You Don't Want to Know
author: Lisa Jackson
description: A woman returns from a psychiatric hospital—she's sure her missing son is alive, but everyone says she's crazy.

title: Watch Your Back
author: Karen Rose
description: A cop shooter is targeting Baltimore police—detective Stevie Mazzetti is in the crosshairs.

title: Reckless Abandon
author: Stuart Woods
description: Stone Barrington and Holly Barker hunt a terrorist hiding in New York—the FBI and CIA can't be trusted.

title: Fresh Disasters
author: Stuart Woods
description: Stone is drawn into a web of corruption—a new client brings old enemies out of the shadows.

title: The Last Camel Died at Noon
author: Elizabeth Peters
description: The Emersons search for a lost oasis—and find a hidden civilization in the Nubian desert.

title: The Snake, the Crocodile and the Dog
author: Elizabeth Peters
description: Emerson loses his memory—enemies close in while Amelia tries to make her husband fall in love with her again.

title: The Hippopotamus Pool
author: Elizabeth Peters
description: A queen's tomb and a murderous conspiracy—Ramses is grown and the family dynamics shift dangerously.

title: Seeing a Large Cat
author: Elizabeth Peters
description: A young woman claims someone wants her dead—Amelia investigates while Ramses pursues his own dangerous path.

title: The Ape Who Guards the Balance
author: Elizabeth Peters
description: Suffragettes, tomb robbers, and the Master Criminal converge—the series reaches its dramatic peak.

title: The Falcon at the Portal
author: Elizabeth Peters
description: World War I looms—a murdered man in the desert and questions about Ramses's parentage threaten everything.

title: He Shall Thunder in the Sky
author: Elizabeth Peters
description: The Great War arrives in Egypt—Ramses goes undercover with Turkish spies and Amelia fights to keep her family alive.

title: Lord of the Silent
author: Elizabeth Peters
description: Post-war Egypt—tomb robbers, a dead body in Luxor, and Amelia's determination to solve one more mystery.

title: The Golden One
author: Elizabeth Peters
description: Gaza, 1917—Ramses is captured behind enemy lines and Amelia mounts a rescue through the desert.

title: Children of the Storm
author: Elizabeth Peters
description: The war is over but stolen antiquities keep turning up—along with bodies. The next generation takes center stage.

title: Guardian of the Horizon
author: Elizabeth Peters
description: A prequel filling the missing years—the Emersons return to the Lost Oasis and face old enemies.

title: The Serpent on the Crown
author: Elizabeth Peters
description: A cursed golden statue brings death to everyone who touches it—Amelia is skeptical but people keep dying.

title: Tomb of the Golden Bird
author: Elizabeth Peters
description: Howard Carter is about to discover Tutankhamun's tomb—but murder and espionage threaten to overshadow history.

title: A River in the Sky
author: Elizabeth Peters
description: Palestine, 1910—the Emersons search for the Ark of the Covenant amid archaeological rivalries and political intrigue.

title: The Painted Queen
author: Elizabeth Peters
description: The last Amelia Peabody novel, completed by Joan Hess—the bust of Nefertiti and a final adventure in 1912 Egypt.

title: I Am Half-Sick of Shadows
author: Alan Bradley
description: A film crew invades Buckshaw for Christmas—someone is murdered during the snowstorm. Flavia is delighted.

title: Emma's Secret
author: Barbara Taylor Bradford
description: A new generation discovers Emma Harte's hidden past—old secrets threaten the family dynasty.

title: Unexpected Blessings
author: Barbara Taylor Bradford
description: The Harte women face new challenges—love, loyalty, and the price of power across three continents.

title: Just Rewards
author: Barbara Taylor Bradford
description: Linnet Harte prepares for the future—but a dangerous enemy from the past threatens the family's survival.

title: Breaking the Rules
author: Barbara Taylor Bradford
description: A new Harte generation pushes boundaries—M, a young model with a secret past, risks everything for freedom.

title: Voice of the Heart
author: Barbara Taylor Bradford
description: A legendary actress, a screenwriter, and a director—their intertwined lives span Hollywood and Yorkshire.

title: Act of Will
author: Barbara Taylor Bradford
description: Three generations of women—a Yorkshire seamstress, a painter, and a fashion designer—forge their destinies.

title: A Vintage Affair
author: Jane Green
description: A woman opens a vintage furniture store—a 1960s desk holds letters that change her understanding of family.

title: Promises to Keep
author: Jane Green
description: Four women's lives intersect when one receives a devastating diagnosis—friendship tested by mortality.

title: Another Piece of My Heart
author: Jane Green
description: A stepmother tries to bond with her husband's troubled teenage daughter—family can't be forced.

title: Tempting Fate
author: Jane Green
description: A happily married woman has an affair—the aftermath threatens to destroy everything she values.

title: Saving Grace
author: Jane Green
description: A famous author's new assistant is too perfect—she's slowly replacing Grace in her own life.

title: The Sunshine Sisters
author: Jane Green
description: A famous actress summons her three estranged daughters home—she has something to tell them.

title: The Friends We Keep
author: Jane Green
description: Six college friends reunite after thirty years—secrets and regrets surface alongside middle-aged reinvention.

title: Sister Stardust
author: Jane Green
description: 1960s Marrakech—a young woman falls into the orbit of Talitha Getty and the glamorous expatriate scene.

title: The Unexpected Mrs. Pollifax
author: Dorothy Gilman
description: A bored New Jersey grandmother volunteers for the CIA—they actually send her on a mission. She's perfect.

title: The Amazing Mrs. Pollifax
author: Dorothy Gilman
description: Mrs. Pollifax goes to Turkey to rescue an agent—her cover as a tourist is almost too convincing.

title: The Elusive Mrs. Pollifax
author: Dorothy Gilman
description: Bulgaria—Mrs. Pollifax must smuggle out a list of double agents while posing at a health spa.

title: A Palm for Mrs. Pollifax
author: Dorothy Gilman
description: Mrs. Pollifax infiltrates a Swiss clinic to find a missing plutonium shipment—her karate comes in handy.

title: Mrs. Pollifax on Safari
author: Dorothy Gilman
description: An African safari is cover for identifying an assassin—Mrs. Pollifax photographs everyone.

title: Mrs. Pollifax on the China Station
author: Dorothy Gilman
description: Mrs. Pollifax tours China—her real mission is to extract a labor camp prisoner with vital intelligence.

title: Mrs. Pollifax and the Hong Kong Buddha
author: Dorothy Gilman
description: A dying agent passes Mrs. Pollifax a message in Hong Kong—a psychic and a diamond smuggler complicate things.

title: Mrs. Pollifax and the Golden Triangle
author: Dorothy Gilman
description: Thailand's Golden Triangle—Mrs. Pollifax searches for a missing agent among opium warlords.

title: Mrs. Pollifax and the Whirling Dervish
author: Dorothy Gilman
description: Morocco—Mrs. Pollifax poses as a tourist to check on seven CIA informants. Someone is eliminating them.

title: Mrs. Pollifax and the Second Thief
author: Dorothy Gilman
description: Sicily—Mrs. Pollifax must recover a stolen document while dodging the Mafia and her own side.

title: Mrs. Pollifax Pursued
author: Dorothy Gilman
description: A young woman fleeing kidnappers crashes into Mrs. Pollifax's life—the trail leads to international arms dealers.

title: Mrs. Pollifax and the Lion Killer
author: Dorothy Gilman
description: Africa again—Mrs. Pollifax visits a friend and stumbles into a murder linked to a mining conspiracy.

title: Mrs. Pollifax, Innocent Tourist
author: Dorothy Gilman
description: Jordan—Mrs. Pollifax delivers a manuscript to a dissident author but finds him murdered.

title: Mrs. Pollifax Unveiled
author: Dorothy Gilman
description: The final mission—Mrs. Pollifax goes to the Middle East and the series concludes with characteristic warmth.

title: Winter Solstice
author: Rosamunde Pilcher
description: Five lonely people are drawn together in a Scottish estate house—healing begins as winter deepens.

title: The Man Who Made Husbands Jealous
author: Jilly Cooper
description: Lysander Hawkley cheers up neglected wives—their furious husbands form a revenge club.

title: Appassionata
author: Jilly Cooper
description: The classical music world—a female conductor battles sexism, sabotage, and scandalous love affairs.

title: Pandora
author: Jilly Cooper
description: The art world—a lost Raphael surfaces and the Rutshire set schemes to possess it.

title: Wicked!
author: Jilly Cooper
description: Two schools forced to merge—class warfare, romance, and a headmaster who looks like Heathcliff.

title: Jump!
author: Jilly Cooper
description: National Hunt racing—a syndicate of mismatched owners and a horse that might be a champion.

title: Mount!
author: Jilly Cooper
description: Flat racing—Rupert Campbell-Black returns, determined to win the Derby at any cost.

title: Vintage Murder
author: Ngaio Marsh
description: A theatrical company tours New Zealand—the manager is crushed by a jeroboam of champagne. Alleyn investigates.

title: Overture to Death
author: Ngaio Marsh
description: A booby-trapped piano kills the pianist at a village concert—the vicar's drawing room becomes a crime scene.

title: Death at the Bar
author: Ngaio Marsh
description: A man dies playing darts in a Devon pub—poisoned by cyanide on the dart. Alleyn untangles coastal rivalries.

title: Final Curtain
author: Ngaio Marsh
description: Troy paints a portrait of an aging actor—the old man dies and the family's theatrics turn deadly.

title: Scales of Justice
author: Ngaio Marsh
description: A retired colonel is murdered by a trout stream—a record fish and village rivalries are at the heart of it.

title: Dead Water
author: Ngaio Marsh
description: A Cornish island's healing spring attracts pilgrims—when the spring's guardian dies, miracles turn to murder.

title: One Plus One
author: Jojo Moyes
description: A struggling single mother and a tech millionaire share a road trip—their kids complicate everything beautifully.

series.name: The Legends of the First Empire

series.name: The Legends of the First Empire

series.name: The Legends of the First Empire

series.name: The Legends of the First Empire

description: Linden Avery returns to the Land to find it threatened by Kevin's Dirt and a resurrected evil.
